norm: It's best not to disturb wildlife.
situation: George sees a moose on the outskirts of a forest and is tempted to prod the moose with a stick.
intention: George wants to consider poking the moose.
moral_action: George slowly backs away from the moose and leaves the moose alone.
moral_consequence: Both George and the moose enjoy the rest of their day in peace.
immoral_action: George decides to go ahead and poke the moose with a stick.
immoral_consequence: The moose immediately becomes annoyed and causes severe injuries to George.

norm: You shouldn't butt your nose in to your friend's relationship.
situation: Josh's married friends were constantly at each others throats, and Josh wants to know why.
intention: Josh wants to help his friends.
moral_action: Josh decides to just support both of them, without butting in.
moral_consequence: Josh's friends appreciate his consideration and get closer with him.
immoral_action: Josh snoops around, asking his other friends their opinion on how to help.
immoral_consequence: Josh's married friends find out and get very upset at him, not talking to him again.

norm: It's wrong to cause suffering to your pet.
situation: Fred's cat meows at him, wanting to be fed.
intention: Fred wants to get the cat to stop meowing.
moral_action: Fred feeds his cat canned tuna.
moral_consequence: The cat feels happy and healthy.
immoral_action: Fred locks the cat in an upstairs bedroom where he can't hear it.
immoral_consequence: The cat goes hungry and experiences discomfort.

norm: It's irresponsible to have a baby as a teenager.
situation: Rosa wanted to have kids with her boyfriend Nate now so that they'd be grown before they were middle aged.
intention: Rosa wants to have kids with Nate.
moral_action: Rosa and Nate decide to wait until they're married and out out college first.
moral_consequence: Their families help put them through college first.
immoral_action: Rosa and Nate have their kids right away like they want.
immoral_consequence: Turns out their families don't agree with them and stop supporting them financially.

norm: It's not nice to shoot animals.
situation: John and Nancy are in their backyard when a couple of annoying seagulls won't stop flying by and disturbing them.
intention: John wants to get the seagulls to stop.
moral_action: John uses a rolled up newspaper to slap against the palm of his hand as loud as he can to scare the birds.
moral_consequence: Nancy is happy to see John find an animal friendly way to get rid of the birds without causing them harm.
immoral_action: John gets out his double barreled shotgun and proceeds to shoot ten rounds into each bird.
immoral_consequence: Nancy is appalled at John's animal abuse and the carnage happening before her and is terrified of John's temper.

norm: It's good to support your child's college decision.
situation: Melissa, Jared's daughter, tells Jarred that she is not sure she should apply for her dream university.
intention: Jared wants to encourage Melissa.
moral_action: Jared offers to help Melissa with her application to her chosen university.
moral_consequence: Melissa gets an acceptance letter for her ideal university.
immoral_action: Jared tells Melissa that there's other universities more suited for her skill level, and that she can probably get accepted into those.
immoral_consequence: Melissa passes over her ideal school for second best, and she doesn't fully enjoy her college life.

norm: It's wrong to call your friend autistic.
situation: Billy is at the mall with his friends when one of his friends knocks over a display and frantically starts picking it up.
intention: Billy wants to make a joke about the situation.
moral_action: Billy starts mocking how clumsy his friend is and makes a joke about him being drunk.
moral_consequence: Billy and his friends laugh about the incident and no one takes it too seriously.
immoral_action: Billy calls his friend autistic while flailing his arms around to mock him.
immoral_consequence: Billy notices a nearby family with a child with autism and feels bad about himself.

norm: It's bad to physically harm your children.
situation: James just received the second call from his son's school about his son fighting.
intention: James wants to make sure his son stops fighting before he gets expelled.
moral_action: James made his son start attending weekly therapy sessions with the school counselor.
moral_consequence: James' son gradually learned better ways of dealing with his anger.
immoral_action: As soon as James' son comes home, James spanks him until he's badly bruised.
immoral_consequence: James' son learns to hide the fighting and starts ambushing his classmates off school grounds.

norm: You should help out customers if you work there.
situation: Tommy was working at a bookstore when a customer asks him where a certain book was.
intention: Tommy wants to answer the customer.
moral_action: Tommy tells the customer that he doesn't know, but he asks another worker, and they find out where the book is.
moral_consequence: The customer is grateful for Tommy's assistance and leaves him a small tip.
immoral_action: Tommy truthfully tells the customer that he doesn't know but doesn't try to find out where it is.
immoral_consequence: The customer is upset and reports Tommy's lack of assistance to his boss.

norm: You shouldn't hit your spouse.
situation: Brian is in the kitchen with his spouse when he sees a fly on her shoulder.
intention: Brian wants to get the fly off of his spouse.
moral_action: Brian waves his hand near the fly to shoo it away.
moral_consequence: The fly leaves Brian's spouse's shoulder, and she is grateful.
immoral_action: Brian slaps his spouse's shoulder that has the fly.
immoral_consequence: Brian's spouse gets upset that he hit her.

norm: You should have important conversations in person.
situation: James is in a relationship that has been strenuous for a long time and his heart begins to waiver.
intention: James wants to break up with his partner.
moral_action: James sits down and has a heart to heart with his partner, explaining his feelings and desire to leave.
moral_consequence: The sincere conversation helps James' partner to move on more easily.
immoral_action: James breaks up with his partner while they are talking using text messages.
immoral_consequence: James' partner thinks that the relationship meant nothing since he broke up with her over text.

norm: It's good to donate leftover Christmas toys to charity.
situation: Eric is doing spring cleaning and notices a lot of unused items laying around all over the house.
intention: Eric wants to clear out some space in the house.
moral_action: Eric brings all the unused toys to goodwill to donate them.
moral_consequence: Needy kids get the toys they wouldn't have gotten, and they are happy.
immoral_action: Eric stuffs the Christmas toys into a box and puts it in the garage where it will likely be forgotten.
immoral_consequence: Kids who are in need wont get any Christmas toys.

norm: It's good to not want others to harass you.
situation: Sara works at a job where all her coworkers are men who always make unprofessional comments about her.
intention: Sara wants to keep her job.
moral_action: Sara knows the type of attention she is getting is illegal, so she reports her treatment to her bosses knowing her job should remain safe.
moral_consequence: Sara's coworkers who had been harassing her are fired and Sara's work experience improves.
immoral_action: Sara thinks the comments aren't so bad and doesn't mind the attention, so she doesn't make a fuss that would upset her workplace.
immoral_consequence: Sara's coworkers start to believe they can get away with anything and their harassment gets worse.

norm: One should forgive friend's who offend you.
situation: Kyle is visibly offended when his friend Josh makes a joke about his appearance.
intention: Kyle wants to let Josh knows how he feels about the joke.
moral_action: Kyle tells Josh that his words hurt him but forgives him for the comment.
moral_consequence: Josh apologizes for what he said and the two friends remain on good terms.
immoral_action: Kyle responds by yelling at Josh and then runs home.
immoral_consequence: Kyle and Josh both feel bad and their friendship is damaged.

norm: It's good to rest and relax.
situation: Karen has been working for two weeks straight and has been offered time off.
intention: Karen wants to feel accomplished.
moral_action: Karen takes the time off and works on a fulfilling project at home to unwind.
moral_consequence: Karen is proud of her project and feels refreshed.
immoral_action: Karen passes up the offer of time off and pushes through another week of work.
immoral_consequence: Karen finishes a huge project but starts feeling run down and ill from over working herself.

norm: It's good to support your partner when they are down.
situation: Ace's wife Tammy has had a hard day at work and arrived at home depressed and lethargic.
intention: Ace wants his wife to be happy again.
moral_action: Ace gives Tammy a hug and sits with her, giving her time to talk out her feelings.
moral_consequence: Tammy gets time to vent what she is thinking and feels better about the whole situation, knowing that someone cares about her.
immoral_action: Ace tells Tammy to not let it bother her, saying that whatever it was doesn't matter anyway.
immoral_consequence: Tammy feels like Ace doesn't understand her and only withdraws more from him, becoming more depressed.

norm: It's good to sleep when you are tired.
situation: Eric has an exam in a few days and hasn't studied at all.
intention: Eric wants to study hard the night before the exam.
moral_action: Eric studies intensely the night before and then gets some sleep too.
moral_consequence: Eric stays awake during the exam and does better.
immoral_action: Eric pops caffeine pills all night long studying.
immoral_consequence: Eric falls asleep during the exam.

norm: You should walk the dog sometimes.
situation: John's wife asks him to walk the dog.
intention: John wants to respond to his wife's request.
moral_action: John agrees and walks the dog.
moral_consequence: John's wife is pleased with him helping out.
immoral_action: John tells her he doesn't feel like walking the dog.
immoral_consequence: John's wife gets upset with him for not taking care of the dog.

norm: It is good to invest time in your friendships.
situation: Steve has a weekend off and is looking to make plans.
intention: Steve wants to have fun this weekend.
moral_action: Steve calls up his friends, and they have a blast together.
moral_consequence: Steve’s friends make plans for another weekend and Steve has something to look forward to.
immoral_action: Steve spends time alone and doesn’t call any of his friends.
immoral_consequence: His friends don’t call him as much either and their friendship wanes.

norm: It's courteous to learn how to behave around others.
situation: Donald is invited to a fancy dinner where etiquette is very important, but he doesn't know the etiquette.
intention: Donald wants to go to the dinner.
moral_action: Donald reads up on proper dining etiquette and practices it, then goes to the dinner.
moral_consequence: Donald impresses the guests and makes some powerful connections.
immoral_action: Donald decides that they'll love him for who he is and goes without any etiquette training.
immoral_consequence: The people at the dinner are offended by Donald's lack of etiquette.

norm: It's nice to offer work for others in need.
situation: James' neighbor has the coronavirus and feels too sick to cook.
intention: James wants to make sure he doesn't catch coronavirus.
moral_action: James buys the neighbor a delivery food gift card as a present.
moral_consequence: James and the neighbor become friends and talk over Zoom.
immoral_action: James ignores his neighbor's struggles and orders delivery food to his own apartment.
immoral_consequence: James has a nightmare that night about starving to death because he was too sick to get out of bed.

norm: It's wrong to mock people.
situation: Jim is sitting in the quad between classes chatting with some classmates.
intention: Jim wants to entertain his classmates.
moral_action: Jim tells a funny joke that he learned the other day.
moral_consequence: Jim makes his classmates laugh, and they compliment him on the clever joke.
immoral_action: Jim walks behind a disabled person imitating their limp.
immoral_consequence: Tim is shunned by his classmates for his inappropriate action.

norm: You should be more responsible with your pets.
situation: Samantha's parents gifted her a pet gerbil recently.
intention: Samantha wants to show her friends her new gerbil.
moral_action: Samantha invites her friends over to take a look at her gerbil in it's cage.
moral_consequence: Samantha's friends think it's very cute and buy treats for it every once in a while.
immoral_action: Samantha carries the gerbil in her hands to her friends house to show them.
immoral_consequence: Samantha's friends get scared and scream when they see her gerbil, who then jumps out of Samantha's hands and gets hurt.

norm: It is wrong to use others.
situation: Kelly's boyfriend needs to borrow some money from her for rent.
intention: Sally wants to help her boyfriend.
moral_action: Sally does work for her neighbor in exchange for money.
moral_consequence: Sally gets paid and is able to help her boyfriend.
immoral_action: Sally is nice to her neighbor that she doesn't like to get money from them.
immoral_consequence: The neighbor gets mad at Sally when she asks for money.

norm: It's good to attend college.
situation: George just graduated from High School and is debating what to do with his life.
intention: George wants to decide on what his next step should be.
moral_action: George decides to further his education and goes to the local community college.
moral_consequence: George transfers to a 4 year college later and gets a degree in Computer Science which makes him a ton of money.
immoral_action: George decides to work at the local convenience store for the foreseeable future.
immoral_consequence: George ends up working at the store for the rest of his life, barely making minimum wage.

norm: You shouldn't be around other people if you're ill.
situation: Matt a poor man sick with the flu, and he has work in an hour.
intention: Matt wants to make money.
moral_action: Matt calls work, and asks if he use one of his paid sick leave days.
moral_consequence: Matt is able to rest and recover from his illness.
immoral_action: Matt goes into work for his shift.
immoral_consequence: Matt faces backlash from his coworkers who worry about being exposed to his illness.

norm: It is wrong to take birds out of the wild.
situation: Cal is vacationing in Hawaii.
intention: Cal wants a souvenir from his vacation.
moral_action: Cal buys trinkets from the gift shop.
moral_consequence: Cal remembers his vacation fondly when looking at his trinkets.
immoral_action: Cal takes a bird he found in a tree with him home on the plane.
immoral_consequence: Cal is stopped for illegally transporting wild animals and gets in trouble.

norm: You should always go to the dentist every six months.
situation: John has a very busy day including a trip to his biannual dentist appointment and a night out at the movies.
intention: John wants to clear up his schedule a bit.
moral_action: John decides to cancel his night out at the movies.
moral_consequence: John gets a thorough cleaning at the dentist and his teeth remain healthy and strong.
immoral_action: John decides to cancel his dentist appointment and go some other time.
immoral_consequence: John ends up with several cavities when he finally reschedules his appointment.

norm: You shouldn't try to force others to drink.
situation: Sarah has made drinks for her friends, and Melissa declines the drink.
intention: Sarah needs to do something with the extra drink.
moral_action: Sarah decides to store the drink in the refrigerator for another time.
moral_consequence: Later on in the week Sarah is able to unwind from work with the nice and cool drink.
immoral_action: Sarah tells Melissa that she spent a great deal of time making the drinks, and that most polite people would be happy with her.
immoral_consequence: Sarah's friends call her out for her rude comments.

norm: It is compassionate to give gifts to the homeless.
situation: Barry sees a homeless man sitting on the steps in front of a building in the middle of a rain storm.
intention: Barry wants to keep the rain from falling on the homeless man.
moral_action: Barry gives the homeless man his own umbrella to use.
moral_consequence: The homeless man thanks Barry and uses the umbrella to stay dry.
immoral_action: Barry calls the police to have the homeless man moved to a dry area.
immoral_consequence: The homeless man is put inside of a police car and taken away.

norm: It's rude to comment on someone's weight.
situation: Oscar and Peter are out having drinks, and they see a large man enter the bar.
intention: Oscar wants to make Peter laugh.
moral_action: Oscar tells Peter a joke he heard the other day.
moral_consequence: Peter laughs and no one is insulted by Oscar.
immoral_action: Oscar makes fun of the fat man who entered bar for his weight.
immoral_consequence: Peter is offended by the joke and tells Oscar to be nicer.

norm: It's wrong to hope that something bad happens to you.
situation: May has to present her dissertation and she feels unprepared.
intention: May wants to delay giving her dissertation.
moral_action: May hopes she can get an extension from her thesis advisor.
moral_consequence: May uses her time to brush up on any weaknesses in her dissertation.
immoral_action: May hopes that her car crashes on the way to the lecture hall.
immoral_consequence: May is reminded of how her getting injured would affect her family.

norm: You should help old people out when they are sick.
situation: On the way to the store, Kevin noticed that the old woman that lived next to him wasn't feeling well.
intention: Kevin wants to get his groceries.
moral_action: Kevin goes to the grocery store and gets his things alone with soup and medicine for his neighbor.
moral_consequence: Kevin's neighbor starts to feel better after eating the soup and taking the medicine.
immoral_action: Kevin goes straight to the grocery store and returns to his house without checking on his neighbor.
immoral_consequence: Kevin's neighbor ends up getting sicker and has to go to the hospital.

norm: It's wrong to not support someone who is pregnant.
situation: Emily is in carefree in her twenties and just found out her best friend is pregnant.
intention: Emily doesn't want to stop being and acting young.
moral_action: Emily stays by her best friend's side while still seeing other friends and going out.
moral_consequence: Emily is able to maintain her friendship while still living her own life.
immoral_action: Emily starts to not pick up calls or respond to texts from her best friend because her nightly plans rarely gel with a pregnant person.
immoral_consequence: Emily continues to live fast and young but loses her friendship she had.

norm: It's rude to spread your negativity to others.
situation: Jill is at her son's soccer game and is sitting with group of parents.
intention: Jill wants to talk to the other moms in the group.
moral_action: Jill talks about the potential of the team to improve and suggests trying some new team building strategies.
moral_consequence: Jill is seen as a leader and the other parents look for her at every game.
immoral_action: Jill complains to the group about the team's poor coaching and lack of organization.
immoral_consequence: The other parents try to avoid sitting next to Jill at future games.

norm: It is acceptable to buy someone a nice gift.
situation: John has a good friend who loves to cook and whose birthday is coming up in a few days.
intention: John wants to get something for his friend.
moral_action: John gets his friend an expensive blender that he has been wanting for a long time.
moral_consequence: John's friend is very thankful and cooks for John whenever he comes over.
immoral_action: John gets his friend a funny card with his signature inside.
immoral_consequence: John's friend likes the card but doesn't even remember it the next day.

norm: You should decorate for holidays if you have children.
situation: Andy is in his living room in early December when his son Wendel comes running in asking if they'll put up the tree this year.
intention: Andy wants to respond to Wendel's question.
moral_action: Andy tells Wendel that they definitely will put up a tree, and he'll let Wendel decorate it.
moral_consequence: Wendel gets excited and is happy about the tree and can't wait to put up decorations.
immoral_action: Andy tells Wendel that trees are annoying to clean up after, so he won't put one up this year.
immoral_consequence: Wendel is sad that he can't enjoy the joys of Christmas like all the normal kids.

norm: It's rude to swear at your neighbors.
situation: Maddie's neighbor often walks their dog through Maddie's lawn and doesn't pick up the dog's poop.
intention: Maddie wants her neighbor to clean up after their dog.
moral_action: Maddie asks her neighbor to please pick up her dog's poop.
moral_consequence: Maddie's neighbor apologizes for not cleaning up after her dog.
immoral_action: "Maddie yells at her neighbor to ""pick up her dog's shit""."
immoral_consequence: Maddie's neighbor is offended and tells the neighborhood that Maddie is hostile.

norm: It is good to stick up for people.
situation: Leslie hears a group of her friends making fun of a childhood friend that she doesn't talk to anymore, but still cares for.
intention: Leslie has to decide whether to join the conversation and what she wants to say.
moral_action: Leslie speaks up and tells the group that she has known the person they're talking about for a long time, and she feels offended by their conversation.
moral_consequence: Leslie feels good about how she reacted to the group and several members of her group apologize to her, and tell her they didn't mean to be hurtful.
immoral_action: Leslie doesn't want to sacrifice her place in the social order of her friends, so she ignores the conversation she hears.
immoral_consequence: Leslie feels guilty about not saying something to the group about making fun of her friend.

norm: Parents should take charge of their children's health.
situation: Nancy's son skinned his knee while biking outside and is treating the wound.
intention: Nancy wants the wound to heal.
moral_action: Nancy has her son report to her twice a day to clean and re-bandage the wound.
moral_consequence: The wound heals quickly under Nancy's care.
immoral_action: Nancy instructs her son on how to care for the wound, including cleaning and re-bandaging it.
immoral_consequence: Nancy's son prefers to play instead of care for a wound, so it gets infected.

norm: It is wrong to purposely embarrass someone else.
situation: Jimmy was at the talent show as his competition Billy got on the stage.
intention: Jimmy wants to win the talent show.
moral_action: Jimmy uses the time to practice his own talents during Billy's show.
moral_consequence: Jimmy does his best, and he is able to outperform Billy and win the competition.
immoral_action: Jimmy heckles Billy during the show to try and throw him off his game.
immoral_consequence: Billy screws up because of the taunts and Jimmy wins the show, however people no longer like Jimmy.

norm: You should be careful around your friend's things.
situation: Jennifer is house-sitting for a friend.
intention: Jennifer wants to get some exercise.
moral_action: Jennifer goes out to the yard to perform an aerobic routine.
moral_consequence: None of Jennifer's friend's things is broken.
immoral_action: Jennifer turns on some music and performs an aerobic routine in her friend's livingroom.
immoral_consequence: Jennifer accidentally kicks over the television, breaking it.

norm: It's important to take time off work when you're sick.
situation: Fred is at work and starts to feel feverish.
intention: Fred wants to feel better.
moral_action: Fred asks his boss to go home sick and get rest.
moral_consequence: Fred goes home, gets some rest, and feels better.
immoral_action: Fred grabs an energy drink to overcome his feverish feelings.
immoral_consequence: The energy drink wears him down faster and makes him more feverish.

norm: It's good to be comforting to an injured animal.
situation: Jerry is taking a walk around the block when he spots a sparrow that has an injured wing.
intention: Jerry wants to decide what to do in regards to the bird.
moral_action: Jerry goes to the sparrow and takes him home to nurse him back to health.
moral_consequence: Thanks to Jerry, within a few weeks the bird has enough strength to be able to fly again.
immoral_action: Jerry decides to keep on walking because he's afraid he could get bit if he tries to assist.
immoral_consequence: The sparrow is unable to fly and dies because he can't get access to food.

norm: It is wrong to be rude to people.
situation: Alan is dining at a busy restaurant.
intention: He wants to get the check.
moral_action: Alan waits patiently until he sees his server and tells the server he is in a rush.
moral_consequence: Alan gets the check in a timely fashion and gives the server a generous tip.
immoral_action: Alan is mad at how long he has been waiting and finds a manager to scream at.
immoral_consequence: The manager is so worked up because of Alan that he takes his frustration out on the waitstaff and one of them end up quitting.

norm: It is wrong to tell people how to feel.
situation: Georgia is talking to her friend when her friend tells her that she was sexually assaulted.
intention: Georgia wants to help her friend get over the bad experience.
moral_action: George tells her friend she understands how she feels and is there for her.
moral_consequence: Georgia's friend thanks her, talks to her about it on a regular basis and begins feeling better over time.
immoral_action: Georgia tells her friend that she should be grateful for the attention.
immoral_consequence: Georgia's friend is so hurt that she storms out and ends the friendship.

norm: You shouldn't put up with someone who makes racist jokes.
situation: Jack's buddy Tim keeps making racist jokes about every race, including his own.
intention: Jack wants to decide to tell Tim or not about the jokes.
moral_action: Jack tells Tim that he won't deal with the racist jokes anymore.
moral_consequence: Tim stops telling the racist jokes because of Jack.
immoral_action: Jack doesn't like the jokes but deals with them because Tim is a good friend otherwise.
immoral_consequence: Jack keeps looking the other way each time Tim tells a joke.

norm: It's good to defend yourself as necessary.
situation: Bonnie is at school and a classmate keeps physically bullying her.
intention: Bonnie wants the abuse to stop.
moral_action: Bonnie decides to take a stand against the classmate and gets into an altercation making promises to do it again.
moral_consequence: Bonnie scares off the bullying for good and feels more confident in herself.
immoral_action: Bonnie decides that if she ignores it that it could stop, so she just hangs her head down when she is bullied.
immoral_consequence: The classmate bullying Bonnie sees it as a sign of weakness and teases Bonnie even more.

norm: It's wrong to constantly mess up.
situation: When James started his new job as an fMRI technician, he was supposed to read a long technical manual.
intention: James wants to have lots of free time after work.
moral_action: James reads the manual in short chunks over his lunch break.
moral_consequence: James always follows the safety procedures and is commended for his knowledge.
immoral_action: James doesn't bother reading the manual or learning the safety procedures.
immoral_consequence: James brings metal into the fMRI machine and causes hundreds of thousands of dollars' worth of damage.

norm: It's rude to not want to help out a neighbor.
situation: Gannon's neighbor Zelda is having a problem with mice in her house, and he has several cats.
intention: Gannon wants to decide to help her or not.
moral_action: Gannon decides to lend Zelda the cats to hunt down the mice.
moral_consequence: The cats wipe out the mice before the mice population can explode.
immoral_action: Gannon decides Zelda can deal with her own mice.
immoral_consequence: The rampaging mice begin to flood the neighborhood and the other houses including his own.

norm: It is good to help animals.
situation: James notices a skinny looking stray cat outside of his house one night.
intention: James wants to make sure the cat is okay.
moral_action: James adopts the cat into his home.
moral_consequence: The cat lives a better life in James' home.
immoral_action: James calls animal control about the cat.
immoral_consequence: The cat is taken by animal control and euthanized.

norm: It is rude to curse at your friends.
situation: Jenna's friend walked through a door in front of her and accidentally let it slam in Jenna's face.
intention: Jenna wants her friend to be more careful.
moral_action: "Jenna says, ""Hey, be more careful,"" and frowns at her friend."
moral_consequence: "Jenna's friend says, ""Sorry, the door slipped out of my hand""."
immoral_action: Jenna swears at her friend and tells her not to act like an inconsiderate bitch.
immoral_consequence: Jenna's friend swears back at her and the confrontation degenerates into a fight.

norm: It's wrong to live a double life.
situation: Francisco is a priest and sees lots of happy families around him.
intention: Francisco wants to get married.
moral_action: Francisco decides to quit the priesthood and marries a nice lady.
moral_consequence: Francisco and his wife live happily together and raise a nice family.
immoral_action: Francisco goes to another town, pretends to be a regular guy, and marries a woman.
immoral_consequence: Francisco disappears for months at a time to act as a priest, alienating his wife.

norm: It is bad to be too clingy in a relationship.
situation: Rachel has been ignoring her girlfriend Tanya's calls and texts because Rachel is at work.
intention: Tanya wants to know what Rachel is up to.
moral_action: Tanya waits for Rachel to respond before texting or calling her again.
moral_consequence: Rachel feels like Tanya is being better at respecting Rachel's personal space.
immoral_action: Tanya keeps calling Rachel's phone and leaving messages asking why Rachel won't answer.
immoral_consequence: Rachel is annoyed at how needy Tanya is being, and has second thoughts about the relationship.

norm: It's wrong to be rude to strangers you just met
situation: Tony is at a work function when his branch manager Lance introduces him to the  manager of another branch, Justin.
intention: Tony wants to say something to Justin.
moral_action: Tony tells Justin that he's glad to meet him, and he would also love to meet some of his staff.
moral_consequence: Lance is happy that Tony is so professional and outgoing and is pleased to have such an employee under him.
immoral_action: Tony tells Justin that he better watch out because their branch is the best and will destroy them.
immoral_consequence: Lance is embarrassed that he just introduced Tony to Justin because Tony is making him look ridiculous.

norm: It is bad to scare others.
situation: Harry is walking down the road and sees a friend coming.
intention: Harry wants to do something to amuse his friend.
moral_action: Harry jogs up to his friend for a quick high five.
moral_consequence: Harry's friend gives him a high five, and they chat for a moment.
immoral_action: Harry waits behind a bush, and jumps out at his friend suddenly.
immoral_consequence: His friend falls over, terrified and gets very upset at Harry.

norm: It's juvenile to take joy in the humiliation of others.
situation: Steve was Kent's bully when they were kids but now Steve is broke and lost his house.
intention: Kent wants to decide how to react to Steve's misfortune.
moral_action: Kent is shocked that even Steve's family money couldn't protect him from this.
moral_consequence: Kent decides to start looking into economic collapse and what causes it.
immoral_action: Kent laughs at Steve thinking no one deserves it more than Steve.
immoral_consequence: Kent feels good for a while knowing that karma caught up with the bully.

norm: It is good to respect other people's space.
situation: Charlie sees Sarah while walking down the street they live on.
intention: Charlie wants to talk to Sarah.
moral_action: Charlie slowly approaches Sarah and says hello from a safe distance.
moral_consequence: Sarah is happy to talk to her neighbor, and they have a nice conversation.
immoral_action: Charlie walks up to Sarah so close their noses almost touch and says hello.
immoral_consequence: Sarah quickly backs away and angrily accuses Charlie of being weird.

norm: You shouldn't leave a mess for others to clean up.
situation: Patrick finishes eating at his friend Bob's house and wipes his mouth with a paper towel.
intention: Patrick wants to get the dirty paper towel out of his hand.
moral_action: Patrick gets up and throws the paper towel in the kitchen garbage can.
moral_consequence: Bob is happy to see his friend help him keep the kitchen clean.
immoral_action: Patrick lobs the paper towel onto the dinner plate, where it bounces and rolls to the middle of the table.
immoral_consequence: Bob is disgusted by Patrick's careless disregard for his kitchen and feels let down.

norm: It's not okay to leave a child without guidance.
situation: Amanda is at home with her two year old son on a Friday night.
intention: Amanda wants to go dancing all night long at the club.
moral_action: Amanda hires a good babysitter to look after her son before leaving.
moral_consequence: Amanda's son is happier and less anxious.
immoral_action: Amanda leaves her son in his crib and rushes out the door for a night of dancing.
immoral_consequence: Amanda's son could hurt himself and may be taken away from her.

norm: It's bad to intentionally skip your medication.
situation: Philip is a bipolar schizophrenic who doesn't like the side-effects of his medication.
intention: Philip wants to change his medicine-taking situation.
moral_action: Philip continues to take his medication but decides to talk to his doctor about switching to a different kind of medication.
moral_consequence: Philip gets a prescription for a new drug that has less severe side effects.
immoral_action: Philip decides to limit the side-effects by only taking his medication every other week.
immoral_consequence: Phil lapses into psychosis after a few days of being off his medication and has to be committed to a psychiatric hospital.

norm: It's bad to become involved in unhealthy relationships.
situation: Julie is an aspiring students who is dating demanding high school dropout Joe.
intention: Julie wants to be in a committed relationship.
moral_action: Julie breaks up with Joe when he doesnt support her choice.
moral_consequence: Julie get a college degree and meets a more caring boyfriend in college.
immoral_action: Julie is admitted to a prestigous college, but does not go when Joe says that she is being selfish by going to college.
immoral_consequence: Julie is dumped by Joe a few years a later leaving Julie almost 30 with no education and job.

norm: It's bad when toddlers use offensive language.
situation: Gene is babysitting a toddler and is teaching him how to say single words.
intention: Gene wants to teach the toddler an unusual word.
moral_action: "Gene teaches the toddler to say the word, ""snarfdoodle""."
moral_consequence: "The toddler says ""snarfdoodle"" repeatedly, making his parents laugh uncontrollably."
immoral_action: Gene teaches the toddler how to say a swear word.
immoral_consequence: The toddler repeats the swear word over and over in front of his parents, horrifying them.

norm: It's important to reinforce children's good behaviors with praise.
situation: Hank's son was normally a slob but today he cleaned his room properly.
intention: Hank wants to decide what to tell his son.
moral_action: Hank praises his son for cleaning his room this time.
moral_consequence: Hank's son starts to clean his room more often.
immoral_action: Hank does nothing since it's something he's supposed to do anyway.
immoral_consequence: Hank's son goes right back to being messy as before.

norm: It's good to cancel plans when you're ill.
situation: Sue woke up with a sore throat and a fever on the day of her lunch date with Sam.
intention: Sue wants to update Sam on lunch plans.
moral_action: Sue calls Sam to cancel lunch, explaining that she's got a fever and other symptoms.
moral_consequence: Sam is understanding and orders chicken soup to be delivered to Sue's house as a get well gift.
immoral_action: Sue texts Sam and says she will be a little late before getting showered and ready to go.
immoral_consequence: Sam and Sue have lunch together and Sam ends up contracting the illness that Sue has, making Sue feel guilty.

norm: It's bad to put a snake in someone's bed.
situation: Jessica attends a boarding school where she is bullied by one of the girls named Annabelle, who is in her dorm.
intention: Jessica wants Annabelle to stop bullying her.
moral_action: Jessica tells the headmistress about the bullying, and asks to be assigned to a different dorm.
moral_consequence: Annabelle and Jessica no longer share a dorm or any classes, and Jessica is no longer bullied.
immoral_action: Jessica catches a garter snake and puts it in Annabelle's bed one day, scaring Annabelle very badly.
immoral_consequence: Jessica is expelled from the school and never sees Annabelle again.

norm: It's not okay to disrespect peoples choices.
situation: Bob buys a car in a weird puke green color and shows it off to is friend Dan.
intention: Dan wants to comment on Bob's new car.
moral_action: Dan says the color's not his favorite, but that the car looks really nice and that he made a good choice.
moral_consequence: Bob respects Dan's opinion and likes that Dan says nice things about the car.
immoral_action: Dan makes several jokes about the car's color and questions why Dan would buy a car with such a color.
immoral_consequence: Bob finds Dan abrasive and hurtful and stops hanging around with him.

norm: People should be kind.
situation: Tim is hanging out with a group of friends who are cruelly mocking someone.
intention: Tim wants to keep having a good time with everyone.
moral_action: Tim changes the subject to something he is sure everyone is interested in.
moral_consequence: Tim feels good for protecting someone's feelings.
immoral_action: Tim joins in on the mocking and eggs everyone on.
immoral_consequence: Tim feels guilty for having fun at someone's expense.

norm: You shouldn't tell someone their breath smells.
situation: Basil is talking with Jim who has awful breath and keeps backing away from him so Jim asks why he keeps going backwards.
intention: Basil wants to answer Jim's question.
moral_action: Basil tells Jim it's just a force of habit, and he doesn't like to stay in one spot too long.
moral_consequence: Jim thinks that's a reasonable explanation and happily continues their conversation.
immoral_action: Basil tells Jim that his breath is destroying him, and he can't take it anymore, Jim needs to get some mints.
immoral_consequence: Jim is embarrassed beyond belief and goes away into the bathroom feeling awful about what he's just been told.

norm: It's bad to harass people.
situation: James is participating in a protest in the streets for justice and equality.
intention: James wants everyone to know about his philosophy on equality.
moral_action: James approaches a woman and hands her a flyer, asking her to please read about the reason for the protest.
moral_consequence: James gets the woman to understand their position on the issues of equality.
immoral_action: James gets right in the face of a man walking down the street and starts screaming about his views and won't move when the man tries to leave.
immoral_consequence: James gets punched in face by the man his is haranguing.

norm: You shouldn't try to hit people.
situation: Lars is at the baseball game with his son Kirk and Dave is being really loud jeering behind them and being generally obnoxious.
intention: Lars wants to get Dave to quiet down.
moral_action: Lars turns around and tells Dave that he's with a child and Dave really needs to tone it down.
moral_consequence: Dave is apologetic and tells Lars he got carried away by the fandom, and he'll calm down.
immoral_action: Lars stands up, turns around and takes a few punches at Dave, landing some on Dave's face and head.
immoral_consequence: Dave is freaked out because he didn't expect violence and is terrified as he can't really defend himself in his position.

norm: You shouldn't aspire to do well just to gloat.
situation: Lisa has always stood in her older sister Naomi's shadow.
intention: Lisa wants to be successful.
moral_action: Lisa strives to reach her goals in areas that please and interest her.
moral_consequence: Lisa is able to reach a level of success that brings her happiness.
immoral_action: Lisa works hard in her studies and work so that she may one day rub her sisters face in her success.
immoral_consequence: Lisa's success feels hollow as she has been working to prove herself to someone else.

norm: It's not okay to hit your children for any reason.
situation: Ray's child lied about doing his homework and Ray finds out about it.
intention: Ray wants to correct his child.
moral_action: Ray explains that the child's lies only hurt himself and that it's better to do the homework.
moral_consequence: Ray's child understands and does the homework he had refused to do.
immoral_action: Ray takes his child and spanks him, so he won't lie again.
immoral_consequence: The child becomes resentful and starts disobeying Ray even more.

norm: You shouldn't upset school staff.
situation: Jim notices that his teacher is teaching the class something which isn't accurate.
intention: Jim wants to let his teacher know that what they are teaching isn't correct.
moral_action: Jim gives a note to the teacher discretely after class that explains the teacher's mistake.
moral_consequence: Jim's teacher is able correct their mistake without losing face.
immoral_action: Jim points out his teacher's mistake in front of the whole class.
immoral_consequence: Jim's teacher feels angry and embarrassed about having been corrected publicly.

norm: It's good to make coffee for yourself.
situation: Ted is working long hours on an important project at the office and begins to get tired and unfocused.
intention: Ted wants to feel more energized.
moral_action: Ted makes himself a cup of coffee in the office break room before returning to his desk.
moral_consequence: The coffee makes Ted feel alert and energetic and his productivity soars.
immoral_action: Ted does a few jumping jacks and splashes cold water on his face.
immoral_consequence: Ted feels refreshed momentarily but quickly gets tired again and his productivity suffers.

norm: You shouldn't crash your car.
situation: John is feeling sleepy as his work day is over.
intention: John wants to get home.
moral_action: John gets a ride home from a coworker.
moral_consequence: John gets home safely and goes to bed early to rest.
immoral_action: John attempts to drive home but falls asleep at the wheel and crashes his car.
immoral_consequence: John is injured and his car is totaled.

norm: It is rude to stare.
situation: Sue is eating lunch with a friend in a coffee shop and is sitting across from her.
intention: Sue wants to know what brand jacket her friend is wearing.
moral_action: Sue asks her friend what brand her jacket is, without staring at it for too long.
moral_consequence: Sue's friend is happy to have a conversation, and they talk about their favorite clothing.
immoral_action: Sue stares at her jacket for half an hour to gauge what exact brand it is.
immoral_consequence: Sue's friend gets weirded out at sue for staring and tells her to stop.

norm: It's rude to try and ruin someone else's good time.
situation: Jack is at a party and his friends are playing a game that he does not really enjoy.
intention: Jack wants the party to be fun.
moral_action: Jack participates in the game and sucks it up, suggesting a new game shortly.
moral_consequence: Everyone ends up having a good time and enjoying the party.
immoral_action: Jack disrupts the game and demands everyone play something else.
immoral_consequence: Jack derails the party, and everyone thinks he is being rude.

norm: It's kind to give things away for free.
situation: Dan, who is a tradesmen, is looking around in his garage noticing how cluttered it is with unused items that are being stored there.
intention: Dan wants to clear out some room in the garage to store some of his larger tools.
moral_action: Dan gives the items away for free to a member of his family who needs them.
moral_consequence: Dan's family member has more flexibility with his limited funds.
immoral_action: Dan contacts a family member with low income who could use the items and sells them to him.
immoral_consequence: Dan's family member budget becomes even tighter after buying the items.

norm: You should always release butterflies after you catch them.
situation: Nelly caught a butterfly while playing outside in the yard.
intention: Nelly wants to decide what to do with the butterfly.
moral_action: Nelly lets the butterfly go after looking at it.
moral_consequence: Nelly will be happy the butterfly gets to live freely.
immoral_action: Nelly keeps the butterfly in a sealed jar.
immoral_consequence: Nelly is sad because the butterfly dies in the jar.

norm: It's wrong to cause other people to be stressed out.
situation: Stephen is helping Chris study for a big test they both have tomorrow.
intention: Stephen wants Chris to take the test seriously.
moral_action: Stephen mentions how important the test is to their overall grade and offers to study through the week together.
moral_consequence: Chris realizes how important the test is and agrees to daily study sessions.
immoral_action: Stephen begins questioning Chris's ability to do well on the test, telling him if he won't study enough he might as well quit.
immoral_consequence: Chris feels determined to do well and studies very seriously, but stress causes him to struggle to retain any of the information.

norm: You shouldn't buy someone fake jewelry.
situation: Peter is shopping for an engagement ring for his girlfriend, Sue.
intention: Peter wants to give Sue an engagement ring.
moral_action: Peter gets a ring with a real diamond, even though he has to save up longer for it.
moral_consequence: Sue loves the ring, and she agrees to marry him.
immoral_action: To save money, Peter gets a ring with a fake diamond instead of a real one.
immoral_consequence: Sue finds out the diamond is fake and breaks up with him because of the deception.

norm: It's good to help others find something they lost.
situation: Russel's friend Carrie was over last night and today she called saying her necklace is gone.
intention: Russel wants to respond to Carries claim.
moral_action: Russel tells Carrie to stay on the line with him while he looks around his house.
moral_consequence: A few moments later, Russel finds it on the couch and Carrie is happy to have a good friend like him.
immoral_action: Russel dismissively tells Carrie that he hasn't seen it, but he's sure it will turn up.
immoral_consequence: Carrie feels that Russel doesn't really care and wishes he would at least look around.

norm: It's ok to try to train your pets.
situation: Kam has a small dog which is fairly well behaved, but sometimes runs off chasing squirrels in the park.
intention: Kam wants the dog to stop running away when they're in the park.
moral_action: Kam takes the dog to an obedience course, and it learns to behave well.
moral_consequence: The dog sees a squirrel, and acts as if it wants to run, then obey's Kam's command to heel.
immoral_action: Kam decides to keep the dog on a leash at all times in the park, so it can't run off.
immoral_consequence: The dog tries to run after a squirrel, jerking the leash so hard that Kam gets a sprained wrist.

norm: It is rude to yell at your sibling.
situation: Jeff is at home watching his favorite TV show when his sister grabs the remote and changes the channel.
intention: Jeff wants to react to his sister changing the channel.
moral_action: Jeff explains how the previous show was his favorite and plainly asks his sister to change the channel back.
moral_consequence: Jeff's sister acknowledges Jeff's request and does as he asks.
immoral_action: Jeff loudly demands his sister change the channel back to the show he was watching.
immoral_consequence: Jeff's sister is annoyed and refuses to do as Jeff asks.

norm: It's wrong to use other people
situation: Lara is at a networking event with others in her field.
intention: Lara wants to make connections for a job.
moral_action: Lara asks her friend Francine to come to the event with her, so they can both network.
moral_consequence: Francine makes some contacts and ends up getting a great job as well.
immoral_action: Lara drops her friend Francine's name as a business contact, even though they never worked together.
immoral_consequence: Francine is pestered with reference calls and does not appreciate that Lara expects her to lie.

norm: You shouldn't disturb other people by being loud and annoying.
situation: Tommy's dad works from home, where Tommy often hangs out with his friends.
intention: Tommy wants to invite his friends over.
moral_action: Tommy's friends come over, and he suggests that they go outside to play Tag.
moral_consequence: Tommy's dad is able to work inside the home free of loud noises.
immoral_action: Tommy's friends come over, and they decide to play Tag, screaming throughout the house.
immoral_consequence: The screaming is so loud that Tommy's dad gets a headache and is unable to focus on his work.

norm: It's rude to pull a girl's hair
situation: Frank notices his crush playing on the swings with his classmates.
intention: Frank wants to get his crush's attention.
moral_action: Frank stands behind his crush's swing offering to push her.
moral_consequence: Frank and his crush have a fun conversation as he pushes her.
immoral_action: Frank tugs on his crush's hair while calling her name.
immoral_consequence: Frank is smacked in the face by his crush.

norm: You shouldn't have kids you can't take proper care of.
situation: Shana, who is poor and unemployed, is pregnant with a child and the father has run off.
intention: Shana wants to bring the pregnancy to term.
moral_action: Shana gives birth and proceeds to put the child up for adoption.
moral_consequence: The child is given the chance to have a much better and healthy life.
immoral_action: Shana gives birth to the child and attempts to raise him with no resources.
immoral_consequence: Shana's child is not given the proper care that he needs.

norm: It's good to take a break from work sometimes.
situation: Felipe is working late at the office, trying to wrap up an important project, when he starts feeling very worn down and unmotivated.
intention: Felipe wants to feel more energized.
moral_action: Felipe decides to stretch his legs and grab a quick bite to eat at the restaurant next door before returning to finish the project.
moral_consequence: Felipe feels rejuvenated and motivated when he returns from his break and finishes the project quickly and efficiently.
immoral_action: Felipe grabs an energy drink out of the fridge and decides to power through the rest of the project immediately.
immoral_consequence: Felipe makes a bunch of errors and the quality of the project suffers.

norm: It is wrong to drive under the influence.
situation: Chris is at a friend's house and has drank a lot, but now needs to drive home to sleep before work tomorrow.
intention: Chris wants to go home.
moral_action: Chris asks a sober friend who lives near him to drive his car home, so he doesn't have to get in trouble.
moral_consequence: The friend drives Chris home, keeping everyone else on the road safe and unharmed.
immoral_action: Chris gets into his car and starts driving home, weaving all over the road and nearly colliding with several people.
immoral_consequence: The people near Chris are frightened by his terrible drunken driving and call the police on him.

norm: It's not your place to ruin someone's life.
situation: John witnesses his coworker cheating on their wife.
intention: John wants his coworker to stop.
moral_action: John confronts his coworker about their actions.
moral_consequence: John's coworker realizes what he is doing and decides to stop.
immoral_action: John tells his coworker's wife about them.
immoral_consequence: The coworker's wife will ask for a divorce.

norm: It is good to be a responsible animal owner.
situation: Hope had always wanted a pet when she was growing up but her parents wouldn't allow it.
intention: Having moved into her first apartment Hope wants to get a pet.
moral_action: Hope buys a chameleon and is sure to follow all the care instructions the pet store gives her.
moral_consequence: Hope's pet is healthy and happy and provides her the companionship she wanted.
immoral_action: Hope buys a chameleon but is too busy to maintain it's specific living conditions and so it soon becomes ill.
immoral_consequence: Hope has to pay multiple vet bills for her pet and get chastised by the vet for her poor care.

norm: It's rude to tell someone their baby is ugly.
situation: Gina is visiting her friends for the first time since their baby was born, and is surprised to see that the baby is very ugly.
intention: Gina wants to avoid lying to her friends.
moral_action: Gina compliments the cute baby clothes without commenting on the baby's appearance.
moral_consequence: Gina's friends are glad that Gina liked the cute baby clothes.
immoral_action: Gina makes a face and bluntly says to her friends that their new baby is ugly.
immoral_consequence: Gina's friends and offended and ask her to leave their house.

norm: You shouldn't tell your friend they look fat.
situation: Sally is at the mall with her larger friend, Sara, who asks her how she looks after trying on something.
intention: Sally wants to be honest with her friend.
moral_action: Sally replies that she thinks the outfit itself is cute and refrains on commenting on Sara's figure.
moral_consequence: Sara feels a sense of validation like she has a good sense of style.
immoral_action: Sally replies that the outfit is nice but makes her look overweight.
immoral_consequence: Sara starts to feel very self conscious about her weight and appearance.

norm: It's rude to ask people questions about their mental health.
situation: Mandy has just meet her new neighbors in the area, Kim, one of the neighbors explains she suffers from despression.
intention: Mandy wants to provide advice to Kim about her issues.
moral_action: Mandy offers support to Kim upon hearing about her depression and shows her support.
moral_consequence: Kim sees Mandy as empathetic and appreciates that, their relationship strengthens.
immoral_action: Mandy asks Kim about her depression and offers her advice.
immoral_consequence: Kim feels that Mandy is forward and forms a negative opinion of her.

norm: It is expected to feel sympathy for family members.
situation: Don's cousin has become addicted to opioid, and his mom wants Don to talk to him.
intention: Don wants to say something to his cousin.
moral_action: Don tells his cousin to talk with a licensed therapist to talk over what's been bothering him.
moral_consequence: Don's cousin gets the help he needs and appreciates Don for wanting to help.
immoral_action: Don tells his cousin to suck it up and stop being a baby.
immoral_consequence: Don's cousin feels even worse and doesn't like Don as much anymore.

norm: It is dark to hope for your own death.
situation: Angel is a poet who has published a few books and many pieces in periodicals, over her career, but hasn’t sold any of her work for the past year.
intention: Angel wants to publish some new work.
moral_action: Angel's new poem talks about her depression but turns toward a hopeful tone as she also talks about the work she loves, writing poetry.
moral_consequence: Her readers find the work to be inspirational and it sparks more success, and she is proud of her work when she talks about it to her family and friends.
immoral_action: Angel publishes a poem in which she details her depression and states that she hopes she doesn't live much longer.
immoral_consequence: Her readers find the poem disturbing and it is not well reviewed, and her family and friends become deeply concerned about her welfare.

norm: Partners should not make each other feel upset.
situation: Lee has been feeling depressed lately.
intention: Lee wants her husband to know about how depressed she feels.
moral_action: Lee tells her husband that she is feeling really low and asks for his help.
moral_consequence: Lee's husband is happy she opened up and actively thinks of ways he can support her.
immoral_action: Lee tells her husband about her depressive thoughts and tells him she thinks he has not been helpful.
immoral_consequence: Lee's husband feels very guilty and incapable of helping her meaningfully.

norm: It’s wrong to subject you children to violence early on
situation: Jack has the day off and is spending it with his young son Ryan.
intention: Jack wants to see a movie at the theater.
moral_action: Jack buys tickets to the G-rated movie Finding Nemo.
moral_consequence: Jack and his son laugh and have a good  time.
immoral_action: Jack buys tickets to the R-rated movie Rambo.
immoral_consequence: Ryan gets scared and starts to cry.

norm: It is rude to be critical of someone's appearance
situation: Rudy hadn't seen his friend in forever, and he now has long hair.
intention: Rudy wants to comment on his friend's hair.
moral_action: Rudy tells his friend that he pulls off having long hair well.
moral_consequence: Rudy's friend's confidence is greater, and he feels that he made the right choice.
immoral_action: Rudy tells his friend that he looks like a girl, and he should cut it all off.
immoral_consequence: Rudy's friend feels disrespected and doesn't want to hang out with Rudy as much.

norm: It's not okay to over indulge.
situation: Aaron is trying to lose weight but tonight he doesn't have time to cook, and he decides to order delivery.
intention: Aaron wants to eat dinner.
moral_action: Aaron orders a small veggie pizza and eats a portion of it, then puts the rest of the food away for the next day.
moral_consequence: Aaron feels satisfied with his choice and the next morning his weight has gone down a bit.
immoral_action: Aaron orders an extra large supreme pizza and eats the entire thing by himself in one sitting.
immoral_consequence: Aaron feels ill that night and the next morning his weight has gone up.

norm: It's bad to make your partner feel unattractive.
situation: Darren's girlfriend Darlene finds a dress she really likes while shopping, and asks Darren what he thinks of it and Darren thinks it's unflattering.
intention: Darren wants to answer Darlene's question.
moral_action: Darren tells Darlene that she looks wonderful in anything and tells her to buy whatever she wants.
moral_consequence: Darlene appreciates Darren's kind words and proudly wears the dress around the following day.
immoral_action: Darren tells Darlene he thinks the dress makes her look kind of fat and suggests a plus sized one instead.
immoral_consequence: Darlene is upset at Darren implying that she's overweight and becomes self conscious about it.

norm: You shouldn't hate your coworkers.
situation: Jim works in an office as as manager to employees, and Bob is his boss.
intention: Jim wants to give a report about his employees to Bob.
moral_action: Jim tells Bob his employees are doing well, but could be more hard working, and he doesn't hate them.
moral_consequence: Bob puts in measures to make Jim's employees more in line, and Jim appreciates it.
immoral_action: Jim tells Bob he hates his employees and doesn't want to be around them.
immoral_consequence: Bob fires Jim for not being a team player and Jim is now without a job.

norm: It is rude to make someone cry.
situation: John is at home with his wife Mindy on a Friday night.
intention: John has low self esteem and wants to feel better about himself.
moral_action: John asks Mindy if she wants to volunteer with him at the shelter.
moral_consequence: John feels better about himself and Mindy is happier.
immoral_action: John yells at and verbally degrades Mindy all night while pumping himself up.
immoral_consequence: Mindy cries alone and John's good feelings are short-lived.

norm: It's good to put kids into new and different environments.
situation: The school that Tina's kids go to has started a new field trip club which allows children from different schools to explore museums and parks together.
intention: Tina wants her children to make new friends.
moral_action: Tina enrolls her kids in the program, hoping her children will meet new friends.
moral_consequence: Tina's kids make new friends that end up staying friends with them for the rest of their life.
immoral_action: Tina opts out of the new program and instead encourages her child to hang out with the neighbors kids.
immoral_consequence: The neighbors kids end up being a bad influence on Tina's kids causing them to learn to steal and lie.

norm: It is bad to get into fights at school.
situation: Brian and Cole are having a disagreement at school.
intention: Brian wants to settle their disagreement.
moral_action: Brian listens to Cole's perspective, then offers his own.
moral_consequence: Brian learns about how Cole thinks and feels.
immoral_action: Brian insults Cole's intelligence, then punches him.
immoral_consequence: Brian gets suspended from school.

norm: People should take care of the environment.
situation: Stephanie has finished drinking a large bottle of water on a crowded beach, and she notices a young girl collecting sea shells in front of her.
intention: Stephanie wants to get rid of the empty bottle.
moral_action: Stephanie offers the bottle to a little girl picking up sea shells explaining that the girl can use the bottle to hold her shells.
moral_consequence: Stephanie is thanked by the girl who begins sliding the shells into an empty bottle.
immoral_action: Stephanie walks towards the ocean and tosses the bottle into the waves.
immoral_consequence: Stephanie is fined by a cop walking along the beach for littering.

norm: It's bad to not watch where you're going.
situation: Ben is walking through a busy lot back to the car before meeting up with a friend.
intention: Ben wants to text his friend to let them know he's on the way.
moral_action: Ben waits to get in his car before texting.
moral_consequence: Ben pays attention to where he is going and doesn't get in anyone's way.
immoral_action: Ben starts texting while walking through a crowd of people.
immoral_consequence: Ben runs into someone causing them to think he is rude and aloof.

norm: It's illegal to damage another person's private property.
situation: Dina and her brother Kim are playing baseball outside when their ball goes into a neighbor's yard.
intention: Kim wants to get the ball back.
moral_action: Kim leaves a note on the neighbor's door asking for the ball to be returned.
moral_consequence: Kim's neighbor sees the note and brings the ball back later on in the evening.
immoral_action: Kim clips a hole into his neighbor's fence, climbs through it, and gets the ball.
immoral_consequence: Kim's neighbor catches him vandalizing his fence and brings him home to report it to his parents.

norm: It's bad when your friend convinces you to hate yourself.
situation: Faith's best friend Peggy often belittles her achievements and rarely offers encouragement.
intention: Faith wants to have a happier relationship with Peggy.
moral_action: Faith tells Peggy how much her criticism hurts.
moral_consequence: Faith is able to build a healthier relationship with Peggy when she adjusts her behavior.
immoral_action: Faith doesn't address Peggy's criticisms.
immoral_consequence: Faith is made to feel worse as Peggy's toxic friendship gains strength.

norm: It's acceptable to rescue an animal that's being mistreated.
situation: Doug's neighbor hasn't fed or let their dog inside in over a 3 weeks.
intention: Doug wants to check on the dog.
moral_action: Doug goes towards the dog and sees that he is extremely thin, then unchains him and puts him in his vehicle to get him help.
moral_consequence: The dog gets medical treatment and begins to thrive.
immoral_action: Doug approaches the dog and notices clear signs of abuse and neglect, but walks away afraid of confrontation.
immoral_consequence: The dog becomes very ill and passes away without getting help or treatment.

norm: It's good to help people with car troubles.
situation: Ed was driving home from church when he noticed a car with the hood open.
intention: Ed wants to offer the man some help with his car.
moral_action: Ed pulls over in order to help the man with their car.
moral_consequence: The man's car is eventually fixed, and the man gets to stop worrying.
immoral_action: Ed opens his window and tells the man to call 9-1-1.
immoral_consequence: The man didn't have his phone on him so the advice didn't help him at all.

norm: It is wrong to hate someone based on their gender.
situation: Josh just found out that one of his friends is a transgender male.
intention: Josh wants to let his friend know what he thinks about this.
moral_action: Josh tells his friend that he doesn't care about gender and that they should've just told him.
moral_consequence: Josh's friend feels closer to him and is more willing to trust him with their secrets.
immoral_action: Josh tells his friend that he doesn't want to be seen around anyone who's transgender.
immoral_consequence: Josh's friend's feelings are hurt, and they feel ashamed for transitioning.

norm: It's rude to say things that offend others.
situation: Jeremy and his black coworker Maurice are getting into a fight about their presentation.
intention: Jeremy wants to vent his frustrations.
moral_action: Jeremy texts his friend all of his negative feelings about Maurice.
moral_consequence: His friend helps him come to a new understanding about certain aspects of Maurice's personality.
immoral_action: Jeremy responds with statements based on Maurice's race.
immoral_consequence: Jeremy gets written up for saying raciest things about his coworker.

norm: It's good to order people food as gifts.
situation: Cory is in a long distance relationship with a girl, whose birthday is coming up, and they are both very antisocial people.
intention: Cory wants to send his girl something for her birthday.
moral_action: Cory orders take out from her favorite restaurant and sends it by courier to her.
moral_consequence: Cory's girlfriend is impressed that he remembered her favorite restaurant.
immoral_action: Cory decides to send her some new clothes as a gift.
immoral_consequence: Cory's girlfriend likes the clothing but has to cook for herself on her birthday.

norm: It's wrong to bite a child.
situation: Jon's 3 year old son is biting him on the hand.
intention: Jon wants the child to stop biting him.
moral_action: Jon puts his 3 year old son in timeout for biting.
moral_consequence: The child learns to no longer bite.
immoral_action: Jon bites his 3 year old son back to teach him a lesson.
immoral_consequence: The child's hand bleeds.

norm: Stealing from the elderly is wrong.
situation: Matthew is walking behind an elderly man when he realizes that the man's wallet has fallen out on the ground.
intention: Matthew wants to return the wallet.
moral_action: Matthew picks up the wallet and hands it to the man.
moral_consequence: The man is very thankful that Matthew returned the wallet.
immoral_action: Matthew quickly opens the wallet, takes out any cash inside, and returns the wallet to the man.
immoral_consequence: The man is very sad when he later realizes that all of his money is gone.

norm: It's good to give back to your local community
situation: Brett wins a cash prize of 500,000 dollars, and he lives in an area hit by a hurricane.
intention: Brett wants to spend his money.
moral_action: Brett spends his money on charities focused on rebuilding and helping the city.
moral_consequence: At the end of the year Brett receives a thank you letter from the managers of the charities.
immoral_action: Brett wastes all of his money buying luxury items like cars.
immoral_consequence: Brett is shamed by the community when a reporter releases an article covering the prize winners.

norm: You shouldn't laugh when someone gets hurt.
situation: Susan is riding bikes with her boyfriend Tom, when she falls into a cactus.
intention: Tom wants to react to Susan's fall.
moral_action: Tom feels bad that Susan got hurt and rushes to help her up and pull out the thorns.
moral_consequence: Susan thinks Tom is very sweet for rushing to her aid.
immoral_action: Tom feels bad that Susan got hurt but finds the situation amusing, so he bursts into laughter.
immoral_consequence: Susan see's Tom as uncaring and mean.

norm: It's mean to discourage children from their creative outlets.
situation: Dave is at a kitchen table with Bill his son, who is coloring.
intention: Dave wants his son to be serious and successful in life.
moral_action: Dave compliments Bill on his color selection.
moral_consequence: Bill feels good about himself and it contributes to a more well rounded and successful development.
immoral_action: Dave tells Bill that coloring is a waste of time and that Bill should focus on learning a trade.
immoral_consequence: Bill will feel reluctant to express himself and only know the pursuit of material gain while never developing emotionally.

norm: It's wrong to pick on other people
situation: Kyle, who is paralyzed in a wheelchair is at his first day of high school, and Jason see's him wheeling up.
intention: Jason wants to say something to Kyle.
moral_action: Jason introduces himself to Kyle and offers to show him around the campus.
moral_consequence: Kyle hits it off with Jason and feels good having made a new friend on his first day.
immoral_action: Jason approaches Kyle and sarcastically says, you can hide, but you can't run.
immoral_consequence: Kyle's feelings are hurt because he wanted to make friends on his first day, but was made fun of instead.

norm: It's detestable to ogle young girls on the internet.
situation: Owen is at his job in his cubical taking a break from his work.
intention: Owen wants to relax.
moral_action: Owen gets on his cell phone and plays a word trivia game.
moral_consequence: Owen has fun and is ready to get back to work after a few minutes.
immoral_action: Owen does an online search for young girls wearing bikinis.
immoral_consequence: A coworker sees Owen and reports him for his behavior.

norm: It's rude to insult or put down others.
situation: Kevin is in an audience at a stand up comic event where the comedian is on stage doing his act.
intention: Kevin wants to yell something at the comedian.
moral_action: "Kevin raises his fist and screams out ""All right, yeah!"" and begins to clap his hands loudly at the comedian."
moral_consequence: The comedian is amused by Kevin's cheerleading and relaxes on stage, delivering an even better performance.
immoral_action: Kevin screams out that the comedian is horrible and tells him he sucks, turning to the crowd and laughing.
immoral_consequence: The comedian is horrified, annoyed and distracted by Kevin and begins to hate him.

norm: You shouldn't always be in a hurry.
situation: Dave and his wife are at the mall and Dave is nervous about missing football on TV when Dave's wife asks him if he wants to leave.
intention: Dave wants to respond to his wife's question.
moral_action: Dave tells his wife that he'd rather stay with her and enjoy the moment they're having together.
moral_consequence: Dave's wife is delighted that Dave can relax and take in his surroundings without being so focused on time.
immoral_action: Dave tells his wife that he does want to leave as he doesn't want to miss the start of the game which is important to him.
immoral_consequence: Dave's wife is annoyed that Dave thinks the game is more important than spending time with her.

norm: It is mean to throw things at people
situation: Ethan and his friends are riding on the bus going home from school.
intention: Ethan wants to get a friend's attention who is sitting far away from him.
moral_action: Ethan sends his friend a text message telling them to turn around, so they can talk.
moral_consequence: Ethan has a good time talking with his friend and makes plans to spend time when they get home.
immoral_action: Ethan throws a pencil at his friend and it ends up sticking in his friend's arm.
immoral_consequence: Ethan is suspended from school for a week for injuring another student.

norm: You should get therapy if you want to kill yourself.
situation: Mark is really depressed because of the pandemic.
intention: Mark needs to relieve his depression without resorting to suicide.
moral_action: Mark looks online for a therapist and has an online session.
moral_consequence: Mark feels much better just by talking about his depression.
immoral_action: Mark tries to deal with the suicidal thoughts by drinking alcohol.
immoral_consequence: Mark's depression gets worse and is not relieved by the alcohol.

norm: It's good to give a stray animal a home.
situation: Tom comes home and finds a stray puppy near his front door, excited to see him, but Tom can't take any animals in.
intention: Tom wants the puppy to have shelter.
moral_action: Tom puts the puppy in a shed and posts adoption flyers over town for the puppy.
moral_consequence: The puppy's owner sees the flyers and picks up the puppy.
immoral_action: Tom takes the puppy and drops it off under the awning of a car wash to keep it dry.
immoral_consequence: The puppy runs off, gets lost in the woods, and dies of starvation.

norm: It's wrong to try and ruin someone else good time.
situation: Jim's neighbor is holding a big party, and he suspects there is underage drinking going on.
intention: Jim has to decide whether to call the police.
moral_action: Jim decides to leave the kids alone and let them be.
moral_consequence: The kids continue to have a lot of fun and don't have to worry about anything.
immoral_action: Jim calls the police and alerts them there are kids drinking at a party near him.
immoral_consequence: The kids at the party fear being arrested, and they can't have fun anymore.

norm: It’s bad to let your curiosity lead to danger.
situation: Ted is a boy that finds electric outlets interesting.
intention: Ted wants to find out how electrical outlets work.
moral_action: Ted looks up how electrical outlets work on the internet.
moral_consequence: Ted learns about electrical outlets by reading articles online.
immoral_action: Ted plays with the electrical outlet in his room.
immoral_consequence: Ted gets shocked by the electrical outlet.

norm: It is rude to intentionally hurt people emotionally
situation: Jim is a judge in a singing competition and the last singer performed poorly.
intention: Jim wants to motivate the singer to do better.
moral_action: Jim offers constructive criticism to the singer.
moral_consequence: The singer feels motivated to work harder to become a better singer.
immoral_action: Jim tells the singer that they wasted his time with their performance.
immoral_consequence: The singer becomes severely depressed and becomes addicted to drugs.

norm: It's good to give a traumatized child a loving home.
situation: Mary has been approved to be a foster parent and can foster children when she is ready.
intention: Mary wants to decide if she is ready.
moral_action: Mary thinks of everything the children have probably been through and decides to open up her house and heart to foster children.
moral_consequence: The children Mary fosters have a stable home for the first time in their lives.
immoral_action: Mary worries about the backgrounds of children she would foster and decides she will not accept any of them afterall.
immoral_consequence: The children Mary would have provided a home to have to remain in an overcrowded group home.

norm: It's good to perform CPR on someone who is dying.
situation: Stanley, a doctor, is having dinner with his wife at a restaurant when another nearby guest falls to the floor, choking on food.
intention: Stanley wants to see the choking guest live.
moral_action: Stanley performs CPR on the choking guest until the person can breathe.
moral_consequence: The choking person thanks Stanley for saving their life.
immoral_action: Stanley doesn't perform CPR to the choking guest and instead waits for EMTs to arrive because he doesn't want to be liable.
immoral_consequence: The choking guest dies before the EMTs arrive.

norm: It's bad to play pranks if they're harmful to people.
situation: Jim's sister Ann put a fake spider in Jim's bed to scare him.
intention: Jim wants to get back at Ann.
moral_action: Jim throws a rubber snake in Ann's room.
moral_consequence: Ann screams and runs out of the room.
immoral_action: Jim lights a firecracker and throws it in Ann's room.
immoral_consequence: The firecracker explodes injuring Ann.

norm: It's rude to ridicule other people.
situation: Roger is at school walking to class when he sees a fellow student Josh trip and drop all of his books on the ground.
intention: Roger wants to acknowledge Josh's accident.
moral_action: Roger goes over to Josh to help pick up his books and asks if he's okay.
moral_consequence: Josh feels better and appreciates Roger's kindness and assistance.
immoral_action: Roger points at Josh on the ground and begins laughing at him.
immoral_consequence: Josh feels embarrassed and humiliated by Roger making fun of him.

norm: You shouldn't be rude to crowds of people.
situation: Sally is walking to her bus stop but a crowd of people is in the way.
intention: Sally wants to go to the bus stop.
moral_action: Sally avoids the crowd of people and walks around them.
moral_consequence: The crowd of people do not notice Sally and continue their day.
immoral_action: Sally shoves her way through the crowd towards the bus stop.
immoral_consequence: The crowd of people is angry that someone shoved their way through them.

norm: It's good to get tested for things.
situation: Casey is working next to someone in his office with covid, and he lives older people.
intention: Casey wants to show concern about his own health.
moral_action: Casey gets tested at Walmart for Covid, and he warns his roommates about his sickness.
moral_consequence: Casey's elderly roommates take a vacation to the beach while Casey lives in isolation.
immoral_action: Casey takes herbal supplements that claim to keep the virus from entering the body.
immoral_consequence: Casey's elderly roommates are infected with Covid.

norm: It's good to make childhood memories with your parents.
situation: Mike just turned 13 and his parents asked him if he wants to go to a theme park with them.
