intention: Mike wants to respond to the suggestion.
moral_action: Mike decides to join his parents on the outing.
moral_consequence: Mike has a wonderful time and endeavors to join his parents on more outings.
immoral_action: Mike tells his parents he is too old to be hanging out with them any longer.
immoral_consequence: When Mike is older he regrets not spending more time with his parents.

norm: It is important to be grateful for generosity.
situation: Neil has just finished handing Todd the money for a loan that Neil is giving Todd to pay off the bank.
intention: Todd wants to tell Neil he's going to pay off the bank today.
moral_action: Todd takes the money from Neil and thanks him profusely then tells him he can finally pay off the bank and is going to do so right away.
moral_consequence: Neil is happy that Todd was thankful and is glad to see his friend off to deal with the problem right away.
immoral_action: Todd takes the money from Neil and says that has to go to go deal with the bank right away.
immoral_consequence: Neil is annoyed that Todd isn't thanking him or even looking very relieved about getting the loan.

norm: You shouldn't do horrible things to people.
situation: Jenna's little sister is always sneaking into her room to steal her clothes, which makes Jenna very angry.
intention: Jenna wants her sister to stop touching her stuff.
moral_action: Jenna starts locking her door when she isn't in her room.
moral_consequence: Jenna is happy that her sister can no longer steal her clothes.
immoral_action: Jenna catches her sister going through her closet and proceeds to lock her in the dark closet overnight.
immoral_consequence: Jenna gets in trouble for traumatizing her sister and is punished by her parents.

norm: Cheering someone on at a sporting event is good.
situation: Tom has the evening off and doesn't know what to do with the time.
intention: Tom wants to find someone fun to occupy himself with.
moral_action: Tom goes to the sporting event and cheers like crazy getting into the game.
moral_consequence: Tom's friend notices him going wild and is grateful for the support.
immoral_action: Tom goes to a sporting event to support his friend who is playing but spends most of the time on his phone.
immoral_consequence: Tom's friend notices he isn't even watching and feels bad.

norm: It's good to respect your roommate's space.
situation: Trevor has a roommate who's not home and who doesn't like people going in his room.
intention: Trevor wants to borrow a book from his roommate's room.
moral_action: Trevor waits until his roommate gets home and asks to borrow the book, and they give it to Trevor.
moral_consequence: Trevor's roommate is relieved that Trevor didn't go into their private room to get the book himself.
immoral_action: Trevor goes into his roommate's room and goes through their things until he finds the book he wants.
immoral_consequence: Trevor's roommate is very angry that Trevor violated their private area.

norm: It is wrong to harm others.
situation: A popular Instagram influencer just posted something that really angered Jess.
intention: Jess doesn't want to be bothered by the influencer anymore.
moral_action: Jess unfollows the influencer on Instagram, so she doesn't see her posts anymore.
moral_consequence: The influencer doesn't notice that she unfollowed her, and Jess becomes calmer.
immoral_action: Jess calls the influencer fat in her comments section and laughs about it.
immoral_consequence: The influencer's feelings are hurt, and Jess is seen as a rude person.

norm: It's wrong to kill a living being.
situation: Jeff's dog keeps pooping on Roy's lawn and Roy is tired of it.
intention: Roy wants to stop the dog from ruining his lawn.
moral_action: Roy tells Jeff that his dog is pooping on his lawn.
moral_consequence: Jeff makes sure that his dog doesn't go near Roy's lawn in the future.
immoral_action: Roy kills the dog while it's in the middle of pooping on his lawn.
immoral_consequence: Jeff sees Roy kill the dog and attacks him out of anger.

norm: It's rude to give someone bad service on purpose.
situation: Amy is a waitress at a Waffle House, and a cop came in to eat something.
intention: Amy has to serve the cop.
moral_action: Amy gets to the cop as fast as she could and makes sure that they get their food quickly.
moral_consequence: The cop is satisfied and are glad that they aren't being judged for their uniform.
immoral_action: Amy takes her time getting to the cop and uses an attitude with the cop.
immoral_consequence: The cop's feelings are hurt, and they feel guilty for no reason.

norm: It's not okay to leave someone without a home.
situation: Dorian is a landlord to Jasmine and a father to Callie, who will soon be returning home from college.
intention: Dorian wants Jasmine out so that he can give her space to Callie.
moral_action: Dorian gives Jasmine 60 days to move out.
moral_consequence: Jasmine is able to find new living arrangements and Callie moves into her father's property.
immoral_action: Dorian evicts Jasmine without warning.
immoral_consequence: With no family, money, or anywhere to go, Jasmine is left homeless.

norm: You shouldn't make someone who is positive cry.
situation: Bart's friend Kip is very excited about a video game that is coming out.
intention: Bart wants to comment on Kip liking the video game.
moral_action: Bart says he can't wait to play the game with Kip.
moral_consequence: Kip will feel happy that Bart wants to play the game with him.
immoral_action: Bart says that video games are for babies and dumb people.
immoral_consequence: Kip will become upset and cry.

norm: It's bad to marry someone who hurts animals.
situation: John is a known animal abuser who takes Jenny out on a date and proposes to her.
intention: Jenny wants to answer John's marriage proposal.
moral_action: Jenny tells John that animal abuse is how serial killers get their start and tells him no.
moral_consequence: Jenny lives a safe any happy life and eventually meets a more reasonable man.
immoral_action: Jenny tells John that she would be honored to be his wife because she loves him.
immoral_consequence: John eventually abuses Jenny as well and hurts her physically many different times.

norm: You shouldn't practice road rage.
situation: Walt is driving in his car when another driver honks at him.
intention: Walt wants to respond to the honk.
moral_action: Walt signals to another lane and gets out of the way of the other driver.
moral_consequence: Walt and the other driver avoid each other on the road.
immoral_action: Walt honks back many times and drives very close to the other driver to tell them to pull over.
immoral_consequence: Walt and the other driver stop their cars and get into a fight.

norm: It's nice to tip people.
situation: Tom is eating out at a restaurant and has a limited amount of cash on hand.
intention: Tom wants to save some cash to use at the store.
moral_action: Tom decides to pay with his credit card and writes in a tip.
moral_consequence: Tom's server ends up making a fair amount of money during his shift.
immoral_action: Tom skips out on tipping for the meal service to hold onto enough cash for the store.
immoral_consequence: Tom's server does not make enough money to earn a living wage on his shift.

norm: You should offer help if you see somebody needs it
situation: Jeff is walking home when he sees a frail homeless man begging for food.
intention: Jeff wants to give something to the homeless man.
moral_action: Jeff gives the homeless man some money and tells him to buy some food with it.
moral_consequence: The homeless man is grateful for Jeff's kindness and is able to feed himself for a week with the money Jeff gave him.
immoral_action: Jeff walks over to the homeless man and hands him a job application.
immoral_consequence: The man cries as a result of Jeff's action before going to bed hungry.

norm: You shouldn't grab someone's arm abruptly.
situation: Ulysses is walking down the street when he notices a pretty ladies shoes are untied, and she is in danger of tripping of her own laces.
intention: Ulysses wants to get her attention so that he can let her know about her laces being untied.
moral_action: Ulysses waves at her to get her attention, and then tells her about her laces.
moral_consequence: The girl is very grateful and offers to buy Ulysses a drink afterwards.
immoral_action: Ulysses runs over and grabs her by the arm in order to turn her to face him.
immoral_consequence: The girl screams at Ulysses's, slaps him, and tries running away resulting in her tripping, falling and hurting herself.

norm: It's wrong to call people making complaints cry babies.
situation: Nathan looks out of the window annoyed as his classmate Charles makes negative comments about their field trip and conditions of the bus.
intention: Nathan wants Charles to stop talking about negative things.
moral_action: Nathan tells Charles that they should play a game on the ride home.
moral_consequence: In the future Charles plays the game whenever he's in a car with someone.
immoral_action: Nathan tells Charles that his criticism is akin to a two year old having a temper tantrum, and that he should stop before he continues to embarrasses himself.
immoral_consequence: Charles blushes and sinks down into his seat completely humiliated.

norm: It's nice to help your friends fix their vehicles.
situation: Randy is at home when his friend Lawrence calls him and tells him his car has broken down.
intention: Randy wants to respond to Lawrence's news.
moral_action: Randy tells Lawrence to tell him where he is, so he can come pick him up and get his car fixed.
moral_consequence: Lawrence is delighted Randy will come right away, and they can start fixing the car.
immoral_action: Randy tells Lawrence that it sounds pretty bad, and he hopes Lawrence will get help.
immoral_consequence: Lawrence is annoyed because he called Randy for help, not for Randy to tell him it was a bad situation.

norm: It's mean to push children.
situation: Connor is on the playground and sees a lineup of children waiting to use the slide.
intention: Connor wants to use the slide.
moral_action: Connor waits his turn in line and then goes down the slide.
moral_consequence: Connor and the other children talk to each other about how much fun they had going down the slide as the wait in line to go again.
immoral_action: Connor pushes the other children out of the way and goes down the slide.
immoral_consequence: Connor is removed from the playground by a teacher and doesn't get to ride the slide anymore.

norm: It's wrong to sexually harass people.
situation: John sees his co-worker Jill standing at the water cooler.
intention: John wants to give Jill a compliment.
moral_action: John walks over to Jill and tells her he likes the color of her shirt.
moral_consequence: Jill is flattered and compliments John on his clothing as well.
immoral_action: John walks over to Jill and says the shirt she is wearing make her breasts look good.
immoral_consequence: Jill is offended and files a complaint against John with human resources.

norm: It's understandable to look for your father when he's missing.
situation: Dan is at home one evening when he notices that its getting late and his father, who lives with him, still hasn't returned home after work.
intention: Dan wants to locate his father.
moral_action: Dan goes out looking for his father at all the places he thinks he might be.
moral_consequence: Dan finds his father having drinks at a local bar and feels relieved that everything is okay.
immoral_action: Dan sends his father a text message asking where he is but receives no response.
immoral_consequence: Dan doesn't figure out where is father is and worries all night about it.

norm: It's not okay to risk a friendship.
situation: Tammy has been single for a while, and she realizes she can't stop thinking about the crush she has on a friend's ex-boyfried.
intention: Tammy wants to pursue a relationship with her friend's ex-boyfriend.
moral_action: Tammy contacts her friend to see if her friend would have any feelings about her dating the ex-boyfriend.
moral_consequence: Tammy's friend is supportive of Tammy calling the ex-boyfriend and wishes Tammy the best.
immoral_action: Tammy contacts her friend's ex-boyfriend, and they start dating and run into Tammy's friend while out one night.
immoral_consequence: Tammy's friend is upset and does not understand when she sees Tammy with her ex-boyfriend.

norm: It's good to overcome the disease of alcoholism.
situation: Tim's is a frequent drinker who is under a lot of stress and finds himself drinking more every year.
intention: Tim wants to relieve his stress and choose how much to drink.
moral_action: Tim recognizes that his drinking is out of control and gets help to fix it.
moral_consequence: Tim learns other ways to cope with stress and now only drinks one or two beers on special occasions.
immoral_action: Tim does not cut back on drinking and now drinks large amounts every day.
immoral_consequence: Tim loses his job and family and has major health issues by his early 40's.

norm: It's good to encourage other people
situation: Matt sees his friend Ian out jogging.
intention: Matt wants to make small talk with Ian.
moral_action: Matt jogs along with Ian for a bit, chatting about how impressive Ian's commitment to fitness is.
moral_consequence: Ian continues to jog everyday, getting in better shape.
immoral_action: Matt makes fun of Ian for how lame a hobby jogging is.
immoral_consequence: Ian stops jogging, growing morbidly obese.

norm: It's nice to compliment someone when they look good.
situation: Joe has a partner who is dressed up quite nicely for an event they are attending together.
intention: Joe wants to have a good time at the event.
moral_action: Joe compliments his partner in front of his friends while they have drinks together.
moral_consequence: Joe's partner feels beautiful and loved.
immoral_action: Joe ignores the effort his partner put in to look good and focuses on having fun with his friends there.
immoral_consequence: Joe's partner feels underappreciated and is very sad.

norm: You should apologize if you've accidentally injured someone.
situation: Nathan and Pablo are playing basketball when Nathan accidently trips Pablo.
intention: Nathan wants to deal with who is responsible.
moral_action: Nathan says he's sorry and asks Pablo if he's okay.
moral_consequence: Pablo says he's fine and Nathan helps him up off the ground.
immoral_action: Nathan tells Pablo that he should have watched where he was going.
immoral_consequence: Pablo gets pissed at Nathan and tackles him to the ground.

norm: It's wrong to blow someone up.
situation: Spencer is an explosive expert working at the local mine where local teenagers have been trespassing at lately.
intention: Spencer wants to stop the trespassing.
moral_action: Spencer calls the local authorities to crack down on the trespassers since he is conducting blasts soon.
moral_consequence: Spencer is able to do his job effectively after the trespasser are thwarted by the local authorities.
immoral_action: Spencer knows there are proper warning signs, so he initiates a mining blast despite knowing a trespasser is in the area.
immoral_consequence: Spencer is arrested and found guilty for his role in blowing up a trespasser.

norm: People should keep their temper.
situation: Benny watches his clumsy coworker Miles drop a coffee cup on the ground.
intention: Benny wants the mess cleaned up.
moral_action: Benny hugs Miles before asking Miles to clean up the mess.
moral_consequence: Miles feels a greater sense of responsibility, and he changes his actions to account for his clumsiness.
immoral_action: Benny criticizes Mile's clumsiness in a loud tone of voice as he cleans the mess.
immoral_consequence: Mile's breaks down crying in the office.

norm: It is good to stop being a bully.
situation: Ollie had a habit of picking on his classmates every day at school.
intention: Ollie wants to walk home from school.
moral_action: Ollie helped one of his classmates carry her bags home because she was on crutches.
moral_consequence: Ollie's classmate invited him inside her house to have cookies and milk to thank him.
immoral_action: Ollie shoved one of his classmates off of his bicycle when he passed him on the way home.
immoral_consequence: Ollie eventually had to go to juvenile hall because of the way that he treated others.

norm: It's kind to cook a meal for friends and family.
situation: Dave and a tired Wendy are in the kitchen as dinner time is approaching when their son Max tells them he's hungry.
intention: Dave wants to decide what the family will eat.
moral_action: Dave tells Max that he's going to look up some recipes online and cook a meal for the family since Wendy can't.
moral_consequence: Wendy is delighted that Dave cares enough to cook for the family even though he doesn't normally do so.
immoral_action: Dave tells Max that since Wendy's tired today and can't cook he's just going to order a pizza tonight.
immoral_consequence: Wendy feels concerned that Max is going to eat unhealthy and that Dave doesn't seem to care.

norm: You shouldn't feed local wildlife.
situation: Erin is an amateur photographer hiking in a local park when she encounters some cute squirrels.
intention: Erin wants to take a good picture of the squirrels.
moral_action: Erin lays still in the grass, so she doesn't scare the squirrels and waits for them to come closer.
moral_consequence: Erin gets a good natural shot and the squirrels go about their lives undisturbed.
immoral_action: Erin puts some food out to lure the squirrels closer, so she can get a good picture of them.
immoral_consequence: Erin gets her pictures but the squirrels get sick from eating food that they weren't built to digest.

norm: Siblings are expected to do nice things for each.
situation: Mila and her sister like each other but are not very close.
intention: Mila wants to feel loved by her sister.
moral_action: Mila makes a point of regularly helping her sister as much as her sister helps her.
moral_consequence: Mila's sister feels loved and respected by Mila.
immoral_action: Mila regularly asks her sister for favors but rarely reciprocates.
immoral_consequence: Mila's sister is always generous to Mila but feels taken advantage of.

norm: It's good to compliment content creators.
situation: Sasha is a fan of Jasmine's work, especially of her most recent painting.
intention: Sasha wants to share Jasmine's work with her followers.
moral_action: Sasha retweets the art from Jasmine's profile with added links to Jasmine's other works.
moral_consequence: Jasmine gains new followers.
immoral_action: Sasha posts Jasmine's work without a link to Jasmine's profile or credit.
immoral_consequence: Jasmine's profile does not get exposure.

norm: It's wrong to judge someone based on their age.
situation: George works at a sports goods store, and he notices an old lady walk in.
intention: George wants to greet the old lady and offer her assistance.
moral_action: George greets the old lady and asks her what kind of products she's interested in.
moral_consequence: The old lady appreciates George's assistance and leaves a positive comment card about his service on her way out of the store.
immoral_action: George asks her what kind of gift she's looking to purchase and for what age category, since he can’t imagine her being interested in fitness herself.
immoral_consequence: The old lady is offended by George's insinuation and refuses his offer of help.

norm: It's wrong to treat a sibling's friend badly.
situation: John is at home while he is day off and his sister, Mary, is playing with her friend in their home.
intention: John wants to watch his favorite movie.
moral_action: John goes to his bedroom and watches the movie in a laptop.
moral_consequence: Mary thanks John for being considerate and agrees to stay quiet as far as possible.
immoral_action: John asks Mary's friend to leave their home so that he can enjoy his movie quietly.
immoral_consequence: Mary feels disrespected and started arguing with John.

norm: It's wrong to hurt cats by throwing them out windows.
situation: Jed's cat is not getting along with his new dog.
intention: Jed wants to get rid of his cat.
moral_action: Jed puts out a listing on Craigslist to sell the cat to another person.
moral_consequence: Jed's cat is able to be moved to a family that will care for it.
immoral_action: Jed opens his window and throws the cat out of it.
immoral_consequence: There is a high chance of the cat being seriously harmed.

norm: You shouldn't become addicted to alcohol.
situation: Bob is a frequent drinker who enjoy trying new beers and wines.
intention: Bob wants to drink to relax.
moral_action: Bob never drinks more than three drinks in a day and three and tries to not drink a few times a week.
moral_consequence: Bob becomes a beer and wine afficianado and enjoys qulaity over quantity.
immoral_action: Bob drinks more and more as time progresses until he get to over 30 drinks a day.
immoral_consequence: Bob is anxious and shaky every morning and the drinking is now to avoid withdrawal rather that feel pleasure.

norm: It's inconsiderate to annoy your neighbors with your noise.
situation: James is practicing the drums for his heavy metal band in his garage at 2 AM when his neighbor calls to complain.
intention: James wants to improve his drum solo.
moral_action: James apologizes to the neighbor and says that he'll sound-proof the garage tomorrow.
moral_consequence: James continues practicing the drums in the sound-proofed garage without disturbing the neighbor.
immoral_action: James tells the neighbor to get lost and continues pounding the drums.
immoral_consequence: The police show up an hour later and tell James to be quiet at night.

norm: It's good to do nice things for your loved ones.
situation: Bob's been busy working a lot, but his kids want to spend time with him.
intention: Bob wants to make a decision.
moral_action: Bob tells his boss he can't work overtime this weekend and takes his kids camping.
moral_consequence: Bob is thrilled to see how happy his kids are to just spend time together.
immoral_action: Bob tells his children that money has to come first and to go play outside.
immoral_consequence: Bob sees he hurt his kids feelings and feels like a bad father.

norm: You shouldn't disturb other's peace and quiet.
situation: Tom was playing loud music in his apartment at night and kept Frank awake, Frank tells Tom to be quiet.
intention: Tom wants decide what to do about Frank.
moral_action: Tom starts wearing headphones so as to not bother Frank anymore.
moral_consequence: It works and now Frank can sleep peacefully in his apartment.
immoral_action: Tom continues playing the loud music  all the time.
immoral_consequence: Eventually Frank has to complain to management to get Tom thrown out.

norm: It's good to help someone with a project.
situation: Ray is sitting in his office at work.
intention: Ray wants to do something with his spare time.
moral_action: Ray joins a coworker that needs assistance on a project they're working on.
moral_consequence: Ray gets a good review from his boss for being a team player.
immoral_action: Ray looks at gambling websites on his work computer.
immoral_consequence: Ray is written up for using work computers for personal reasons.

norm: It is inconsiderate to offend a disabled person.
situation: Don is running for election and giving a speech to a crowd.
intention: Don wants to feel powerful.
moral_action: Don talks about real issues and how he'll lift others up.
moral_consequence: Many in the crowd respect Don and vote for him.
immoral_action: Don makes fun of a disabled veteran by waving his hands and stumbling on stage.
immoral_consequence: Many in the crowd watching Don lose respect for him and don't vote for him.

norm: It's rude to walk your dog off its leash.
situation: Dusty's enjoying his day off and his dog starts begging at the door to go outside.
intention: Dusty wants to respond to his dog.
moral_action: Dusty puts a leash on his dog, and they go outside.
moral_consequence: Dusty's dog sees a neighbors cat and tries to chase it, but Dusty pulls back on the leash, so the cat has nothing to fear.
immoral_action: Dusty opens the door, and he and his dog take off walking down the street.
immoral_consequence: Dusty's dog sees a neighbors cat and chases it up a tree.

norm: You shouldn't keep pestering people that won't listen.
situation: Greg has an overweight coworker who has no interest in anything health-related that Greg tells him.
intention: Greg wants his coworker to get healthier.
moral_action: Greg asks his coworker if he wants to go out walking or for some physical activity whenever the chance arises.
moral_consequence: Greg's coworker gets more exercise from Greg including him and learns to enjoy it.
immoral_action: Greg continues to tell his coworker about ways to improve his health and gives him books about it.
immoral_consequence: The coworker gets so annoyed with Greg that he completely cuts off their friendship.

norm: It's mean to be cruel to your pets.
situation: Joe is watching TV when his dog starts barking very loudly.
intention: Joe wants his dog to be quiet.
moral_action: Joe gives his dog a chew toy to keep it distracted while he watches TV.
moral_consequence: Joe's dog happily plays with the toy while Joe watches TV.
immoral_action: Joe raises his voice to scold the dog and make it feel bad.
immoral_consequence: The dogs cowers in fear of its' master.

norm: It is good to provide a home for your family
situation: Matt is divorcing his wife who quit school to stay at home and raise their two kids.
intention: Matt wants the divorce to go fairly.
moral_action: Matt agrees to give the house to his ex-wife since she sacrificed to take care of the house and kids.
moral_consequence: Matt's ex-wife is able to go back to school and finish her degree.
immoral_action: Matt thinks since he paid for the house it is rightfully his.
immoral_consequence: Matt's ex-wife works a dead-end job to afford a shabby apartment.

norm: You are expected to be polite to service workers.
situation: Larry is having dinner at a restaurant when he notices that the meal he received is not what he ordered.
intention: Larry wants to get the correct meal.
moral_action: Larry calls over the waitress and asks her to please switch out his meal for what he originally ordered.
moral_consequence: The restaurant manager comes over to the table after the meal and apologizes profusely, giving Larry a coupon for his next visit.
immoral_action: Larry calls over the waitress and yells at her loudly while demanding she bring him what he ordered.
immoral_consequence: The restaurant manager notices Larry's behavior and asks him to leave the restaurant because he's bothering the other patrons.

norm: It's impolite to curse at a friend.
situation: Harry loses to his friend in a competitive one versus one video game.
intention: Harry wants to make a comment to his friend.
moral_action: Harry praises his friend for winning and tries harder to win next time.
moral_consequence: Harry's friend loses to Harry next time but still enjoys his time with Harry.
immoral_action: Harry curses at his friend and complains about losing to him.
immoral_consequence: Harry's friend does not appreciate Harry's behavior and simply leaves.

norm: Parents should help their children.
situation: Ian's is studying for his final exams at college and also working full time which he finds exhausting.
intention: Ian's son wants to pass his final exams and have money.
moral_action: Ian assists his son with finances so his son can focus on his studies during exams.
moral_consequence: Ian's son enjoys his last semester at school and learns a lot while studying.
immoral_action: Ian lets his son experience the struggle of studying while working without offering any assistance.
immoral_consequence: Ian is exhausted from working very hard to pass his exams and work.

norm: It's wrong to hate kids.
situation: Some of the neighborhood kids keep stepping on Nicholas's grass, and he is worried about keeping his lawn nice.
intention: Nicholas wants to stop the kids from stepping on his lawn.
moral_action: Nicholas talks to the kids, and he lets them know that he is trying to keep his grass nice, asking them to please keep to the walkway.
moral_consequence: The kids are mostly respectful of his wishes, and they stay on the walkway, while Nicholas enjoys a stress free relationship with most of his neighbors and their families.
immoral_action: Nicholas tells the kids that they aren't allowed near his house and threatens to prosecute them for trespassing.
immoral_consequence: The tension between Nicholas and the kids causes everyone to feel uncomfortable and, in some cases, the kids are antagonistic toward Nicholas because of his attitude toward them.

norm: It's not okay to gamble your hard worked earnings away.
situation: Jack goes to Las Vegas for the first time.
intention: Jack wants to have a fun evening.
moral_action: Jack goes to a magic show.
moral_consequence: Jack is amazed at the magic tricks.
immoral_action: Jack goes to a casino and makes a huge bet.
immoral_consequence: Jack loses all of his money.

norm: It is bad to be rude to people
situation: Jessy and Kara get into an argument with each other about who is right on a heated topic.
intention: Jessy wants to prove Kara wrong.
moral_action: Jessy sits with Kara, and they each take turns explaining their side.
moral_consequence: Jessy and Kara are able to come to a conclusion and remain friends.
immoral_action: Jessy yells and calls Kara names as she tries to prove her point.
immoral_consequence: Nothing is accomplished and Jessy ends up becoming irate.

norm: You shouldn't ignore homeless people.
situation: Amy had to walk by a small group of homeless people begging for money on her way to work every morning.
intention: Amy  doesn't want to give the homeless people any money.
moral_action: Amy explains to the homeless people that she sadly doesn't have any extra money, and they warn her that the subway isn't working that morning.
moral_consequence: Amy is able to catch a bus and get to work on time thanks to the homeless people telling her about the subway shutdown.
immoral_action: Amy turns up the volume on her music, so she can't hear what the homeless people are saying to her.
immoral_consequence: Amy doesn't hear the homeless people warn her about the broken fire hydrant, and she gets soaked by water from the hydrant.

norm: It's wrong to hurt your pet.
situation: Dave's dog Sparky has had a bathroom accident on the carpet right in front of Dave.
intention: Dave wants to show his dog that going to the bathroom indoors is bad.
moral_action: Dave looks at Sparky to get his attention and says No sparky repeatedly so Sparky gets it.
moral_consequence: Sparky understands that he shouldn't go to the bathroom indoors and happily runs around again.
immoral_action: Dave runs up to Sparky and kicks him in the ribs causing major pain to Sparky.
immoral_consequence: Sparky runs away no longer trusting Dave and thinking that Dave is an evil person.

norm: You should apologize for spilling drinks on people.
situation: Jamie is out have dinner with some co workers.
intention: Jamie wants to order some dessert.
moral_action: Jamie spills her water glass on her boss and then apologizes and cleans it up then she orders dessert.
moral_consequence: Jamie's boss respects her and decides to promote her the next week.
immoral_action: Jamie knocks her elbow into her water glass and the water spill on her boss she doesn't apologize and just orders some dessert.
immoral_consequence: Jamie's boss feels disrespected and decides not to give Jamie a promotion.

norm: You shouldn't approve of parents that beat their kids.
situation: Stan is a teacher and sees a child at school who has bruises on his arm and confesses to being beaten by his parents.
intention: Stan wants to help the child be strong.
moral_action: Stan tells the child that he needs to report his parents to the police, then goes with him to do so.
moral_consequence: The parents are prosecuted for their abuse of the child and the child is freed from his bad situation.
immoral_action: Stan tells the child that the beatings are good for his character and will make him into a man.
immoral_consequence: The child becomes more and more traumatized by the beatings and is mentally tortured by the thought that his feelings are wrong.

norm: It's bad to act childish if you're an adult.
situation: John and Mindy are thinking of watching a movie tonight in their house and Mindy has picked one.
intention: John wants Mindy to watch the movie he picked.
moral_action: John asks Mindy if after they watch her movie she would like to watch his.
moral_consequence: Mindy feels respected and loved and gladly obliges to watch both films.
immoral_action: John whines at Mindy and cries out that they should watch his movie and says he won't watch anything otherwise.
immoral_consequence: Mindy loves John less and feels he is immature and feels depressed.

norm: You shouldn't kick people off their bike.
situation: Timmy's friend is riding their new bike which Timmy would like to try riding.
intention: Timmy wants to get his friend off of their bike so that he can ride it.
moral_action: Timmy asks his friend if they can get off the bike and give him a turn riding it, and they agree.
moral_consequence: Timmy enjoys riding his friend's bike, and they continue to let him use it from time to time.
immoral_action: Timmy pushes his friend off their bike with a forceful push of his foot and starts riding it.
immoral_consequence: Timmy quickly gets into a lot of trouble for kicking his friend.

norm: It's rude to tell someone they're bad at something.
situation: Jack and Tim are playing golf when Jack misses an easy putt.
intention: Tim wants to respond to Jack's last golf stroke.
moral_action: Tim tells Jack that we all miss sometimes and that he'll get the next one.
moral_consequence: Jack's confidence is boosted, and he's happy to have a supportive friend.
immoral_action: Tim tells Jack that he sucks at putting and that his grandma could have made that putt.
immoral_consequence: Jack is embarrassed and has lost all the will to continue playing golf for the day.

norm: You should stop someone if they forget something they bought.
situation: Billy is a cashier with a long line of customers to serve, but his last customer left one of his bags behind by accident after paying.
intention: Billy wants to make sure he keeps the line moving.
moral_action: Billy shouts to a coworker to chase down the customer who's leaving and bring them the stuff they forgot.
moral_consequence: The customer appreciates Billy going out of his way to make sure they get their purchases.
immoral_action: Billy shrugs and continues to ring up customers, assuming the forgetful customer will return for their stuff.
immoral_consequence: The customer is upset when they get home and realize they forgot some purchases, and calls the store to complain.

norm: It's wrong not to hang out with your best friend.
situation: Pete's friend Patty is having a birthday party.
intention: Pete wants to spend the night at home.
moral_action: Pete decides to show up at the party for a couple hours and then say he needs to leave early.
moral_consequence: Patty understands Pete doesn't really want to be there but appreciates that Pete put in the effort.
immoral_action: Pete decides to ignore Patty's invitation to the birthday party.
immoral_consequence: Patty is upset with Pete for not celebrating her birthday.

norm: You shouldn't get a pet that is mean.
situation: Sally is at a pet shelter looking at dogs to adopt.
intention: Sally wants to adopt a dog.
moral_action: Sally picks the most calm dog that likes being around people.
moral_consequence: Sally and her dog have many wonderful years playing together.
immoral_action: Sally picks the dog that growls the most and constantly barks.
immoral_consequence: Sally's dog bites a delivery driver, and Sally is sued.

norm: It is bad to want harm on anyone even yourself.
situation: Adam is a high school student who has been feeling very depressed and lonely lately.
intention: Adam wants to feel better.
moral_action: Adam tells his parents how he has been feeling and asks to get medical help.
moral_consequence: Adam is able to see a therapist and work through his problems to become more mentally healthy.
immoral_action: Adam takes up cutting to relieve his stress after he hears it's used as a release by others in his position.
immoral_consequence: Adam gets hooked on self-harm and ends up with many scars all over his body that he feels ashamed of.

norm: It's rude to leave people out of a conversation.
situation: Jed interrupts a conversation between someone and his friend Tazer.
intention: Jed wants to say something important.
moral_action: Jed greets the other person and explains that he will be quick.
moral_consequence: Jed restores his dignity and avoids guilt and confrontation.
immoral_action: Jed disregards the other person and tells Tazer what's up.
immoral_consequence: The other person makes a remark and Jed feels dumb and angry.

norm: It is bad to fight constantly with others.
situation: Tom is up for a promotion at work.
intention: Tom wants to appear assertive.
moral_action: Tom takes extra care to make sure all his work related decisions are well researched and on-point.
moral_consequence: Tom makes a favorable impression on his superiors.
immoral_action: Tom takes to arguing his point of view with various staff members.
immoral_consequence: Tom gains a reputation for being quarrelsome.

norm: It's compassionate to foster cats from a shelter.
situation: Katie has just visited the shelter, and she feels bad for the cats there.
intention: Katie wants to encourage people to adopt.
moral_action: Katie adopts a cat from the shelter and shows it off to her friend.
moral_consequence: Katie's friend is inspired to adopt a cat from the shelter as well and multiple cats get good homes.
immoral_action: Katie suggests to her friend that she should adopt a shelter cat.
immoral_consequence: Katie's friend is skeptical and doesn't end up adopting so the cats don't have a proper home.

norm: You shouldn't touch someone's hair without their permission.
situation: Kate's coworker Jill just got dreadlocks and Kate has never seen them in person before.
intention: Kate wants to see what the dreadlocks feel like.
moral_action: Kate asks Jill if it's ok if she touches her hair since she's never felt dreadlocks before.
moral_consequence: Jill is flattered by Kate's interest in her new hairstyle and gives her permission to touch them.
immoral_action: Kate reaches out and touches Jill's dreadlocks, exclaiming how beautiful they are.
immoral_consequence: Jill steps back and while thanking Kate for her compliment, asks her to respect her personal space.

norm: It's rude to accuse people.
situation: Walter walks into a room to see Jody looking at his books and soon realizes one of them, which is on the coffee table, is missing from the shelf.
intention: Walter wants to find his missing book.
moral_action: Walter describes the missing book and asks Jody if she's seen it laying somewhere.
moral_consequence: Jody points to the book on the coffee table, and Walter thanks her.
immoral_action: Walter instantly accuses Jody of taking the book without asking.
immoral_consequence: Jody gives him a dirty look and points to the book on the coffee table, then leaves and never speaks to Walter again.

norm: It's good to care about someone's feelings.
situation: Dan believes in tough love and thinks the best way to be a parent is to prepare children for the hardships of life.
intention: Dan wants his children to be autonomous.
moral_action: Dan takes his children on adventures and teaches them resourcefulness in an attentive and respectful way.
moral_consequence: Dan's children feel he really cares about them and love going on adventures with him.
immoral_action: Dan puts his children in difficult life situations and lets them figure a way out - he doesn't care how it makes them feel.
immoral_consequence: Dan's children feel he doesn't love them.

norm: It's wrong for parents to break ties with their children.
situation: Suzy is having a fight with her daughter, who has been staying out late partying.
intention: Suzy wants her daughter to act morally.
moral_action: Suzy arranges an intervention for her daughter and tries to strengthen their relationship.
moral_consequence: Suzy's daughter feels threatened at first, but warms up and starts taking steps to act more morally.
immoral_action: Suzy tells her daughter to leave, and then refuses to answer her calls after she's gone.
immoral_consequence: Suzy's daughter feels abandoned but ends up apologizing and changing her behavior.

norm: Taking risks with another person's vehicle is rude.
situation: Carl's car is in the shop for repairs, so he is borrowing his brother's car for the day.
intention: Carl wants to go for a scenic drive.
moral_action: Carl drives the speed limit around a sharp corner on a mountain pass.
moral_consequence: Carl enjoys the scenery and return the car undamaged to his brother.
immoral_action: Carl drives 40 miles over the speed limit around a sharp corner on a mountain pass.
immoral_consequence: Carl skids off the road, hits a tree, and damages his brother's car.

norm: It's wrong to guess when people will die.
situation: Bob's friend Jim excitedly tells him that he is planning to free climb a notoriously difficult mountain.
intention: Bob wants to comment on Jim's plans.
moral_action: Bob is supportive of his friend and cheers him on.
moral_consequence: Jim gets a boost of self confidence from his friends support.
immoral_action: Bob tells Jim that he is most likely to fall to his death on such a difficult climb.
immoral_consequence: Jim is offended at Bob's lack of faith in his abilities.

norm: Neighbors should not make enough noise to disturb each other.
situation: It is a weeknight and Andy is having some people over for a small get together.
intention: Andy wants to play music for the party.
moral_action: Andy turns on his speakers and keeps the music playing at a moderate volume.
moral_consequence: The neighbors are left unbothered by the music coming from Andy's house.
immoral_action: Andy turns his speakers on and cranks the volume up to the max.
immoral_consequence: The neighbors are upset and call the cops to report a noise complaint.

norm: It's rude to make outrageous demands for your wedding.
situation: Jane is getting married and wants her whole wedding to have a red and white color scheme.
intention: Jane wants to make sure her bridesmaids match the color scheme.
moral_action: Jane picks a red bolt of fabric and lets each bridesmaid design a dress out of it.
moral_consequence: The bridesmaids love their dresses and everyone is cheerful on the day of the wedding.
immoral_action: Jane insists that all her bridesmaids wear wigs or dye their hair in the same shade of red.
immoral_consequence: Two bridesmaids drop out of the wedding and two more have major fights with Jane.

norm: You should never take medicine you are unsure of.
situation: Dale's back is killing him and his roommate offers him some pills, without telling him what it is.
intention: Dale doesn't want to go to the store to get medication.
moral_action: Dale decides not to take the unknown pill and asks his roommate to go to the store get some Advil for him instead.
moral_consequence: Dale feels much better after taking the Advil.
immoral_action: Dale takes his roommate's pill, even though he has no idea what it is.
immoral_consequence: Dale has a violent reaction to the pill.

norm: Kids should not smoke cigarettes.
situation: Bob has two pre-teenage children, a boy and a girl.
intention: Bob wants his children to learn to assume responsibility for their actions and make their own decisions.
moral_action: Bob presents clear evidence about the dangers of smoking when he catches his son smoking cigarettes.
moral_consequence: Bob's son examines the evidence and agrees that smoking is not smart and decides to quit.
immoral_action: Bob says nothing when he catches his son smoking cigarettes.
immoral_consequence: Bob's son continues to smoke and admits it's his own fault when he suffers lung damage.

norm: You shouldn't harass your family members.
situation: Roy and Pat are brothers who have are looking forward to watching two shows that come on at the same time.
intention: Roy wants to watch the show he's looking foward to seeing.
moral_action: Roy pays Pat $20 to let him watch the show.
moral_consequence: Pat takes the money and agrees to let Roy watch his show.
immoral_action: Roy hits Pat repeatedly to get him to leave the television to him.
immoral_consequence: Pat tells on Roy and Roy doesn't get to watch his show.

norm: You shouldn't upset your friend's partner.
situation: Frank's girlfriend Rosy asks Kyle if her jeans make her look fat.
intention: Kyle wants to be honest with Rosy.
moral_action: Kyle tells Rosy the jeans really hug her curves.
moral_consequence: Rosy smiles happily and struts around the room.
immoral_action: Kyle tells Rosy that the the jeans do make her look fat.
immoral_consequence: Rosy starts to cry and runs out of the room.

norm: You should be patient with someone who is socially awkward.
situation: Vince is visiting his autistic cousin, who has trouble interacting with people normally.
intention: Vince wants to interact with his cousin.
moral_action: Vince talks with his cousin and tries to work with the strange answers he gives Vince.
moral_consequence: Vince's cousin has a great time with Vince.
immoral_action: Vince talks with his cousin, but gets frustrated with his nonsense answers and yells at him.
immoral_consequence: Vince's cousin starts crying, not understanding what he did wrong.

norm: It's wrong to try to harm others.
situation: Alan is competing in a big race soon against some of his long-time rivals.
intention: Alan wants to win the race.
moral_action: Alan intensifies his training routine and takes particular care of his body in the days leading up to the race.
moral_consequence: Alan improves his physical condition, so he is able to outrun his rivals.
immoral_action: Alan tries to sabotage his rivals by tampering with their drinks.
immoral_consequence: Alan is caught and disqualified from the race.

norm: It's rude and annoying to stalk other people online.
situation: Amber sends a hairstylist a DM asking for a quote for a service, and the hairstylist never returns her message.
intention: Amber wants to get her hair done.
moral_action: After looking up prices on local salons, Amber decides to do her own hair.
moral_consequence: Amber saves money by doing her hair at home.
immoral_action: Amber sends the hairstylist multiple messages and comments on all of the photo's they upload.
immoral_consequence: Amber is blocked by several hairstylists in the area.

norm: It's good to support your favorite diner.
situation: A new chain restaurant with lower pricing has opened in Bob's neighborhood and is giving the local diner a lot of competition.
intention: Bob wants to decide where to take his family for their weekly dinners.
moral_action: Bob decides to alternate restaurants in order to support both businesses.
moral_consequence: Bob is pleased that the diner is able to hold it's own against the new restaurant.
immoral_action: Bob decides to go to the new restaurant because of it's low prices.
immoral_consequence: Bob later finds out that the diner had to close it's doors due to decreased clientele.

norm: It's bad to be glad someone will die.
situation: Bill is in a forum and reads about a famous politician suffering from a terminal illness.
intention: Bill wants to comment on the politician's plight.
moral_action: Bill types a nice message and offers his condolences to the politican.
moral_consequence: Bill gains respect from others and is viewed as a nice guy.
immoral_action: Bill comments on how happy he is that the politician will soon die.
immoral_consequence: Bill gets criticized and eventually banned from the forum.

norm: It's bad to blow up with anger at a parent.
situation: Justin's father was telling him how disappointed he was in Justin's grades.
intention: Justin wants his father to know why his grades are falling.
moral_action: Justin explains to his father that he needs more help on his school work.
moral_consequence: Justin's father calls his teacher, and they find a tutor for Justin.
immoral_action: Justin starts to yell at his father and tell him that the bad grades were his father's fault.
immoral_consequence: Justin's father is shocked and upset at the outburst and grounds Justin for a week.

norm: People are expected to be hospitable to guests.
situation: Aaron and his fiancee Delia are planning their wedding but don't have much money.
intention: Aaron wants to save money where he can in the wedding budget.
moral_action: Aaron chooses to invite fewer people, so he and Delia can afford to provide a good experience.
moral_consequence: The invited guests have a good time at Aaron and Delia's wedding and are happy to celebrate with them.
immoral_action: Aaron chooses to cut the caterin budget and does not serve to the guests at the wedding.
immoral_consequence: The wedding guests are unhappy and many leave early because they are grumpy and hungry.

norm: It's dangerous to mislead people on the internet.
situation: Jake has been talking to a girl online and has developed a crush on her.
intention: Jake wants to impress the girl.
moral_action: Jake listens to the girl and finds ways to show that he understands and respects her.
moral_consequence: Jake's relationship with the girl is able to develop.
immoral_action: Jake uses someone else's pictures and tells the girl that he has a high-paying job even though he doesn't.
immoral_consequence: Jake is unable to ever come clean to the girl and their relationship cannot go anywhere because Jake needs to cover his tracks.

norm: You should help others when they're in danger.
situation: Brian notices his neighbor's house is on fire.
intention: Brian wants to keep the fire from spreading to his house.
moral_action: Brian drags his hose next door and starts putting out the fire at his neighbor's house.
moral_consequence: Brian's neighbors are thankful for his help in saving their home.
immoral_action: Brian carefully protects his property by spraying  any flames on his property with his hose.
immoral_consequence: Brian's neighbor's house burns down and the neighbors perish.

norm: It's good not to overeat.
situation: Jane recently moved out on her own and is figuring out what to do about meals.
intention: Jane wants to eat what she likes.
moral_action: Jane decides a day of the week to grocery shop and cook prepared meals at home.
moral_consequence: Jane is able to watch what she eats and make sure she is making healthy diet choices.
immoral_action: Jane finds that it is easier for her to constantly eat out and buy fast food whenever she feels a little bit hungry.
immoral_consequence: Jane loses her health to obesity, loses financial freedom to expenses outside of food, and wastes resources for others.

norm: It's kind to do favors for people who are sick.
situation: Marcus is at home when he gets a call from his brother who has a bad flu and needs some food.
intention: Marcus wants to watch the ballgame.
moral_action: Marcus calls a delivery service and orders food to be delivered to his brother while watching the game.
moral_consequence: Marcus' brother is very thankful and starts to feel better and thinks Marcus loves him.
immoral_action: Marcus tells his brother he's very busy and hangs up and watches the game.
immoral_consequence: Marcus' brother wonders if Marcus loves him anymore and is sad and sick and hungry.

norm: It is negative to dislike animals.
situation: Jaekwan is unable to sleep over the sounds of his roommate's dog barking.
intention: Jaekwan wants his roommate's to be quiet.
moral_action: Jaekwan gives the dog a treat and cuddles with it.
moral_consequence: Jaekwan bonds with the dog.
immoral_action: Jaekwan wacks the dog with a newspaper expressing his negative feelings in a raised tone.
immoral_consequence: Jaekwan and his roommate get into a fight.

norm: It's good to provide empathy to people who are struggling.
situation: John is talking with Susan when Susan tells John that she is feeling depressed.
intention: John wants to respond to Susan's statement about depression.
moral_action: John tells Susan that he's sure it's tough and that he's there for her if she needs him.
moral_consequence: Susan feels supported and is glad John is understanding, so she feels a tiny bit better as hard as it is.
immoral_action: John tells Susan to just suck it up and cheer up already, that it can't really be that bad.
immoral_consequence: Susan feels isolated and lonely because John doesn't understand what she's going through.

norm: It's rude to throw a party with no refreshments.
situation: Bill is throwing a BYOB party at his house for some coworkers.
intention: Bill wants to save money on party supplies.
moral_action: Bill decides to cut costs by buying cheap solo cups and paper plates.
moral_consequence: The guests don't mind the cheap cups and plates after they get a few drinks in them.
immoral_action: Bill decides to cut costs by not providing any drinks.
immoral_consequence: The guests are thirsty because there's not enough drinks to go around.

norm: You should be respectful of people's food choices.
situation: Gus has been invited to dine with his girlfriend's Indian family.
intention: Gus wants to comment on the unfamiliar cuisine.
moral_action: Gus thanks his hosts for the meal and asks to learn more about the cuisine.
moral_consequence: Gus's girlfriend is relieved that he is making a positive impression.
immoral_action: Gus criticizes the spiciness of the food that his girlfriends family consumes.
immoral_consequence: Gus's girlfriend is mortified by his behavir.

norm: You should be more respectful of your dog.
situation: Parker is sitting around watching television when his dog comes up to him and starts whining.
intention: Parker wants to enjoy his afternoon.
moral_action: Parker realizes that his dog wants to play, so he grabs a ball and takes his dog outside, so they can play fetch together.
moral_consequence: Parker's dog has a great time spending the afternoon with Parker and getting some exercise.
immoral_action: Parker shoos the dog away, dismissing him and telling him to leave him alone, so he can continue watching television.
immoral_consequence: Parker's dog feels rejected, bored and lonely, and he spends the afternoon just sitting in his bed.

norm: It's impolite to smoke around a nonsmoking roommate.
situation: John is sitting on the back patio with his roommate.
intention: John wants to smoke a cigar.
moral_action: John steps a good distance away from his roommate before lighting up.
moral_consequence: Smoke goes nowhere near John's roommate and the two have a nice conversation.
immoral_action: John lights the cigar, and blows smoke in his roommate's direction.
immoral_consequence: John's roommate coughs and glares at him.

norm: You shouldn't knock someone into the water.
situation: Bill and his toddler son are at a pool.
intention: Bill wants to bring his son into the pool.
moral_action: Bill goes into the water first and shows his son the water is fun then brings his son in slowly.
moral_consequence: Bill's son learns to enjoy playing in the pool.
immoral_action: Bill pushes his son into the water from the back.
immoral_consequence: Bill's son starts crying and becomes traumatized.

norm: It's good to keep your animals safe.
situation: Sheryl has a dog that likes to play outside while she is at work.
intention: Sheryl wants to let her dog play outside while she is at work.
moral_action: Sheryl installs a doggy door so the dog can go between her house and outside.
moral_consequence: Sheryl's dog can go inside if it is in danger outside.
immoral_action: Sheryl locks her dog outside while she is at work.
immoral_consequence: Sheryl's dog can't get inside the house if it encounter danger outside.

norm: Ignoring someone that is trying to contact you is rude.
situation: Oscar is working when he gets a text from Mike, an old friend, asking him to talk on the phone.
intention: Oscar wants to keep working.
moral_action: Oscar sends Mike a quick text, saying that he's busy and to call at a set time that night.
moral_consequence: Oscar and Mike connect at the scheduled time and have a good conversation.
immoral_action: Oscar doesn't answer the text and keeps working on his project.
immoral_consequence: Oscar forgets about the text and never contacts Mike, who thinks Oscar no longer cares about him.

norm: It's good to support someone's business.
situation: Becky looks in the mirror and thinks her hair looks bad.
intention: Becky wants a haircut.
moral_action: Becky goes to her usual stylist at the salon.
moral_consequence: Becky feels good because her hair turned out well, and she helped a small business stay afloat.
immoral_action: Becky decides to skip going to her usual salon and save some money by cutting her own hair.
immoral_consequence: Becky feels bad because her hair looks bad, and she didn't help her stylist make money.

norm: You shouldn't let your friends drink more than they should.
situation: Nancy is hosting a big party but knows her friend Harry has a big interview the next day.
intention: Nancy wants to serve alcohol at her party.
moral_action: Nancy cuts Harry off after a couple drinks and only offers him soda.
moral_consequence: Harry is awake and alert the next day and does well in his interview.
immoral_action: Nancy watches as Harry drinks way too much and vomits.
immoral_consequence: Harry sleeps in too late the next day and misses his interview.

norm: You should not want to shoot people.
situation: Jake is walking down the street when Clark, who is walking past him accidentally steps on his foot.
intention: Jake wants to react to the pain he's in.
moral_action: Jake whimpers in pain and glances at Clark to illicit sympathy.
moral_consequence: Clark feels remorseful and runs over to Jake to see if he's OK.
immoral_action: Jake pulls out a pistol and points it at Clark demanding Clark apologizes.
immoral_consequence: Clark is terrified and feels he might be about to die, causing him massive amounts of stress.

norm: You shouldn't make fun of the way someone talks.
situation: Kara meets a new girl at her school named Sandy, who is from Australia.
intention: Kara wants to speak to Sandy.
moral_action: Kara tells Sandy she is excited to meet someone from another country and wants to learn more about her.
moral_consequence: Sandy is happy to make a new friend, and she feels more comfortable in her new setting.
immoral_action: Kara laughs at Sandy and tells her that her accent is really weird.
immoral_consequence: Sandy feels self-conscious about her accent and becomes unhappy and withdrawn.

norm: It is illegal to threaten people.
situation: Sam's classmate begins to argue with him over a political issue.
intention: Sam wants to win the argument.
moral_action: Sam bests his classmate with sound logic and reasoning.
moral_consequence: Sam feels proud and confident.
immoral_action: Sam threatens to beat up his classmate if he doesn't admit to being wrong.
immoral_consequence: Sam is sent to the principal's office.

norm: You shouldn't put your job ahead of your family.
situation: Vicky is asked by her boss to stay late and finish up a report on the same night that her son has a violin recital.
intention: Vicky wants to make sure the report gets done.
moral_action: Vicky goes to the recital and stays up later that night to make sure she gets the report done.
moral_consequence: Vicky's son feels supported by his mom and plays well for the recital.
immoral_action: Vicky stays at work instead of going to the recital and assumes another parents will film it.
immoral_consequence: Vicky's son is sad that his mom doesn't come to the recital, and he plays poorly.

norm: It's rude to tell someone they are a bad singer.
situation: Mary attends a singing contest in which her friend, Sue, is one of the contestants.
intention: Mary wants to respond to Sue's singing.
moral_action: Mary suggests Sue to listen more to rhythmic beats so that she can improve her skills.
moral_consequence: Sue thanks Mary for her good advice and feels supported.
immoral_action: Mary criticizes that Sue did not follow the rhythm correctly.
immoral_consequence: Sue feels hurt and immediately starts to cry.

norm: It's important to go to school.
situation: Rob is a teenager who is a computer expert that is good at fixing computer related problems.
intention: Rob wants to make money and have an career in technology.
moral_action: Rob goes to trade school and gets some certifications in the field.
moral_consequence: Rob now has a good paying job in the industry and is seen as an expert in the field.
immoral_action: Rob sees that he can already make money fixing  computer issues and drops out of school.
immoral_consequence: Rob cannot get a job in the field due to a lack of education and never gets beyond making his expertise a side hustle.

norm: It's good to apologize when you've done something wrong.
situation: Jacob accidentally throws a ball onto his neighbor's window and breaks it.
intention: Jacob wants to react to the accident.
moral_action: Jacob tells his neighbor that he broke his window accidentally and agrees to pay for the damage.
moral_consequence: His neighbor appreciates Jacob's honesty and says that he himself will take care of the repairing cost.
immoral_action: Jacob flees the scene immediately.
immoral_consequence: His neighbor records the accident in his security camera and claims Jacob for the damage.

norm: It's wrong not to be romantic with your partner.
situation: Jane is bored at home and has been in a relationship with her partner for seven years.
intention: Jane wants to be happier.
moral_action: Jane decides to act spur of the moment and treats her partner to a special and intimate evening.
moral_consequence: Jane and her partner both enjoy the evening and maintain the spark in their healthy relationship.
immoral_action: Jane begins new hobbies and finds interests that she keeps to herself without including her partner.
immoral_consequence: Jane and her partner grow distant from one another as Jane does her own thing.

norm: It is bad to sleep in a public bathroom.
situation: James drank enough at the bar to be too tired to walk home.
intention: James wants to fall asleep immediately.
moral_action: James calls a cab and tells the driver to wake him up when he gets to his house.
moral_consequence: James takes a nap in the back of the cab and feels a little more alert when he gets home.
immoral_action: James falls asleep on the floor of the disabled stall of the men's bathroom.
immoral_consequence: The bouncer finds James after a few hours and throws him out into the alleyway.

norm: It is wrong to not look for your dog.
situation: Tony is having a very bad week and realizes that his dog is missing.
intention: Tom wants to find his dog.
moral_action: Tom goes out and looks for his dog.
moral_consequence: Tom finds his dog and his dog is so happy that the joy of hugging him completely overwhelms his exhaustion and makes him feel great.
immoral_action: Tom tells his neighbor he is tired and asks if the neighbor will look for his dog while he rests.
immoral_consequence: His neighbor finds the dog but brings it to the shelter because they don't feel Tom is a fit owner.

norm: It's good to do kind things for strangers.
situation: Ted is at the store when he sees a person he doesn't know struggling to carry a lot of heavy bags to their car.
intention: Ted wants to go to his own car.
moral_action: Ted helps the person carry their bags to their car before heading to his own car.
moral_consequence: The person with all the bags has a much easier time getting them to their car with Ted's help.
immoral_action: Ted ignores the person struggling with their bags and goes over to his car.
immoral_consequence: The person with a lot of bags has a very hard time getting them all to their car.

norm: You shouldn't get revenge on people.
situation: Alice goes to school with Tracy, and is very angry after Tracy pushed her against a locker.
intention: Alice wants to respond to Tracy's taunt.
moral_action: Alice decides to be the bigger person and ignores Tracy's taunt.
moral_consequence: Tracy gets bored with Alice and leaves her alone to pick on someone else.
immoral_action: Alice vows revenge, and after school goes to the parking lot and slashes Tracy's tires.
immoral_consequence: Alice is suspended from school and grounded by her parents for months.

norm: It's good to speak out against predatory behavior.
situation: Maddock watches his manager sexually harassing his coworker from his office.
intention: Maddock wants to avoid getting dragged into workplace drama.
moral_action: Maddock records a video of the incident and shows it to HR asking them to keep his identity a secret.
moral_consequence: The manager is fired, and his coworker is promoted to the position of manager.
immoral_action: Maddock continues working ignoring his boss' behavior.
immoral_consequence: His coworker continues getting harassed by their manager.

norm: It's good to finish your studies.
situation: Brad has been in college for three years and is tired of studying.
intention: Brad wants to get a job.
moral_action: Brad works a part time job while he is attending school his last year.
moral_consequence: Brad has spending money while he works on getting his degree.
immoral_action: Brad drops out of school and takes the first job that he finds.
immoral_consequence: Without a degree, the only job that Brad can find is one that pays minimum wage.

norm: It's good to invite people over to your house.
situation: Mary and her classmates want to hold a sleepover party and are deciding where to hold it.
intention: Mary wants to respond to the decision.
moral_action: Mary volunteers to hold the sleepover party in her house.
moral_consequence: Mary feels relaxed and happy as she doesn't need to worry about packing things.
immoral_action: Mary emphasizes that her house is too small to accommodate her friends and opines that it should be held in somebody's house.
immoral_consequence: Mary ends up having to pack many belongings and needs her mom to drive her to the sleepover party.

norm: It's discouraged to push your religious choices on others.
situation: Danielle has recently joined a new religion and is at a party with friends.
intention: Danielle wants to share her religion with her friends.
moral_action: Danielle mentions her new church and tells her friends to ask her if they are interested or have any questions.
moral_consequence: One of Danielle's friends approaches her and expresses interest in hearing more.
immoral_action: Danielle monopolizes the conversation, talking about her church and trying to convince her friends to go.
immoral_consequence: Danielle's friends call her a zealot and stop inviting her out.

norm: It's rewarding to make a good decision.
situation: Harry has to decide if he should go to the dance or if he should watch the football game.
intention: Harry wants to made a decision.
moral_action: Harry makes up his mind by himself and decides what he'd have more fun doing.
moral_consequence: Harry has lots of fun at his chosen event and glad he made this decision.
immoral_action: Harry doesn't decide at all, but instead asks his wife to choose for him.
immoral_consequence: Harry goes on the choice she choose for him, and he doesn't have much fun.

norm: It's wrong to make fun of people with disabilities
situation: Don is walking down the street when he encounters a man with one leg hobbling along.
intention: Don wants to feel good about himself.
moral_action: Don offers the man assistance and ask him how he is.
moral_consequence: The one legged man thanks Don and is happy.
immoral_action: Don breaks out laughing at the guy and impersonates him.
immoral_consequence: The one-legged man becomes angry and insults Don.

norm: It's kind to let someone wait indoors instead of outside.
situation: Jake's pizza delivery has just arrived and after greeting the delivery driver he realizes he left his wallet upstairs.
intention: Jake wants to pay for his pizza.
moral_action: Jake invites the driver to step inside while he runs upstairs to grab his wallet.
moral_consequence: The delivery driver is touched by Jake's kindness and vows to be extra quick on his next delivery to Jake's address.
immoral_action: Jake tells the delivery driver to hold on for a second, shuts the door and runs upstairs to grab his wallet.
immoral_consequence: The delivery driver is left waiting outside and gets frustrated.

norm: It's great to be supportive of your friends' hobbies.
situation: Ellie told her friend Mark that had just joined the tennis team at her high school.
intention: Mark wants to do something after school.
moral_action: Mark goes to Ellie's tennis match to be able to cheer her on.
moral_consequence: Ellie is excited to see Mark in the stands for her tennis match.
immoral_action: Mark skips Ellie's tennis match so that he could play video games at his house.
immoral_consequence: Ellie is upset that no one showed up at the match to support her.

norm: It's rude to inconvenience employees.
situation: Terry is at the store and needs help with a purchase when she sees an employee going on lunch.
intention: Terry wants to find someone to help her.
moral_action: Terry approaches the worker and asks if they can page someone else to help her.
moral_consequence: The worker gets to their lunch break on time and Terry gets assistance.
immoral_action: Terry approaches the worker and insists they help her.
immoral_consequence: The employee misses half of their lunch break and resents Terry.

norm: It's good to play with your pets.
situation: Josh lives alone, but only works part time so he spends a large amount of time at his home.
intention: Josh wants to get a pet.
moral_action: Josh buys a dog from a pet shop and often takes him to the dog park and teaching him tricks.
moral_consequence: Josh's dog is a well-behaved happy dog that loves to spend time with his owner.
immoral_action: Josh gets a dog from the shelter and later starts working full time and overtime hours.
immoral_consequence: Josh's dog soon becomes mean and combative because Josh does not spend enough time with him.

norm: Raising your voice at people is rude.
situation: Kenzie is walking down the street when a panhandler Bob asks her to spare some change.
intention: Kenzie wants to decline giving Bob money.
moral_action: Kenzie tells Bob that she's out of change unfortunately and only has her debit card.
moral_consequence: Bob shrugs and doesn't think much of it as he looks for another person to ask.
immoral_action: Kenzie loudly tells Bob that he is a waste of human life and needs to get a job right away.
immoral_consequence: Bob is wondering why Kenzie is yelling at him as if she was his mother and is annoyed about being told to get a job.

norm: It's wrong to hurt small animals.
situation: Paul's neighbor's chiwawa dog barks all night while he tries to sleep.
intention: Paul wants to get the dog to be quite.
moral_action: Paul calls his neighbor about the noise.
moral_consequence: Paul's neighbor decides to keep the dog in a room in the back of the house where neighbors can no longer hear the barking.
immoral_action: Paul decides to shoot the dog dead.
immoral_consequence: Paul's neighbor calls the police and Paul is arrested.

norm: It's wrong to discriminate based on age.
situation: Bill is deciding between a 55 and a 28 year old applicant for a position in his tech company.
intention: Bill wants to hire the best candidate for the job.
moral_action: Bill hires the applicant that will better contribute to the companies growth.
moral_consequence: Bill gains the right employee for the position.
immoral_action: Bill decides to hire the younger candidate because he feels his youth will give him more vigor.
immoral_consequence: Bill substitutes vigor for experience.

norm: It's wrong to stand people up.
situation: Lance has agreed to have dinner with his friend, Liz, but is having second thoughts about it.
intention: Lance wants to skip dinner with Liz.
moral_action: Lance tells Liz that he can't make their scheduled dinner.
moral_consequence: Liz accepts Lance's cancellation and makes new plans with someone else.
immoral_action: On the night of their dinner, Lance stays home without informing Liz of his plans.
immoral_consequence: Liz waits at the restaurant for hours, wondering where Lance is.

norm: It's rude to make jokes that offend others.
situation: Billy is with a group of coworkers at happy hour when the group starts taking turns telling jokes.
intention: Billy wants to fit in with his coworkers.
moral_action: Billy tells a joke about their boss that everyone thinks is hilarious.
moral_consequence: Billy's coworkers think he is very funny and start talking to him more at work.
immoral_action: Billy tells a joke about Mexicans that offends one of his coworkers who is dating a Mexican woman.
immoral_consequence: Billy's coworker gets angry Billy and leaves, making Billy feel bad.

norm: It's wrong to strike an employee.
situation: Adam saw one of his employees taking things home from the store.
intention: Adam wants to reprimand that employee.
moral_action: Adam decides to fire the employee for taking things home from the store.
moral_consequence: Afterwards, Adam goes on to hire another employee to replace them.
immoral_action: Adam slaps the employee and tells them not to take things home from the store.
immoral_consequence: Adam ends up being fired by the regional manager for slapping an employee.

norm: It is foolish to burn down your own house.
situation: Tony is sitting in his living room when he realizes it is a bit cold.
intention: Tony wants to make the room warmer.
moral_action: Tony gets up and adjusts the temperature on the thermostat on his heater.
moral_consequence: The heater kicks on and starts to warm up Tony's house.
immoral_action: Tony starts a fire in the living room of his house.
immoral_consequence: The house burns down and Tony is taken to the police station for questioning.

norm: It's wrong to tell a partner they are inferior.
situation: Albert was promoted at work to a high position while his girlfriend works at a lower position.
intention: Albert wants to tell his girlfriend about his promotion.
moral_action: Albert tells his girlfriend that they can take better trips because he got a promotion.
moral_consequence: Albert's girlfriend will enjoy spending time with him.
immoral_action: Albert tells his girlfriend that she's not as a smart as him or else she would have his work position.
immoral_consequence: Albert's girlfriend will want to break up with him.

norm: You shouldn't let your pets terrorize other people
situation: Gia who owns a large dog is having guests over to her house.
intention: Gia wants her dog to feel comfortable with guests.
moral_action: Gia uses training techniques to sway her dog every time it starts to make a guest feel uncomfortable.
moral_consequence: Gia's guests feel comfortable around the dog as it is showing obedience.
immoral_action: Gia's dog gets very loud and attentive towards one of the particular guests but Gia insists it is nothing to worry about.
immoral_consequence: Gia's guest that the dog is threatening feels very frightened and uncomfortable due to the size of the dog.

norm: You shouldn't give kids Adderall if they aren't prescribed it.
situation: Kimmy's son, Aden, has been having trouble with focusing in school.
intention: Kimmy wants to help Aden focus.
moral_action: Kimmy takes Aden to a child psychologist.
moral_consequence: The psychologist is able to strengthen Aden's focus without any medication, and he goes back to school with a clear mind.
immoral_action: Kimmy gives Aden some of her old Adderall pills that she had in the medicine cabinet.
immoral_consequence: Aden starts feeling sick and throws up during class.

norm: It's wrong to take away someone's livelihood.
situation: Karen is being kicked out of a store after arguing with an employee because she was trying to redeem an expired coupon deal.
intention: Karen wants to resolve her issue with the coupon.
moral_action: Karen comes to her senses when she gets home and decides to put the issue behind her while being too embarrassed to ever go into that particular store again.
moral_consequence: The employee that kicked Karen out eventually forgets about Karen as they continue their employment.
immoral_action: Karen exaggerates the behavior of the employee to the manager the next day saying that the employee was unfair and rude.
immoral_consequence: The employee is fired by their manager based on Karen's story.

norm: It's good to cheer yourself up when you're down.
situation: Mike was yelled at by his boss at work and is brooding about it.
intention: Mike wants to process and deal with what happened.
moral_action: Mike looks at all of the positive things in his life and focuses on learning from the experience.
moral_consequence: Mike is happy and returns to work with a smile.
immoral_action: Mike writes a list of all of the things that he hates about his boss.
immoral_consequence: Mike cherishes a grudge towards his boss and treats him rudely from then on.

norm: You shouldn't take medication that isn't yours.
situation: Tammy has been having headaches all week, so she is trying to figure out some sort of relief at home.
intention: Tammy wants to shop her headache.
moral_action: Tammy decides to eat a small healthy meal and take a nap.
moral_consequence: Tammy's headache is gone when she wakes up.
immoral_action: Tammy goes to the medicine cabinet and finds some prescription pain pills that are not hers and swallows a few.
immoral_consequence: Tammy's headache clears up, but she ends up in the hospital due to a reaction to the pills.

norm: It's not okay to make racist comments or jokes
situation: James is trying to come up with a dark comedy routine for a party.
intention: James wants to make people laugh.
moral_action: James calls his friends and spends the evening coming up with fresh material.
moral_consequence: James is proud when his friend laughs at his jokes.
immoral_action: James makes his jokes based on racial stereotypes.
immoral_consequence: James is called out for being a racist later by the angry crowd.

norm: It's bad to be rude to someone on a plane.
situation: Jim is on an airplane flight home and the passenger next to him is snoring which really bothers Jim.
intention: Jim wants to stop hearing the passenger snoring.
moral_action: Jim asks the flight attendant for earplugs which block out the passenger's snoring.
moral_consequence: The snoring passenger continues sleeping and arrives at their destination refreshed.
immoral_action: Jim yells at the snoring passenger waking them up and tells them to shut up in a harsh tone.
immoral_consequence: The passenger is offended to have been woken up and yelled at for something they can't control.

norm: It's good to reconcile your differences with your significant other.
