immoral_consequence: Al, who is stealing because he's hungry is appalled that someone would wish death upon him for a relatively minor crime.

norm: It's wrong to be abusive.
situation: Mark's wife is cooking breakfast when she burns the pancakes.
intention: Mark wants to respond to the burned pancakes.
moral_action: Mark tells his wife that they can eat something else.
moral_consequence: Mark's wife will think he is very forgiving.
immoral_action: Mark walks over to his wife and slaps her in the face.
immoral_consequence: Mark's wife will leave him for hitting her.

norm: It's wrong not to help injured animals.
situation: Bert was driving down the road when he sees a dog with a broken leg on the side of the road.
intention: Bert wants to get home.
moral_action: Bert stops and takes the dog to his house to care for it.
moral_consequence: The dog is up and back on his feet within just a few days.
immoral_action: Bert drives past the injured dog and pays no mind to it on the way to his house.
immoral_consequence: The dog suffers from his injuries and eventually dies a day later.

norm: You shouldn't drive fast in a residential neighborhood.
situation: Alex is trying to get home in time to watch the game.
intention: Alex wants to get home quickly.
moral_action: Alex takes a short cut to arrive home more quickly.
moral_consequence: Alex gets home safely just in time to watch the game.
immoral_action: Alex exceeds the speed limit and doesn't slow down at stop signs on his way home.
immoral_consequence: Alex gets pulled over, is issued a ticket and misses the beginning of the game.

norm: It is unkind to hurt people.
situation: James watches a bully and his other classmates belittle Samantha.
intention: James wants to fit in.
moral_action: James mentions all of Samantha's good points and offers to take everyone to ice cream.
moral_consequence: People stop bullying Samantha, and she gains more friends.
immoral_action: James joins the popular kids in making fun of Samantha.
immoral_consequence: Samantha goes home and hurts herself to get rid of emotional pain.

norm: You shouldn't smoke around others.
situation: Laura is hanging out with her friends after having had a stressful day at work.
intention: Laura wants to relax her nerves.
moral_action: Laura excuses herself to smoke outside.
moral_consequence: Laura smokes her cigarette and then goes back to hanging out with her friends.
immoral_action: Laura lights up a cigarette without asking her friends if it's okay.
immoral_consequence: Laura's friends start to cough and become upset with her.

norm: It is rude to be greedy.
situation: Tiffany just won $1,000 on a scratch-off and her friend Judy needs $50 for her electric bill.
intention: Tiffany wants to give Judy some money.
moral_action: Tiffany gives Judy a fifty dollar bill to help her out.
moral_consequence: Judy is really gateful to Tiffany for the assistance and sees her as a great friend.
immoral_action: Tiffany cashes in her winnings and hands Judy just one dollar.
immoral_consequence: Judy see's this tiny amount as insulting and is now angry at Tiffany.

norm: You shouldn't say mean things to girls about their looks.
situation: James felt disappointed that Jenna beat him at the chess tournament.
intention: James wants to feel less inferior to Jenna.
moral_action: James mentally thinks that at least he's more attractive than Jenna, while outwardly congratulating her.
moral_consequence: James' ego is soothed without offending anyone, and he keeps practicing to get better at chess.
immoral_action: James tells Jenna she's a fat, pimply nerd no one will ever love.
immoral_consequence: James' friends laugh, but the chess club advisor overhears and kicks him off the team.

norm: It's good to get rid of a vicious pet.
situation: Amys dog has behavioral issues, and was never trained.
intention: Amy wants the best for her dog.
moral_action: Amy find a new owner who helps dogs with behavioral issues.
moral_consequence: Amy is sad, but happy to see her dog get the help it needs.
immoral_action: Amy decided to keep her dog after it bit the neighbors child.
immoral_consequence: The mother of the child sues Amy.

norm: It is comforting for loved ones to die peacefully
situation: Kylie's grandmother has been battling dementia and her family has been preparing to say goodbye to her.
intention: Kylie wants to be there for her grandmother when she passes.
moral_action: Kylie brings a stereo to her granmother's bedside and plays her favorite music.
moral_consequence: Kylie's grandmother passes with a smile on her face.
immoral_action: Kylie brings party horns to her grandmother's bedside and blows them hoping they will make her happy.
immoral_consequence: Kylie's grandmother kicks around uncomfortably due to the noise and passes away with a scowl on her face.

norm: It is rude to be a bad house guest.
situation: Ronin house is undergoing renovations and is a guest at his friends place for the weekend.
intention: Ronin wants to have a lot of fun with his friend.
moral_action: Ronin buys a couple beers and drinks with his friend but cleans up after himself.
moral_consequence: Ronin's friend really enjoys his company and invites him to come back over sometime.
immoral_action: Ronin buys a bunch of alcohol, gets drunk with his friend and ends up blacking out and trashing the house.
immoral_consequence: Ronin's friend is very upset at how trashed the house is and kicks him out.

norm: It is bad to hurt animals.
situation: Ryan's dog misbehaves, pooping all over the house out of spite.
intention: Ryan wants to discipline his dog.
moral_action: Ryan takes the dog to obedience school to get taught how to behave better.
moral_consequence: Ryan's dog learns a lot, and starts to respect his master and home.
immoral_action: Ryan smacks his dog very hard on the butt as punishment.
immoral_consequence: Ryan's dog cries out, hurt that his master would strike him.

norm: It's hurtful to point out others personal flaws.
situation: Neva is dictating to Holden because he types fast, but he Is hard of hearing.
intention: Neva wants to let Holden know that he should listen carefully.
moral_action: Neva tells him this and asks for feedback on her speech volume.
moral_consequence: Neva finishes the project and is relieved.
immoral_action: Neva tells him this and points out his hearing problem.
immoral_consequence: Holden gives up and Neva is stuck with work, ashamed.

norm: It's wrong to make fun of someone's makeup.
situation: Margret is a beauty influencer doing a live stream with another influencer Barbra, and Barbra insults an instagram model's makeup.
intention: Margret wants to entertain her audience.
moral_action: Margret gives constructive criticism to the model along with doing a small demonstration on how to fix the makeup.
moral_consequence: Margret's followers uses the tutorials to create unique looks.
immoral_action: Margret joins Barbra comparing the model's makeup to a clown.
immoral_consequence: Margret's actions alienate her followers who dislike harsh humor.

norm: It's bad to destroy property for no reason.
situation: John asks to borrow his brother's luxury car that he received as a graduation gift from their parents because John doesn't have one since he didn't graduate.
intention: John needs transportation, so he can take him and his friends on a trip to Vegas.
moral_action: John drives the car to transport himself and his friends to Vegas and returns it to his brother in perfect condition.
moral_consequence: John's brother is satisfied with the car's care and tells John he can borrow it anytime he needs it.
immoral_action: John uses his brother's car for the trip but intentionally breaks the windshield out of jealousy.
immoral_consequence: John's brother is very upset  because it's expensive to repair, and he never lets John use the car again.

norm: It's especially wrong to hurt children.
situation: Gary's son Larry tells him that he broke Gary's expensive guitar because he was kicking a ball indoors which he was told not to do.
intention: Gary wants to punish Larry.
moral_action: Gary tells Larry that he was told not to kick a ball around inside and will be grounded for a month for this.
moral_consequence: The idea that he should follow the instructions of the adults around him sinks in to Larry, and he vows to better his behavior.
immoral_action: Gary slaps Larry across the face and shoves him to the ground as he yells obscenities at him.
immoral_consequence: Larry is traumatized and terrified as he endures the physical pain of the beating.

norm: It's wrong not to help out people in need.
situation: Tom is running late but has a friend who urgently needs a ride.
intention: Tom wants to get to work on time.
moral_action: Tom pays for an Uber ride for his friend.
moral_consequence: Tom feels good for helping his good friend.
immoral_action: Tom ignores his friend and hurries off to work.
immoral_consequence: Tom feels bad for not helping his friend.

norm: It's good to introduce people to new things.
situation: Jan's mom still uses a flip phone and doesn't use the internet.
intention: Jan wants to decide whether to tell her mom about smart phones.
moral_action: Jan buys her mom a new phone and tells her how to use all of the different features.
moral_consequence: Jan's mom is amazed by the phone, and her life will become more convenient.
immoral_action: Jan doesn't want her mom to think too hard, so she lets her continue using her flip phone.
immoral_consequence: Jan's mom is missing out on a better experience and others will judge her.

norm: You shouldn't binge drink because it's bad for you.
situation: Peter is at a party where he doesn't know too many people.
intention: Peter wants to make new friends.
moral_action: Peter decides to drink moderately  and entertains everyone with fun stories.
moral_consequence: Peter feels great the next day.
immoral_action: Peter starts drinking with a few guys he just met, and they bond while drinking all night.
immoral_consequence: Peter is terribly sick the next day.

norm: It's wrong to be racist.
situation: Ron is deciding between potential hires to fill a position at his company and has to choose between a white candidate and a black candidate.
intention: Ron wants to make a hiring decision by the end of the day.
moral_action: Ron focuses solely on their credentials to make sure he picks the best hire.
moral_consequence: The company hires the better qualified candidate, and they end up being a very effective employee.
immoral_action: Ron doesn't pay much attention to their experience and decides to hire the white candidate, assuming that the black candidate won't work as hard.
immoral_consequence: The company hires the white candidate, who ends up being unqualified for the job, and they have to rehire.

norm: You should be supportive of your partner's job.
situation: Logan's wife Heidi had a hard day at work and comes to Logan for comfort.
intention: Logan wants to help his wife's stress.
moral_action: Logan tells her to keep pushing through, and she'll improve.
moral_consequence: Heidi tackles her work with renewed vigor and succeeds.
immoral_action: Logan tells her that she may not be cut out for it and should quit.
immoral_consequence: Heidi loses self-esteem, thinking that she's not good enough to hold a job.

norm: A professional should not embarrass their patient.
situation: Ryan is a doctor that has a patient had a heart attack from a poor diet.
intention: Ryan wants to talk to their patient.
moral_action: Ryan tells his patient that there are diets they can go on to improve their health.
moral_consequence: Ryan's patient will be open to changing their diet.
immoral_action: Ryan tells his patient that they are a slob and it is killing them.
immoral_consequence: Ryan's patient will feel discouraged from changing their diet.

norm: It's polite to offer your services to others.
situation: Kerry is driving home and sees his friend Lisa walking on the side of the road.
intention: Kerry wants to get home.
moral_action: Kerry pulls up beside Lisa and offers her a ride.
moral_consequence: Lisa gets in the car and thanks Kerry for the ride.
immoral_action: Kerry passes by Lisa without giving a thought to offering her a ride.
immoral_consequence: Lisa gets mad at Kerry for passing by her without saying anything.

norm: It's bad to date someone that causes you pain.
situation: Julie's boyfriend is verbally abusive and her sister urges her to leave him.
intention: Julie wants to respond to her sisters suggestion.
moral_action: Julie listens to her sister and ends her relationship.
moral_consequence: Julie is free to pursue a healthier relationship.
immoral_action: Julie decides to ignore her sister and give her boyfriend another chance.
immoral_consequence: Julie's self-esteem continues to degrade as his abuse continues.

norm: It's mean to tell someone they don't have talent.
situation: Katie is going to try out for the talent competition at her school next month.
intention: Katie wants to get feedback about her singing from a family member.
moral_action: Katie asks her brother to listen to her sing, and he tells her that if she keeps practicing she'll get better.
moral_consequence: Katie tells her brother that she intends to practice every day.
immoral_action: Katie sings a song for her brother, and he says that she shouldn't enter the contest because she has no talent.
immoral_consequence: Katie gets upset and runs out of the room crying.

norm: Outbursts are frowned upon.
situation: Mary's kid broke a vase at home by accident.
intention: Mary wants her kid  to answer for the damage.
moral_action: Mary asks her kid to explain the accident and promise to be careful next time.
moral_consequence: Her kid feels relieved and hugs Mary for her kindness and understanding.
immoral_action: Mary gets very furious while questioning her kid about the accident.
immoral_consequence: Her kid feels scared and immediately starts to cry.

norm: It's rude to call someone when they're sleeping.
situation: James was watching cartoons at 2 AM and heard a really funny joke.
intention: James wants to share the joke with his best friend.
moral_action: James waits until the next day at school to tell his friend the joke.
moral_consequence: James' friend laughs and tells James that's the funniest thing he's heard all year.
immoral_action: James calls his friend at 2 AM to recite the joke.
immoral_consequence: James' friend swears at him and throws the phone across the room.

norm: It's wrong to intentionally cause harm to someone's relationship.
situation: Ken and Bob are talking about Bob's girlfriend one day at lunch, and Ken does not like Bob's girlfriend.
intention: Ken wants to give Bob some advice.
moral_action: Ken keeps his mouth shut and gives Bob good advice while keeping his personal opinion aside.
moral_consequence: Bob and Ken's friendship is preserved.
immoral_action: Ken tells Bob he hates his girlfriend and tries to sabotage the relationship.
immoral_consequence: Bob gets mad at Ken, and they get into a fight.

norm: It's good to avoid hurting someone's feelings.
situation: Nancy walks into the house where her children are cleaning up as a surprise for her.
intention: Nancy wants to react to the children's efforts.
moral_action: Nancy tells the children what a great job they did and overlooks all the imperfections.
moral_consequence: The children feel great about themselves and learn how to do a better job as they grow older.
immoral_action: Nancy points out all the things the children could have done better.
immoral_consequence: The children feel like they're not good at cleaning and don't try again.

norm: It's good to share food with others.
situation: Thiago is at school on lunch break and notices a classmate doesn't have any lunch.
intention: Thiago wants to find out why the classmate doesn't have any lunch.
moral_action: Thiago offers half of his sandwich to his classmate and asks what happened to their lunch.
moral_consequence: Thiago's classmate is thankful for the food and explains what happened to their lunch.
immoral_action: Thiago asks the classmate in front of everybody why they don't have a lunch.
immoral_consequence: Thiago's classmate is embarrassed by the attention and runs away from the table.

norm: You shouldn't control your partner.
situation: Kevin is watching TV when his wife Ann tells him that she's going to her friend Susan's house.
intention: Kevin wants to react to Ann's statement.
moral_action: Kevin tells Ann to have a great time with her friend and to drive safe.
moral_consequence: Ann is pleased Kevin wisher her a pleasant goodbye and that he's worried about her safety.
immoral_action: Kevin tells Ann that he despises Susan and is forbidding Ann to go see her.
immoral_consequence: Ann is shocked that her husband is preventing her from seeing her friend and is worried he's acting crazy.

norm: It's good to help out people down on their luck.
situation: Mary has decided to do her community service at the local soup kitchen.
intention: Mary wants to complete her community service as part of her high school graduation requirements.
moral_action: Mary goes for her shifts at the soup kitchen and helps prepare, cook, serve and clean up for every meal.
moral_consequence: Mary receives credit for the community service and get her diploma, as well as helps those in the community that need it most.
immoral_action: Mary goes for her shifts at the soup kitchen but doesn't do much and spends all her time on her phone.
immoral_consequence: Mary does not receive credit for the community service and fails to meet her graduation requirement.

norm: It's good to teach children about abuse.
situation: The school Jim's daughter attends recently had a sexual misconduct issue.
intention: Jim wants to talk to his daughter about the issue.
moral_action: Jim talks to his daughter about ways to identify predators and about how to protect herself.
moral_consequence: Jim's daughter is able to keep herself safe and helps apprehend the predator in the school.
immoral_action: Jim tells his daughter not to worry about what happened and not to ask her friends about it.
immoral_consequence: Jim's daughter isn't aware of any way to protect herself and falls victim to the same sexual predator.

norm: It's wrong to start fights at school.
situation: Ken gets picked on every day by a bully at schoool.
intention: Ken wants to make the bully stop bothering him.
moral_action: Ken goes to the school counselor and informs them about the situation with the bully.
moral_consequence: Ken isn't bothered by the bully any longer after the bully is disciplined.
immoral_action: Ken goes up to his bully and kicks him in the crotch then punches him in the face.
immoral_consequence: Ken is suspended from school for two weeks.

norm: It's not okay to start fights with your family.
situation: Brody's brother owed him ten dollars, and he saw him at his mother's house one day.
intention: Brody wants his ten dollars back.
moral_action: Brody walks up to his brother and asks him if he has the ten dollars.
moral_consequence: Brody's brother gets his wallet out and gives him the money that he owes him.
immoral_action: Brody puts his brother in a headlock and starts a fight over the ten dollars.
immoral_consequence: Brody's brother punches him in the face and their mother kicks them out the house.

norm: You shouldn't ignore someone who is suicidal.
situation: Gary gets a text from his friend Don who says he is thinking about killing himself.
intention: Gary wants to decide whether to respond to the text.
moral_action: Gary immediately gives Don a call and does his best to talk Don out of suicide.
moral_consequence: Don feels slightly better after talking to Gary and decides to seek professional help.
immoral_action: Gary ignores the text because he thinks Don is just being overly dramatic.
immoral_consequence: Don feels he has no one left to turn to and kills himself later that night.

norm: It is wrong to tell on a friend.
situation: Gina has just found out that her friend Tim has been having unprotected sex with strangers, and she feels worried.
intention: Gina wants to keep Tim safe.
moral_action: Gina has a heart-to-heart with Tim and explains how she is scared for him and asks him if there is anything wrong.
moral_consequence: Tim opens up to Gina and agrees to stop his risky behavior.
immoral_action: Gina tells Tim's parents what he has been doing.
immoral_consequence: Tim is punished and no longer trusts Tina.

norm: It is not okay to have rude nicknames for people.
situation: Hank is a high school student in a class that has a new transfer student who is a bit overweight.
intention: Hank wants to talk to his friends about the new student.
moral_action: Hank talks to his friends and asks if they should invite the new student to their next movie night.
moral_consequence: The new student is excited to get the invitation and feels welcomed at the new school.
immoral_action: "Hank refers to the new student as ""fatso"" when he is talking to his friends about the new student."
immoral_consequence: "The new student overhears Hank calling him ""fatso"" and hates Hank from that moment forward."

norm: You shouldn't do violent things to people.
situation: Janet is frustrated with a rude customer at the movie theater concession stand where she works.
intention: Janet wants to get rid of the customer.
moral_action: Janet gets her supervisor, so he can tell the customer to leave.
moral_consequence: Janet is rewarded for doing the right thing with a bonus.
immoral_action: Janet hits the customer in the face with a bucket of popcorn.
immoral_consequence: Janet gets fired using violent behavior with one of the customers.

norm: It's hurtful to lead someone on in a relationship.
situation: Mary  has been dating Fred since high school but lately she has also been interested in a guy she works with.
intention: Mary wants to explore new relationships.
moral_action: Mary tells Fred she wants to take a break and see other people.
moral_consequence: Fred agrees and the two part on good terms.
immoral_action: Mary asks her coworker out but wants to keep seeing Fred incase her new relationship doesn't work out.
immoral_consequence: Fred finds out about Mary's coworker and causes a scene at her job which gets her reprimanded by her boss.

norm: It is bad to shove people
situation: Charles is standing in a long line at the grocery store, and wants to ask the cashier if he can run back to grab milk without losing his place.
intention: Charles wants to try to be heard by the clerk.
moral_action: Charles asks the man in front of him if he can please get through to ask a question to the clerk.
moral_consequence: The man is happy to oblige, and Charles is able to get a response to his question.
immoral_action: Charles pushes the man in front of him trying to get to where the cashier can hear him.
immoral_consequence: The man that Charles pushes gets angry, and punches him in the face.

norm: It is wrong to be a batterer.
situation: Karl's wife spent all their food money for the month.
intention: Karl wants to confront his wife.
moral_action: Karl tells his wife he has to sell of her possessions for food money.
moral_consequence: Karl's wife will feel remorse for spending the money.
immoral_action: Karl physically accosts his wife.
immoral_consequence: Karl's wife will call the police to arrest Karl.

norm: It's careless to not pay attention while driving.
situation: Adam is driving on the highway when he receives a text from his boss.
intention: Adam wants to respond to his boss.
moral_action: Adam presses a button on his Bluetooth headset calling his boss.
moral_consequence: Adam and his boss grow closer talking about their jobs before work.
immoral_action: Adam glances at his phone sending a text while driving.
immoral_consequence: Adam gets into a car accident.

norm: It's good to communicate with your friends.
situation: John sees that his phone is ringing and that on call display it's his friend Tony.
intention: John wants to stop the phone from ringing.
moral_action: John picks up the phone and talks to Tony thereby stopping it from continuing to ring.
moral_consequence: Tony is happy to hear his friend's voice and talks to him for a while.
immoral_action: "John presses ""block"" on his phone thereby silencing the call and sending it to voicemail."
immoral_consequence: Tony is annoyed that his friend is not picking up the phone since he wants to talk with him.

norm: There is no need to be mean with your siblings.
situation: Janet goes to her brother Joe's room to ask him if he thinks her shoes go with her dress.
intention: Joe wants to answer Janet's question.
moral_action: Joe tells Janet that they do go together and that she looks great.
moral_consequence: Janet is delighted that her brother helped her choose her outfit and that he's so complimentary toward her.
immoral_action: Joe tells Janet that everything on her looks hideous and that she shouldn't bother trying.
immoral_consequence: Janet is hurt and embarrassed and feels self conscious so she begins to cry.

norm: It's rude to mistreat people in the service industry.
situation: Carlita just received her order from the food counter and it does not match what she ordered.
intention: Carlita wants to get the order corrected.
moral_action: Carlita lets the worker know that the order is wrong and explains clearly what her order was.
moral_consequence: The worker apologizes for the mistake and quickly fixes Carlita's order, and she also receives a full refund.
immoral_action: Carlita draws attention to herself with volume and tosses the food she received back over the counter all over the worker that gave it to her.
immoral_consequence: Carlita is perceived as crazy and rude and promptly removed and barred from the premises.

norm: It's wrong to wish harm on a family member.
situation: Jasmine is having an argument with her mother who is leaving to work.
intention: Jasmine wants to express her anger.
moral_action: Jasmine speaks frankly with her mother informing her how upset she is and requests that they talk things throw when her mother gets home.
moral_consequence: Jasmine's mother is impressed with her daughter's maturity and agrees to hear her out when she gets home.
immoral_action: Jasmine blurts out that she doesn't care about her own mother's safety because of the disagreement.
immoral_consequence: Jasmine's mother is hurt that her daughter would wish ill of her even if it was out of frustration.

norm: It's good to hug your dog to show your love.
situation: Andy comes home after a long day to find his dog Rex in front of the door.
intention: Andy wants to respond to Rex.
moral_action: Andy gives Rex a big hug and tells him he is a good dog.
moral_consequence: Rex is excited and happy and feels very loyal to Andy.
immoral_action: Andy loudly tells Rex to get out of the way.
immoral_consequence: Rex slinks away feeling sad and unloved.

norm: It's good to warn parents of dangers to their children.
situation: Joe is looking out of Joe's window at the children playing on his lawn.
intention: Joe wants to avoid children being injured by falling into a dry well on his property.
moral_action: Joe explains to the parents of the children that there is an old dry well on Joe's property which might be fallen into.
moral_consequence: Parents in the neighborhood appreciate Joe and make sure to keep children safe following Joe's advice and no one is injured or upset.
immoral_action: Joe emerges from his house with a pistol aimed at the children and screams at them to go without explaining why.
immoral_consequence: The children are frightened and many parents in the neighborhood are mistrustful of Joe and do not like Joe.

norm: It's rude to tell someone that they eat too much.
situation: Adrian's sister has been putting on a lot of weight recently, and he is worried about her health.
intention: Adrian wants his sister to be at a healthy weight.
moral_action: Adrian has a conversation with his sister about how he is worried for her health.
moral_consequence: Adrian's sister tells him that she will try to work on it and ends up losing some weight.
immoral_action: Adrian tells his sister over lunch that she is becoming fat and ugly.
immoral_consequence: Adrian's sister swears at him, storms off, and stops talking to him altogether.

norm: It's not okay to wish death on someone.
situation: Mary and her mother were having an argument and Mary was upset.
intention: Mary wanted her mother to know her anger.
moral_action: Mary talks about her feelings with her mother.
moral_consequence: They reach and agreement and both understand each other's feelings.
immoral_action: Mary tells her mother she wished she was dead.
immoral_consequence: Mary's mother is horribly upset and gets depressed.

norm: It's rude to laugh at other peoples misfortune
situation: Tom is walking down the street at night, and he sees someone fall down and drop all of their groceries.
intention: Tom wants to react to the situation.
moral_action: Tom rushes to the man's aid and asks if he's okay.
moral_consequence: The man is grateful for Tom's help and is happy for it.
immoral_action: Tom laughs at the man falling down and does not help him.
immoral_consequence: The man feels bad and is left alone to gather his things.

norm: it's wrong to do something that could intentionally hurt someone.
situation: Joseph lives in an apartment building and recently bought a new gun.
intention: Joseph wants to test out his gun.
moral_action: Joseph tests his gun by shooting at a gun range.
moral_consequence: Joseph gets to safely see how his gun works.
immoral_action: Joseph tests his gun by shooting at his neighbor.
immoral_consequence: Joseph gets arrested for shooting his neighbor.

norm: It's wrong to make light of people's death.
situation: Casey has just found out that his best friend's brother has died in a car accident.
intention: Casey wants to drive his friend home.
moral_action: Casey drives carefully on the way to his friend's house and makes sure he obeys all the traffic laws.
moral_consequence: Casey's friend thanks him for the ride and asks him to visit for a while.
immoral_action: Casey drives fast down the highway and laughs when his friend starts to cry.
immoral_consequence: Casey's friend slams the door as he gets out and doesn't talk to him for a month.

norm: You shouldn't bother people when they are working.
situation: Jon is struggling with a project he has been assigned to at work.
intention: Jon wants to ask a coworker to help.
moral_action: Jon waits until a coworker is on break to ask for help.
moral_consequence: Jon's coworker feels like he can mentor people at work boosting his confidence.
immoral_action: Jon asks a coworker who is busy working on another task.
immoral_consequence: Jon's coworker's performance has been slowed down.

norm: It's unacceptable to scream at children that you don't know.
situation: Wanda is at a grocery store when an unsupervised child rushes by her.
intention: Wanda wants the child to be with their parents.
moral_action: Wanda asks the child if they know where their parents are.
moral_consequence: Wanda is able to reunite the child with their parents.
immoral_action: Wanda yells at the child and tells them to go find their parents.
immoral_consequence: The child begins to cry because they don't know where their parents are.

norm: It is good to return someone's lost pet.
situation: Karen is an animal lover who took in a cat and later sees a lost pet poster with the cat on it.
intention: Karen wants to have a pet cat.
moral_action: Karen returns the cat to the owners and adopts a different one.
moral_consequence: The owners of the cat are happy and relieved to be reunited with her.
immoral_action: Karen decides to keep the cat because she has bonded with it.
immoral_consequence: The owners of the cat are devastated and believe something happened to her.

norm: It's wrong to do hurtful things towards others.
situation: Holly gets in an argument with her classmate, Allison.
intention: Holly wants to win the argument.
moral_action: Holly counters Allison's argument with a valid point and evidence to back it up.
moral_consequence: Allison considers Holly's argument and admits that it makes her see things differently.
immoral_action: Holly personally attacks Allison when she can't think of a counterpoint in the argument to shut her down.
immoral_consequence: Allison feels vulnerable and sad that she's been personally attacked.

norm: People are expected to keep their clothing clean.
situation: Dave has a job interview for an accounting position at a downtown office.
intention: Dave wants to commute to the interview without driving his car.
moral_action: Dave calls a taxi cab to take him to the office.
moral_consequence: Dave arrives at the interview looking professional, making a good first impression.
immoral_action: Dave walks through a muddy forest to reach the office.
immoral_consequence: Dave looks dirty and unprofessional when he arrives at the interview, making a bad first impression.

norm: It's wrong to injure a small child
situation: Sam is trying to get his son to eat his vegetables, but he is throwing a temper tantrum.
intention: Sam wants his son to eat vegetables.
moral_action: Sam figures out a way to combine vegetables with food his son likes.
moral_consequence: Sam's son ends up eating more vegetables and respects his dad more.
immoral_action: Sam hits his son with a belt for not eating his vegetables.
immoral_consequence: Sam's son becomes scared of his dad and trusts him less.

norm: It's wrong to make obscene gestures at people.
situation: Jim is at a party with his friends when he sees a beautiful woman across the room.
intention: Jim wants to get the attention of the woman.
moral_action: Jim goes near the woman and introduces himself to her and her friends.
moral_consequence: The woman is charmed by Jim and agrees to go with him on a date.
immoral_action: Jim goes near the woman and starts to make sexually charged gestures.
immoral_consequence: The woman is upset and gets her friends to throw Jim out of the party.

norm: You shouldn't ride a skateboard down the staircase.
situation: Ryan is carrying his skateboard home when he notices his crush hanging out by the stairs.
intention: Ryan wants to impress his crush.
moral_action: Ryan demonstrates his extensive trivia knowledge to his crush and her friends.
moral_consequence: Ryan's crush asks to him to hang out with him next weekend.
immoral_action: Ryan slides down the rail on the stairs while calling out his crush's name.
immoral_consequence: Ryan falls off his skateboard spraining his ankle.

norm: It is good to visit your sick grandmother.
situation: Ash receives a phone call from his grandmother inviting him to visit her on Saturday.
intention: Ash wants to enjoy his weekend.
moral_action: Ash talks to his grandmother about how she's doing and play games with her.
moral_consequence: Ash learns more about his grandmother and apperciates her more.
immoral_action: Ash spends his weekend skateboarding at a nearby park.
immoral_consequence: Ash skates all day while his grandmother spends her day alone not doing much.

norm: It's bad to have road rage.
situation: Taylor is in traffic and is stuck behind a driver who does not notice the light turned green.
intention: Taylor wants to get the driver to press on their gas pedal.
moral_action: Taylor beeps her horn once to let the driver know about the light, and the driver goes forward.
moral_consequence: The other drives waves in a friendly way to Taylor, and they both get to their destinations stress free.
immoral_action: Taylor screams and yells at the car in front of her, hurling expletives, until the drives goes forward.
immoral_consequence: The other driver shouts back and Taylor feels guilty.

norm: You shouldn't touch your coworkers.
situation: Becca is happy that her favorite coworker is finally back from going on vacation.
intention: Becca wants to greet and welcome the coworker back.
moral_action: Becca buys her coworker her favorite coffee drink giving it to her while welcoming her back.
moral_consequence: Becca has made a good impression on her coworker and maintained professionalism.
immoral_action: Becca runs up and embraces her coworker in a tight hug.
immoral_consequence: Becca may be seen as unprofessional by her work peers and boss.

norm: It's good to say a well-meaning remark to someone struggling.
situation: Claire sees that her friend Ashley is down in the dumps about a breakup.
intention: Claire wants to give Ashley her advice.
moral_action: Claire tells Ashley that she is a strong person, and she will be fine.
moral_consequence: Ashley gets strength from Claire's support and feels better about herself everyday.
immoral_action: Claire tells Ashley that it's her fault for the breakup because she has bad taste in men.
immoral_consequence: Ashley cries and feels like everything in her life is hopeless.

norm: People shouldn't try to pigeonhole others into convenient holes.
situation: Bill has known Tom for many years but now Tom wants to start hanging out more with Bill, and Bill isn't sure they are very good friends.
intention: Bill wants to decide if Tom is a good enough friend to hang out with.
moral_action: Bill doesn't label Tom in any particular way and hangs out with him when he is not busy.
moral_consequence: Tom and Bill find that they both enjoy life more thoroughly as a result of the increased social interaction.
immoral_action: Bill decides that Tom is an acquaintance, not really a friend, and declines to hang out with him.
immoral_consequence: Bill is hurt by Tom's refusal to hang out with him and Tom misses an opportunity to go out more often and socialize.

norm: It's rude to critque others' appearance.
situation: Mary has a new coworker who dresses in an unusual style.
intention: Mary wants to feel like part of a team in a pleasant work environment.
moral_action: Mary keeps her opinion of her new coworker's style to herself.
moral_consequence: Mary's work environment remains harmonious.
immoral_action: Mary comments loudly to other coworkers judging the way their new coworker dresses.
immoral_consequence: Mary's coworkers think poorly of her and are less friendly and cooperative toward her.

norm: It's wrong to hurt animals.
situation: Stacy has a son named Steve, and they catch a mouse that is running around in her house.
intention: Stacy wants to figure out what to do with the mouse.
moral_action: Stacy releases the mouse in her backyard.
moral_consequence: Stacy's son Steve will learn not to harm animals even if they are bothersome.
immoral_action: Stacy puts the mouse in a cage and does not feed it food while poking it.
immoral_consequence: Stacy's son Steve will develop a lack of empathy towards animals.

norm: It is wrong to be controlling of your girlfriend.
situation: Max has been thinking about seeing a comedy movie with his girlfriend Tina, but she wants to see a romance movie.
intention: Max wants to see the comedy movie.
moral_action: Max suggests that he and Tina watch the comedy and romance movies in the same day.
moral_consequence: Tina agrees with the idea, and they buy tickets for both movies.
immoral_action: Max tells Tina that she will only watch the movies he wants to watch.
immoral_consequence: Tina doesn't like Max's controlling tone and breaks up with him.

norm: It's wrong to date teens.
situation: Steven had been single for a while and was scrolling through his social media page.
intention: Steven wanted to get a date.
moral_action: Steven sent a message to a woman his age asking if she wanted to meet him for drinks.
moral_consequence: Steven and the woman had a great time getting to know each other at the bar.
immoral_action: Steven sent a message to a fifteen year old asking if she wanted to meet for dinner.
immoral_consequence: Steven was arrested for dating a minor and spent time in jail.

norm: It's good to make time for others.
situation: Tom has a busy week at work but his daughter asks if they can spend some time together during the week.
intention: Tom wants to tell his daughter if he can spend time with her during the week.
moral_action: Tom says that he'll cancel some of his meetings to spend time with his daughter that week.
moral_consequence: Tom's daughter feels like Tom wants to spend time with her even when it's difficult to find the time for it.
immoral_action: Tom tells his daughter that he can't spend time with her that week because he's just too busy at work.
immoral_consequence: Tom's daughter feels like he cares more about his job than he does about her.

norm: It is unprofessional to cry at work.
situation: Dave is a grocery store clerk who is restocking a shelf when he gets a text that his wife is dead, and he immediately has an urge to cry.
intention: Dave wants to find a place to cry.
moral_action: Dave sneaks outside by the loading dock at the back of the store and finds a place between two parked semi trucks to cry in private.
moral_consequence: The customers go on shopping without any clue that there is a man outside whose life has been torn to pieces.
immoral_action: Dave gets up from the floor where he was stocking and wanders into the cashier area where he begins to bawl his eyes out.
immoral_consequence: The customers all feel pity on Dave and are saddened by his public display of sorrow and start to cry too.

norm: You should appreciate it when someone gives you something.
situation: It's Hal's birthday and his friend gives him a present.
intention: Hal wants to accept the gift.
moral_action: Hal accepts the gift and thanks his friend and give him a hug.
moral_consequence: Hal's friend will think Hal really enjoys receiving the gift.
immoral_action: Hal takes the gift without saying anything in return.
immoral_consequence: Hal's friend will think Hal doesn't care about the gift.

norm: It's bad to yell at other people.
situation: Nicole and her husband Mike are having a disagreement.
intention: Nicole wants to win the disagreement.
moral_action: Nicole calmly explains the reasons why she thinks she is right.
moral_consequence: Mike sees the wisdom of Nicole's reasoning.
immoral_action: Nicole shouts at Mike, hoping to intimidate him by raising her voice.
immoral_consequence: Mike shouts back at Nicole, escalating the disagreement.

norm: It's rude to tell your girlfriend she is fat.
situation: Bobby has noticed that his girlfriend is starting to gain weight.
intention: Bobby wants his girlfriend to stay healthy.
moral_action: Bobby starts buying healthier food to keep in the house.
moral_consequence: Bobby and his girlfriend both start to lose weight and feel healthier.
immoral_action: Bobby makes pig noises whenever his girlfriend starts to eat.
immoral_consequence: Bobby's girlfriend gets upset and eventually breaks up with him.

norm: It is good to recognize the sacrifices Soldiers made
situation: Adam is the leader of a country that recently exited a long bloody war and has to make a speech about it.
intention: Adam wants to make a speech about the long and bloody war.
moral_action: Adam talks about all the sacrifices soldiers have made for the country and honors them with a monument.
moral_consequence: Adam maintains some popular support and gets new recruits for the country's military.
immoral_action: Adam never mentions the sacrifices made by his soldiers and simply focuses on his own agendas.
immoral_consequence: Adam's soldiers becomes demotivated and Adam loses popular support in his country.

norm: You shouldn't let people tell you how to eat.
situation: John's friend tells John not to eat the french fries, as they are fattening.
intention: John doesn't want to offend his friend.
moral_action: John tells his friend that it's his cheat day for his diet and it's a one-time treat.
moral_consequence: John's friend agrees and leaves John alone.
immoral_action: John agrees with his friend and doesn't eat the french fries.
immoral_consequence: John wastes his money that he spent on the fries and goes hungry.

norm: It's rude to exclude others in social interactions.
situation: John is talking to his friend at a party when Billy, an acquaintance walks up to him.
intention: John wants to continue his conversation with his friend.
moral_action: John steps back to invite Billy to listen in on what he and his friend are discussing while he continues talking.
moral_consequence: Billy stands there and politely waits his turn to engage in the conversation, but he is happy to be part of the interaction.
immoral_action: John turns his body to ignore Billy and keeps talking to friend as if nothing happened.
immoral_consequence: Billy stands there awkwardly before leaving in a bad mood.

norm: It's bad to lash out at someone.
situation: Gary is waiting at a coffee shop to meet a client that is vital to his company's survival.
intention: Gary wants to order a coffee while he meets with the client.
moral_action: Gary smiles at the frazzled waiter when he finally visits the table.
moral_consequence: The client is impressed by Gary's sympathy and signs the contract.
immoral_action: Gary calls out to the waiter to hurry up and take their order as they are in a rush.
immoral_consequence: The client is put off by Gary's rudeness and does not sign the contract.

norm: It's nice to be hospitable to other people.
situation: Ben made a room of his available on Airbnb, and the person staying it in thinks it's too cold.
intention: Ben wants to say something to the person.
moral_action: Ben tells the person that he'll change the A/C to make it hotter.
moral_consequence: The person is more willing to leave a positive review on Airbnb for Ben.
immoral_action: Ben tells the person to put more clothes on and tells them to suck it up.
immoral_consequence: The person leaves a bad review on Ben's Airbnb profile.

norm: It's good to be direct with someone who upset you.
situation: Bill and his friend Bob get into an argument after Bob said something rude to Bill's sister.
intention: Bill wants to move past the transgression.
moral_action: Bill lets Bob know that what he said is not OK and that if they are to remain friends that cannot happen again.
moral_consequence: Bill settles the problem with Bob, and they put it behind them.
immoral_action: Bill does not say anything to Bob about the transgression and let's Bob get away with it.
immoral_consequence: Bill builds resentment towards Bob and their friendship deteriorates.

norm: It is wrong to punch children.
situation: Bobby's son John knocks over their big screen TV and breaks it.
intention: Bobby wants to punish his kid and teach a lesson.
moral_action: Bobby holds his anger in and sends his son to his room telling him to be more careful around electronics.
moral_consequence: John makes sure to be careful around the house and his relationship remains strong with Bobby.
immoral_action: Bobby allows his anger to take hold of him, and he punches his son in the arm as punishment.
immoral_consequence: John stops liking his dad Bobby and their relationship deteriorates to nothing.

norm: It's hurtful to ridicule your child.
situation: Joe's son Josh is in the high school play.
intention: Joe wants to give Josh some feedback.
moral_action: Joe gives Josh some constructive criticism.
moral_consequence: Josh uses Joe's criticism to hone his craft, growing up to be a famous actor.
immoral_action: Joe says Josh's acting skills are awful, and he should be ashamed.
immoral_consequence: Josh abandons his dream of being a professional actor, working in retail for the rest of his life.

norm: It's rude to deposit snow onto a roommate's car.
situation: Emily is getting ready to leave the house and head to school.
intention: Emily wants to get to her destination quickly.
moral_action: Emily digs the snow out from her own car and piles it in the yard where it is not in anyone's way.
moral_consequence: Emily gets her car out quickly and leaves no mess behind.
immoral_action: Emily digs snow from around her car and dumps it on her roommate's car to save time.
immoral_consequence: Emily's roommate is angry at Emily because she has to take extra time to dig her car out.

norm: It's wrong to make threats.
situation: Sam lives with his father Bill, who notices that his son's room is a mess.
intention: Bill wants Sam's room to be cleaned.
moral_action: Bill kindly asks Sam if he'll clean his room and tempts him with a treat as a reward.
moral_consequence: Sam cleans his room immediately and it looks spotless.
immoral_action: Bill threatens to spank Sam if he doesn't clean his room immediately.
immoral_consequence: Sam is defiant and runs away to his friend's house in response to the threat.

norm: It's good for a parent to treat their children well.
situation: Don's son David comes in second place at his track meet and is disappointed.
intention: Don wants to motivate David to do better.
moral_action: Don tells David that his performance was excellent and that there is no shame in taking second place.
moral_consequence: It lifts David's spirits to hear his father say that his performance was excellent, and he is motivated to keep training and trying to win.
immoral_action: Don tells David that he is ashamed of his lack of effort and that coming in second place is just as bad as coming in last.
immoral_consequence: David is angry and hurt that his father called his performance bad, and he does not feel motivated at all.

norm: It's good to care about your kids and their health.
situation: Mary is a busy single mother whose son tells her he feels sick on the day of an important conference.
intention: Mary wants to maintain a good balance between work and family.
moral_action: Mary arranges for a coworker to cover her while she takes her son to the doctor.
moral_consequence: Mary ensures her sons health is taken care of.
immoral_action: Mary feels he doesn't have a fever and so sends him to school, so she can attend her conference.
immoral_consequence: Mary has to rush to her son's side when he collapses at school.

norm: It's bad to show disrespect toward other people's beliefs.
situation: Sarah is looking through her social media feed and notices that an acquaintance of hers believes in a popular conspiracy theory.
intention: She wants to respond to the person who is expressing this belief.
moral_action: Sarah responds respectfully, that she does not believe this theory, and she posts links that disprove the conspiracy.
moral_consequence: The person is still not convinced, but they feel respected and are much more inclined to keep an open mind about their beliefs.
immoral_action: Sarah responds to the post by indicating that the conspiracy theory is completely crazy, and so must be anyone who believes it to be true.
immoral_consequence: The person is hurt and outraged by Sarah's response and their belief in the conspiracy is galvanized by the personal attack.

norm: You shouldn't hurt your dog.
situation: Marv walks into his bedroom and realizes that his dog peed on his bed.
intention: Marv wants his dog to learn not to pee on the bed.
moral_action: Marv starts bringing the dog out for walks more often.
moral_consequence: Marv's dog starts to use the restroom outside rather than in the house.
immoral_action: Marv takes off his belt and beats his dog with it.
immoral_consequence: Marv's dog runs away and Marv never sees it again.

norm: You shouldn't throw your future away.
situation: Steve is trying to decide on what he wants to do with himself after school.
intention: Steve wants to take it easy for a while after high school.
moral_action: Steve takes two night classes a week to keep educated and involved.
moral_consequence: Steve takes a little longer than normal to get through school, but finds a great job after.
immoral_action: Steve decides to scrounge off his parents for a few years, doing absolutely nothing.
immoral_consequence: Life passes Steve by, and nobody wants to hire him for the gaps in his resume.

norm: It's bad to be a glutton.
situation: Karen is at a buffet selecting food to eat.
intention: Karen wants a satisfying meal that tastes good.
moral_action: Karen eats sensible portions of food.
moral_consequence: Karen feels full, but not ill, and everyone around has enough to eat.
immoral_action: Karen loads several plates with more food than it takes to make a person full because it's the same price.
immoral_consequence: Karen makes herself sick, and others around KAren have less food and see that KAren is greedy and piggish.

norm: It is bad to judge races.
situation: Claire is white and her daughter has made a new friend who is black.
intention: Claire wants to keep her daughter safe.
moral_action: Claire asks to meet her daughter's new friend's parents, so they can all feel more comfortable with each other.
moral_consequence: Claire feels reassured and her daughter can keep seeing her new friend.
immoral_action: Claire tells her daughter she is not allowed to play with her new friend anymore because of her skin color.
immoral_consequence: Claire's daughter is upset and is influenced by her mother's bigotry.

norm: You shouldn't engage in road rage with people.
situation: Bob is late to work and is stuck in traffic on his way there.
intention: Bob wants to get to work.
moral_action: Bob turns up his music, ignores traffic and starts jamming out to himself.
moral_consequence: Eventually traffic clears up and despite being late Bob arrives to work in a great mood.
immoral_action: Bob gets angry and starts yelling at people in traffic through his window.
immoral_consequence: One of the people Bob yells at get's angry at him and rear ends his car on purpose.

norm: Complimenting someone is a nice thing to do.
situation: Brian's coworker Bertha walks in to the office with a brand new hairstyle that she's visibly proud of.
intention: Brian wants to mention Bertha's new haircut.
moral_action: Brian mentions that he thinks Bertha's new hairstyle is fashionable.
moral_consequence: Bertha is happy that someone recognized and appreciated her new hairstyle.
immoral_action: Brian mentions that he thinks he likes Bertha's old hairstyle better.
immoral_consequence: Bertha is embarrassed that she might've been mistaken about her new style looking good.

norm: You should always remember people's names at your wedding.
situation: Emma is standing in the receiving line at her wedding when an older lady walks up who Emma doesn't immediately recognize.
intention: Emma wants to greet the woman.
moral_action: Emma thinks back to who she invited, realizes that the lady is her mom's friend, Joan, and greets her by name.
moral_consequence: Joan is happy to speak with Emma and has a good time at the wedding.
immoral_action: Emma asks the lady what her name is and then thanks her for coming to the wedding.
immoral_consequence: The lady is upset that she came to the wedding and Emma doesn't even remember who she is.

norm: It's good to forgive people that hurt you by accident.
situation: Max burst into the room smacking Lilly, who was standing behind the door, in the face with the door.
intention: Lilly wants to make max understand that she's been hurt.
moral_action: Max apologies to Lilly, and she let's him know she understands he didn't see her.
moral_consequence: Max tends to Lilly's injured face before hanging out with her for the rest of the day.
immoral_action: Lilly glares at Max and walks away when he tries to apologize.
immoral_consequence: Max spends the rest of the day thinking about the end of his friendship with Lilly.

norm: It's rude to disturb people in a public setting.
situation: Carol is eating at a restaurant when she notices her district's political representative whose policies she disagrees with sitting nearby, so she walks over to his table.
intention: Caroline wants to express her displeasure at the representative.
moral_action: Caroline asks for the representative's contact info and then later that evening sends him an email stating her problems with his policies.
moral_consequence: Caroline feels strong and accomplished, like perhaps she made a difference.
immoral_action: Caroline  raises her voice at him and begins asking him questions about his policies and motives.
immoral_consequence: Caroline is asked to leave the restaurant by the management who calls the police.

norm: It's wrong to make excuses for bad behavior.
situation: Ernie cleans out his garage and piles trash in his backyard, which he sets on fire causing a terrible smell throughout his neighborhood, and his neighbors come to his door.
intention: Eric needs to talk to his neighbors about the smelly trash fire.
moral_action: Eric admits to creating an environmental nuisance and apologizes to his neighbors, promising to never do that again.
moral_consequence: Eric's neighbors accept his apology and some of them help him put out the terrible smelling fire that he started.
immoral_action: Eric explains that he only meant to burn the wood, and that the tires, plastic and carpets were burned by mistake.
immoral_consequence: Eric's neighbors don't believe him because it is all in one big pile and Eric loses some of his standing as a good member of the community.

norm: It's wrong to deceive other people about your identity.
situation: Ryan and his friends are standing outside of a bar downtown.
intention: Ryan wants to get into the bar with his friends.
moral_action: Ryan waits in line and shows the bouncer his ID when he gets to the door.
moral_consequence: Ryan gets through the door and sits at the bar to order a drink.
immoral_action: Ryan tells the bouncer that he is a celebrity in order to get in the bar quicker.
immoral_consequence: The bouncer throws Ryan out on the street and bans him for life.

norm: It's wrong to take things from people that aren't yours.
situation: Giselle's mom only buys regular wooden pencils for her to bring to school, and she sees her schoolmates with fancy mechanical pencils.
intention: Giselle wants a mechanical pencil.
moral_action: Giselle brings her allowance with her to the store and buys a mechanical pencil.
moral_consequence: Giselle's mom is proud of her for the responsibility that she has shown.
immoral_action: Giselle picks a mechanical pencil out of someone else's art box when they are not looking.
immoral_consequence: Giselle's mom sees the pencil in her backpack and punishes her for stealing.

norm: You should ask before taking something.
situation: Cooper is working on his car and needs a specific tool that his dad has.
intention: Cooper wants to acquire the tool for his car.
moral_action: Cooper asks his dad to use the tool.
moral_consequence: Cooper's dad lets Cooper use the tool and knows where the tool is.
immoral_action: Cooper uses the tool without informing his dad.
immoral_consequence: Cooper's dad will think someone stole the tool and will become anxious.

norm: It's wrong to steal something that isn't yours.
situation: Sun's favorite musician has just put out a new album that Sun has been dying to listen to.
intention: Sun wants to download the album.
moral_action: Sun decides to purchase the album from a digital store.
moral_consequence: Sun supports his favorite artist and gets to listen to their music on his smart phone.
immoral_action: Sun downloads the album for free via online torrenting.
immoral_consequence: Sun now has the entire album on his smartphone without having to pay for it.

norm: It's not good to lie.
situation: Frasier who is 31 is interviewing for a contest with contest director Roz when she asks him how old he is.
intention: Frasier wants to state his age.
moral_action: Frasier tells Ros that he just recently turned thirty one years old.
moral_consequence: Ros then asks Frasier for his ID which shows he is indeed thirty one and tells him he qualifies to be entered in the draw.
immoral_action: Frasier assumes he'll have a better chance of winning the contest if he's older, so he says he's 40.
immoral_consequence: Ros then asks Frasier for ID which then clearly shows he's not 40, and she disqualifies him from the contest.

norm: You should not judge others financial situation.
situation: Toni has a niece she loves.
intention: Toni wants her niece to go to a good school.
moral_action: Toni offers to help her sister with her niece's tuition.
moral_consequence: Toni's sister and grateful and Toni's niece is able to go to a good school.
immoral_action: Toni tells her sister that she needs to stop being lazy and get a better job, so she can actually provide for Toni's niece.
immoral_consequence: Toni's sister gets angry at her and cuts off her relationship with her niece.

norm: You shouldn't do things that get you banned.
situation: Fred, who has a concealed carry permit and a gun, goes to a nightclub, where a drunk person picks a fight with Fred.
intention: Fred wants to deal with the drunk person.
moral_action: Fred goes to the bartender and asks for help dealing with the drunk person.
moral_consequence: The bartender calls security, who throws the drunk person out and bans him.
immoral_action: Fred pulls his gun on the drunk person and tells him to back off immediately.
immoral_consequence: Security sees Fred with the gun, throws him out of the nightclub, and bans him from ever returning.

norm: It is wrong to be lazy at work.
situation: Unbeknownst to his colleagues, Lev is ahead of his schedule at work this week and still has 6 hours left on his shift.
intention: Lev wants to decide what to do.
moral_action: Lev talks with his coworkers to see if they need help.
moral_consequence: He gets a project for next week that would have been tough and manages to get a head start.
immoral_action: Lev takes it easy all shift and doesn't ask for any work from anyone else.
immoral_consequence: Next week Lev gets hammered with work and regrets not getting a heads up.

norm: It's not okay to impose on others and their homes.
situation: Adam's parents are moving to another state, but he has no desire to move with them and resolves to stay where he is.
intention: Adam wants to find a place to live.
moral_action: Adam gets a job and finds an apartment to rent with a few other guys to keep the costs down.
moral_consequence: Adam's friends maintain a good relationship with him and Adam preserves his sense of self-worth.
immoral_action: Adam goes to a friend's house and starts living on his couch without paying anything for it.
immoral_consequence: Adam's friend gets disgusted with Adam's lack of responsibility and eventually kicks him out of the house.

norm: It's wrong to commit fraud to insurance companies
situation: Dick got into a car accident after miscommunicating with the driver of another car.
intention: Dick wants his insurance company to help him out.
moral_action: Dick takes responsibility for causing the car accident when talking to his insurance agent.
moral_consequence: The insurance company is not that burdened by helping Dick out with his car.
immoral_action: Dick tells the insurance company that the other driver was completely at fault.
immoral_consequence: Money that's going to help Dick could be helping people who really need it.

norm: You should not expect to get things for free.
situation: Karen goes to the clothing store but the line to checkout is too long.
intention: Karen wants a shirt.
moral_action: Karen waits patiently in line until she is waited on at the register.
moral_consequence: Karen brings the shirt home to wear at the party later.
immoral_action: Karen demands to get the shirt for free because the line was too long.
immoral_consequence: The manager of the store bans Karen for a year for causing a scene.

norm: It's rude to skip in line.
situation: It's lunchtime and Kelly is near a popular cafe but she's running late for class too.
intention: Kelly wants to grab lunch and get to class.
moral_action: Kelly places an online order with the cafe through her phone, so she doesn't have to wait in line.
moral_consequence: Kelly gets her food quickly and makes it to her class on time.
immoral_action: Kelly goes straight to the front of the ordering line because she wants to get her food as quickly as possible.
immoral_consequence: Kelly is called out by the other patrons for being rude and shamed into leaving the cafe.

norm: People should take responsibility for their legal duties.
situation: Devin divorced his wife and was ordered by the court to pay child support for his child.
intention: Devin wants to respond to the court order.
moral_action: Devin pays the child support the court orders him to pay.
moral_consequence: Devin's child grows up with an adequate amount of material support.
immoral_action: Devin ignores the court order and does not pay the child support.
immoral_consequence: Devin's child grows up lacking in material support.

norm: It's okay to have dreams.
situation: Charlie is having nightmares about doing horrible things to people and is upset by them.
intention: Charlie wants to avoid the emotional pain from the nightmares.
moral_action: Charlie sees a therapist about the nightmares, who teaches him that dreams don't affect us.
moral_consequence: Charlie has the nightmares but can now deal with them properly.
immoral_action: Charlie refuses to sleep so that he doesn't have dreams or nightmares to worry about.
immoral_consequence: Charlie falls asleep at random points in the day and his work suffers.

norm: It's wrong to scam money from people.
situation: Craig is out of a job and has a computer with a good internet connection at home.
intention: Craig wants to earn money from home.
moral_action: Craig signs up for some online survey sites and does surveys.
moral_consequence: The survey creators get usable data from Craig's work.
immoral_action: Craig runs a Nigerian email scam and gathers people's account numbers, then takes their money for himself.
immoral_consequence: The people that give their account numbers lose their savings and have a hard time surviving.

norm: It's rude to not share food with your friends.
situation: Lucas brought a giant sandwich to work for lunch while Greg forgot to bring his lunch.
intention: Lucas wants to eat his sandwich.
moral_action: Lucas eats half of the sandwich and gives Greg the other half.
moral_consequence: Greg happily eats the sandwich.
immoral_action: Lucas eats the entire sandwich.
immoral_consequence: Greg watches sadly with a rumbling stomach.

norm: You shouldn't lie on your dating bio.
situation: Charles is looking for a new girlfriend on a dating app that just came out.
intention: Charles wants his profile to be interesting.
moral_action: Charles writes a fun and unique story on his profile that involves his potential girlfriend.
moral_consequence: Women who love writing and fantasy are interested in his profile and message him.
immoral_action: Charles overestimates his income and how exciting his life is in the description he writes for his profile.
immoral_consequence: Women are disappointed when they meet Charles and find that he doesn't meet their expectation.

norm: Lying to people is wrong.
situation: Denny's rent is due and his roomate has been asking him all week for his portion of the rent money.
intention: Denny wants his roomate to stop asking him for rent money.
moral_action: Denny wires his roomate his portion of the rent money.
moral_consequence: The rent is paid on time and Denny's roomate stops asking him about it.
immoral_action: Denny tells his roomate that he has already paid his portion of the rent to their landlord.
immoral_consequence: Their landlord never receives the full rent payment and both Denny and his roomate are evicted.

norm: It's best to be reasonable when asserting your authority.
situation: John is a police officer who catches some kids loitering on the sidewalk by the corner store.
intention: John wants to let the kids know loitering is illegal.
moral_action: John tells the kids calmly that loitering is illegal and that it's a law so that people feel safe to walk in certain areas and that they should go home.
moral_consequence: The kids are happy that John gave them a reason behind his actions and was so polite in asking them to leave.
immoral_action: John slams down his nightstick onto the concrete pavement making a loud bang as he proclaims loudly that he'll have them all arrested if they don't disperse immediately.
immoral_consequence: The kids are terrified of John and think he's a maniac and also start to learn that police cannot be trusted or respected.

norm: It's frowned upon to spread conspiracy theories.
situation: Kyle hears a friend at school strongly defending their opinion that the Earth is flat.
intention: Kyle wants to gain his friend's approval.
moral_action: Kyle looks up information online and has a civil conversation with his friend about what he read.
moral_consequence: Kyle is able to convince his friend that his information makes sense and his friend sees him as smart.
immoral_action: Kyle decides to tell others that the Earth is flat when his friend is around and uses his friend's arguments when doing so.
immoral_consequence: Kyle starts to gain a reputation at school for spreading untrustworthy information.

norm: It's rude to disallow a roommate from having guests over.
situation: Penny recently began renting a room in her house to Hope, but Penny gets upset when Hope has friends over and they make a mess in the living room.
intention: Penny wants to keep a tidy living space.
moral_action: Penny sits down and comes up with house rules regarding guests and cleanup with Hope.
moral_consequence: Penny is relieved that conflict with her roommate is avoided.
immoral_action: Penny bans Hope from having friends over.
immoral_consequence: Penny starts to feel uncomfortable because Hope gets hostile towards her.

norm: It's frowned upon to tell people what to do.
situation: Wyatt is relaxing at home when his wife Jody tells him she's going out shopping.
intention: Wyatt wants to react to Jody's statement.
moral_action: Wyatt tells Jody to drive safely and to have a great time out and about.
moral_consequence: Jody is delighted that Wyatt has wished her well and is concerned about her safety.
immoral_action: Wyatt tells Jody she can't go because he doesn't want her spending their money and forbids her from leaving.
immoral_consequence: Jody is terrified that Wyatt is acting abusive and is stressed that she's being confined.

norm: You can't expect people to exercise all the time.
situation: On a heavily rainy day Benard is waiting in his dance studio for his students.
intention: Benard wants to communicate with his students.
moral_action: Benard decides to cancel the class for the evening.
moral_consequence: Benard has a relaxing night reading in the studio.
immoral_action: Benard sends his students a text noting that they will never lose weight if they avoid exercising.
immoral_consequence: Benard is scolded by the owner of the studio for placing his students in unsafe conditions.

norm: It's wrong to give racist names to people.
situation: Henry is friends with a foreign boy, and he has trouble pronouncing his name.
intention: Henry wants to give the boy a nickname.
moral_action: Henry gives the boy a nickname based on one of his talents.
moral_consequence: The boy is thrilled that Henry thinks so highly of him, and they become best friends.
immoral_action: Henry gives the boy a nickname that ends up sounding racist.
immoral_consequence: The boy is offended by the name Henry gives him and stops hanging around him.

norm: You shouldn't skip ahead in line of someone.
situation: Jane forgot to pack a lunch today and needs to get something to eat from her workplace's cafeteria.
intention: Jane wants to finish up a presentation during lunch.
moral_action: Jane anticipates a long checkout line, so she brings her laptop to the cafeteria and works on her presentation while she waits.
moral_consequence: Jane's coworkers recognize her hard work and compliment her at the next meeting.
immoral_action: Jane starts a conversation with someone she knows to sneak into the front of the checkout line in order to get back to her office more quickly.
immoral_consequence: Two of Jane's coworkers see her cut in line and confront her in her office after lunch.

norm: It's immoral to drive up prices of your own auctions.
situation: Colin is auctioning off some old family heirlooms that have been gathering dust, and the first item is up for bids.
intention: Colin wants money for his item.
moral_action: Colin lets the auction go on without interference.
moral_consequence: People bid on the item, and Colin gets some money for his item.
immoral_action: Colin has a friend bid on the item to drive up the cost.
immoral_consequence: The auction organizers find out about the scheme and ban Colin from any more auctions.

norm: You shouldn't take money that you didn't earn.
situation: Scott is finishing his breakfast at a diner when he receives the bill and realizes that he doesn't have enough cash in his wallet to cover it.
intention: Scott wants to pay for his meal.
moral_action: Scott uses his credit card instead of cash to pay his bill.
moral_consequence: Scott's payment is accepted, and he leaves the diner.
immoral_action: Scott takes a tip left by another diner for the waitress off of their table and uses it to pay for his meal.
immoral_consequence: Scott is caught taking the tip and banned from returning to the diner.

norm: It's wrong to pretend to be pregnant.
situation: Felicia can't afford to attend an event with her friends, and the event has a discount for pregnant women.
intention: Felicia wants to attend the event.
moral_action: Felica borrows money from her friends to pay for the event.
moral_consequence: Felica has an amazing night with her friends.
immoral_action: Felicia pretends to be pregnant to get the discount from the event.
immoral_consequence: Felicia is exposed when the person standing in line tells the ticket holder the truth.

norm: People shouldn't give their word unless they mean it.
situation: Paul is flying home for Christmas tonight when his friend Josh asks him for a ride to the airport the next morning.
intention: Paul wants to respond to Josh's request for a ride to the airport.
moral_action: Paul tells Josh that he would help if he could, but he's leaving town tonight.
moral_consequence: Josh finds another friend to give him a ride and makes his flight.
immoral_action: Paul tells Josh not to worry and promises he'll give him a ride the next morning.
immoral_consequence: Josh misses his flight when Paul doesn't show up to take him to the airport, and he has to buy another plane ticket.

norm: It is wrong to make someone late.
situation: Mary's friend is driving them to work when she realizes that she needs to use the restroom.
intention: Mary wants to go to the bathroom.
moral_action: Mary waits until they get to work to use the bathroom that is there.
moral_consequence: Mary's friend is happy that she got to the job site on time.
immoral_action: Mary makes her friend pull over to the restroom, so she can go to the bathroom.
immoral_consequence: Mary's friend gets annoyed with her because she made her late to work.

norm: You shouldn't be against people of certain religions.
situation: Catherine is a feminist atheist who thinks that Islam hurts women, and the father of an Islamic family asks her for directions.
intention: Catherine wants to respond to the man.
moral_action: Catherine explains the layout of the city, and she buys the family a map while talking to them.
moral_consequence: Catherine changes her opinion on Islam after reflecting on her time with the family.
immoral_action: Catherine tells the man that she refuses to help someone with bad values before walking away.
immoral_consequence: Catherine is called a bigot when someone uploads her actions on social media.

norm: It's good to return things that don't belong to you.
situation: Annie is a student who has borrowed her textbooks from the library all year, and they are due to be returned the same week she'll be on vacation.
intention: Annie wants to avoid going to the library before her vacation.
moral_action: Annie mails her textbooks to the library.
moral_consequence: The library receives their textbooks and Annie is able to leave for vacation without having to go there.
immoral_action: Annie never returns her textbooks.
immoral_consequence: Annie incurs a late fee, and students who need the textbooks are unable to use them.

norm: It's good to be on time to school.
situation: James woke up late and didn't have time to eat breakfast before catching the school bus.
intention: James doesn't want to be hungry at school.
moral_action: James buys a bag of chips from the vending machine in the school hallway.
moral_consequence: James has an unsatisfying breakfast but at least feels full and gets to class before the bell rings.
immoral_action: James makes the ten-minute walk to a Starbucks near campus and arrives to class after the bell rings.
immoral_consequence: James is written up as tardy and forced to go to lunch detention.

norm: You shouldn't be greedy when fishing.
situation: Zach runs a crab fishing boat and has been out catching crab on the ocean for days, and the crab in the boat are not healthy.
intention: Zach wants to catch more crab.
moral_action: Zach catches crab for a few more hours, then decides that he needs to drop off his load.
moral_consequence: The crab all survive and Zach gets a healthy payment.
immoral_action: Zach catches crab for a few more days, ignoring the sick ones.
immoral_consequence: Some crab die, which causes other crab to die through dead loss, cutting Zach's profits.

norm: It is rude to be hypocritical in your actions.
situation: Scott tells his son Nathaniel not to eat ice cream for breakfast, but Scott always craves ice cream.
intention: Scott wants to eat ice cream.
moral_action: Scott waits until the evening to eat a bowl of ice cream.
moral_consequence: Nathaniel join his father in eating ice cream in the evening.
immoral_action: Scott devours an ice cream cone early in the morning.
immoral_consequence: Nathaniel becomes mad that he can't eat ice cream in the morning, while his dad can.

norm: It's wrong to be a burden on your partner's family.
situation: Joe, who has a girlfriend, decides to move out of his parents' house and in with his girlfriend's family.
intention: Joe wants to live in a place for cheap.
moral_action: Joe arranges a utility payment agreement with his girlfriend's parents and moves in.
moral_consequence: Joe's parents are satisfied that Joe is helping to pay the bills.
immoral_action: Joe lives with his girlfriend's parents and doesn't help pay any of the house bills.
immoral_consequence: Joe's girlfriend's family gets frustrated with Joe and eventually kicks him out of the house.

norm: You shouldn't get so angry when commuting.
situation: Jerry is on the way to his customer service job when he is cut off by a driver.
intention: Jerry wants to react to the incident.
moral_action: Jerry resists the urge to react and breaths deeply as he drive.
moral_consequence: Jerry is soon calm again and has a good day at work.
immoral_action: Jerry follows the driver and shouts at the top of his lungs at him.
immoral_consequence: Jerry's voice becomes too hoarse to take calls, and he must use sick time.

norm: You shouldn't change the difficulty setting on someone else's game.
situation: Jimmy tells Joe that Joe can play games on Jimmy's computer while Jimmy gets chores done.
intention: Joe wants to play challenging games.
moral_action: Joe makes sure he undoes any changes to game settings before closing the game.
