moral_consequence: Jimmy feels respected and tells everyone that Joe can be trusted.
immoral_action: Joe opens the settings to several games and changes the difficulties until he finds one that is challenging enough.
immoral_consequence: When Jimmy tries to play games he discovers the tampering and loses respect for Joe.

norm: It is bad to be disruptive on public transportation
situation: Annie was taking the bus home from her local library.
intention: Annie wants to get the bus driver's attention because her stop is coming up.
moral_action: Annie pulls on the stop cord to alert the bus driver that her stop is coming up.
moral_consequence: The bus driver safely stops the bus.
immoral_action: Annie yells at the back the bus driver's head that her stop is coming up.
immoral_consequence: The bus driver is startled and runs the bus into a ditch.

norm: You shouldn't be rude and petty to people.
situation: Dan is getting groceries at the store.
intention: Dan wants to finish shopping as fast as he can.
moral_action: Dan asks the manager to open up another check-out line to speed up service.
moral_consequence: Dan is thanked for his resourceful thinking by other people waiting in line.
immoral_action: Dan refuses to let a person with only a couple of items go in front of him in line.
immoral_consequence: Dan is given dirty looks by all the people around him.

norm: It's frustrating when customers use goods or services without paying.
situation: Nate was in a grocery store, and he noticed that they just recently brought in mangoes.
intention: Nate wants to try a mango.
moral_action: Nate grabs a bunch of mangoes and puts them in a bag so that he can take them home.
moral_consequence: The store gets properly compensated for all of the mangoes that Nate took.
immoral_action: Nate grabs a mango and eats it in a way so that no employees can see him.
immoral_consequence: The store doesn't get compensated for the mango that Nate stole.

norm: It's rude to take someone's food without permission.
situation: Jared is on lunch break at work and realizes he forgot to bring a lunch that day.
intention: Jared wants to eat something for lunch.
moral_action: Jared picks a nearby fast food restaurant and lets his coworkers know he is going out to get some food there, so he can take orders.
moral_consequence: Some coworkers take Jared up on the offer, and they all enjoy the food for lunch.
immoral_action: Jared looks in the refrigerator in the break room and helps himself to some of the food being stored in there.
immoral_consequence: Jared's coworkers are annoyed that somebody ate their food and suspect Jared which leads to him being punished.

norm: It's wrong to contact your ex's partner.
situation: Valerie is dating Will and is considering moving in with him.
intention: Valerie wants to know more about how Will is as a roommate.
moral_action: Valerie messages Will's college roommate and friend to ask how he is as a rommate.
moral_consequence: Valerie will get a fair perspective because Will's roommate has no grudge against Will.
immoral_action: Valerie messages Will's ex-girlfriend to ask how he is as a roommate.
immoral_consequence: Valerie will get a skewed perspective because Will's ex might bear a grudge.

norm: You should always try to work hard.
situation: Kent was just hired to mow grass for a large institution and isn't sure how to handle all of the work.
intention: Kent wants to earn a paycheck.
moral_action: Kent works overtime to cut all of the grass everywhere.
moral_consequence: Kent gets paid extra for his overtime work.
immoral_action: Kent mows the more well-traveled areas and neglects the more out-of-the-way ones.
immoral_consequence: Kent's supervisor sees the unmowed areas and gives Kent a pay cut.

norm: It's bad to expect others to pay for your vacations.
situation: Isaac was invited by his friend Amy to go on a trip to Berlin.
intention: Isaac wants to attend the trip.
moral_action: Isaac accepts Amy's invitation and takes his savings with him to help pay for his way and some gifts.
moral_consequence: Amy and Isaac are both able to afford to do everything they wanted.
immoral_action: Isaac accepts Amy's invitation but doesn't take any funds with him.
immoral_consequence: Amy is left to cover the cost of everything and it causes tension between her and Isaac.

norm: It's bad to not follow through with your commitments.
situation: Archie collapses onto his bed after working eighteen hours, and his mother sends him a reminder text about his promise to clean her yard.
intention: Archie wants to relax.
moral_action: Archie calls his friend asking him to help his mother with the yard work.
moral_consequence: Archie's mom has fun hanging around with Archie's friend.
immoral_action: Archie turns off his phone and goes to sleep.
immoral_consequence: Archie's mother is upset that his son broke his promise to her.

norm: You shouldn't try to get back at your ex.
situation: Sam has just proposed to his partner Jo.
intention: Jo wants to show off how happy she is.
moral_action: Jo privately sends a picture of her ring to her close friends.
moral_consequence: Jo's friends are happy and excited for her.
immoral_action: Jo posts an engagement announcement on social media bad mouthing her ex and tags her ex.
immoral_consequence: Jo's friends think she is petty and shallow.

norm: It's bad to quit last minute and leave people hanging.
situation: George promised to help pull Fred's truck out of the ditch today, but then he won baseball tickets.
intention: George wants to go to the baseball game.
moral_action: "George tells Fred, ""How about we pull your truck out a few hours sooner, and we both go to the game?""."
moral_consequence: Fred and George pull the truck out together, and then they both go to the baseball game.
immoral_action: George goes to the baseball game instead, leaving Fred literally ditched.
immoral_consequence: Fred is furious at George for abandoning him like that.

norm: Is wrong to lie about your experience
situation: Henry is being interviewed for a job by Tim when Tim asks Henry if he has any managerial experience.
intention: Henry wants to respond to Tim's question.
moral_action: Henry admits that he has no managerial experience but that he's a quick learner and would love the opportunity to show he's capable in the job.
moral_consequence: Tim decides to take a chance of Henry as he's appreciative of Henry's honesty and ambition.
immoral_action: Henry falsely tells Tim that he has plenty of managerial experience at his most recent job.
immoral_consequence: Tim follows up with Henry's references, finds out Henry was lying and declines to offer Henry a job.

norm: It's wrong to try to be someone you are not.
situation: Jill ran the wrong way and scored a safety against her own team, thus she dreads going to school the next day.
intention: Jill wants to avoid embarrassment for her mistake.
moral_action: Jill decides to practice in the gym all day to avoid the cafeteria and the playground.
moral_consequence: Jill is able to avoid the other students until the event is mostly forgotten.
immoral_action: Jill pretends to be another student in her class to avoid embarrassment.
immoral_consequence: Jill is eventually found out and is far more embarrassed with this then the original mistake.

norm: People shouldn't go back on their word.
situation: A major sale at the mall is happening on the same day that Charlie promised to take Alex to the museum.
intention: Charlie wants to go to the sale.
moral_action: Charlie takes Alex to the museum and goes to the mall after they leave.
moral_consequence: Alex has a good time and buys something at the sale with Charlie.
immoral_action: Charlie goes to the mall and makes Alex go to the museum by himself.
immoral_consequence: Alex gets mad at Charlie and feels lonely at the museum.

norm: It's expected that you tip the server at a restaurant.
situation: Phil takes Jenny to a fancy restaurant he can barely afford on their first date.
intention: Phil wants to save money.
moral_action: Phil tips the waiter for his service.
moral_consequence: Jenny is impressed with her dates generosity.
immoral_action: Phil decides to skip the tip.
immoral_consequence: Jenny takes note of Phil's lack of generosity and declines a second date.

norm: It's rude to judge someone who hasn't seen Blade Runner.
situation: Chris is talking with a group of his friends about different movies the they've seen.
intention: Chris wants to find out who has seen Blade Runner.
moral_action: When Chris finds out one of his friends hasn't seen Blade Runner, he invites her over to his house for a movie night.
moral_consequence: Chris and his friend share a fun evening together watching films.
immoral_action: When his friend says he's never seen Blade Runner, Chris makes fun of him.
immoral_consequence: Chris is admonished by his friends for his rude behavior.

norm: It's good to try to earn enough money for rent.
situation: Ruth has one pay period left before rent is due.
intention: Ruth wants to take time to herself.
moral_action: Ruth works extra hours during the week but makes sure to give herself two hours of self-care every day.
moral_consequence: Ruth feels tired but finds that her relaxation time makes her feel a lot better every day.
immoral_action: Ruth takes the week off of work to stay home for a spa day and other self care rituals.
immoral_consequence: Ruth's paycheck isn't big enough to cover her rent, and she is charged a late fee.

norm: You shouldn't blame people for things they didn't do.
situation: Rosa tries to use the household computer and finds that it's broken.
intention: Rosa wants to make sure she isn't blamed.
moral_action: Rosa tells the truth that she has no idea what happened, and she just found the computer this way.
moral_consequence: Her parents fix the computer, and they all move on with thier lives.
immoral_action: Rosa quickly blames Drew for breaking the computer before anyone can blame her.
immoral_consequence: Drew of course has no idea what Rosa's talking about and gets into an arguement.

norm: You should not take advantage of others.
situation: Ed was been out of work and his friend Brian has offered to let him stay at his place for a while.
intention: Ed wants to stay with Brian.
moral_action: Ed tries hard to get a job so that he can split the rent with Brian and pay his share of everything.
moral_consequence: Brian tells Ed that he likes having him stay with him.
immoral_action: Ed spends his days playing computer games and doesn't try to find a job or offer Brian any money for food or rent.
immoral_consequence: Brian gets upset with Ed and tells him that he'll have to leave soon if he doesn't go find a job.

norm: You shouldn't drink other people's beverages.
situation: Greg is exercising at a gym and wants some water when he sees someone's water bottle that was left behind an hour ago.
intention: Greg wants to drink some water.
moral_action: Greg walks several minutes to the water fountain in the gym and drinks from it.
moral_consequence: The owner of the water bottle returns and drinks the water, quenching his thirst.
immoral_action: Greg grabs the abandoned water bottle and drinks it all to quench his thirst.
immoral_consequence: The owner returns shortly after and tries to drink the water, only to find it empty, and is disappointed.

norm: It's wrong to not work hard at your job.
situation: Fred hates his job and sometimes does what he wants instead of what he should be doing.
intention: Fred wants to avoid the feeling of dread at work.
moral_action: Fred gets a new job that is better suited for him.
moral_consequence: Fred begins to work hard every day because he doesn't want to lose his job.
immoral_action: Fred comes into the office and plays video games for an hour before starting his work.
immoral_consequence: Fred's boss notices his lack of effort and fires him.

norm: It's not okay to be late in paying your dues.
situation: Liv wants an eyeshadow pallet on Mercari, but her rent is due tomorrow.
intention: Liv wants the eyeshadow pallet.
moral_action: Liv asks the seller if they could place the eyeshadow pallet on hold for her.
moral_consequence: Liv and the seller become friends as they negotiate.
immoral_action: Liv buys the eyeshadow pallet, and she tells her land lord that her payment will be late.
immoral_consequence: Liv has to pay late fees on top of her normal rent.

norm: It's bad to compare people based on appearances.
situation: Sarah's mother asks Sarah to compare two of her friends in terms of which one is a better friend.
intention: Sarah wants to compare her two friends.
moral_action: Sarah says that the friend who is kinder and more generous is a better friend because they set a good example for Sarah.
moral_consequence: Sarah's mother is proud of Sarah for judging her friends on important qualities.
immoral_action: Sarah says that the friend who is skinnier and prettier is a better friend because she's just more attractive and popular.
immoral_consequence: Sarah's mother is disappointed in Sarah for judging her friends based on shallow criteria.

norm: You shouldn't badmouth someone's religion.
situation: Joe is invited to attend his best friend Zack's church.
intention: Joe wants to respond to the invitation.
moral_action: Joe thanks Zack for the invitation and says he will check it out.
moral_consequence: Zack feels supported by his best friend.
immoral_action: Joe tells Zack that his religion is stupid, and he has no interest in going to church.
immoral_consequence: Zack is upset and ends his friendship with Joe.

norm: It is rude to mess up areas.
situation: Kelly has just finished cleaning in the living room when her son Jim walks into the room.
intention: Jim wants to play with his toys.
moral_action: Jim plays with his toys and then picks them up after himself.
moral_consequence: Jim's toys stay in the toy box until the next time that he plays with them.
immoral_action: Jim plays with his toys and leaves them on the floor that Kelly just cleaned.
immoral_consequence: Kelly is annoyed and picks up and throws all Jim's toys in the trash.

norm: You should share the household chores with your partner.
situation: Roy hates doing housework but his girlfriend, Shelly, can't stand it either, but neither of them want to live in a dirty house.
intention: Tom wants to decide on a proposal to put forth, about the household chores.
moral_action: Tom suggests that the two of them sit down and negotiate a housework schedule, to keep things in order.
moral_consequence: Shelly agrees with this approach, and they hash out a schedule that they can both live with, and the house stays relatively clean.
immoral_action: Tom takes a hard line approach and refuses to do any of the household chores, because he doesn't have time for that.
immoral_consequence: Shelly tells Tom that she will not be the only one cleaning the house, leaving the two at an impasse.

norm: It's rude to pester people.
situation: Cody is playing video games with his older brother in the living room.
intention: Cody wants a turn to play the game.
moral_action: Cody sits and waits for his turn as his brother finishes playing his.
moral_consequence: Cody gets to play the video game after his brother loses his game.
immoral_action: Cody jumps in front of the television so that his brother loses the game.
immoral_consequence: Cody's brother gets annoyed and throws the game controller at Cody.

norm: It's rude to park in front of your neighbor's house.
situation: Sam has just arrived home and realized that someone has taken his usual parking spot.
intention: Sam wants to park near his house.
moral_action: Sam parks in front of his own driveway blocking the other cars in.
moral_consequence: Sam's neighbor notices this, and he asks the others not to park in his spot.
immoral_action: Sam parks in front of his next door neighbor's house taking up their spot.
immoral_consequence: Sam's neighbor has to park even further away from their house.

norm: You shouldn't bother someone while they are eating.
situation: Cash often plays pranks on his mother, Tiffany, with scary props.
intention: Cash wants to prank Tiffany with a fake snake.
moral_action: After dinner, Cash puts a fake snake in the dishwasher.
moral_consequence: Tiffany has a good laugh after finding the snake while loading the dishwasher.
immoral_action: Cash puts a fake snake beneath the lid of a serving dish at dinner.
immoral_consequence: Tiffany goes to open the serving dish while chewing on her food, and chokes as she begins to scream at the sight of the snake.

norm: You should fire someone who breaks the rules.
situation: Bela is a manager at a company.
intention: Bela wants to review employee performance.
moral_action: Bela tells an employee that has numerous violations that they're being released.
moral_consequence: Bela is able to train a replacement employee properly and have increased efficiency.
immoral_action: Bela notices numerous violations on an employee's record but decides to keep them.
immoral_consequence: Bela has to deal with the employee's poor behavior which takes away from other work.

norm: It is expected to tip workers for their services
situation: John is at a cafe and he has just ordered a drink.
intention: John wants to pay for his drink.
moral_action: John pays with his credit card and leaves a generous tip.
moral_consequence: The barista is very grateful and prepares John's drink with extra care.
immoral_action: John pays with exact change.
immoral_consequence: The barista feel unappreciated and this affects his attitude negatively.

norm: Driving fast when you are inexperienced is reckless.
situation: Max just got his license last week and is glad that other family members don't have to drive him anywhere now.
intention: Max wants to drive the family car by himself.
moral_action: Max obeys all the traffic laws and is mindful of the fact that he is a new driver.
moral_consequence: Max arrives safely at his destination without violating any traffic laws.
immoral_action: Max drives down the road carelessly exceeding the speed limit and almost gets in an accident.
immoral_consequence: Max is pulled over by a police officer for speeding.

norm: It's wrong to deceive people out of loneliness.
situation: Jake is lonely and hasn't had a girlfriend in over eight years.
intention: Jack wants to get a date off Tinder.
moral_action: Jack decides to use his real photo and wait, after which a woman decides to go on a date with him.
moral_consequence: Jack and his date enjoy getting to know each other's real selves and fall in love.
immoral_action: Jack decides to catfish and put a fake photo of a male model and gets a date quickly.
immoral_consequence: Jack's date is horrified at Jack's dishonesty and Jack continues feeling alone.

norm: You shouldn't talk with food in your mouth.
situation: Greg is having dinner with his family.
intention: Greg wants to tell his family about his day.
moral_action: Greg chews and swallows his food, then talks about his day at work.
moral_consequence: Greg has a nice conversation with his family.
immoral_action: Greg tries to talk with a mouthful of chicken nuggets.
immoral_consequence: Greg chokes on his chicken and dies from asphyxiation.

norm: It is important to stick with commitments.
situation: Jack promised to go to the fair with his daughter but then his buddy Bill gives him football tickets.
intention: Jack wants to decide what to do.
moral_action: Jack goes to the fair with his daughter just like he promised.
moral_consequence: Jack's daughter loved going to the fair with her father.
immoral_action: Jack takes the tickets and goes to the football game.
immoral_consequence: Jack's daughter cries because she didn't get to go to the fair.

norm: It's rude to express that another culture's gatherings look ridiculous.
situation: Kyle is invited to a Diwali festival parade by his Indian friend Geet.
intention: Kyle wants to say something to Geet about the festival parade.
moral_action: Kyle tells Geet that the festival participants look very exotic and unlike anything he's ever seen.
moral_consequence: Geet proceeds to explain to Kyle the meaning and significance behind the costumes and dances in the parade.
immoral_action: Kyle tells Geet that the appearance, dress, and dance of the people in the parade seems primative and backwards.
immoral_consequence: Geet is insulted and gives Kyle the silent treatment for the rest of the day.

norm: You shouldn't spam the internet.
situation: Mark is running for mayor of his town.
intention: Mark wants to get more people to know who he is and that he is running.
moral_action: Mark goes door to door letting people know he is running.
moral_consequence: Many people that talked to him end up voting for him.
immoral_action: Mark posts requests for people to vote for him to every message board he can find and sends email to an address list from his work.
immoral_consequence: People are annoyed by the spam and do not vote for him.

norm: It's wrong to keep things that are not your's.
situation: Toby had a study session with Neal and Neal left his only jacket at Toby's place.
intention: Toby wants to decide what to do with the jacket.
moral_action: Toby decides to give the jacket back to Neal.
moral_consequence: Neal will have his jacket and not have to get a new one.
immoral_action: Toby decides to keep the jacket and not give it back to Neal.
immoral_consequence: Neal will have to buy a new jacket.

norm: It's wrong to do something your partner doesn't want.
situation: Hallam wants to spend the evening at a swingers club, but his wife Daphne doesn't like the idea.
intention: Hallam wants to go somewhere on a date.
moral_action: Hallam takes Daphne to her favorite cafe for desert.
moral_consequence: Daphne and Hallam enjoy a nice meal together.
immoral_action: Hallam takes his wife to the swingers club without telling her.
immoral_consequence: Daphne leaves Hallam alone in the club for the evening.

norm: It's rude to mess with other people's personal property.
situation: Lacy is in the living room with her roommates laptop on the couch.
intention: Lacy wants to lay down.
moral_action: Lacy asks her roommate if she can move the laptop.
moral_consequence: Lacy's roommate moves the laptop and Lacy has a guilt free nap.
immoral_action: Lacy throws the laptop on the floor and it's stepped on.
immoral_consequence: Lacy's roommate is angry and demands she replace the laptop.

norm: It's good when justice is done.
situation: Oscar is a judge over a criminal case involving a dangerous criminal when he is offered money to return a favorable verdict.
intention: Oscar wants to respond to the offer.
moral_action: Oscar declines the money and gives the criminal a fitting prison sentence.
moral_consequence: The criminal is behind bars for years, keeping society safer.
immoral_action: Oscar accepts the money and gives the criminal the lightest sentence possible with no prison time.
immoral_consequence: The family and authorities are outraged while the criminal returns to the world to continue his crimes.

norm: It's bad not to carry your weight in a group.
situation: Al, Bert, and Jill are in an office, working on coding a computer program for a client.
intention: Al wants leave early to start the weekend.
moral_action: Al works competently and focused and takes no breaks for lunch or anything else to get finished.
moral_consequence: Al turns in efficient work and because Al worked a little harder, gets to spend a little longer relaxing, while the team presses on and the client receives quality work.
immoral_action: Al types a lot of bad code because it is quicker to do.
immoral_consequence: Al turns in efficient work and gets to spend a little longer relaxing, while the team presses on and the client receives quality work.

norm: It's wrong to not take your studies seriously.
situation: Bob was contemplating going out with his friends on a Friday night.
intention: Bob wants to pass his history exam that is scheduled for Saturday morning.
moral_action: Bob tells his friends he needs to study and stays in, getting a full night of sleep.
moral_consequence: Bob takes the test and gets an A on it.
immoral_action: Bob decides to go out to the bar and drinks until two am.
immoral_consequence: Bob goes to the exam extremely hungover and ends up getting a D which puts him on academic probation.

norm: It is good to return things that are lost.
situation: Crystal is a bartender and a customer left behind a jacket after having some drinks.
intention: Crystal wants to pick up the jacket.
moral_action: Crystal takes the jacket and hangs it in the stock room just in case the customer comes looking.
moral_consequence: The customer comes in the next day looking for his jacket and Crystal goes into the stock room and gives it back which makes him happy.
immoral_action: Crystal takes the jacket and tosses it in the trash, thinking that the customer will never come back for it.
immoral_consequence: The customer comes in the next day looking for his jacket and Crystal is unable to give it back which disappoints him.

norm: It's wrong to take more than your share.
situation: Jan's family are forced to stay inside, and toilet paper is running out in stores.
intention: Jan wants to buy some toilet paper at the grocery store.
moral_action: Jan only buys one pack of toilet paper.
moral_consequence: Other families will be able to have enough toilet paper.
immoral_action: Jan buys up all of the toilet paper at the toilet store.
immoral_consequence: Others will not be able to buy some toilet paper for themselves.

norm: It's good to expect family to help around the house.
situation: Jenna's daughter just turned ten and started asking for an allowance.
intention: Jenna wants her daughter to learn about managing money.
moral_action: Jenna tells her daughter that she can earn money by doing chores around the house.
moral_consequence: Jenna has more free time and her daughter feels proud of her contributions to the family.
immoral_action: Jenna gives her daughter an unconditional allowance and does all the housework herself.
immoral_consequence: Jenna often snaps at her children because she's frustrated and overworked.

norm: It's wrong to steal donuts.
situation: Omar is at a cafe and having just ordered a coffee that the cashier left to go make, he notices some delicious looking donuts on the counter.
intention: Omar wants some donuts.
moral_action: Omar takes a couple of donuts and asks the cashier to add them to his order when he pays for his coffee.
moral_consequence: The cashier rings up Omar for the coffee and the donuts.
immoral_action: Omar takes a couple of donuts and puts them in his backpack without paying for them.
immoral_consequence: The cashier notices Omar steal the donuts and calls the police.

norm: You shouldn't referee a sports match you're betting on.
situation: Dean is a soccer referee and has been struggling financially lately.
intention: Dean wants to make more money to help with his finances.
moral_action: Dean asks his boss if he can referee more games in the near future.
moral_consequence: Dean is given more games to work and is able to catch up financially.
immoral_action: Dean places a large bet on the outcome of the game he will referee and then tries to influence the outcome of the game.
immoral_consequence: Dean's bet is discovered, and he is fired from his job and disbarred from the industry.

norm: It's rude to talk non-stop to people.
situation: Sharon is a greeter for a hotel and sees a customer coming inside to stay a few nights.
intention: Sharon wants to make the customer feel welcome.
moral_action: Sharon says hello to the customer and exchanges a few questions and answers with him.
moral_consequence: The customer feels welcome at the hotel and continues to the front desk to check in.
immoral_action: Sharon engages the customer in conversation and begins to tell her about every detail of the past week.
immoral_consequence: The customer gets uncomfortable and annoyed, then finally leaves the hotel to stay somewhere else.

norm: It is rude to return things in an unusable condition.
situation: Danny promised to return Jim's bike after a week, but both of its tires blew while riding it.
intention: Danny wants to return the bike to Jim.
moral_action: Danny gets the tires replaced before giving the bike back to Jim.
moral_consequence: Jim thanks Danny for returning his bike in working order.
immoral_action: Danny gives the bike back to Jim as it is.
immoral_consequence: Jim gets mad at Danny for ruining the tires on his bike.

norm: You shouldn't cheat on your projects.
situation: Paul is struggling to come up with ideas for a big project at work.
intention: Paul wants his project to be well received.
moral_action: Paul reviews his past work and is able to find inspiration from what he learned from them.
moral_consequence: Paul is able to present an original project.
immoral_action: Paul spies on some of his coworkers and incorporates their ideas into his project.
immoral_consequence: Paul's subterfuge is discovered and he faces censure.

norm: It's rude to interrupt others.
situation: John is at home with his wife Mindy having an argument.
intention: John wants his wife to listen to him.
moral_action: John listens to everything Mindy has to say and responds respectfully.
moral_consequence: Mindy feels happier and is more likely to listen to John and respect him.
immoral_action: John talks over Mindy, yelling at her and makes her listen to him.
immoral_consequence: Mindy feels resentful toward John and hurt.

norm: It's ineffectual to withhold necessary information.
situation: Dr. Jones was being paid by a pharmaceutical company to prescribe their opioid drug more, but the drug is effective.
intention: Dr. Jones wants to help his patients.
moral_action: Dr. Jones tells his patients that he's being paid by the company before prescribing the drug.
moral_consequence: Dr. Jones is following the laws required by doctors, and his patients trust him more.
immoral_action: Dr. Jones prescribes the opioid drug to his patients in pain without disclosing that he's getting paid.
immoral_consequence: Dr. Jones gets in trouble for taking money to prescribe a drug without disclosing that.

norm: It's important to practice reading when you struggle with it.
situation: Mina is in school and reads at a slower pace compared to other students.
intention: Mina wants to address her reading speed.
moral_action: Mina spends her free time trying to increase her reading speed.
moral_consequence: Mina becomes faster at reading.
immoral_action: Mina thinks she is just a slow reader and should concentrate on other subjects.
immoral_consequence: Mina will not become faster at reading.

norm: You shouldn't exclude people from your events.
situation: Henry moves into a new apartment, which is much nicer than his old place.
intention: Henry wants to have a party to show off his new home.
moral_action: Henry invites everyone he knows, including all of his work colleagues, even if they are people he is not particularly fond of.
moral_consequence: Everyone comes to Henry's party and has a good time, he spends his time entertaining and having fun with many of his guests.
immoral_action: Henry invites everyone he knows except one person from work that he doesn't like.
immoral_consequence: The person who was excluded is very upset and some of his other coworkers find it unacceptable that he excluded one colleague.

norm: It's rude to date two people at the same time.
situation: Brittney has been asked out next week by Todd and then her new neighbor Henry asks her out too.
intention: Brittney wants to go on a date.
moral_action: Brittney turns down Henry and only goes out with Todd since her asked her first.
moral_consequence: Brittney has a nice date with Todd, and he asks her out again.
immoral_action: Brittney makes a date with Todd for Friday night and a date with Henry for Saturday night.
immoral_consequence: Todd happens to see Brittney on her date with Henry, and he awkwardly asks her what's going on.

norm: It is bad to try to sabotage people's jobs
situation: Rania and Julian are both competing for a manager position at work but only one can get it.
intention: Rania wants to get the job.
moral_action: Rania begins working harder at her job and is nicer to everyone around her to make a good impression.
moral_consequence: The promotion committee notice's Rania' efforts and give her the promotion.
immoral_action: Rania logs into Julian's computer and messes with his files, so he will look bad.
immoral_consequence: Rania gets caught and is fired while Julian gets the promotion.

norm: You shouldn't fake mental illness.
situation: Joe, who forgot to bring his wallet to the store, sees a mentally ill man pan handling on the sidewalk.
intention: Joe wants to earn some money for the store.
moral_action: Joe does some back flips and break dances on the sidewalk.
moral_consequence: Joe gets money from strangers who are amazed by his dancing skills.
immoral_action: Joe panhandles while prentending that the has a personality disorder.
immoral_consequence: Joe gets called out and booed by strangers who notice that he's faking.

norm: It's discouraged to act out for attention in public.
situation: Jacob and Marcy are at the mall together looking for a new pair of shoes for Marcy.
intention: Jacob wants Mary to be more interested in him.
moral_action: Jacob helps Marcy find the perfect shoes and asks if he can buy her lunch.
moral_consequence: Marcy and Jacob have a great dinner together and get to know each other more.
immoral_action: Jacob gets up onto a display and begins singing to Marcy to catch her attention.
immoral_consequence: Marcy stares at Jacob, feels embarrassed and leaves the mall, asking Jacob not to call her anymore.

norm: It's prudent to be prepared for important exams.
situation: Kelly is feeling nervous about an upcoming physics exam that she doesn't expect to do well on.
intention: Kelly wants to stop feeling anxious about the exam.
moral_action: Kelly organizes a group study session with her classes.
moral_consequence: Kelly's classmates do better than they expected because of the group study session.
immoral_action: Kelly decides to skip studying and convinces her classmates to go out drinking to relax instead.
immoral_consequence: Kelly's classmates end up doing poorly on the exam because they skipped studying.

norm: It is bad to lie about cheating.
situation: Warren has been out all night with his mistress and when he comes home late his wife, Ellen, confronts him, asking if he's been cheating on her.
intention: Warren wants to explain his whereabouts.
moral_action: Warren admits to Ellen that he has indeed been having an affair and that he thinks their marriage is no longer working out.
moral_consequence: Ellen is shocked but finds herself in agreement with Warren and tells him that this is a good time to get a divorce.
immoral_action: Warren tells Ellen that he was out comforting his best friend who had been having some personal problems.
immoral_consequence: Ellen apologizes to Warren for suspecting him of cheating on her.

norm: You shouldn't shoplift from stores.
situation: Tara is shopping at the mall when she spots a lovely pink sweater that is quite expensive.
intention: Tara wants to add a pink sweater to her wardrobe.
moral_action: Not wanting to pay the hefty price of the sweater, Tara finds a lookalike for half the price.
moral_consequence: Tara saves money while adding a new pink sweater to her wardrobe.
immoral_action: Not wanting to pay the hefty price of the sweater, Tara sneaks it into her purse.
immoral_consequence: The alarm goes off as Tara is leaving the store, and she is arrested for shoplifting.

norm: It's wrong to go on a vacation without your partner.
situation: Violet is planning a vacation for the following weekend, and she knows that her husband Harry doesn't have any obligations.
intention: Violet wants to book airplane tickets.
moral_action: Violets checks with Harry to make sure he wants to go with her and then books their airplane tickets.
moral_consequence: Harry is thrilled by the unexpected surprise and is looking forward to spending the weekend with Violet.
immoral_action: Violet books an airplane ticket for herself but doesn't invite Harry along.
immoral_consequence: Harry becomes very jealous and skeptical of Violet's motives, wondering if she's cheating on him.

norm: It's good to write privately about very personal feelings.
situation: Meredith has been considering leaving her husband but is conflicted.
intention: Meredith wants to vent her feelings through writing.
moral_action: Meredith uses a journal app in her phone to write and safely store her thoughts about her relationship.
moral_consequence: Meredith is able to sort through her thoughts and feelings more effectively, helping her make up her mind.
immoral_action: Meredith uses a shared family notebook to journal about her feelings regarding her marriage.
immoral_consequence: Meredith's child and husband read the journal entry, causing lots of distress and upset in the household.

norm: It's wrong to eat something that doesn't belong to you.
situation: Leigh is at a dinner buffet at a fine restaurant in town.
intention: Leigh wants to try a bit of everything on the menu.
moral_action: Leigh notices an item she wants has run out, so she asks the service staff if they have more.
moral_consequence: Leigh gets to enjoy that item after they bring out a fresh batch.
immoral_action: Leigh sees the spare ribs have run out, so she grabs one from the plate of the person in front of her.
immoral_consequence: Leigh is caught doing this, and barred from the restaurant.

norm: You shouldn't award scholarships to students with awful grades.
situation: Mr. Green was the head of a college that Harry had applied to.
intention: Mr. Green wants Harry to play on his football team even though he has bad grades.
moral_action: Mr. Green tells Harry to reapply after working harder at a junior college.
moral_consequence: When Harry attends Mr. Green's college, he excels in both his schoolwork and on the field.
immoral_action: Mr. Green gives Harry a scholarship even though his grades aren't up to par.
immoral_consequence: Harry fails all his classes and is not eligible to play football anyway.

norm: It's rude to dismiss someone based on their issues.
situation: Henry is a supervisor at a grocery store and has a shelf stocker with Down syndrome who isn't doing very well.
intention: Henry wants to get the shelves stocked well.
moral_action: Henry assigns another employee to work with the troubled employee and train him thoroughly.
moral_consequence: With time, the mentally handicapped employee learns to do his job well and contributes to society.
immoral_action: Henry fires the employee with Down syndrome and hires someone else who can learn the job quickly.
immoral_consequence: The employee with Down syndrome never manages to find another job and feels terribly about life.

norm: You shouldn't aggressively sell to people.
situation: Tim works as a sales person for a home delivery company.
intention: Tim wants to be a top seller.
moral_action: Tim maintains a client list that he uses to track what his customers like to buy.
moral_consequence: Tim is able to keep track of his best clients and contact them only when what they like is available.
immoral_action: Tim targets elderly clients and calls them repeatedly to pressure them to buy.
immoral_consequence: Tim gets numerous complaints about his tactics.

norm: It's wrong to cheat on a test in school.
situation: Rob is taking his final exams.
intention: Rob wants to get a high score.
moral_action: Rob stays up all night studying extra hard for the exams.
moral_consequence: Rob is treated to his favorite meal when his parents see his high test score.
immoral_action: Rob hides his study notes in his sleeve and uses them to ace the exam.
immoral_consequence: Rob is flunked when the instructor finds out about him cheating.

norm: It is bad to hurt the planet for personal gain.
situation: Dana is a CEO of a large industrial company at a board meeting and is told one of the plants is losing money.
intention: Dana needs to find a way to cut costs at the plant.
moral_action: Dana tells the board that they will have to temporarily lay off a percentage of the staff.
moral_consequence: The board is in agreement that superfluous staff will have to be cut for the time being and can be rehired later.
immoral_action: Dana tells the board that she's ordering the plant to cease the costly waste disposal method and to dump waste in the nearby river.
immoral_consequence: The board is disturbed that Dana is proposing purposefully damaging the environment in order to save money.

norm: You should always pay people back.
situation: Jack's friend loaned him $10,000 in order to help him start a business, and he never gave it back.
intention: Jack doesn't want his friend to hate him.
moral_action: Jack gives his friend $12,000 as gratitude for helping him out.
moral_consequence: Jack's friend feels closer to Jack and ends up with more money.
immoral_action: Jack says that he donated the money to charity as a way of repayment.
immoral_consequence: Jack's friend has financial problems and can't find anyone to help him.

norm: It's annoying when people talk while the movie's playing.
situation: Kevin and Alex are at a movie when Alex taps Kevin's shoulder, wanting to tell him something.
intention: Kevin wants to respond to Alex.
moral_action: Kevin makes a motion as if he's zipping his lips, then points at the movie screen.
moral_consequence: Alex holds his peace until the movie is over.
immoral_action: Kevin asks Alex, who then starts loudly cracking jokes about one of the main characters.
immoral_consequence: The audience around Kevin and Alex tell them to be quiet or else.

norm: It's wrong to shoplift.
situation: Bobby is at the mall when he sees a shirt he thinks he'd look good in.
intention: Bobby wants to take the shirt he saw back home with him.
moral_action: Bobby pays for the shirt with his credit card and takes it home in a bag.
moral_consequence: Bobby enjoys wearing the shirt and thinks it suits him well.
immoral_action: Bobby stuffs the shirt down his pants and walks out of the store casually and goes home.
immoral_consequence: Bobby is caught on camera taking the shirt and later gets in trouble for it.

norm: It's wrong not to say sorry for your wrongdoings.
situation: James is meeting his cousins for the first time since they were kids.
intention: James wants to make a good impression on them.
moral_action: James apologizes for candy he took from them when they were kids.
moral_consequence: They accept the apology and consider that maybe James has indeed changed.
immoral_action: James says hello to them, they mention candy he took decades ago and James denies everything.
immoral_consequence: They like James even less that he continues to not tell the truth.

norm: It's not okay to falsify school grades.
situation: Reese has been struggling in many classes at school and works in the school's office.
intention: Reese wants to get better grades.
moral_action: Reese puts his nose to the grindstone and starts working harder in the classes he is struggling in before it is too late.
moral_consequence: Reese is able to raise his grades to an acceptable level in all the classes he was having trouble with.
immoral_action: Reese uses his office access to adjust his school records to reflect better grades than he earned.
immoral_consequence: Reese is eventually caught by the school and is suspended with his grades changed to failures.

norm: it's wrong to blame your faults on other people.
situation: Tom recently lost his job and worries that his stressed out family will blame him for it.
intention: Tom wants to explain why he lost his job to his family.
moral_action: Tom tells his family that, despite the mental health of his family, his job loss was his own fault.
moral_consequence: Tom feels relieved he had the courage to admit this, and his family thanks him for it.
immoral_action: Tom tells his family that the emotional problems of other family members caused him to lose his job.
immoral_consequence: Tom's family begins to think less of him for not taking responsibility and Tom feels guilty.

norm: It's wrong not to give things back.
situation: Lisa finds a dollar on the ground and sees a little girl looking for it.
intention: Lisa wants to tell the little girl that she found her dollar.
moral_action: Lisa walks up to the little girl, shows her the dollar and gives it back to her.
moral_consequence: The little girl is thrilled and starts to smile and laugh.
immoral_action: Lisa walks up to the little girl, shows her the dollar and tells her that finders are keepers before walking away.
immoral_consequence: The little girl gets upset and starts to cry and whine.

norm: Gambling more than you can afford is unwise.
situation: Jim is going to the casino to have some fun with his friends.
intention: Jim wants to stick some money in the slot machines and see how lucky he can get.
moral_action: Jim puts in a small amount and after it is gone he quits.
moral_consequence: Jim is able to still afford all the rest of his monthly bills.
immoral_action: Jim spends way too much money and loses it all.
immoral_consequence: Jim has trouble paying his rent the next month because he is short cash.

norm: It is wrong to discriminate against people for being different
situation: Ron is annoyed by his new friend, Bruce, who eats strange foods and behaves differently from Ron's other friends.
intention: Ron has to decide whether or not to end his friendship with Bruce.
moral_action: Ron talks to Bruce about what is bothering him, and Bruce explains some of his behaviors in more detail.
moral_consequence: Ron understands Bruce more than before and actually respects some of the behaviors that he previously found strange, and Bruce feels more confident and comfortable in their friendship.
immoral_action: Ron decides that his new friend is just too different from what he is used to, and stops responding to his calls and text messages.
immoral_consequence: Bruce is hurt and confused by Ron's refusal to communicate with him, and Ron feels like he has failed to be a real friend for inconsequential reasons.

norm: It's wrong to sell things that don't belong to you.
situation: Sue and her housemates are having a yard sale together.
intention: Sue wants to make a lot of money.
moral_action: Sue puts out a lot of her unwanted items and works hard to sell them all.
moral_consequence: Sue makes a good amount of extra money for her efforts.
immoral_action: Sue sells a book that belongs to one of her housemates.
immoral_consequence: Sue has to buy a new book at full price to make to satisfy her angry housemate and loses money.

norm: It's bad to re-gift something you have been gifted.
situation: Sara is low on cash and her best friend, Lori, is about to celebrate her birthday.
intention: Sarah wants to get Lori a birthday gift.
moral_action: Sarah uses old t-shirts to sew Lori a new quilt at no cost.
moral_consequence: Lori loves the sentimental gift and cries tears of joy.
immoral_action: Having no money, Sarah digs through old gifts she never used and found one to gift Lori.
immoral_consequence: Turns out, the gift Sarah gave Lori was one that Lori had given Sarah, and she is deeply hurt.

norm: You shouldn't let people steal from you.
situation: Jill runs a store, and she was looking into proper loss prevention measures.
intention: Jill wants to make a decision.
moral_action: Jill signs up with a security system to protect her assets from theft.
moral_consequence: After three arrests, people stop stealing from Jill's store.
immoral_action: Jill decides that the stuff stolen from her is cheaper than what the security bill would be.
immoral_consequence: People continue right on stealing form Jill's store with impunity.

norm: You shouldn't talk while others are enjoying a movie.
situation: Britney is watching a movie with her friends in a movie theater.
intention: Britney wants her friends to know how she feels about what is happening in the movie.
moral_action: Britney communicates with her friends by making silent faces, i.e. sad, amused etc. during the movie.
moral_consequence: The people seated near Britney can enjoy the movie in peace.
immoral_action: Britney talks to her friends during the movie to let them know how she feels about it.
immoral_consequence: People seating near Britney can't hear the movie because of her incessant talking.

norm: It's rude to show up late for an engagement.
situation: John has an important work meeting at 10:00 am.
intention: John wants to attend the meeting.
moral_action: John shows up a few minutes early for the work meeting.
moral_consequence: John is praised for his preparedness and contributions in the meeting.
immoral_action: John arrives 20 minutes late for the meeting.
immoral_consequence: John is reprimanded for being tardy.

norm: It's wrong to not be honest with a roommate.
situation: Mack ate his roommate's sandwich in the fridge and is now confronted by his roommate about it.
intention: Mack wants to respond to his roommate.
moral_action: Mack admits he ate his roommate's sandwich and offers to replace his sandwich.
moral_consequence: Mack's roommate forgives Mack, and they enjoy a nice meal together.
immoral_action: Mack repeatedly denies eating his roommate's sandwich and even states he never saw a sandwich in the fridge.
immoral_consequence: This incident created a rift between Mack and his roommate that was never resolved.

norm: It is wrong to waste your employer's money.
situation: Alex is granted a large amount of autonomy at his job and the company will cover fairly high food costs while traveling.
intention: Alex wants to eat a quality meal and have some drinks to recharge.
moral_action: Alex spends a reasonable amount on food and drinks at a casual restaurant.
moral_consequence: Alex is granted continued free reign on food expenses he reports.
immoral_action: Alex orders very expensive food and drink at an expensive restaurant.
immoral_consequence: Alex's boss notices the high charge and limits his priveleges.

norm: You are expected to tip your server in most situations.
situation: Sam has just received the bill at a restaurant and is surprised by how much it has added up to.
intention: Sam wants to spend less money.
moral_action: Sam leaves a very modest tip.
moral_consequence: The server is happy to get something.
immoral_action: Sam completely stiffs the server on the tip in order to save money.
immoral_consequence: The server is upset at the lack of tip.

norm: It's not okay to judge someone on their height.
situation: John is at work when his boss Tony comes and introduces their new coworker Peg who is under five feet tall.
intention: John wants to say something to Peg.
moral_action: John tells Peg that she's welcome to the team and that he's glad to see a new face around.
moral_consequence: Peg feels welcomed and loves the warm receptions she is receiving in a nerve-wracking moment.
immoral_action: John tells Peg that she is so tiny and that he can't believe it, that she must have an impossible time driving a car.
immoral_consequence: Peg is annoyed that john is assuming things about her and basing his judgement simply by her appearance.

norm: It is irresponsible to neglect paying your fees.
situation: Steve had been parking his car illegally and found a ticket on his car.
intention: Steve wants to keep his car from getting towed.
moral_action: Steve went to the city hall and paid off the fine for the ticket that he got.
moral_consequence: Steve got the ticket and fine off of his driving record.
immoral_action: Steve left the parking ticket on the window so the meter maids would think he had already been ticketed that day.
immoral_consequence: Eventually, Steve was cited for not paying the original ticket on the car.

norm: It's wrong to be loud on the phone around others.
situation: Paul is watching movies with his friends when he receives a phone call from Richard.
intention: Paul needs to contact the Richard.
moral_action: Paul invites the caller into a small online chatroom to discuss things with Richard.
moral_consequence: In one chat session Paul and Richard are able to map out their schedule for the next two weeks.
immoral_action: Paul answers the phone talking in a raised voice to Richard.
immoral_consequence: Paul gets scolded by his friends for interrupting the movie.

norm: It's wrong to be jealous of other people.
situation: Phil was jealous that one of the jocks was trying to take his girlfriend.
intention: Phil wants to decide what to do about his jealously.
moral_action: Phil ignores these feelings and continues on with his life.
moral_consequence: Phil still has his girlfriend a few years later when the jocks graduate.
immoral_action: Phil acts rudely to all the jocks whenever they are around.
immoral_consequence: Phil's girlfriend dumps him for his rude behavior.

norm: People are expected to be generous on Halloween.
situation: Thelma likes to see the kids' costumes on Halloweeen and is giving out candy, so they'll visit.
intention: Thelma wants to keep some of the Halloween candy.
moral_action: Thelma buys an extra bag of candy, so she has enough for the kids and herself.
moral_consequence: The kids love her candy and visit her again next year.
immoral_action: Thelma only allows each kid to have one tiny piece of candy.
immoral_consequence: Thelma gets a reputation for stinginess, and the kids don't visit her next Halloween.

norm: You shouldn't let your kids damage store merchandise.
situation: Kayla is at the store with her young son who is hyperactive and destructive.
intention: Kayla wants to distract her son with something.
moral_action: Kayla gives her son a stress ball to play with at the store.
moral_consequence: Kayla's son interacts with the stress ball and people at the store think he's cute.
immoral_action: Kayla gives her son a store product to keep him busy.
immoral_consequence: Kayla's son ends up causing damage to the merchandise causing onlookers to think badly of him.

norm: Cheating is wrong.
situation: Dale finds an answer sheet for an upcoming test on his math teacher's desk.
intention: Dale wants to figure out what to do with the answer sheet.
moral_action: Dale leaves the answer sheet and studies for the test.
moral_consequence: Dale will learn how to properly do the math.
immoral_action: Dale takes the answer sheet and copies down the answers.
immoral_consequence: Dale never learns how to correctly due the math.

norm: It is bad to lie on things.
situation: Jake is at a sleepover trying to sleep and misplaced his phone, but it is dark, and he can't seem to find the phone.
intention: Jake wants to get rest tonight.
moral_action: Jake crawls on his hands and knees searching the floor in the dark until he finally locates his phone, then goes back to sleep.
moral_consequence: Jake gets his phone and keeps it safe, avoiding the loss of several hundred dollars and his pictures that are stored there.
immoral_action: Jake searches for a minute or two, then gives up, rolls over, and goes to sleep.
immoral_consequence: Jake rolls onto his phone, eventually breaking it from the pressure of his body during the night.

norm: You shouldn't expect others to cover for you at work.
situation: Jon is currently at work and is about to go on his lunch break.
intention: Jon wants to take an extended lunch to take care of a personal matter.
moral_action: Jon doesn't mention anything to his coworker, so he can say he doesn't know.
moral_consequence: Jon's friend is able to finish his work shift comfortably.
immoral_action: Jon instructs his friend and coworker on a story to tell his boss if asked.
immoral_consequence: Jon's friend feels pressured into having to lie to the boss.

norm: It is wrong to ruin someone else's party.
situation: Hank is working on a major project at work and needs help, while Steve is celebrating his birthday.
intention: Hank wants to get a subordinate to help him.
moral_action: Hank finds someone who is bored and orders him to come in.
moral_consequence: Steve enjoys his birthday party.
immoral_action: Hank contacts Steve and orders him to come in or else be fired.
immoral_consequence: Steve is angry with Hank for ruining his birthday.

norm: It's wrong to lie about your feelings to others.
situation: Jake is at home feeling stressed out and depressed after a very bad week when his mother calls him.
intention: Jake wants to find a new job.
moral_action: Jake tells his mom that he is feeling depressed, and he thinks a new job would help him.
moral_consequence: Jake starts seeing a counselor with the help of his mom, and this ends up leading him to a new and fulfilling career.
immoral_action: Jake tells his mom that he is feeling great and asks for job search advice.
immoral_consequence: Jake gets advice from his mom on how to find a new job, but he's too sad to act on it.

norm: You shouldn't fake your own death.
situation: Mary is in an online relationship with Tim, and is growing bored with it.
intention: Mary wants to break up with Tim.
moral_action: Mary is honest with Tim that the relationship isn't working out and breaks up with him.
moral_consequence: Tim is sad, but moves on and finds a new girlfriend.
immoral_action: Mary pretends she has cancer and makes Tim think she has died.
immoral_consequence: Tim is very distraught, and spends months in a depression.

norm: It is good to clean up spilled syrup.
situation: Jillian accidentally drops a bottle of maple syrup on the ground and it breaks.
intention: Jillian doesn't want the floor to be sticky.
moral_action: Jillians wipes up the spilled syrup.
moral_consequence: The floor is clean and no extra work is needed.
immoral_action: Jillian picks up the kitchen mat, puts it over the puddle of syrup, and leaves it there.
immoral_consequence: The kitchen mat absorbs some syrup and becomes even harder to clean up later.

norm: It's unacceptable to steal.
situation: James was mad that his parents wouldn't give him money to buy candy.
intention: James wants to eat candy.
moral_action: James asked the shopkeeper if there were any chores he could do in exchange for store credit.
moral_consequence: The shopkeeper let James start making deliveries in exchange for a small wage, allowing James to buy candy.
immoral_action: When the shopkeeper isn't looking, James sneaks the candy bar into his pocket.
immoral_consequence: James enjoyed his candy bar but felt guilty every time he went back to that store.

norm: It is greedy to horde all the cookies for yourself.
situation: Charlie and his friends often get treats after playtime at his day care.
intention: Charlie wants to enjoy some cookies.
moral_action: Charlie takes two cookies after playing and eats them slowly.
moral_consequence: Charlie enjoys the snack which makes the rest of the day easier for him.
immoral_action: Charlie stops playing a little early sneaks away and eats all of the cookies.
immoral_consequence: Charlie gets sick from eating too much and is not allowed to have cookies for a week as a punishement.

norm: People should give content a chance before deciding it's inferior.
situation: Hank is surfing on a video website when he sees an excellent video with very few reviews.
intention: Hank wants to decide whether to view the video or not.
moral_action: Hank decides to watch the video and promotes it once he realizes that it's good.
moral_consequence: The video gets more views and goes viral, helping many people who watch it.
immoral_action: Hank decides that the video doesn't have reviews because it's not as good as it seems and ignores it.
immoral_consequence: The video doesn't get the views that it deserves and people are not benefited by it.

norm: It is wrong to steal school property.
situation: Mike had a report to work on, but he didn't have a computer at home.
intention: Mike wants to use the school computer.
moral_action: Mike asks his teacher if he can work in the computer lab during lunch.
moral_consequence: Mike is able to finish his report on the computer in the lab.
immoral_action: Mike takes a laptop from school without permission from the staff.
immoral_consequence: Mike gets suspended for the removal of school property from the campus.

norm: It's wrong to lie to someone about their heritage.
situation: Jane has an adopted son named Jonas who doesn't know he's adopted who asks if she's his real mother.
intention: Jane needs to decide how to respond.
moral_action: Jane tells Jonas that he was adopted at a young age.
moral_consequence: Jonas appreciates the honesty and continues to love Jane dearly.
immoral_action: Jane tells Jonas that of course she's his real mother.
immoral_consequence: Jonas later finds out the truth and never forgives Jane for lying.

norm: You should never exclude someone for their religion.
situation: Richard has recently opened a new retail business in his neighborhood.
intention: Richard wants to hire some employees.
moral_action: Richard puts out a help wanted ad in order to find qualified candidates and hires people from a variety of religious backgrounds.
moral_consequence: Richard's business is successful due to the quality and diversity of his staff.
immoral_action: Richard puts out a help wanted ad that makes it clear that Muslims are not invited to apply.
immoral_consequence: Richard is seen as anti-Islamic and loses a lot of business because of his discrimination.

norm: It's good to be eager about controlling your life.
situation: Billy has just graduated high school and is asked about what he will do.
intention: Billy wants to talk about what he will do after high school.
moral_action: Billy says he wants to be an attorney and will be attending college.
moral_consequence: Billy will be able to achieve the goals he has.
immoral_action: Billy says that he will go with the flow and doesn't have plans.
immoral_consequence: Billy will not be able to have control over his life and will be directionless.

norm: It's improper to profit off of desperate people.
situation: Jane is a requester on Mechnical Turk.
intention: Jane needs to get some creative writing submissions from turkers.
moral_action: Jane posts creative writing HITs for a 1.00 payout.
moral_consequence: Jane's hits make a large contribution to the financial wellbeing of both the workers and the platform itself.
immoral_action: Jane posts creative writing HITs for .10 payouts.
immoral_consequence: Jane's HITs are completed by poverty-striken workers, which normalizes underpaid creative writing.

norm: You just can't expect someone to like everything you like.
situation: Zara is on a lake trip with her best friend Layla, and they plan on renting a boat.
intention: Zara wants to play some music while they hang out on the boat.
moral_action: Zara plays her music over the speakers but switches to let Layla play her music every other song.
moral_consequence: Both Layla and Zara sing their hearts out while enjoying a fun day on the lake.
immoral_action: Zara takes over the stereo system on the boat and never asks Layla what she wants to listen to.
immoral_consequence: Layla is so disinterested in the music that Zara plays, she falls asleep on the boat and has an overall boring time on the lake.

norm: It's bad to lie about using condoms.
situation: Dan is about to have sex with Jen, but she wants him to wear a condom before doing it.
intention: Dan wants to have sex.
moral_action: Dan puts on the condom, because he thinks it's the responsible thing to do.
moral_consequence: Jen is put at ease as they have sex, and she doesn't have to worry about anything.
immoral_action: Dan pretends to put a condom on and then penetrates Jen.
immoral_consequence: Dan is putting Jen at risk of becoming pregnant when she doesn't want to be.

norm: You shouldn't expect help or services for free.
situation: Jess is planning a celebration for her 10th anniversary, so she approaches her event coordinator friend.
intention: Jess wants her friend to help plan her party.
moral_action: Jess pffoers her friend a fair rate if she will take on the party planning job.
moral_consequence: Jess gets to work with an event planner that is familiar with her preferences.
immoral_action: Jess asks her friend if she can use her event planning business for free.
immoral_consequence: Jess puts her friend in the awkward position of having to disappoint a friend or squander her resources.

norm: It's rude not to tip your server at a restaurant.
situation: Sebastian just finished eating breakfast at a local diner and is getting ready to leave.
intention: Sebastian wants to say thank you to the waiter.
moral_action: After paying for his meal, Sebastian slips the server 15 dollars and says he is grateful for the service.
moral_consequence: The server is pleased because this has been his best tip today.
immoral_action: Sebastian pays for the meal only, and tells the waiter that he appreciates the service.
immoral_consequence: The server is disappointed, because simply being appreciated isn't going to pay the bills.

norm: You can't be expected to pay everyone's way.
situation: Cherry doesn't have a lot of money, and her friends are pressuring her into treating them to lunch.
intention: Cherry wants to make everyone happy.
moral_action: Cherry offers everyone to drop by her house for a nice home cooked meal.
moral_consequence: Cherry and her friends become closer over eating in a quiet environment.
immoral_action: Cherry takes her friends to an expensive buffet, and she pays for the entire party with her debit card.
immoral_consequence: Cherry is unable to pay for her bills.

norm: It's wrong to take advantage of your grandparents.
situation: Larry's grandmother, who can be forgetful, gave him some money, but forgot and offered to give him some more money.
intention: Larry wants to decide whether to take the money or not.
moral_action: Larry reminds his grandmother that she already gave him money and thanks her again.
moral_consequence: Larry's grandmother is very thankful that he reminded her and decided to give him the extra money anyway.
immoral_action: Larry takes the second bit of money from his grandmother and thanks her for it without mentioning the first payment.
immoral_consequence: Larry's grandmother realizes she paid him twice when she checks her bank account and angrily demands the money back.

norm: It is immature to take back a gift.
situation: Mike's birthday present to his brother is a trip to a football game together.
intention: Mike wants to have fun at the football game.
moral_action: Mike buys his brother's favorite food and drinks for the tailgate party before the game.
moral_consequence: Mike's brother has a great time at the game and can't wait to hang out with Mike again.
immoral_action: Mike meets a girl at the bar that night and decides to give her the ticket to the football game instead of his brother.
immoral_consequence: Mike's brother is heartbroken and has the worst birthday ever.

norm: It is wrong to commit voter fraud.
situation: Tyrell is at the local polling place for the election.
intention: Tyrell wants his candidate to win.
moral_action: Tyrell hands out pamphlets at the polling place to explain his candidate's platform.
moral_consequence: Tyrell convinces several people to vote for his favored candidate.
immoral_action: Tyrell casts his vote and then goes to anther polling place and votes again.
immoral_consequence: Tyrell is arrested for voter fraud.

norm: You shouldn't blame others unless you have proof.
situation: Bob is an accountant for a marketing firm and notices a discrepancy that indicates possible fraud.
intention: Bob wants the discrepancy investigated.
moral_action: Bob points out the accounting discrepancy to his boss.
moral_consequence: Bob's boss turns the matter over to an independent auditor who finds the discrepancy was merely a data entry error.
immoral_action: Bob points out the discrepancy to his boss and says he suspects the regional manager.
immoral_consequence: The regional manager is investigated, and although he is found to be innocent, his chances for advancement in the company are ruined.

norm: It's polite to cancel an appointment that you can't make.
situation: Frank is due to see his doctor this afternoon, but he feels better now.
intention: Frank wants to decide what to do.
moral_action: Frank calls up to inform the doctors office he won't be coming in.
moral_consequence: The doctor is able to fit someone in Franks place, so they can get medical attention.
immoral_action: Frank decides not to see his doctor but doesn't call to inform the office.
immoral_consequence: The doctor wastes time that could have been spent helping someone who needed help.

norm: You shouldn't take someone else's cigarettes.
situation: Tom is a social smoker and is out at a bar with friends on a weekend night.
intention: Tom wants to smoke a cigarette.
moral_action: Tom buys a pack of cigarettes from a vending machine inside the bar.
moral_consequence: Tom enjoys having a relaxing smoke on the bar's back patio.
immoral_action: Without asking, Tom takes a cigarette from the pack of a stranger who left it on their table.
immoral_consequence: Tom is yelled at by the stranger for stealing a cigarette, and he feels bad about it.

norm: Is wrong to expect your guests pay for your wedding
situation: Lily was planning a large wedding, and she realized the total bill was going to be pretty high.
intention: Lily wants to find a way to help pay the bill.
moral_action: Lily decides to get a part time job waiting tables for a few months to help with costs.
moral_consequence: Lily has a great wedding and is able to pay it off after a few months.
immoral_action: Lily decides to charge all of her guests a $50 door charge to attend her wedding.
immoral_consequence: Lily is disappointed when the majority of her guests cancel and do not attend.

norm: It's wrong to sleep on someone else's dime.
situation: Glenn did not sleep well the night before and is feeling very tired during his shift.
intention: Glenn wants to stop feeling so tired at work.
moral_action: Glenn makes himself a few cups of strong coffee to get the caffeine kick.
moral_consequence: Glenn perks up a little thanks to the caffeine, and he is able to make it through the rest of his shift before going home to sleep.
immoral_action: Glenn goes to the supply closet when no one is looking to take a quick nap.
immoral_consequence: Glenn gets caught by his manager and is written up for sleeping at work.

norm: Both partners should put forth equal effort in a relationship.
situation: Grant is upset with the amount of chores that his wife assigns him.
intention: Grant wants to explain how he feels.
moral_action: Grant explains that since he works and his wife doesn't, that she should be responsible for more of the chores.
moral_consequence: Grant and his wife come to a compromise about how many chores that they each preform.
immoral_action: Grant complains that the household chores are 'the wife's job' and he shouldn't have to do them.
immoral_consequence: Grant argues with his wife, and he ends up sleeping on the couch.

norm: You shouldn't fake your wealth.
situation: Joey, who is an impoverished artist, has met a girl who he is quite excited about.
intention: Joey wants to impress the new girl.
moral_action: Joey decides to impress her instead with his artistic talents.
moral_consequence: The girl is impressed by Joey and wants to spend more time with him.
immoral_action: Joey exaggerates his income in an effort to impress the girl.
immoral_consequence: The girl finds out and is put off because he lied to her.

norm: You shouldn't rummage through other's belongings.
situation: Han was over at his friend Luke's house waiting for Luke to finish using the restroom.
intention: Han wants to do something while waiting for Luke.
moral_action: Han looks at the posters on the wall that Luke has up.
moral_consequence: Han is curious where he got some of these cool posters.
immoral_action: Han looks through Luke's things and finds a wad of $1000 bills.
immoral_consequence: Han is freaked out and wonders in his head the rest of the time where it came from.

norm: It's wrong to do all the work in a relationship.
situation: Pam and Oscar live together in an apartment.
intention: Oscar wants the apartment to be maintained.
moral_action: Oscar and Pam divide up the daily tasks that they need to do.
moral_consequence: Oscar and Pam feel like they both contribute and are both happy.
immoral_action: Oscar does all of the household chores while Pam does nothing.
immoral_consequence: Oscar gets upset with Pam at the unfair arrangement.

norm: It's rude to hold people up because you are reading.
situation: Jim is reading a book when he is called to go out for dinner by his mom.
intention: Jim wants to respond to his mom.
moral_action: Jim tells his mom he's ready to go and puts his book down.
moral_consequence: Jim's mom will get to eat dinner on time.
immoral_action: Jim says he needs to finish reading to his mom.
immoral_consequence: Jim's mom will be annoyed with him for delaying dinner.

norm: It's wrong to take advantage of one's partner.
situation: Alex is sleeping in his bed with his wife Jen, and he is sexually aroused.
intention: Alex wants to get rid of his sexual arousal.
moral_action: Alex asks Jen to have sex and decides to just masturbate after she says now.
moral_consequence: Jen trusts Alex to not force her to have sex when she doesn't want to.
immoral_action: Alex has sex with his wife while she is sleeping and without her consent.
immoral_consequence: Jen feels violated and doesn't feel safe sleeping in the same bed with Alex anymore.

norm: It's good to test things out before you use them.
situation: Tim gets a new remote-control boat from the hobby store that is much nicer, and more complicated, than anything he has ever used before.
intention: Tim wants to try out his new boat.
moral_action: Tim takes his boat home to get a feel for it, in his bathtub, before he takes it out into the river.
moral_consequence: He gets a feel for how the boat operates, and he learns about its power at home, and when he takes it out he has a great time.
immoral_action: Tim takes his boat straight to the river and puts it in the water to try it out.
immoral_consequence: The boat stops working when it is too far from shore to retrieve and Tim realizes it was not fully charged, so he loses his boat.

norm: It's wrong to take something that belongs to someone else.
situation: Stacey always brings lunch to work, but today she forgot her lunch.
intention: Stacey wants to wants to get something to eat.
moral_action: Stacey decides to go to the cafeteria to see if she can get something for lunch.
moral_consequence: Stacey enjoys her lunch and thinks that she will eat in the cafeteria more often.
immoral_action: Stacey looks in the fridge in the break room and takes a coworker's sandwich to eat.
immoral_consequence: Another coworker sees Stacey take the sandwich and tells her she shouldn't take someone else's lunch.

norm: It is wrong to steal somebody else's chocolate.
situation: Martha is visiting her friend, Edna, in the hospital and notices that she has been given a box of fancy chocolates.
intention: Martha wants some chocolate.
moral_action: Martha asks Edna if she can try one of her fancy chocolates.
moral_consequence: Edna tells her to help herself, and they enjoy the chocolate together.
immoral_action: When the nurse comes to check on Edna, Martha quickly takes a handful of the chocolates without asking.
immoral_consequence: The nurse sees Martha take the chocolates and tells her it's not very nice to just take a patient's gift like that.

norm: It's rude to invite yourself to someone's house for dinner.
situation: Mark starts working closely with his new co-worker Amy.
intention: Mark wants to become friends with Amy.
moral_action: Mark asks Amy if she wants to join him at his house for dinner tomorrow.
moral_consequence: Mark and Amy have dinner at his house and have a good time.
immoral_action: Mark asks Amy if she will cook him dinner at her house tomorrow.
immoral_consequence: Mark makes Amy feel uncomfortable, and she spends less time with him.

norm: It's morally unacceptable to manipulate another person.
situation: Alan's girlfriend Jessica has a best friend, Kary, who Alan finds very annoying.
intention: Alan wants to deal with Kary's annoying tendencies.
moral_action: Alan tries to limit his time with Kary and encourages Jessica and Kary to hang out together without him.
moral_consequence: Jessica and Kary continue to be best friends, and Alan doesn't have to deal with Kary so much.
immoral_action: Alan convinces Jessica that Kary doesn’t value her as a friend and is only using her to get free food and drinks, for which Alan has no evidence.
immoral_consequence: Jessica cuts Kary out of her life and stops trusting anyone but Alan.

norm: It is wrong to be anti-semetic.
situation: Ray lives in a multicultural town, needs to do repairs to his home, and wants to hire a professional.
intention: Ray wants to choose a contractor from those available.
moral_action: Ray hires the best contractor in town who happens to be Jewish.
moral_consequence: Ray gets the best work done on his home.
immoral_action: Ray learns the best contractor in town is Jewish and chooses a lesser skilled contractor.
immoral_consequence: Ray does not get the best work done on his home.

norm: It's wrong to ruin something someone else enjoys for them.
situation: Frasier just finished watching the new hit movie and is talking to a friend who hasn't seen it but wants to.
intention: Frasier wants to talk about the movie.
moral_action: Fraiser expresses to his friend how much he enjoyed the movie but avoids spoiling the plot so his friend can see it for themself.
moral_consequence: Fraiser's friend becomes even more excited to see the movie.
immoral_action: Fraiser spoils all the twists and turns of the movie to his friend.
immoral_consequence: Fraiser's friend is upset by the spoilers because they enjoy watching movies without them.

norm: It is rude not to share.
situation: It's lunch time at school and Bobby is eating lunch next to his friend Dave, who forgot to bring his lunch from home.
intention: Bobby wants to react to Dave's dilemma.
moral_action: Bobby rips half of his sandwich off and hands it to Dave, so he has at least something to eat.
moral_consequence: Dave appreciates Bobby being kind and sharing with him, and is glad to have something to eat.
immoral_action: Bobby tells Dave that in the future he should keep some snacks in his locker in case of situations like this.
immoral_consequence: Dave is still hungry and jealously watches Bobby eat his lunch.

norm: You shouldn't block people on your partner's phone.
situation: Chris' girlfriend, Meg, often messages her male coworkers and it makes him jealous.
intention: Chris wants to stop feeling jealous about Meg talking to her coworkers.
moral_action: Chris explains to Meg how he's been feeling.
moral_consequence: Meg assures Chris that her communication with coworkers is strictly professional.
immoral_action: Chris blocks all of the male contacts in Meg's phone.
immoral_consequence: Meg misses an important work update that was sent to her by a male coworker, causing her to fall behind on her work.

norm: You shouldn't expect people to always be on time.
