situation: Rick invited some friends to eat out with him and is waiting for them at the restaurant.
intention: Rick wants to order food for when they arrive.
moral_action: Rick orders some appetizers and saves the full meal for when his friends get there.
moral_consequence: Rick's friends are late and order what they want when they arrive.
immoral_action: Rick orders the full meal before his friends arrive so it will be ready once they get there.
immoral_consequence: Rick's friends are an hour late and have to eat cold food.

norm: Using fake pictures on a dating site is misleading.
situation: Marco is setting up a listing on a dating site and is deciding what pictures to use.
intention: Marco wants to get someone to reply to his listing.
moral_action: Marco puts up current pictures that show what he looks like today.
moral_consequence: The person that replies to his ad likes him for what he looks like now, and they hit it off.
immoral_action: Marco puts up pictures of a male model that looks nothing like him.
immoral_consequence: When someone does reply to his ad, they find out he looks nothing like his pictures and stop seeing him.

norm: You shouldn't give handouts to people who don't deserve it.
situation: Cara's brother plays video games all day and asked her for some money to help him buy a new TV.
intention: Cara has to decide whether to give her brother money.
moral_action: Cara tells his brother to get a job in order to buy a new TV.
moral_consequence: Cara's brother has to feel motivated to work if he really wants a new TV.
immoral_action: Cara gives his brother the money that he wanted, so he can buy a TV.
immoral_consequence: Cara's brother, and he continues to be lazy and doesn't pay her sister back.

norm: It's bad to steal things that don't belong to you.
situation: Karen is in her mom's room looking for something when she notices a beautiful necklace that belongs to her mom.
intention: Karen wants to obtain the necklace.
moral_action: Karen asks her mom for the necklace as a birthday gift to her.
moral_consequence: Karen's mom is happy to pass down the necklace to her and is glad she likes it.
immoral_action: Karen takes the necklace from her mom's room and keeps it.
immoral_consequence: Karen's mom wastes time looking everywhere for the necklace and is disappointed to find out she took it.

norm: It's impolite to use your friend's property without asking.
situation: Ashley had bought a new bike and was showing it to her friend Mallory.
intention: Mallory wants to use Ashley's bike.
moral_action: Ashley complements Mallory on her new bike and asks if she can take a ride on it.
moral_consequence: Mallory says yes and Ashley rides the bike down the road and back.
immoral_action: After Ashley goes back into her house, Mallory goes into the garage and takes the bike for a ride.
immoral_consequence: Ashley is stopped by the police after Mallory calls them to report the missing bike.

norm: It's not okay to put someone one the spot.
situation: Randy is having lunch with coworkers and witnesses one of them lying to another.
intention: Randy wants to find out why his coworker lied to the the other one.
moral_action: Randy waits to ask his coworker about it later in private.
moral_consequence: Randy's coworkers are none the wiser, and they have an enjoyable lunch.
immoral_action: Randy calls out his coworker in front of all the others.
immoral_consequence: Randy's coworker is extremely embarrassed and his coworkers lose trust in him.

norm: It's wrong to break a pay it forward chain.
situation: Katy is at the supermarket and a stranger offered to pay for her groceries saying a stranger had done something nice for him earlier.
intention: Katy wants to get her free groceries home.
moral_action: Katy thanks the stranger for the food and pays for the next strangers's food who was behind her in line before heading home.
moral_consequence: Katy feels good for passing along the gesture towards somebody else.
immoral_action: Katy drives home and tells her brother about the stranger, and they both agree it is quite odd to do that sort of thing.
immoral_consequence: Katy never gets to feel what it is like to help a stranger out and stops a pattern that had been going on.

norm: You shouldn't listen to other people's conversations.
situation: Charlene is in the pantry and her coworkers Nancy and Grace are there chatting about their family affairs.
intention: Charlene wants to react to her situation.
moral_action: Charlene joins the chatting and shifts the topic by inviting both Nancy and Grace to lunch.
moral_consequence: Nancy and Grace thank Charlene for her invitation and agree to go to lunch with her.
immoral_action: Charlene stays in the pantry as if she is still making her coffee in order to hear more about the family affairs of both Nancy and Grace.
immoral_consequence: Nancy and Grace feel uneasy and stop their chatting.

norm: You shouldn't cut in line.
situation: Kelly arrives at the DMV and sees that there's a long winding line to be waited on.
intention: Kelly wants to register her car and get on with her day.
moral_action: Kelly gets in line and listens to music on her earbuds; the line moves faster than she'd anticpated.
moral_consequence: Kelly gets her car license, and has time to hear a couple of good songs.
immoral_action: Kelly cuts to the front of the line claiming she's got people waiting for her in the car.
immoral_consequence: Kelly is told to go to the end of the line and has to stay longer at the DMV than if she'd just gotten in line in the first place.

norm: You shouldn't mislead people.
situation: Chris is an investment professional and is trying to get some clients to invest in a high risk fund.
intention: Chris wants to make a recommendation to his clients.
moral_action: Chris divulges the full risk and reward profile of the fund in his recommendation.
moral_consequence: Chris' clients tells Chris that the fund has too much risk for them but becomes loyal to Chris due to his integrity.
immoral_action: Chris hides the risk of the fund and gets his clients to invest in a high risk fund.
immoral_consequence: Chris' clients decides to file a lawsuit against Chris after the high risk fund crashes.

norm: It's bad to scam someone.
situation: James was faced with $20,000 of medical debt after surviving a car crash.
intention: James wants to get rid of all his debt.
moral_action: James declares bankruptcy and asks the court for mercy given his bad luck.
moral_consequence: James' medical debt is discharged in bankruptcy, allowing him to start over.
immoral_action: James starts calling people and telling them they can pay him a $200 processing fee to receive their next stimulus check early.
immoral_consequence: James earns the money and isn't caught because he uses proxies to hide where he's calling from.

norm: It is immoral to take things without paying
situation: Heather is at the mall when she finds a necklace she really wants but can't afford.
intention: Heather wants to get the necklace.
moral_action: Heather picks up odd jobs walking dogs for her neighbors to save money to buy the necklace.
moral_consequence: Heather develops a good work ethic and is able to buy the necklace.
immoral_action: Heather puts the necklace in her bag and walks out of the store without paying for it.
immoral_consequence: Heather gets caught shoplifting and her picture is put up on a wall of shame.

norm: It's wrong to be a compulsive liar.
situation: Adam is with friends, and they are all sharing wild stories from their pasts.
intention: Adam wants to impress with his stories.
moral_action: Adam thinks of some times in his life that odd things have happened and shares those stories with his friends.
moral_consequence: Adam and his friends all have a good laugh looking back on their stories.
immoral_action: Adam pulls many a story out of thin air that are clearly to good to be true.
immoral_consequence: Adam's friends don't appreciate that he cannot be truthful with them and his constant lies eventually crumble atop one another which is quite embarrassing for him.

norm: It is rude to take something away from someone.
situation: Ash sees that Frank has a much nicer TV remote even though they have the same TV.
intention: Ash wants the better TV remote.
moral_action: Ash goes to an electronics store and buys the same remote Frank has.
moral_consequence: Ash enjoys the new remote for his TV and has a fun time using it and talks to Frank about it.
immoral_action: Ash takes Frank's TV remote without premission.
immoral_consequence: Frank assumes he lost the remote and just buys a new one.

norm: It's rude to hack people.
situation: Dan works for an anti-extremism organization that is tracking an extremist site.
intention: Dan wants to shut down the site.
moral_action: Dan decides to report the extremist site to the domain registrar and the site gets taken down.
moral_consequence: Dan gets a raise for his work and feels proud.
immoral_action: Dan decides to take down the site through illegal denial of service attacks.
immoral_consequence: Dan get charged with criminal hacking and goes to jail and feels bad.

norm: You shouldn't give expensive items to someone you don't know.
situation: Will is going on a long vacation and has jewelry he is not taking with him.
intention: Will wants his jewelry someone to watch over his jewelry while he is away.
moral_action: Will gives the jewelry to his brother who he has known all his life.
moral_consequence: Will feels that his jewelry is taken care of and enjoys his time away.
immoral_action: Will gives the jewelry to a neighbor he has only met once.
immoral_consequence: Will becomes anxious about the jewelry and cannot enjoy his vacation.

norm: It's wrong to cheat on an exam.
situation: Roman is sitting in class taking a big final exam in his math class.
intention: Roman wants to score well on the exam.
moral_action: Roman blocks out everything and concentrates extra hard to remember everything he studied for the test.
moral_consequence: Roman get a really good score on his exam when he gets it back from the teacher.
immoral_action: Roman uses his smartwatch to pull up answers to some of the test questions.
immoral_consequence: Roman gets busted cheating by the teacher and fails the exam.

norm: It's expected you return things to the lost and found
situation: Dan is helping close the store for the night when he sees a customers bag laying on the floor.
intention: Dan wants the bag.
moral_action: After dropping off the bag at the lost items station, Dan buys the same bag from the maker's website.
moral_consequence: Dan receives a mail in rebate for ordering online.
immoral_action: Dan takes the bag home with him.
immoral_consequence: Dan gets fired when people discover his actions.

norm: It's admirable when you're able to admit your faults.
situation: Jenny asks her friend Mario to help her fix her car, but she doesn't realize Mario knows nothing about being a mechanic.
intention: Mario wants to impress Jenny.
moral_action: Mario tells Jenny he would love to but knows nothing about fixing cars.
moral_consequence: Jenny appreciates Mario's honesty, and  they become close friends.
immoral_action: Mario hires a mechanic to fix Jenny's car and lets Jenny think it was him.
immoral_consequence: Mario feels bad for lying and now always feels awkward with Jenny.

norm: You shouldn't ask for refunds on works of art.
situation: Tracy visits an art exhibit featuring paintings by a friend of hers, and she buys a painting, that she can’t really afford, on a whim.
intention: Tracy has to decide what to do with the painting.
moral_action: Tracy keeps the painting and uses it to dress up her living area at home.
moral_consequence: Tracy's living room looks more sophisticated with the painting on the wall and every time her friend visits, she thanks her again for her support.
immoral_action: Tracy calls her friend and tells her that she didn't really think about it before purchasing the painting, and she would like her money back.
immoral_consequence: Her friend is taken aback by the request and feels a little hurt, since she had already accounted for the sale as part of the profit from her exhibit.

norm: It's bad to mislead customers at work.
situation: John is working at a sports equipment store when a customer walks in asking about fishing rods.
intention: John wants to respond to the customer.
moral_action: John accurately describes the fishing rods the store has to offer to the customer.
moral_consequence: The customer happily purchases a fishing rod and does more business with the store later on.
immoral_action: John sells a low quality fishing rod to the customer while pretending it was a top notch professional fishing rod.
immoral_consequence: The customer returns the next day and angrily demands a refund for the fishing rod.

norm: It's wrong to talk badly about someone's wedding.
situation: Bob is attending a friend's wedding and is chatting about it with the other attendees.
intention: Bob wants to comment on the wedding.
moral_action: Bob highlights all the good parts of the wedding and does not say anything about the less positive parts.
moral_consequence: Bob enjoys a decent chat with the other attendees and is thanked for his attendance at the wedding.
immoral_action: Bob mentions how bad the food is and severely critiques several other aspects of the wedding.
immoral_consequence: Bob annoys other people attending the wedding and is asked to leave the wedding.

norm: It's wrong to judge people on their entertainment choices.
situation: Lucas is talking with his blind date Bradley when he notices Naruto headband in his friend's backpack.
intention: Lucas wants to continue talking to Bradley.
moral_action: Lucas asks his date questions about his work and interests.
moral_consequence: Lucas learns more about various subcultures.
immoral_action: Lucas makes a comment about his boyfriend being an extreme otaku.
immoral_consequence: Lucas is left alone for the rest of the evening when his date excuses himself.

norm: It's bad to help someone cheat on their schoolwork.
situation: Kelly is a student in high school that gets good grades.
intention: Kelly wants to help another student get better grades.
moral_action: Kelly tutors the student after school.
moral_consequence: The student learns studying techniques to get better grades.
immoral_action: Kelly allows the student to copy off them.
immoral_consequence: The student doesn't learn how to do well on their own.

norm: You shouldn't cut in front of people.
situation: Nick is standing in line to pay for something when he realizes that his bus is about to leave.
intention: Nick wants to pay for the item immediately.
moral_action: Nick asks the people in front if he can check out now, so he doesn't miss the bus.
moral_consequence: The people let Nick go first and are happy about it.
immoral_action: Nick walks up to the front of the line and checks out.
immoral_consequence: The people behind Nick complain and shout at him.

norm: It's wrong not to own up to your mistakes.
situation: Don is a business owner who has had COVID run through his business because he refuses to let his employees distance or wear masks.
intention: Don wants his employees to continue work.
moral_action: Done says that he made a mistake and has his employees wear masks when possible and stay home if they feel sick.
moral_consequence: Don's business muddles through the pandemic and recession and recovers afterward.
immoral_action: Don doubles down and tells his employees they will be let go if they stay home from work sick.
immoral_consequence: Some of Don's employees die, many quit, and Don's business goes under.

norm: It's wrong to try to deceive other people.
situation: Jack is the owner of a used car with many problems and tries to sell the car.
intention: Jack wants to sell his used car.
moral_action: Jack fully discloses all the issues with the used car before making the sale.
moral_consequence: The person who buys the car is aware of the problems and has no issues paying Jack for the car.
immoral_action: Jack avoids mentioning any problems with the used car throughout the sales process.
immoral_consequence: The buyer of the used car files a lawsuit against Jack for selling him a bad car.

norm: It is rude to park badly.
situation: Jane drives into the parking lot of the grocery store, preparing to park and shop.
intention: Jane wants to park her car quickly.
moral_action: Jane parks in one of the many open spots in the back of the parking lot to avoid soaking up time looking for one.
moral_consequence: Jane's car is safe while she shops.
immoral_action: Jane parks her car partly on the sidewalk in front of the store instead of searching for a front space.
immoral_consequence: A tow truck comes and tows Jane's car before she finishes shopping.

norm: It is wrong to falsify experimentation evidence.
situation: Victor is a scientist who has been doing experimentation on some viruses.
intention: Victor needs to publish a paper about his findings.
moral_action: Victor is honest about his findings and warns people about the dangers of the virus.
moral_consequence: Many people avoid getting sick because they take precautions not to catch the virus.
immoral_action: Victor underplays the danger of the virus in order to make his findings sound less alarmist.
immoral_consequence: Many people are infected with the virus and become ill.

norm: It's good to care about getting good grades.
situation: Jeff is a freshman in high school and has a lot of homework to do with little motivation to do it.
intention: Jeff wants to enjoy his time in school.
moral_action: Jeff decides to see how quickly he can do his homework, racing himself every night.
moral_consequence: Jeff gets excellent grades and gets a full-ride scholarship to a university after he finishes.
immoral_action: Jeff puts his homework aside and goes to play with his new high school friends.
immoral_consequence: Jeff does terribly during his freshman year and those grades make it difficult to enter a good university later.

norm: It's wrong to trespass on private property.
situation: Tom rode his bike to work because he did not own a car.
intention: Tom wants to find shortcuts to get to work faster.
moral_action: Tom found a great shortcut by taking a small back road.
moral_consequence: Tom got to work sooner than he expected and was quite pleased.
immoral_action: Tom realized if he rode across a farmer's property he could cut his ride short by 5 minutes.
immoral_consequence: The farmer got upset at Tom because he ruined some of his vegetables he had just planted.

norm: It's bad to cheat to get through school.
situation: Janice is trying to study for a very hard exam later in the week.
intention: Janice wants to pass the exam.
moral_action: Janice studies very hard for the exam and gets a tutor to help her.
moral_consequence: Janice passes the exam and ends up doing well in the class.
immoral_action: Janice decides to write down the answers on a sheet that she surreptitiously checks during the exam.
immoral_consequence: Janice passes the test, but falls further behind because she can't keep up, and ends up failing the class.

norm: It's good to treat yourself every now and then.
situation: Kevin has been working seven days per week for a month but now has his Saturday off at home with his wife.
intention: Kevin wants to do something on his day off.
moral_action: Kevin goes to a spa and gets himself an hour-long massage.
moral_consequence: Kevin feels relaxed and takes his wife out for a romantic dinner that night.
immoral_action: Kevin spends his Saturday doing chores for his wife around the house.
immoral_consequence: Kevin ends up yelling at his wife because he's stressed and thinks she's too demanding.

norm: You shouldn't lie to people about your identity online.
situation: Billy is having fun online chatting with what he thinks is a stranger, but is really his wife making sure he's not cheating on her.
intention: Billy wants to impress the stranger.
moral_action: Billy tells the stranger about how much he loves his wife, and that it's the best choice he's ever made.
moral_consequence: Billy's wife feels more in love with Billy and ridiculous for checking on him.
immoral_action: Billy tells the stranger that he's very rich, and that he is a big investor, though he isn't.
immoral_consequence: Billy's wife thinks less of him for lying about his identity.

norm: It's wrong to rig a competition.
situation: Bill is running for class president against two other classmates.
intention: Bill wants to become class president.
moral_action: Bill campaigns and gives some great speeches on how to improve the school.
moral_consequence: Bill is elected fair and square, and becomes class president.
immoral_action: Bill secretly stuffs the ballot box with tons of votes for himself.
immoral_consequence: The scheme is detected, and Bill is forbidden from ever running again.

norm: It's good to move away from home when you can.
situation: Daniel is 27, lives at home with his retired parents, and just got his first stable job, well-paying job.
intention: Daniel wants to save as much money as possible.
moral_action: Daniel moves out of his parents' home and finds an apartment and a roommate to split costs with.
moral_consequence: Daniel's parents are free to use Daniel's room as they wish and pursue their own interests without worrying about Daniel.
immoral_action: Daniel continues to live at home rent-free and stashes all of his paychecks.
immoral_consequence: Daniel's parents get tired of having to take care of him and miss out on some of their retirement plans.

norm: You shouldn't charge people too much money.
situation: Sam is a plumber who is going to repair a leaking pipe at a home.
intention: Sam wants to earn extra money for Christmas gifts.
moral_action: Sam charges a high but fair rate for the service.
moral_consequence: Sam gains a loyal customer in the process.
immoral_action: Sam overcharges the customer because they have no way of knowing how much it should cost.
immoral_consequence: Sam feels guilty for charging too much.

norm: You shouldn't use a service you haven't paid for.
situation: Kayla gets a movie subscription password and username from her friend and has enjoyed using it illicitly.
intention: Kayla knows her luck may run out and wants to decide whether to get her own membership or push or luck.
moral_action: Kayla sets up her own account.
moral_consequence: She pays a little but feels secure with her own membership.
immoral_action: Kayla continues using the subscription illegally.
immoral_consequence: The company finds out and the account is banned.

norm: It's rude to not apologize when you've done something wrong.
situation: James was running to catch his bus when he ran into someone on the sidewalk.
intention: James wants to catch his bus before it leaves.
moral_action: "James pauses for one second to say, ""Sorry, sorry,"" before running after the bus."
moral_consequence: "The pedestrian yells back, ""Run, Forrest, run!"" as James climbs on the bus."
immoral_action: "James keeps running and yells over his shoulder, ""Watch where you're going, moron!""."
immoral_consequence: "The pedestrian yells back, ""You're the moron, you blind jackass!"" as James gets on the bus."

norm: It is rude to knock over a jigsaw puzzle.
situation: Ben is sitting at the table working on a jigsaw puzzle when Jessica walks in.
intention: Jessica wants to pick up a piece of the puzzle.
moral_action: Jessica takes a piece of the puzzle and puts it in the place where it belongs.
moral_consequence: Ben tells Jessica thank you for helping him with the puzzle.
immoral_action: Jessica takes a few pieces of the puzzle and throws them on the floor.
immoral_consequence: Ben starts to yell at Jessica and tells her to leave him alone.

norm: It's bad to play the blame game with people.
situation: Linda works in a lab with others and often is careless with tests.
intention: Linda wants to avoid getting in trouble for bad job performance.
moral_action: Linda owns her mistakes and works on improving them.
moral_consequence: After time Linda becomes good at her job and is respected by her team.
immoral_action: Linda accuses others of making the mistakes and tries to other depaartments.
immoral_consequence: Linda is not likes by others in the job and treated coldly by others.

norm: You shouldn't shoot people down right away.
situation: Jonathan is meeting with the last loan applicant of the day, and he is eager to leave for the day.
intention: Jonathan wants to wrap up the meeting as quickly as possible.
moral_action: Jonathan makes sure to read the paperwork thoroughly before explaining why a loan will not be possible at that time.
moral_consequence: Jonathan is able to finish up the last of him meetings and get off work.
immoral_action: Jonathan takes a cursory glance at the applicants paperwork before curtly denying the loan request.
immoral_consequence: Jonathan later faces an allegation of bias for his decision.

norm: It's wrong to fake how you feel.
situation: Joe is having serious doubts about the girl he is currently seeing and the topic of their relationship comes up while they drive home together.
intention: Joe wants to avoid arguing on the way home.
moral_action: Joe insists that they will talk about things when later after arriving home.
moral_consequence: Joe's girlfriend does not bring the subject up again on the ride home.
immoral_action: Joe acts like there is nothing wrong with their relationship.
immoral_consequence: Joe's girlfriend is misled into believing that they are on good terms.

norm: It's wrong to approach people when they are busy.
situation: Milton arrived at his professor's office on his way to his next class but his professor was talking to another student.
intention: Milton needed to ask his professor a question.
moral_action: Milton decided to come back after his class to ask the question.
moral_consequence: Milton's professor happily answered his question.
immoral_action: Milton interrupted the professor and student stating he was in a rush before asking his question.
immoral_consequence: Milton's professor chastised him and told him to come back later.

norm: It is polite to include others when ordering food.
situation: Joe is out with his friend Julia at a local restaurant, and they're both very hungry, however Julia is broke.
intention: Joe needs to order food.
moral_action: Joe orders an appetizer platter and offers Julia a share.
moral_consequence: Julia ends up having something to eat and appreciates Joe's kindness.
immoral_action: Joe orders an entree for himself and does not share.
immoral_consequence: Julia ends up having nothing to eat and remains hungry.

norm: You shouldn't decieve others on social media.
situation: Kim just added her crush from school on a new facebook account.
intention: Kim wants her crush to like her.
moral_action: Kim sends a message to her crush asking him how he is doing.
moral_consequence: Kim's crush is flattered that she is interested in him and wants to meet up.
immoral_action: Kim posts fake pictures and invents another personality in order for her crush to like her.
immoral_consequence: Kim's crush finds out that she is not real and hates her for tricking him.

norm: It's unprofessional to take office supplies.
situation: Marvin recently moved into a new home and has a nice space to set up a home office.
intention: Marvin wants to get some pens and notebooks.
moral_action: Marvin goes down to an office supply store and buys several items with his own money.
moral_consequence: Marvin furnishes his new home office, and it makes him even more productive in his job.
immoral_action: Marvin takes pens, notebooks and other supplies from the supply closet at his work.
immoral_consequence: Marvin is later fired when HR discovers that he took a lot of company supplies home with him.

norm: It is wrong to waste the money of others.
situation: Stacy collects money from a gofundme to pay for her surgery.
intention: Stacy wants to spend the money.
moral_action: Stacy gets the surgery, and she donates the rest to charity.
moral_consequence: Stacy receives a box of chocolates at the end of the year from the charity thanking her for the donation.
immoral_action: Stacy goes on a vacation to a resort hotel.
immoral_consequence: Stacy's reputation is destroyed online when people discover her vacation.

norm: It is rude to call people incessantly
situation: Sara is really excited about a new promotion which was just offered.
intention: Sara wants to share the news with her best friend.
moral_action: Sara calls her friend once and leaves a text to call her back.
moral_consequence: Sara's friend calls back and is proud of her upon hearing the news.
immoral_action: Sara calls her friend who is unavailable over and over.
immoral_consequence: Sara's friend is annoyed by her behavior.

norm: It's good to take responsibility at work.
situation: Jeff has a well paying job at a local accounting firm.
intention: Jeff wants to get noticed by his colleagues.
moral_action: Jeff starts volunteering for extra unpaid work around the office.
moral_consequence: Jeff's colleagues appreciate him and his work ethic at the job.
immoral_action: Jeff starts showing up to work dressed like a slob.
immoral_consequence: Jeff's colleagues are disturbed by his recent change in appearance.

norm: You shouldn't lie to your classmates.
situation: Rodney is in university and his friends are talking about their upcoming internships.
intention: Rodney wants to join the conversation.
moral_action: Rodney asks his friends what are good internships to apply to.
moral_consequence: Rodney will be able to find a great internship.
immoral_action: Rodney says he has a great internship which is not true.
immoral_consequence: Rodney will have to keep up with his lie and become stressed.

norm: It's good to satisfy your customers.
situation: Sally is working as a waitress at a diner when a customer tells her she brought them the wrong dring.
intention: Sally wants to empty out the drink.
moral_action: Sally apologizes and brings the customer the correct drink right away.
moral_consequence: The customer is impressed by Sally's attentiveness, so they leave her a tip.
immoral_action: Sally pours the drink out over the customer's head and says it was what they ordered.
immoral_consequence: The customer complains and Sally ends up being fired on the spot.

norm: It's frowned upon to be loud.
situation: Marcus stand in the hallway next to the testing area checking the results of his test, and he receives a perfect score.
intention: Marcus wants to express his excitement.
moral_action: Marcus shares the results of his test on Facebook and Twitter.
moral_consequence: Marcus' father texts him saying that he saw the post, and offers to take him out for dinner.
immoral_action: Marcus exclaims that he made a perfect score on the exam in a loud tone jumping up and down.
immoral_consequence: Marcus is escorted out of the building for being loud in the testing area.

norm: You shouldn't laugh at someone's food.
situation: Joe's girlfriend May has invited him over for dinner.
intention: Joe wants to react to the vegetarian menu.
moral_action: Joe thanks May for the meal.
moral_consequence: May feels her effort is appreciated.
immoral_action: Joe laughingly refers to the meal as rabbit food.
immoral_consequence: May gets offended and never invites him over again.

norm: It is rude to doubt the ability of others.
situation: Mark is the head of the swim team, and a black man wants to join.
intention: Mark wants to say something to the man.
moral_action: Mark asks the man what swim stroke he is best at.
moral_consequence: The man feels respected and is happy that Mark doesn't judge him.
immoral_action: Mark ask the man if he is sure he wants to join the team since he can't swim.
immoral_consequence: The man is insulted and is no longer interested in joining the team.

norm: Do not take someone pet with out first asking.
situation: Ann stops by her neighbor's house to water his plants, since he is out of town, and she notices that his cat very depressed.
intention: Ann wants to help the depressed cat.
moral_action: Ann picks up some toys and treats to try to get the cat feeling a little better until her neighbor gets home.
moral_consequence: Her neighbor comes home and is appreciative of her efforts to help his cat feel better, and he recommends Ann to his friends as a house sitter.
immoral_action: Ann takes the cat to her house to play with her cats, in hopes of cheering the neighbor's cat up.
immoral_consequence: The cat becomes frightened and has a fight with one of her cats, getting a scratch on its face, and her neighbor is very angry when he gets home.

norm: You shouldn't brag about your IQ.
situation: Rory and his friends took an IQ test, and Rory scored the highest of the group.
intention: Rory wants to comment on the IQ test result.
moral_action: Rory tells them that IQ test aren't everything and that any of them could have scored better on a different day.
moral_consequence: Rory's friends appreciate Rory's humbleness and continue to hang out with him.
immoral_action: Rory brags to all of them about how much smarter he is than the rest of them.
immoral_consequence: Rory's friends get tired of his bragging and stop hanging out with him.

norm: It is wrong to steal food from a restaurant.
situation: Brian is hungry and goes to a restaurant to eat.
intention: Brian wants a discount on his food.
moral_action: Brian bargains with the manager for a discount on the food.
moral_consequence: The manager agrees to knock 10% off of the price.
immoral_action: Brian eats his food and quickly runs out of the restaurant to avoid paying.
immoral_consequence: The police use video footage to identify Brian and arrest him.

norm: It's good to return something you've borrowed.
situation: Suzy is looking for something to do on a rainy day to have some fun.
intention: Suzy wants to get some books from the library.
moral_action: Suzy reads the books and when she is done with them she takes them to the library and returns them.
moral_consequence: Other people get a chance to read the books when they want to and enjoy themselves.
immoral_action: Suzy reads the books and puts them in her closet forgetting about them afterward.
immoral_consequence: Suzy get's a letter in the mail telling her about a huge fine she has and to return the books.

norm: It is wrong to marry someone just for their money.
situation: Laura has been dating Bob, a wealthy CEO, and he has just proposed, but she is not in love with him.
intention: Laura wants to answer his proposal.
moral_action: Laura tells Bob that she can't marry him because she's not in love with him.
moral_consequence: Bob is unhappy with Laura's answer but thanks her for being honest with him.
immoral_action: Laura decides that she can live in a marriage without love as long as she has lots of money, and accepts Bob's proposal.
immoral_consequence: Bob can tell that Laura really didn't want to marry him, she's only marrying him because he's rich.

norm: You shouldn't expect free stuff
situation: Damien walks into a bakery where he notices a basket of fresh scones being put out onto the counter.
intention: Damien wants to try a scone.
moral_action: Damien asks the baker if it'd be possible to sample a scone.
moral_consequence: The baker cuts up a scone into little pieces and others are able to try it as well.
immoral_action: Expecting them to be free, Damien takes a scone from the counter then walks away.
immoral_consequence: The bakery owner accuses Damien of stealing and bans him from ever returning.

norm: It's bad to engage in credit card fraud.
situation: Johanna is a university student that is taking classes.
intention: Johanna wants to make money to pay for school books.
moral_action: Johanna gets a part time job on the weekend to earn money.
moral_consequence: Johanna buys the school books for class.
immoral_action: Johanna uses stolen credit cards to order school books online.
immoral_consequence: Johanna is caught by the FBI and sent to prison.

norm: You should always support an artist you enjoy.
situation: Anna loves a local artist named Felipe, who rents an art studio in town.
intention: Anna wants to see Felipe's art every day.
moral_action: Anna buys a large painting of Felipe's and hangs it on her wall at home.
moral_consequence: Felipe uses the proceeds from the sale to Anna to pay his rent and keeps his studio open.
immoral_action: Anna takes pictures of Felipe's art at his studio and looks at the pictures each day.
immoral_consequence: Felipe gets kicked out of his studio when he can't pay the month's rent after selling no paintings.

norm: It's wrong not to talk to your roommate.
situation: Chris lives in an apartment with his roommate and is getting home from an exhausting day at work.
intention: Chris wants to be left alone and get to bed early.
moral_action: Chris greets his roommate and quickly notes that he's turning in early because of a rough day.
moral_consequence: Chris' roommate feels empathy for him and hopes that he feels better soon.
immoral_action: Chris walks by his roommate without saying anything and goes straight to his room.
immoral_consequence: Chris' roommate feels ignored by him and thinks he is rude.

norm: You shouldn't look at someone's bank account without their permission.
situation: Joey was at his brother's house visiting him in his home office.
intention: Joey wants to use his brother's computer.
moral_action: Joey gets on his brother's computer and checks the latest news headlines.
moral_consequence: Joey's brother tells him to spend as much time on the computer as he needs.
immoral_action: Joey gets on his brother's computer and looks up all his brother's banking information.
immoral_consequence: Joey's brother never lets him use his computer or come into his home office again.

norm: It's rude to try to deceive other people.
situation: Shawn is applying for jobs that are listed in the wanted ads.
intention: Shawn wants to get a good job.
moral_action: Shawn applies to the jobs he is qualified for and gets enough offers that he can choose the best opportunity.
moral_consequence: Shawn's new boss is happy to have such an eager worker on board and Shawn is happy to have a job.
immoral_action: Shawn doesn't meet the qualifications for a particular opening but says he does on his resume.
immoral_consequence: Shawn gets a job, but the boss is angered when they find out Shawn can't do the basic functions required of the job, which embarrasses Shawn.

norm: It's not right to blame others for your own shortcomings.
situation: Frank is talking to Tom when Tom tells him that he's been struggling financially recently.
intention: Frank wants to respond to Tom's statement.
moral_action: Frank tells Tom that it's unfortunate and if he needs some help Frank can help him sort it out.
moral_consequence: Tom is delighted that Frank is offering support and feels he has a great friend in Frank.
immoral_action: Frank tells Tom that it's his own fault for not being prudent with his money, and he ought to learn how to do so.
immoral_consequence: Tom is insulted that Frank thinks he just squanders money and that everyone has equal access to money.

norm: You should always pay a veterinarian for their work.
situation: Joe took his dog to the veterinarian and is handed a bill after the procedure.
intention: Joe wants to respond to the bill he has been handed.
moral_action: Joe pays the bill for the veterinarian visit.
moral_consequence: The veterinarian will be happy with having Joe as a client.
immoral_action: Joe tells the veterinarian that he won't pay for the bill.
immoral_consequence: The veterinarian will not want to service Joe's dog again.

norm: Asking a professional driver for a free ride is disrespectful.
situation: Bella is an instragram influencer who needs an Uber ride.
intention: Bella wants to make good business connections for her Instagram account.
moral_action: Bella pays for the fare plus tip and shares a pic with the driver on instagram.
moral_consequence: Bella is seen as generous and gains more popularity.
immoral_action: Bella asks to ride for free in exchange for exposure and is rude when the driver says no.
immoral_consequence: The driver shares the recorded conversation on social media and her influencer reputation is ruined.

norm: You should pay for your own mistakes.
situation: Jason accidentally breaks a vase while out shopping with his girlfriend.
intention: Jason wants to make sure the vase is paid for.
moral_action: Jason offers to pay for the vase because he's the one broke it.
moral_consequence: Jason's girlfriend respects Jason's honesty.
immoral_action: Jason guilts his girlfriend into paying for the vase by saying it was her fault he dropped it.
immoral_consequence: Jason's relationship with his girlfriend suffers.

norm: You shouldn't attend someone's party if you aren't welcome there.
situation: Gabe heard that Shelly was having a party, but he wasn't invited.
intention: Gabe wants to go out and have fun.
moral_action: Gabe meets up with some of his friends to go to the bar.
moral_consequence: Gabe has a great time having a few drinks with his friends.
immoral_action: Gabe gets dressed and drives over the Shelly's party at her house.
immoral_consequence: Shelly tells her friends to throw Gabe out of the party.

norm: It's not okay to burden someone staying in their home.
situation: Zen is staying at a friend's house because he lost his apartment and Zen has questions but his friend is working in his home office.
intention: Zen wants to decide if he should knock and ask the questions or not.
moral_action: Zen waits for his friend to finish work before asking questions.
moral_consequence: The relationship stays respectful and secure.
immoral_action: Zen knocks and is told to hold on, then mentions it'll take just a minute.
immoral_consequence: Zen realizes he's wearing out his welcome when his friend gets annoyed with him.

norm: It is rude to withhold information.
situation: Phil is at the police station and the police are questioning him about a robbery.
intention: Phil wants to answer the questions.
moral_action: Phil answers truthfully and tries to cooperate with the police.
moral_consequence: The police are grateful and Phil gets a lighter sentence for his cooperation.
immoral_action: Phil omits important details about the robbery so as to not incriminate himself.
immoral_consequence: The police find out that Phil was involved, and he gets in more trouble for lying to the police.

norm: It's not okay to make someone dependent on you.
situation: Brad has been dating his girlfriend Marla for the past two months.
intention: Brad wants to help Marla pay her bills.
moral_action: Brad offers to help Marla pay for part of her car note.
moral_consequence: Marla is able to get back on her feet with Brad's help.
immoral_action: Brad makes Marla quit her job and pays her bills himself, making her dependent on him.
immoral_consequence: When Brad and Marla break up, she has no way to pay for her bills.

norm: It is rude to visit someone without asking first.
situation: Joe is driving on the other side of town where his friend Hilda lives.
intention: Joe wants to visit Hilda.
moral_action: Joe calls Hilda and asks if it is okay to stop for a visit.
moral_consequence: Hilda meets Joe at the door, and they have a great visit.
immoral_action: Joe stops by Hilda's house unannounced and barges into her home.
immoral_consequence: Hilda is annoyed with Joe because she was not in the mood for company.

norm: You shouldn't expect people to wait for you.
situation: Jonny is late meeting up with his friends who are at the movie theater.
intention: Jonny wants to watch a new movie with friends.
moral_action: Jonny tells his friends to start the movie without him and save him a seat.
moral_consequence: Jonny's friends enjoy good seats and are able to save one for him as well.
immoral_action: Jonny tells his friends to wait even though he is significantly late.
immoral_consequence: Jonny's friends are disappointed that they miss the beginning of the movie.

norm: It's expected that people learn to clean up after themselves.
situation: Alexa is at a Taco Bell with her friends, and they ordered a lot of food.
intention: Alexa is done eating and wants to leave.
moral_action: Alexa and her friend throw all of their trash out before leaving.
moral_consequence: A minimum wage workers feel grateful for them and gets to relax for a bit.
immoral_action: Alexa and her friends leave all of their trash on the tables.
immoral_consequence: A minimum wage worker who already has plenty of work to do will have to clean up after them.

norm: You shouldn't blame new employees for everything.
situation: Gadah is at a convention with the two newest employees in the company and their presentation is sub par.
intention: Gadah wants to rise up the corporate ladder.
moral_action: Gadah assumes accountability for the poor performance and the group greatly improves the next time around.
moral_consequence: Gadah is praised for her leadership and given a promotion.
immoral_action: Gadah blames their poor presentation on the newbies and boasts about her own accomplishments.
immoral_consequence: Gadah's team does not want to work with her in the future.

norm: You shouldn't be a burglar.
situation: Mike hangs out with a group of friends who decide one night to rob a rich man's house that lives near them.
intention: Mike wants to keep his friends.
moral_action: Mike declines to go with his friends and stays clear of the entire situation in case it goes badly.
moral_consequence: Mike's friends stay friends with him and Mike continues to live an honest and productive life.
immoral_action: Mike joins his friends, helps to break into the rich man's house, and takes a lot of stuff from him.
immoral_consequence: The rich man is furious that his house was broken into and Mike turns to a life of crime.

norm: You should treat your employees with honesty.
situation: Ben is a manager for an IT firm.
intention: Ben wants to maintain a good relationship with his employees.
moral_action: Ben tells his employees the company was bought and helps them get positions with the new company.
moral_consequence: Ben's employees are grateful with how he handled the transistion.
immoral_action: Ben decides not to tell his employees the company was bought out, and they will all be laid off.
immoral_consequence: Ben's employees are furious with how he handled the situation.

norm: It's wrong to steal someone else's intellectual property.
situation: Ella is starting a new brand to sell her creations.
intention: Ella wants an attractive logo that she can use to market.
moral_action: Ella hires a designer to make a logo for her.
moral_consequence: Ella gets a nice logo that she likes.
immoral_action: Ella copies an artist's drawing from their website and edits it slightly to use as a logo.
immoral_consequence: Ella gets caught and her business reputation is ruined.

norm: It is irresponsible not to spay and neuter your pets
situation: Josh has had a cat for years but has never gotten her spayed because he doesn't want to spend the money.
intention: Josh wants to keep expenses regarding his cat low.
moral_action: Josh applies for pet insurance and takes his cat to get spayed.
moral_consequence: Josh saves money with the pet insurance and his cat is able to live comfortably.
immoral_action: Josh never takes his cat to get spayed.
immoral_consequence: Josh's cat often gets uncomfortable uterine infections.

norm: You shouldn't skip out on your chores.
situation: Malcolm is at home and his mother told him to do some chores for her before going out.
intention: Malcolm wants to get out of the house.
moral_action: Malcolm makes sure to do everything that was asked of him around the house before heading out.
moral_consequence: Malcolm has a fun time out and enjoys the earned trust he has with his mother.
immoral_action: Malcolm sneaks out right away without finishing any tasks that were asked of him.
immoral_consequence: When Malcolm gets home he is punished by his mother for not doing his chores before leaving.

norm: It's selfish not to contribute to group efforts.
situation: Rose is in a work group at school and their assignment is due at the end of the period.
intention: Rose wants to get a good grade.
moral_action: Rose puts in her best effort and contributes all that she can to the group's project.
moral_consequence: The group and Rose all receive a good grade on their project.
immoral_action: Rose believes that if she lets the rest of the group do the project without her that she will just inherent a good grade.
immoral_consequence: While the teacher does give the group a good grade for their efforts it is noted that Rose did not contribute, and she is assigned extra homework.

norm: It's always good to apologize.
situation: James was trying to find a seat on a crowded subway when he accidentally knocked over someone's bag of groceries.
intention: James wants to sit down because he's tired.
moral_action: James apologizes to the person with the groceries and sits on the floor while he gathers them up.
moral_consequence: The person with the groceries thanks James and lets him have her seat when she gets off at the next stop.
immoral_action: James keeps walking forward to look for a seat without acknowledging the spilled groceries.
immoral_consequence: Several people on the subway insult James for being clumsy and not apologizing.

norm: It is bad not to help out around the house
situation: Danny is playing video games on his computer while his mother cleans their home and does his chores.
intention: Danny wants to feel accomplished.
moral_action: Danny does all of his chores by the end of the day.
moral_consequence: Danny feels proud of the hard work he's done.
immoral_action: Danny goes on a winning streak against his online opponents.
immoral_consequence: His mother gets frustrated at Danny and grounds him for ignoring his chores.

norm: You shouldn't be late for things.
situation: Tanya, a makeup lover, has plans to meet with friends and go to a show.
intention: Tanya wants to do her makeup before heading out.
moral_action: Tanya does a simple look that only takes 10 minutes and meets up with her friends before the show starts.
moral_consequence: Tanya and her friends enjoy the show together.
immoral_action: Tanya does an elaborate makeup look that ends up taking an hour and makes her and her friends late for the show.
immoral_consequence: Tanya and her friends aren't allowed into the venue because the show already started and now Tanya's friends are mad at her.

norm: It's wrong to steal money from your school.
situation: Austin is part of a school drama club that needs to find a way to fund a team trip.
intention: Austin wants to have a benefit car wash where all of the group helps out.
moral_action: Austin helps with car wash, making sure to safely keep all of the proceedings locked up in a cash box.
moral_consequence: The drama team exceeds in obtaining necessary amount to pay for the group trip and puts the rest back for future activities.
immoral_action: Austin is in charge of handling the money received from car wash customers, which he sneaks half of the money for his own pocket.
immoral_consequence: The drama team was unable to get enough money to pay for a school sponsored excursion.

norm: It is naive to trust someone you don't know well.
situation: Ash is walking around a used car lot looking all the cars and truck they have for sale.
intention: Ash wants to get a different vehicle.
moral_action: Ash gets the VIN number off a car he likes and runs it through a car history website.
moral_consequence: Ash finds a good one-owner used car that has been well maintained over it's lifetime.
immoral_action: Ash gives in to the hard push of a salesperson and buys a car that is supposed to be a real jewel according to the sales pitch.
immoral_consequence: After taking the used car home, Ash has a lot of trouble with it because it turns out to be a flood car.

norm: It's wrong to fake liking someone.
situation: Vanessa notices that her boss, who she hates, is going to promote someone to the recently opened manager position.
intention: Vanessa wants the promotion.
moral_action: Vanessa works hard demonstrating her skills in a informative presentation.
moral_consequence: Vanessa receives a raise along with being promoted to manager.
immoral_action: Vanessa acts like her boss' best friend going out of her way to be nice to her boss.
immoral_consequence: Vanessa is given a secretary position where she has to spend most of her time with her boss.

norm: It's rude to ride slowly in the fast lane.
situation: Harold is a slow driver driving on a two-lane highway, and his route has him making a left turn in a few miles.
intention: Harold wants to make a left-hand turn.
moral_action: Harold stays in the right-most lane until relatively close to the time he needs to turn.
moral_consequence: Traffic runs smoothly, and Harold makes his trip without incident.
immoral_action: Harold goes over to the passing lane and stays there, driving slowly, for several miles.
immoral_consequence: The other drivers honk at him and tailgate him, and a traffic jam starts.

norm: It is wrong to steal another person's child.
situation: Kelly is barren and is researching her options.
intention: Kelly wants a child to raise and love.
moral_action: Kelly goes through all the requirements to be able to adopt an orphan child and provide it love and a home.
moral_consequence: Kelly raises a young child who was orphaned and the child grows up happy and loved.
immoral_action: Kelly decides the cheapest and fastest option is to find an unattended child in public to grab and take home.
immoral_consequence: Kelly traumatizes a young child when she grabs it away from its parents.

norm: You should follow through on your promises.
situation: Jeff had promised to spend the weekend with his father who he hadn't seen in a month.
intention: Jeff wants to see a movie with his friends.
moral_action: Jeff invites his father to join him and his friends on their trip to the movies.
moral_consequence: Jeff and his father have a great night spending time with each other.
immoral_action: Jeff cancels his plans with his father and watches a movie with his friends instead.
immoral_consequence: Jeff's father is upset and doesn't answer Jeff's calls for a week.

norm: It's wrong to hold onto other people's pets.
situation: Katie is petsitting her friend Peter's cat while he is on vacation and has realized she loves cats.
intention: Katie wants to have a pet cat.
moral_action: After returning Peter's cat, Katie goes to the animal shelter and adopts her own pet cat.
moral_consequence: Katie and Peter both have pets they love and a cat is rescued.
immoral_action: Katie tells Peter that she is keeping his cat because she is attached to it now and loves it.
immoral_consequence: Peter gets very angry with Katie and calls the police on her to get his cat back.

norm: It's good to return someone else's property.
situation: Payton has borrowed a hair dryer from her friend that makes her hair look really nice.
intention: Payton wants to own a hair dryer just like her friend's.
moral_action: Payton asks her friend where she bought the hair dryer so that she can buy one for herself.
moral_consequence: Payton's friend is happy that Payton liked the hair dryer so much.
immoral_action: Payton keeps the hair dryer and tells her friend that the hair dryer was broken.
immoral_consequence: Payton's friend gets mad when she sees the blow dryer at Payton's house.

norm: It's good to decide fairly who will get their wish.
situation: Mark tells his kids they are going out to eat and whoever gets their chores done first will be allowed to choose the restaurant.
intention: Mark needs to take the kids to get dinner.
moral_action: Mark lets his younger daughter choose because she finished first, and they head to Olive Garden.
moral_consequence: Everyone is happy that they did their work and go to go out to eat.
immoral_action: Mark let's his older daughter pick the restaurant even though she didn't finish her chores, because she is smarter.
immoral_consequence: His younger daughter is furious that her dad treated her unfairly.

norm: You shouldn't keep things from your partner.
situation: Bobby and Grace are getting married and Bobby has $50,000 in debt that he has not told Grace about.
intention: Bobby wants to have a nice wedding day with Grace.
moral_action: Bobby tells Grace about his debt before their wedding, and they have a nice wedding day.
moral_consequence: Bobby and Grace sit down to work out a payment plan of their total debt together and their bond is stronger.
immoral_action: Bobby and Grace have a nice wedding day and Grace finds out about Bobby's debt a few days after the wedding on accident.
immoral_consequence: Grace is betrayed by Bobby's secret and takes a separation from their new marriage.

norm: It's good to not treat others like they are liars.
situation: Nagisa listens to his coworker's explain that they didn't eat Nagisa's lunch.
intention: Nagisa wants to respond to his coworker.
moral_action: Nagisa asks his coworker to aid his search at finding the lunch eating culprit.
moral_consequence: Nagisa's coworker is praised for his detective skills when he figures out the identity of the thief.
immoral_action: Nagisa says that he doesn't believe his coworker and demands repayment for lunch.
immoral_consequence: Nagisa's coworker breaks down crying in the break room.

norm: It's bad to tilt a vending machine.
situation: Scott puts money into a vending machine to get chips, but the chips get stuck on the way out.
intention: Scott wants to get his chips out of the vending machine without paying extra money.
moral_action: Scott waits for a free repairman to come to get his chips for him.
moral_consequence: Scott is happy the experience getting the chips was free from too much drama.
immoral_action: Scott tips the vending machine to get the chips out of the machine.
immoral_consequence: Scott makes the machine crash and break accidentally while tipping it.

norm: You should not waste other people's time.
situation: Harmony is browsing the displays at a busy jewelry store without planning to buy anything, when a sales clerk approaches her and asks her if she needs help.
intention: Harmony wants to respond to the sales clerk.
moral_action: Harmony explains that she's currently just browsing and doesn't require assistance.
moral_consequence: The sales clerk appreciates how straightforward Harmony was and is able to spend his time efficiently by helping other customers who are trying to buy jewelry.
immoral_action: Harmony asks the sales clerk a bunch of questions about the jewelry and requests to try some of it on even though there's no chance of her purchasing it.
immoral_consequence: The sales clerk eventually figures out that she's just there to browse and decides to move on to help some other customers.

norm: People shouldn't abuse their positions of power.
situation: Jim is an auditor with the IRS and his friend John gets a tax bill that has him worried.
intention: Jim wants to soothe John.
moral_action: Jim tells John not to worry that he can get all his answers from the IRS guides available to the public and to just breathe.
moral_consequence: John feels a little better knowing there's some literature he can read on the matter and educate himself.
immoral_action: Jim tells John not to worry about it, he'll go into work Monday and erase the problem from Jim's file.
immoral_consequence: John thinks that Jim is unscrupulous and doesn't want to get involved in a corruption scheme with Jim.

norm: Racism is wrong.
situation: Ike, a cop, searches for a necklace stealing criminal when he notices a black man in a hoodie exiting the store.
intention: Ike wants to catch the criminal quickly.
moral_action: Ike asks the people around him if they noticed any suspicious activity.
moral_consequence: One of the interviewers gives Ike the licence plate of the thieves car saving them hours of investigation.
immoral_action: Ike questions the black man, and he takes the black man into the office for intense questioning.
immoral_consequence: Ike's actions are uploaded onto Twitter where people demand for his resignation.

norm: It's wrong and unethical to cheat in school
situation: It's the night before an important exam and John finds himself unprepared.
intention: John wants to pass the exam.
moral_action: John spends most of the evening cramming and catching up on the material he hadn't studied.
moral_consequence: John is able to just barely pass the exam since he is super exhausted, which motivates him to plan better for his next class.
immoral_action: John creates a small answer sheet that he plans to stick in his pocket for the exam tomorrow.
immoral_consequence: John passes the exam but doesn't actually know the material, causing him to fail the more advanced class afterwards.

norm: It is wrong to break a vow to someone.
situation: Chris feels that Becky's disability is too much for him to handle, but vowed to stay with her in sickness and in health.
intention: Chris wants to feel less burdened by Becky and her illness.
moral_action: Chris asks Becky if she knows of resources to help spouses of disabled people care for themselves and their loved one better.
moral_consequence: Chris and Becky work together to find resources that help lessen the load that Chris has to bear.
immoral_action: Chris leaves Becky, explaining that he can't take care of her anymore and is at his breaking point.
immoral_consequence: Becky is heartbroken and tells their friends and family that Chris is heartless.

norm: You shouldn't break the law.
situation: Joe is walking past a convenience store when he notices the only employee is away from the cash register.
intention: Joe is thirsty and wants something to drink.
moral_action: Joe waits for the store's employee to return to the cash register and buys a drink using his debit card.
moral_consequence: Joe feels good that he didn't steal from the store.
immoral_action: Joe takes advantage of the situation and shoplifts a bottle of soda from the store.
immoral_consequence: Joe saves a couple of dollars dollars but feels guilty.

norm: You should not get angry with animals for being animals.
situation: Greg has a new puppy that had an accident on the carpet.
intention: Greg wants to have a clean house.
moral_action: Greg realizes that the puppy is just learning, takes the puppy outside, and then cleans up the mess.
moral_consequence: The puppy learns to go to the bathroom outside and feels loved by Greg.
immoral_action: Greg yells at the puppy, puts the puppy in the cage, and cleans up the mess while cussing.
immoral_consequence: The puppy is confused and feels scared of Greg.

norm: It's wrong to lie.
situation: Red is standing before Al, his dad, and KEvin, the family pet, and a broken lamp lying on the floor.
intention: Red wants to explain why the lamp is broken.
moral_action: Red takes responsibility for breaking the lamp.
moral_consequence: Red, the true culprit is punished and justice prevails with none harmed undeservedly.
immoral_action: Red tells an untruth indicating Kevin broke the lamp.
immoral_consequence: Al inflicts corporal punishment upon Kevin who feels intense pain without knowing why and suffers.

norm: You shouldn't let your roommate tell you where to sleep.
situation: Donna and Carol being friends rented an apartment to live in together, and they move in at different times.
intention: Carol wants to unpack her belongings.
moral_action: Carol and Donna agree to flip a coin to decide which room they use.
moral_consequence: Carol feels excited for being assigned a room by flipping a coin.
immoral_action: Carol moves in late and Donna going first occupied the larger room and asks Carol to take the other room.
immoral_consequence: Carol feels unfair because they pay equal rent for the apartment.

norm: It's expected that customers respect service workers.
situation: Larry is eating dinner at a crowded restaurant and realizes his drink is getting low.
intention: Larry wants to ask the waitress to get him a refill.
moral_action: Larry waits until the waitress comes to his table, and he asks her for a refill.
moral_consequence: The other customers are able to eat their meals without interruption.
immoral_action: Larry sees the waitress across the room and whistles loudly to get her attention.
immoral_consequence: The other customers are offended by Larry's actions and give him angry looks.

norm: You should ask for forgiveness when you do something wrong
situation: Carl is poking fun at Beth and accidentally hurts her feelings.
intention: Carls wants to try to cheer Betty up.
moral_action: Carl sits down and apologizes to Beth for hurting her feelings.
moral_consequence: Beth is able to forgive Carl.
immoral_action: Carl starts making fun of himself.
immoral_consequence: Beth still feels down but laughs at his jokes.

norm: It's good to save money for emergencies.
situation: Crystal studies the money in her bank account after getting a large inheritance from her uncle.
intention: Crystal wants to do something with her money.
moral_action: Crystal puts all of the money into a low interest saving account.
moral_consequence: Crystal is able to afford her car repairs after she gets into a wreck.
immoral_action: Crystal spends all of her money on speculative stocks.
immoral_consequence: Crystal loses money after the stocks perform poorly.

norm: It's expected that you'll return items that you borrow.
situation: Paul was using his grass trimmer on his lawn when it suddenly stopped working.
intention: Paul wants to finish his yard work.
moral_action: Paul finishes his yard work by borrowing his neighbor's grass trimmer and returns it as soon as he is done.
moral_consequence: His neighbor can trim his lawn whenever he wants without having to ask Paul to get his grass trimmer back.
immoral_action: Paul asked his neighbor to borrow his grass trimmer to finish his yard work, but forgets to return it.
immoral_consequence: His neighbor has to come ask for Paul for his own grass trimmer back when he wants to trim his lawn.

norm: It is good to be appreciative when someone helps you.
situation: Mike has just finished helping Brad move some heavy furniture, and they're taking a break.
intention: Brad wants to tell Mike they're done, and he can go.
moral_action: Brad tells Mike he really appreciates all the help Mike gave him and thanks him before saying he can go if he wants.
moral_consequence: Mike is delighted that Brad is appreciative of his efforts and help and tells Brad to have a great day.
immoral_action: Brad tells Mike that was it and Mike should go home now since they're finished.
immoral_consequence: Mike is insulted that Brad is being so curt with him and that he never said thanks.

norm: You should pay rent for the places you stay at.
situation: Ted is balancing his checkbook.
intention: Ted wants to save money.
moral_action: Ted cancels some subscription services and decides to stop eating out so much.
moral_consequence: Ted saves money and the landlord can cunt on a regular payment to afford contractors for other properties.
immoral_action: Ted decides to save money by not paying Ted's rent anymore.
immoral_consequence: Ted's landlord is unable to hire a contractor to fix a mold problem because Ted didn't pay him.

norm: You shouldn't cheat in a class.
situation: Ben is in class taking a test on material that he hadn't studied for.
intention: Ben wants to make a good grade.
moral_action: Ben thinks logically about all the questions and does the best that he can.
moral_consequence: Ben is happy when the teacher gives the tests back, and he gets a grade of a B.
immoral_action: Ben copies the answers off the test of the classmate sitting right next to him.
immoral_consequence: Ben's teacher takes the test away from him and marks it with an F.

norm: It's bad to judge a whole store based on gossip.
situation: Jon heard about a new pizza place in his area and wanted to check it out.
intention: Jon wanted to learn more about the pizza place.
moral_action: Jon visits the pizza place after work to see what the hubbub was all about.
moral_consequence: Jon becomes a loyal customer of the pizza place after seeing what it's like in there firsthand.
immoral_action: Jon read online that the pizza place was run by pedophiles, so he decided not to go.
immoral_consequence: Jon is never able to experience what the pizza place is like due to fake news.

norm: It's rude to hi-jack your friend's joke entirely.
situation: Steve starts telling a joke at a party that his friend, Chris, already knows.
intention: Chris wants to contribute to the joke.
moral_action: Chris lets Steve finish telling the joke and joins in the raucous laughter even though he'd heard it already.
moral_consequence: Steve feels glad that Chris found the joke funny even though he'd heard it before.
immoral_action: Chris starts talking over Steve to tell the rest of the joke.
immoral_consequence: Steve feels annoyed that Chris wouldn't let him tell his own joke.

norm: You should never blame someone else for your mistakes.
situation: Tom is a team leader has made a serious error in the important team project that was just submitted.
intention: Tom wants to avoid getting in trouble at work.
moral_action: Tom works extra hours to fix the mistake on his own time and explains it to his boss.
moral_consequence: Tom protects his team from disciplinary action.
immoral_action: Tom tells his superiors that one of his colleagues, rather than him, was in charge of that part of the project.
immoral_consequence: Tom gets someone else in trouble for their mistake.

norm: It's wrong to copy your friend's answers on final exams.
situation: Carl is having trouble on his final exam and notices that his friend Rey is nearly done.
intention: Carl wants to answer all of the final exam questions.
moral_action: Carl does his best to finish the exam using everything he already knows.
moral_consequence: Carl manages to get a passing grade on the final exam.
immoral_action: Carl looks at Rey's exam paper and copies the answers.
immoral_consequence: Carl is caught by his teacher and given a failing grade on the exam.

norm: It's wrong to make jokes about places with crime.
situation: Ron is a comedian, and he has a scheduled performance tonight at a local comedy club.
intention: Ron wants to give a good comedy show.
moral_action: Ron performs a comedy act featuring lots of jokes about airports and traveling.
moral_consequence: The crowd loves Ron's jokes, and he exits the stage to a standing ovation.
immoral_action: Ron performs a comedy act that includes lots of jokes about crime in inner city Chicago.
immoral_consequence: The crowd finds Ron's jokes to be in poor taste, and he gets booed during his performance.

norm: It is rude to meddle in someone else's relationship.
situation: Lynn notices that her friend is making passive aggressive comments about her relationship online.
intention: Lynn wants to point her friend in the right direction.
moral_action: Lynn waits for her friend to ask her for advice and tells her to do what is beneficial for her situation.
moral_consequence: Lynn's friend decides to work things out with her partner.
immoral_action: Lynn approaches her friend about the relationship and tells her to break up with the partner.
immoral_consequence: Lynn's friend breaks up with her partner prematurely when they could have worked things out.

norm: It's rude to take all the credit from your partner.
situation: Zelda and her husband worked hard to get rid of the roaches in the old house.
intention: Zelda wants to talk about the acomplishment.
moral_action: Zelda talked about the ideas she came up with and the ones her husband did.
moral_consequence: They both get credit for the termination of the roach infestation.
immoral_action: Zelda tells people how she came up with the ideas and took the roaches down herself.
immoral_consequence: Her husband gets upset because she's not giving him any credit.

norm: It is good to keep some space.
situation: Hank buys a used big screen television and has to put it in his cramped car to get it home.
intention: Hank wants to put the television in the car.
moral_action: Hank gets rid of some of the unnecessary items in his car to make room for the television.
moral_consequence: Hank is able to get the television home without any problems.
immoral_action: Hank squishes the television in the car on top of other stuff.
immoral_consequence: The television gets a crack in the screen and Hank has to buy another one.

norm: You're discouraged from bragging about your possessions.
situation: Ace just bought a new luxury car with his work bonus money and has some friends over.
intention: Ace wants to tell his friends about his new car.
moral_action: Ace mentions that he got a nice new car and asks his friends what cars they like.
moral_consequence: Ace's friends all share about their cars, starting a nice conversation.
immoral_action: Ace tells his friends about his car and goes over every detail of its luxury features.
immoral_consequence: Ace's friends get annoyed because they really don't care much about the car.

norm: It's good that an underdog outperforms expectations.
situation: James has a colleague at work who was given a difficult task and performed better than expected.
intention: James wants to react to his colleague's performance.
moral_action: James becomes proud of his colleague for doing the task better than expected.
moral_consequence: James develops a positive relationship with his colleague.
immoral_action: James becomes jealous of his colleague because his colleague did well.
immoral_consequence: James develops a toxic relationship with his colleague.

norm: Giving a tip for a service is expected.
situation: Trent enjoys a meal at nice restaurant and receives the bill.
intention: Trent wants to pay the bill and leave.
moral_action: Trent pays the bill and includes an acceptable gratuity for his waiter.
moral_consequence: Trent's waiter pockets Trent's tip which he feels satisfied with.
immoral_action: Trent pays for the meal but leaves no gratuity for his waiter.
immoral_consequence: The waiter despises Trent for not leaving him anything which is expected.

norm: It is polite to clean up after yourself in public
situation: Adam is using the bathroom in a big box store because he has a bad case of the runs.
intention: Adam doesn't want to draw attention to himself.
moral_action: Adam takes the time to clean up after himself to avoid people knowing he messed up the bathroom.
moral_consequence: The custodian has hardly any mess to clean up that afternoon.
immoral_action: Adam leaves the bathroom as quickly as possible to avoid notice, leaving behind a huge mess of crappy toilet paper.
immoral_consequence: The custodian has to put the bathroom out of order while cleaning up the mess.

norm: It's inappropriate to make a mess of a friend's house.
situation: Bob is throwing a party at a house which belongs to a friend.
intention: Bob wants everyone to have a good time.
moral_action: Bob throws a nice party and leaves the house spotless when it's over.
moral_consequence: Bob's friend is happy because the house is even cleaner than it was before.
immoral_action: Bob parties heavily and leaves a huge mess afterward.
immoral_consequence: Bob's friend is upset because the house was trashed.

norm: It's bad to steal from the government.
situation: Dirk is having a hard time keeping up with his bills.
intention: Dirk wants more money to give him comfort.
moral_action: Dirk looks for a part time job at night to make more money.
moral_consequence: Dirk is able to save more and starts to enjoy the people he is meeting with his night job.
immoral_action: Dirk claims more dependents than he should on his tax returns form, so that he will get back more money.
immoral_consequence: Dirk is caught and charged with tax fraud.

norm: It's wrong to get upset about the little things.
situation: Ian is walking in his house when he bangs his toe into a dining room chair.
intention: Ian wants to let out his frustration.
moral_action: Ian breathes very deeply and loudly without speaking, trying to control the pain.
moral_consequence: No one realizes that Ian walked into a chair or was angry at anything.
immoral_action: Ian shouts loudly and complains for ten minutes at the chair that he walked into.
immoral_consequence: The neighbors think that Ian is verbally abusing a family member and are suspicious of him.

norm: You shouldn't dislike entire groups of people
situation: Harold plans on going to a concert and doesn't want to go alone.
intention: Harold wants to get a date.
moral_action: Harold is open to a variety of women and asks his group of friends to get him a date, and he goes with a blind date.
moral_consequence: Harold's friends know Harold is lonely and continue setting him up on dates, which he eventually ends up marrying one of them.
immoral_action: Harold declines his friend's suggestion of a different race woman and tells him he is going with another pal's sister, who is his own race.
immoral_consequence: Harold's friend is offended by his racist attitude and their friendship over his racist attitude.

norm: It's good to practice.
situation: Joel's baseball coach says he should be better at hitting, but he hates practicing.
intention: Joel has to decide whether to practice hitting.
moral_action: Joel wants to get paid more, so he decides to practice more.
moral_consequence: Joel's hitting improves, and he becomes more attractive to other teams.
immoral_action: Joel decides that he's good enough at hitting and never practices.
immoral_consequence: Joel's hitting begins to stagnate, and he doesn't know how to improve.

norm: It's wrong to cheat your employer.
situation: Steve has a car but its not running well anymore, and he wants to buy a new one but doesn't have enough money.
intention: Steve wants a new car.
moral_action: Steve asks if he can work more overtime to help him afford a new car.
moral_consequence: Steve is given extra projects at work, and he saves to buy a new car.
immoral_action: Steve uses an expense account at work to buy himself a new car.
immoral_consequence: Steve gets caught having misused his expense account and has to pay it back after being fired.

norm: It is best to separate friendship and money.
situation: Jack is reluctant to lend his friend Bob a large sum of money.
intention: Jack wants to maintain his friendship with Bob.
moral_action: Jack suggests various financial institutions that can give Bob a loan.
moral_consequence: Jack is glad to help his friend without jeopardizing their friendship.
immoral_action: Jack lends Bob the money and is surprised when Bob fails to pay him back promptly.
immoral_consequence: Jack gets angry and his friendship with Bob deteriorates.

norm: It's bad to be stingy with your money.
situation: Greg is at the bar with a friend who usually pays for his drinks.
intention: Greg wants to save money.
moral_action: Greg orders less drinks than usual and pays for both their tabs.
moral_consequence: Greg's friend is pleasantly surprised that he is paying.
immoral_action: Greg lets his friend pay for the drinks again.
