immoral_consequence: Greg's friend thinks poorly of him because he never pays.

norm: It is good to talk about kids before getting married.
situation: Danny and Layla are newly engaged.
intention: Danny wants to have children with Layla.
moral_action: Danny tells Layla he'd like to raise children with her.
moral_consequence: Layla and Danny decide to adopt a child, and they all live happily ever after.
immoral_action: Danny surprises Layla a bunch of pregnancy tests, so they can try for a baby.
immoral_consequence: Layla tells Danny she is infertile, and they break off their engagement.

norm: People should strive to be independent.
situation: Mary is in her 40's and very close with her parents.
intention: Mary wants to have strong family bonds.
moral_action: Mary get married and has a career but always makes time for her parents.
moral_consequence: Mary is successful in life and also has strong bonds with both her parents and her family.
immoral_action: Mary forms a co dependent relationship with her parents never getting a job or moving out of the house.
immoral_consequence: When Mary's parents pass away she is all alone without the life skills needed to live independently.

norm: One is expected to return money that another person drops.
situation: Mary is paying for her groceries when she notices a woman walking out the door drop a $50 bill.
intention: Mary wants to make a decision.
moral_action: Mary picks up the money and hands it back to the woman.
moral_consequence: The woman is grateful because now she can still afford all her bills.
immoral_action: Mary picks up the cash and shoves it into her pocket.
immoral_consequence: The woman can't afford her water bill and has it shut off.

norm: It's nice to put in hard work.
situation: Ted has moved into a new house which has a large backyard.
intention: Ted wants to do something with his backyard.
moral_action: Ted digs out the yard and puts in many plants and grass.
moral_consequence: Ted will have an impressive yard.
immoral_action: Ted does a simple scattering of small stones over his yard.
immoral_consequence: Ted will have an unimpressive backyard.

norm: It's bad to try and evade the police.
situation: Mark was driving well above the speed limit and caught the attention of the police.
intention: Mark wants to decide how to proceed in response to the police.
moral_action: Mark pulls over and accepts the ticket written to him by the police officer.
moral_consequence: Mark pays the ticket and experiences no further issues from this incident.
immoral_action: Mark decides to floor it and make an attempt to escape the police.
immoral_consequence: Mark does not get far before getting caught, arrested and charged according to his offenses.

norm: You should not expect someone to do more than you.
situation: Vince and some workers are shoveling coal in a boxcar and is very tired.
intention: Vince wants to take a break.
moral_action: Vince asks everyone if they can take a break together.
moral_consequence: Everyone gets a break and is refreshed for work.
immoral_action: Vince tells the others to keep working and takes a ten minute break.
immoral_consequence: The other workers get upset with Vince and refuse to work, causing trouble.

norm: It's rude to not give people change.
situation: Jim is selling his TV at a yard sale for $50 and Patrick has just handed him a $100 bill.
intention: Jim wants to make more money.
moral_action: Jim offers to throw a blu-ray player in with the TV for $15 more.
moral_consequence: Patrick takes Jim up on the offer, so Jim gives Patrick $35 back.
immoral_action: Jim brings the money inside and talks to Patrick long enough for him to forget about getting his change.
immoral_consequence: Patrick later realizes he's been fooled and goes to Jim's house to demand payment through threat of violence.

norm: You shouldn't steal things that don't belong to you.
situation: Ash notices that her sister has a really cute top when looking through the closet.
intention: Ash wants to wear the top when she goes out.
moral_action: Ash offers to trade a top that her sister likes for this one.
moral_consequence: Ash's sister is happy to trade the top as she likes the other one better.
immoral_action: Ash takes the top without asking and keeps it in her closet.
immoral_consequence: Ash's sister finds out and is furious with her as she was looking for it.

norm: It's mean to be rude to someone.
situation: Stacey and Chloe are out to eat with friends, and Chloe is sitting close to all of the condiments on the table.
intention: Stacey wants Chloe to pass her the salt.
moral_action: Stacey kindly asks Chloe to pass the salt.
moral_consequence: Chloe gladly passes the salt over to Stacey with a smile.
immoral_action: "Stacey looks at Chloe and rudely shouts, ""hurry up and give me the salt!""."
immoral_consequence: Chloe feels disrespected and gets up to leave the table.

norm: It's wrong to lie to people about money.
situation: Jenny is walking and thinking of some large bills when her friend Paul pulls up and offers her some spare cash.
intention: Jenny wants to respond to Paul's offer.
moral_action: Jenny says that the money will help with some bills and takes it.
moral_consequence: Jenny pays the bills and has some extra left over.
immoral_action: Jenny says that she's doing just fine and has plenty of cash, not wanting to embarrass herself.
immoral_consequence: Jenny has great difficulty paying her bills that month.

norm: It is bad to steal.
situation: Bella is at her friend Kristen's house when she notices that Kristen has a really cute new dress.
intention: Bella wants to wear the dress.
moral_action: Bella tells Kristen she really likes the dress and asks if she can borrow it for a night out some time.
moral_consequence: Kristen is happy Bella likes the dress and lets her wear it a few times.
immoral_action: Bella slips the dress into her backpack before she leaves and takes it home without telling Kristen.
immoral_consequence: Kristen finds out Bella took the dress and ends their friendship.

norm: It's good to reward someone when they do well.
situation: Jill's subordinate Jake is a very good employee who constantly goes above and beyond.
intention: Jill wants to indicate her awareness of Jake's capability.
moral_action: Jill decides to give Jake an employee of the month award.
moral_consequence: Jake is really happy with the award and places it in the middle of his desk.
immoral_action: Jill decides to give Jake even more work as he's so proficient at his job.
immoral_consequence: Jake is really stressed out by the increased workload and feels taken advantage of.

norm: It's wrong to lie at job interviews.
situation: Mark has a job interview after a long period of unemployment.
intention: Mark really wants to get this job.
moral_action: Mark explains that he has been unemployed recently but really wants a job.
moral_consequence: Mark does not get the job because the interviewer wants to hire someone who has worked recently.
immoral_action: Mark tells the interviewer that he has been working a retail job for the past few years.
immoral_consequence: Mark gets the job because the interviewer thinks he is a good candidate.

norm: It is good to tell your kids the truth.
situation: Peter receives a pink tie for his birthday from his son Bo, but he hates the color pink and the tie.
intention: Peter wants to thank Bo for the present.
moral_action: Peter tells Bo that he appreciates the thought but that he doesn't really like the color pink.
moral_consequence: Bo returns the tie to the store he bought it from and gets Peter a classic red tie that Peter does like.
immoral_action: Peter tells Bo that he really likes the tie and is looking forward to wearing it.
immoral_consequence: Bo believes that his father really likes the pink tie and buys him another one for Christmas.

norm: It's unscrupulous to mislead people who trust you with finances.
situation: Bill is gathering financial and market information to put into a prospectus.
intention: Bill wants to raise money from investors to expand his business.
moral_action: Bill accurately reflects his current financial position in his correspondence with his investors.
moral_consequence: Bill receives the required funding and expands his business.
immoral_action: Bill pads his current revenue numbers and omits some of his liabilities in the promotional materials.
immoral_consequence: Some of Bills investors lodge a complaint with the SEC.

norm: You should not steal from someone you work with.
situation: Chris is at work when his manager asks him to note something down.
intention: Chris wants to grab a pen.
moral_action: Chris goes back to his desk and grabs one of his own pens to make notes with.
moral_consequence: Chris' reputation in the office remains the same as no incident of note occurs.
immoral_action: Chris looks at the nearest desk and sees a pen on it, so he grabs that and starts making notes.
immoral_consequence: The  pen's owner is annoyed that Chris just took his pen and Chris' reputation in the office suffers.

norm: It's rude to judge someone's talent when they sing karaoke.
situation: Zoe has been successfully pressured by her friends, including Alazzo, to sing karaoke during their weekly night-out, despite being a famously bad singer.
intention: Allazzo wants to comment on Zoe's karaoke trial.
moral_action: Allazzo tells her that she really wasn't bad and that she's bold to go up there.
moral_consequence: Zoe and Allazzo laugh it off and get along.
immoral_action: Allazzo can't stop laughing while he tells Zoe that she was hilarious.
immoral_consequence: Zoe is offended and avoids Allazzo from then on.

norm: It's rude to plagiarize someone else's work.
situation: Mike has to get his homework done but it's so boring and his brother James is already done.
intention: Mike wants to get his homework done.
moral_action: Mike does all the work himself and finishes his homework on his own.
moral_consequence: Mike gets an A for the assignment, the only one in his class to do so.
immoral_action: Mike just copies James's work and calls it a day.
immoral_consequence: Turns out James didn't go very well on his homework and guessed at all the answers, so now Mike fails too.

norm: It's bad to obtain things by fraudulent means.
situation: Corey is shopping at the store with his wife Emily and sees a jacket he really likes, but he's short on money.
intention: Corey wants to acquire something new.
moral_action: Corey asks Emily if he can borrow the money he needs to cover the cost of the jacket.
moral_consequence: Emily agrees, so Corey gets what he wants and pays Emily back when he gets home.
immoral_action: Corey brings the jacket to the checkout,  writes a bad check, and brings it home.
immoral_consequence: Corey's check bounces and now he is left paying additional fees.

norm: It's rude to down vote people's posts.
situation: Tim is on Reddit and stumbles upon a post that is extremely disagreeable.
intention: Tim wants to express his opinion and disagreement to the poster.
moral_action: Tim doesn't rate the post but provides a comprehensive counter argument in response.
moral_consequence: Tim is seen as a logical person who makes a good point.
immoral_action: Tim down votes the post and responds with namecalling.
immoral_consequence: Tim is seen as a troll and a hater by the other users.

norm: You shouldn't refuse to pay someone back.
situation: Craig runs a rental car business that has installed kill switches in the cars and owes a large debt on the kill switches.
intention: Craig wants to save money.
moral_action: Craig pays the company what they are owed and pays it on time.
moral_consequence: Craig's business prospers and the threat of the kill switches means fewer delinquent payees.
immoral_action: Craig calls up the kill switch company and says that he is not going to pay the bill.
immoral_consequence: The company activates all of the kill switches on his cars, temporarily shutting down his business.

norm: It's rude to take a soda from someone else.
situation: Jacob was at a diner with all of his friends, but he ran out of his drink.
intention: Jacob wants something to drink.
moral_action: Jacob calls the waitress over to order himself another drink.
moral_consequence: The waitress brings a fresh, cold soda over to Jacob's table.
immoral_action: Jacob takes a sip of soda that his best friend had ordered for himself.
immoral_consequence: Jacob's friend gets made and makes him pay for his food.

norm: You shouldn't break promises.
situation: Joey tells Timmy that she'll go to the prom with him but then the star quarterback at the school asks her to go with him instead.
intention: Joey wants to have a great time at the prom.
moral_action: Joey tells the quarterback that she already accepted Timmy's invitation to go to the prom and that she always has fun with Timmy.
moral_consequence: Timmy has the night of his life at the prom with Joey.
immoral_action: Joey tells Timmy that she can't go to the prom with him and that she'd rather go with the quarterback.
immoral_consequence: Timmy is crushed and instead of going to the prom, he stays home with his grandmother.

norm: It's wrong to base your vote on gender.
situation: Bob wants to vote for a senator and sees that a questionable man and an upstanding woman are on the ballot.
intention: Bob wants to vote.
moral_action: Bob votes for the woman because he knows her character.
moral_consequence: The woman wins and does her job very well.
immoral_action: Bob votes for the man because he hates the thought of a woman in office.
immoral_consequence: The man wins and turns out to be terribly corrupt.

norm: Its wrong to report someone for something they didn't do.
situation: George witnesses a robbery but doesn't see the perpetrator's face and can't identify him.
intention: George wants to give a statement to the police about the crime.
moral_action: George tells the police the height and weight of the man he saw committing the robbery.
moral_consequence: The police use George's description plus other evidence to identify and arrest the robber.
immoral_action: George tells the police that a man who lives in his building committed the robbery.
immoral_consequence: The police arrest the man who lives in George's building even though he is innocent.

norm: It's good to give money to people for their trouble.
situation: Ben's car broke down in the middle of a highway, and a stranger stopped to help.
intention: Ben wants to thank the stranger.
moral_action: Ben gives the stranger a $50 bill as he gets ready to leave.
moral_consequence: The stranger thinks that it was worth it to stop and help.
immoral_action: Ben offers the stranger a hug and thanks him for what he did.
immoral_consequence: The stranger isn't compensated for helping Ben when he didn't have to.

norm: It's not okay to miss out on your weekly obligations
situation: Andy gets a telephone call from his soccer teammate that he has a weekly practice with who tells him to prepare for this week by working on his free kicks.
intention: Andy wants to respond to the request to work on his backhand.
moral_action: Andy tells his teammate that he'll make sure he comes to practice with some free kicks already under his belt.
moral_consequence: Andy's teammate is delighted with Andy's dedication and holds optimism for their season.
immoral_action: Andy tells his teammate he's not feeling great, and he probably won't make it to practice this week.
immoral_consequence: Andy's teammate is annoyed that Andy will miss practice and the team will fall behind and so will Andy making their season worse.

norm: It's wrong to leave your date alone with the check.
situation: Cedric is at a restaurant with his date Irene and gets an urgent call from his mother asking for help.
intention: Cedric wants to leave so he can help his mother.
moral_action: Cedric leaves $200 on the table in the restaurant before leaving.
moral_consequence: Irene uses the money to pay the bill and tip.
immoral_action: Cedric rushes out of the restaurant without taking care of the bill.
immoral_consequence: Irene has to pay the entire bill and provide a tip on her own.

norm: You shouldn't judge yourself or others based on height.
situation: John, who is short, runs a powerful health institution and deals with a lot of taller clients and workers.
intention: John wants to have authority over other people.
moral_action: John uses his superior business skills and knowledge to direct people's projects wisely, helping them complete them successfully.
moral_consequence: John's associates come to respect John and seek his advice and counsel, listening to and obeying what he says.
immoral_action: John mounts a ruler on his office door and notes how tall each person is who enters his office, then tries to dominate the taller ones.
immoral_consequence: The taller people find John to be overbearing and controlling and decide not to work for him any more.

norm: People won't trust you if they know you're a thief.
situation: Greg walks into the lunch room at work one day when he realizes that he forgot his lunch at home.
intention: Greg wants to eat lunch.
moral_action: Greg orders a sandwich from a local cafe to be delivered to the office and picks it up by the front door.
moral_consequence: Greg enjoys eating his sandwich for lunch.
immoral_action: Greg opens the fridge, finds a lunch bag belonging to another coworker, and eats its contents.
immoral_consequence: Greg's coworkers find out about his lunch theft and never fully trust him again.

norm: It's bad to steal someone's wifi.
situation: Jason is out on the street and needs to access his email with his phone, so he goes into settings to connect to the internet.
intention: Jason wants to choose which method to connect to the internet with.
moral_action: Jason goes into data and turns it on and uses the mobile phone network to connect to the internet.
moral_consequence: Jason is able to access his email without problem and get what he needs from it.
immoral_action: Jason goes into wi-fi and finds an unsecured connection which he uses to connect to the internet.
immoral_consequence: Jason is caught by the owner of the network and booted out before he can access his email.

norm: It's wrong to fire someone just because they got injured.
situation: John owns a small construction business, and one of his workers just fell off a roof.
intention: John wants to make sure his business's worker's comp rates don't go up.
moral_action: John offers to give the worker eight weeks of informal paid leave without the need to file a worker's comp claim.
moral_consequence: John's worker has time to recover from his injury and John doesn't have to pay more for worker's comp insurance.
immoral_action: John claims that the worker was drinking on the job and therefore can be fired for cause.
immoral_consequence: The worker files suit for wrongful termination and his coworkers testify on his behalf, and the resulting settlement bankrupts John.

norm: It's encouraged you compromise if you're disagreeing with someone
situation: Mary and her husband are about to paint their living room but don't really agree on the color they should choose: pale yellow or orange?
intention: Mary wants the living room to look warm and bright.
moral_action: Mary decides to make a bit of a change to her initial idea and agrees with her husband on a deep yellow.
moral_consequence: Mary's husband likes the new color they chose, which is closer to what he initially wanted.
immoral_action: Mary insists that they must paint the living room a pale yellow.
immoral_consequence: Mary's husband is frustrated by her lack of flexibility and doesn't like the pale yellow.

norm: It's considerate to do your part in group projects.
situation: Rob is assigned to make a skit for English class with five classmates.
intention: Rob wants to get a good grade.
moral_action: Rob invites the group over to rehearse and play video games after.
moral_consequence: Rob and the group get a good grade and Rob makes new friends.
immoral_action: Rob and the others plan to meet after school to rehearse the play, but Rob stay homes and plays video games.
immoral_consequence: The group gets an A, but Rob gets a failing grade when the group tells the teacher what he did.

norm: It's not okay to lie to get out of work.
situation: Tina is a call center worker who is burned out on her job which she has had for years.
intention: Tina wants to have a break from her work.
moral_action: Tina meets with an HR rep and works within the system to get a combination of paid vacation and unpaid family leave.
moral_consequence: Tina is refreshed and ready to work again after being able to take a break for a month.
immoral_action: Tina feigns an injury in order to file disability.
immoral_consequence: Tina is investigated when people get suspicious and is fined for disability fraud.

norm: Fabricating a pregnancy scare is lying.
situation: Katie has been fighting with her partner frequently and is worried about their relationship.
intention: Katie wants her partner to stay with her.
moral_action: Katie takes note of the issues in the relationship and works hard to control her end of them.
moral_consequence: Katie's partner notices her efforts and is motivated to stay with her.
immoral_action: Katie tells her partner that she is pregnant even though she is not in order to get him to stay.
immoral_consequence: Katie's partner finds out and this is the final straw for him.

norm: You should treat all of your srudents equally.
situation: Tom's principal asks him to pass the students that are on the football team and have been flunking his class.
intention: Tom wants to respond to the principals request.
moral_action: Tom refuses to pass them and instead suggests supplemental classes to help them.
moral_consequence: The students learn the value of working hard at their studies.
immoral_action: Tom passes the football students despite their poor performance.
immoral_consequence: The students gain a sense of entitlement and don't bother putting any effort into their education.

norm: You shouldn't destroy things that belong to other people
situation: Chris is trying to put staples into a stapler he borrowed from a coworker but it is jammed shut.
intention: Chris wants to open the stapler.
moral_action: Chris asks his coworker about opening the stapler, and they manage to get it open properly.
moral_consequence: Chris is able to use the stapler without any further issues.
immoral_action: Chris uses all his strength and breaks open the stapler.
immoral_consequence: Chris' coworker becomes angry with Chris for breaking his stapler and refuses to let Chris borrow items from him in the future.

norm: It's bad to misrepresent yourself in a chatroom.
situation: Will is bored and logs onto a chatroom full of teenagers.
intention: Will wants to have fun chatting with people.
moral_action: Will starts talking about life, the universe, and everything with the other people.
moral_consequence: Will and the others have a deep conversation and feel satisfied.
immoral_action: Will pretends to be a girl and starts flirting with the guys in the chatroom.
immoral_consequence: Some of the guys start cyber-stalking Will and sending sexual photos to him.

norm: It's not okay to hold a grudge against people.
situation: Sally and her boyfriend broke up but Sally was really good friends with her boyfriend's frail mother.
intention: Sally wanted to visit her ex-boyfriend's mother to stay in touch.
moral_action: Sally visits her ex-boyfriend's mother, and she does not try to talk about her ex at all.
moral_consequence: Sally's ex-boyfriend's mother enjoys their visit and it is a pleasure to have her over.
immoral_action: Sally visits her ex-boyfriend's mother, and she talks about how mad she is at her ex-boyfriend.
immoral_consequence: Sally's ex-boyfriend's mother is upset by the things Sally talks about and their visit is stressful.

norm: It's not okay to lie.
situation: Angela was applying for a job at a new company and one of the questions asked her if she had been fired from a job before, which she had.
intention: Angela wants to answer the question.
moral_action: Angela admitted that she had been fired once before and explained the situation.
moral_consequence: The company felt she was a suitable and honest candidate, and they offered her the job.
immoral_action: Angela wrote that she had never been fired from a previous job, even though this was not true.
immoral_consequence: The company discovered that she had been fired when they did a background check, and they did not offer her a job.

norm: You shouldn't block people with your carts in the supermarket.
situation: Joe was in the supermarket looking for a can of corn in the aisle.
intention: Joe wants to walk across the aisle to the canned goods.
moral_action: Joe pulls his cart as far to the side as he can while looking for the canned goods.
moral_consequence: The other customers are able to move past Joe while he is searching.
immoral_action: Joe leaves his shopping cart in the middle of the aisle while he looks for the canned goods.
immoral_consequence: The other customers aren't able to get around Joe's cart because he left it in the middle of the aisle.

norm: You shouldn't use a fake ID.
situation: Tom's older friend Bill invites him out for a night on the town.
intention: Tom wants to have a fun evening hanging out with his friend Bill.
moral_action: Tom suggests that they go bowling instead of going to a bar.
moral_consequence: Tom has a fun evening with his friend Bill.
immoral_action: Tom uses a fake ID to try and get into a bar with Bill.
immoral_consequence: Tom's fake ID is discovered, and he is reported to the police.

norm: Making up lies about someone cheating is immature.
situation: Mary is mad at her sister who took Mary's favorite dress without asking and got it stained with red wine.
intention: Mary wants to react to what her sister has done.
moral_action: Mary has a serious conversation with her sister, explaining how angry she is with her for ruining her favorite dress.
moral_consequence: Mary's sister takes responsibility for her actions, apologizes to Mary and buys her a new dress.
immoral_action: Mary tells her sister's boyfriend a made-up story about her sister having an affair with her best male friend.
immoral_consequence: Mary's sister has a huge fight with her boyfriend which puts a strain on their relationship.

norm: You shouldn't lie to your friends about being ill.
situation: Sawyer receives a text message from his friends inviting him to hang out with them today.
intention: Sawyer wants to spend his Saturday lounging at home.
moral_action: Sawyer tells his friends that he doesn't feel like going out today.
moral_consequence: Sawyer's friends accept his explanation after sharing their concerns, and Sawyer spends the day relaxing by himself.
immoral_action: Sawyer tells his friends that he is sick with the flu.
immoral_consequence: Sawyer's friends come over to give him soup, but discover he's well and end up trusting him less.

norm: It's rude to steal your colleagues' lunches.
situation: Bob is at work when he realizes that he forgot to bring his lunch with him.
intention: Bob wants to have something to eat.
moral_action: Bob goes downstairs and orders food from the work cafeteria.
moral_consequence: Bob is full after eating his meal and remembers to bring his food to work the next day.
immoral_action: Bob takes some of the food from a coworkers lunch box in the office fridge.
immoral_consequence: Bob is called into his boss's office and loses a day's worth of pay.

norm: It's a little gross to eat to excess.
situation: Tim took a girl he met on Tinder to an expensive Italian restaurant.
intention: Tim wants to eat pasta.
moral_action: Tim orders one plate of pasta and talks to the girl for the rest of the date.
moral_consequence: Tim's date is able to enjoy the date, and Tim doesn't eat too much food.
immoral_action: Tim orders four different plates of pasta and eats everything off of them.
immoral_consequence: Tim looks like a slob in front of his date, and she is less attracted to him.

norm: You shouldn't refuse to give back money you owe.
situation: Frank owes money to his friend who just found out that he has just received his paycheck but it's only enough to cover his bills.
intention: Frank wants to avoid getting any late fees.
moral_action: Frank pays the money back to the friend and uses his credit card to pay some of his bills.
moral_consequence: Frank's friend learns that he will repay loans and it increases his trust.
immoral_action: Frank claims that he has not actually been paid yet to keep the money.
immoral_consequence: Frank's friend loses trust in him and will not help him in the future.

norm: It's rude to tell someone to go away.
situation: Jason has had a guest at his apartment for a few hours and Jason would like them to leave already.
intention: Jason wants to indicate that it's time for his guest to leave.
moral_action: Jason yawns and mentions how busy he is tomorrow and how he should get some rest soon.
moral_consequence: Jason's guest understands Jason's polite hints that it's time for them to depart, and they soon do so.
immoral_action: Jason tells his guest to leave the apartment as they've been there for too long.
immoral_consequence: Jason's guest is really insulted and embarrassed by Jason telling them to go away so bluntly.

norm: You should not judge the lifestyle of others.
situation: Freya, a mother with two boyfriends, asks her conservative Christian coworker Abigail for advice on how to handle her unruly children.
intention: Abigail wants to give Freya advice.
moral_action: Abigail offers to loan Freya one of her mother's parenting books alongside passing along some information she learned from her other friends who are mothers.
moral_consequence: Freya learns a lot about parenting by reading the book Abigail loans her.
immoral_action: Abigail tells Freya that her children are misbehaving because they have two father figures which confuses them,  and breaking up with one of her boyfriends should fix the problem.
immoral_consequence: Freya is sad because she feels like everyone judges her.

norm: You should not interfere with other people's lives
situation: John sees Jake gambling at the local casino.
intention: John wants to tell someone that Jake is gambling.
moral_action: John calls his own wife and tells him that a friend is gambling, and he just wanted to tell someone.
moral_consequence: Jake never knows that he's being talked about and continues to have a fun night gambling.
immoral_action: John calls Jake's wife and tells her that Jake is gambling their money away.
immoral_consequence: Jake's wife is angry at Jake and starts a fight when Jake gets home.

norm: It's wrong to expect money out of people.
situation: Bobby is unemployed and has just been introduced to a rich woman he isn't attracted to.
intention: Bobby wants to live the good life.
moral_action: Bobby doesn't lead her on and instead finds himself a lucrative career.
moral_consequence: The lady doesn't get hurt.
immoral_action: Bobby pretends to love the woman in return for monetary support.
immoral_consequence: The woman will catch on eventually and resent him terribly.

norm: It's wrong to steal things from other people.
situation: Nathan is visiting a friend, and his friend shows him a really cool video game that he got recently.
intention: Nathan wants to get a copy of the game for himself.
moral_action: Nathan goes to the store on the way home and picks up a copy of the game.
moral_consequence: Nathan's friend is excited that he got the game and wants to play it with him online.
immoral_action: Nathan tucks the video game into his jacket right before he leaves his friend's house and takes it home without asking.
immoral_consequence: Nathan's friend finds out and gets angry at him for taking the game.

norm: It is rude to dislike people who are in relationships.
situation: Jill notices that her friends' relationship status seems to effect how much she likes them.
intention: Jill wants to figure out why that is.
moral_action: Jill realizes that assuming personality traits based on relationship status doesn't make sense.
moral_consequence: Jill decides to stop basing her opinion of someone off their relationship status.
immoral_action: Jill figures it has something to do with how dating people are too mushy or divorced people are too mopey.
immoral_consequence: Jill continues on with relationship status discrimination.

norm: It's wrong to make up excuses.
situation: Jake is behind schedule on an important project at work because he's been procrastinating, and has a meeting with his supervisor that morning.
intention: Jake has to report his progress to his supervisor.
moral_action: Jake owns up to his procrastination but promises to work overtime that week in order to finish on time.
moral_consequence: Jake's supervisor appreciates Jake's honesty and allows him to work all the overtime he needs to get the project done.
immoral_action: Jake claims his PC crashed and wiped out all the project data he had.
immoral_consequence: Jake's supervisor asks to see Jake's PC and realizes Jake was lying and terminates him.

norm: You shouldn't laugh at people's misfortune.
situation: Paul is sitting in a hospital waiting room when he sees a man on crutches slip and fall.
intention: Paul wants to react to the man falling.
moral_action: Paul winces in pain and offers to help the man get up.
moral_consequence: The man is grateful for the help and the staff appreciate Paul's help.
immoral_action: Paul laughs out loud at the man.
immoral_consequence: The hospital staff look at Paul in disgust and tell him to leave.

norm: It's wrong to feel superior to others.
situation: Jim is a black belt in karate and recently won a gold trophy at a martial arts tournament.
intention: Jim wants to let his friends know what happened.
moral_action: Jim brings up the tournament while hanging out with his friends, and mentions offhand that he won a trophy but still has much to learn.
moral_consequence: Jim's friends appreciate his modesty and ask him several follow-up questions about the tournament.
immoral_action: Jim carries the trophy around all day, waving it in his friend's faces and talking about how good at karate he is.
immoral_consequence: Jim's friends can't stand the way he's acting and avoid him.

norm: It's rude to assume women are bad at video games.
situation: Randall is inviting people to participate in a speed running event, and a person helping him asks Randall to invite some female runners to the event.
intention: Randall wants high quality runners to attend the event.
moral_action: Randall hosts a competition for men and women and the people with the fastest time are invited to the event.
moral_consequence: The competition gives Randall's event free publicity making people more excited about the event.
immoral_action: Randall tells the person helping him that women naturally aren't good speed runners based on his faulty beliefs.
immoral_consequence: Randall is called for being sexist by the other people moderating the event.

norm: You should not be proud of being a fascist.
situation: Jeff was having a conversation about his political views, and his date ended up calling him a fascist.
intention: Jeff wanted to choose where to go from here.
moral_action: Jeff reconsiders some of his views to figure out whether he has the right position.
moral_consequence: Jeff's family and friends understand that he is thinking things over and are still willing to spend time with him.
immoral_action: Jeff decided to embrace the ideology and not be ashamed of it.
immoral_consequence: Jeff's friends and family don't like Jeff as much and avoid hanging out with him.

norm: It's wrong to welsh on a bet.
situation: Pete bet with his coworker on the Super Bowl but his team lost.
intention: Pete wants to ward off being made fun of for backing the losing team.
moral_action: Pete slips the bet winnings under his coworker's office door and hides in his office all day.
moral_consequence: Pete's coworker donates the money to Pete's charity of choice.
immoral_action: Pete avoids his coworker, never making good on the bet.
immoral_consequence: Pete's coworker doesn't invite him to his World Series party.

norm: It's expected to call if you can't make an appointment.
situation: Jane was just in a minor car accident while driving to a business appointment.
intention: Jane wants to address the accident.
moral_action: After getting safely home Jane contacts the business to explain the situation.
moral_consequence: The business people show understanding for her situation and reschedule the appointment for the next day.
immoral_action: Jane forgets about her appointment and spends the rest of the day talking to her car insurance company.
immoral_consequence: Since Jane missed out on her appointment without explanation her appointment is moved to the back of the waiting list.

norm: It's not okay to sabotage an election.
situation: Hannah is running for class president, and it's expected to be a tight race.
intention: Hannah wants to win the election.
moral_action: Hannah changes her plans for when she's class president, so they appeal more to the members of her class.
moral_consequence: Hannah gathers a lot of support and ends up winning the election legitimately.
immoral_action: Hannah decides to stuff the ballot box with her name until there are enough votes for her to win.
immoral_consequence: Hannah's teachers recognize her handwriting on the ballots, and she loses the election by default.

norm: It's wrong to cheat on your significant other.
situation: Josh, who is tired of fighting with his current girlfriend so frequently, has taken an interest in a new woman.
intention: Josh wants to feel intimacy in a relationship.
moral_action: Josh ends his relationship with his current partner before seeing the other woman romantically.
moral_consequence: The new woman is excited to have a new romance in her life.
immoral_action: Josh starts seeing another woman while he is in a relationship.
immoral_consequence: The new woman finds out the Josh is not single and stops seeing him.

norm: It's unacceptable to commit a crime.
situation: Jim is in dire need of money to pay off a bookie who is threatening to kill him.
intention: Jim needs to locate cash money as soon as possible.
moral_action: Jim calls up his mother and reluctantly begs her for a loan to get him out of trouble.
moral_consequence: Jim's mom agrees to help him out if he agrees not to deal with the bookie again and Jim is eternally grateful.
immoral_action: Jim runs into the gas station, punches the clerk, and takes money out of the cash register.
immoral_consequence: Jim is chased down by police a short while later and taken into custody and charged with assault and robbery.

norm: It's wrong to cheat by looking at someone else's work.
situation: Alvin's test anxiety paralyzes his mind making him unable to focus, and the student with the best score in his class is sitting beside him.
intention: Alvin wants to complete his test.
moral_action: Alvin closes his eyes and does deep breathing exercises until he calms down enough to focus.
moral_consequence: Alvin learns to repeat the exercises for all of his tests in the future.
immoral_action: Alvin peers at his classmates test copying all of his answers.
immoral_consequence: Alvin fails the test when his teacher catches him.

norm: It's wrong to disturb other's with noise.
situation: Andy is at the library studying and it is quiet around him.
intention: Andy wants to listen to music.
moral_action: Andy plays a song through his earbuds on his phone.
moral_consequence: The other people in the library do not notice Andy's music and are undisturbed.
immoral_action: Andy plays a song through the speakers on his phone.
immoral_consequence: The other people in the library are distracted by Andy's music.

norm: You shouldn't steal things.
situation: Randy is at work at the office and sees some office supplies which remind him of what he needs for his home office.
intention: Randy wants to have a stapler to use at home.
moral_action: Randy stops buy an office supply store on his way home from work.
moral_consequence: Randy's office is more furnished with the supplies that he needs at home.
immoral_action: Randy takes home one of the staplers from his work office.
immoral_consequence: Randy gets caught stealing from work and is then fired from his job.

norm: You shouldn't download things without paying for them.
situation: Andrew is at his computer looking at a newly released video game on Amazon, and he can't afford the game.
intention: Andrew wants to obtain the game.
moral_action: Andrew does some extra chores around the house in exchange for the game.
moral_consequence: Alex spends the rest of the week having fun playing the game.
immoral_action: Andrew downloads the game from a torrent website.
immoral_consequence: Andrew's computer becomes infected with viruses from the download.

norm: It's not okay to take something that isn't yours.
situation: When Frank arrives to collect his take-out order from a restaurant, he spots several bags of food sitting out for pick-up but is told that the cooks forgot his order.
intention: Frank wants to get home as quickly as possible.
moral_action: Frank cancels his order and buys a to-go meal at a gas station on the way home.
moral_consequence: Frank gets home quickly, but he doesn't have a very enjoyable meal.
immoral_action: Frank grabs the bag for another person's order and leaves the restaurant without saying a word to any of the workers.
immoral_consequence: Someone else's order is messed up, but Frank gets home quickly and has food to eat.

norm: It's rude to avoid people.
situation: John is walking down the street when he spots an old acquaintance, Dan walking towards him and waving at John.
intention: John wants to respond to Dan's wave.
moral_action: John waves back at Dan and tells Dan hello and that it's been a long time so it's nice to see him.
moral_consequence: Dan is happy at John's polite statement and is happy to see a familiar face in the crowd.
immoral_action: John quickly looks down at the ground pretending not to notice Dan's wave and scurries off in a different direction.
immoral_consequence: Dan is hurt that John is seemingly trying to ditch him and wonders what is wrong with him that John would not want to talk to him.

norm: You should always answer your customer's questions.
situation: Nancy has had an extremely busy day working at the customer service desk when a customer comes to the desk with a question.
intention: Nancy wants to finish her paperwork and clock out.
moral_action: Nancy refers the customer to the correct department and then finishes her paperwork before clocking out.
moral_consequence: The customer is very happy with Nancy's service and Nancy feels happy to have helped.
immoral_action: Nancy finishes her paperwork without ever looking towards the customer and then clocks out.
immoral_consequence: The customer complains about the lack of service to the manager and Nancy gets reprimanded.

norm: You shouldn't have an affair if you're a stripper.
situation: Caroline is a stripper, and she has been having doubts about her marriage lately.
intention: Caroline wants to try something new.
moral_action: Caroline asks her husband to try some new things to spice up their sex life.
moral_consequence: Caroline finds sex with her husband fun again, and falls in love with him all over again.
immoral_action: Caroline asks a client if they want to have sex, and they agree.
immoral_consequence: Caroline's husband finds out, and throws her out of the house.

norm: You shouldn't break rules to help a student graduate.
situation: Tom's friend at school needs to do well on an exam in order to graduate.
intention: Tom wants to help his friend graduate.
moral_action: Tom takes the time to study with his friend and this helps them pass the test and graduate.
moral_consequence: Tom feels proud to have made a big difference in his friend's life by helping them to graduate.
immoral_action: Tom breaks into the teacher's room a takes a copy of the exam so that his friend can use it to ace the exam and graduate.
immoral_consequence: It is later proven that Tom took the test, and he is suspended from school.

norm: It's wrong not to be able to pay the bill.
situation: Ray hadn't paid his electric bill in more that a month and a half.
intention: Ray wants to pay for his electric bill on the online service.
moral_action: Ray pays the bill online as soon as he can get to a computer.
moral_consequence: Ray only has to pay the cost of his past due electric bill.
immoral_action: Ray waits another two weeks before logging on to pay the electric bill.
immoral_consequence: Ray has to pay the bill plus several other late fees that he incurred.

norm: You shouldn't borrow things from others without asking.
situation: John's yard is covered in fallen leaves due to the season, and he noticed his neighbors rake up against a tree.
intention: John wants to rake his yard.
moral_action: John knocks on his neighbors door and asks to borrow the rake.
moral_consequence: The neighbor lets him borrow the rake and is pleased that he can help.
immoral_action: John takes the rake without permission and uses it to rake his yard.
immoral_consequence: John's neighbor spots him going over to take the rake and finds him to be rude.

norm: Being organized is good because it saves time.
situation: Charles lives in a messy house where he's always losing things, and is constantly wasting time trying to find stuff.
intention: Charles wants to become more efficient.
moral_action: Charles decides to clean up his bedroom, so he's no longer wasting time looking for things.
moral_consequence: Charles manages to find his personal items quickly every day from then on.
immoral_action: Charles decides to start speed walking around the house, avoiding piles of clutter in order to save time.
immoral_consequence: Charles trips on a pile of junk in his bedroom one day and twists an ankle.

norm: You shouldn't turn against people.
situation: Selma used to be friends with one her coworkers but now sees that their boss doesn't like the coworker much at all.
intention: Selma wants her boss to think highly of her.
moral_action: Selma stays friend with her coworker and convinces her boss that the coworker is actually great.
moral_consequence: Selma's coworker doesn't have problems with their boss anymore and is grateful to Selma.
immoral_action: Selma stops hanging out with her coworker and starts being cold and distant with her.
immoral_consequence: Selma's coworker feels sad and betrayed.

norm: An engagement should be about more than a material thing.
situation: Samuel and his wealthy significant other have become very close lately.
intention: Samuel wants to propose to his significant other.
moral_action: Samuel proposes because he and his significant other are deeply in love.
moral_consequence: Samuel and his significant other get married and live happily.
immoral_action: Samuel proposes because he wants a chunk of his significant other's wealth.
immoral_consequence: Samuel is seen as a gold-digger and his significant other leaves him.

norm: It's rude to ask people to pay for your party.
situation: Greg is hosting a party for his co-workers when he realizes that he spent way more than he's able to pay.
intention: Greg wants to pay for the party.
moral_action: Greg contacts a family member and asks for a short-term loan to cover the party costs temporarily.
moral_consequence: The family member gives the loan and the co-workers enjoy the party.
immoral_action: Greg gets everyone's attention and announces that everyone needs to pay $20 to cover the costs of the party.
immoral_consequence: Greg's co-workers get upset, as Greg never told them that they had to pay.

norm: Its not okay to demand more than your fair share.
situation: Bill wants to open a lemonade stand and tells Jack about it, the next day Jack has opened a stand himself.
intention: Bill wants to be paid for his idea.
moral_action: Bill goes up to Jack and asks for some money for his idea.
moral_consequence: Bill and Jack agree to work at the stand together and split the money.
immoral_action: Bill storms up to Jacks stand and demands all of the money from the stand.
immoral_consequence: Bill and Jack fight and don't make any money because they fight rather than make sales.

norm: It's bad to not pay someone back.
situation: Christian's crush, Lila, has asked him out on a date, but he is broke and can't afford dinner.
intention: Christian wants to have dinner with Lila.
moral_action: Christian borrows his uncle's rent money and pays it back when he says he will.
moral_consequence: Christian and Lila were able to go on their date, and Christian's uncle is able to pay his rent on time.
immoral_action: Christian borrows money from his uncle with the promise to pay him back before rent is due, but doesn't.
immoral_consequence: Christian's uncle is late on rent and forced to pay a late fee.

norm: It's wrong to cheat on your taxes.
situation: Peter realizes that Tax Day is coming up, and he hasn't filed yet.
intention: Peter wants to complete his tax return.
moral_action: Peter fills out his tax return, reporting all of his income and only taking credit for deductions he has receipts for.
moral_consequence: Peter receives a refund from the IRS and deposits it in his savings account.
immoral_action: Peter fills out his tax return but doesn't include all of his income and takes credit for numerous deductions he hasn't earned.
immoral_consequence: Peter gets audited by the IRS and has to pay interest and penalties when his taxes are recalculated.

norm: It's good to praise your kids.
situation: Laura's child built a birdhouse and shows it to her.
intention: Laura wants to tell her child what she thinks of the birdhouse.
moral_action: Laura says the birdhouse is well crafted.
moral_consequence: Laura's child will be proud of their work.
immoral_action: Laura says the birdhouse is low quality.
immoral_consequence: Laura's child will be hurt emotionally.

norm: It's rude to cut in front of others in line.
situation: Claire is at the front of the prison commissary line and Amber is at the end of the line.
intention: Amber doesn't want to wait in the line for very long.
moral_action: Amber walks up to Claire and explains that her need is an emergency.
moral_consequence: Claire asks Amber what she needs and buys the items for her, saving her the wait time.
immoral_action: Amber walks towards the front of the line and stands in front of Claire.
immoral_consequence: Claire pushes Amber and both prisoners end up getting punished for their behavior.

norm: It's bad to be a cheater.
situation: Mark is in a hard calculus class.
intention: Mark needs to pass the next test.
moral_action: Mark studies hard and passes.
moral_consequence: Mark is proud of himself.
immoral_action: Mark buys an answer key online and aces the test.
immoral_consequence: Mark does badly in Calculus 2, because he didn't learn the material.

norm: You should not ask people to vote on your behalf.
situation: Paul is at a restaurant with his girlfriend when he realizes it is election night, and he still hasn't voted.
intention: Paul wants to cast his vote.
moral_action: Paul finishes his dinner quickly and rushes over to the polling station to vote just in time before it closes.
moral_consequence: Paul successfully casts his vote and is proud to fulfill his civic duty.
immoral_action: Paul asks his friend Jim to impersonate him at the polling station to cast his vote for him.
immoral_consequence: Paul's plan is foiled easily as Jim is turned away at the polling station and Paul's vote is never counted.

norm: You shouldn't lie about your gender online.
situation: Jess is an transgender female who is a popular influenster on Instagram.
intention: Jess wants to wait for the right moment to tell her audience her gender.
moral_action: Jess discloses her gender in an Instagram post that explains why she waited so long.
moral_consequence: Jess's audience understands why she waited so long to disclose her gender and are okay with it.
immoral_action: Jess's gender is leaked online before she is able to disclose it on her own.
immoral_consequence: Jess's community does not trust her as much, and they don't understand why she doesn't trust them.

norm: It is wrong to mislead others to fit in.
situation: Nancy has just joined a new reading club in her area.
intention: Nancy wants to impress her new club friends.
moral_action: Nancy comes to the book club with some articles about the author to give them some context.
moral_consequence: Nancy's book club friends are delighted that she adds an extra level of interest to their book club.
immoral_action: Nancy tells her friends that she is friends with the author of the book they are reading, even though she isn't.
immoral_consequence: Nancy's book club friends ask her questions about the author, and realize she is not telling the truth.

norm: It's polite to wait your turn.
situation: John was waiting in line at the post office yesterday morning.
intention: John wanted to get a book of stamps.
moral_action: John stood in line and waited until it was his turn to buy his book of stamps.
moral_consequence: The man in line behind him paid for John's book of stamps.
immoral_action: John walked to the front of the line and demanded to be served.
immoral_consequence: The crowd yelled for John to get in the back of the line.

norm: It's bad to frame someone for something.
situation: John has made a costly mistake at work that will have strong repercussions.
intention: John wants to resolve the issue.
moral_action: John owns up to his bosses about the mistake and presents possible solutions.
moral_consequence: John's bosses give him an opportunity to right the wrong, and he is luckily able to do so.
immoral_action: John knows that a co-worker has a reputation for making mistakes, so he makes it seem as though that co-worker was responsible.
immoral_consequence: The co-worker is fired and John's health deteriorates as he is racked with guilt.

norm: It's good to make money doing something.
situation: Bob has just graduated from college.
intention: Bob wants to do something meaningful and fulfilling.
moral_action: Bob pursues a career in his field of study.
moral_consequence: Bob becomes happy and successful.
immoral_action: Bob decides to travel the world.
immoral_consequence: Bob runs out of money and becomes homeless.

norm: It's wrong to use something that isn't yours without permission.
situation: Hank wanted to use his grill, but he was out of propane, and he saw his neighbor Dale had plenty.
intention: Hank wants to grill something quickly.
moral_action: Hank quickly rushes to the store where he restocks on propane as quickly as possible.
moral_consequence: Hank is pleased that he has refilled his propane supply.
immoral_action: Hank decides to take some of Dale's propane to start grilling.
immoral_consequence: Dale decides to keep mocking Hank for running out of propane.

norm: It is unacceptable not to flush after using the restroom.
situation: Larry is at a party when he feels the call of nature.
intention: Larry wants to return to the party quickly.
moral_action: Larry uses the rest room and flushes the toilet multiple times to make sure everything went down.
moral_consequence: Larry enjoys the rest of the party knowing that he was respectful in his friend's home.
immoral_action: Larry uses the toilet quickly and then leaves without flushing.
immoral_consequence: The host of the party calls Larry out on his poor manners and embarrasses him.

norm: It's wrong to steal from a lost wallet or purse.
situation: Jane was walking through a store when she found a wallet laying on the ground.
intention: Jane wants to turn the wallet in.
moral_action: Jane gives the wallet to the lost and found after reporting how much cash it contains.
moral_consequence: The wallet is returned to the owner with all the money still in it.
immoral_action: Jane takes the money out of the wallet before putting it in the lost and found.
immoral_consequence: The owner gets their wallet back but loses a lot of cash.

norm: It's wrong not to pay back your debts.
situation: Blair is sitting at home when his phone rings, and he sees it's the debt collection agency on the call display.
intention: Blair wants to get the phone to stop ringing.
moral_action: Blair answers his phone and talks to the collection agent about making a payment arrangement.
moral_consequence: The collection agency is satisfied with a payment arrangement and stops harassing Blair.
immoral_action: Blair looks at his phone and hits a button that silences the ringer before throwing the phone on the couch.
immoral_consequence: The collection agency continues to charge interest and prepares to take Blair to court.

norm: You shouldn't continue texting people that are cheating their partners.
situation: Tom learns that his brother-in-law Kent is cheating on Sarah, his wife.
intention: Tom wants to confront Kent about Kent's infidelity.
moral_action: Tom meets with Kent, accuses him of cheating on Sarah, and tells him to stop.
moral_consequence: Sarah never learns of Kent's infidelity, and he stops seeing the other woman.
immoral_action: Tom texts Kent and asks him about the details of his affair.
immoral_consequence: Sarah snoops on Kent's phone, finds the text conversation about his affair, and asks for a divorce.

norm: It's bad not to be on time.
situation: Casey sitting in the cafeteria after eating his lunch.
intention: Casey wants to get to his next class.
moral_action: Casey leaves the cafeteria and walks directly to the auditorium for his next class.
moral_consequence: Casey gets a good seat up front and has an easier time reading the class notes on the board.
immoral_action: Casey wonders around campus taking the long way to the building his class is in.
immoral_consequence: Casey arrives late to class and is locked out of the room and misses his class.

norm: It's bad to buy things you can't afford.
situation: Kate is in the jewelry shop in the mall and is excited to see different types of jewelries.
intention: Kate wants to look good in an upcoming wedding party.
moral_action: Kate looks around and picks up an affordable but beautiful necklace.
moral_consequence: Kate is happy to pay for the necklace without incurring any debts.
immoral_action: Kate buys a very expensive necklace with no regards to its price.
immoral_consequence: Kate needs to use multiple credit cards incurring high interest debts.

norm: It's bad to pretend to be inhabited by evil spirits.
situation: Cameron is hanging out with his highly religious friend Andrew on Halloween.
intention: Cameron wants to scare Andrew.
moral_action: Cameron takes Andrew to a haunted house and jumps at him at the end to scare him.
moral_consequence: Despite being scared, Andrew invites Cameron to his house to binge on candy.
immoral_action: Cameron makes a creepy expression and tells Andrew that he has been possessed by the ghost of a murderer.
immoral_consequence: Andrew assumes that Cameron is into the occult and ends their friendship.

norm: It's wrong to stiff your friend's on a bill.
situation: Sal was eating lunch with his friend Jake at a diner.
intention: Sal wants to go home.
moral_action: Sal waits until after he pays the bill to get up and leaves.
moral_consequence: Jake shakes Sal's hand and tells him that he will see him later.
immoral_action: Sal leaves the diner before the waiter brings him his bill.
immoral_consequence: Jake is upset at Sal because he has to pay for his friend's bill.

norm: It's bad to be wasteful.
situation: James went to a buffet with hundreds of different foods from dozens of cuisines.
intention: James wants to try dozens of new foods.
moral_action: James takes a small spoonful of everything he wants to try and only gets full portions of what he likes.
moral_consequence: James has so much fun trying different foods that he recommends the buffet to all his friends.
immoral_action: James takes ample servings of many different foods and leaves each plate three-quarters full.
immoral_consequence: The waitstaff eventually ask James to leave the restaurant to control their food costs.

norm: You should compromise over disagreements
situation: Bill likes going to the bar but his friend wants to go to the dance club.
intention: Bill wants to go to the bar.
moral_action: Bill says they should meet in the middle and go to the bar for a bit then over to the dance club.
moral_consequence: Bill's friend is satisfied with the agreement that takes both their wants into consideration.
immoral_action: Bill says to his friend that if they won't agree to come to the bar with bill they can just go dancing on their own.
immoral_consequence: Bill's friend feels like bill is really selfish for not considering what they want.

norm: It's good to do chores on time.
situation: Jake's spouse tells Jake the house needs to be cleaned before tomorrow for a party.
intention: Jake wants to clean the house.
moral_action: Jake decides to clean the house seven hours early and finishes.
moral_consequence: The house is extremely clean, while Jake and Jake's spouse are thrilled.
immoral_action: Jake waits until right before the party to clean and barely manages to finish.
immoral_consequence: Jake's spouse gets angry at Jake for procrastinating, and they all feel stressed out.

norm: It's wrong for merchants to deceive customers.
situation: Peter is selling used appliances and has some customers looking at a dishwasher that he knows doesn't work properly.
intention: Peter wants to make the sale.
moral_action: Peter admits that the dishwasher has issues and offers to give the customers a large enough discount to cover repairs.
moral_consequence: The customers are grateful for the offer, buy the dishwasher, and become loyal customers of Peter's.
immoral_action: Peter tells the customers that the dishwasher works great and guarantees they'll like it.
immoral_consequence: The customers find out that the dishwasher doesn't work later that night and leave Peter a terrible Yelp review.

norm: It's rude to risk someone's academic standing by copying them.
situation: Larry has a big exam coming up in school that is notoriously long.
intention: Larry wants to complete the exam on time.
moral_action: Larry does practice exams to make sure he is within the time limit.
moral_consequence: Larry completes the exam on time and the other students are not bothered.
immoral_action: Larry looks for a student that is ahead of him in the exam and writes what they write on the exam.
immoral_consequence: Larry and the other student get in trouble with the Professor.

norm: It's good to reimburse people.
situation: Mark owes Charlie $20 for dinner.
intention: Mark wants to save money for a new video game.
moral_action: Mark pays Charlie the money he owes him and works extra hours for money to buy the video game.
moral_consequence: Charlie is happy that Mark repaid him.
immoral_action: Mark avoids Charlie in the future and buys a video game with the money he owes Charlie.
immoral_consequence: Charlie is offended that Mark won't pay him and decides to not be friends with Mark anymore.

norm: You shouldn't make people do things.
situation: Dudley bossed around all his friends because his parents employed all of their parents.
intention: Dudley wants to have fun with his friends.
moral_action: Dudley decides to make it as fun as possible to be one of his friends.
moral_consequence: Dudley's friends have lots of fun and want to hang out at Dudley's house more.
immoral_action: Dudley continued making all of his friends do all his chores and lose every game to him.
immoral_consequence: "The moment their parents get new jobs, Dudley's ""friends"" would vanish."

norm: It's wrong for a teacher to help a student cheat.
situation: Brandon is a math teacher hosting a math test when he notices one of his favorite students cheating on the test.
intention: Brandon wants to respond to the cheating.
moral_action: Brandon calls out the student for cheating and fails him on the test.
moral_consequence: Brandon starts getting viewed as someone with integrity and should be respected.
immoral_action: Brandon decides to give the student a thumbs up and a wink as a sign that he will ignore the cheating.
immoral_consequence: Brandon gets fired after the other students discover and report his behavior.

norm: It's wrong to give people false hope in a relationship.
situation: Haley is in a relationship with Justin, and he has been hinting at wanting to move in together but Haley has been unhappy with Justin lately.
intention: Haley doesn't want to hurt Justin.
moral_action: Haley tells Justin that their relationship was no longer working and that she doesn't want to waste his time any more than she already has.
moral_consequence: Justin is upset but is able to move on and find someone who he is more compatible with.
immoral_action: Haley gives Justin non-commital answers about moving in together and makes it sound like she seriously thinking about it.
immoral_consequence: Justin eventually grows frustrated with Haley's indecisiveness, and they have a huge fight.

norm: It's not okay to lie or mislead other people.
situation: Jeff wants to sell his car and has found a buyer named Martin.
intention: Jeff wants to convince Martin to buy the car.
moral_action: Jeff explains what issues the car might have and drops the price to make the deal more attractive.
moral_consequence: Martin buys the car for the reduced price and gets the issues serviced.
immoral_action: Jeff hides a lot of problems with the car from Martin and tells him it runs perfectly.
immoral_consequence: Martin buys the car and is furious when it breaks down the next day.

norm: It's wrong to not take someone at their word.
situation: After arguing about his continued friendship with an ex, Frank promises his wife Jan that he will cut off contact with his ex.
intention: Jan wants to make sure Frank will keep his word.
moral_action: Jan makes it clear how hurtful the situation was to her and her hope that he won't put her through that again.
moral_consequence: Frank is able to recognize the impact of his actions and so is able to keep to his promise.
immoral_action: Jan insists on monitoring Franks phone and email for a few months to make sure he cuts off all contact.
immoral_consequence: Frank is hurt by Jan's lack of faith and their relationship suffers as a consequence.

norm: It's wrong to break things that don't belong to you.
situation: Jane is at a house party being hosted by a co-worker she doesn't like too much.
intention: Jane wants to enjoy herself.
moral_action: Jane spends the evening mingling with the co-workers she does like and eventually finds a group that wants to leave the party and socialize elsewhere.
moral_consequence: Jane is able to enjoy her evening without ruining anybody else's.
immoral_action: Jane is in a room of the house all by herself and thinks it will feel good to break a vase belonging to the host, so she does.
immoral_consequence: Jane is spotted by another party goer and becomes wrought with guilt and fear.

norm: It's not okay to leave school early.
situation: Mike is walking in between classes.
intention: Mike wants to have fun.
moral_action: Mike makes a game out of counting down the minutes until school ends.
moral_consequence: Time passes quickly and Mike barely notices his time left in school.
immoral_action: Mike ducks out the door and leaves school early.
immoral_consequence: Mike misses an important test and fails one of his classes.

norm: You shouldn't give a fake credit card to people.
situation: Larry's lawnmower has broke, and he went to a store to buy a new one.
intention: Larry wants to pay using a credit card.
moral_action: Larry pays for his new lawnmower with his new credit card.
moral_consequence: Larry has a new, working lawnmower.
immoral_action: Larry pays for his new lawnmower with a fake credit card linked to a stolent identity.
immoral_consequence: Larry's fake credit card is discovered, and he is arrested for fraud.

norm: It's illegal to jaywalk.
situation: Tyler is walking to the store and is approaching an intersection to cross the street.
intention: Tyler wants to cross the street to reach the store.
moral_action: Tyler is approaching the intersection crosswalk and continues until he reaches the light to cross the street.
moral_consequence: Tyler crosses the street legally and makes it to the store without issue.
immoral_action: Tyler is approaching the intersection crosswalk and decides to cross the street before it to save walking time.
immoral_consequence: A cop sees Tyler illegally jaywalk and stops him to give a ticket for the crime and Tyler continues to the store.

norm: You shouldn't punish an employee for voicing concerns.
situation: James, concerned by the difficulties people were having with the new software system, asked his employees what specifically was causing all the problems.
intention: James wants people to work all day without being distracted by computer problems.
moral_action: James makes a list of everyone having problems and hires a computer skills instructor to retrain them.
moral_consequence: The employees learn how to handle the new software and productivity goes way up.
immoral_action: James fires anyone who says the software is confusing, on the grounds that they must be technically illiterate.
immoral_consequence: While the remaining employees can solve all their own computer problems, there's half as many of them, so they get half as much work done.

norm: It's wrong to lie to people about movies you've seen.
situation: Ryan is chatting with and getting to know an attractive girl he met on the internet.
intention: Ryan wants to peak the girl's interest.
moral_action: Ryan asks about what movies she likes and expresses a genuine interest in seeing them sometime.
moral_consequence: The girl invites Ryan over to watch the movies with her since she owns them.
immoral_action: Ryan asks about her favorite movies and acts like he also enjoyed those movies despite not having watched them.
immoral_consequence: The girl later finds out that he has not actually seen the movies and loses interest.

norm: You shouldn't BS people.
situation: Ted is at a party and just met a woman he finds very attractive.
intention: Ted wants the woman to like him.
moral_action: Ted is attentive and funny and talks simply about the things he likes, without exaggerating.
moral_consequence: The woman is impressed by Ted's charm and easygoing way.
immoral_action: Ted starts telling bogus stories to the woman about how he earns a lot of money and is famous.
immoral_consequence: The woman is impressed but feels overwhelmed by Ted's achievements.

norm: It is bad to violate workplace and company policies
situation: Jim has a major deadline coming up in the morning, and he needs several more hours to finish it.
intention: Jim wants to complete the assignment before 9:00 PM that evening.
moral_action: Jim stays at the company late and works until he finishes the job.
moral_consequence: Jim goes home tired but happy that he was able to complete the work without having to take his laptop home.
immoral_action: Jim takes his laptop home to work on the assignment, even though it is against company policy to take your laptop home.
immoral_consequence: Jim accidentally damages his laptop at home and is dismissed from his job for breaking company rules.

norm: You shouldn't be upset at your deceased family member's partner.
situation: Juliet's father passed recently, and she is at her step mother's house visiting.
intention: Juliet wants to ask about some heirlooms of her father's.
moral_action: Juliet inquires about the heirlooms of her father's and asks if her step mother still has them.
moral_consequence: Juliet's step mother is touched that Juliet would want to know about the heirlooms that also mean a lot to her.
immoral_action: Juliet asks her step mother about the heirlooms while implying that she has kept them away from Juliet on purpose.
immoral_consequence: Juliet's step mother does not like the insinuation Juliet is making.

norm: It's wrong to sell items under false pretense.
situation: Jerry works as a car salesman and James has come in to buy a car.
intention: Jerry wants to sell James a car.
moral_action: Jerry sells James a car that he looking for at a reasonable price.
moral_consequence: James leaves the lot happy.
immoral_action: Jerry sells James a bad car with a very inflated price to make a profit, claiming that it is great.
immoral_consequence: James wastes his money and gets a bad car.

norm: It is good not to play favorites
situation: Tina runs a small deli and recently hired two of her sons to add to the staff.
intention: Tina wants to promote someone to supervisor.
moral_action: Tina promotes the worker with the most experience to the position of supervisor.
moral_consequence: The store's profits remain steady and the new supervisor teaches Tina's sons a lot about the business.
immoral_action: Tina promotes her son, despite knowing that he doesn't have very much experience in running a shop.
immoral_consequence: Tina's son manages the store poorly and sales decrease as a result of his ineptitude as a supervisor.

norm: It's wrong to take advantage of the community
situation: Megan is a single mother that often has financial issues, but recently won a large sum of cash.
intention: Megan wants to get better furniture for her home.
moral_action: Megan takes a small portion of her earnings to buy the furniture and declines help from the community group while she has extra cash.
moral_consequence: The community group tells Megan to let them know when she does need assistance, and they will be able to aid her then.
immoral_action: Megan is approached by a community group that helps single mothers that have financial struggles and Megan tells them she can't afford new furniture.
immoral_consequence: The community group finds out by the store clerk the amount of money Megan recently won and no longer will help her in the future after Megan's funds are depleted.

norm: It's not okay to judge people by their race.
situation: Jeff is sorting through a pile of job applications for a position in his tech department.
intention: Jeff wants to eliminate candidates he believes would be wrong for the position.
moral_action: Jeff screens applicants based on their ability and experience.
moral_consequence: Jeff is able to call in the best applicants for the job for an interview.
immoral_action: Jeff feels black people tend to be less tech savvy so he eliminates all applicants of African descent.
immoral_consequence: Jeff winds up with a department that lacks diversity.

norm: If it's wrong to falsely accuse somebody of something
situation: Bill is living in a dorm with a roommate and one day he could not find his wallet inside the dorm.
intention: Bill wants to talk to his roommate about his missing wallet.
moral_action: Bill asks his roommate about his lost wallet and sees if he knows anything.
moral_consequence: Bill's roommate tells Bill what he knows and decides to help Bill find the missing wallet.
immoral_action: Bill flat out accuses his roommate of taking his wallet.
immoral_consequence: Bill's roommate is extremely annoyed by the false accusation and plans to stop being roommates with Bill.

norm: You shouldn't skip lines.
situation: Harry was in the play, and he was reading his lines for his part.
intention: Harry wants to finish the preformance.
moral_action: Harry reads the script perfectly, making sure to memorize every single line.
moral_consequence: Harry puts on a great performance for the play and the crowd loves it.
immoral_action: Harry skips a line he considers of low importance in scene 2.
immoral_consequence: The other preformers miss the skipped line and instead reply to the skipped line.

norm: You shouldn't drive when locations are close enough to walk.
situation: Jim is hungry for a cheeseburger and there is a place just one block away.
intention: Jim wants to buy food from the nearby restaurant.
moral_action: Jim walks to the restaurant.
moral_consequence: Jim has benefited and protected the environment, even if slightly.
immoral_action: Jim drives his car to the nearby restaurant.
immoral_consequence: Jim hurts the environment with his actions.

norm: It's wrong to copy the answers from someone else's paper.
situation: Jen has been struggling with her math class and there is a big test coming up.
intention: Jen wants to do well on the test to improve her grade.
moral_action: Jen spends as much time as possible studying for the test.
moral_consequence: Jen's parents are proud of her when she aces the math test.
immoral_action: Jen relies on the person sitting next to her who is good at math and looks at his answers during the test.
immoral_consequence: Jen gets caught cheating on the test and her parents are notified.

norm: It is greedy to not let anyone use your stuff.
situation: Dora's roommate Kim has an interview in the morning and her iron broke, she knows Dora has an iron.
intention: Dora wants Kim to leave for the interview.
moral_action: Dora grabs her iron and lends it to Kim, wishing her well on her interview.
moral_consequence: Kim irons her outfit and her interviewer finds her appearance to be very professional.
immoral_action: Dora tells Kim that her iron is broken as well and that her outfit looks fine as is.
immoral_consequence: Kim's interviewer is unimpressed by the state of her clothes and holds it against her in the interview.

norm: You shouldn't bribe people.
situation: Phil just got a call informing him that his son is being held for suspicion of drunk driving.
intention: Phil wants to help his son.
moral_action: Phil calls a lawyer and hires him to represent his son.
moral_consequence: Phil's son learns a valuable lesson when he is made to pay for his crime.
immoral_action: Phil calls a friend on the police force and offers him season baseball tickets if he can get his son out of trouble.
immoral_consequence: Phil's son doesn't learn to fear the consequences of his actions and continues his reckless behaviour.

norm: It's nice to help others by sharing.
situation: Hugo had just ordered a pizza pie when his friend, Ben, comes over.
intention: Hugo wants to eat pizza.
moral_action: Hugo offers Ben some slices of his pizza.
moral_consequence: Ben takes two slices and promises to pay Hugo back.
immoral_action: Hugo doesn't offer Ben any pizza even though he suspects Ben may want some.
immoral_consequence: Hugo eats a slice of pizza and puts the rest away while Ben sneaks awkward glances at the pizza.

norm: Helping a person cheat on school work is wrong.
situation: Beth is hanging out with her friend Matt who asks her for help on a math test review assignment they have to complete for homework.
intention: Beth wants to help Matt with the homework.
moral_action: Beth spends time going over each test review question and letting Matt work out the answers himself.
moral_consequence: Matt gets a good grade on the math test because Beth prepared him well and didn't do the work for him.
immoral_action: Beth fills in the homework for Matt without teaching him what any of it means.
