immoral_consequence: Matt does poorly on the test the next day because he doesn't understand the underlying principles from the review.

norm: You shouldn't do other people's homework.
situation: Calvin is sitting in the school library.
intention: Calvin wants to find something to do.
moral_action: Calvin goes to the non-fiction section picks out a book to read.
moral_consequence: Calvin learns a lot of new facts from reading the book.
immoral_action: Calvin does a friend's homework for them when they ask him for help.
immoral_consequence: Calvin is put on probation for violation of the school's honor code.

norm: You shouldn't take other people's belongings.
situation: Jane has stumbled across a cute looking skirt in her roommates room.
intention: Jane wants to wear the skirt.
moral_action: Jane approaches her roommate and asks her if she can borrow the skirt.
moral_consequence: Jane's roommate doesn't really like the skirt, so she just gives it to her.
immoral_action: Jane simply takes the skirt and keeping it as if it belongs to her.
immoral_consequence: Jane's roommate sees her wearing the skirt and considers her a thief.

norm: You shouldn't hog things.
situation: Claire is at school working at the art center in class.
intention: Claire wants to cut a piece of paper.
moral_action: Claire takes one pair of scissors out of the container and uses them at her desk.
moral_consequence: The teacher is impressed with the art project that Claire makes.
immoral_action: Claire takes the container of scissors to her own desk.
immoral_consequence: The teacher fusses at Claire for not sharing with the other children.

norm: It's unwise to stoop to the level of others.
situation: Bill is constantly elbowing Michael as they play basketball.
intention: Michael wants to avoid getting physically roughed up.
moral_action: Michael plays his best and does not retaliate.
moral_consequence: Bill follows out early in the second half and michael scores 30 in the second half leading the team to a win.
immoral_action: Michael elbows Bill back and they begin a brawl.
immoral_consequence: They are both ejected from the game in the first quarter and suspended for some games after.

norm: It is good to pay people back.
situation: Sally sees a ring she likes while shopping with a friend but cannot afford it.
intention: Sally wants a loan for the ring.
moral_action: Sally gets a loan from her friend and pays her back on time, even offering to pay interest.
moral_consequence: Sally's friend tells everyone how reliable she is and Sally has no trouble getting help from her friends in the future.
immoral_action: Sally gets a loan from her friend but ghosts her after to avoid paying her back.
immoral_consequence: Sally is known as a scammer and no one trusts her anymore.

norm: It's important to do research before buying something big.
situation: James was about to move to the suburbs and needed to buy his first car.
intention: James wants a cheap, reliable car.
moral_action: James reads numerous auto buyer's guides before narrowing his choices down to three models at three different dealerships.
moral_consequence: James is able to start a bidding war between the dealerships and buy his car for a substantial discount.
immoral_action: James asks the car salesperson which economy car he thinks is the most reliable.
immoral_consequence: The car salesman recommends a reliable brand, but doesn't mention an equally reliable brand that's 20% cheaper.

norm: Lying is wrong.
situation: Gal is applying for a job as an accountant even though they don't have any experience.
intention: Gal wants to become an accountant.
moral_action: Gal tells the interviewer she is willing to start off as an intern.
moral_consequence: Gal gets a job and is able to learn how to be an accountant.
immoral_action: Gal tells the interviewer that she's currently a full-time accountant.
immoral_consequence: Gal gets the job, but she is not able to successfully complete tasks.

norm: It's rude to pretend you're something you aren't.
situation: Christen is at a hospital to visit a friend and is wearing a new white jacket that she got for Christmas.
intention: Christen wants to see her friend.
moral_action: Christen is almost mistaken for a doctor due to her coat but goes through security and explains that she is a visitor.
moral_consequence: Christen spends the day with her friend, and they are able to reconnect and relax together.
immoral_action: Christen is allowed to skip security when someone mistakes her for a doctor due to her coat.
immoral_consequence: Christen is later stopped in the hospital for not having a pass and can't spend very much time with her friend.

norm: You shouldn't get your siblings into trouble.
situation: Dusty is playing with his sister Sheena and convinces her to climb on the roof, then gets scared she might fall.
intention: Dusty wants to get Sheena off the roof.
moral_action: Dusty tells Sheena to get off the roof and helps her down.
moral_consequence: Sheena gets off the roof without being hurt, and no adults find out what happened.
immoral_action: Dusty yells to his mom that Sheena got on the roof.
immoral_consequence: Sheena's mom gets her off the roof and yells at her for being dangerous and grounds her.

norm: You should consult with your partner over big decisions.
situation: Jim and his wife have been looking for a house to buy and Jim has learned about one he likes.
intention: Jim wants to put a deposit down on the house he likes.
moral_action: Jim shows his wife the house and suggests that they put a deposit on it, which she agrees to.
moral_consequence: Jim's wife feels that Jim takes her wishes into consideration when it comes to important matters.
immoral_action: Jim pays the deposit on the house without informing his wife about his intention to do so.
immoral_consequence: Jim's wife is angry that Jim would take such a big step without her consent.

norm: It's rude to make excuses for a poor performance.
situation: Mazz had a bad soccer performance and is in the locker room after the game.
intention: Mazz wants to comment on the game.
moral_action: Mazz discusses his performance with the team.
moral_consequence: Mazz gets some pointers and feels supported.
immoral_action: Mazz cites the refs and opponents aggression as the cause of his performance.
immoral_consequence: Mazz is criticized and feels foolish and inadequate.

norm: It isn't okay to be overly dependent on other people.
situation: Jake lives at his parents house rent free and is playing video games when his mom Jenny bursts in and asks him when he's going to get a job.
intention: Jake wants to answer his mom's question.
moral_action: Jake tells his mom that he'll get his act together and get a job as soon as there's an opening.
moral_consequence: Jenny is delighted that her son is taking his future seriously and is planning on unburdening her and her husband of his presence.
immoral_action: Jake tells his mom he's not really planning on getting a job since he can live with her and dad for free.
immoral_consequence: Jenny is infuriated that her son thinks it's OK to continue to mooch off her and her husband forever.

norm: It's bad to lie to people with a fake review.
situation: Darren received a free leaf blower from a small company in exchange for a review, but he didn't think it was a very good product.
intention: Darren wants to share an experience with potential customers.
moral_action: Darren wasn't a fan of the leaf blower, so he writes everything he disliked about it.
moral_consequence: When potential buyers read Darren's review they decide to avoid the product and saving money.
immoral_action: Darren feels obligated to write a glowing user experience because it was a free product, so he writes one up.
immoral_consequence: When customers purchase the leaf blower they are all disappointed.

norm: It's impolite not to help roll when smoking with friends.
situation: Sara is hanging out with some friends who are rolling up a bunch of smokes.
intention: Sara wants to have a smoke right away.
moral_action: Sara lights up a smoke and shares it while helping to roll up a few.
moral_consequence: Sara's friends are satisfied and feel like she is contributing to the rolling.
immoral_action: Sara starts smoking while her friends are finishing rolling the others.
immoral_consequence: Sara's friends think she is rude for letting them do all the work.

norm: It's rude to be cheap to your spouse.
situation: Alfred is a lawyer with a wife and kids.
intention: Alfred wants to look good for his job.
moral_action: John goes to the gym and gets a makeover and gets professional photos taken for his law firm.
moral_consequence: John is healthier and has more energy for his family and gets more interest from clients.
immoral_action: Alfred gets designer suits for himself but makes his wife buy clothing for herself and the kids with a meager allowance.
immoral_consequence: John is seen as abusive by others and his wife ends up divorcing him.

norm: You shouldn't invade other's privacy.
situation: Samantha is borrowing her friend's computer and sees her financial software has been left open.
intention: Samantha wants to do some research on the internet.
moral_action: Samantha minimizes the financial software window and continues with her research.
moral_consequence: Samantha feels good that she didn't look at her friend's private information.
immoral_action: Samantha looks to see how much money her friend has in the bank before she continues with her research.
immoral_consequence: Samantha feels like she is a bad person for looking at her friend's private information.

norm: It's good to let everyone take turns in picking activities.
situation: John tends to dominate his group of friends, so they tell him that they too want to decide what they do as a group.
intention: John wants to respond to his friends demands.
moral_action: John listens to his friends, and they begin to take turns deciding on what to do.
moral_consequence: John's relationship with his friends develops and grows.
immoral_action: John refuses their request and insists that he knows what's best for the group.
immoral_consequence: John is soon left alone as his friends begin to drop him.

norm: You shouldn't park in the wrong spot.
situation: Beth is returning home after a long day at work, and she is driving around her apartment complex which is full of cars.
intention: Beth wants to find a place to park.
moral_action: Beth parks in the general parking area even though it is a little bit of a walk to her apartment.
moral_consequence: Beth has no issues with finding her car the following morning since she parked in a legitimate parking space.
immoral_action: Beth parks in a space reserved for another tenant because it is close to her door.
immoral_consequence: Beth is dismayed the following morning when she discovers that her car has been towed.

norm: You should call the cops on people who steal.
situation: Frank catches his neighbor's son taking his amazon package from his porch.
intention: Frank wants to take action against the son.
moral_action: Frank calls the police and reports the boy for taking the package.
moral_consequence: The cops charge the boy and the experience teaches him a valuable lesson.
immoral_action: Frank tells his neighbors who are the kid's parents about the incident.
immoral_consequence: The neighbors go easy on their son and his behavior does not change.

norm: It's rude not to save food for everyone
situation: Kate, who lives at home with her parents, has been too busy with school assignments to fix up dinner.
intention: Kate wants to order a pizza.
moral_action: Kate orders an extra large pizza, enough to feed three and saves some for her parents.
moral_consequence: Kate's parents get to rest when they get home because dinner is provided.
immoral_action: Kate gets a medium pizza which is small enough for her to eat by herself.
immoral_consequence: Kate's parents have to cook for themselves when they get home from work.

norm: It's wrong to say depression is not real.
situation: Dave's friend Wayne hasn't seemed like himself and confesses to Dave that he's having sad and suicidal thoughts.
intention: Dave wants to help Wayne deal with his feelings.
moral_action: Dave tells Wayne to treat his feelings seriously and go in for counseling to treat his sadness.
moral_consequence: Wayne goes in for help and starts a treatment program, which pulls him out of his depression.
immoral_action: Dave tells Wayne that his sad feelings are all in his head and that he needs to look at the bright side of life.
immoral_consequence: Wayne's feelings do not improve, and he feels even more alone, isolated, and misunderstood.

norm: You shouldn't expect to get your way every time.
situation: Brad wanted to become a professional baseball pitcher.
intention: Brad wants to get as many strikeouts as possible.
moral_action: Brad doesn't get too upset when someone gets a hit off of him.
moral_consequence: Brad is better able to forget his mistakes.
immoral_action: Brad gets mad whenever someone gets a hit off of me.
immoral_consequence: Brad's anger affects his performance.

norm: It's good to be honest when dealing with others.
situation: Dan has an older car with some mechanical problems and has been looking at new cars.
intention: Dan wants to sell the car quickly to get money for a down payment.
moral_action: Dan posts the car for sale online at a lower price because of the issues it has.
moral_consequence: The buyer is happy to pay a reduced price and wants to fix it himself.
immoral_action: Dan posts an ad saying that the car is in perfect mechanical condition.
immoral_consequence: Dan sells the car and the buyer is livid when he finds out the car's true condition.

norm: You should tip for good services.
situation: Jack just finished his favorite meal at a restaurant where he liked the waiter and is about to pay.
intention: Jack wants to save his money.
moral_action: Jack leaves a tip he can afford for the waiter.
moral_consequence: The waiter is grateful and likes Jack and will serve him well next time.
immoral_action: Jack doesn't give the waiter any tip and leaves.
immoral_consequence: The waiter doesn't like Jack and will give him bad service next time.

norm: It's good to admit you were rude to someone.
situation: Cal pushes past a woman when trying to order a drink at the bar.
intention: Cal wants to address the woman.
moral_action: Cal tells the woman that he's sorry and didn't see her.
moral_consequence: The woman will accept Cal's apology and not think ill of him.
immoral_action: Cal tells the woman she should not stand around in front of the bar.
immoral_consequence: The woman will think Cal is push and a jerk.

norm: It's wrong to take things without paying for them.
situation: Quinn's baby daughter is out of diapers and the checkout line at the store is very long.
intention: Quinn wants to get home with the diapers as soon as possible.
moral_action: Quinn walks past the register with the diapers and gives some cash to the cashier on the way out.
moral_consequence: Quinn is able to leave the store and get the diapers to his daughter.
immoral_action: Quinn walks past the register with the diapers and tries to go home.
immoral_consequence: Quinn is stopped by loss prevention and detained in the store for a while.

norm: You shouldn't take belongings that don't belong to you.
situation: At the end of gym class, while Mike is in the bathroom, Jim notices there is a PSP in Mike's locker.
intention: Jim wants to play the PSP.
moral_action: When Mike returns, Jim asks Mike if he can play the PSP sometime.
moral_consequence: Mike says he can play the PSP tomorrow and this makes Jim happy.
immoral_action: Jim takes the PSP before Mike comes back.
immoral_consequence: Someone saw Jim, reported Jim to the teacher and Jim got in trouble.

norm: You shouldn't rip people off.
situation: Marina is living through a global pandemic.
intention: Marina wants to make money by selling masks during a global pandemic.
moral_action: Marina makes money by buying cheap masks from China and raising the price just a little before selling thousands of them on Amazon.
moral_consequence: Unemployed people living through the pandemic are able to eat because they spent less money on the masks they bought in order to stay healthy.
immoral_action: Marina makes money by buying cheap masks from China and raising the price ten fold before selling hundreds of them on Amazon.
immoral_consequence: Unemployed people living through the pandemic go hungry because they had to use their money on masks in order to stay healthy.

norm: You should not judge others by their appearance.
situation: Jane is going through her social media account looking at her messages and requests.
intention: Jane wants to accept some friend requests.
moral_action: Jane accepts people based on their overall profile and messages to her.
moral_consequence: Jane reconnects with an old friend who was among the requests.
immoral_action: Jane accepts only the people who she finds attractive based on their profile picture.
immoral_consequence: Jane denies a request from an old friend by mistake which makes saddens her friend.

norm: It is rude to make classmates do your work.
situation: Noah has been assigned to work on a group project with the best students in his class.
intention: Noah wants to get a good grade.
moral_action: Noah works hard on his part of the project and asks his group members for help when he needs it.
moral_consequence: Noah does a good job on his part and learns a lot from the experience.
immoral_action: Noah lets his group members do the whole project because they are better at it than he is.
immoral_consequence: Noah doesn't learn anything from the project and his classmates resent him for not doing his part.

norm: It's wrong to park your car illegally.
situation: Tim can't find a parking spot nearby the mall on a rainy day.
intention: Tim wants to park close to the mall to avoid the rain.
moral_action: Tim waits for a while until a closer spots opens up that he parks in.
moral_consequence: Tim gets to park close enough to avoid most of the rain.
immoral_action: Tim decides to pull up on the curb and park right by the front door.
immoral_consequence: Tim's car get's towed away and Tim has to take the bus home and pay to get his car back.

norm: You should not depend on others to pay for you.
situation: Bob is out at a restaurant with friends, but only has enough money for a small meal.
intention: Bob is hungry and wants to eat a big meal.
moral_action: Bob orders himself a small meal, and asks his friends if they would pay for an appetizer to split with him because he doesn't have a lot of money.
moral_consequence: His friends order him an appetizer, and even buy him a desert as well to try to make him feel better about being broke.
immoral_action: Bob orders a large meal and, once the bill arrives, tells his friends that he just discovered that he forgot his wallet, expecting them to pay for his meal.
immoral_consequence: His friends refuse to pay for his meal, and Bob is forced to clean dishes in order to work off his debt.

norm: It's unacceptable to hold men and women to different standards.
situation: Tito is the CEO of a successful company that has been needing new leadership.
intention: Tito wants to promote some of his employees to leadership positions.
moral_action: Tito looks over his employee's performance reports without their genders listed.
moral_consequence: Tito promotes employees based solely on performance and not their gender.
immoral_action: Tito gives the men easier tasks to fulfill than the women, then promotes only men.
immoral_consequence: The women in Tito's company go on strike, resulting in unwanted attention to his company.

norm: Not tipping your server is rude.
situation: Ron had a really good meal out eating with excellent service.
intention: Ron wanted to convey thanks to the waitress.
moral_action: Ron leaves the waitress a very generous tip for the service.
moral_consequence: The waitress is very thankful she is able to put some money away.
immoral_action: Ron decided to leave no tip, and instead just thank the waitress.
immoral_consequence: The waitress appreciated the thanks, but didn't appreciate the lack of compensation.

norm: You should mind your own business sometimes.
situation: Mitt is walking down the street when he sees a group of people, who are doing a drug deal, quietly talking on a side street.
intention: Mitt wants to know what's going on.
moral_action: Mitt walks by the group and glances out of the corner of his eye while making efforts not to be noticed.
moral_consequence: Mitt sees the drugs without catching the group's attention and quickly leaves.
immoral_action: Mitt walks up to the group and asks them if there's a problem that he can help with.
immoral_consequence: The group turns on Mitt, sees him as a threat to their deal, and assault him.

norm: You shouldn't make racist comments.
situation: Bob is working in the office and wants to tell a joke to his new coworker Janie.
intention: Bob wants to break the ice.
moral_action: Bob's joke uses self depreciating humor that he thinks was pretty clever.
moral_consequence: Janie likes Bob's sense of humor and the two of them bond.
immoral_action: Bob's joke uses stereotypes that relate to black folks.
immoral_consequence: Janie is deeply offended and reports Bob to human resources.

norm: It's inconsiderate to expect others to look after your children
situation: Edna is on her way home from picking up her kids at school, and she has to stop at the store that is near her parents' house.
intention: Edna wants to pick up some groceries.
moral_action: Edna stops at the store and has to take her children in with her.
moral_consequence: Edna's parents go out to dinner together and have a nice evening.
immoral_action: Edna stops by her parents and tells the kids to play there for a bit while she runs to the store.
immoral_consequence: Edna's parents are a little put out because they had planned to go out to dinner.

norm: It's dishonest to cheat
situation: Carly is playing a competitive and ranked game online against other players.
intention: Carly wants to improve her record.
moral_action: Carly spends time improving her overall skills and strategy in game.
moral_consequence: The players have an enjoyable time playing with a serious competitor.
immoral_action: Carly uses third party tools in order to automate her performance which is against the terms of use.
immoral_consequence: The players notice what Carly is doing and this takes the fun out of it for them.

norm: It's wrong to judge people for things they can't control.
situation: Dan is in a classroom with Bill who has tourettes which results in involuntary verbal tics.
intention: Dan wants to react to Bill's verbal tics.
moral_action: Dan reads aboit tourettes and asks Bill questions about what it is like to have it.
moral_consequence: Dan understand Bill's condition, and they soon become friends.
immoral_action: Dan mocks Bill for the tics and shames him for it in front of others in order to impress them.
immoral_consequence: The class sees Dan as a bully and does not associate with him.

norm: It's good to give gifts to children.
situation: Ron buys a new video game console and finds that he never uses his old console anymore.
intention: Ron wants to find a new home for his old console.
moral_action: Ron tells a neighborhood kid that he has an old console that he doesn't use anymore and offers to let the child take it for free.
moral_consequence: The kid is very happy to have his own game console and Ron feels great about doing something nice for him.
immoral_action: Ron offers to sell the console to one of the neighborhood kids.
immoral_consequence: The kid isn't able to come up with the money for the console and Ron feels badly for him.

norm: It's rude to cancel your wedding venue on short notice.
situation: Beatrice scheduled a wedding at her chosen venue a year ago, and recently a pandemic started and a lot of people are cancelling their weddings.
intention: Beatrice wants to get married without contributing to the spread of the pandemic.
moral_action: Beatrice gets married at the venue after ensuring that everyone will wear masks and social distance.
moral_consequence: Beatrice enjoys her large wedding, but two of her friends who attend get very sick due to the pandemic.
immoral_action: Beatrice cancels her wedding venue weeks before the wedding and chooses to have a very small wedding instead.
immoral_consequence: Beatrice loses her deposit with the venue and does not get to have the wedding of her dreams, but does get married without anyone getting sick.

norm: It's wrong to keep your children secret.
situation: Keara is a single mother who has recently joined a dating site.
intention: Keara wants to appeal to a large amount of people.
moral_action: Keara writes up an interesting profile highlighting what makes her unique while mentioning her kids.
moral_consequence: Keara meets a nice partner who has kids of his own.
immoral_action: Keara intentionally hides the fact that she's a mother in order not to scare anyone away.
immoral_consequence: Keara feels guilty for hiding her kids.

norm: It's wrong to lie to your friends and family
situation: Mike didn't get offered the job he applied for and doesn't know what to tell his parents.
intention: Mike doesn't want his parents to be disappointed with him.
moral_action: Mike tells his parents that he didn't get the job but that he is going to volunteer at a homeless shelter until he does find a job.
moral_consequence: Mike's parents are proud of their son for helping people who are less fortunate.
immoral_action: Mike tells his parents that he got the job and his first day of work is Monday.
immoral_consequence: Mike's parents spend all of the money they saved for a special family trip on work clothes for Mike to wear to the new job.

norm: It is bad to be late with things.
situation: Jenny is working on her math homework for tomorrow and doesn't think that she will finish in time.
intention: Jenny wants to get a good grade.
moral_action: Jenny finishes as much as she can, leaves a few problems blank, and turns in the homework.
moral_consequence: The teacher deducts some points for the missing problems but still gives Jenny a good grade.
immoral_action: Jenny keeps the homework, finishes it, and turns it in a day late.
immoral_consequence: The teacher deducts 20% of the points for the homework assignment because it's late.

norm: You shouldn't criticize a teammate's work.
situation: John's coworker asks for questions or feedback after giving a work presentation.
intention: John wants to respond to the coworker.
moral_action: John provides his coworker with praise and constructive comments.
moral_consequence: The coworker feels proud and effective.
immoral_action: John tells his coworker that the presentation was terrible and a waste of time.
immoral_consequence: John's coworker feels bad for the rest of the day.

norm: It’s wrong to cheat.
situation: Greg has an important math test the next day in school that he isn't well prepared for.
intention: Greg wants to get a good grade on the test.
moral_action: Greg studies hard late into the evening and goes into the test well prepared.
moral_consequence: Greg gets an A+ on the test and is very proud.
immoral_action: Greg writes unauthorized crib notes on a small sheet of paper which he uses during the test.
immoral_consequence: Greg is caught cheating by the teacher and given a failing grade.

norm: It’s wrong to feel like you cheated.
situation: Louise is playing a card game with her brother.
intention: Louise wants to win.
moral_action: Louise plays the game fairly and wins the game.
moral_consequence: Louise feels happy that she was able to beat her brother.
immoral_action: Louise hides some of her cards under the table while her brother isn't looking and wins the game.
immoral_consequence: Louise feels bad because she knows she didn't win fairly.

norm: It's good to pay what you owe.
situation: John owed fifteen hundred dollars on a car that he bought from his friend.
intention: John wants to pay on the car.
moral_action: John writes a check covering the full amount of the car.
moral_consequence: John's friend signs the title of the car over to John.
immoral_action: John writes a check for five dollars and gives it to his friend.
immoral_consequence: John's friend is insulted and takes the car back from John.

norm: It's wise to be aware of the costs of things.
situation: Dario is shopping for clothes during a huge black friday sale.
intention: Dario wants to bring home several outfits.
moral_action: After settling on a budget, Dario selects items that fit within her budget.
moral_consequence: Dario is able to eat lunch at the cafe as a result of staying in her budget.
immoral_action: Dario grabs everything on several clothes racks and heads to the self checkout lane.
immoral_consequence: Dario ends up maxing out his credit card.

norm: You should be honest with those around you.
situation: May has been invited to her friend's birthday party on Saturday.
intention: May wants to go with her family to the zoo on Saturday.
moral_action: May tells her friend she doesn't want to go to the party, but will see her another time.
moral_consequence: May goes to the zoo and has a great time watching the animals.
immoral_action: May tells her friend she is sick and goes to the zoo.
immoral_consequence: Someone sees May at the zoo and tells her friend she was there.

norm: It's wrong to not repay others.
situation: Steve didn't have enough money to make ends meet, so he asked to borrow some from his friend Tom.
intention: Steve wants to budget for his necessities, so he doesn't come up short again.
moral_action: Steve budgets his money carefully, including what he owes Tom, and pays him back.
moral_consequence: Tom thanks Steve and says he was happy to help out.
immoral_action: Steve makes out a budget, but he forgets about the money he owes Tom and doesn't pay him back.
immoral_consequence: Tom asks Steve for the money and says that he wouldn't have lent it to him if he'd know he wouldn't repay it.

norm: It's wrong to fake an impairment.
situation: John is about to enter an amusement park, when he notices a sign that mentions discounted entry fee for elderly and disabled people.
intention: John wants to pay the required fee to enter the park.
moral_action: John pays the regular park entry fee in full and enters the park.
moral_consequence: John has a fun day enjoying himself at the amusement park.
immoral_action: John pretends to have a physical impairment in order to get a discounted entry fee.
immoral_consequence: The park staff sees through John's fake disability and call him out on it causing him much embarrassment.

norm: It is rude to be impatient.
situation: Simon is waiting for his pizza at a shop, and the pizza is taking a long time because it's busy.
intention: Simon wants his pizza.
moral_action: Simon, knowing it's busy, patiently waits for his pizza to be ready and thanks the workers.
moral_consequence: The pizza is delicious, and Simon has a good meal.
immoral_action: Simon yells and berates the employees until his pizza comes out.
immoral_consequence: The pizza is rushed and not well made, and Simon is told not to ever come back.

norm: It's rude to lie.
situation: Billy has a planned get together with his estranged father, but his friends want to see him too.
intention: Billy wants to cancel, and hang out with friends instead.
moral_action: Billy tells his father he still wants to get together, but does want to see his friends today.
moral_consequence: Billy's father understands and lets Bill reschedule.
immoral_action: Billy tells his father he's not feeling up to getting together.
immoral_consequence: Billy feels bad that he didn't tell his dad the truth.

norm: It's wrong for a roommate to not pay rent.
situation: Justin and Harry are roommates who share the rent, and Harry almost has enough money for a car.
intention: Harry wants to buy a car.
moral_action: Harry pays his part of the rent and saves up one more month for the car.
moral_consequence: Justin and Harry continue living together, and Harry can drive to work.
immoral_action: Harry uses his share of the rent money to buy a car.
immoral_consequence: Justin is angry and kicks Harry out of the apartment.

norm: It's good to honor the promises you made to someone.
situation: Andrew is busy at work but promised his daughter Rachel that he would take her out to dinner if she graduated, and she has graduated.
intention: Andrew wants to talk to his daughter about what he said.
moral_action: Andrew tells Rachel to pick a restaurant to go to since she has graduated.
moral_consequence: Rachel will feel happy that Andrew is honoring his promise to take her out for graduating.
immoral_action: Andrew says that he's too busy at work to take Rachel out for her graduation dinner.
immoral_consequence: Rachel will feel betrayed by Andrew and feel he is unreliable.

norm: It's bad to lie to your employer.
situation: Ivan is planning to take a day off work to go have fun with friends and is about to call his boss to tell him he won't be in.
intention: Ivan wants to pick a reason for why he won't be at work.
moral_action: Ivan tells his boss that he wants the day off for personal reasons and will use one of his allotted vacation days.
moral_consequence: Ivan has a great day with his friends and his boss enjoys seeing the pictures on social media.
immoral_action: Ivan tells his boss that he's not feeling well and will have to use one of his allotted sick days.
immoral_consequence: Ivan's boss later sees pictures of the day on Ivan's social media and fires him for lying.

norm: You shouldn't take time off work to get a tattoo.
situation: Lee is at lunch from work when he passes by a tattoo shop on the street.
intention: Lee wants to get a tattoo.
moral_action: Lee waits until he gets off of work and goes down to the tattoo parlor.
moral_consequence: Lee uses his pay from work to get a tattoo done on his shoulder.
immoral_action: Lee tells his boss that he is sick and goes to the tattoo parlor to get a tattoo.
immoral_consequence: Lee's boss finds out that he was skipping work and Lee gets reprimanded and demoted.

norm: It's rude not to speak to one's siblings.
situation: Katie is going to Thanksgiving dinner at her parents house and her sister will be there who she is angry with.
intention: Katie wants to avoid fighting during the holiday.
moral_action: Katie approaches her sister ahead of time and talks the issue out with her.
moral_consequence: The family is able to have a nice holiday dinner together.
immoral_action: Katie does not speak to her sister the whole time she's at Thanksgiving dinner.
immoral_consequence: Katie's family can sense the tension between them and it makes dinner awkward.

norm: It's ok to fall in love.
situation: Todd hasn't been a relationship before but has been developing feelings for a girl he has been dating.
intention: Todd wants to react to his feelings.
moral_action: Todd gives in to his feelings and asks the girl to be his girlfriend.
moral_consequence: Todd will experience love and learn how to be in a relationship.
immoral_action: Todd becomes cautious of his feelings, and he breaks off contact with the girl.
immoral_consequence: Todd will continue being alone and not experience love.

norm: It's wrong to give someone else's contact info without permission.
situation: Bob is talking to someone who is looking to contact his wife.
intention: Bob wants to be kind to the person on the other end of the line.
moral_action: Bob asks his wife if she wants to give out her phone number.
moral_consequence: "Bob's wife says ""Yes"" and accepts full responsibility for what happens."
immoral_action: Bob gives his wife's phone number to the person.
immoral_consequence: The person calls Bob's wife and harasses her, making her angry.

norm: It's wrong to abandon a friend that needs help.
situation: Sam is hanging out with his friend Amanda and notices she has new cut scars on her arms.
intention: Sam wants to help Amanda.
moral_action: Sam asks Amanda about the cuts and even though she gets mad he sticks by her and keeps checking on her.
moral_consequence: Eventually Amanda confesses to Sam that she's been really depressed and thanks him for sticking by her when she was at her worst.
immoral_action: Sam asks Amanda about the cuts and Amanda tell him to mind his own business and leave her alone, to which he complies and avoids her thinking she needs space.
immoral_consequence: Sam later finds out that Amanda tried to hurt herself and is in the hospital in critical condition.

norm: It's great to propose to your partner.
situation: Jimmy has been with his partner for years, and they are having a special evening together.
intention: Jimmy wants the relationship to continue.
moral_action: Jimmy uses the special evening to get down on one knee and ask his partner to spend the rest of their life with him.
moral_consequence: Jimmy's partner is deeply touched by the gesture and says yes to the proposal.
immoral_action: Jimmy enjoys the special evening with his partner and takes things a day at a time with the relationship.
immoral_consequence: Jimmy's partner eventually grows tired of Jimmy not making a long term commitment to the relationship.

norm: It's wrong to publish someone's secret emails.
situation: Crystal receives an email from a male friend, expressing deep feelings of attraction and love.
intention: Crystal wants get advice from her friends about the possibility of forming a new romantic relationship.
moral_action: Crystal calls a close friend and discusses the email she received, in private, asking for advice on how to proceed in the potential relationship.
moral_consequence: The friend who wrote the email feels that his privacy has been respected and Crystal's other friends have no reason to question her trustworthiness.
immoral_action: Crystal posts the full text of her friend's email to her Facebook account as a status, asking her friends for comments.
immoral_consequence: The friend who wrote the email feels betrayed and hurt, and all of Crystal's friends question her trustworthiness and judgement.

norm: It's harmful to ignore your significant other.
situation: Bobby was playing a video game and his girlfriend wanted to talk to him.
intention: Bobby wants to play his video game.
moral_action: Bobby pauses his video game, listens to his girlfriend, and returns to his game later.
moral_consequence: Bobby's girlfriend is happy that she has such a caring boyfriend.
immoral_action: Bobby focuses on his video game and doesn't listen to a word his girlfriend says.
immoral_consequence: Bobby's girlfriend gets annoyed with him and breaks up with him.

norm: It's bad to wish your kid hated her own dad.
situation: Sally is tucking Rita, her daughter, in at night.
intention: Sally wants to answer a question about why Rita has never met Rita's father.
moral_action: Sally says that Rita's father is a spy on a secret mission that will take years to complete.
moral_consequence: Rita's grows up feeling like Rita has a special father until Rita is old enough to know the truth.
immoral_action: Sally says that Rita's father thought Rita was ugly, so he never wanted to see Rita.
immoral_consequence: Rita grows up hating Rita's father but feeling as though Rita is so ugly no one will ever love Rita.

norm: You shouldn't disobey your partner's parents.
situation: Mark and Natalie have been dating for a while but Natalie's parents don't allow her to stay at his house.
intention: Mark wants to take Natalie on a weekend trip.
moral_action: Mark talks to Natalie's parents and asks for their permission to take Natalie, and they agree with a few stipulations.
moral_consequence: Natalie's parents are happy that their daughter is dating such a respectable person.
immoral_action: Mark tells Natalie not to tell her parents about the trip, and that they will deal with them when they get back.
immoral_consequence: Natalie's parents are very upset when they don't know where Natalie is.

norm: It's wrong to deceive your spouse.
situation: Joe's coworkers are all going out for a drink after work.
intention: Joe wants to go to the bar with his coworkers.
moral_action: Joe texts his wife to let her know he'll be joining his coworkers for a drink after work.
moral_consequence: Joe's wife is not worried and Joe has a great time with his colleagues.
immoral_action: Joe goes out to the bar without telling his wife.
immoral_consequence: Joe and his wife get into a fight when he comes home late smelling of alcohol.

norm: You shouldn't leave your partner for someone else.
situation: Mark and Anna are in a long distance relationship, and Mark is lonely on a Friday night while his ex asks him out.
intention: Mark wants to do something to make himself less lonely.
moral_action: Mark calls Anna and asks to watch a movie with her over Zoom.
moral_consequence: Mark has a fun night.
immoral_action: Mark accepts his ex's offer, and being with her Mark stops talking to Anna to be with his ex.
immoral_consequence: Mark is stuck in a rough relationship which ends in heartbreak.

norm: It's nosy to get involved with other people's relationships.
situation: Carmen is talking to her friend Stella about an argument she had with her boyfriend earlier that day.
intention: Stella wants to help Carmen.
moral_action: Stella listens to Carmen vent about the argument and asks if there's anything she can do to help.
moral_consequence: Carmen feels supported by her friend.
immoral_action: Stella contacts Carmen's boyfriend and tells him he should apologize.
immoral_consequence: Carmen's boyfriend thinks Carmen asked Stella to intervene, and breaks up with her.

norm: It's wrong to give your parents ultimatums.
situation: Charlie has siblings and is arguing with his parents about cleaning his room, which he does not want to do.
intention: Charlie wants to avoid cleaning his room.
moral_action: Charlie bribes one of his siblings to clean his room for him, in exchange for a nice toy.
moral_consequence: The sibling is happy with the toy, the parents are happy for a clean room, and Charlie is happy to avoid cleaning it.
immoral_action: Charlie tells his parents to let him go without cleaning or else he will trash the house.
immoral_consequence: Charlie's parents exert their authority and ground Charlie for a month.

norm: It's wrong to exclude family from weddings.
situation: Gary is having a wedding and is trying to decide who to invite when he realizes that the hall is too small for everyone.
intention: Gary wants to solve the invitation problem.
moral_action: Gary searches for a larger venue, finds one, and books it for his wedding, then invites everyone who is close.
moral_consequence: Gary's family all come to the wedding and have a great time, feeling included in Gary's life.
immoral_action: Gary invites his close friend and some family and doesn't invite some cousins, aunts, and uncles.
immoral_consequence: The uninvited family feel left out and shunned, eventually holding a grudge against Gary.

norm: It is wrong to be with multiple partners.
situation: John is on a dating website when he receives date requests from five women.
intention: John doesn't want to hurt anyone.
moral_action: John chooses one of the women to date, and he sets the other women up with his attractive single friends.
moral_consequence: John gains friends and a wonderful new girlfriend.
immoral_action: John dates all of the women at the same time.
immoral_consequence: John is discovered by the women, and his reputation on the dating site is tarnished.

norm: It is bad to betray your siblings' trust.
situation: Blake is hanging out at home when his sister's boyfriend Mitch flirts with him.
intention: Blake doesn't want to hurt Mitch's feelings.
moral_action: Blake turns on the television to Mitch's favorite tv show and asks him information about the characters.
moral_consequence: Blake uses the information he learns from Mitch to impress another cute guy.
immoral_action: Blake returns Mitch's advances allowing Mitch to kiss him.
immoral_consequence: Blake's relationship with his sister is destroyed when she walks in on them.

norm: It's wrong to value money over one's friends.
situation: Lexie has a chance to work an extra shift at work tonight, but she had already promised her friend Dana to go to the night club.
intention: Lexie wants to talk with Dana.
moral_action: Lexie tells her employer that she already made plans, and calls Dana to tell her to get ready to go out.
moral_consequence: Dana has a fun night out with her friend and appreciates Lexie for sticking to their plans.
immoral_action: Lexie calls Dana to tell her she's going to get in some extra hours instead of going to the club.
immoral_consequence: Dana is disappointed because she got all ready to go out for nothing.

norm: It can be stressful when you're late to work.
situation: Ross has work in the morning and has to get up at seven o clock.
intention: Ross wants to set his alarm.
moral_action: Ross sets his alarm clock for five thirty in the morning to get ready for work.
moral_consequence: Ross has plenty of time to get dressed and eat breakfast before taking his time to get to work.
immoral_action: Ross sets his alarm for six thirty in the morning to go to work.
immoral_consequence: Ross is late to work and his boss is on him all day.

norm: It's rude to give someone the silent treatment.
situation: Jill is angry with her girlfriend because she forgot about their dinner plans and stayed late at work.
intention: Jill wants to express her disappointment towards her girlfriend.
moral_action: Jill lets her girlfriend know the second she gets home why she is mad.
moral_consequence: Jill's girlfriend understands what she did wrong and makes it up to Jill.
immoral_action: Jill avoids speaking to her girlfriend for several days.
immoral_consequence: Jill's girlfriend never learns why she was mad and doesn't know how to change her behavior for next time.

norm: It is important to spend time with your spouse.
situation: Peter and his wife are home for the evening after a long week.
intention: Peter wants to watch the evening basketball game.
moral_action: Peter asks his wife to watch the game with him like a stay at home date.
moral_consequence: Peter's wife is so happy Peter included her in something he loves.
immoral_action: Peter ignores is wife and views the basketball game for the evening.
immoral_consequence: Peter's wife feels sad that Peter would rather watch the game than spend time with her.

norm: It's considered inappropriate to have two different valentine's day vaentines.
situation: Paul's best friend May asks him if he will still get her a Valentine's gift now that he has a girlfriend.
intention: Paul wants to answer May's question.
moral_action: Paul lets May know that it would be inappropriate for him to give her a gift as well.
moral_consequence: Paul avoids unnecessary strife in his relationship with his girlfriend.
immoral_action: Paul decides to go ahead and buy May a Valentines day gift.
immoral_consequence: Paul has a fight with his girlfriend when she finds out he gave May a gift too.

norm: You shouldn't expect your friends to do certain things.
situation: Greg, who loves extreme sports, is on a trip with some friends when he sees a bungee jumping place nearby.
intention: Greg wants to go bungee jumping.
moral_action: Greg asks his friends to drop him off at the bungee jumping place and to pick him up in a few hours.
moral_consequence: Greg's friends work to please Greg and are grateful that they don't have to do it with him.
immoral_action: Greg tells his friends that they need to go bungee jumping and keeps telling them until they give in.
immoral_consequence: Greg's friends have a bad experience bungee jumping and resent Greg for his selfishness.

norm: It's bad to lose your friend's property.
situation: Sue has a date with Aaron and borrowed her friend June's bracelet for the night.
intention: Sue wants to meet with June afterwards.
moral_action: After Sue's date she is washing her hands in a public restroom, but leaves the bracelet on, so she can avoid getting it lost, then goes to June's.
moral_consequence: Sue is able to give June back her bracelet and feels grateful for her letting her borrow it.
immoral_action: After Sue's date she is washing her hands in a public restroom and takes off the bracelet, sets it on the sink and leaves to go to June's.
immoral_consequence: When June asks for the bracelet, Sue realizes it's gone and has to buy June a new one.

norm: Families are expected to attend the weddings of it's members.
situation: James's father's brother's nephew's cousion is getting married.
intention: James wants to make a decision.
moral_action: James decides to go to the wedding and see what happens.
moral_consequence: James has a fun time with his family at the wedding of distant relatives.
immoral_action: James decides not to go because he can't go to every wedding of every far flung relative.
immoral_consequence: James gets angry calls from various distant relative wo are upset.

norm: It's wrong to push your ideas on others.
situation: Steve is at a party.
intention: Steve wants to be admired.
moral_action: Steve keeps his beliefs to himself and acts cordially.
moral_consequence: people want to be his friend.
immoral_action: Steve goes on and on about atheism.
immoral_consequence: Everyone ignores him.

norm: It's hurtful to go behind someone's back.
situation: Terry is married and has an old car that is breaking down.
intention: Terry wants to buy a new vehicle.
moral_action: Terry talks with his wife about what to buy, and they decide on a truck.
moral_consequence: Terry's wife will feel appreciated in the marriage.
immoral_action: Terry buys a truck without consulting with his wife.
immoral_consequence: Terry's wife will not feel like an equal partner in the marriage.

norm: One shouldn't feel hatred toward a family member.
situation: Joey is a teenager and his dad recently moved away because his parents got divorced.
intention: Joey wants to react to his father moving away.
moral_action: Joey tries to understand his parents' reasoning for divorce and does not blame his father for moving away.
moral_consequence: Joey continues a relationship with his father.
immoral_action: Joey blames his father for the divorce and moving away and doesn't want to talk to him.
immoral_consequence: Joey does not continue a relationship with his father.

norm: You shouldn't ignore your significant other at social events.
situation: Adam and his wife are going to a party after being cooped in together for months because of the pandemic.
intention: Adam wants to hang out with someone else than his wife at the party.
moral_action: Before the party, Adam suggest to his wife that they try to connect with other people instead of hanging out together.
moral_consequence: Adam's wife thinks it's a good idea and enjoys an evening spent with other people than Adam.
immoral_action: Once at the party, Adam purposefully ignores his wife every time she comes close.
immoral_consequence: Adam's wife sadly tells herself that Adam does not like her anymore.

norm: It's good to try to look out for your family.
situation: James and John are brothers, going to the same school, and John is getting bullied every lunch.
intention: James wants to do something during his lunch hour.
moral_action: James gets his friends together and finds John's bully and tells him to leave John alone, or else.
moral_consequence: John is left alone and has fun playing on the jungle gym and teeter-totter.
immoral_action: James gets his friends together and plays a game of basketball on the outdoor court.
immoral_consequence: John gets beaten up on the playground by his bully.

norm: It is normal for relation ships to be monogamous
situation: Mark feels he is being neglected by his wife.
intention: Mark wants to have a hot sex life.
moral_action: Mark tells his wife he loves her dearly and would like their sex life to be more exciting.
moral_consequence: Mark's wife happily welcomes his ideas and feels their marriage is getting a second wind.
immoral_action: Mark talks to his wife about opening up their marriage to include affairs with other partners.
immoral_consequence: Mark's wife accepts, only because she is afraid he will leave her otherwise - but she is not happy about it.

norm: You should give candy to children on Halloween.
situation: Jason is looking forward to celebrating Halloween at home this year.
intention: Jason wants to answer the door for trick-or-treaters.
moral_action: Jason gives out full size chocolate bars to the trick-or-treaters.
moral_consequence: The trick-or-treaters are very pleased and thank Jason for making their Halloween happier.
immoral_action: Jason decides to give out toothbrushes to the trick-or-treaters.
immoral_consequence: The trick-or-treaters are unhappy about the toothbrushes and don't want to come to Jason's house next year.

norm: It's mean to give someone a gift that isn't real.
situation: James is a poor college student and needs to think of a Christmas present for a cousin he doesn't really like.
intention: James doesn't want to spend too much money.
moral_action: James makes his cousin a home-made pan of his favorite fudge.
moral_consequence: James' cousin thanks James for the treat and offers him a piece.
immoral_action: James gives his cousin a gift card which he's already completely used up.
immoral_consequence: James' cousin notices the gift card isn't working but doesn't bother to bring it up.

norm: It's good for someone to stick up for their family.
situation: Bill's younger brother Bob is getting picked on at school and Bob asks him for help.
intention: Bill needs to decide how to help.
moral_action: Bill tells Bob that tomorrow he'll scare off the bully for him.
moral_consequence: Bob gets left alone by the bully after Bill tells the bully to knock it off or else.
immoral_action: Bill tells Bob that he needs to learn to stand up for himself and that Bill can't fight all Bob's battles.
immoral_consequence: Billy continues to get picked on at school because Bob refused to intercede.

norm: It is good to be committed to your relationship.
situation: Kent's girlfriend is going to serve in a foreign country for a year.
intention: Kent wants to say goodbye to his girlfriend.
moral_action: Kent says goodbye and promises to be faithful to her while she's gone.
moral_consequence: Kent's girlfriend is encouraged and feels supported.
immoral_action: Kent tells his girlfriend goodbye and that their relationship is over because she is leaving.
immoral_consequence: Kent's girlfriend cries and becomes depressed.

norm: It's mean to ruin your friend's vacation plans.
situation: Chelsea was planning on going on vacation when Kelly called her.
intention: Kelly wants Chelsea to come to her party.
moral_action: Kelly reschedules her party so that Chelsea would be able to attend.
moral_consequence: Chelsea has a great time at both the party and on vacation.
immoral_action: Kelly calls the traveling agency to cancel Chelsea's vacation plans.
immoral_consequence: Chelsea isn't able to board the plane even though she paid for the trip.

norm: It's good to have dinner with friends.
situation: Luke is about to leave work and head to dinner by himself but his friends offer to go with him.
intention: Luke wants to eat dinner.
moral_action: Luke decides to go with his friends for dinner.
moral_consequence: Luke and his friends have a great time at dinner talking about work.
immoral_action: Luke goes by himself since he doesn't feel like waiting.
immoral_consequence: Luke is kinds bored by himself and wishes his friends were there.

norm: It's wrong to deceive your partner.
situation: Dave wins money at the casino and comes home with a wad of cash which his wife Jane sees and asks him where he got it.
intention: Dave wants to respond to Jane's question.
moral_action: Dave tells Jane he was gambling, and he won it at the casino.
moral_consequence: Jane is not happy Dave was gambling but is happy he is telling her the truth.
immoral_action: Dave tells Jane that he recently got a bonus from work but that for some reason they had to pay him in cash.
immoral_consequence: Jane is suspicious of Dave's explanation and doesn't trust him as much.

norm: You shouldn't share information about someone without their consent.
situation: Melissa sees her friend Andrea out on a date with a guy who is not her boyfriend.
intention: Melissa wants Andrea to be happy.
moral_action: Melissa has a conversation with Andrea about what she saw and does not tell anyone else.
moral_consequence: Melissa and Andrea remain good friends and Andrea continues to cheat on her boyfriend.
immoral_action: Melissa tells Andrea's boyfriend about what she saw because she thinks Andrea will be happier if they break up.
immoral_consequence: Andrea's boyfriend breaks up with her, and Andrea stops talking to Melissa because she ruined her relationship.

norm: Dating the ex of a friend is sneaky.
situation: Sandra, who used to be married to Dave's best friend, asks Dave out on a date.
intention: Dave wants to respond to Sandra's request for a date.
moral_action: Dave tells Sandra that his friendship is worth more than sex and declines her offer.
moral_consequence: Dave's best friend finds out Dave declined and is delighted that he has such a supportive and upstanding friend.
immoral_action: Dave tells Sandra that he's always lusted after her and that he'd be delighted to get serious with her.
immoral_consequence: Dave's best friend finds out and is deeply hurt that Dave is dating his ex, who so badly hurt him.

norm: It's wrong to disrespect the people who raised you.
situation: Bill is mad that his parents have grounded him for a month for sneaking out the house.
intention: Bill wants to say something to his parents about his punishment.
moral_action: Bill apologizes and asks for a shorter punishment.
moral_consequence: Bill gets two days knocked off his punishment.
immoral_action: Bill tells his parents that they're the worst parents ever.
immoral_consequence: Bill is grounded for an extra month.

norm: You shouldn't ignore someone's texts.
situation: Sam is on vacation when he receives text messages from his boss about an urgent work matter.
intention: Sam wants to decide whether to respond to the text messages.
moral_action: Sam responds to the texts and takes care of the work matter his boss needed help with.
moral_consequence: Sam receives credit and commendation at work and looks good to the boss.
immoral_action: Sam ignores the texts because he is on vacation and wants to relax.
immoral_consequence: Sam's boss is upset with him for failing to respond to the urgent matter and reprimands him in front of his colleagues.

norm: It's good to stick up for your friend.
situation: Brandon is sitting and having lunch with the new friend he met in class today when his friends approach them and start making jokes about the new kid.
intention: Brandon wants to respond to his friends.
moral_action: Brandon tells his friends to take it easy with the ribbing and introduces them to the new student.
moral_consequence: The new student is happy to meet them and feels good about his first day of school.
immoral_action: Brandon meets his friend's jokes with more jokes of his own about the new kid.
immoral_consequence: The new student begins to feel bullied and leaves the group feeling upset.

norm: You should be patient and understanding with family members.
situation: Billy's mom had a bad day at work and was stressed out at home.
intention: Billy's mom wanted Billy to take care of his responsibilities.
moral_action: After a bad day at work Billy's mom took a deep breath and had patience with her son and motivated him to get his chores done.
moral_consequence: Billy felt like he impressed his mom doing so well and it motivated him to take care of his responsibilities for a long time after.
immoral_action: Billy's mom was in a bad mood from work and got mad a Billy just because he didn't do his dishes.
immoral_consequence: Billy felt threatened by her and it took them weeks to get back to a good relationship.

norm: It's bad to flake out at the last moment.
situation: Cody has a birthday party he RSVP'd for, but a concert by a band he loves is on the same day.
intention: Cody wants to go to the concert.
moral_action: Cody informs the birthday hosts before hand that he cannot make it and apologizes.
moral_consequence: The birthday hosts will accept that Cody cannot come and reduce the party materials.
immoral_action: Cody tells the birthday hosts he cannot come the day of the party.
immoral_consequence: The birthday hosts will think Cody is rude and have excess party materials.

norm: You shouldn't make false accusations about someone.
situation: Robert is a high school student who isn't too fond of his math teacher, Mrs. Greene.
intention: Robert wants to go to school without having to see Mrs. Greene.
moral_action: Robert speaks with his guidance counselor, telling him that he won't succeed if he has to continue on in Mrs.Greene's class.
moral_consequence: Robert's guidance counselor switches him to a different math class taught by someone else.
immoral_action: Robert files an anonymous report to the school saying that Mrs.Greene hits her students, although she never has.
immoral_consequence: Mrs. Greene is temporarily suspended pending the investigation of the claim.

norm: It's wrong to say bad things about your kids.
situation: Stephanie's daughter has decided not to go to college, but become a tattoo artist instead.
intention: Stephanie wants to talk to her child about her career decision.
moral_action: Stephanie approaches Samantha and speaks to her about taking her time and carefully weighing her options.
moral_consequence: Samantha heeds her mother's advice, and ends up changing her mind about her choices after high school graduation.
immoral_action: Stephanie has a conversation with her daughter, Samantha, but gets angry and tells her daughter she is going to be a loser if she doesn't go to college.
immoral_consequence: Samantha is so hurt by her mother, that she doesn't speak to her for many months.

norm: Grandparents are expected to part of their grandchildren's lives.
situation: Nico and his grandson Juan had a big argument and Nico told him he never wanted to see him again.
intention: Nico needs to decide whether to follow through on his statement.
moral_action: Nico decides to call Juan and apologize for what Nico said.
moral_consequence: Juan accepts the apology and says Juan was at fault too, their relationship is restored.
immoral_action: Nico decides to keep his word, and ignores all future attempts at contact from Juan.
immoral_consequence: Juan grows up without any contact with Nico and suffers emotionally.

norm: Partners should limit flirting with other people.
situation: Kate and Barry have been dating for a while, but Kate likes to flirt with other men, which Barry tells her he is uncomfortable with.
intention: Kate wants to reply to Barry's request not to flirt with other men.
moral_action: Kate apologizes to Barry and stops flirting all the time.
moral_consequence: Barry and Kate become closer partners and eventually marry each other.
immoral_action: Kate says the flirting is harmless and continues to do it.
immoral_consequence: Barry becomes very upset and breaks up with Kate.

norm: Making your friends feel unwelcome is wrong.
situation: Mark is hosting a Christmas party and invited his friends and family.
intention: Mark wants to talk to people at the party.
moral_action: Mark talks to all the guests at his party, including his friends.
moral_consequence: Mark's friends enjoy the party and invite Mark to a New Year's Eve party the next week.
immoral_action: Mark spends all his time talking to family members and doesn't speak to his friends.
immoral_consequence: Mark's friends feel ignored and unappreciated, leave the party early, and don't talk to Mark until the new year.

norm: It's good to plan for a trip.
situation: Steve has a weekend off from work.
intention: Steve wants to go to Texas.
moral_action: Steve packs his bags the night before and goes through a checklist.
moral_consequence: Steve forgets nothing and enjoys his trip.
immoral_action: Steve jumps in his car and drives off without packing or planning.
immoral_consequence: Steve forgets a bunch of stuff and has a bad trip.

norm: It's wrong to not believe your friends.
situation: Noah tells Gary that Noah will lose his job because he saw some emails at work and the company is about to go bankrupt.
intention: Gary wants to comfort Noah.
moral_action: Gary tells Noah that it sounds pretty bad but that he'll bounce back and find another job since he's a good worker.
moral_consequence: Noah is happy and relieved to hear that Gary thinks he's a good employee and that his reputation should land him another job.
immoral_action: Gary tells Noah that he's making up nonsense and that some emails don't mean anything and that he'll be fine.
immoral_consequence: Noah is annoyed that Gary is dismissing the evidence and that Gary's not seeing the gravity of the situation.

norm: It is bad to be upset when people are happy.
situation: Mark who's foot is hurting a lot is at the office when Kim wins employee of the month and everyone starts celebrating loudly.
intention: Mark wants to respond to the loud celebration.
moral_action: Mark tells everyone that he thinks Kim is awesome and deserves the award.
moral_consequence: Kim is delighted that Mark is so complimentary and that he's happy for her recognition.
immoral_action: Mark tells everyone around him to pipe down because he's in pain, and they're annoying him.
immoral_consequence: Kim is annoyed that Mark is making her moment about himself and can't stomach five minutes of celebration.

norm: It's bad to ditch your significant other on a holiday.
situation: Paul is invited to go on a camping trip with his friends, but it is on the same day as Valentine's day, which he normally spends with his girlfriend Laura.
intention: Paul wants to respond to the invitiation.
moral_action: Paul turns down the camping invitation and spends Valentine's day with his girlfriend instead.
moral_consequence: Laura is glad to spend Valentine's day with Paul, and she gets him a nice gift.
immoral_action: Paul decides to go on the camping trip and leaves his girlfriend alone on Valentine's day.
immoral_consequence: Laura is angry and upset, and she breaks up with Paul a week later.

norm: It's bad to date someone a friend has dated.
situation: John is single and knows his best friend Matt just broke up with his girlfriend Mona, who John has always been attracted to, and John and Mona still talk.
intention: John wants to get in a relationship.
moral_action: John decides to ask Mona if she has any single friends she could recommend him to.
moral_consequence: Mona sets John up with a friend of hers and the two start dating.
immoral_action: John decides to ask Mona out on a date which goes well, and the two start seeing each other regularly.
immoral_consequence: Matt stops talking to John after he finds out John is dating his ex.

norm: It's good to compromise when you're in a relationship.
situation: Bob is discussing with his wife about how to spend their Saturday afternoon, going to the park, or to the museum?
intention: Bob wants to go to the museum.
moral_action: Bob comes to an agreement with his wife that they will do both the park and the museum.
moral_consequence: Bob's wife is happy to go to the museum and even enjoys the park.
immoral_action: Bob talks over his wife, and badgers her until she agrees to do what he wants.
immoral_consequence: Bob's wife is frustrated for the whole afternoon.

norm: It's good to pray when you're religious.
situation: Mike wakes up early and has an hour before the day starts.
intention: Mike wants to do some religious activites.
moral_action: Mike prays and asks God for help with his day.
moral_consequence: Mike develops a personal relationship with God.
immoral_action: Mike reads a spiritual book written by a guru.
immoral_consequence: Mike enjoys the book, but fails to connect with God.

norm: You shouldn't demand things from your spouse
situation: Sypha was upset at her husband Trevor for leaving his clothes on the floor.
intention: Sypha wants the clothes off the floor.
moral_action: Sypha asks Trevor why he left the clothes on the floor.
moral_consequence: Trevor explains that the hamper was full last time he was in the bathroom before putting them away.
immoral_action: Sypha yells at Trevor and demands he pick up the clothes.
immoral_consequence: Trevor picks up the clothes, but he gives Sypha the silent treatment.

norm: It is good to share with family members
situation: Becca and John are siblings and are watching a movie together, and Becca has a large tub of popcorn that John wants some of.
intention: Becca wants to eat her popcorn.
moral_action: Becca pours out some of the popcorn into a plastic tray and passes it to John.
moral_consequence: John munches on the popcorn contentedly as he watches the movie.
immoral_action: Becca hogs all the popcorn to herself during the course of the movie.
immoral_consequence: John can't focus on the movie because he's too busy thinking about how hungry he is.

norm: It's rude to abruptly cut someone out of your life.
situation: Ann has a friend who has always been a detrimental influence and lately is becoming more difficult to deal with.
intention: Ann wants to separate from her friend.
moral_action: Ann is upfront about her intentions and slowly distances herself from the friend.
moral_consequence: Ann's friend understands the consequences of her behavior.
immoral_action: Ann cuts her friend out of her life and deletes her from social media.
immoral_consequence: Ann's friend thinks she did nothing wrong and that Ann is a bad friend.

norm: It's bad to think your boyfriend is cheating.
situation: Melissa has been dating Chris for 6 months, and Chris is planning to have a guys night out with some friends.
intention: Melissa wants to respond to Chris's outing.
moral_action: Melissa tells Chris to have a good time, but to please not stay out too late.
moral_consequence: Chris promises he won't be gone long and appreciates Melissa trusting him.
immoral_action: Melissa tells Chris she doesn't want him to go because she thinks he will be unfaithful.
immoral_consequence: Chris gets angry because he feels Melissa doesn't trust him.

norm: It's bad to snoop on your neighbor's correspondence.
situation: Deloris and her husband Ned offered to help out their neighbor while she's away on vacation.
intention: Deloris needs to pick up their neighbor's mail every day.
moral_action: Deloris picks up all her neighbor's mail and puts it in a pile without looking at any letters.
moral_consequence: Ned tells Deloris that it's good she's picking up the neighbor's mail every day.
immoral_action: When Deloris picks up her neighbor's mail she looks her letters to see who writing to her.
immoral_consequence: Ned sees Deloris looking at the neighbor's letters and tells her she shouldn't be so nosy.

norm: It is rude to cancel on someone for a date.
situation: Ezra is about to go on a date with Shelly he met online when he notices Shelly's new profile picture makes her look drastically different.
intention: Ezra wants to go on a date.
moral_action: Ezra asks Shelly about her dramatic change in appearance before he agrees to go on their date.
moral_consequence: Shelly becomes more confident in herself as Ezra warms up to her.
immoral_action: Ezra ditches his original date for another young woman who works at the bar.
immoral_consequence: Shelly is heartbroken that Ezra canceled their date.

norm: It's wrong to read someone else's mail without their permission.
situation: Larry's neighbor had been out of town for a couple of weeks.
intention: Larry wanted to bring in the mail for his neighbor so it wouldn't pile up in the mailbox.
moral_action: Larry picks up the neighbor's mail and passes each piece under their door.
moral_consequence: Larry's neighbor buys him a gift card for doing the favor for them.
immoral_action: Larry opens and reads each piece of mail before slipping it under the neighbor's door.
immoral_consequence: The neighbor calls the police on Larry for mail tampering.

norm: It's wrong to have a crush on your friend's partner.
situation: Justin has noticed that his friend Nick's new girlfriend is very kind and pretty.
intention: Justin wants to get to know Nick's girlfriend better.
moral_action: Justin knows that he can't act on his feelings, and decides to just treat Nick's girlfriend as one of the guys.
moral_consequence: They all become best friends and Nick and Justin's friendship is stronger than ever.
immoral_action: Justin goes behind Nick's back and tries to kiss Nick's girlfriend.
immoral_consequence: Nick's girlfriend tells him about Justin's behavior, and both decide never to speak to him again.

norm: You should help family members in need.
situation: Eric gets a calls from his brother Bryan, who needs a temporary place to stay after losing his job and apartment.
intention: Eric wants to respond to Bryan.
moral_action: Eric tells Bryan that he can sleep in the guest room until he gets back on his feet.
moral_consequence: Bryan stays with Eric for a few weeks until he eventually finds a new job and gets his own place.
immoral_action: Eric tells Bryan that he doesn't have enough room to accommodate him.
immoral_consequence: Bryan has no one else that he can turn to and becomes homeless.

norm: Showing excitement over a gift is considerate.
situation: Chris's grandma gives him a hideous knitted sweater for christmas.
intention: Chris wants to respond to his Grandma's gift.
moral_action: Chris smiles as his grandmother gives him the sweater and jumps to his feet to try it on immediately.
moral_consequence: Chris's grandmother appreciates that her hard work is being valued.
immoral_action: Chris accepts his grandma's gift impassively, before turning and tossing it in a trashcan.
immoral_consequence: Chris's grandmother is devastated that her heard work was not appreciated.

norm: It's wrong to break one's word.
situation: Karen promised his son that she would take him to a Cubs game if he did his homework.
intention: Karen wants to reward his son if he listens to her.
moral_action: Karen surprises her son with multiple tickets to a Cubs game so that he can bring his friends.
moral_consequence: Karen's son trusts that his mom will never break a promise that she makes.
immoral_action: Karen brings her son to a minor league baseball game.
immoral_consequence: Karen's son is disappointed and doesn't trust her mom anymore.

norm: It is rude not to attend a funeral.
situation: Sara was notified by a friend of an upcoming funeral of an acquaintance.
intention: Sara wants to express their condolences to the family.
moral_action: Sara attends the funeral along with her friend.
moral_consequence: The family appreciates the support from Sara.
immoral_action: Sara sends a card to the family.
immoral_consequence: The family is sad that she did not attend the funeral since they considered her a friend.

norm: You shouldn't move away from your kids.
situation: Natalie has just been offered her dream job on the other side of the country but her husband and children don't want to move.
intention: Natalie wants to take her dream job.
moral_action: Natalie negotiates with the company to make the position a remote one with occasional travel to the cross-country office.
moral_consequence: Natalie has a fulfilling career and is an involved parent with a good relationship with her kids.
immoral_action: Natalie moves by herself to take the job and comes back to see her family a few times a year.
immoral_consequence: Natalie's kids grow up not knowing their mother well and do not have much of a relationship with her.

norm: You shouldn't out your friends after their death.
situation: Luke's friend Han dies and everyone they know goes to his funeral.
intention: Luke wants to talk about Han at the funeral.
moral_action: Luke talks about all the good times he had with his friend Han and all the ties Han helped him.
moral_consequence: Han's family is pleased to hear all these wonderful stories from Luke.
immoral_action: Luke tells Han's family all about Han smuggling drugs and weapons and such in his life.
immoral_consequence: Han's family doesn't believe Luke's stories and calls him a liar.

norm: You shouldn't call out your customers on social media.
situation: Fiona sits in her car after getting into a fight with a rude customer.
intention: Fiona wants to vent her frustrations.
moral_action: Fiona rants to another one of her coworkers on the phone.
moral_consequence: With all of the anger out of her system, Fiona is able to enjoy the rest of her night.
immoral_action: Fiona makes a posts about the incident on her Twitter.
immoral_consequence: Fiona is written up after the customer discovers the tweet and reports it to her boss.

norm: It's rude to block your family on social media.
situation: Tami has been engaged in a heated argument with her sister on facebook.
intention: Tami wants to put an end to the argument.
moral_action: Tami just stops responding and gives it some time to cool off.
moral_consequence: Tami's sister is willing to forget about the whole thing and brush it off.
immoral_action: Tami blocks her sister so that she cannot respond to her anymore.
immoral_consequence: Tami's sister cannot believe that her own sister blocked her, and they have a worse relationship.

norm: You shouldn't exclude people from important events.
situation: Sandra is graduating from school and her brother asks her if he can attend her graduation.
intention: Sandra wants to give her brother a reply about him going to her graduation.
moral_action: Sandra says that her brother can come to her school graduation but that he should behave well.
moral_consequence: Sandra's brother feels included in Sandra's life by being allowed to attend her important event.
immoral_action: Sandra tells her brother that he can't go to her graduation because he might embarrass her.
immoral_consequence: Sandra's brother is insulted that she won't let him come to her graduation.

norm: It is okay to be concerned about your children.
situation: Amy has a six year old son.
intention: She wants to let him do something fun.
moral_action: Amy takes her son to the playground and watches him closely.
moral_consequence: Her son has a great time and stays safe.
immoral_action: Amy lets her son go to a nearby playground by himself.
immoral_consequence: Her son falls off a slide and breaks his arm.

norm: It's wrong to cut everyone out of your life.
situation: Doug is having a really difficult time lately with his mental health.
intention: Doug wants to feel better.
moral_action: Doug decided to call up his friend to see if he wanted to hang out and do something.
moral_consequence: Doug was able to take his mind off of his problems, and he realized no problem is too large to tackle.
immoral_action: Doug decided to turn his phone off, hang out alone in his bedroom, and drink his sorrows away.
immoral_consequence: Doug woke up feeling hungover, sad, and more alone than ever.

norm: It's not okay to lie to someone in a relationship.
