situation: George does not have to work this weekend.
intention: George wants to go to the football game but knows that his girlfriend wants to go to the Oktoberfest.
moral_action: George tells his girlfriend how much it means for him to go to the football game with his buddies.
moral_consequence: George's girlfriend understands and agrees that they can do something together the next time George has off for the weekend.
immoral_action: George tells his girlfriend that he has to work and instead goes to the football game with his buddies.
immoral_consequence: George's girlfriend ends the relationship when she learns that George did not have to work and actually went to a game.

norm: You should always be there for your child's birthday party.
situation: Dennis' wife is throwing a birthday party for their son, Brayden, on the same day as an important work meeting.
intention: Dennis wants to tend to his work meeting.
moral_action: Dennis schedules the meeting for earlier in the day so that he can attend Brayden's party.
moral_consequence: Dennis' meeting concludes early enough that he is even able to help with Brayden's party setup.
immoral_action: Dennis attends his meeting, missing nearly all of Brayden's party.
immoral_consequence: Brayden feels let down by his dad and is unable to enjoy the party.

norm: It's wrong to be late for your classes.
situation: Shay is currently in nursing school, but she also has another job that she has to spend time at.
intention: Shay wants to focus on becoming a nurse.
moral_action: Shay moves her schedule around so that she can get to class early each day.
moral_consequence: Shay's instructor respects that Shay is dedicated and is more willing to help her.
immoral_action: Shay makes sure she gets to every class, but she's often late.
immoral_consequence: The instructor doesn't believe that Shay is that dedicated, and Shay disrespects the instructor.

norm: It is bad to look through someone else's things.
situation: Tom's mother Susan suspects that he has been dating a girl without telling her about it.
intention: Susan wants to find out if Tom has a girlfriend.
moral_action: Susan sits down with Tom and asks him if he is seeing anyone.
moral_consequence: Tom tells his mother that he has been dating someone, and he feels relieved.
immoral_action: Susan goes into Tom's room and reads his journal to see if he has written about a girlfriend.
immoral_consequence: Tom discovers that Susan went through his journal and feels upset and violated.

norm: You shouldn't trust strangers.
situation: Sara is returning to work after maternity leave and needs to find a nanny to take care of her infant.
intention: Sara wants to find a caretaker for her child.
moral_action: Sara interviews numerous candidates, checks their references and makes sure they have CPR training.
moral_consequence: The babysitter is a positive influence and caretaker of the child and Sara is able to work without any concern for her child's safety.
immoral_action: Sara puts an ad in the paper for a babysitter and hires the first one she interviews, that doesn't have experience.
immoral_consequence: The babysitter ends up neglecting her child, and she has to take off work again to replace her.

norm: It's good to be there for your partner.
situation: John and his girlfriend have a school test coming up in a subject that John is much better at than his girlfriend.
intention: John wants to be prepared for the test.
moral_action: John spends part of his time studying for the test but also spends some time tutoring his girlfriend.
moral_consequence: John's girlfriend get's a good grade on the test and passes the class.
immoral_action: John spends his time studying for the test and leaves his girlfriend to fend for herself.
immoral_consequence: John's girlfriend fails the test and receives a low grade for the class.

norm: It's wrong to date your sibling's ex.
situation: Charlie needs a date for the upcoming dance and learns that his big brother Alex just got dumped by his ex-girlfriend Stacy.
intention: Charlie wants to ask a girl to the dance.
moral_action: Charlie asks a girl named Eve to the dance when he overhears someone saying she needs a date.
moral_consequence: Alex lets Charlie borrow some of his cologne, so he smells good for his date with Eve.
immoral_action: Charlie asks Stacy if she'll go to the dance with him now that she's single.
immoral_consequence: Alex learns that Charlie asked out Stacy and punches him for the act of disloyalty.

norm: You shouldn't tell your parent what to do.
situation: Joe's mother has trouble with gambling irresponsibly, and he catches her before she goes to the casino again.
intention: Joe wants to discourage his mom from gambling.
moral_action: Joe suggests to his mother that the money would be better spent elsewhere.
moral_consequence: Joe's mother starts to ruminate over what he said and agrees with him.
immoral_action: Joe tells his mother directly not to go to the casino anymore.
immoral_consequence: Joe's mother thinks he is trying to control her and refuses to listen.

norm: It's unhealthy to want to make somebody jealous
situation: Joy is looking to buy new heels, but she's deciding between an expensive one and a budget one.
intention: Joy wants to buy new heels.
moral_action: Joy buys the budget heels because she thinks the expensive ones would be a waste of money.
moral_consequence: Joy's friends respect that Joy is being modest with her heels.
immoral_action: Joy buys the expensive heels because she thinks they will impress others.
immoral_consequence: Joy's friends become jealous of Joy, and they don't like that she's trying to show off.

norm: You shouldn't be with someone you don't have feelings for.
situation: Dan is looking for a romantic partner online.
intention: Dan wants to bring someone along to an event he is attending.
moral_action: Dan realizes he can't find a date so quickly, so he asks a friend to join him.
moral_consequence: Dan and his friend end up enjoying the event, they always have a great time together.
immoral_action: Dan asks Tina, who likes him and will agree to go to the event, even though he doesn't like her.
immoral_consequence: Dan leads Tina on by inviting her, and she is really upset when he reveals he isn't actually interested.

norm: It's not okay to leave someone when they need you.
situation: Lars has been happy with his girlfriend Maria for three years but recently Maria got very sick and is hospitalized.
intention: Lars wants to only have happy times with his girlfriend.
moral_action: Lars sticks with Maria and comforts her and finds the joy in helping her and loving her.
moral_consequence: Maria feels closer to Lars and gets better and Lars has a successful happy relationship.
immoral_action: Lars tells Maria it's over and looks to date other girls.
immoral_consequence: Maria is shattered and Lars leaves every girl he dates and never has a happy relationship.

norm: Being honest is considered good.
situation: Chris's friend John called to say he'd be in town and asked to hang out, but Chris has a date that night.
intention: Chris wants to respond to John.
moral_action: Chris tells John that he already made plans with a pretty woman to go out that night.
moral_consequence: John totally understands and tells Chris he will catch him next time and to have fun.
immoral_action: Chris tells John his sister is sick, so he can't come.
immoral_consequence: John finds out Chris's sister is just fine and now he looks like a liar.

norm: It's good to take pride in your possessions.
situation: Jake doesn't really care about cars, but he bought one so that he could get to work.
intention: Jake has to decide whether to have his car washed.
moral_action: Jake decides that he'll hire someone that will clean his car for him.
moral_consequence: Jake's coworkers expect Jake's car to always be clean and notice how clean it looks compared to everyone else's.
immoral_action: Jake decides that the car would just need to be cleaned again in the future so it would be a waste of time.
immoral_consequence: Jake's coworkers judge him based him on how dirty his car is.

norm: It's wrong to sleep with someone else's husband.
situation: Camille is feeling lonely when her handsome married coworker Steven compliments her and touches her thigh.
intention: Camille wants companionship.
moral_action: Camille quickly excuses herself, and has a girls night with her friends after work.
moral_consequence: Camille meets an attractive guy while she's hanging out with her friends.
immoral_action: Camille returns Steven's affections hooking up with him in a nearby hotel.
immoral_consequence: Camille is attacked by Steven's wife after she discovers the affair.

norm: It's wrong to hang out with your ex's.
situation: Paisley's taken ex boyfriend sends her a flirty text message asking to meet her at her favorite cafe.
intention: Paisley doesn't want to spend time alone.
moral_action: Paisley visits her family for a family movie night.
moral_consequence: Paisley meets a cute guy at her mother's house.
immoral_action: Paisley hangs out with her ex boyfriend.
immoral_consequence: Paisley is accused of not being over her ex by her other friends.

norm: It's best to forgive others' misdeeds.
situation: David is a teacher and his grader Steve hasn't done a good job in the past.
intention: David wants Steve to do better.
moral_action: David gives Steve some general tips on how to grade, avoiding the past issues.
moral_consequence: Steve takes on the grading with renewed confidence.
immoral_action: David resurrects all of Steve's past mistakes and reminds him of them.
immoral_consequence: Steve gets frustrated and wants to feel trusted.

norm: It's kind to invite your friends to a party.
situation: Lance wants to throw a party for his friends.
intention: Lance wants to decide who to invite.
moral_action: Lance invites all of his friends for the party regardless.
moral_consequence: Not all of his friends can make it, but they appreciated the invite.
immoral_action: Lance invites only the people he knows want to come.
immoral_consequence: Some of the friends he didn't invite were upset at Lance.

norm: It is expected to share with family.
situation: John is at his mom's house where he ordered a large pizza and as he's at the door getting the pizza his mom asks him for a slice.
intention: John wants to respond to his mom's request for a slice.
moral_action: John tells his mom she can have half the pizza, forget a slice, that's why he ordered a large.
moral_consequence: John's mom is delighted and is excited to finally get that yummy pizza in her mouth.
immoral_action: John tells his mom that he paid for the pizza and if she wants a slice she can order her own.
immoral_consequence: John's mom is sad that John won't share his food with her and that he's so selfish.

norm: Ghosting someone is rude.
situation: Rodney has been going on a few dates with Alex and isn't really feeling much chemistry with them.
intention: Rodney wants to end the relationship before it gets too serious.
moral_action: Rodney explains to Alex that he likes them but isn't feeling the romantic vibe.
moral_consequence: Rodney maintains a positive impression with Alex, and they are able to remain friends.
immoral_action: Rodney decides to just stop texting Alex altogether and start seeing someone else.
immoral_consequence: Rodney runs into Alex out in public one day and gets yelled at.

norm: You should always celebrate the holidays with people.
situation: Carlie just moved to the big city by herself and the holidays are approaching.
intention: Carlie wants to do something for her first holiday in the big city.
moral_action: Carlie tells her new friends and coworkers of her holiday wish and is invited to a holidays gathering.
moral_consequence: Carlie gets to know some people more and enjoys her first holiday in the big city.
immoral_action: Carlie decides she isn't close enough with anybody yet and makes herself a home cooked meal to enjoy by herself for the holidays.
immoral_consequence: Carlie feels incredibly lonely as the night goes on and regrets choosing to spend the holidays alone.

norm: It's rude not to believe a friend
situation: Britney is listening to her friend Derrick and Bobby argue about Bobby's broken CD, and Derrick insists that he didn't damage the toy.
intention: Britney needs to pick a side to support.
moral_action: Britney suggests that Bobby investigate the area around the CD for more clues to explain the CD's broken state.
moral_consequence: Derrick's trust in Britney increases.
immoral_action: Britney sides with Bobby suggesting that Derrick must have done something to damage the CD.
immoral_consequence: Derrick is hurt that Britney sided with Bobby.

norm: You should be happy for other people's good news.
situation: Gene just proposed marriage to Missy, and she is telling her single friend, Cathy at work.
intention: Cathy wants to respond to the news.
moral_action: Cathy hugs Missy and tells her that she is very happy for her, while thinking she will find love for herself one day.
moral_consequence: Missy is glad to be able to share her happiness with her friend.
immoral_action: Cathy is jealous of Missy getting engaged, so she tells her that sounds nice, and changes the subject to work related topics.
immoral_consequence: Missy feels foolish because she expected Cathy to have a little more exitement.

norm: Siblings should celebrate each others victories.
situation: Mario just won an award for his work at the same time that his brother got a big promotion.
intention: Mario wants his family to celebrate his success.
moral_action: Mario congratulates his brother for his promotion and insists that both their successes should be celebrated.
moral_consequence: Mario's brother feels recognized and happy.
immoral_action: Mario refuses to acknowledge his brother's promotion and constantly brings the conversation back to his award.
immoral_consequence: Mario's brother feels that Mario does not care about him.

norm: You shouldn't call your partner crazy.
situation: Jason and his wife are having some issues with their relationship.
intention: Jason wants to do something about his marital problems.
moral_action: Jason tries working the problems out with his wife with the help of a marriage counselor instead of taking the problems out on her.
moral_consequence: Jason's wife works with him to resolve their problems and their marriage is better for it.
immoral_action: Jason decides everything is his wife's fault, and he screams at her and calls her a crazy bitch.
immoral_consequence: Jason's wife becomes angry, and she throws Jason out of the house.

norm: It's wrong to sleep with someone else's spouse.
situation: Mark is alone at a bar with his friend Jerry's wife, Maria, and it is time to go home.
intention: Mark wants to get Maria home safely as she is drunk.
moral_action: Mark drives Maria home and says goodnight and goes home to his own home alone.
moral_consequence: No one is hurt and no friendships are worsened.
immoral_action: Mark asks Maria if she wants to go home with him, and they have sex.
immoral_consequence: Jerry finds out and gets very angry.

norm: It's rude to point at people in public.
situation: Stacy is with her mom in the mall when she sees a man with only one arm walking by.
intention: Stacy wants to ask her mom about the man's missing arm.
moral_action: Stacy tugs on her mom's arm and asks her where the man's arm is.
moral_consequence: Stacy's mom answers her while the man walks by, ignorant of their conversation.
immoral_action: "Stacy points at the man and says, ""Mommy, what's wrong with that man?""."
immoral_consequence: The man looks embarrassed and quickly walks away.

norm: It's bad to hit on your friend's girlfriend.
situation: Josh and his best friend's girl Jill are hanging out at the local pub having a few drinks, and Josh notices Jill is wearing a new outfit.
intention: Josh wants to compliment Jill on her new outfit.
moral_action: Josh says Jill's new outfit is really stylish and distinct.
moral_consequence: Jill smiles and thanks Josh, and begins to tell him all about where and how she bought it.
immoral_action: Josh says Jill's new outfit really makes her look sexy.
immoral_consequence: An awkward silence ensues, before Jill pays her tab and says she needs to leave.

norm: It's good to be dedicated.
situation: Phil sees his friend Jane building an awesome treehouse in her yard.
intention: Phil wants to build an awesome treehouse in his yard.
moral_action: Phil gets to work on the treehouse right away and just keeps building with all of his spare time.
moral_consequence: 2 months later the treehouse is done and it looks awesome.
immoral_action: Phil gathers the materials and starts building, but then keeps putting it off.
immoral_consequence: 10 years later Phil finally finishes the treehouse, which is now too small for the tree.

norm: It's expected that partners will sleep in the same bed.
situation: Jill and her husband Lee bought a house and are setting it up.
intention: Jill wants to plan the sleeping arrangements.
moral_action: Jill sets up the master bedroom for her and Jim to share.
moral_consequence: Lee will feel happy that he's sharing a room with his wife.
immoral_action: Jill sets up a bedroom for herself separate from Lee.
immoral_consequence: Lee will feel that Jim doesn't love him that much.

norm: It's hurtful to disparage your friends.
situation: Ken is playing basketball with some friends when a friend on his team misses the throw.
intention: Ken wants to respond to the missed throw.
moral_action: Ken encourages his friend and tells him that he will do better next time.
moral_consequence: Ken's friend appreciates Ken more and becomes motivated to play better.
immoral_action: Ken heavily criticizes and talks down to his friend that missed the throw.
immoral_consequence: Ken's friends all start thinking Ken is a pompous jerk and likes him less.

norm: It's wrong to reveal one's secrets just to spite them.
situation: Anthony had recently told Billy a deep dark secret about himself, and a few days later they are now in a large fight in front of their other friends.
intention: Billy wants to win the argument with Anthony.
moral_action: Billy uses something Anthony said prior in the argument to prove he is wrong.
moral_consequence: Anthony continues arguing for a while, but eventually concedes the argument.
immoral_action: Billy uses the secret Anthony told him against him, to try to get points with everyone else.
immoral_consequence: Anthony is devastated, and runs off, never to talk to Billy or the rest ever again.

norm: It's wrong to create a jealous household.
situation: Pam's kids come home from school with their grade cards to show her.
intention: Pam wants to talk to the kids about their grades.
moral_action: Pam takes John and Jake aside individually and looks over their cards pointing out something good on each one.
moral_consequence: John and Jake both feel great about themselves and spend the evening playing together.
immoral_action: Pam looks at both cards together and points out how much better John's is than Jake's.
immoral_consequence: John feels spiteful toward Jake and the two start having problems getting along.

norm: It's nice to loan your tools to friends.
situation: Bill's friend is repairing their car and asks Bill if they can borrow some of his tools for the job.
intention: Bill wants to give his friend an answer about borrowing the tools.
moral_action: Bill tells his friend that they can borrow the tools but just asks that they are careful not to misplace them.
moral_consequence: Bill's friend is able to finish the repairs on their car quickly with the help of Bill's tools.
immoral_action: Bill says he can't lend his friend the tools because he's worried they'll get lost.
immoral_consequence: Bill's friend has to buy a new set of expensive tools to finish his repairs.

norm: You should be happy for people getting married.
situation: John and Chelsea are getting married and invite Hank, who is under a lot of stress, to the wedding.
intention: Hank wants to come to the wedding and eat at the reception.
moral_action: Hank puts on a smile and forces himself to congratulate John and Chelsea at the wedding.
moral_consequence: John and Chelsea remember that moment and send him a thank you card.
immoral_action: Hank comes to the wedding loaded with stress and is in a sour mood the whole time.
immoral_consequence: John and Chelsea think that Hank doesn't care about their wedding and are more reluctant to invite him in the future.

norm: You shouldn't snoop around your partner's things.
situation: Mary and John are out on a date but Mary's phone keeps vibrating.
intention: John wants to know who is calling Mary.
moral_action: John tells Mary it is ok if she needs to take the call as it won't be a big interruption on their date.
moral_consequence: Mary is grateful for John's understanding and explains who has been trying to contact her.
immoral_action: When Mary goes to the restroom John goes through her phone to see who is calling her.
immoral_consequence: Mary is livid when she catches John invading her privacy and cuts the date short.

norm: You shouldn't sleep with partners your friends are interested in.
situation: Sabrina has a crush on Roger, who her friend Danielle has shown interest in the past year.
intention: Sabrina wants to make a choice.
moral_action: Sabrina takes notice of Roger's attractive brother and asks the two of them to go on a double date with Danielle.
moral_consequence: Danielle is grateful to Sabrina for bringing her closer with Roger.
immoral_action: Sabrina seduces Roger, and Danielle sees them in bed together.
immoral_consequence: Danielle feels betrayed by Sabrina and ends the friendship.

norm: It's fun to go to the fair with people.
situation: Jamie hears that there is a fair in town a few miles from his home.
intention: Jamie wants to go to the fair.
moral_action: Jamie convinces several of his friends to go with him, and he drives them all to the fair that evening.
moral_consequence: Jamie has a wonderful evening at the fair with all his friends.
immoral_action: Jamie buys a ticket online and goes to the fair by himself that evening.
immoral_consequence: Jamie doesn't know anyone else there, and he doesn't really enjoy himself that much.

norm: You shouldn't scheme around your family.
situation: Joe is mourning the loss of his father, and he attends a family meeting regarding his father's estate.
intention: Joe wants to get a keepsake item that his father owned.
moral_action: Joe tells his siblings that the item meant a lot to him and asks if it could be worked out for him to have it in remembrance of his father.
moral_consequence: Each sibling has something that they hope to keep in their father's memory and are able to come to an agreement.
immoral_action: Joe tells his siblings that his father once told him that he should get the item, when the time comes, even though it isn't true.
immoral_consequence: Joe's siblings don't believe that he is telling the truth and it becomes an argument between them, making it unlikely that they will allow him to take the item.

norm: Ignoring your friends when other people are around is hurtful
situation: Cambell is at a business party with his friend Charles.
intention: Cambell wants to make new friends.
moral_action: Cambell invites Charles to play games with himself and other people at the party.
moral_consequence: Charles leaves the party with new friends.
immoral_action: Cambell leaves his friend in the other room to hang out with a group of well dressed people.
immoral_consequence: Charles is sad that Cambell abandoned him.

norm: People should advocate for ways of increasing public safety.
situation: Dale lives on a dangerous street that has a lot of car racing.
intention: Dale wants to figure out a way to avoid the danger.
moral_action: Dale writes letters to the government asking for more police patrolling on the street.
moral_consequence: The car racing is stopped by the police.
immoral_action: Dale moves to a different area of town.
immoral_consequence: The car racing is still prevalent on the street Dave used to live on.

norm: It's rude to be antisocial at work.
situation: John has been suffering in his personal life but still has to work with others.
intention: John just wants to get through his work day and have it over with.
moral_action: John keeps his personal life out of the workplace and tries to stay busy at work so the day goes by faster.
moral_consequence: John's co-workers are undisturbed by John's problems which are not theirs, and they work flow goes smoothly for the day.
immoral_action: John avoids people all day at work because of his sour mood about his personal life hoping it'll make the day easier for him.
immoral_consequence: John's co-workers find him difficult to work with and John becomes a bottleneck for everybody's work flow.

norm: It's bad to move away from your partner.
situation: Ryan was offered a better position within his company, but it requires him to relocate away from his girlfriend Samantha to a big city.
intention: Ryan wants to accept the job.
moral_action: Ryan tells Samantha what a good opportunity the job is, and asks her to move with him.
moral_consequence: Samantha gladly accepts and their relationship flourishes in the new city.
immoral_action: Ryan tells his boss he will take the position and tells Samantha that he will fly in every weekend to see her.
immoral_consequence: The long distance puts a strain on their relationship, so Ryan and Samantha break up.

norm: You should stick up for your family.
situation: Greg is with his mother Barb at the mall when a strange, drunken man approaches and calls his mother fat.
intention: Greg wants to respond to the insult.
moral_action: Greg tells the drunken man that he better apologize before Greg removes his tongue.
moral_consequence: Barb is delighted that her son is sticking up for her and feel safe with him by her side.
immoral_action: Greg tells the drunken man that his mom could definitely stand to lose some weight and is glad someone agrees.
immoral_consequence: Barb is insulted that her son is agreeing with the belligerent drunken man accosting her.

norm: One should try to work out their differences with classmates.
situation: John is just starting his first year of college and had a disagreement with a classmate.
intention: John wants to put the disagreement in the past.
moral_action: John approaches the classmate the following day and openly listens to their point view until they reach a mutual apology.
moral_consequence: John and the classmate become great friends over the course of John's college years and John makes lifelong memories.
immoral_action: John just ignores the issue and the classmate he had the issue with going forward.
immoral_consequence: John develops a reputation that causes people to avoid him and makes his college experience lonely and unpleasant.

norm: It's pathetic to disregard taking care of familial elders.
situation: Manny's elderly dad, who took care of him when he was younger, needs help getting around the house after a car accident.
intention: Manny wants to help out his dad.
moral_action: Manny makes taking care of his father his number one priority.
moral_consequence: Manny's dad feels like he's in good hands and doesn't have to worry as much.
immoral_action: Manny helps his dad whenever he's not playing video games.
immoral_consequence: Manny's dad barely receives any help and feels neglected.

norm: It's wrong to ditch people.
situation: Ian and two friends are working on a geocache location project for school, but Ian's two friends are very slow and taking forever.
intention: Ian wants to finish the project.
moral_action: Ian waits for his friends and does his best to encourage them to hurry up, eventually finishing the project.
moral_consequence: Ian's two friends have some motivation to continue and end up getting a good grade thanks to Ian.
immoral_action: Ian goes on ahead without his friends and locates each of the geocaches, finishing the project on his own.
immoral_consequence: Ian's two friends feel left out and abandoned, and Ian's teacher fails him because it was supposed to be a group effort.

norm: It's good to forgive others for their past.
situation: Nick meets his former fifth grade teacher, who made his life very difficult, on the street one day.
intention: Nick wants to say something to her.
moral_action: Nick tells his ex-teacher that it's good to see her again and that he learned to deal with difficulties that year.
moral_consequence: Nick's teacher apologizes for the difficulties and is glad to hear some encouragement.
immoral_action: Nick tells his ex-teacher that she made his fifth grade year very difficult.
immoral_consequence: Nick's teacher feels unappreciated and questions why she even bothers teaching.

norm: It's wrong to meddle in other people's romantic relationships.
situation: Josh dislikes his brother's wife even though their relationship is good.
intention: Josh wants his brother to be happy.
moral_action: Josh does not say anything to his brother because he can tell he is happy with his wife.
moral_consequence: Josh's brother and his wife continue to have a good marriage and Josh is still welcome in their life.
immoral_action: Josh introduces his brother to a friend he thinks his brother would like more than his wife and tries to push them together.
immoral_consequence: Josh's brother, Josh's friend, and the brother's wife all feel uncomfortable and their relationship with Josh becomes strained.

norm: You should help out your partner financially.
situation: Josh just had a big payday and his girlfriend Rya is stressed out, struggling to pay her bills.
intention: Josh wants to spend his paycheck.
moral_action: Josh decides to help Rya pay her bills and gives her half his check.
moral_consequence: Rya appreciates Josh's financial aid and uses it to catch up on her bills.
immoral_action: Josh decides to spend his paycheck on a brand new TV.
immoral_consequence: Rya continues to be stressed out about her financial situation and is angry at Josh.

norm: You shouldn't end a friendship over petty reasons.
situation: Bill and his friend had agreed to meet at the movie theater but Bill's friend arrives half an hour late, so they miss the movie.
intention: Bill wants to show his friend that he's unhappy with them being late for the movie.
moral_action: Bill tells their friend that he's angry that they're late and that they are to blame for Bill missing the movie .
moral_consequence: Bill's friend apologizes to Bill for being late and promises to be on time for things from then on.
immoral_action: Bill says that he doesn't want to be friends with the person who was late anymore because he can't even count on them to be on time.
immoral_consequence: The person who was late can't believe that Bill would end their friendship over such a little thing like being late.

norm: It's impolite to keep plans from your spouse.
situation: Mark gets invited on a weekend trip with friends.
intention: Mark wants to go this trip without his wife.
moral_action: Mark asks his wife if it would be alright if he goes on a trip next month with friends.
moral_consequence: Mark's wife will be able to prepare for him not being there that weekend.
immoral_action: Mark goes on a weekend trip without telling his wife.
immoral_consequence: Mark's wife will be upset because she had no idea he was going away.

norm: It's good to go to your family reunions.
situation: Kevin just received an invitation to go to a large get-together with the entire family.
intention: Kevin wants to respond to the invite.
moral_action: Kevin sends back a response that says he looks forward to seeing everyone again.
moral_consequence: Kevin's family is pleased that he was able to spend some time with them, and he really enjoys himself.
immoral_action: Kevin decides he would rather spend his time playing video games and writes back that he can't make it to the event.
immoral_consequence: Kevin's family is disappointed, and he later regrets his decision when he hears what a fun time everyone had.

norm: It's rude to ignore someone after kissing them.
situation: Carl went out with Krista and kissed her good night at the end.
intention: Carl wants to break things off with Krista because he didn't enjoy their time together.
moral_action: Carl lets Krista down gently and tells her that he doesn't see them working out together.
moral_consequence: Krista is upset but understands.
immoral_action: Carl blocks Krista's number and ignores her.
immoral_consequence: Krista worries about Carl and feels hurt that he is dumping her without explanation since she thought the night went well.

norm: It's bad to leave without telling anyone.
situation: Jenna has been at a party for three hours and is getting tired, but knows her friends will try to convince her to stay.
intention: Jenna wants to go home and sleep.
moral_action: As Jenna walks out the door, she tells her friends she had a great time, but she feels really tired.
moral_consequence: Jenna's friends only have time to wave goodbye before she's on the sidewalk walking home.
immoral_action: Jenna sneaks out the back door without talking to her friends.
immoral_consequence: Jenna goes home and sleeps while her friends panic about her going missing.

norm: It's nice to support a kid's fundraiser.
situation: Lucy was walking into her local grocery store when she sees some girl scouts selling cookies for their trip.
intention: Lucy wants cookies.
moral_action: Lucy buys two boxes of girl scout cookies when she walks out of the store.
moral_consequence: The girl scouts are able to raise enough money to go on their trip.
immoral_action: Lucy goes into the grocery store and buys a package of cookies from the shelf.
immoral_consequence: The girl scouts are not able to raise enough money to go on their trip.

norm: You should respect other people's friendships.
situation: Alan is friends with Doug who joined the basketball team and is now friends with the basketball players.
intention: Alan wants to talk to Doug about being friends with the basketball players.
moral_action: Alan will tell Doug it's cool that he made the team, and he's hanging out with all the players.
moral_consequence: Doug will think Alan is a chill guy and will introduce him to the players.
immoral_action: Alan tells Doug that being friends with the basketball players is taking away from their friendship.
immoral_consequence: Doug will think Alan is being jealous and unreasonable.

norm: Dating a friend's ex is frowned upon.
situation: Jenna's friend Samantha just broke up with the guy she'd been dating for three years.
intention: Jenna wants to find a new boyfriend.
moral_action: Jenna creates an account on OkCupid to find a boyfriend who doesn't know any of her friends.
moral_consequence: Jenna eventually hits it off with someone and goes on a double date with Samantha and her new boyfriend.
immoral_action: Jenna immediately asks Samantha's ex out on a dinner date.
immoral_consequence: Samantha confronts Jenna crying and says she can't be friends anymore.

norm: It's rude to ignore your friends for no reason.
situation: Ben is reading a book when his friend asks him a question.
intention: Ben wants to consider responding to his friend.
moral_action: Ben quickly answers his friend's question before returning to his book.
moral_consequence: Ben's friend appreciates and thanks Ben for the answer before leaving Ben to his book.
immoral_action: Ben decides to ignore his friend completely and simply continue reading his book.
immoral_consequence: Ben's friend start to think Ben is an unfriendly person and becomes less of a friend to him.

norm: You should not favor some friends over others.
situation: Linda, Greeta, and Gertude are out at the mall having lunch together.
intention: Linda wants to share her cheesecake.
moral_action: Linda gives both Greeta and Gertude a taste of a cheesecake.
moral_consequence: Greeta and Gertude love the cheesecake so much they order slices for themselves.
immoral_action: Linda lets Greeta taste her cheesecake, but when Gertude asks to taste, Linda tells her that she wants to eat the rest.
immoral_consequence: Gertude feels left out and wonders if Linda is actually her friend.

norm: Don't be bossy with your SO.
situation: Rob and Tia are walking through the food court in a mall.
intention: Rob wants to get a pretzel.
moral_action: Rob asks Tia if she would like to get a pretzel with him.
moral_consequence: Tia agrees and even buys Rob an extra large pretzel.
immoral_action: Rob grabs Tia by the arm and drags her to the pretzel counter.
immoral_consequence: Tia pulls away from Rob and goes to another part of the food court on her own.

norm: You shouldn't lie to your children.
situation: Sam's son asks where babies come from.
intention: Sam wants to answer his son's question.
moral_action: Sam explains accurately how babies are made.
moral_consequence: Sam's son learns about babies and human biology.
immoral_action: Sam tells his son that babies come from a stork.
immoral_consequence: Sam's son is misinformed and does not know the truth.

norm: It's rude not in include family in important events.
situation: Leanne is getting married and has just realized that there are too many people on her guest list.
intention: Leanne wants to trim her guest list.
moral_action: Leanne chooses not to invite some friends that she is not very close with.
moral_consequence: Leanne is able to celebrate her wedding with the people who know her best.
immoral_action: Leanne chooses not to invite her aunts, uncles, or cousins even though she has a good relationship with them.
immoral_consequence: Leanne's family are angry at her and family tensions arise.

norm: It's good to make your parents proud.
situation: Jamie is a high school student.
intention: Jamie wants get an upcoming exam over with.
moral_action: Jamie studies hard for the exam and gets an A.
moral_consequence: Jamie's parents are proud and call his grandparents to brag about his good grades.
immoral_action: Jamie does not study for the exam and gets an F.
immoral_consequence: Jamie's parents are disappointed and have to punish Jamie.

norm: You should attend events you are invited to.
situation: Greg has been playing a great video game all day when he realizes his friend Bill's birthday party is coming up that evening.
intention: Greg wants to enjoy his night.
moral_action: Greg stops playing video games and goes to attend Bill's birthday party.
moral_consequence: Bill is happy to see Greg, and they both have a great time.
immoral_action: Greg decides to continue playing video games and skip the birthday party.
immoral_consequence: Bill is hurt that Greg didn't come and their relationship is damaged.

norm: It’s bad to leave your spouse out of things.
situation: Randy and Ryan have been married for two years and are considering buying a home.
intention: Randy wants to surprise Ryan.
moral_action: Randy buys a special set of china to use with Ryan when they finally pick a home and gift wraps it for him.
moral_consequence: Ryan is touched by such an intimate and kind gesture and the two eagerly house shop together.
immoral_action: Randy signs a mortgage on a home without telling Ryan and surprises him with it after work one day.
immoral_consequence: Ryan is off-put by Randy's willingness to make such a big decision alone and needs time from the relationship.

norm: People should be friendly with their neighbors.
situation: Nick has a lawn mower and his neighbor wants to borrow the mower for a price.
intention: Nick wants to let him borrow the mower.
moral_action: Nick lets his neighbor use the mower for free.
moral_consequence: Nick's neighbor lets Nick borrow things for free in return.
immoral_action: Nick agrees and charges his neighbor $15/hr to use the mower.
immoral_consequence: Nick's neighbor feels ripped-off and never invites Nick for events.

norm: It's not okay to betray your friends.
situation: Jake and Earl made a pact many years ago not to date each other's exes.
intention: Earl has a crush on Jake's ex-girlfriend and wants to ask her out.
moral_action: Earl tells Jake about his feelings and asks to renegotiate their agreement.
moral_consequence: Jake sees that Earl is sincere and decides to drop the pact they made when they were young.
immoral_action: Earl goes behind Jake's back and start's dating his ex-girlfriend secretly.
immoral_consequence: Jake feels betrayed when he finds out and ends the friendship.

norm: No one respects a disloyal friend.
situation: Greg was hanging out with his friends at the mall when a popular jock asked him to come with his friends instead.
intention: Greg has to pick who he wants to hang out with.
moral_action: Greg tells the jocks that he's hanging out with his friends, so he's not leaving.
moral_consequence: Greg's friends feel better about Greg and trust him more.
immoral_action: Greg decides to hang out with the jocks and leaves his friends.
immoral_consequence: Greg's friends feel betrayed and don't like him as much as they used to.

norm: Leaving your sister on read is disrespectful.
situation: Jen has a sister who messaged about a problem but also has a serious a work deadline.
intention: Jen wants to get her work done on time.
moral_action: Jen replies quickly explaining her work deadline that she will make sure to get back to her ASAP.
moral_consequence: Jen's sister understands and thanks her for responding anyway.
immoral_action: Jen sees the message but ignores it and focuses on her work.
immoral_consequence: Jen's sister sees that it was read and is upset after being ignored.

norm: It's wrong to break up with someone over something silly.
situation: Jenny's boyfriend got her a chocolate cake for her birthday, but she doesn't like chocolate cake, she prefers vanilla cake.
intention: Jenny wants to react to the cake her boyfriend got her.
moral_action: Jenny shows appreciation for the cake her boyfriend got for her birthday, feeling that it's the thought that counts.
moral_consequence: Jenny's boyfriend is happy that him buying the cake was appreciated by Jenny.
immoral_action: Jenny tells her boyfriend that she's parting ways with him because he doesn't even know what she likes.
immoral_consequence: Jenny's boyfriend is really upset about the breakup especially since it was caused by something so irrational.

norm: It's good to lend items to friends.
situation: Tom's friend asks him if he can borrow Tom's movie collection.
intention: Tom wants to answer his friend.
moral_action: Tom says that his friend can borrow his movie collection and just asks him to be careful with it.
moral_consequence: Tom's friend happily watches some of Tom's movies which he greatly appreciates.
immoral_action: Tom says that his friend can't borrow the movie collection as he might lose it.
immoral_consequence: Tom's friend is unhappy that he can't enjoy some good movies he was hoping to see.

norm: One should always cheer those close to them on.
situation: Jeff is supposed to go to his son’s baseball game tonight, but he doesn’t feel like going.
intention: Jeff wants to decide what he should do tonight.
moral_action: Although he doesn’t feel like it, Jeff decides to go to his son’s game to be supportive.
moral_consequence: Jeff’s son hits a home run, and he is super excited his dad got to see it.
immoral_action: Jeff decides to tell his son he can’t come to the game because he has a work meeting even though he really doesn’t have a meeting.
immoral_consequence: Jeff’s son hits a home run, but he is very upset because his dad did not get to see it.

norm: It's good to get to know y our spouse's feelings.
situation: Fred is confused by his wife Meg's sudden changes in mood.
intention: Fred wants to avoid disagreements with Meg.
moral_action: Fred makes sure he and Meg spend time talking and asking each other about everything and anything.
moral_consequence: Fred gains insight into what drives Meg.
immoral_action: Fred starts buying Meg expensive gifts to cheer her up.
immoral_consequence: Fred fails to get to the root of Meg's dissatisfaction.

norm: You shouldn't be jealous of your partner's good fortune.
situation: Billy worked hard at his job providing for his family while his wife Jill stayed home, then Jill won the lotto.
intention: Billy needs to decide what to do.
moral_action: Billy is quite pleased that his family's happiness no longer depends on his job.
moral_consequence: Billy relaxes and enjoys a much better lifestyle since his wife is rich now.
immoral_action: Billy is envious of his wife's wealth and upset he's not the provider any more.
immoral_consequence: Billy grouses and complains and generally can't enjoy his much higher standard of living.

norm: It is good to discuss issues with your friends.
situation: Sarah has been dealing with a breakup, but hasn't yet told anyone.
intention: Sarah wants to feel better.
moral_action: Sarah tells her friends about the breakup.
moral_consequence: Sarah's friends are able to guide her through the breakup in a healthy manner.
immoral_action: Sarah keeps the breakup a secret from her friends, and instead goes to the bar every night to deal with it alone.
immoral_consequence: Sarah develops a drinking problem that could have easily been prevented by talking to her friends.

norm: It's expected to have good communication with ones spouse
situation: David 's friends are going to the bar but his wife is going to stay home.
intention: David wants to go to the bar with his friends.
moral_action: David goes to the bar and keeps his phone on so that his wife can contact him.
moral_consequence: David's wife is pleased that she can call and talk to him while he's out.
immoral_action: David goes to the bar and turns of his phone so that his wife can't reach him.
immoral_consequence: David's wife is angry at David for not allowing her to call him.

norm: It is good to support and spend time with family
situation: Jack's mother has invited Jack to Christmas dinner at her house this year.
intention: Jack wants to spend Christmas at home this year.
moral_action: Jack goes for a brief time to his mom's dinner, then slips back to his house to celebrate the rest of the eve.
moral_consequence: Jack's mom is happy and feels that Jack truly loves her.
immoral_action: Jack declines the invitation and stays home by himself.
immoral_consequence: Jack's mother is very sad and upset and wonders if Jack loves her anymore.

norm: It's good to listen when your friends care about something.
situation: jane's friend Jeff is hosting a charity event that he wants to talk about.
intention: Jane wants to listen to her favorite song.
moral_action: Jane decides to listen to the song when she gets home, and listens to Jeff talk.
moral_consequence: Jeff appreciates Jane acting as a sounding board for his ideas.
immoral_action: Jane leaves her airpods on while Jeff is talking.
immoral_consequence: Jeff is upset that Jane doesn't react to anything he's saying.

norm: It's rude to make someone lose their job.
situation: Frank's eating at a restaurant and his waiter Joan has gone what Frank feels is a considerable amount of time without refilling Frank's drink.
intention: Frank wants to communicate his displeasure with the waiter's service.
moral_action: Frank leaves a very small tip for the waiter when he receives his check, and suggests Joan try harder in the future, or she might lose her job.
moral_consequence: Joan learns from this experience and provides better service to other restaurant goers.
immoral_action: Frank demands to speak to a manager and when he does, demands Joan should be fired for poor service.
immoral_consequence: Joan gets terminated and everyone else in the restaurant thinks Frank is mean.

norm: It's expected that you place your family first.
situation: Max is with his parents in a theme park and loves the rides.
intention: Max wants to go on the roller coaster.
moral_action: Max brings his parents to a roller coaster, and they ride together.
moral_consequence: Max's parents thank Max for the time together and Max feels accomplished.
immoral_action: Max jumps into the line of a roller coaster without consulting his parents first.
immoral_consequence: Max feels overwhelmed at being alone and his parents fear for him.

norm: It's rude to not accept a gift from someone.
situation: Sasha is celebrating her birthday and somebody brought her a gift she didn't ask for.
intention: Sasha wants to enjoy her birthday.
moral_action: Sasha appreciates the thought that went into the gift and thanks the guest for it.
moral_consequence: The guest feels good about themselves and enjoys the party.
immoral_action: Since Sasha didn't ask for the gift she tells the person to just keep it.
immoral_consequence: The guest is offended that Sasha didn't accept the gift and leaves the party.

norm: You should spend special events with your friends.
situation: Kyla has is graduating from college and her friends asked her if she wants to celebrate with them after the ceremony.
intention: Kyla wants to respond to the invite of her friends.
moral_action: Kyla tells her friends that she will go out with them.
moral_consequence: Kyla will create bonds with her friends over graduating together.
immoral_action: Kyla tells her friend that she's going to go home and sleep.
immoral_consequence: Kyla will miss out bonding with her friends over their mutual graduation.

norm: It's good to give a spouse a special holiday gift.
situation: Robert and his spouse Sally recently moved in with one another and their first Christmas together is coming up.
intention: Robert wants to give Sally something nice for Christmas.
moral_action: Robert buys Sally a silver necklace that she had been wanting for a long time.
moral_consequence: Sally is thrilled to receive the necklace and very thankful for the thoughtful gift.
immoral_action: Robert gives his spouse Sally a big hug and tells her he loves her.
immoral_consequence: Sally is a bit disappointed and feels that Robert should have bought her a nice gift for Christmas.

norm: It's wrong to tell someone else's secrets without their permission.
situation: Jack's friend has had a crush on the same coworker for several years but has a girlfriend.
intention: Jack wants to help his friend figure out who he belongs with.
moral_action: Jack sets up an appointment with a therapist so his friend has a space to work through his attraction.
moral_consequence: Jack's friend is grateful to talk over his girl troubles.
immoral_action: Jack tells the coworker about his friend's attraction to her.
immoral_consequence: Jack's friend is furious as his girlfriend has now dumped him.

norm: You should not abandon your family obligations.
situation: David is going to a family reunion when his friend, Tom, calls and asks him to go see a movie.
intention: David wants to go see the movie.
moral_action: David tells Tom he can go see the movie with him tomorrow.
moral_consequence: David's family is happy to see him at the reunion.
immoral_action: David decides to not go to the family reunion and sees the movie with Tom instead.
immoral_consequence: David's family is disappointed he did not show up to the reunion.

norm: You shouldn't invite yourself to events.
situation: Jack and Jill are planning on attending a party this upcoming weekend, while John eavesdrops on their conversation, unbeknownst to them.
intention: John wants to find plans to fill his empty weekend.
moral_action: John waits until a conversation is being had between all three of them, and asks them if they have any plans for the weekend.
moral_consequence: Jill tells John about the party and both tell John he is more than welcome to come if he has no plans.
immoral_action: John buts in and asks where his invite is.
immoral_consequence: The atmosphere becomes tense and awkward as Jack and Jill try to think of excuses for why they may not end up going.

norm: You should be supportive of your partner and their choices.
situation: Tom notices his wife Sue recently got a tattoo, when he had previously told her not to get one.
intention: Tom wants to talk to Sue about her new tattoo.
moral_action: Tom compliments Sue on her tattoo and is respectful of her choice.
moral_consequence: Tom and Sue laugh together about previous disagreement and Sue thanks Tom.
immoral_action: Tom immediately tells Sue she must remove her tattoo and that he hates it.
immoral_consequence: Sue feels overwhelmed and lets Tom know that he is out of line.

norm: It's not okay to let jealousy ruin family.
situation: Tate's brother is very successful now, and he came to their Christmas party in a very expensive car.
intention: Tate wants to say something to his brother.
moral_action: Tate tells his brother that he must be bagging so many chicks now.
moral_consequence: Tate's brother laughs and is better able to relax at the party.
immoral_action: Tate asks his brother if he thinks he's better than them now.
immoral_consequence: Tate's brother feels uncomfortable and wants to leave the party.

norm: being unfaithful to a partner is morally wrong.
situation: Matt and Natalie are dating and Natalie is going to be away on a work trip for a week.
intention: Matt wants to avoid feeling lonely.
moral_action: Matt calls a friend and invites him over to play video games.
moral_consequence: Natalie is glad Matt had fun with his friend.
immoral_action: Matt calls his ex-girlfriend and invites her over to his house for an intimate evening.
immoral_consequence: Natalie finds out and breaks up with Matt.

norm: It's rude to ignore your parents.
situation: Jacob is a teenager living with his parents and is asked to clean up his messy bedroom.
intention: Jacob needs to decide whether to obey his parents.
moral_action: Jacob decides to clean up his room before playing some video games.
moral_consequence: Jacob's parents appreciate him obeying and offer to buy him more games.
immoral_action: Jacob decides to play video games all day instead of cleaning up his room.
immoral_consequence: Jacob's parents are annoyed at him for not listening and ground him for the weekend.

norm: It's not okay to lose something valuable of someone else's.
situation: Sara was invited to a football tailgate but doesn't have any of the teams apparel, so she borrows a sweatshirt from her sister.
intention: Sara wants to have fun at the tailgate.
moral_action: Sara takes off the sweatshirt and ties it around her waste, so she doesn't lose it.
moral_consequence: Sara's sister is mildly upset that the sweatshirt smells like beer, but gets over it and Sara is thankful.
immoral_action: Sara takes off the sweatshirt, but she can't remember where she put it because she had too much to drink.
immoral_consequence: Sara's sister is mad that her sister comes without the sweatshirt, and she makes Sara buy her a new one.

norm: You shouldn't cut ties with siblings.
situation: Steve's on the phone with his brother, Jonah, who tells Steven that he's gay.
intention: Steven wants to respond to Jonah's statement.
moral_action: Steven tells Jonah that he supports him and that he'll come with him when Jonah tells their parents.
moral_consequence: Jonah feels relieved that he's being accepted and delighted that Steven will be there for him when he tells his parents.
immoral_action: "Steven tells his Jonah that he disapproves of his ""lifestyle choice"" and that he doesn't want to speak to him again."
immoral_consequence: Jonah feels devastated that his brother hates him and begins to cry and contemplate suicide.

norm: It's wrong to hate your family.
situation: Lauren is on the phone with her mom while her husband Peter is beside her and Lauren's mom just said something that upset Lauren.
intention: Lauren wants to tell her mom she doesn't like what she said.
moral_action: Lauren tells her mom that what she said is inappropriate and that she should take it back.
moral_consequence: Peter thinks to himself that his wife is really good at being diplomatic yet straightforward with people.
immoral_action: Lauren tells her mom that she is being ridiculous and that she hates her and doesn't want to talk to her anymore.
immoral_consequence: Peter is stunned that Lauren can be so callous towards her mother and wonders if she'll be like that to him eventually.

norm: You should't kick out your in-laws.
situation: Meg's mother and father-in-law had been staying with her and her husband for a month because they were out of work.
intention: Meg wants to be alone with her husband.
moral_action: Meg tells her mother and father in-law that she and her husband will be going out to dinner.
moral_consequence: Meg and her husband are able to enjoy a pleasant night out.
immoral_action: Meg tells her mother and father in-law that they have to leave by the afternoon.
immoral_consequence: Meg's husband is extremely upset when he finds out how she was treating his family.

norm: You shouldn't distrust your friends.
situation: Tina's friend tells her that she has some serious concerns about Tina's new boyfriend.
intention: Tina wants to respond to her friend's comments about her new boyfriend.
moral_action: Tina thanks her friend for her genuine concern and promises to be cautious and keep in mind what her friend has told her.
moral_consequence: Tina's friend is glad that Tina trusts her, cherishes her opinions and takes into consideration her concerns.
immoral_action: Tina tells her friend that she is just jealous of Tina for having a boyfriend and rudely dismisses all her friend's concerns.
immoral_consequence: Tina's friend feels hurt and offended and questions her friendship with Tina.

norm: You should be courteous to customers
situation: Jen is working a busy line and notices a customer appearing lost nearby.
intention: Jen wants to clear the queue as fast as possible.
moral_action: Jen asks the customer if they need help finding something and multitasks while telling them what aisle to look in.
moral_consequence: The customer is grateful and buys the item from the store.
immoral_action: Jen ignores the customer and focuses solely on the people waiting in line.
immoral_consequence: The customer doesn't find what they need, and they shop elsewhere.

norm: It's good not to string people along in a relationship.
situation: Lila is dating Ken who she doesn't think is long term material but enjoys short term benefits with.
intention: Lila wants to talk to Ken about their relationship.
moral_action: Lila tells Ken they should find other people.
moral_consequence: Ken will be able to find someone who is long term material for him.
immoral_action: Lila tells Ken she wants to keep dating him, and they have a future together.
immoral_consequence: Ken will think Lila really likes him when she is only using him.

norm: It's bad for one to hate their partner's child.
situation: Barbara is talking to her husband Jim and his daughter Cara.
intention: Barbara wants Jim to pay more attention to her.
moral_action: Barbara tells Jim that the three of them should do more activities as family.
moral_consequence: Jim is happy that Barbara cares about his daughter and Cara trusts her more.
immoral_action: Barbara tells Jim that she doesn't want Cara living with them.
immoral_consequence: Jim tells Barbara he won't choose her over his child and divorces her.

norm: You should invite people over for the holidays.
situation: Every year, Janet hosts a Christmas party for her big family.
intention: Janet wants to have a more relaxed holiday this year.
moral_action: Janet decides to have a smaller gathering with a few close friends.
moral_consequence: Janet has a great time with her friends that night.
immoral_action: Janet decides to spend Christmas by herself, binge-watching Netflix.
immoral_consequence: Janet enjoys the alone time, but feels lonely without anyone there.

norm: It's good to want what is best for your country.
situation: Donald and Hillary are discussing the state of politics when Hillary asks Donald what his opinion is of the government.
intention: Donald wants to answer Hillary's question.
moral_action: Donald tells Hillary that the government is a wonderful institution that should be preserved at any cost.
moral_consequence: Hillary smiles as she agrees with Donald thankful that he loves his country.
immoral_action: Donald tells Hillary that the country needs to be destroyed from within to drain the swamp.
immoral_consequence: Hillary thinks Donald is evil because he wants to destroy the very thing that allows them both to be so prosperous.

norm: It's not okay to be insulting to a partner's family.
situation: Trevor is taking Max, his life partner, to meet Trevor's family.
intention: Max wants to be able to joke around with Trevor's family.
moral_action: Max thinks carefully and gently jokes and teases with the members of Trevor's family.
moral_consequence: Trevor's family are all smiling when Max and Trevor leave and the family is more approving of their relationship.
immoral_action: Max is nervous and blurts out inappropriate things making some family members embarrassed while others laughed at them.
immoral_consequence: Max causes much unrest and discontent among Trevor's family causing stress on Max and Trevor's relationship.

norm: It's good to go on a second date.
situation: Julie meets a man named Michael for a first date at a coffee shop, but she isn't sure if they have romantic chemistry.
intention: Julie wants to find a partner for a long term relationship.
moral_action: Julie agrees to go on a second date with Michael and has a great experience.
moral_consequence: Julie and Michael continue to date and eventually get married.
immoral_action: Julie ignores Michael's calls and looks for other dating partners on an app.
immoral_consequence: Julie meets someone else on the app and begins a relationship with them.

norm: It is good to not give up.
situation: Terry failed a class in college which is disheartening to him.
intention: Terry wants to consider what to do next.
moral_action: Terry continues taking classes and studies more.
moral_consequence: Terry gets a degree.
immoral_action: Terry drops out of college.
immoral_consequence: Terry does not get a degree.

norm: You should not intervine  in other peoples relathionships.
situation: Josh is at a restaurant eating when he notices a couple at the table next to him somewhat loudly arguing about the man's lack of sex drive.
intention: Josh wants to say something to the couple.
moral_action: Josh tells the couple that they're both being very loud and that he can hear every word of their private conversation.
moral_consequence: The couple are both grateful that Josh pointed out they're embarrassing themselves in public.
immoral_action: Josh tells the couple that it's the guy's fault, and he needs to get his bedroom game down quick,  or she should leave him.
immoral_consequence: Both the man and the woman are infuriated that Josh interjected into their conversation and had the audacity to talk about their sex life.

norm: You shouldn't betray your friends confidence in you to others.
situation: Lisa's friend Robin is pregnant and has asked Lisa to keep it a secret because she is planning to get an abortion.
intention: Lisa wants to help Robin.
moral_action: Lisa looks up organizations that can help Robin through the process of getting a safe abortion.
moral_consequence: Robin is able to get the help she needs and her friendship with Lisa is stronger.
immoral_action: Lisa tells Robin's parents that Robin is pregnant and doesn't plan to keep the baby.
immoral_consequence: Robin stops trusting Lisa and her parents are upset with her.

norm: It's bad to be with someone that you don't want.
situation: Jane has been sending expensive gifts to Nathan in an attempt to woo him into dating her, but he has no interest in her.
intention: Nathan wants to talk to Jane.
moral_action: Nathan tells Jane that he cannot accept her gifts, and she deserves to be with someone who takes interest in her.
moral_consequence: Jane respects Nathan's rejection, and she eventually finds love.
immoral_action: Nathan agrees to date Jane because he enjoys the gifts.
immoral_consequence: Jane never gets any love from Nathan and begins to feel sad and unloved.

norm: It's good to talk with your parents.
situation: Abby is in a kitchen sitting with Herb and Ann, her parents.
intention: Abby is feeling depressed and wants to communicate with Ann and Herb.
moral_action: Abby explains to Ann and Herb how Abby feels.
moral_consequence: Herb and Ann recommend a doctor visit because Herb and Ann have experience with these type of issues.
immoral_action: Abby keeps her feelings internalized and talks about tv shows.
immoral_consequence: Abby begins to feel worse and worse and stops taking care of herself eventually.

norm: it's expected that parents go to their daughter's recitals.
situation: Brian's daughter had just finished her fifth year in ballet dancing.
intention: Brian wants his daughter to get a bouquet of flowers at her recital.
moral_action: Brian goes to the recital and presents his daughter with the bouquet of flowers in person.
moral_consequence: Brian's daughter is happy to see him and smiles during her whole dance number.
immoral_action: Brian goes to work instead of the recital, opting to send a bouquet of flowers in his place.
immoral_consequence: Brian's daughter was upset at her father's absence and forgot several of her steps.

norm: You shouldn't use someone else's social media account.
situation: Jenny is sitting next to her friend's computer and sees that their social media site account is signed in.
intention: Jenny wants to send someone a message on the social media site.
moral_action: Jenny signs into her own account to send something over on the social media site.
moral_consequence: Jenny's friend is glad Jenny didn't use their account on the social media site which would have been weird.
immoral_action: Jenny uses her friend's social media account to send a message to someone.
immoral_consequence: Jenny's friend is angry at Jenny for using their account which they see as a violation of privacy.

norm: It is good to ignore people who are ignorant.
situation: Ted is walking down the street when a group of boys start to call him names.
intention: Ted wants to stop hearing the taunting.
moral_action: Ted puts on his headphones and turns the music on loud.
moral_consequence: Ted walks the rest of the way to his house without hearing the boys.
immoral_action: Ted rushes up to the boys and starts to beat them all up.
immoral_consequence: The police are called to the brawl and Ted is taken to the police station.

norm: You shouldn't try to steal from work.
situation: Ross's band is playing their first show soon.
intention: Ross wants to print out some flyers to advertise the show.
moral_action: Ross goes to a print shop and prints the flyers theres.
moral_consequence: Ross keeps his job and successfully advertises the show.
immoral_action: Ross takes paper and printer ink from the supply closet at work, without asking for permission, to print the flyers.
immoral_consequence: Ross is caught by the security cameras and fired.

norm: It's not okay to let drugs ruin your relationships.
situation: Mike is a pot user and has a hard time holding a job, making him poor, and his parents are urging him to quit for his own good.
intention: Mike wants to decide between his parents or pot.
moral_action: Mike decides to make efforts to quit and asks his parents for help to do so.
moral_consequence: Mike's parents are very happy and send Mike to rehab, which successfully breaks his addiction.
immoral_action: Mike decides to keep using pot and starts selling his parents’ belongings to support his addiction.
immoral_consequence: Mike's parents give up on him and kick him out, putting him on the street with no home.

norm: It's good to stay close to your friend group.
situation: Lilly is on a school field trip at a museum to look at paintings with her classmates.
intention: Lilly wants to go see the sculptures.
moral_action: Lilly asks her classmates to go see the sculptures with her.
moral_consequence: Lilly feels safe knowing she has friends with her, and they all enjoy looking at the exhibit together.
immoral_action: Lilly goes off on her own.
immoral_consequence: Upon returning, Lilly's classmates are nowhere to be found, and she's alone in an unfamiliar town.

norm: It is wrong to ditch a friend.
situation: Kasey is waiting for his friend to return from the bathroom when his friends invite him to play a carnival game.
intention: Kasey wants to have fun.
moral_action: Kasey plays games on his cell phone until his friend comes out of the bathroom.
moral_consequence: Kasey's friend feels happy that his friend is by his side.
immoral_action: Kasey goes with other friends to play the carnival games.
immoral_consequence: 's friend wanders around depressed as he searches for Kasey.

norm: You shouldn't avoid your parent's significant other.
situation: Dan calls his father Mark to let him know he is coming over, but learns that only Mark's new wife Sarah is at the house.
intention: Dan wants to pick up a lawn mower Mark has decided to give to him.
moral_action: Dan stops inside to say hi to Sarah and see how she is doing before inviting her to point him towards the lawn mower.
moral_consequence: Sarah feels included as part of Dan and Mark's family, improving her relationship with Dan and his relationship with Mark.
immoral_action: Dan enters the house through the garage and picks up the lawn mower without saying anything to Sarah, then leaves abruptly.
immoral_consequence: Sarah will feel as if Dan's avoiding her, which will poison Dan's relationship with his father Mark.

norm: It's rude to point out embarrassing things about someone.
situation: Henry is at work when Jane walks and says hello with chocolate stains on her teeth.
intention: Henry wants to tell Jane she has a stain on her teeth.
moral_action: Henry motions for Jane to come close and whispers that she might want to check her teeth in the bathroom.
moral_consequence: Jane is thankful that Henry saved her from embarrassing herself in front of the other coworkers.
immoral_action: Henry loudly points at Jane and while laughing, tells her she looks ridiculous with her teeth all stained.
immoral_consequence: Jane is embarrassed and begins to cry and runs to the bathroom.

norm: You shouldn't get involved with your friend's ex.
situation: Robert's friend recently broke up with his girlfriend, and Robert is finding himself attracted to her.
intention: Robert doesn't want to make things uncomfortable with his friend.
moral_action: Robert talks to his friend and asks if he would be ok with Robert asking his ex out.
moral_consequence: Robert's friend is hesitant, but he eventually agrees to let Robert ask his ex out without any hard feelings.
immoral_action: Robert asks his friend's ex out and keeps it secret from his friend.
immoral_consequence: Robert's friend finds out that Robert asked out his ex and feels betrayed.

norm: It's wrong to cut off your kids from your life.
situation: Cindy and her adult daughter get into an argument and stop speaking to each other for a while, which hurts Cindy.
intention: Cindy wants to deal with the pain.
moral_action: Cindy tries to make up and start a positive relationship with her daughter to avoid fighting.
moral_consequence: Cindy and her daughter reconnect over time and no longer fight.
immoral_action: Cindy refuses to talk to her daughter any longer, so she doesn't have another fight.
immoral_consequence: Cindy never gets to see her grandchildren and is never satisfied with the situation.

norm: It's expected that you'll help family members.
situation: Tom notices his family is doing Spring cleaning in another room, while he is lying on the couch.
intention: Tom wants to see the house get cleaned in person.
moral_action: Tom decides to watch the cleaning by helping out his family in the room.
moral_consequence: Tom's family thanks Tom and Tom feels generous.
immoral_action: Tom decides to enter the room and watch his family clean without helping them.
immoral_consequence: Tom's family insults Tom and Tom feels selfish for not helping.

norm: It's bad to hit on a friend's wife.
situation: Thomas is out to dinner with his friend Phil and Phil's wife Susan.
intention: Thomas wants to spend more time with Susan.
moral_action: Thomas tells Phil and Susan he enjoys hanging out with them as friends, and they should more often.
moral_consequence: Susan thinks Thomas is polite and hangs out with him more.
immoral_action: Thomas waits for Phil to go to the bathroom and then tells Susan they should go on a date.
immoral_consequence: Susan tells Phil what happened when Phil gets back and Phil ends his friendship with Thomas.

norm: You shouldn't cheat on anyone.
situation: Carla is on a girls weekend out of town and meets George at a bar who buys her drinks and dances with her.
intention: Carla wants to have a carefree weekend.
moral_action: Carla thanks George for the drink and dances with him, but tells him she has a boyfriend.
moral_consequence: Carla calls her boyfriend to check in and tells him about George, he is proud of her honesty and suggest they talk about taking their relationship to the next level.
immoral_action: Carla decides since it is a girls weekend she can do what she wants and takes George back to her hotel room that night.
immoral_consequence: Carla's friends find out what she did and tell her boyfriend who breaks up with her.

norm: It's not expected that a baby be invited to parties.
situation: Joseph is throwing a big party for all his friends and is sitting at the dinner table making invitations.
intention: Joseph wants to invite the Davis family to the party.
moral_action: Joseph writes a card to Baron and Bette Davis and invites the two of them over.
moral_consequence: Baron and Bette Davis are both delighted to be invited to the party and look to book a babysitter for the night.
immoral_action: Joseph writes a card to Baron and Bette Davis and their infant son, Glenn.
immoral_consequence: Baron and Bette are both mildly insulted that Joseph thinks they'd bring a baby to a party.

norm: You should be honest about having children
situation: Casey is on the fence about having children, and his wife is asking him whether he wants kids.
intention: Casey has to tell his wife whether he wants kids.
moral_action: Casey tells his wife that he doesn't think he's ready to have kids.
moral_consequence: Casey feels good that he wasn't afraid to tell his wife the truth.
immoral_action: Casey tells his wife that he would love to have kids.
immoral_consequence: Casey regrets telling his wife this and is scared about having to take care of kids.

norm: It's wrong to take advantage of your parent's kindness.
situation: Tommy moved away from home to attend college in another state and, though his parents pay for his education, he struggles with finances as a young student.
intention: Tommy wants to get the money together to go on a weekend trip.
moral_action: Tommy finds a job cleaning out an abandoned house, off campus, that pays enough for him to go away for the weekend.
moral_consequence: His parents are proud of him because he found a way to make some money for himself, and he enjoys his weekend more because he worked to get there.
immoral_action: Tommy calls his parents and claims that he needs some money to pay for an extracurricular, school related activity, instead of telling them it’s for a personal trip.
immoral_consequence: Tommy's parents are duped into sending money under false pretenses and Tommy feels guilty about going on his trip because he lied to get there.

norm: It's wrong to attack old friends.
situation: Ed is on Facebook and sees that a friend of his from high school posted a video on his social media account that Ed doesn't agree with.
intention: Ed wants to comment on the video.
moral_action: Ed writes a post pointing out some flaws in the video and agreeing with it on some things.
moral_consequence: Ed's friend is grateful for Ed's insightful post and strikes up a pleasant conversation with Ed.
immoral_action: "Ed tells his friend to wake up and see that he is deceived by ""the man"", calling him a fool."
immoral_consequence: Ed's old friend is disgusted by Ed's behavior and reacts angrily to Ed's comment.

norm: You should always think that cheating is wrong.
situation: Tim is taking a test when a classmate whispers to him for help on his own test.
intention: Tim wants to respond to his classmate.
moral_action: Tim says no and focuses on his own test.
moral_consequence: Tim manages to get a good grade on his test.
immoral_action: Tim provides test answers to his classmate.
immoral_consequence: Tim's teacher catches both of them and gives them a failing grade.

norm: You should always help people out at work.
situation: Gary is in his office at work when Glen, a coworker, comes over and asks for help on an Excel macro.
intention: Gary wants to respond to Glen's request.
moral_action: Gary tells Glen that he'll come over to his desk and show him how to do it.
moral_consequence: Glen is delighted that Gary will help him and relieved he'll figure out how to create a macro.
immoral_action: Gary tells Glen that they probably have a manual somewhere around here, and he should look there.
immoral_consequence: Glen is annoyed because he's aware that there are manuals, and he was requesting human help.

norm: It's fun to go out with a lot of people.
situation: Ron is very bored on the weekend, not sure what to do.
intention: Ron wants to go out with someone.
moral_action: Ron invites his best friend, along with a few of their other close friends out.
moral_consequence: Ron and them spend the entire night together, having a blast.
immoral_action: Ron invites his best friend out to get dinner and a movie.
immoral_consequence: Ron has a nice time, but admits it could be better.

norm: It's good to show pride in your Alma Mater.
situation: Dominick is opening his mail and one item is a solicitation for donations from his Alma Mater.
intention: Dominick wants to decide what to do about the solicitation.
moral_action: Dominick decides to make a sizable donation to his Alma Mater because he remembers the school fondly.
moral_consequence: Thanks to donations Dominick's Alma Mater is able to provide quality education to more students which makes Dominick feel satisfaction.
immoral_action: Dominick throws the letter from his Alma Mater in the trash deciding that his college years are in the past and do not matter anymore.
immoral_consequence: Future students of Dominick's Alma Mater do not get the same quality education as he got but Dominick is unbothered.

norm: You're expected to use Christmas presents given to you.
situation: Bobby's office is holding a secret Santa gift exchange.
intention: Bobby wants to take part in secret Santa.
moral_action: Bobby takes his secret Santa gift and mentions all the things he can do with it.
moral_consequence: The gift giver will feel appreciated.
immoral_action: Bobby takes his secret Santa gift and wants to exchange it with a gift someone else received.
immoral_consequence: The gift giver will feel awkward and think they are bad at picking presents.

norm: You shouldn't back out on an agreement with your partner.
situation: Tom promised his wife, Susan, that he will mow the lawn today but remembered that he is supposed to meet some friends at the bar in less than an hour.
