intention: Tom wants to enjoy his evening.
moral_action: Tom quickly mows the lawn as promised before heading out to the bar to meet his friends.
moral_consequence: Susan sees that the yard looks great and is pleased with the job Tom has done.
immoral_action: Tom forgoes mowing the lawn in order to go out to the bar with his friends.
immoral_consequence: Susan is upset and disappointed that Tom broke his promise.

norm: It's wrong to date others when you're already dating someone.
situation: Ryan is dating a girl but has lost his feelings for her when he meets a new girl that peaks his interest.
intention: Ryan wants to date the new girl.
moral_action: Ryan breaks up with his current girlfriend before dating the new girl.
moral_consequence: The new girl appreciates having a new man in her life.
immoral_action: Ryan starts going out with the new girl while he's still in a relationship.
immoral_consequence: The new girl finds out and no longer wishes to see him.

norm: It's wrong to keep secrets from family members
situation: Susan just went to the hospital to get an abortion and has not told her family about it.
intention: Susan doesn't want to be rejected by her mother by telling her about the abortion.
moral_action: Susan tells her mom about the abortion and how she felt like had no other valid option.
moral_consequence: Susan's mom is sympathetic and Susan feels heard and accepted.
immoral_action: Susan doesn't tell her mom about the abortion.
immoral_consequence: Susan feels lonely and dishonest.

norm: It is rude to ignore guests.
situation: Tom has the day off of work and wants to have some friends over.
intention: Tom wants to invite some friends over for drinks.
moral_action: Tom hosts a small get together and catches up with his buddies.
moral_consequence: Everyone has a good time at Tom's house.
immoral_action: Tom has friends over but focuses more on playing his video game.
immoral_consequence: Tom's friends feel that he is rude and don't have a good time.

norm: It is good to talk through situations with partner.
situation: Jane has been married to her partner for three years.
intention: Jane wants to move on from a disagreement she is having with her partner.
moral_action: Jane and her partner sit down and discuss their disagreement and communicate openly.
moral_consequence: Jane and her partner get over the disagreement and grow closer to one another thanks to the communication.
immoral_action: Jane and her partner bury and ignore the issue as if it never happened.
immoral_consequence: Jane becomes more passive aggressive and resentful towards her partner eventually leading to their separation.

norm: It's bad to give your significant other an ultimatum.
situation: William and his wife Anne are having an argument regarding Ann's excessive use of the family credit card.
intention: William wants to tell Anne that she should spend less money.
moral_action: William gently explains to Anne that mathematically they're on a path to bankruptcy, and he doesn't want to see their future in jeopardy.
moral_consequence: Anne feels like William really cares about their family and although she feels slightly ashamed about the situation she is determined to do better.
immoral_action: William curtly tells Anne that if she doesn't stop using the credit card immediately, he is going to leave her and ask for a divorce.
immoral_consequence: Anne begins to feel insecure about the relationship and beings to question whether William is controlling.

norm: You shouldn't bail on hanging out with your partner.
situation: Chelsea has plans to see a movie with her girlfriend Shelby but her friends are inviting her out to a club.
intention: Chelsea wants to go out with her friends.
moral_action: Chelsea tells her friends she can meet up with them after the movie is over and asks if Shelby wants to join them.
moral_consequence: Shelby feels happy to be included by Chelsea and the two have a fun night out.
immoral_action: Chelsea tells Shelby that she can't go to the movie because she just made new plans with her friends.
immoral_consequence: Shelby is hurt that she is not a priority for Chelsea and begins to question their relationship.

norm: It's wrong to be unfaithful to someone you love
situation: Brad and Jennifer are married, and one of Brad's old girlfriends calls him up.
intention: Brad wants to communicate with his old girlfriend.
moral_action: Brad talks to his old girlfriend on the phone and tells Jennifer about it later.
moral_consequence: Jennifer is happy Brad told her about the conversation.
immoral_action: Brad meets up with his old girlfriend, and they sleep together.
immoral_consequence: Jennifer finds out and files for a divorce.

norm: It's good to recommend places to your friends and family.
situation: Jane was talking to her friend Amanda about restaurants in the city.
intention: Jane wants to finish the conversation.
moral_action: Jane helps Amanda with several recommendations for restaurants that day.
moral_consequence: Amanda enjoys a fantastic meal at one of those recommendations that evening.
immoral_action: Jane doesn't recommend any places because she wants to keep them to herself.
immoral_consequence: Amanda ends up choosing a terrible restaurant to eat at that evening.

norm: It's good to go to a concert with your friends.
situation: Steve is invited to a concert by his college friends and also by a girl, Rani, that he really likes.
intention: Steve wants to attend the concert.
moral_action: Steve tells his friends that he will go with them and asks Rani to join him.
moral_consequence: Steve's friends enjoy spending time with Steve and with Rani and are happy.
immoral_action: Steve tells his friends that he cannot go but tells Rani he will go with her.
immoral_consequence: Steve's friends see him at the concert and are angry with him.

norm: It's wrong to not include disabled people in events.
situation: Joan needs to book a restaurant for the office Christmas party.
intention: Joan wants a restaurant with top reviews.
moral_action: Joan ensures the top rated restaurant she selects can accommodate handicapped patrons.
moral_consequence: Joan is able to pull off a party that everyone can attend.
immoral_action: Joan selects a top rated restaurant that doesn't accommodate handicapped patrons.
immoral_consequence: Joan faces censure as the handicapped members of staff are unable to attend the party.

norm: You shouldn't talk shit behind someone's back.
situation: Heather is a college student, and is friendly with a large number of other students including Dave and Ray.
intention: Heather wants Ray to pay attention to her.
moral_action: Heather tells Ray a story she heard about Dave helping out during campus clean up.
moral_consequence: Ray reacts favorably to the story, and suggests they get together with Dave later in the week for a study session.
immoral_action: Heather tells Ray a story she heard about Dave, most of it unfavorable and only partly true.
immoral_consequence: Ray doesn't like how Heather is talking about her friend, and refuses to speak to her for the rest of the semester.

norm: It's bad to be overly dramatic.
situation: Jill just met Bill at a baseball game and instantly is attracted to him.
intention: Jill wants Bill to pay attention to her.
moral_action: Jill strikes up a conversation with Bill and asks him about himself.
moral_consequence: Bill thinks Jill is sweet and develops an interest in her.
immoral_action: Jill walks up to Bill and tells him that if he doesn't date her she'll kill herself.
immoral_consequence: Bill wonders about Jill's sanity and stays away from her.

norm: You shouldn't share certain things with other people.
situation: James' best friend is staying at his house for a sleepover and forget his toothbrush.
intention: James wants to help his friend brush his teeth.
moral_action: James tells his friend to order a new toothbrush from Amazon with 2-hour Prime delivery.
moral_consequence: James' friend orders a new toothbrush, and they continue enjoying the sleepover.
immoral_action: James tells his friend that he can use James' toothbrush for that night.
immoral_consequence: James' friend calls him gross and insists on being driven home to get his toothbrush.

norm: You should not reveal someone's personal information.
situation: Aaron is a teacher with a dyslexic student and the class is asking why this student gets more exam time.
intention: Aaron wants to give the class an answer.
moral_action: Aaron tells the class that they have no place asking that question and that it's confidential.
moral_consequence: The student's secret is safe and no one thinks less of him.
immoral_action: Aaron tells the class that the student is dyslexic and can't read well.
immoral_consequence: The students mock and bully the dyslexic student because they think he's dumb.

norm: It's good to lend your support in an election.
situation: Riley lives in a country with a democracy and it is election day.
intention: Riley wants to spend her day wisely.
moral_action: Riley knows many positions and issues on the ballot can affect her day to day life, so she makes the time to cast her votes in the election.
moral_consequence: Riley makes sure her voice is heard along with others and the country's citizens keep their government in check.
immoral_action: Riley spends all day at work earning a living and misses out on a chance to vote.
immoral_consequence: Riley and people like her grow apathetic about their government since they don't participate and the government abuses their apathy more and more over time.

norm: It's wrong to post someone's conversation online without their permission.
situation: Tom has a long and important business conversation with his friend during lunch.
intention: Tom wants to let her friend know what he thought about their conversation.
moral_action: Tom posts in a private message to his friend what he thought about the lunch conversation.
moral_consequence: Tom's friend thanks Tom for respecting his privacy, and they exchange further details.
immoral_action: Tom posts publicly on Facebook to his friend what he thought about their conversation.
immoral_consequence: Tom's friend gets mad at Tom for revealing private info in public and Tom feels sorry.

norm: It's good to communicate about problems with a friend.
situation: Eric's friend Joe is totally absorbed with his girlfriend, and Eric isn't sure how to deal with it.
intention: Eric wants to stop feeling annoyed by the situation.
moral_action: Eric has a conversation with Joe about the amount of time he spends with his girlfriend.
moral_consequence: Joe realizes he's spending too much time with his girlfriend and starts seeing his friends again.
immoral_action: Eric leaves Joe with his girlfriend and stops calling him, ignoring the pain.
immoral_consequence: Joe is left without any other friends and feels lonely.

norm: It's wrong not to invite your friends over.
situation: Jerry's friend Dan hears that he's going to have a party to show off his new house.
intention: Jerry wants to invite people to his house party.
moral_action: Jerry invites Dan and all of his friends to the house party.
moral_consequence: Dan has a good time at the party and loves Jerry's new house.
immoral_action: Jerry invites everyone to come to the house party except Dan.
immoral_consequence: Dan feels left out and doesn't want to be friends with Jerry anymore.

norm: It's polite to be friendly with an ex-partner.
situation: Lily and boyfriend, Phillip, had a bad break up a week ago and now she has just spotted him at the grocery store.
intention: Lily wants to avoid a scene with Phillip.
moral_action: Lily stops to chat with Phillip and keeps it simple with friendly small talk.
moral_consequence: Phillip tells Lily that it was nice to see her.
immoral_action: Lily pretends she doesn't see Phillip and quickly turns around and walks the other way.
immoral_consequence: Phillip notices Lily walking away from him and doesn't understand why she's behaving like that.

norm: It's nice to keep heirlooms within the family.
situation: Joni's grandmother just passed away and was left her jewelry in the will.
intention: Joni wants to decide what to do with the jewlery.
moral_action: Joni keeps the jewelry in a safety deposit box.
moral_consequence: When Joni wants to remember her grandma, she goes to the safety deposit box to reminisce.
immoral_action: Joni sells the jewelry to a pawn shop, so she can buy some new speakers for her car.
immoral_consequence: Later on in life Joni wishes she had something to remind her of her grandmother.

norm: It is unacceptable to snoop in someone's phone or computer.
situation: Heidi was always curious about what other people did.
intention: Heidi's boyfriend is outside, and she wants to find out what he does on his phone.
moral_action: Heidi waits until her boyfriend comes in and asks what is on his phone.
moral_consequence: Heidi's boyfriend shows her everything she is curious about.
immoral_action: Heidi snoops on his phone while her boyfriend is outside.
immoral_consequence: Heidi's boyfriend comes in without her noticing and catches her.

norm: It's important to communicate when your friend upsets you.
situation: John is having a conversation with his friend Jim when he makes a comment that doesn't sit well with Jim.
intention: Jim wants to react to the comment.
moral_action: Jim tells John that his comment upset him and explains why.
moral_consequence: John apologizes to Jim for making him feel that way and the two become closer over it.
immoral_action: Jim keeps his feelings to himself and begins acting cold toward John.
immoral_consequence: Things become tense between the two as John wonders why Jim is acting so strangely.

norm: You shouldn't force your friends to walk home.
situation: Randy had drove some friends over to his place to hang out, and they ended up drinking, but he promised to give them a ride home.
intention: Randy wants to avoid getting a drunk driving charge.
moral_action: Randy apologizes that he can't drive himself but pays for them to get an Uber home.
moral_consequence: Randy's friends understand and are glad he was able to keep his word.
immoral_action: Randy lets them know he is in no condition to drive and suggests they walk since it's not very far.
immoral_consequence: Randy's friends feel let down by him and think twice about hanging out in the future.

norm: You should always be honest with your friends.
situation: Linda thinks Raylene's new boyfriend is dangerous, and Raylene has just asked Linda what she thinks of him.
intention: Linda wants to answer Raylene's question.
moral_action: Linda tells Raylene that her boyfriend seems to have abusive tendencies.
moral_consequence: Raylene starts seeing the tendencies and breaks up with him before the relationship gets far.
immoral_action: Linda tells Raylene that her new boyfriend seems like an OK person.
immoral_consequence: Raylene's boyfriend becomes abusive and cuts Raylene off from her friends.

norm: It's wrong to steal from your parents.
situation: Amy is looking for some money to go shopping with friends and notices her father's wallet on the table.
intention: Amy wants to consider taking money from her father's wallet.
moral_action: Amy decides against taking money out of her father's wallet and simply waits to ask him for money.
moral_consequence: The father of Amy obliges and gives Amy some spending money.
immoral_action: Amy quickly takes money from her father's wallet and goes shopping with her friends.
immoral_consequence: Amy's father finds out Amy stole money out of his wallet and severely punishes her.

norm: You shouldn't lie to your romantic partner
situation: Barbara has been laid off by her company, and she lives with her boyfriend Daniel who works a night shift.
intention: Barbara wants to explain her recent situation to Daniel.
moral_action: Barbara tells Daniel that she has been laid off due to reconstruction of her company.
moral_consequence: Daniel takes a week off and goes on a vacation with Barbara to make her happy.
immoral_action: Barbara still goes out every morning and stays at a coffee shop surfing on the internet without telling Daniel about her layoff.
immoral_consequence: Daniel feels disrespected after knowing that Barbara has actually been laid off.

norm: You should not shirk household chores.
situation: Greg's mother has ordered him to clean his messy room but his friend James has invited him over to play video games.
intention: Greg needs to decide whether to accept James' invitation.
moral_action: Greg tells James that he can play tomorrow and spends the next couple of hours cleaning his room.
moral_consequence: Greg's mother is happy with him and rewards him with a cookie.
immoral_action: Greg accepts James' invitation to play video games and skips out on cleaning his room like he was supposed to.
immoral_consequence: Greg's mother gets upset and grounds him for one week when he gets home.

norm: It's wrong to lie about going to commit suicide.
situation: Kim has been punished by her parents, but she thinks that she didn't do wrong.
intention: Kim wants her parents to know it feels to hurt.
moral_action: Kim sits down and talks to her parents and explains how she feels.
moral_consequence: Kim and her parents feel much more open after they have their discussion.
immoral_action: Kim tells her parents that she wants to kill herself because she is so sad.
immoral_consequence: Kim's parents call a specialist and have her committed to a hospital.

norm: It's rude to break a commitment you've made.
situation: Leo is at home in the afternoon when his friend Jim calls to confirm they're still going bowling tonight.
intention: Leo wants to tell Jim his plans for the night.
moral_action: Leo tells Jim that he is definitely still going bowling and that he'll meet Jim at the alley tonight.
moral_consequence: Jim is delighted that Leo has confirmed their plans and is excited for some bowling fun.
immoral_action: Leo tells Jim that he's actually got some old friends coming from out of town, so he'll have to skip bowling.
immoral_consequence: Jim is annoyed that Leo hasn't told him this until now and that is cancelling plans at the last minute.

norm: You shouldn't cheat on people you love.
situation: Jeff is at a bar without his wife, when a beautiful woman starts flirting with him and invites him back to her place.
intention: Jeff wants to spend intimate time with a woman.
moral_action: Jeff is flattered but turns her down and returns home to be with his wife.
moral_consequence: Jeff's wife enjoys spending the rest of the evening with him.
immoral_action: Jeff leaves the bar with the woman and spend the night at her apartment.
immoral_consequence: Jeff's wife finds out he's cheated on her and files for divorce.

norm: It's good to help your family with their money problems.
situation: Ian's brother Mike can't afford to pay the rent for his apartment and may be evicted.
intention: Ian wants to make sure that Mike has a place to live.
moral_action: Ian gives Mike money to pay the rent.
moral_consequence: Mike is able to stay in his apartment for another month.
immoral_action: Ian gives Mike the address of a good homeless shelter.
immoral_consequence: Mike gets evicted and goes to the shelter.

norm: It's good to invite your family to an important event.
situation: James was about to graduate from college in the same town where all his family lived.
intention: James wants to enjoy his graduation day.
moral_action: James invites all his family members to his graduation and eats dinner with them before leaving to party with his friends.
moral_consequence: James receives many congratulations and graduation gifts from his family.
immoral_action: James doesn't mention his graduation date to his family and instead spends the day partying with his friends.
immoral_consequence: Over the next two weeks, James gets several reproachful phone calls from his relatives.

norm: You should be welcoming of family members.
situation: Greg just started a new job in a new city and his aunt and uncle want to visit him.
intention: Greg wants to set up his new apartment.
moral_action: Greg tells his aunt and uncle that they can visit, but they'll likely need to help him set up his furniture.
moral_consequence: Greg's aunt and uncle visit Greg and help him get his apartment set up.
immoral_action: Greg tells his aunt and uncle that he doesn't want them to visit because he's too busy.
immoral_consequence: Greg's aunt and uncle understand but are afraid to ask Greg about visiting another time.

norm: It's wrong to protect someone who did something illegal.
situation: Stan and James are at an internet cafe when James lies to the cashier about his playing time.
intention: Stan wants to interject in the conversation.
moral_action: Stan tells James to fess up and pay the man what he deserves.
moral_consequence: James pays what he actually owes and leaves the cafe safely.
immoral_action: Stan says that he's confident that James is telling the truth and supports Jame’s story.
immoral_consequence: The man checks the camera footage and verifies that both Stan and James are lying, then calls the police.

norm: You should eat dinner with your family.
situation: Tom and his family ran into some of his coworkers at a restaurant when out for dinner.
intention: Tom wanted to talk about problems he'd been having at work.
moral_action: Tom invited his coworkers to join him and his family for dinner.
moral_consequence: Tom's family learned about the stress he and his coworkers were under.
immoral_action: Tom sat at a table with this coworkers leaving his family alone at another table.
immoral_consequence: Tom's family gave him the cold shoulder on the way home.

norm: Children shouldn't secretly work against their parents.
situation: Joe's dad spanked him for violating curfew.
intention: Joe is angry at his dad and wants to express that.
moral_action: Joe tells both his parents he's upset at dad and feels mistreated.
moral_consequence: Joe and his parents calmly talk things over and come to an understanding.
immoral_action: Joe secretly plants a pair of women's panties in his dad's car where his mom will find it.
immoral_consequence: Joe's mother is mad at his dad but eventually talk it out and punish Joe.

norm: It is wrong to just like someone for their looks.
situation: Bert has hired a matchmaker.
intention: Bert wants to go on a date.
moral_action: Bert chooses a girl to date who has multiple things in common with him.
moral_consequence: Bert has a fun date and develops a connection with another person.
immoral_action: Bert chooses a pretty girl to date who does not have anything in common with him.
immoral_consequence: Bert is bored out of his mind during the date.

norm: It's good to visit people's houses.
situation: Mary is lounging around the house and gets a phone call from Beth to come over.
intention: Mary wants to relax.
moral_action: Mary goes over to Beth's house, and they watch a funny movie on Beth's couch.
moral_consequence: Beth is glad that Mary came over to spend time with her.
immoral_action: Mary stays at home on the couch instead of going out.
immoral_consequence: Beth is annoyed that Mary is too lazy to visit her.

norm: It is wrong to lead someone on with false emotions.
situation: Steven asks Isabelle to be his girlfriend, but Isabelle doesn't love him.
intention: Isabelle wants Steven to be happy.
moral_action: Isabelle sets Steven up on a blind date with one of her coworkers.
moral_consequence: Steven has a great time on his date.
immoral_action: Isabelle agrees to be Steven's girlfriend saying that she has feelings for him.
immoral_consequence: Steven is heartbroken when he learns the truth.

norm: It is rude to tell everyone about private messages.
situation: Sally gets a private text message from her husband while she is visiting her friend.
intention: Sally wants to make conversation with her friend.
moral_action: Sally talks to her friend about topics of mutual interest but doesn't bring up the text.
moral_consequence: Sally's husband asks her if she told her friend about his text and is happy when she says she didn't.
immoral_action: Sally tells her friend what her husband wrote to her in the private text message.
immoral_consequence: Sally's husband is upset when he finds out that she told her friend about what was meant to be a private matter.

norm: It's good to care about your performance at work.
situation: Sally is working on a team project that is due tomorrow.
intention: Sally wants to get home in time for her child's soccer game.
moral_action: Sally works hard all day and skips lunch to make sure her work is done well before she leaves the office.
moral_consequence: Sally's coworkers use her work in the project and everyone goes home on time.
immoral_action: Sally rushes through her tasks and leaves a draft of her work which her coworkers have to stay late to finish.
immoral_consequence: Sally's coworkers have to stay late to finish her part of the project.

norm: It's bad to not invite close friends to important events.
situation: Sue is getting married and is sending out wedding invitations, keeping in mind that her two best friends Betty and Jane aren't getting along.
intention: Sue doesn't want to upset her friends and wants to have a great wedding without any drama.
moral_action: Sue doesn't take sides and invites both friends to the wedding.
moral_consequence: Betty is happy to go the wedding and feels close with Sue.
immoral_action: Sue is closer friends with Jane, so she doesn't invite her friend Betty to the wedding.
immoral_consequence: Betty is very offended by Sue's decision and her feelings are hurt.

norm: It's rude to leave someone because their body changed.
situation: Tammy feels less attracted to her partner after they have put on a lot of weight.
intention: Tammy wants to be in a passionate relationship.
moral_action: Tammy makes going to the gym a couple's activity.
moral_consequence: Tammy's partner regains confidence and health.
immoral_action: Tammy leaves her partner for someone who is more attractive.
immoral_consequence: Tammy's partner is devastated that she has left.

norm: People feel it's rude to leave a family gathering early.
situation: Garrett is attending a family reunion, where numerous family members have yet to arrive.
intention: Garrett wants to go home.
moral_action: Garrett decides to hold on and wait for the remaining members to show up before heading home.
moral_consequence: The family members who arrived late are sad Garrett must leave, but are very happy to have gotten to see him.
immoral_action: Garrett tells those in attendance that he must leave the gathering and goes home.
immoral_consequence: Later arriving family are upset to learn that they have just missed Garrett.

norm: A marriage proposal should be memorable.
situation: Xavier has been with his girlfriend for years, and they are watching a movie together on their couch.
intention: Xavier wants to marry his girlfriend.
moral_action: Xavier lets his mind wander during the movie and plots out a unique marriage proposal plan which he goes on to execute.
moral_consequence: Xavier's girlfriend is moved by the personal thoughts and care that went into the proposal and says yes.
immoral_action: Xavier broaches the subject of marriage and after confirming his girlfriend is interesting in it he simply that they should indeed do it.
immoral_consequence: Xavier's girlfriend doesn't even realize at first that Xavier considered that a proposal, and she is somewhat let down when she does find out.

norm: It's wrong to give away something that isn't yours.
situation: Oakley needs to make his student loan payment, and someone wants to pay him forty dollars for his roomate's favorite figure.
intention: Oakley wants to get money.
moral_action: Oakley sells some of his old things at a thrift store for cash.
moral_consequence: Oakley feels less stressed after paying for his student loans.
immoral_action: Oakley sells his roomate's figure to get cash.
immoral_consequence: His roommate takes Oakley's apartment key and locks him out of the house for a week.

norm: Partners are expected to recall their wedding anniversary.
situation: Frank remembers that his wedding anniversary is coming up in a few weeks and buys his wife something special.
intention: Frank wants to hide the gift for his wife.
moral_action: Frank  hides the gift and sets a calendar reminder to retrieve the gift the day before the anniversary.
moral_consequence: Frank's wife is surprised and delighted by the wedding fit.
immoral_action: Frank puts the gift in a secret place at work and forgets about it.
immoral_consequence: The wedding anniversary comes and goes without notice and Frank's wife becomes distraught.

norm: It is not okay to exclude people.
situation: Sara is playing volleyball at the beach when a girl from school, Misty, shows up asking to play too.
intention: Sara wants to respond to Misty's request.
moral_action: Sara tells Misty she would enjoy having her play.
moral_consequence: Misty feels like part of a group.
immoral_action: Sara tells Misty there are already enough players and maybe some other time.
immoral_consequence: Misty feels left out and alone.

norm: It's important to agree on big things with your partner.
situation: Paul and Robert are looking for a school to enroll their 5 year daughter in.
intention: Robert wants to send his daughter to a school he feels comfortable with.
moral_action: Robert tells Paul about a school he found, and they end up enrolling their daughter there after they visit the school together.
moral_consequence: Paul feels confident in the decision and is excited about the first day of school.
immoral_action: While Paul was at work, Robert went to an open house for a school with limited openings and signed their daughter up.
immoral_consequence: Paul feels hurt that he wasn't consulted and feels uncomfortable about their daughter starting school.

norm: Using profanity against family members is rude.
situation: James' mother asked him to clean his room when he was in the middle of a video game.
intention: James wants to keep playing his video game.
moral_action: James tells his mom he'll start cleaning his room in ten minutes, betting she'll forget by then.
moral_consequence: James' mom forgets what she asked him to do, and he gets to keep playing his game.
immoral_action: James screams at his mom to leave his room and calls her a c-word.
immoral_consequence: James' mom leaves him alone for that afternoon, but ends up shipping him out to military school.

norm: It is wrong to replace a dead partner quickly.
situation: Tammy just divorced her husband after ten years and is upset.
intention: Tammy wants to stop feeling lonely at home.
moral_action: Tammy decides to get a cat instead of seducing someone online, and is no longer lonely.
moral_consequence: Tammy helps the cat grow, and is satisfied in her decision.
immoral_action: Tammy seduces someone online in order to replace her husband quickly, and stops feeling lonely.
immoral_consequence: Tammy gets into an abusive relationship and doesn't feel secure in herself.

norm: It is good to only do it with one person.
situation: Nate is invited to an orgy by Jane.
intention: Nate wants to have sex.
moral_action: Nate only has sex with Jane at the orgy.
moral_consequence: Nate has a fun time.
immoral_action: Nate has sex with multiple people at the orgy.
immoral_consequence: Nate gets an infection.

norm: It's uncaring to avoid your friends.
situation: Kyle is playing video games when his phone starts ringing, and he can see it's from his friend John.
intention: Kyle wants the phone to stop ringing.
moral_action: Kyle answers the phone and says hello to his friend John.
moral_consequence: John is delighted to hear Kyle's voice and tells him what he was calling about.
immoral_action: Kyle quickly puts the phone on mute, stopping the phone from ringing.
immoral_consequence: John is annoyed that Kyle isn't answering his phone and wonders where he could be.

norm: You shouldn't cause family drama.
situation: Joe was at his mother's house visiting when his brother says something rude to him.
intention: Joe wants to let his brother know that he is being rude.
moral_action: Joe pulls his brother aside and asks him if he can tone it down.
moral_consequence: Joe's brother apologizes and promises to act better in the future.
immoral_action: Joe punches his brother in the jaw because of the things he said.
immoral_consequence: Joe's mother tells Joe he has to leave the house.

norm: It is bad to cheat on your partner.
situation: Tina has a boyfriend and is out with her friends at the bar when an attractive man tries to kiss her.
intention: Tina wants to respond to the man's kiss attempt.
moral_action: Tina stop the man and tells him she is in a relationship.
moral_consequence: Tina will feel proud that she stayed faithful to her boyfriend.
immoral_action: Tina leans towards the man and accepts his kiss.
immoral_consequence: Tina will feel guilty for kissing a man that isn't her boyfriend.

norm: It's nice to celebrate Father's Day with your family.
situation: Dave has already planned a party with family for fathers day which is next week, when his boss asks him to work overtime that day.
intention: Dave wants to answer his boss' request for overtime.
moral_action: Dave tells his boss that while the money would be nice, family is more important, and he can't work.
moral_consequence: Dave's father is delighted to find out Dave will come to the party and spend time with him.
immoral_action: Dave tells his boss that he'll be there as the boss needs him, and he's loyal to the company.
immoral_consequence: Dave's father is really hurt his son isn't there to celebrate fathers day, rendering it useless.

norm: You shouldn't screw around at work.
situation: Jacob's manager is on a lunch break, leaving Jacob in charge of the store while his friend browses.
intention: Jacob wants to help his friend shop for a game.
moral_action: Jacob approaches his friend how he would any customer, but greets him by name.
moral_consequence: Jacob's friend asks for advice and decides on a game to buy.
immoral_action: Jacob takes his friend into the stockroom of the store to see the full inventory.
immoral_consequence: Jacob's friend picks a game but the boss returns and catches him with a customer in the back and gives him a disciplinary warning.

norm: It isn't right to cheat on your partner.
situation: Ava argued with her boyfriend before leaving on her family vacation, and her attractive ex boyfriend offers to buy her a drink.
intention: Ava wants to have fun on vacation.
moral_action: Ava ignores her ex and asks her family members to play some games in the hotel's pool.
moral_consequence: Ava enjoys spending time with her family.
immoral_action: Ava hangs out with her ex boyfriend slipping away to sleep with him.
immoral_consequence: Ava's boyfriend breaks up with her after a member of Ava's family sends him a picture of Ava and her ex.

norm: it is impolite to tell your parents to go away.
situation: Jules is living with her parents while she is studying for her degree and enjoys watching tv in the living room.
intention: Jules wants to watch a violent thriller.
moral_action: Jules asks her parents if they would be okay with watching something graphic.
moral_consequence: Jules parents decide to go out for dinner while Jules watches her film.
immoral_action: Jules doesn't want to have hear her parents complain about the bloody scenes and tells them to scram.
immoral_consequence: Jules enjoys the film but her parents feel disrespected and ask her to leave after the semester ends.

norm: It's rude to record someone without permission.
situation: Akihito notices a model standing in front of him while he's gathering footage for an advertisement.
intention: Akihito wants to make a good advertisement.
moral_action: Akihito decides to move to another location where he films the local wildlife.
moral_consequence: People make animal memes from his advertisement boosting sales of his product.
immoral_action: Akihito films the model from a distance from various angles.
immoral_consequence: Akihito is later sued by the model.

norm: It's wrong to steal from a parent.
situation: James hears about a new arcade game and sees his dad's coin jar in his bedroom.
intention: James wants to play the arcade game.
moral_action: James goes to his dad and asks for money to play a game.
moral_consequence: James' dad give him $5 to play the game.
immoral_action: James takes quarters from the coin jar and goes to play the game.
immoral_consequence: James gets away with it, but has a guilty conscience for years afterwards.

norm: It's disgusting to betray your partner's trust.
situation: Cory has a girlfriend who he is losing interest in and is developing an interest in another woman.
intention: Cory wants to hang out with the other woman.
moral_action: Cory is open to his partner about how he has been feeling.
moral_consequence: Cory's partner is given proper notice and does not expect their relationship to endure.
immoral_action: Cory tells his partner that he is with a male friend of his.
immoral_consequence: Cory's partner finds out about the other girl and feels betrayed.

norm: You shouldn't back out of a friend's plans.
situation: Larry got invited to dinner with Ken, and he said yes, but later in the day he was feeling lazy.
intention: Larry wants to enjoy his day.
moral_action: Larry goes out to dinner with his friend.
moral_consequence: Larry has a great time with his friend, and they get closer.
immoral_action: Larry cancels on his friend and stays home.
immoral_consequence: Larry hurts his friend's feelings.

norm: You shouldn't tell your friend how to plan her wedding.
situation: Francine is listening to Kyla talk about her upcoming wedding.
intention: Francine wants to respond to Kyla's wedding plans.
moral_action: Francine tells Kyla that her wedding sounds like a dream come true for Kyla.
moral_consequence: Kyla will think Francine is a supportive friend.
immoral_action: Francine tells Kyla that she should have a certain theme and exactly what to do.
immoral_consequence: Kyla will think Francine is bossy.

norm: It's bad to keep things a secret from your wife.
situation: Hal has a hobby of knitting that he is embarrassed tell his wife about, and she is out shopping.
intention: Hal wants to do some knitting.
moral_action: Hal does some knitting and realizes it is silly to keep it a secret, so he shows his wife when she gets home.
moral_consequence: Hal's wife is tickled by the harmless nature of Hal's hobby and begins to learn to knit too.
immoral_action: Hal does some knitting and quickly hides it when his wife pulls up in the driveway.
immoral_consequence: Hal's wife notices he is acting odd and hiding something and becomes suspicious it is something far worse than knitting.

norm: You shouldn't refuse to do things with your parents.
situation: Dan is thinking of going to the movies, but his dad Jack wants to go with im.
intention: Dan wants to watch a movie.
moral_action: Dan lets Jack come with him to the movies since it would make his day.
moral_consequence: Jack feels excited, and he gets to spend time with his son.
immoral_action: Dan tells his dad that he doesn't want to be seen in public with him.
immoral_consequence: Jack's feelings are hurt, and he starts to feel worthless.

norm: It's good to respect people for the jobs helping others.
situation: Frank has been sick for weeks with a virus and enters a hospital clinic.
intention: Frank wants to get over his virus.
moral_action: Frank asks a doctor for help and says he admires all they do for others.
moral_consequence: The doctor feels appreciated and helps Frank a lot and Frank gets better.
immoral_action: Frank tells the doctor in a raised voice he wants help right away, and he better get well fast.
immoral_consequence: The doctor resents Frank's tone and helps him less than he would otherwise and Frank stays sick.

norm: It's expected that you go to your friend's birthday parties.
situation: Today is Ton'y best friends birthday, and he has been invited to their birthday party.
intention: Tony is tired and wants to catch up on rest.
moral_action: Tony let's his friend know how exhausted he is, and shows up to the party to drop a gift off and wish him a happy birthday before going back home.
moral_consequence: Tony's friend invites him out for drinks the next weekend, so they can make up for time lost at the birthday party.
immoral_action: Tony ignores his best friends calls and doesn't go to the party.
immoral_consequence: Tony's friend stops hanging out with him as often, and fails to show up to Tony's birthday later that year.

norm: It's bad to be unemployed.
situation: Jennifer is in her late 20's and burned out from working at a job that she does not like.
intention: Jennifer wants to avoid stressful work and enjoy her life.
moral_action: Jennifer finds a job that she is a better fit for and changes career.
moral_consequence: She is happy in her new career and able ot retire at an early age.
immoral_action: Jennifer quits her job and stays unemployed for over a decade.
immoral_consequence: Jennifer has limited job options later in life, resulting in low social security, no retirement when older and working into her 80's.

norm: It's wrong to use your friends.
situation: Tammy is hanging out with her friends at a restaurant but is broke.
intention: Tammy wants to get some food.
moral_action: Tammy asks a friend if he can buy her a simple sandwich and pays him back the next day after getting her paycheck.
moral_consequence: Her friends respect Tammy and trust her and help her in the future.
immoral_action: Tammy asks her friends to buy her some food but never pays them back.
immoral_consequence: Her friends refuse to ever buy her anything in the future and resent her.

norm: It's good to get over your ex.
situation: Bob is feeling lonely one evening at home.
intention: Bob wants some female companionship.
moral_action: Bob goes out to meet new people, possibly a new girlfriend.
moral_consequence: Bob finds new friends and a new girlfriend.
immoral_action: Bob calls up his ex girlfriend and wants to make up.
immoral_consequence: They get back together, but the relationship is just as bad as the first time.

norm: It's best to get out of abusive situations.
situation: Jaclyn and her new boyfriend, Joshua, recently got into an argument that turned abusive, leaving her with bruises.
intention: Jaclyn wants to move on from the incident.
moral_action: Jaclyn realizes this is not what she wants in a relationship and breaks up with Josh.
moral_consequence: Jaclyn moves on and never allows anyone to abuse her again.
immoral_action: Jaclyn forgives Josh and stays with him.
immoral_consequence: Josh continues to abuse Jaclyn throughout their time together.

norm: It is rude to abandon your family.
situation: Ian watches his little brother get onto the teacup ride when his friends ask him invite him to the roller coaster.
intention: Ian wants to have fun.
moral_action: Ian plays a multiplayer game on his cell phone while he waits for his younger brother.
moral_consequence: His younger brother is able to ride more rides with his brother.
immoral_action: Ian gets onto the roller coaster with his friends.
immoral_consequence: His little brother wanders around alone after getting off of the teacup ride.

norm: You should ask before you take photos of someone.
situation: Hank is at a convention and notices someone cosplaying as a character he likes.
intention: Hank wants to get a picture of the cosplayer.
moral_action: Hank approaches the cosplayer, compliments her choice of costume and asks to take a picture with her.
moral_consequence: The cosplayer is flattered and agrees to take the picture with him.
immoral_action: Hank takes out his camera and starts taking pictures of the cosplayer.
immoral_consequence: The cosplayer notices Hank and feels creeped out by him suddenly taking pictures.

norm: You shouldn't disrespect your parents.
situation: Katherine makes dinner every night for her daughter Janine who thinks that Katherin’s food is bland.
intention: Janine wants to let her mom know that her food is bad.
moral_action: Janine gifts Katherine a loaded spice rack.
moral_consequence: Katherine starts experimenting with the spices, and her food quality improves.
immoral_action: After being served a plate of food by Katherine, Janine dumps the food in the trash.
immoral_consequence: Katherine feels hurt and disrespected.

norm: It's bad to hate the opposite gender.
situation: Jill was badly physically abused by her last ex-boyfriend.
intention: Jill wants to make sure she's never hurt by a man again.
moral_action: Jill becomes a nun and rarely leaves her nunnery.
moral_consequence: After years of prayer and meditation, Jill is much less impacted by her trauma.
immoral_action: Jill starts ranting at every man she meets about how they're all rapists.
immoral_consequence: Jill is effectively unable to work, since pretty much every job has male employees or customers.

norm: It's wrong to break someone's heart.
situation: Ron and Romilda have been dating fora while, and for Romilda, the love isn't just there anymore.
intention: Romilda wants to break-up with Ron.
moral_action: Romilda invites Ron over, and they talk a long time about the situation, and end up breaking it off.
moral_consequence: Ron understands where Romilda is coming from, and they part amicably.
immoral_action: Romilda calls up Ron and tells him over the phone that she doesn't love him, and thinks he's a bad person.
immoral_consequence: Ron's feelings get immeasurably hurt, and he doesn't know how to respond.

norm: It's hurtful to cheat on your spouse.
situation: John, who is married, is talking to Wendy at a bar when Wendy suddenly tells him she's attracted to him and asks him to sleep with her.
intention: John wants to respond to Wendy's question.
moral_action: John declines Wendy's request and reminds her that he is married and faithful.
moral_consequence: John's wife is told that John declined a woman's advances and feels happy and safe in their relationship.
immoral_action: John agrees to sleep with Wendy, and they leave the bar together.
immoral_consequence: John's wife finds out about the affair and is deeply hurt and ashamed and ends their relationship.

norm: Partners aren't supposed to make each other cry.
situation: Jeff was having an argument with his wife Jen over what private school to take their kid to.
intention: Jeff wants to end the argument.
moral_action: Jeff says Jen can make the decision since it's not that big of a deal.
moral_consequence: Jen feels happy that she gets to decide, and she feels like part of a team.
immoral_action: Jeff says he should make the decision since he's the only parents that works.
immoral_consequence: Jen starts crying since Jeff embarrasses her and makes her feel worthless.

norm: You shouldn't cancel on your family members.
situation: Arna is a loner and is invited to her sister's anniversary and is at home deliberating on whether to attend.
intention: Arna wants to call her sister with an answer.
moral_action: Arna says she'll go but admits her desire to be alone.
moral_consequence: Arna bonds with her sister and they compromise.
immoral_action: Arna tells her sister that she doesn't feel comfortable attending.
immoral_consequence: Arna hears less often from her sister and misses the social contact.

norm: It's good to travel with your partner.
situation: Adam is married and has many vacation days he can use to take time off of work, but his wife does not.
intention: Adam wants to go travelling.
moral_action: Adam waits for his wife to get time off approved, and they go travelling together.
moral_consequence: Adam and his wife have a wonderful trip and decides to do it again every few years.
immoral_action: Adam goes traveling without his wife.
immoral_consequence: Adam has a great time but comes home to find out she has left him.

norm: It's rude to bring unannounced guests to an event.
situation: Ron is going to a dinner party over the weekend and asks his friend if she would like to join him.
intention: Ron has to decide whether to call the host of the dinner party, or just show up.
moral_action: Ron calls the host of the party to make sure it's alright to bring a guest with him.
moral_consequence: The host is fine with having another guest and plans to prepare more food, and Ron's friend enjoys the party and feels welcomed at the event.
immoral_action: Ron decides that it's no big deal and just brings his friend to the dinner party, with no prior announcement.
immoral_consequence: The host is concerned that they haven't prepared enough food and Ron's friend feels awkward about being there without an invitation.

norm: It is good to stand up for family.
situation: Nick is walking down the street with his mom Nancy when a strange man calls Nancy ugly.
intention: Nick wants to talk to the strange man about what he just said.
moral_action: Nick tells the strange man to back off and apologize before he makes him ugly, too.
moral_consequence: The strange man gets a little scared and apologizes to Nancy and learns to keep his rude comments to himself.
immoral_action: Nick tells the strange man that his mom is ugly because she's old so his point is too obvious.
immoral_consequence: Nancy is insulted that Nick is agreeing with the man that she's ugly and that he's not being very forceful with the man.

norm: You shouldn't be a lowlife to your family.
situation: Tom's is 37 and still living with his parents, who continually threaten to kick him out.
intention: Tom wants to tell his parents his future plans.
moral_action: Tom decides to tell his parents he will do lots of chores and work if he can stay with his parents.
moral_consequence: Tom's parents feel this is a fair deal and Tom feels proud of his decision.
immoral_action: Tom decides to tell his parents he is never moving out and will sue if they try.
immoral_consequence: Tom's parents call the police on Tom and then give him a 30 day notice to move out, and Tom feels sad.

norm: It's good to listen to your in-law's stories.
situation: Bill is having some drinks with the family while his in-law is telling the group a story from the past.
intention: Bill wants to go get more drinks.
moral_action: Bill asks his in-law to wait a moment because he wants to hear the rest of the story and offers to get them another drink.
moral_consequence: Bill's in law appreciates that he wants to hear the rest of the story.
immoral_action: Bill gets up in the middle of the story to go get more beer.
immoral_consequence: Bill's in-law is annoyed and thinks he is being rude.

norm: You shouldn't just bail on others.
situation: Caren and Kelly are best friends in a nightclub where Caren doesn't quite like the music.
intention: Caren wants to go to a nightclub that plays different music.
moral_action: Caren tells Kelly she would prefer different music and asks if she wants to check out another club.
moral_consequence: Kelly agrees and the two end up having fun elsewhere.
immoral_action: While Kelly is at the bar, Caren decides to go to a different club.
immoral_consequence: Kelly feels abandoned, and never speaks to Caren again.

norm: It's wrong to lead people on by flirting.
situation: Jesus is very lonely and hasn't had a partner in a long time.
intention: Jesus wants to find himself a girlfriend.
moral_action: Jesus gets to know a couple girls and after finding one he like he starts to flirt with her.
moral_consequence: She flirts back, and they eventually get in a relationship together.
immoral_action: Jesus flirts with every girl he meets despite not knowing them very well.
immoral_consequence: A girl starts to like Jesus, but he realized he didn't like her, causing her to get upset and say some very mean things hurting Jesus's feelings.

norm: It's rude to ignore an old friend.
situation: Missy is walking to an appointment when she sees an old friend.
intention: Missy wants to get to her appointment on time.
moral_action: Missy waves to her friend and calls out that she is in a hurry but will call them later.
moral_consequence: Missy gets to her appointment on time without hurting her friend's feelings.
immoral_action: Missy ducks her head and ignores her friend when they call her name.
immoral_consequence: Missy's friend is angry with her and tells everyone that she is stuck up.

norm: It's ok to cut ties with an ex.
situation: Mark just broke up with his ex for cheating on him and knows that she has few to no friends.
intention: Mark wants to move onto someone else.
moral_action: Mark cuts off all contact with his ex and focuses solely on finding someone else.
moral_consequence: Mark's ex has to further reap what she sowed when she betrayed him.
immoral_action: Mark feels bad for his ex, so he stays in touch with her strictly as friends and pursues other romantic options.
immoral_consequence: Mark's ex does not understand how serious her actions were.

norm: It's not okay to embarrass your friends.
situation: Erin is hanging out with a group of friends, and they are sharing stories.
intention: Erin wants to bring up a private story about her friend.
moral_action: Erin waits until she's alone with the friend and brings up the story in private with her.
moral_consequence: Erin's friend remembers the story and has a good laugh about it.
immoral_action: Erin brings up the story to the group to make them laugh.
immoral_consequence: Erin's friend becomes very embarrassed that the group finds out about the incident.

norm: You should always visit your parents.
situation: Terry's mother and father had been in the nursing home for years.
intention: Terry wants to go to the nursing home.
moral_action: Terry stops by the nursing home after work and visits his mother and father.
moral_consequence: Terry's mother and father are happy and grateful that their son visits often.
immoral_action: Terry goes to the nursing home to pay the bill, but doesn't visit his parents.
immoral_consequence: Terry's mother and father are sad that their son never visits and go into a depression.

norm: It's fun to play sports with your kids.
situation: Vince's child comes and asks Vince, who is working on his laptop, to play soccer with him out in the field.
intention: Vince wants to be outside with his child.
moral_action: Vince closes his laptop, puts on some shorts, and plays soccer with his child.
moral_consequence: Vince and his child have a good bonding time together.
immoral_action: Vince agrees to come out and watch and continues working on his laptop.
immoral_consequence: Vince's child feels neglected and gets upset.

norm: You're expected to put extra effort into long distance relationships.
situation: Mike, who has a girlfriend, is going to Honduras for a year but intends to stay with his girlfriend.
intention: Mike wants to call his girlfriend.
moral_action: Mike calls his girlfriend every night and talks with her about deep, personal issues.
moral_consequence: Mike's girlfriend feels like a part of Mike's life and maintains a connection with him while he's away.
immoral_action: Mike calls his girlfriend once a week and talks with her for an hour to catch up on the week's events.
immoral_consequence: Mike's girlfriend feels unsatisfied and grows more and more distant from Mike, eventually separating from him.

norm: It's frowned upon to drink at work.
situation: Tony's coworker offers him a drink, from a bottle of alcohol he keeps in his desk drawer.
intention: Tony has to decide whether to drink alcohol, while at work.
moral_action: Tony declines to take a drink with his colleague, and lets him know that he would enjoy a drink after work, but not during his shift.
moral_consequence: Tony continues his work day without needing to be concerned with having alcohol on his breath, or getting into trouble.
immoral_action: Tony decides that one drink won't hurt anything, so he accepts the drink he has been offered.
immoral_consequence: Later in the day, Tony's boss lets him know that he smelled the alcohol and gives Tony a verbal warning not to drink on the job.

norm: It's nice to visit other's.
situation: Charlie has been busy at work with no time to see anyone, but he finally has a day off.
intention: Charlie wants to do something during his day off.
moral_action: Charlie goes to see his friend and hangs out with them.
moral_consequence: Charlie gets to enjoy the company of his friend and build upon the relationship.
immoral_action: Charlie stays at home and plays video games all day.
immoral_consequence: Charlie does not see anyone and does not forge social bonds.

norm: It's important to do your chores.
situation: Jay hadn't washed any of his clothes for well over a week and has a date.
intention: Jay wants to change his clothes.
moral_action: Jay puts some clothes in the wash and gets them clean in time for his date.
moral_consequence: Jay's date tells him that he looks very handsome and asks him for a second date.
immoral_action: Jay can't find any clothes to wear, so he puts on the same clothes as he wore the day before.
immoral_consequence: Jay's date says that he stinks, and she leaves halfway through the date.

norm: You shouldn't ask someone else's boyfriend to prom.
situation: Kelsi was hanging out with her friend, her friend's boyfriend and several other kids in her class talking about the prom.
intention: Kelsi wants to ask one of the guys to prom.
moral_action: Kelsi asks one of the other guys in the group to go to the prom with her.
moral_consequence: Kelsi's friend is excited that they will both have a date to the prom.
immoral_action: Kelsi asks her friend's boyfriend if he wants to go to the prom with her.
immoral_consequence: Kelsi's friend gets mad at her and gives her the silent treatment for a week.

norm: You should be grateful for help.
situation: Sun is struggling to lift a heavy box from the floor when Bruce comes by and helps him.
intention: Sun wants to move the heavy box onto a shelf.
moral_action: Sun smiles at Bruce and offer to repay Bruce for helping him.
moral_consequence: Sun buys Bruce a coffee and they chat about themselves.
immoral_action: Sun grumbles to Bruce that he could have lifted the box onto the shelf by himself.
immoral_consequence: Bruce tells Sun to go screw himself and walks away.

norm: Celebrating with family is encouraged to keep familial bonds strong.
situation: Bradley wrote a book and received a prestigious award for it.
intention: Bradley wants to celebrate his award.
moral_action: Bradley goes out to dinner at a restaurant with family members.
moral_consequence: Bradley will be able to talk about his award with his family members.
immoral_action: Bradley goes out to dinner at a restaurant by himself.
immoral_consequence: Bradley will have no one to talk to about his award.

norm: It's unacceptable to break a promise on prom night.
situation: Leah calls Rick to see if he's ready to pick her up to take her to prom as he promised.
intention: Rick wants to respond to Leah.
moral_action: Rick tells Leah that of course he'll be over in just a few minutes.
moral_consequence: Leah is delighted that Rick is coming to pick her up and excited about going to prom.
immoral_action: Rick tells Leah that he really doesn't want to go to prom anymore there's too many people there.
immoral_consequence: Leah is devastated as she had been dreaming of the prom for a long time.

norm: People shouldn't constantly look to trade up on relationships.
situation: Emily has always been impressed by people with money, and it's a factor in how she chooses her partners.
intention: Emily wants to feel good about herself.
moral_action: Emily decides that making money herself is better than dating a rich guy, and she lands a well-paying job.
moral_consequence: Emily's friends are impressed by her career and admire her.
immoral_action: Emily just ditched her boyfriend for someone richer, after doing the same thing last year.
immoral_consequence: Emily's friends are secretly starting to feel that she is superficial.

norm: You should always respect your partner's choices.
situation: Jasmine and her partner are very hungry, she wants hamburgers but her partner is vegetarian and wants salad.
intention: Jasmine wants to eat a hamburger.
moral_action: Jasmine goes and get's herself a hamburger and gets her partner salad.
moral_consequence: Jasmine's partner says thank you and promises to go get food for them both next time.
immoral_action: Jasmine get's hamburgers for her and her partner.
immoral_consequence: Her partner is very upset she bought them food they won't eat and ends the relationship.

norm: It's immoral to abandon unwanted pets.
situation: Carrie adopts a cat and then realizes her boyfriend is allergic.
intention: Carrie wants to make her boyfriend comfortable in her home.
moral_action: Carrie calls the facility she adopted the cat from, expressing her problem to them.
moral_consequence: The facility offers to take the cat back to give it to another interested family.
immoral_action: Carrie drives the cat to a wooded area and leaves it behind.
immoral_consequence: Carrie feels terrible guilt at abandoning the cat she just adopted.

norm: You should always make sure your friends are doing OK.
situation: Sharon is at a large party in a mansion with a few girlfriends, and one of them seems pretty drunk.
intention: Sharon wants to enjoy the party till the end.
moral_action: Sharon calls a cab for her drunk friend, sends her home and goes back to partying.
moral_consequence: Sharon's drunk friend spends a safe and peaceful night in her own bed.
immoral_action: Sharon figures her friend is an adult and will take care of herself.
immoral_consequence: Sharon's drunk friend is taken advantage of by a group of guys in a remote corner of the mansion.

norm: It is wrong to deceive your loved ones
situation: Mary tells her kids that if they go to the doctor, she'll take them out for ice cream afterwards.
intention: Mary wants to get her kids to the doctor.
moral_action: Mary takes the kids to a local ice cream shop after the trip to the doctor.
moral_consequence: The kids are excited that they get to eat a scoop of ice cream.
immoral_action: Mary takes the kids straight home after visiting the doctor's office.
immoral_consequence: The kids are upset that they didn't get their ice cream.

norm: It's hurtful to exclude members from family events.
situation: Lenny is at his dinner table with his wife, and they're planning a baby shower when Lenny's wife asks him if he'll invite his mom.
intention: Lenny wants to answer his wife's question.
moral_action: Lenny tells his wife that of course he's going to invite his mom and can't wait to see her.
moral_consequence: Lenny's wife is glad to see that Lenny treats family as an important part of his life.
immoral_action: Lenny tells his wife that he will not be inviting his mom since he doesn't want to see her.
immoral_consequence: Lenny's wife is grossed out by how insensitive he is being towards his mother and wonders if he'll be like that to her.

norm: It's good to be married when you have a baby.
situation: When May tells John she is pregnant he proposes marriage to her.
intention: May wants to give John an answer.
moral_action: May accepts John's proposal.
moral_consequence: May shares the nurden and joy of raising a child with it's father.
immoral_action: May refuses to Marry John and decides to have the baby alone.
immoral_consequence: May struggles under the pressure of being a single parent.

norm: It is mean to cut your mother off.
situation: Fred, who pays the rent for his elderly mother's apartment has recently been unhappy with how she talks to him.
intention: Fred wants to show his mom he's mad at her.
moral_action: Fred decides to call up his mother and leaves a long message on her answering machine explaining his feelings about her tone.
moral_consequence: Fred's mother is glad Fred communicated with her about what made him so mad so she can work on fixing it.
immoral_action: Fred decides to call up the bank and cancel all outstanding bill payments on his account, essentially ensuring his mother's eviction.
immoral_consequence: Fred's mother is devastated and in her frail old condition is unable to cope physically or emotionally.

norm: It is wrong to intentionally exclude friends
situation: Bob is planning a party with his wife, Wendy when she asks him if he'll invite his friend Jim.
intention: Bob wants to answer Wendy's question.
moral_action: Bob tells Wendy that of course he's inviting Jim they're best friends after all.
moral_consequence: Wendy is happy to hear that Bob is going to invite Jim and that he is good at maintaining friendships.
immoral_action: Bob tells Wendy that he's definitely not inviting Jim because Jim doesn't deserve to be there.
immoral_consequence: Wendy is surprised at how mean Bob is being to Jim for seemingly no reason and feels awkward around Bob.

norm: It's important to be honest and communicative in a relationship.
situation: John spent the whole day partying with some friends.
intention: John wants to have fun with his friends.
moral_action: John tells his girlfriend about going to the party with his friends.
moral_consequence: John's girlfriend is happy for John and asks to join John next time.
immoral_action: John does not tell his girlfriend about the party.
immoral_consequence: John's girlfriend thinks John is cheating on her and breaks up with John.

norm: It's nice to ask to visit people.
situation: Lucas was planning a big road trip over the summer, and he was driving right past where Nolan lives.
intention: Lucas wants to plan all the stops on his trip.
moral_action: Lucas asks Nolan if he'd want a visit since he'll be driving through Nolan's town on his trip.
moral_consequence: Nolan invites Lucas to visit his house while he's in town.
immoral_action: Lucas doesn't say anything to Nolan about visiting him even though he'll be driving through his town.
immoral_consequence: Nolan is shocked and wonders if Lucas doesn't like him very much.

norm: It's bad to tempt someone who's already in a relationship.
situation: Ash has a crush on a guy at work, but she knows he's married.
intention: Ash has to decide whether to hit on the guy.
moral_action: Ash decides to leave the guy alone and doesn't say anything to him.
moral_consequence: The guy's relationship is safe, and he doesn't have to deal with any temptations.
immoral_action: Ash tells the guy that she's really good at giving oral sex.
immoral_consequence: The guy's relationship is in danger if he decides to have sex with Ash.

norm: It's wrong to question how someone else live's their lives.
situation: Erin feels a strong sense of disapproval as she watches her brother sign up for classes in school that won't help him earn a big salary.
intention: Erin wants to talk to her brother about his educational decisions.
moral_action: Erin explains to her brother that she is taking a different set of classes and talks about what she hopes to achieve in life, with her education.
moral_consequence: Her brother listens to her and considers what she has said, and they continue to have an open dialogue together.
immoral_action: Erin tells her brother that the classes he selects are worthless and a complete waste of money.
immoral_consequence: Her brother will be highly offended by her remarks and their relationship will become strained.

norm: It's okay to stay true to your morals and values
situation: Steve is broke and down on his luck when his friend Bob asks him to help rob a liquor store for cash.
intention: Steve wants to decide whether to agree to Bob's request.
moral_action: Steve refuses to participate in the activity suggested by Bob because it is against his morals and values.
moral_consequence: Steve feels good about his decision and soon finds a good job which helps him turn his life around.
immoral_action: Steve participates in helping Bob hold up the liquor store for cash.
immoral_consequence: Steve is later arrested and sentenced to prison for his crime.

norm: You shouldn't let a drunk friend walk home alone.
situation: Joe and Bill have been catching up over drinks but Bill has overindulged and is quite drunk by the end of the night.
intention: Joe wants to get some sleep before work the next day.
moral_action: Joe escorts Bill home and then heads home himself.
moral_consequence: Joe is able to look back at an enjoyable evening spent with his friend.
immoral_action: Joe parts ways with Bill outside the bar and heads home.
immoral_consequence: Joe is dismayed when he finds out that Bill was found passed out and mugged in an alley.

norm: Revealing too much information about someone is not appropriate.
situation: Helen is talking with her sister, who is very distraught about a psychological issue.
intention: Helen wants to make her sister feel better.
moral_action: Helen tells her sister about something else who solved a similar issue and whose ordeal was public.
moral_consequence: The person given as an example has gained another admirer.
immoral_action: Helen tells her sister others are worse off than her and tells her about a more serious issue one of her friends told her about in confidence.
immoral_consequence: The friend's psychological issue is now public knowledge.

norm: You shouldn't lie about your whereabouts.
situation: Samantha is a teenager and is invited to a party.
intention: Samantha wants to go out to a party with her girlfriends.
moral_action: Samantha tells her parents she is going to a party and gives them the address.
moral_consequence: Samantha’s parents call to check on her later that evening and permit her to continue going to parties in the future.
immoral_action: Samantha tells her parents that she is going to stay the night at the neighbor’s house, and spends the night partying.
immoral_consequence: Samantha's parents call the neighbors to wish her a good night, find out she's not there, and proceed to ground her for a month.

norm: Fantasizing constantly is excessive.
situation: Norm is a published writer and his agent expects him to deliver another novel soon.
intention: Norm wants his next novel to be a great and original idea.
moral_action: Norm spends some time musing about some ideas then starts putting together an outline he is satisfied with.
moral_consequence: Norm is able to compile a novel he is proud of and it becomes his bestseller.
immoral_action: Norm spends his weeks day dreaming about different scemarios for his next novel without writing anything down.
immoral_consequence: Norm never gets his next novel written because he spends all his time just imagining it and his agent drops him.

norm: It's rude to record a friend without their permission.
situation: Sam is walking near school when he hears his friend Wayne singing.
intention: Sam wants to record Wayne.
moral_action: Sam compliments Wayne and asks if he can record him singing.
moral_consequence: Wayne agrees and sings for Sam while being recorded.
immoral_action: Sam walks up unseen, pulls out his phone, and records Wayne.
immoral_consequence: Wayne eventually sees Sam and demands that he delete the recording.

norm: It's hurtful to leave a parent.
situation: Thomas has been living with his handicapped mother Sandra and just got a new job.
intention: Thomas wants to live on his own.
moral_action: Thomas moves to a house close by Sandra and continues looking after her needs too.
moral_consequence: Sandra feels loved by Thomas and is happy and healthy too.
immoral_action: Thomas moves across the country from Sandra and doesn't talk to her anymore.
immoral_consequence: Sandra becomes very depressed and gets sick.

norm: It's wrong to hide things from your significant other.
situation: Margo wants to meet-up with Barb, an old friend from college whom her husband dislikes.
intention: Margot wants to have a quiet lunch with Barb and catch up.
moral_action: Margot tells her husband she is having lunch with Barb, and he can just deal with it.
moral_consequence: Margot enjoys her lunch with Barb and reminiscing about college days.
immoral_action: Margot tells her husband she is having lunch with a different person.
immoral_consequence: Margot feels guilty throughout her lunch date about telling a lie to her husband.

norm: It's poor form to date a friend's former partner.
situation: Serra, who is single, used to flirt with her friend Gloria's handsome boyfriend and just learned that Gloria broke up with him.
intention: Serra wants a handsome boyfriend.
moral_action: Serra decides against dating Gloria's ex and starts dating another handsome man she just met.
moral_consequence: Gloria feels comfortable knowing that her ex will not share intimate details about her with Serra.
immoral_action: Serra starts dating Gloria's ex boyfriend, and he tells her about Gloria's sexual fantasies.
immoral_consequence: Gloria finds out about it and feels violated.

norm: You shouldn't make out with your friend's crush.
situation: Sara's best friend Julie has a crush on one of their classmates, but is too shy to talk to him.
intention: Sara wants to speak to the classmate about Julie.
moral_action: Sara stops the classmate in the parking lot to tell him about Julie's crush, but he tries to kiss her instead, where Sara push him away.
moral_consequence: Julie finds out from Sara about the classmate's inappropriate behavior, and thanks her for stopping him and being honest.
immoral_action: Sara meets the classmate at the park to talk to him about Julie, but she begins to like him and soon is kissing him on the bench.
immoral_consequence: Julie hears rumors that Sara and the classmate were seen in an embrace at the park, so she stops being friends with Sara.

norm: It's wrong to hate your country.
situation: James is attending a Fourth of July barbecue with his family and about a hundred neighbors.
intention: James wants to express his political views.
moral_action: "James leads all his neighbors in singing ""The Star Spangled Banner""."
moral_consequence: James and his neighbors feel closer after singing together to celebrate the holiday.
immoral_action: James sets a paper American flag on fire to protest the Trump administration.
immoral_consequence: James is asked to leave because his actions are contrary to the spirit of the holiday.

norm: It's good to help coworkers do their job correctly.
situation: Tilly is a manager at a corporation.
intention: Tilly wants to give information to the new employees.
moral_action: Tilly sets up a comprehensive training program for the new employees.
moral_consequence: The new employees become efficient at their jobs.
immoral_action: Tilly gives vague suggestions to the new employees.
immoral_consequence: The new employees struggle doing the job and feel lost.

norm: It's rude to flake out on someone.
situation: Christina made dinner plans to go out to dinner with her friend Eugene but when the time comes to go out she's exhausted.
intention: Christina wants to have some dinner.
moral_action: Christina drinks an energy drink and heads out to the restaurant to meet up with Eugene.
moral_consequence: Eugene has a nice time with Christina but can tell that she's tired, so he offers to cut their outing short, so she can get some rest.
immoral_action: Christina grabs some food from the fridge and texts Eugene minutes before their agreed upon time, letting him know that she won't be able to make it.
immoral_consequence: Eugene has dinner at the restaurant by himself and decides not to invite Christina out again.

norm: It's hurtful to disown someone.
situation: Jenna is mad at her sister for wearing a white dress to Jenna's wedding.
intention: Jenna wants to stop dealing with her sister's attention-seeking behavior.
moral_action: Jenna convinces all her sister's friends to tell her sister to apologize.
moral_consequence: Jenna's sister succumbs to peer pressure and apologizes to Jenna.
immoral_action: Jenna refuses to speak to her sister ever again and moves a thousand miles away.
immoral_consequence: Jenna often misses her sister but is too stubborn to recontact her.

norm: You shouldn't get involved in a relationship with an ex.
situation: Katie, who is single, is feeling particularly lonely and has been talking to her ex who cheated on her.
intention: Katie wants to be with someone romantically.
moral_action: Katie keeps her ex at a distance and finds someone new on a dating website.
moral_consequence: The ex regrets what he did when he sees her with a new partner.
immoral_action: Katie rekindles her feelings with her ex and dates him again.
immoral_consequence: The ex gets the impression that his previous actions are forgiven.

norm: It's good to be proud of your friends.
situation: John's friend recently won a chess tournament and was awarded a trophy.
intention: John wants to react to his friend's victory.
moral_action: John congratulates his friend for the win and celebrates with him.
moral_consequence: John and his friend become even better friends to each other over the memory of the win.
immoral_action: John acts dismissively towards his friend's chess tournament win.
immoral_consequence: John's friend thinks John is jealous and becomes less of a friend to him.

norm: It's hurtful to ditch a friend.
situation: Theresa was at the mall shopping with her friend Debbie.
intention: Theresa wants to go home.
moral_action: Theresa tells Debbie that it is about time to go home.
moral_consequence: Debbie is happy that she got to spend time with her friend during the day.
immoral_action: Theresa leaves the mall without telling Debbie she was leaving.
immoral_consequence: Debbie gets scared and starts crying because she thinks she is lost.

norm: It is helpful to quit something with your partner.
situation: Jacob and his wife are both smokers but Jacob's wife has decided to quit the habit.
intention: Jacob wants to show his support for his wife's decision to stop smoking.
moral_action: Jacob decides to stop smoking along with his wife to make it easier for her to kick the habit.
moral_consequence: Jacob's wife is encouraged not to smoke by his decision to also quit and is able to give up the habit.
immoral_action: Jacob praises his wife's decision to stop smoking but continues to smoke around her.
immoral_consequence: Jacob's wife finds it difficult to stop smoking with him smoking around her all the time and soon goes back to smoking.

norm: It's nice to try to be trustworthy.
situation: Bill is sitting at a coffee shop when another customer asks him to watch their things which includes a purse with money inside.
intention: Bill wants to leave the coffee shop.
moral_action: Bill watches the customer's things until they return then leaves the coffee shop.
moral_consequence: The customer is relieved that they put their trust in somebody honorable like Bill.
immoral_action: Bill takes the money from the purse then quickly leaves the coffee shop.
immoral_consequence: The other customer feels stupid for trusting a stranger and misses the money Bill took.

norm: It is not okay to overreact to a breakup.
situation: Jeremy's girlfriend Eva tells him that she wants to start seeing other people, and doesn't want to be with him anymore.
intention: Jeremy wants to express his feelings about the breakup.
moral_action: Jeremy says he's hurt, but he understands, and hopes she finds happiness.
