moral_consequence: Jeremy and Eva remain friends, and make a smooth transition into new relationships, and their circle of friends remains in tact.
immoral_action: Jeremy pounds his fist on the table and calls Eva a slut, and then posts a bunch of nasty messages on Facebook calling her a dirty whore.
immoral_consequence: Eva gets scared and stays away from Jeremy, and their friends split into factions to defend their favorite.

norm: it is bad to betray a friend.
situation: Steve and his friend Arnold bumped into an angry drunk stranger who started aggressively moving towards Arnold.
intention: Steve wants to decide on his course of action.
moral_action: Steve actively tries to support Arnold in dealing with the drunk stranger.
moral_consequence: Steve and Arnold improves their friendship after they successfully calm down the drunk stranger together.
immoral_action: Steve runs away and leaves Arnold to deal with the drunk stranger himself.
immoral_consequence: Arnold gets injured and no longer considers Steve as a friend.

norm: You shouldn't sabotage your ex's new relationship.
situation: Steve and Mary divorced two years ago and Steve is now cleaning out the house.
intention: Steve wants to return Marys favorite little black dress.
moral_action: Steve packs up the dress along with her other belongings and mails it to Mary's house.
moral_consequence: Mary is happy to get her things back and sends Steve a thank you text.
immoral_action: Steve puts the dress in a gift box along with the poem he wrote the night they first met.
immoral_consequence: Mary opens the box in front of her new boyfriend, he gets angry and thinks she is cheating on him.

norm: You shouldn't flirt with people when you're in a relationship.
situation: Luna, who is married, is at work where she is friends with many coworkers.
intention: Luna wants to enjoy work.
moral_action: Luna makes wise cracks with her coworkers and keeps things light throughout the work day.
moral_consequence: Luna has fun at work and her coworkers enjoy working with her.
immoral_action: Luna gets physically close and leads on a coworker who doesn't know she is married.
immoral_consequence: Luna is looked down upon by those that do know she is married.

norm: It is bad to stay with someone who is cheating.
situation: After having forgiven his infidelity once before Paula has just discovered that her husband is cheating again.
intention: Paula wants to respond to her current situation.
moral_action: Paula gives her husband an ultimatum, either he stays faithful or their marriage ends.
moral_consequence: Paula is empowered and her sense of self worth grows.
immoral_action: Scared of losing her husband Paula decides to ignore the infidelity and stay with her husband.
immoral_consequence: Paula's sense of shame grows as her husband becomes more brazen.

norm: It's good to inform your friends of things.
situation: Willie's birthday was coming up, and he wanted to have a party.
intention: Willie wanted his friends to attend his party.
moral_action: Willie sent out invitations to his birthday party two weeks before it was scheduled.
moral_consequence: Most of Willie's friends were able to attend his birthday party.
immoral_action: Willie waited until the day of the party to send out the invitations.
immoral_consequence: None of Willie's friends got the invitations in time to attend his party.

norm: You should always invite your family to your wedding.
situation: Ryan and his bride Maria are getting married and Maria doesn't like Ryan's parents.
intention: Ryan wants his bride to be happy.
moral_action: Ryan invites his parents to the wedding after talking it over with Maria and assuring her she won't be anywhere near them.
moral_consequence: Ryan's parents are happy and feel loved.
immoral_action: Ryan does not invite his parents to his wedding at all.
immoral_consequence: Ryan's parents are depressed and sad and think Ryan doesn't love them.

norm: It's wrong to tell secrets to your friend's partner.
situation: Jenna sees her friend Alanna smoking, even though she reluctantly promised her boyfriend she'd quit smoking.
intention: Jenna wants to help her friend.
moral_action: Jenna tells Alanna she knows she's been really stressed lately, and she (Jenna) won't tell Alanna's boyfriend.
moral_consequence: Alanna hugs Jenna and thanks her for being so understanding about this.
immoral_action: Jenna tells Alanna's boyfriend in the name of improving her health.
immoral_consequence: Alanna trusts Jenna less and stops telling her anything really personal.

norm: It's wrong to not make time for your family.
situation: Jim started a new business, so he hasn't had enough time to spend with his family.
intention: Jim wants to have a successful business to help support his family.
moral_action: Jim has a healthy work-life balance and spends lots of time with his family.
moral_consequence: Jim's business does well, and his family is happy.
immoral_action: Jim spends all of his time at work and never hangs out with his family.
immoral_consequence: Jim's wife divorces him and takes their kids with her.

norm: You shouldn't pry into your partner's things.
situation: Ivy notices that her husband seems more stressed than normal after work, and she stumbles across his unlocked laptop.
intention: Ivy wants to know more about her husband.
moral_action: Ivy gives her husband a hug and asks him what's been bothering him.
moral_consequence: Ivy's gesture of affection makes her husband feel safe enough to open up about his problems.
immoral_action: Ivy reads her husbands emails and Facebook messages from his coworkers.
immoral_consequence: Ivy's husband yells at her when he discovers her reading his things.

norm: It's bad to show up late to your appointments
situation: Jack made an appointment to go to a baseball game with his son Lee this afternoon.
intention: Jack wants to pick up his son for the game.
moral_action: Jack picks his son up early, and they make it to the game on time.
moral_consequence: Lee has a great time with his dad at the baseball game.
immoral_action: Jack fails to manage his time properly and arrives to pick up his son two hours late.
immoral_consequence: Lee is very upset with his dad and no longer wants to go to the game.

norm: It's wrong to be rude to siblings spouses.
situation: Leah is at a family dinner with her parents, her siblings, and their spouses.
intention: Leah wants to avoid discussing why she's still single.
moral_action: Leah acknowledges and deflects the questions she receives about her dating life, while remaining civil.
moral_consequence: Leah is happy to end the dinner without having to discuss dating.
immoral_action: Leah refuses to answer a question from her sister's spouse regarding a date Leah recently went on with a mutual friend.
immoral_consequence: Leah's sister's spouse is annoyed but Leah finishes the dinner without discussing her dating life.

norm: It is good to act selflessly toward others
situation: Billy sees a ball fly over his fence and lands in his yard.
intention: Billy wants to decide what to do with the ball.
moral_action: Billy decides that the other person who lost this ball is more important than him having an extra and throws it back.
moral_consequence: "Billy hears someone call ""Thank you!"" as he sees the ball sail back other the fence."
immoral_action: Billy takes a ball and keeps it for himself, since it landed in his yard.
immoral_consequence: Billy now has one more ball than he already has by claiming this one.

norm: Is wrong to betray your friends
situation: Hillary's friend just confided to her that they are addicted to cocaine.
intention: Hillary wants to help her friend with their addiction.
moral_action: Hillary sits and talks with her friend and offers to do anything to help her quit.
moral_consequence: Hillary's friend uses them as support system and eventually is able to quit cocaine.
immoral_action: Hillary tells his friends parents that they are addicted to cocaine.
immoral_consequence: Hillary's friend feels betrayed, stops being her friend and continues to use drugs.

norm: You shouldn't sleep over at a significant other's friend's house.
situation: Sarah's husband Jay is away on a business trip, and she needs someone to help her study her flashcards for an upcoming nurses exam.
intention: Sarah wants Jay's friend, Tom, to help her study.
moral_action: Sarah asks Tom if he can come over the next morning to help her study.
moral_consequence: Sarah and Tom study over breakfast before Tom has to leave for work.
immoral_action: Sarah goes over to Tom's late in the evening to study, when he suggests that she stay over.
immoral_consequence: One thing leads to another and Sarah and Tom end up sleeping together.

norm: It's wrong for kids to lie to their parents.
situation: Lara is invited to a party with older kids.
intention: Lara wants to go to the party.
moral_action: Lara asks to go to the party and promises to be responsible.
moral_consequence: Lara is allowed to go and her parents extend her curfew.
immoral_action: Lara tells her parents she is studying but goes to the party instead.
immoral_consequence: The police bring Lara home and she is grounded.

norm: You should respect your parent's partner.
situation: Charles walks into the house carrying a whoopie cushion, and he notices his father's new girlfriend  Susan talking to a group of well dressed people in the kitchen.
intention: Charles wants to amuse himself.
moral_action: Charles greets Susan before he watches his favorite television show.
moral_consequence: Susan is touched by Charles' actions and hugs him after their other guests leave.
immoral_action: Charles slips the whoopie cushion on Susan's chair before hiding behind the couch.
immoral_consequence: Susan is humiliated when she sits down on the whoopie cushion.

norm: It's bad to be a tattletale.
situation: Sara sees her little brother playing in the dirt outside after he takes a bath.
intention: Sara wants her brother to go inside the house.
moral_action: Sara tells her brother to go back into the house and cleans him off.
moral_consequence: Sara's mother never knows that her brother had gotten dirty in the first place.
immoral_action: Sara runs into the house screaming that her brother is doing something bad.
immoral_consequence: Sara's mother gets upset at Sara because she thought something was wrong with the boy.

norm: You shouldn't let your friends question your religion.
situation: Jonathan, who is working in the science field and is also Christian, is having a discussion with his friends about religion.
intention: Jonathan wants to respond to his friend who claims that being religious is incompatible with being a scientist.
moral_action: Jonathan tells his friend that he should respect other people's beliefs and explains what his faith means to him.
moral_consequence: Jonathan feels good about standing his ground and communicating his real thoughts to his friend.
immoral_action: Jonathan doesn't like heated discussions, which is why he tells his friend that he is right, even though he doesn't agree with him.
immoral_consequence: Jonathan feels weak and bad for not defending his faith and not telling his friend that he should respect Jonathan's beliefs.

norm: It's rude to ghost someone.
situation: Kylie is talking to a boy through an online dating app when he meets a new boy at her school that she's more interested in.
intention: Kylie wants to focus her attention on the new boy from her school.
moral_action: Kylie tells the boy on the dating app that she is getting serious with someone else and won't be talking to him anymore.
moral_consequence: The boy on the dating app is sad initially, but accepts reality and moves on.
immoral_action: Kylie stops talking to the boy on the dating app and instead exclusively texts the new boy from school.
immoral_consequence: The boy on the dating app is frustrated and stops attempting to talk to women entirely.

norm: It's good to stand up for your siblings.
situation: Antony saw that his little brother Andrew was being berated by their father for acting like a girl.
intention: Antony wants to say something to Andrew.
moral_action: Antony tells Andrew that he's fine the way he is and tells their dad there's nothing wrong with being a girl.
moral_consequence: Andrew feels grateful for his brother's support and feels like he has someone he can trust.
immoral_action: Antony teases Andrew along with their father and tells him to stop acting gay.
immoral_consequence: Andrew is hurt by what his brother and father are saying and grows distant from them.

norm: You should never cheat on your romantic partner
situation: Cory has been losing interest in his current romantic partner.
intention: Cory wants to be in an exciting romance.
moral_action: Cory breaks up with his current partner before exploring his other options.
moral_consequence: Cory's partner understands that it isn't working and is free to move on.
immoral_action: Cory engages in romance with another woman while still with his partner.
immoral_consequence: Cory's partner finds out about this and is devastated that he would do this.

norm: You shouldn't remain with a cheating partner.
situation: Dave is at home when his wife Cindy comes back from being out with friends and admits to him she just slept with someone else.
intention: Dave wants to react to Cindy's admission.
moral_action: Dave tells Cindy that she needs to pack her things and move out as soon as possible as their relationship is finished.
moral_consequence: Dave gains a bunch of self respect and feels stronger and more in control of his life.
immoral_action: Dave tells Cindy that it's OK, and she just made a mistake, and he's sure she won't do it again.
immoral_consequence: Cindy is emboldened to cheat even more and stays with Dave just to exploit him for money.

norm: You should prioritize important pursuits above hobbies.
situation: Dallas' best friend invites her to a concert on a night that she is scheduled to work.
intention: Dallas wants to have a fun, well-rounded life.
moral_action: Dallas tells her friend that she can't make the concert, but can hangout the next night.
moral_consequence: Dallas keeps her job and is still able to have fun with her friend.
immoral_action: Dallas skips her work shift to go to the concert.
immoral_consequence: Dallas is fired from her job.

norm: You shouldn't cheat on people.
situation: Karina and Roland have been a serious couple for years, but recently Karina was asked out on a date by her coworker.
intention: Karina wants to respond to her coworker's invitation.
moral_action: Karina tells her coworker she is flattered, but is very much in love with Roland and must decline.
moral_consequence: Karina's coworker respects her honesty and leaves her alone.
immoral_action: Karina agrees to the date with her coworker.
immoral_consequence: Roland is heartbroken to find out about Karina's date and ends their years-long relationship.

norm: It's wrong to date two men at the same time.
situation: Shelia is looking for someone to date.
intention: Sheila wants to find a husband.
moral_action: Sheila goes to a speed-dating event.
moral_consequence: Sheila finds the perfect man for her to be in a relationship with.
immoral_action: Sheila starts see two different gentlemen suitors.
immoral_consequence: Sheila gets dumped by both men when they find out about each other dating the same woman.

norm: You shouldn't insult your siblings
situation: Rob is working on homework and his little sister is being annoying which is very distracting.
intention: Rob wants to get his sister to leave him alone.
moral_action: Rob promises to play with her later if she lets him finish his homework in peace.
moral_consequence: Rob gets to finish his homework without distraction.
immoral_action: Rob tells her she is an annoying brat and to go away.
immoral_consequence: Rob makes his little sister cry and feels bad for it.

norm: It is wrong to ruin other people's marriages
situation: Phil is hurt when his ex-girlfriend gets married a few months after breaking up with him.
intention: Phil wants relief from the pain he feels.
moral_action: Phil gets emotional support from his friends and family in order to get over his ex.
moral_consequence: Phil is able to move on to a happier relationship with another woman.
immoral_action: Phil sends his ex flirty texts and e-mails which causes her husband to begin distrusting her.
immoral_consequence: Phil is shamed when their mutual friends find out what he has done and get mad at him.

norm: It's bad to betray the trust of your partner.
situation: After living together with Bobby in quarantine for months, Grace notices a change in Bobby’s demeanor.
intention: Grace wants to inquire about Bobby's feelings in the relationship.
moral_action: Grace asks bobby about how he has been feeling in their relationship, and he tells her some of the problems that he has been thinking about.
moral_consequence: Grace and bobby work on their problems and the relationship is able to continue growing.
immoral_action: Grace asks Bobby how he has been feeling in the relationship, and he says that everything is fine.
immoral_consequence: Bobby lies about everything being fine, and he and Grace continue having problems until their relationship unravels.

norm: It is wrong to steal from your brother.
situation: Dave is in his brother's bedroom where his brother John keeps cash openly and Dave wants to raise money for his favorite charity.
intention: Dave wants to decide in which way he will raise money.
moral_action: Dave decides that he will call his brother and some other friends and ask them if they're willing to donate.
moral_consequence: John and the others are delighted that Dave is including them in his charity drive.
immoral_action: Dave decides that he will take the cash that is sitting in john's bedroom and give it to the charity.
immoral_consequence: John finds his cash missing and is stressed out wondering who took it and feeling insecure and violated.

norm: People should not ignore family members when around family.
situation: Tim is sent to visit his grandmother for the day.
intention: Tim wants to find something to do to entertain himself.
moral_action: Tim plays a board game with his grandmother, and they talk while they play.
moral_consequence: Tim's grandmother is glad she got the chance to spend time with Tim while he visited her.
immoral_action: Tim plays a game on his phone and doesn't even look at or talk to his grandmother.
immoral_consequence: Tim's grandmother is sad that Tim won't interact with her at all.

norm: It's bad to cheat with your friend's significant other.
situation: Danica has been single for a long time and envies her best friend for her sexy boyfriend.
intention: Danica wants to sleep with a handsome man.
moral_action: Danica stops lusting after her best friend's boyfriend and finds someone sexy on Tinder.
moral_consequence: Danica's best friend is happy in her love life.
immoral_action: Danica endlessly flirts with her best friend's boyfriend until she gets to sleep with him.
immoral_consequence: Danica's best friend finds out about the affair and is brokenhearted.

norm: You shouldn't expect to be perfect because nobody is.
situation: Janalin has a young toddler, who makes a mess of everything and refuses to obey her.
intention: Janalin wants to improve her parenting skills.
moral_action: Janalin gets a parenting book and implements a little bit of it at a time.
moral_consequence: Janalin slowly improves her parenting skills over time and doesn't stress about it.
immoral_action: Janalin gets a parenting book and tries to follow everything in it to the very letter.
immoral_consequence: "Janalin can't learn everything immediately and criticizes herself for being ""stupid""."

norm: It is unobservant to lose track of your family members.
situation: Kerry was babysitting her younger brother and brings him to the park.
intention: Kerry wants to talk to her friend on her phone.
moral_action: Kerry waits until they get home to call her friend on the phone.
moral_consequence: Kerry is able to keep her brother in her sight the whole time they are on the phone.
immoral_action: Kerry lets her brother run off alone and talks to her friend the whole time they are at the park.
immoral_consequence: Kerry can't find her brother and has to call the police to file a missing persons report.

norm: You shouldn't ditch people when you're out shopping with them.
situation: Mary is out shopping with friends.
intention: Mary feels tired and wants to go home.
moral_action: Mary asks her friends to wrap up their trip, explaining her fatigue.
moral_consequence: Mary's friend's offer to find another way home and allow Mary to leave guilt free.
immoral_action: Mary waits until her friends are trying on clothes and leave.
immoral_consequence: Mary's friends are angry and vow to not speak to her for weeks.

norm: Is it wrong to cheat on your significant other
situation: Jaime has been married to Alyssa for three years, but Alyssa has been so busy with business trips that Jaime has started to feel lonely.
intention: Jaime wants to feel loved.
moral_action: Jaime talks to Alyssa about how he's feeling after she returned from a business trip.
moral_consequence: The two talk about their concerns and plan a long vacation together in a tropical island.
immoral_action: Jaime starts sleeping with a coworker while Alyssa is away in her business trips.
immoral_consequence: Alyssa finds out about Jaime's relationship with his coworker and the two get divorced.

norm: It's good to stay faithful to your partner.
situation: Newly engaged Rob runs in to an old boyfriend and makes plans to meet him for drinks.
intention: Rob wants to reconnect with his former paramour.
moral_action: Rob invites his partner to join him and his ex for drinks.
moral_consequence: Rob's fiancee spends the whole night bragging about how wonderful Rob is.
immoral_action: Rob drinks beyond moderation and ends up sleeping with his old flame.
immoral_consequence: Rob's fiancee pawns the ring Rob gave him and moves to another state.

norm: It's wrong to marry someone you don't love
situation: Philip just had a one night stand with Alexis, who he doesn't really like very much, but it turns out he got her pregnant, so he wants to take responsibility.
intention: Philip wants to be in his child's life.
moral_action: Philip and Alexis decide to work on having a friendship for the sake of their child.
moral_consequence: Philip and Alexis find a mutual respect for one another and are able to co-parent effectively.
immoral_action: Philip proposes to Alexis, and the two of them become husband and wife and move in together.
immoral_consequence: The marriage makes Philip and Alexis both miserable, because there was never any real connection.

norm: It's wrong for partners to spy on each other.
situation: Donald is concerned that his girlfriend, Wilma, has been acting secretively lately.
intention: Donald wants to find out why Wilma has been acting secretively.
moral_action: Donald tells Wilma that he has noticed some secretive behavior in her actions lately and asks to talk about what's going on with her.
moral_consequence: Wilma explains the behavior that he has noticed, and they have an open conversation about it, leaving them both feeling more secure in their relationship.
immoral_action: Donald goes through Wilma's phone and the browser history on her laptop, while she is showering.
immoral_consequence: Wilma catches Donald walking away from her computer when she gets out of the shower and feels violated, and Donald feels guilty.

norm: It is hurtful to exclude a friend from a game.
situation: Dave is a team captain of a baseball game when Will, who can hardly play at all, wants to join his team.
intention: Dave wants to win the game.
moral_action: Dave takes Will onto his team and assigns him to be the catcher.
moral_consequence: Will learns to catch a ball and feels involved in the game without posing a big risk to his team.
immoral_action: Dave decides not to take Will because he's not very good, and he wants to win.
immoral_consequence: Will feels rejected and loses self-esteem, drifting away from his friends.

norm: You're expected to go home for Christmas.
situation: Roy is going to have some extra time off this season for Christmas break, and he usually spends it going home to see his family.
intention: Roy wants to change up what he normally does during Christmas.
moral_action: Roy goes home for Christmas but arranges a new activity for the family to do.
moral_consequence: Roy's family starts up a new holiday tradition thanks to his idea.
immoral_action: Roy spends the holiday going on a trip with friends instead.
immoral_consequence: Roy's family doesn't get the same enjoyment out of Christmas without him.

norm: It's rude to choose a friend over your significant other.
situation: Roger is getting ready for his date with Susan when his friend Antonio invites him to a football game.
intention: Roger wants to watch football.
moral_action: Roger takes Susan to a wings bar where they watch the football game while eating wings.
moral_consequence: Susan learns more about football.
immoral_action: Roger goes to the football game after telling Susan he's sick.
immoral_consequence: Susan is hurt when she finds out the truth.

norm: It's bad to keep secrets from your children.
situation: James sees an invitation for his son in the mail for a birthday party around the same time he has a big test at school.
intention: James wants to make sure his son performs well on the test.
moral_action: James gives the invitation to his son and says he'll take him to the party as long as he studies enough for the test.
moral_consequence: James' son studies hard for the test and is able to go to the party, and he gets a good grade on the test.
immoral_action: James hides the invitation and tries to make sure his son doesn't find out about the party.
immoral_consequence: James' son hears about the party at school and rebelliously studies very little for the exam.

norm: It is good to reconnect with people.
situation: Gabby has lost touch with an old friend and has been feeling lonely.
intention: Gabby wants to have meaningful relationships.
moral_action: Gabby's friend reaches out, and Gabby makes time to have a video chat to catch up.
moral_consequence: Gabby's friend feels valued and is glad to rekindle the friendship.
immoral_action: Gabby's friend tries to reaches out several times, but Gabby always prioritizes hanging out with other friends.
immoral_consequence: Gabby's friend feels rejected and hurt, and stops reaching out.

norm: You shouldn't ignore real people in favor of using technology
situation: Jamie has a date with his girlfriend on the same night his guild has a big event planned online.
intention: Jamie wants to make a decision about how to spend his evening.
moral_action: Jamie bows out of the online event and goes out with his girlfriend.
moral_consequence: Jamie has an enjoyable night in real life.
immoral_action: Jamie makes up an excuse and cancels on his girlfriend.
immoral_consequence: Jamie gets into a fight with his girlfriend when she finds out he bailed on her for a game.

norm: It's not okay to call people names or be insulting.
situation: Maria is in a crowded mall parking lot, and someone just parked into the spot she was trying to park in.
intention: Maria wants to vent out her anger.
moral_action: Maria loudly comments about the other driver, but keeps her windows closed so the other woman doesn't hear.
moral_consequence: Maria feels better, yet the other woman has not been insulted.
immoral_action: Maria yells rude things at the woman in the other car.
immoral_consequence: The woman yells back at her, and they both feel terrible.

norm: It's rude to call the police on a friend.
situation: Andrea's friend Wanda has been smoking weed a lot to cope with her depression and Andrea is concerned.
intention: Andrea wants Wanda to stop smoking so much.
moral_action: Andrea talks to Wanda about her concerns and refers her to a therapist.
moral_consequence: Wanda is able to address her underlying mental health problems, so she can stop using drugs as a crutch.
immoral_action: Andrea calls the police and tells them Wanda is in possession of drugs.
immoral_consequence: Wanda is arrested and her emotional state worsens.

norm: It's wrong to lie to someone you love.
situation: Cody had told his wife that he would be home right after work.
intention: Cody wants to have a beer.
moral_action: Cody comes straight home after work and has a beer with his dinner.
moral_consequence: Cody relaxes with his beer as he watches television with his wife.
immoral_action: Cody tells his wife that he is working late and goes to the bar to have a beer.
immoral_consequence: Cody's wife smells the alcohol on his breathe and makes him sleep on the couch.

norm: You should value personal relationships over inheritances.
situation: Ray has a rich but mean grandfather who offers him his inheritance if he stops talking to the rest of the family.
intention: Ray wants to have a happy fulfilling life in his later years.
moral_action: Ray refuses the offer stating that he loves his family and will put them first.
moral_consequence: Ray is not rich, but he is content and has many strong and happy relationships with his family.
immoral_action: Ray accepts the offer and burns all bridges with his family so that he can get the inheritance.
immoral_consequence: Ray is rich when he is older but also lonely and viewed in the same way that his grandfather was.

norm: It's rude to your family to prioritize work over them.
situation: Blaine has been told that he must work overtime on Saturday and then his mom calls to invite him to a family reunion on Saturday.
intention: Blaine wants to respond to his mom about coming over on Saturday.
moral_action: Blaine tells his mom that he'll call in sick to work on Saturday and be there for sure.
moral_consequence: Blaine's mom is delighted to hear Blaine will attend and is excited to see her son.
immoral_action: Blaine tells his mom that he is being made to work overtime that day and that he can't come.
immoral_consequence: Blaine's mom is sad and feels that the reunion will be meaningless without Blaine there.

norm: Shunning people close to you is hurtful.
situation: Sally is a very religious person, and has found out that her friend Jack is gay.
intention: Sally wants to follow her religion.
moral_action: Sally disagrees with Jack's homosexuality, but agrees to still be his friend.
moral_consequence: Sally and Jack come to understand each other better and become better friends.
immoral_action: Sally says she can't abide by Jack's orientation and cuts him from her life.
immoral_consequence: Jack becomes depressed, and Sally's other mutual friends cut her off, as well.

norm: It is good to talk about experiences.
situation: Caleb is in the break room at work making coffee when a new employee walks in and asks Caleb for advice.
intention: Caleb wants to get back to his office.
moral_action: Caleb tells the new employee to send him an email about it, and he'll respond soon.
moral_consequence: The new employee trusts Caleb and sends him a detailed email about an hour later.
immoral_action: Caleb tells the new employee that he can't talk right now because he has to get back to working.
immoral_consequence: The new employee thinks Caleb doesn't like him and never initiates another conversation with him.

norm: It's not okay to disrespect your spouse.
situation: Terry is playing a board game with her spouse and her spouse loses miserably to her.
intention: Terry wants to talk to her spouse about their game performance.
moral_action: Terry congratulates her spouse on his effort and offers another round.
moral_consequence: Terry's spouse thanks Terry, and they have a fun game together.
immoral_action: Terry makes fun of her spouse for losing and uses expletives.
immoral_consequence: Terry's spouse walks out of the room in a rage and Terry feels guilty.

norm: It's wrong to go through someone else's phone.
situation: Anna heard her boyfriend Kevin talking to someone quietly on the phone.
intention: Anna wants to find out was on the phone.
moral_action: Anna asks Kevin who he was talking to instead of going through his phone to find out.
moral_consequence: Kevin tells Anna that he was talking to a old friend.
immoral_action: When Anna sees Kevin's phone on the table she looks through his phone calls to see who he was talking to.
immoral_consequence: Kevin catches Anna with his phone and asks her what she's doing going through it.

norm: It's wrong not to spend time with your friends.
situation: Rose is tired from working and gets an invite from her friends to have dinner.
intention: Rose wants to get some rest.
moral_action: Rose goes to dinner and then comes back home to sleep.
moral_consequence: Rose's friends are happy that she came.
immoral_action: Rose skips dinner and sleeps until morning.
immoral_consequence: Rose's friends are mad that she never replied to the invite.

norm: It's wrong to have an affair.
situation: Keri is on a business trip out of state from her husband and has a free evening.
intention: Keri wants have a drink and enjoy herself.
moral_action: Keri takes a few drinks out of the mini bar in her hotel room and gives her husband a call to see how he is.
moral_consequence: Keri's husband is happy to talk with her and feels good about himself that she thought of him.
immoral_action: Keri heads to a local bar for some drinks and meets a local man who she brings back to her hotel room.
immoral_consequence: Keri's infidelity is eventually discovered and it makes her husband feel humiliated and betrayed.

norm: It's bad to cheat on one's partner in a relationship.
situation: Terry has been dating the same woman fora year and their relationship hasn't worked for quite some time.
intention: Terry wants to pursue other women.
moral_action: Terry communicates to his partner that things aren't working out, leaving her before exploring his other options.
moral_consequence: Terry and his partner are able to end things amicably.
immoral_action: Terry goes after other women while he's still in the same relationship.
immoral_consequence: Terry's partner finds out what he's been doing, and they have a very dramatic break up.

norm: It's expected that you be gracious towards your in-laws.
situation: John is a newly wed and visiting his wife's family for the first time.
intention: John wants to stay close to his wife.
moral_action: John spends lots of time with all the different members of his wife's family during the visit.
moral_consequence: John and his wife bond over their, now shared, family experience.
immoral_action: John tries to spend as much time alone with his wife as possible during the visit while ignoring her family.
immoral_consequence: John's wife's family grow to not trust John and their comments about it to John's wife eventually sour the marriage.

norm: You shouldn't flirt with people online if you're married.
situation: Dan is married to Jenny but spends a lot of time online talking to women about gardening, which he loves.
intention: Dan wants to talk to a woman online about gardening.
moral_action: Dan talks to a woman online about the fruits and vegetables he's going to try to grow next.
moral_consequence: Jenny finds Dan's online chat and buys him seeds for the fruits and vegetables he wants to try to grow next.
immoral_action: Dan talks to a woman online about gardening and makes many suggestive comments about how their body parts resemble different fruits and vegetables.
immoral_consequence: Jenny finds Dan's suggestive online chat, feels betrayed, and makes him sleep on the couch for a week.

norm: It's rude to exclude coworkers in social gatherings.
situation: Steve was inviting everyone at the office to his birthday party, but Scott was sick that day.
intention: Steve wants to finish inviting people.
moral_action: Steve drives all the way to Scott's house to give him the invitation.
moral_consequence: Scott thanks Steve for the invitation and later enjoys the party.
immoral_action: Steve decides it's too much work to find Scott just to give him the last invitation.
immoral_consequence: Scott hears about the party and believes he's the only one who wasn't invited.

norm: Friends should include each other.
situation: John and his friend Ted find out about a new action movie coming out that they both think looks exciting.
intention: John wants to go see the movie.
moral_action: John invites Ted to go see the movie together the following Saturday.
moral_consequence: Ted is excited and has a great time at the movie.
immoral_action: John goes to see the movie by himself the following Saturday.
immoral_consequence: Ted later finds out and is sad that John went without inviting him.

norm: You should support your partner's dreams.
situation: Kenny is sitting on the porch with his wife Mayra, and she tells him that she is thinking of starting a photography business.
intention: Kenny wants to respond to his wife.
moral_action: Kenny tells Mayra that it is a good idea and that he will do anything he can to support her.
moral_consequence: Mayra is encouraged by her husband, and she begins writing up a business plan.
immoral_action: Kenny tells Mayra that she will never do it and it is a waste of time to think about.
immoral_consequence: Mayra feels disheartened, and she abandons the idea of starting a company.

norm: You shouldn't go into someone's closet without their permission.
situation: Jane is getting dressed and doesn't have any more shirts to wear.
intention: Jane wants to find a shirt to wear.
moral_action: Jane asks her sister if she can borrow a shirt from her closet.
moral_consequence: Jane's sister compromises with Jane and lets her have a shirt.
immoral_action: Jane goes into her sister's closet and grabs a shirt.
immoral_consequence: Jane's sister can't find the shirt and gets upset.

norm: It's wrong to take other people's cutlery.
situation: Pedro is at dinner with a dining companion and notices he is missing a fork.
intention: Pedro wants to get a fork.
moral_action: Pedro asks the server for a fork.
moral_consequence: Pedro's dining companion will think he is well mannered.
immoral_action: Pedro takes the fork of his dining companion.
immoral_consequence: Pedro's dining companion will think Pedro lacks manners.

norm: You shouldn't ditch people while on vacation.
situation: Jen is on vacation with friends and goes out for a walk while everyone is asleep.
intention: Jen wants to hang out with a cool group of people she met during the walk.
moral_action: Jen exchanges contact details with the group to hang out later and returns to the hotel.
moral_consequence: Jen's friends have a great time hanging out with the other group.
immoral_action: Jen spends hours having drinks with the people she met.
immoral_consequence: Jen's friends get up and eventually think she is rude for leaving them for so long.

norm: It's bad to try to punish your friends.
situation: Sal is annoyed with his friend but his friend is inviting Sal over.
intention: Sal needs to choose how to respond.
moral_action: Sal accepts and says that he needs to clear something up with his friend.
moral_consequence: Sal is relieved that the issue is being approached.
immoral_action: Sal tells his friend that he's not in the mood.
immoral_consequence: Sal doesn't feel relief and the tension builds.

norm: It's wrong to ghost your friends.
situation: John's friend Ryan sends him a text asking him to help him move this weekend if he's free, but John has plans already and will be out of town.
intention: John needs to figure out how to react to Ryan's text.
moral_action: John decides to shoot Ryan a text back saying he's going to be out for the weekend, and can't help.
moral_consequence: Ryan is sad he can't get John's help, but appreciates John getting back to him.
immoral_action: John decides not to mesdsage Ryan back, figuring Ryan will realize John's busy by the lack of response.
immoral_consequence: Ryan think's John's a poor excuse for a friend because he doesn't respond.

norm: You shouldn't hate your sister.
situation: Sam finds out his sister was stealing his clothing without him asking, and is with his family.
intention: Sam wants to give his opinion about his sister to his family.
moral_action: Sam explains to his family that he wants his sister to change her behavior, but that he doesn't hate her.
moral_consequence: Sam's family tells him they will think about helping Sam and her sister, and Sam is grateful.
immoral_action: Sam explains to his family that he hates his sister, and wants her to move out.
immoral_consequence: Sam's family tells Sam he is overreacting and Sam feels hopeless.

norm: You shouldn't rub your partner the wrong way.
situation: James knew that his girlfriend was sensitive about being short.
intention: James wants to entertain himself when he is feeling bored.
moral_action: James asks his girlfriend to watch movies or play board games with him.
moral_consequence: James and his girlfriend spend many happy evenings with each other.
immoral_action: "James frequently calls his girlfriend ""Short Stack"" and ""Pint Size""."
immoral_consequence: "James' girlfriend retaliates by calling him ""deadbeat"" and ""Mr. Two Minutes""."

norm: It's mean to kick your family out of your house.
situation: Denise's cousin Jerry fell on hard times and has been staying in her house for months as he is unemployed.
intention: Denise wants to talk to Jerry about his situation.
moral_action: Denise tells Jerry he can continue staying if he does chores.
moral_consequence: Jerry contributes to the upkeep of the house and has a place to live.
immoral_action: Denise tells Jerry he has to leave as he has no money to pay rent.
immoral_consequence: Jerry will be distraught and homeless.

norm: It's not nice to gossip about your ex partners.
situation: Gabriel's boyfriend broke up with her, and she is out at the bar with her friends.
intention: Wants to appear as if the break up didn't hurt her.
moral_action: Gabriel spends the night dancing and telling jokes with her friends, and avoids bringing up her ex.
moral_consequence: Gabriel and her friends have a great night together and make lots of memories.
immoral_action: Gabriel tells her friends that it was her who broke up with her boyfriend, because he wasn’t smart or wealthy enough.
immoral_consequence: Her ex-boyfriend's brother overhears, and tells her friends that she is lying, causing her friends to distrust her.

norm: It's bad to snoop in your parent's personal belongings.
situation: James ordered a pizza but was two dollars short of the total price.
intention: James wants to find two more dollars to pay the pizza guy.
moral_action: James decided against raiding his parents' room and instead checked the couch cushions.
moral_consequence: James found three dollars, allowing him to both pay and tip the delivery person.
immoral_action: James decided to check his parents' top drawer for petty cash.
immoral_consequence: James found a few bucks in change alongside an enormous dildo.

norm: It's rude to borrow things without asking
situation: Anne notices a top that she really likes in her sister's closet.
intention: Anne wants to wear the top out on a date.
moral_action: Anne asks her sister ahead of time if she can borrow the top for a night.
moral_consequence: Anne's sister is pleased because she can borrow something in return.
immoral_action: Anne takes the top without asking and wears it while going out with the intention of returning it.
immoral_consequence: Anne's sister cannot find the top when she goes to wear it herself.

norm: You shouldn't stalk your significant other.
situation: Larry is feeling lonely.
intention: Larry wants to spend time with his partner.
moral_action: Larry texts his partner to ask to spend time together after work.
moral_consequence: Larry and his partner have a fun night watching movies and talking.
immoral_action: Larry follows his partner when they leave work to see where they are going.
immoral_consequence: Larry's partner breaks up with him and threatens a restraining order.

norm: It's nice to welcome unexpected guests into your home.
situation: John and Bill decide to show up at their friend Kevin's house unannounced one evening.
intention: Kevin wants to express his surprise at their visit.
moral_action: Kevin tells the pair that it's a great surprise that they're there and invites them inside.
moral_consequence: John and Bill are happy to be invited inside and look forward to spending some time with Kevin.
immoral_action: Kevin tells the pair that he's shocked they would come unnanounced like that and tells them to go home.
immoral_consequence: John and Bill are sad Kevin is reacting badly and are angry he's just sending them right back home.

norm: It's good to take marriage seriously.
situation: Ben has been married ten years.
intention: Ben wants to celebrate his anniversary.
moral_action: Ben writes his wife a beautiful letter to go along with a thoughtful gift.
moral_consequence: Ben's wife feels loved and appreciated and their marriage gets stronger.
immoral_action: Ben gives his wife a cheap gag gift.
immoral_consequence: Ben's wife storms off and doesn't speak to him for days.

norm: It's rude to tell your siblings you hate them.
situation: Joe watching tv when his brother, Jeff, walks into the room and sits on the couch.
intention: Jeff wants to play a video game.
moral_action: Jeff waits for the show to end and tells Joe he wants to use the tv.
moral_consequence: Joe relinquishes the tv and the brothers enjoy playing a game together.
immoral_action: Jeff yells at Joe and says he hates him when he won't agree to give up the tv.
immoral_consequence: Joe leaves the room crying and upset thinking his brother doesn't like him.

norm: You shouldn't call the cops on your in-laws.
situation: Sally took care of her mother-in-law in return for a small payment each week, but she has not been paid lately.
intention: Sally wanted to obtain payment for the last few weeks of her work.
moral_action: Sally talks to her in-laws to tell them it is important to her that she receive the money they owe her.
moral_consequence: Sally's in-laws arrange to catch up on the payments to Sally by the end of the week.
immoral_action: Sally calls the police to report that her in-laws owe her money to scare them and make them pay her.
immoral_consequence: Sally's in-laws are very upset with the commotion she caused by calling the police.

norm: it is good to be faithful
situation: Tommy has been working at his current job for twelve years.
intention: Tommy wants to work more hours.
moral_action: Tommy asks his boss if he can pick up more hours at work.
moral_consequence: Tommy gets a raise when his boss sees how much initiative he is taking.
immoral_action: Tommy leaves his job and works more hours at a new job.
immoral_consequence: The new job pays less and Tommy ends up making less money than before.

norm: It's not okay to be obsessed with an ex.
situation: Taylor just got into a relationship with Jack and is mainly doing it long-distance.
intention: Taylor wants to text her boyfriend.
moral_action: Taylor decides to text Jack only a couple times a day, to build rapport.
moral_consequence: Jack is impressed with Taylor's texting skills, and they both hit it off and feel elated.
immoral_action: Taylor decides to text Jack every hour to build up a rapport with Jack.
immoral_consequence: Jack feels overwhelmed and tells Taylor to back off, while Taylor feels insulted.

norm: It's kind to forgive someone for a wrongdoing.
situation: Ted asked his mom to pick him up from practice, but she forgot.
intention: Ted wants to say something to his mom.
moral_action: Ted tells his mom that it's okay and that everyone makes mistakes.
moral_consequence: Ted's mom is glad that Ted understands and tries to never disappoint him again.
immoral_action: Ted tells his mom that he'll never forgive her for making him wait there.
immoral_consequence: Ted's mom feels intense guilt and wishes Ted would be nicer.

norm: You shouldn't take things from your parents.
situation: Sally found a beautiful bracelet in her mom's drawer while looking for something else.
intention: Sally wants to obtain the bracelet.
moral_action: Sally asks about the bracelet and requests it for her upcoming birthday.
moral_consequence: Sally gets the bracelet as a gift.
immoral_action: Sally takes the bracelet without asking.
immoral_consequence: Sally gets in trouble for taking the bracelet.

norm: It is bad to lose your patience with your mother.
situation: Kirby's toy broke due to being worn down from use, but Kirby's mother thinks Kirby broke it by being irresponsible.
intention: Kirby wants to explain to his mother that he needs a new toy.
moral_action: Kirby calmly explains that the toy wore out from being played with so much.
moral_consequence: Kirby's mother replaces the worn out toy at the store.
immoral_action: Kirby reacts poorly under pressure and screams that it wasn't his fault.
immoral_consequence: Kirby gets the new toy, but he's grounded for a week and thus can't actually play with it.

norm: It's good to respond to your spouse's text messages.
situation: Ben is doing overtime at work when he receives a text message from his wife.
intention: Ben wants to consider responding to the text message.
moral_action: Ben quickly shoots a reply to his wife's text message and tells her that he is working overtime.
moral_consequence: Ben's wife acknowledges Ben's response and increasingly views Ben as a hardworker.
immoral_action: Ben chooses to completely ignore the text message and just focus on his work.
immoral_consequence: Ben's wife becomes suspicious and less trusting of what Ben as a result of him not responding.

norm: It's bad to not accept in-laws into your family.
situation: John is planning his wedding invitations and is down to the last table with two seats left.
intention: John wants to decide who to invite to the last two seats.
moral_action: John decides to invite his mother in law and father in law to the wedding.
moral_consequence: John's in laws are delighted they have been included in such a big event.
immoral_action: John decides to invite the neighbor couple from next door to fill the last two seats.
immoral_consequence: John's mother and father in law are sad that they've been excluded from such a big event.

norm: It's good to get along with your siblings.
situation: Hannah has a younger sister Alice, and they live together with their parents.
intention: Hannah wants to manage her relationship with Alice.
moral_action: Hannah takes care of many things for Alice when their mom is busy, and they are not just siblings but also best friends.
moral_consequence: Alice makes a delicious dessert for Hannah whenever she feels stressed with her college homework.
immoral_action: Hannah doesn't talk or show love to Alice much because she spends more time with her friends.
immoral_consequence: Alice feels unloved and lonely.

norm: It's wrong to not be there for your friends.
situation: Noelle realizes that she has a date booked for the same night as her friend's party.
intention: Noelle wants to correct the schedule conflict.
moral_action: Noelle goes to her friend's party, and she calls to reschedule the date.
moral_consequence: Noelle has a great time at the party, and she goes on the date the next day anyway.
immoral_action: Noelle tells her friend she's sick and goes on the date instead.
immoral_consequence: Noelle feels bad the entire date so it doesn't go well.

norm: It's rude to arrive late to your engagements.
situation: Jane was invited to her best friend's wedding, and she is running late because she is waiting to finish downloading a movie.
intention: Jane wants to make it to the wedding.
moral_action: Jane stops the movie download and just leaves, arriving on time.
moral_consequence: No one notices anything wrong and everyone is happy.
immoral_action: Jane waits to download the movie and then leaves, making her late.
immoral_consequence: Her friend notices and is disappointed in Jane.

norm: It's wrong to cheat on people.
situation: Jack is married, but a girl he has always had a crush on want to come over to his place.
intention: Jack wants to invite the girl over.
moral_action: Jack invites the girl over to meet his wife and eat lunch together.
moral_consequence: Jack's wife doesn't have to worry about Jack cheating on her since Jack introduced the girl to her.
immoral_action: Jack invites the girl over when his wife isn't home, and they have sex.
immoral_consequence: Jack's wife could end up finding out and be completely heartbroken.

norm: It's good to stand by your friends
situation: Sabrina's friend Dave was accused of taking something from Koga.
intention: Sabrina wants to contribute to Dave's case.
moral_action: Sabrina defends Dave and stands by him because they are friends.
moral_consequence: The case against Dave fails due to lack of evidence.
immoral_action: Sabrina talks about how Dave would usually take things as a kid.
immoral_consequence: Dave is quickly found guilty and forced to replace what was taken.

norm: Families are expected to spend time together on holiday occasions.
situation: Rob finds out that there will be an extended holiday period this year where his company will be closed for an entire week.
intention: Rob wants to switch up where and how he spends the holidays this year.
moral_action: Rob invites his family along for a holiday trip with friends.
moral_consequence: Rob spends the holidays with family and gets a much needed change of pace.
immoral_action: Rob goes on a holiday trip with his friends.
immoral_consequence: Rob feels guilty for not spending the holidays with his family.

norm: It's nice to teach people new games.
situation: Phil knows how to play a game called Knights and Knaves and Bill doesn't.
intention: Phil wants to talk to Bill.
moral_action: Phil explains it's simple - Knights always tell the truth and Knaves always lie, you need to use what they tell you to solve the puzzles.
moral_consequence: Bill is excited to try the puzzle game and make a puzzle to stump Phil.
immoral_action: Phil talks to Bill about the wheather because he's too tired to explain a game to Bill.
immoral_consequence: Phil still has no one to play the game with once he finished talking to Bill.

norm: It's good to play sports with your buddies.
situation: Tyson is home on a Saturday and gets a text from his friends inviting him to play hockey with them.
intention: Tyson wants to spend his Saturday doing something fun.
moral_action: Tyson accepts his friends' offer and goes to the hockey rink to play with his friends.
moral_consequence: Tyson's friends have a good time playing hockey with Tyson, and they invite him to a movie that night.
immoral_action: Tyson declines his friends' offer to play hockey and instead stays home and plays video games.
immoral_consequence: Tyson's friends are disappointed that Tyson didn't come to play hockey, and they stop inviting him to activities.

norm: It's wrong not to help out your partner.
situation: Stephanie and Ron, a married couple, both have the day off of work, but Stephanie has promised to help her parents clean out their attic.
intention: Ron wants to find something to do with his day.
moral_action: Ron asks Stephanie if he can help her get the work done early, so that they can spend the rest of the day together at the park.
moral_consequence: Stephanie is thrilled to get some help with the work, and they both have a great afternoon, together at the park.
immoral_action: Ron leaves early to spend the day at a state park, hiking trails and enjoying the scenery.
immoral_consequence: Ron doesn't have much fun without Stephanie, and Stephanie spends the entire day in a dusty attic.

norm: It is good to support your team.
situation: John is attending a baseball game which his team is playing, and he is frustrated because they are losing badly.
intention: John wants to enjoy his evening.
moral_action: John passionately cheers for his team throughout the game and continuously tries to boost their morale.
moral_consequence: A member of the team notices John's unwavering support and gives him an autographed baseball bat after the game.
immoral_action: John decides to give up on the rest of the game and leave before it's over, going to catch a movie he'd been wanting to watch instead.
immoral_consequence: John misses out on a stunning comeback, a game that will be remembered for decades.

norm: It is expected that people show appreciation to veterans
situation: Jake is meeting his friend's father, Mike,  who is a veteran but struggling mentally and physically.
intention: Jake wants to know about the veteran's story.
moral_action: Jake talks with Mike about his story and thanks him for serving the country.
moral_consequence: Jake receives a piece of souvenir from Mike that he brought from the country he was deployed to.
immoral_action: Jake talks with Mike and tells him he got what he deserved for murdering people.
immoral_consequence: Jake is immediately asked to leave Mike's place.

norm: You shouldn't be with a spouse that cheats on you.
situation: Macy caught her husband cheating months ago and is having trouble moving past it.
intention: Macy wants to talk to her husband about the incident.
moral_action: Macy and her husband talk about the cheating, and she tells him she wants a divorce.
moral_consequence: Macy gets divorced and finally doesn't have to worry whether or not her husband is being faithful.
immoral_action: Macy and her husband talk about the infidelity, and he reassures her it was a mistake that will never happen again.
immoral_consequence: Macy stays with her husband but suffers because she never fully trusts him again.

norm: It's good to take your father to a ball game.
situation: Dick calls in to a radio station and wins a contest for two tickets to the baseball game.
intention: Dick wants to pick who to take with him.
moral_action: Dick decides to take his father, Bill to the ball game as his guest.
moral_consequence: Dick spends a great day bonding with his dad and enjoying life like it used to be when he was young.
immoral_action: Dick decides to take his wife Sandy as his guest to the baseball game.

norm: It's good to take your friends out.
situation: Chris works in an office.
intention: Chris wants to spend time with his friends.
moral_action: Chris offers to treat his friends to a movie.
moral_consequence: Chris has fun with friends, and they offer to treat him to a movie next time.
immoral_action: Chris tells his friends to meet him somewhere and doesn't mean to help pay.
immoral_consequence: Chris's friends know he never pays and go somewhere without him, leaving him alone.

norm: It is wrong to not invite grandparents to a graduation.
situation: Nina is graduating from college soon, and she's preparing for the big day.
intention: Nina wants to finalize her list of invites to her graduation.
moral_action: Nina makes sure to add her grandparents to the list of those invited to her graduation so that they don't miss it.
moral_consequence: Nina's grandparents are very proud of Nina and her accomplishments, so they give her a generous graduation present.
immoral_action: Nina invites her parents, cousins and some friends but neglects to invite her grandparents.
immoral_consequence: Nina's grandparents' feelings are hurt, and they never get a chance to give her the graduation present they were planning on.

norm: It's good to support businesses on campus.
situation: A couple of students at James' school rent space in the student union to open a sandwich shop.
intention: James wants to buy a sandwich for lunch.
moral_action: James stops at the sandwich shop and buys a roast beef sandwich and chips for $10.
moral_consequence: James discovers that the shop makes much tastier sandwiches than any of the chains near the campus.
immoral_action: James walks to the Subway across the street from his college campus.
immoral_consequence: James saves a few dollars, but the other students' store eventually goes out of business due to a lack of customers.

norm: It is hurtful to distance yourself from your family.
situation: Mary had an argument with her parents after telling them about her intention to drop out of college.
intention: Mary wants to be independent and make her own decisions.
moral_action: Mary has a conversation with her parents and explains to them that she had thought this step over and asks them to believe in her.
moral_consequence: Mary's parents understand Mary's reasons and intentions and are proud that their daughter wants to take her life into her own hands.
immoral_action: Mary cuts off all ties with her parents and shuts off their attempts to contact her.
immoral_consequence: Mary's parents are extremely hurt and very worried about their daughter.

norm: You shouldn't have a crush on your partner's friend.
situation: Mary's new boyfriend Ted just introduced her to his best friend Bill.
intention: Mary wants to get to know Bill better.
moral_action: Mary arranges outings for the all three of them.
moral_consequence: Mary grows closer to both Bill and Ted.
immoral_action: Mary spends one on one time with Bill and soon starts to fall for him.
immoral_consequence: Mary causes a rift between the two friends when her feelings are revealed.

norm: It's wrong to get involved in your friend's sex life.
situation: Jane is hanging out with Susan, and they're talking about their boyfriends.
intention: Jane wants to ask Susan a question.
moral_action: Jane asks Susan if her boyfriend ever gets her flowers or other surprise gifts.
moral_consequence: Susan is happy to answer the question and it actually recalls fond memories for her of receiving flowers.
immoral_action: Jane asks Susan if her boyfriend ever performs oral sex on her or if he avoids it.
immoral_consequence: Susan is embarrassed to even discuss the topic and is shocked her friend would get so personal.

norm: It's wrong to want your family to move out.
situation: Gary's mom moved in with him and his wife and has stayed for longer than planned, and her habits are grating on him.
intention: Gary wants to stop being annoyed with his mother.
moral_action: Gary tries to compromise with his mom and get her to change her habits if she wants to continue to live with them.
moral_consequence: Gary's mom is unhappy that she is upsetting them, but understands, and they are able to work things out.
immoral_action: Gary gets into a big fight with him mom and demands that she move out immediately.
immoral_consequence: Gary's mom is insulted and leaves, and it fractures their relationship for a while.

norm: It is wrong to abadon you friends.
situation: Keith drove with Jim to the beach, where Keith met two girls who invited him to go to a movie.
intention: Keith wants to go to the movie with the girls he met.
moral_action: Keith finds Jim and drives him and the two girls to the movie theater.
moral_consequence: Jim partners up with one of the girls, and they end up making dinner plans for later in the week.
immoral_action: Keith takes his car and drives with the girls to the movie theater without informing Jim.
immoral_consequence: Jim wanders around the beach looking for Keith, gets sunburned, and has to walk home.

norm: It's rude to sleep with friends of a friend.
situation: Carolyn who is Dave's friend asks Dave's other friend Jim to hang out and maybe have sex tonight.
intention: Jim wants to respond to Carolyn's proposal.
moral_action: Jim tells Carolyn that out of respect for Dave they shouldn't really get involved with each other that way.
moral_consequence: Dave is delighted that Jim is doing what he can to maintain the trio as a good friendship.
immoral_action: Jim tells Carolyn that he thinks that's a great idea since he finds her very attractive.
immoral_consequence: Dave is sad that he seems to be left out of the trio now as two of his friends are hooking up.

norm: You should respect your friend's partner.
situation: Ashton and his friend, Tim, are bowling with Tim's girlfriend, Tracy.
intention: Ashton wants to talk to Tim about Tracy.
moral_action: Ashton tells Tim that Tracy's bowling game has improved over the course of the night.
moral_consequence: Tim sees Ashton as supportive and invites him to more activities with him and Tracy.
immoral_action: Ashton makes snarky comments to Tim about Tracy's bowling skills.
immoral_consequence: Tim and Tracy get angry and stop bowling with Ashton.

norm: It's good to allow children the freedom to adventure.
situation: James is sitting on the beach with his son, Adam, when Adam asks to go swimming in the ocean.
intention: James wants to protect his son.
moral_action: James tells Adam he may go swimming as long as he wears a life vest.
moral_consequence: Adamexperiences the joys of swimming while James has peace of mind over his son's safety.
immoral_action: James forbids Adam from swimming, telling him that it is too dangerous as he may drown.
immoral_consequence: Adam grows up to be very sheltered and afraid of normal life activities.

norm: It's good to get people gifts for Christmas.
situation: It is getting late in December and Jim is getting excited about the upcoming holiday.
intention: Jim wants to attend Christmas at a family member's house.
moral_action: Jim shows up with various gifts for the people in attendance.
moral_consequence: People open their gifts and all recipients of gifts from Jim thanked him for his thoughtfulness.
immoral_action: Jim shows up to his family member's house with out any presents.
immoral_consequence: People begin to act cold toward Jim when they realize none of the presents were from him.

norm: Don't interject yourself into the romantic relations of others.
situation: Karen sees her best friends's boyfriend talking with another girl.
intention: Karen wants to be a good friend.
moral_action: Karen thinks there must be a reason for him talking to another girl and minds her own business.
moral_consequence: Karen finds out that the friend's boyfriend was just talking to his sister and doesn't cause problems in her friend's relationship.
immoral_action: Karen thinks her best friend's boyfriend is flirting with the girl and calls her best friend right away to inform her.
immoral_consequence: Karen's best friend screams and yells at her boyfriend and their relationship is damaged.

norm: You should apologize when you betray your friends.
situation: Jon kissed his friend's girlfriend wile drunk and his friend confronts him about it.
intention: Jon wants to react to his friend's confrontation.
moral_action: Jon says he's really sorry for what he did saying that he was drunk at the time and asking for forgiveness.
moral_consequence: Although Jon's friend is upset he's happy Jon apologized and forgives him over time.
immoral_action: Jon tells his friend that since their girlfriend was willing to kiss Jon they should blame their girlfriend for what happened.
immoral_consequence: John's friend thinks Jon is scummy for betraying them and doesn't consider Jon a close friend any longer.

norm: It is not okay to tell other people's secrets.
situation: Hank is informed by a co-worker that the workers are planning a surprise party for their boss, who is planning a vacation soon.
intention: Hank wants the boss to be around for the surprise party.
moral_action: "Hank asks for ""help on an important project"" that just happens to be the same day as the surprise party so the boss will be there."
moral_consequence: The boss is surprised and the surprise party goes exactly as planned.
immoral_action: Hank tells their boss that they're planning a party for him and to make sure to be there.
immoral_consequence: The surprise is ruined and the party is nowhere near as special as they planned.

norm: It's good to have a relationship with your parents.
situation: Ivan and Paula are proud of there son Ray being successful and wished they saw him more.
intention: Ray wants to go visit his parents more often.
moral_action: Ray takes a real vacation and immerses himself in his parents, Ivan and Paula, and making memories together.
moral_consequence: Ray and his parents become close knit and the family bonds becomes strong.
immoral_action: Ray schedules meetings and does business on his phone and laptop for most of his visit with his parents, Ivan and Paula.
immoral_consequence: Ray and his parents become distant and the family loses its support system.

norm: It's important to get along with other people
situation: When James met his three roommates at summer camp, it seemed like they had nothing in common.
intention: James wants to spend the summer focused on his own interests.
moral_action: James goes off on his own during the day, but chats with his roommates about their activities at night.
moral_consequence: Some of James' best memories from camp turn out to be the late night conversations.
immoral_action: James insults the other campers' preferred activities and insists his choices are better.
immoral_consequence: The other campers decide to start pulling pranks on James while he sleeps.

norm: It is rude to embarass your significant other online
situation: Mira is reading the embarrassing sex hashtag that's trending on Twitter.
intention: Mira wants to interact with the hashtag.
moral_action: Mira retweets some of the funny stories and tags her friends.
moral_consequence: Mira learns more about her friends by reading their embarrassing stories.
immoral_action: Mira makes a post about her boyfriend's poor stamina in the bedroom the other night.
immoral_consequence: Mira gets an angry text from his boyfriend informing her that people are teasing him.

norm: You should contribute to group work.
situation: Fernando is very tired and is assigned a strenuous group assignment during history class.
intention: Fernando wants to go home.
moral_action: Fernando works hard with his group to quickly knock out the assignment so that he can go home and rest.
moral_consequence: Fernando and his group are given an A on the assignment and are given a voucher to skip one day of class for free.
immoral_action: Fernando wishes his group mates good luck and goes home without helping.
immoral_consequence: Fernando's group mates turn in the assignment but tell the teacher he didn't participate causing him to fail the assignment.

norm: It's bad to stalk your significant other.
situation: Jack is worried that his girlfriend spends tpo much time with a certain male coworker.
intention: Jack wants to make sure his girlfriend isn't cheating.
moral_action: Jack discusses his insecurity with his girlfriend.
moral_consequence: Jack feels reassured when his girlfriend assures him her relationship with her coworker is platonic.
immoral_action: Jack follows his girlfriend after work to make sure she stays faithful to him.
immoral_consequence: Jack gets dumped when his girlfriend catches him following her.

norm: It's bad to lie about your child's real father.
situation: Tina's young daughter, who doesn't know that Tina remarried, asks why she doesn't look anything like her stepdad, Tina's new husband.
intention: Tina wants to answer her daughter's question.
moral_action: Tina sits down with her daughter and explains that she and her biological father had a falling out, and that the stepdad is not the father.
moral_consequence: Tina's daughter is somewhat confused but happy that her mom gave her a truthful answer.
immoral_action: Tina tells her daughter that her stepdad is her biological father and that sometimes accidents happen.
immoral_consequence: Tina's daughter gets contacted by her biological father when she gets older and starts to distrust Tina.

norm: It's wrong to read other people's messages.
situation: Sam and Tina are sitting together for lunch when Tina gets a text, reads it, and smiles.
intention: Sam wants to know what made Tina smile.
moral_action: Sam asks Tina why she is smiling.
moral_consequence: Tina tells Sam that she was texted a joke and shares it with him.
immoral_action: Sam reaches over and grabs Tina's phone and reads the text she received.
immoral_consequence: Tina grabs her phone back, and storms off, ending her friendship with Sam.

norm: It's not okay to betray your vows in life.
situation: Jimmy is at the bar with some friends while his wife is at home when an attractive woman asks Jimmy to come home with her.
intention: Jimmy wants to answer the woman's proposal.
moral_action: Jimmy tells the woman that he is a married man who is faithful, so he'll have to decline.
moral_consequence: Jimmy's friends are proud of him and happy to see that he's a stand up guy who loves his wife.
immoral_action: Jimmy tells the woman that he will definitely go home with her, and they can leave right now.
immoral_consequence: Jimmy's friends are disgusted how easily Jimmy betrayed his marriage and his wife and don't like him anymore.

norm: It is bad to let family down.
situation: John promised his children, Beth and Luke, to decorate the house with Christmas lights, which they want to do right when John’s team is playing a game on TV.
intention: John needs to respond to Beth and Luke.
moral_action: John spends the afternoon decorating the house with Beth and Luke.
moral_consequence: Beth and Luke are happy, and they become closer to John.
immoral_action: John decides to watch the game after telling his kids that they will decorate tomorrow.
immoral_consequence: Beth and Luke are heartbroken that John broke his promise.

norm: It's good to make things for others to enjoy.
situation: Megan places the new fabric she bought at the store next to her sowing machine, and her grandkids need new doll clothes.
intention: Megan wants to create something.
moral_action: Megan uses the fabric to create a wide variety of doll clothes for her grandkids.
moral_consequence: Her grandkids become more popular after showing off their newly dressed dollls.
immoral_action: Megan uses all of the fabric to create a nice sweater for herself.
immoral_consequence: Megan's grandkids are sad that their grandmother didn't make doll clothes for them.

norm: You shouldn't talk to your ex behind your partner's back.
situation: Emily  feels lonely because hasn't seen her boyfriend in three weeks, and her ex boyfriend Ethan sits beside her to discuss her new relationship.
intention: Emily wants to feel less lonely.
moral_action: Emily excuses herself and calls her boyfriend asking him out on a date.
moral_consequence: Emily has fun spending time with her boyfriend.
immoral_action: Emily talks to her ex, and spends the afternoon hanging out with him at the arcade.
immoral_consequence: Emily gets into a fight with her boyfriend after her ex uploads a picture of them on social media.

norm: It's wrong to get too close to someone's spouse.
situation: Becky often asks her married coworker Frank to join her for drinks after work even though he always declines her invites.
intention: Becky wants to hang out with Frank.
moral_action: Becky arranges for a large group, including Frank, to meet at a bar after work.
moral_consequence: Becky is able to get to know Frank better in a platonic setting.
immoral_action: Becky connives to get Frank to join her at a bar after work.
immoral_consequence: Becky is humiliated when rumors spread around work that she is hitting on a married man.

norm: It's wrong to hide your feelings from people.
situation: Susan notices that she doesn't feel the way she used to for her current boyfriend.
intention: Susan wants to avoid dealing with a messy break up in person.
moral_action: Susan sends her boyfriend a sincere text about how she really feels and refuses to see him in person.
moral_consequence: Susan's boyfriend gets the hint and quickly moves on from their relationship.
immoral_action: Susan lets her relationship linger on pretending that everything is fine.
immoral_consequence: Susan's boyfriend is misled into believe that she still loves him.

norm: Family are expected to help each other with their problems.
situation: William's sister needs a ride to her soccer practice and asks William for a ride.
intention: William wants to respond to his sister.
moral_action: William obliges his sister's request and drives her to her soccer practice.
moral_consequence: William's sister thanks William and their relationship improves a bit.
immoral_action: William quickly says no to his sister and goes back to what he was doing.
immoral_consequence: William's sister becomes annoyed with William and does not help him the next time he needs help.

norm: You should not shirk academic duties.
situation: James has been invited to a concert as the same night as a mandatory biology lab.
intention: James doesn't want to miss seeing the band's music.
moral_action: James asks his friends to videotape the concert for him.
moral_consequence: James watches the concert with his friends the next day and gets an A in biology.
immoral_action: James decides to skip his lab in favor of attending the concert.
immoral_consequence: James enjoys the concert, but ends up failing his biology class since he missed the lab.

norm: You should be honest with someone you have feelings for.
situation: Max is approached by a classmate named Emily, asking him to the prom, but he doesn't like Emily.
intention: Max wants to answer Emily's request.
moral_action: Max declines Emily's request, stating he doesn't think they get along well.
moral_consequence: Emily understands and Max feels happy for being honest.
immoral_action: Max tells Emily he loves her, and he would love to go to the prom with her.
immoral_consequence: Max hates his time at the prom and Emily senses it.

norm: It's rude to end a date early.
situation: Victor is on a first date with Sally and it's not going well.
intention: Victor wants to change the situation.
moral_action: Victor tries to get to know Sally better and then livens up their conversation with funny stories for the rest of their date.
moral_consequence: Sally is happy that Victor made an effort on their date.
immoral_action: Victor tells Sally that he just remembered that he has to get up early, so he has to get going.
immoral_consequence: Sally is disappointed that Victor is ending their date all of the sudden.

norm: It's wrong to exclude your friends from outings.
situation: Sam decided to plan for a trip to Disney World with friends.
intention: Sam wants to decide on who to invite.
moral_action: Sam chooses to invite every single one of his friend to the Disney World trip and does not fail.
moral_consequence: Sam has an absolute blast at Disney World with his friends.
immoral_action: Sam decides to invite all of his friends but forgets to invite one.
immoral_consequence: The one friend that Sam forgot to invite is peeved and takes it up with Sam.

norm: You should support your friends in their pursuits.
situation: Jamie's friend Faye has taken up painting and shows her a painting she has worked on that looks pretty bad.
intention: Jamie wants to give feedback on the painting.
moral_action: Jamie says that she likes Faye's effort and feels she can improve the painting with practice.
moral_consequence: Faye appreciates Jamie's encouragement and becomes a great painter after a few years.
immoral_action: Jamie says based on what she sees, Faye should give up painting.
immoral_consequence: Faye feels bad and starts to cry.

norm: You shouldn't marry someone you aren't attracted to.
