situation: Joey is thinking about marrying Misty because of what her status would mean for his family.
intention: Joey wants to decide to marry Misty or not.
moral_action: Joey decides that he wants to marry the girl he's actually attracted to.
moral_consequence: Joey's family is upset but Joey couldn't be happier.
immoral_action: Joey decides to marry Misty for her status and importance regardless of attractiveness.
immoral_consequence: Joey's family is happy for the connections but Joey himself is not.

norm: It's expected that family should openly communicate.
situation: Mindy comes home from work crying and wants to talk to her husband John about what happened.
intention: John wants to watch the ballgame.
moral_action: John puts the game on record and comforts Mindy and talks with her.
moral_consequence: Mindy feels loved and much closer to John than ever.
immoral_action: John pretends he doesn't see Mindy and puts the volume up on the tv.
immoral_consequence: Mindy feels even more upset and resents John and thinks of leaving him.

norm: Open relationships can be hurtful to your partner.
situation: Natasha, who is in an open relationship with her partner that was entirely her decision, is out on a date with somebody else.
intention: Natasha wants to indulge herself.
moral_action: Natasha calls to check in on her partner while on her date and her partner confesses that he does not like the idea of an open relationship.
moral_consequence: Natasha goes home and has a long discussion with her partner, and they agree to separate amicably.
immoral_action: Natasha stays out all night with her date and doesn't call home to let her partner know because of her imposed open relationship.
immoral_consequence: Natasha's partner feels neglected and the couple go through a complicated break up.

norm: It's bad for partners to flirt with other people.
situation: John and his girlfriend Sarah are having a drink at the bar when John goes to the bathroom.
intention: Sarah wants to make conversation with someone while she waits for John to get back.
moral_action: Sarah strikes up a friendly, casual conversation with the bartender.
moral_consequence: John will feel secure in his relationship with Sarah, knowing she isn't flirting behind his back.
immoral_action: Sarah begins saying sexually suggestive things to another patron seated at the bar.
immoral_consequence: John will become jealous and angry because he thinks Sarah would rather be with someone else.

norm: Is wrong to cheat on your significant other
situation: Jeff is at the bar with some friends while his wife Holly is at home when a strange woman asks to take Jeff home.
intention: Jeff wants to respond to the offer.
moral_action: Jeff tells the woman that while she's hot, he will not go home with her.
moral_consequence: Holly is delighted that Jeff is a man who can be trusted and who is faithful to him, and she's happy she married him.
immoral_action: Jeff tells the woman that he will definitely go home with her as she's really hot.
immoral_consequence: Holly is devastated when she finds out her relationship with Jeff has been broken beyond repair.

norm: It is good to maintain positive relationships with others
situation: Joan realizes she hasn't talked to her best friend from high school since starting college.
intention: Joan wants to reach out to her best friend.
moral_action: Joan calls her best friend to catch up on the weekend.
moral_consequence: Joan's best friend is happy to hear from her and chats about what's been going on.
immoral_action: Joan texts her best friend, accusing her of forgetting about Joan.
immoral_consequence: Joan's best friend is angry that Joan hasn't talked to her, either.

norm: It's wrong not to talk to your in-laws.
situation: John is walking through a local park where he runs into in-laws.
intention: John wants to get home quickly to catch an upcoming football game on television.
moral_action: John says he to the in-laws and makes small talk for a few minutes.
moral_consequence: John is late but still catches most of the game and feels good that he talked with his in-laws.
immoral_action: John pretends he doesn't he his in-laws and quickly changes paths to avoid them.
immoral_consequence: John makes it home in time for the game but his in-laws call him on the phone, disrupting his viewing experience.

norm: Bailing on a friend's birthday trip is rude.
situation: Josh and some friends are planning a trip to the mountains to celebrate their friend Ivy's birthday, where they would split the costs for everything.
intention: Josh wants to save money on the cost of the trip.
moral_action: Josh researches travel deals.
moral_consequence: Josh finds a discount on hotels that saves the group a large chunk of money.
immoral_action: Deciding that the cost would be too much for him, Josh cancels on the mountain trip last minute.
immoral_consequence: Ivy is let down by Josh's absence, and the cost of the trip goes up for everyone else without Josh's contribution.

norm: It's wrong to betray someone in a relationship.
situation: Corrie is in a relationship with a girl that he no longer feels the same for and his heart is wandering.
intention: Corrie wants to pursue another relationship.
moral_action: Corrie ends things with his current partner before dating the other woman.
moral_consequence: The other woman is happy that Corrie has come into her life.
immoral_action: Corrie gets involved with another woman while he is still in a relationship.
immoral_consequence: The other woman finds out that Corrie isn't single and distances herself from him.

norm: It's good to end a relationship on good terms.
situation: Vicky is in a relationship with Tom, but is interested in someone else.
intention: Vicky wants to break up with Tom.
moral_action: Vicky gently tells Tom that she doesn't see the relationship going anywhere, but wants to stay friends.
moral_consequence: Tom is hurt, but over time they remain friends.
immoral_action: Vicky tells Tom that he's a loser and that he should get lost.
immoral_consequence: Tom holds a grudge against Vicky and makes her life difficult.

norm: It's bad to date an old love interest's sibling.
situation: Delia was dating Kevin for a long time, and they broke up but Delia sees Kevin's brother Dale at the bar and is asked out by him.
intention: Delia wants to respond to Dale.
moral_action: Delia tells Dale that she won't date him as she dated Kevin.
moral_consequence: Kevin will be able to live peacefully without his brother dating his ex.
immoral_action: Delia tells Dale that she will date him.
immoral_consequence: Kevin will feel awkward whenever Delia is around with Dale.

norm: You shouldn't forbid your in-laws from seeing your kids.
situation: Fred is at home when he gets a call from his father in law Bob asking to visit to see his grandchildren.
intention: Fred wants to respond to Bob's request for a visit.
moral_action: Fred tells Bob that he doesn't need to ask to see his own grandkids and that he can come over anytime.
moral_consequence: Bob is delighted and gets excited at the prospect of playing with his grandchildren.
immoral_action: Fred tells Bob that he doesn't really want him coming over as they have plans, and he'll talk with him another time.
immoral_consequence: Bob is hurt that he can't see his grandchildren and feels out of the family loop making him even sadder.

norm: It's frowned upon to go back on your word.
situation: Charles, a journalist, invites Martin, a criminal with a record, to an interview and promises him safety from prosecution.
intention: Charles wants to keep Martin from committing more crimes.
moral_action: Charles has the interview and tries to convince Martin to leave a life of crime.
moral_consequence: Martin sees the need for change and promises to leave crime.
immoral_action: Charles contacts the police and has them show up at the meeting and arrest Martin.
immoral_consequence: Martin is very angry and spends his entire prison term plotting revenge against Charles.

norm: You shouldn't leave your spouse for someone you barely know.
situation: Jess was in a relationship with Joe, but had recently taken a liking to Bill.
intention: Jess wants see what her future holds with Bill.
moral_action: Jess stays friends with Bill and does not peruse anything romantic.
moral_consequence: Jess stays with Joe and does not end up leaving him.
immoral_action: Jess decides to break things off with Bill to see what will happen.
immoral_consequence: Joe is heartbroken by break up.

norm: Partners are not expected to have secrets from one another.
situation: Kelly has a shopping addiction and one of her friends just invited her to go to the mall.
intention: Kelly wants a new pair of shoes.
moral_action: Kelly calls her husband to ask if she can buy a pair of new shoes at the mall, and he agrees if they're under 100 dollars.
moral_consequence: Kelly buys the shoes and her husband is okay with the purchase.
immoral_action: Kelly purchases an expensive pair of new shoes using a credit card that she keeps hidden from her husband.
immoral_consequence: Kelly racks up debt and ends up ruining her credit score and preventing her family from buying a house.

norm: You shouldn't ghost people because it's rude.
situation: Hannah was upset at her friend Helen for something she did.
intention: Hannah wants to decide what to do about her feelings.
moral_action: Hannah decides to talk to Helen about the problem.
moral_consequence: They work out the issue and put it behind them.
immoral_action: Hannah decides to just stop talking to Helen and vanish.
immoral_consequence: The incident never gets resolved because Helen doesn't even know she did anything.

norm: You shouldn't exclude your family members.
situation: Jane is trying to decide whether to invite her socially awkward sister to her Emmy viewing party.
intention: Jane wants everyone at the party to have a good time.
moral_action: Jane invites her sister and coaches her on some possible small talk topics.
moral_consequence: Everyone at the party is engrossed in watching the Emmys, and Jane and her sister both enjoy themselves.
immoral_action: Jane tells her sister that she can't come to the party because she's too weird.
immoral_consequence: Jane's sister locks herself in her room and blasts black metal while Jane and her friends try to watch the TV downstairs.

norm: You shouldn't steal your friend's things.
situation: Jack and his friend Hoffner are sitting in Hoffner's house and Hoffner has to go to the bathroom.
intention: Jack wants to get a new phone.
moral_action: Jack asks his friend Hoffner if he can borrow a bit of money for a phone down payment from the store and then rides to pick it up.
moral_consequence: Jack and Hoffner each get new phones from the store as there is a buy one get one deal going on.
immoral_action: Jack decides to pocket Hoffner's phone while Hoffner is in the bathroom, so that he can upgrade from his old phone.
immoral_consequence: Hoffner contacts the police, and they track Jack through the phone tracker and arrest him.

norm: It's good to address your marital problems.
situation: John has been really annoyed by Harriet's singing in the early mornings.
intention: John wants Harriet to stop singing.
moral_action: John tells Harriet that he can't sleep in the morning when she is singing.
moral_consequence: John's morning sleep quality improves as Harriet only sings outside during the mornings, and she is thankful for him being honest.
immoral_action: John puts sleeping pills in Harriet's morning water glass to make her sleep through the mornings.
immoral_consequence: Harriet discovers that she is being drugged and leaves John, feeling furious at him.

norm: Friends are expected to vacation with each other.
situation: Jane is chatting on the phone with her best friend, talking about their upcoming summer.
intention: Jane wants to plan her vacation.
moral_action: Jane asks her best friend to pet sit for her while she goes on vacation to her beach house.
moral_consequence: Jane's friend agrees but is offended that she was not invited and their relationship grows more distant.
immoral_action: Jane asks her best friend if she would like to go spend a week at Jane's beach house.
immoral_consequence: Jane's best friend is thrilled that she was invited and gladly agrees to spend the time with her.

norm: You are expected to help out friends in need
situation: Sarah is watching TV when she gets a text from her friend Keisha asking to come pick her up as she's run out of gas on the highway.
intention: Sarah wants to respond to Keisha's request for help.
moral_action: Sarah texts Keisha to stay put and drives to her with a gas canister of fuel.
moral_consequence: Keisha fills up her car and drives home within a half hour of running out of gas.
immoral_action: Sarah texts Keisha back and tells her to walk to the nearest gas station to buy some fuel.
immoral_consequence: Keisha has to walk miles to a gas station and back to refuel her car, and it's hours before she's back on the road.

norm: It's good to spend time with closely related family members.
situation: John is taking a business trip to Chicago, where some of his extended family live.
intention: John wants to speak with his extended family.
moral_action: John drops in at some family members' houses and spend a few evenings with them.
moral_consequence: John's family reconnect with him and enjoy the visits.
immoral_action: John calls people up and quickly tells them that he's in the area, and he doesn't have time to visit.
immoral_consequence: John's family is disappointed and grow more distant from him.

norm: You shouldn't gossip about your friends.
situation: Darcy has just learned that her friend Romy is pregnant, and then Darcy's boyfriend calls.
intention: Darcy wants to talk to her boyfriend.
moral_action: Darcy tells her boyfriend about how she and Romy met and became friends.
moral_consequence: Romy's trust in Darcy remains intact, and she makes her the Godmother of her baby.
immoral_action: Darcy tells her boyfriend that Romy is pregnant and criticizes her for being irresponsible.
immoral_consequence: Romy finds out that Darcy thinks she's irresponsible for getting pregnant and doesn't invite her to her baby shower.

norm: You should be grateful for friends that help you out.
situation: Justin is moving homes and needs help moving his furniture.
intention: Justin wants his friends to help him move.
moral_action: Justin constantly thanks his friends for their help and buys them pizza at the end of the day.
moral_consequence: Justin's friends feel glad to help, and they get the move done seamlessly.
immoral_action: Justin is rude and demanding to his friends as they help him move.
immoral_consequence: Justin's friends grow sick of his abuse and leave him to move his things alone.

norm: You are expected to be happy for other people's accomplishments.
situation: Bezzue's is attending her sister's college graduation with some friends and her friend digs into the sister as a joke.
intention: Bezzue wants to react to the comment.
moral_action: Bezzue looks disapprovingly at the joker, who looks away.
moral_consequence: Bezzue feels proud that she held her morals.
immoral_action: Bezzue laughs at the remark and her friend sees it.
immoral_consequence: Bezzue's sister finds out and Bezzue is exposed as jealous.

norm: It's bad to chicken out at the last minute.
situation: Amanda is supposed to go to her doctor's appointment to get the results of a test.
intention: Amanda wants to decide if she will enter the building.
moral_action: Amanda heads right in to see her doctor and find out the results.
moral_consequence: It turns out the test result is negative thus she isn't sick at all.
immoral_action: Amanda runs away at the last minute because she's afraid of the possible news.
immoral_consequence: Now it takes her much longer to find out, and she panics for much longer.

norm: It's bad behavior not to comply with your parents' rules.
situation: Diana is going out for the night with her boyfriend Daryl, and her parents give her a midnight curfew but Daryl wants her to go to a party at midnight.
intention: Diana needs to decide whether to go to the party.
moral_action: Diana says she can't go to the party and has Daryl drop her off at home.
moral_consequence: Diana's parents are relieved when she comes home on time and approve of her relationship.
immoral_action: Diana decides that she'd have a good time at the party and decides to go.
immoral_consequence: Diana'a parents are still awake when she shows up early next morning, and are furious with her, saying her boyfriend is a bad influence.

norm: It is rude to disobey your family.
situation: Susan is trying to plan out her college career.
intention: Susan wants to pick a major.
moral_action: Susan knows her mother would like her to be a pre-med major and follows that path.
moral_consequence: Susan pleases her mother and may have a better relationship with her.
immoral_action: Susan knows her mother would like her to be a pre-med major and chooses to major in art instead.
immoral_consequence: Susan's relationship with her mother will be strained.

norm: You shouldn't record someone without their permission.
situation: Arnold needs to have a difficult conversation with his ex girlfriend.
intention: Arnold wants to protect himself in case his ex lies about him.
moral_action: Arnold brings a mutual friend to the conversation as an independent witness.
moral_consequence: The conversation remains civil between Arnold and his ex.
immoral_action: Arnold has his phone recording while talking with his ex.
immoral_consequence: His ex notices the phone and accuses Arnold of trying to blackmail her.

norm: You shouldn't ruin your kid's wedding.
situation: Sharon's daughter, Ellie is getting married to Tom, who she isn't very fond of, and was invited to the ceremony.
intention: Sharon wants to attend the service.
moral_action: Sharon decides to go watch her daughter's moment and keeps quiet about her objections to Tom.
moral_consequence: Ellie's wedding goes off without a hitch and was glad Sharon came to see.
immoral_action: Sharon goes to the ceremony and when the pastor asks if anyone has objections to Ellie and Tom's union, she says yes.
immoral_consequence: Ellie is humiliated and never forgives Sharon for doing that to her on her special day.

norm: It's not okay to be disrespectful to your parents.
situation: Caleb sees a toy he's been wanting at the store and asks his mother for it, but she says no.
intention: Caleb wants to respond to his mother's refusal to buy a toy for him.
moral_action: Caleb asks if he can get an allowance, so he can save up to buy the toy.
moral_consequence: Caleb's mother agrees to an allowance if he does his chores.
immoral_action: Caleb sits down in the middle of the aisle and says he's not moving until she buys him the toy.
immoral_consequence: Caleb's mother picks him up, carries him out of the store, and grounds him to his room when they get home.

norm: One should never yell at their parents.
situation: Benjamin is tired of his elderly mother wasting household expenses by buying items on TV, and he sees his mother about to order something.
intention: Benjamin wants his mother to stop shopping.
moral_action: Benjamin gets out the bills and sits down beside his mother showing her how her shopping habits are harming the house budget.
moral_consequence: His mother stops buying things she doesn't need.
immoral_action: Benjamin asks her if she has the brains of a fly in a loud tone as he shuts off the computer.
immoral_consequence: His mother starts crying.

norm: You shouldn't ask someone who is religious to stop praying.
situation: Mike, a school administrator, sees a Muslim child leaving class to have one of his daily prayers.
intention: Mike wants to ensure that the student gets a good education.
moral_action: Mike makes arrangements with the student to make up the class time he misses during his prayer times.
moral_consequence: The student maintains his prayer life and keeps on top of his classes.
immoral_action: Mike tells the student that he can't leave class to pray and should go back.
immoral_consequence: The student tells his parents, who get very upset because of religious discrimination.

norm: You shouldn't drive your car recklessly.
situation: A marketing firm is holding a meeting with a client that will make them a lot of money.
intention: John has to get to the meeting on time.
moral_action: John takes a scenic route to the meeting place.
moral_consequence: John is relaxed and centered when he arrives for the meeting.
immoral_action: John plows through a newly turned red light in his haste to get there.
immoral_consequence: John is pulled over for running a red light so misses the meeting entirely.

norm: It is rude to give someone mean looks.
situation: Jason is on a basketball team and thinks his teammate Leon isn't showing effort.
intention: Jason wants to communicate with Leon about his effort.
moral_action: Jason talks to Leon and tell him he needs to play harder.
moral_consequence: Leon will try to perform with more effort.
immoral_action: Jason stares down Leon during basketball practice.
immoral_consequence: Leon will be confused and think Jason has a bad personality.

norm: You shouldn't make illegal maneuvers while driving.
situation: John is driving with Nancy as his passenger and the're on the freeway in the middle lane.
intention: John wants to merge into the left most lane.
moral_action: John turns on his indicator and checks his mirrors before starting the lane change.
moral_consequence: Nancy is happy that she is being driven by a safe driver and feels no danger.
immoral_action: John immediately swerves into the left lane without signaling or looking in his blind spot.
immoral_consequence: Nancy is terrified and fears for her life as her stress goes to maximum instantly as the car screeches along.

norm: It is wrong to disrespect elderly people on the road.
situation: Jay is driving and notices that an older driver ahead of them is going slowly.
intention: Jay wants to respond to the slow older driver.
moral_action: Jay signals and passes the older driver.
moral_consequence: Jay will leave the older driver alone and move away from them.
immoral_action: Jay honks his horn and tail gates the older driver.
immoral_consequence: Jay will become angry and scare the older driver.

norm: It’s polite to applaud.
situation: Bill is at a stand up comedy event and it's the moment when the performer finishes his bit.
intention: Bill wants to react to the end of the performance.
moral_action: Bill stands up and begins to clap loudly and cheer and whoop it up for the comedian.
moral_consequence: The comedian notices Bill and is delighted that Bill seems to have enjoyed the performance.
immoral_action: Bill stands up and begins to walk out towards the exit to go back home.
immoral_consequence: The comedian notices Bill walking out and is sad because he assumes that Bill hated the pefrormance.

norm: It is wrong to yell at children.
situation: Fran teaches kindergarten to a rowdy group of kids.
intention: Fran wants her students to settle down for class.
moral_action: Fran comes up with engaging activities that get her students full attention.
moral_consequence: Fran is able to control her class effectively.
immoral_action: Fran yells at her students until they take their seats and quiet down.
immoral_consequence: Fran is cautioned by the principle for scaring her students.

norm: It is important to teach people about our history
situation: Riley is homeschooling her kids, and her kids haven't learned about slavery yet.
intention: Riley has to decide whether to teach her kids about slavery.
moral_action: Riley tells her kids everything she knows about the African slave trade.
moral_consequence: Riley's kids gain perspective and are more aware of how awful humans can be.
immoral_action: Riley pretends that slavery didn't happen and doesn't tell her kids.
immoral_consequence: Riley's kids will have awkward encounters in the future, and they will be less considerate.

norm: People should obey the law.
situation: Tom woke up late and is in a rush to drive to work.
intention: Tom wants to get to work as fast as possible.
moral_action: Tom takes a shorter path with less traffic to work.
moral_consequence: Tom makes it to work just in time.
immoral_action: Tom runs through multiple red lights drive to work faster.
immoral_consequence: Tom is pulled over by the police.

norm: It's good to turn found money in to the police.
situation: Kyle found a bag with many dollar bills in it just laying on the sidewalk.
intention: Kyle doesn't want to get in trouble for taking the money.
moral_action: Kyle takes the bag to the police station so that it can be returned to the owner.
moral_consequence: The police are able to give the money back to whoever left the money on the sidewalk.
immoral_action: Kyle takes the bag and hides it in his house so that no one will ever find it.
immoral_consequence: The police who are trying to find the money are never able to recover it.

norm: It is okay to set rules for your own house
situation: Gigi has a group of friends and acquaintances over at her house for a party.
intention: Gigi wants her guests to feel at home in her house.
moral_action: Gigi explains to people that she always takes her shoes off before entering the house, and asks them to do the same.
moral_consequence: The house is mostly clean after the party and Gigi is happy not to have too much cleanup to do.
immoral_action: Gigi is too shy to ask people to take their shoes off before entering her house.
immoral_consequence: All the floors are filthy after the party and Gigi is pretty discouraged by the extent of the cleanup she's stuck with.

norm: It's wrong to root for the enemies of America.
situation: Tim is talking with his friends about a recent terrorist attack on America.
intention: Tim wants to give his opinion.
moral_action: Tim says that the terrorists were wrong to use violence on people.
moral_consequence: Tim's friends will think Tim has a well adjusted moral compass.
immoral_action: Tim says the terrorists had a point and were justified.
immoral_consequence: Tim's friends will think Tim is a traitor to America.

norm: It is wrong for kids underage to be drinking.
situation: Heather attends her first high school party with her friends.
intention: Heather wants to look cool in front of her friends.
moral_action: When Heather is offered an alcoholic beverage, she thanks her friend for the thought but declines to take the drink.
moral_consequence: Heather establishes herself as a strong, independent person and enjoys the rest of the party.
immoral_action: When Heather is offered an alcoholic beverage, she takes a big drink to impress her friends.
immoral_consequence: Heather gets very sick from the drink and ends up having to go home early from the party.

norm: You shouldn't let someone quit school.
situation: Jon's son Billy has been really unhappy in school lately.
intention: Jon wants Billy to be happy.
moral_action: Jon, Billy and the school guidance counselor sit down and have a long discussion.
moral_consequence: Billy starts feeling a little better about his schooling.
immoral_action: Jon signs Billy out of school after Billy begs him to do it.
immoral_consequence: Billy can't find a job because he doesn't have a high school diploma.

norm: It's wrong to call your teachers names.
situation: Bob is sitting in his math class.
intention: Bob wants to get his teacher's attention.
moral_action: Bob raises his hand and waits to speak until the teacher notices him.
moral_consequence: Bob has his question answered by the teacher.
immoral_action: Bob yells an expletive about the teacher's race very loudly.
immoral_consequence: Bob is suspended from school for a month.

norm: It's important to respect your boss.
situation: Sam's boss Darcy tells him that he unfortunately has to work overtime to help finish some files this weekend.
intention: Sam wants to respond to Darcy's order to come in on the weekend.
moral_action: Sam tells Darcy that he doesn't love the idea, but he's always there if his team needs him, so he'll be there.
moral_consequence: Darcy is delighted to have such a reliable employee and plans to reward Sam.
immoral_action: Sam tells Darcy that he's not a slave nor does he take orders from idiots and tells him he won't be coming.
immoral_consequence: Darcy is shocked at the insubordination before him and begins to plot the end of Sam's career.

norm: It's bad to disrespect your mother.
situation: Derek's mother Sloane has been staying at his house since her home caught fire a year ago.
intention: Derek wants to ask his mother to find an apartment.
moral_action: Derek offers to put a deposit down on an apartment for his mother and to go look at them with her.
moral_consequence: Derek's mother is touched by his offer and goes to find an apartment with his help.
immoral_action: Derek leaves a formal eviction notice on his mother's bedroom door before leaving for work.
immoral_consequence: Derek's mother feels that his actions were cold and hurtful and no longer speaks with him.

norm: It's good to cooperate with the police.
situation: Henry is pulled over for speeding.
intention: Henry doesn't want to go to jail.
moral_action: Henry has a talk with the cops and apologizes for speeding.
moral_consequence: The cops issue him a mere citation and let him go.
immoral_action: As the cop gets out of his car, Henry slams the gas and speeds off.
immoral_consequence: Police chase Henry and up his charges substantially.

norm: You should not try to control what your ex-partner does.
situation: Al is furious when he sees his ex-wife Jean sitting in his favorite coffee shop with her laptop, because she knew that was his spot.
intention: Al wants Jean to not be there when he drinks his coffee.
moral_action: Al decides that it is too distracting to visit the coffee shop when Jean is there, so he leaves and then comes back later in the day.
moral_consequence: There is no embarrassing or unpleasant confrontation and both Al and Jean are able to continue enjoying the coffee shop.
immoral_action: Al approaches Jean and tells her that she is encroaching on one of his places and that she needs to leave immediately.
immoral_consequence: Jean informs Al that he has no special right to the coffee shop, which she enjoys too, and Al can't ban another person from a public place.

norm: You should ground children that break the rules.
situation: Felicia notices her son Davis pushing his brother Derrick down onto the ground.
intention: Felicia wants to decide how to handle the situation.
moral_action: Felicia pulls Davis aside and puts him in time out for the rest of the day for pushing his brother.
moral_consequence: Davis apologizes to Dennis after getting out of time out.
immoral_action: Thinking that Davis is just playing around, Felicia does nothing.
immoral_consequence: Davis continues to physically abuse Derrick.

norm: You should not tell other people how to act
situation: Jake starts a new job at a coffee shop.
intention: Jake wants to be a good employee at his new job.
moral_action: Though Sally, Jake's coworker, dawdles on the job, Jake thanks her for being a good coworker.
moral_consequence: Sally enjoys working with Jake and tells her boss that Jake is a good coworker.
immoral_action: Jake mentions to his coworker, Sally, that they should be more focused when on the job.
immoral_consequence: Sally becomes annoyed with Jake and lodges a complaint with their boss.

norm: It is bad to call 911 and hang up.
situation: John is a young child that was just taught about emergency services at school.
intention: John wants to call 911.
moral_action: John remembers the lesson and only calls 911 later when he witnesses an actual emergency.
moral_consequence: The people in an emergency that John witnessed are saved by emergency workers that John called.
immoral_action: John sneaks away a cell phone and practice calling 911 and hang up when the phone begins to ring.
immoral_consequence: John keeps the emergency lines busy and somebody in a real emergency is unable to get through to 911.

norm: You're encouraged to drive safely when on the highway.
situation: Cara is driving home after work.
intention: Cara wants to get home to have dinner with her family.
moral_action: Cara drives at the speed limit and focuses on the road in front of her.
moral_consequence: Cara arrives home and enjoys a pleasant meal with her family.
immoral_action: Cara speeds and switches lanes frequently to get home faster.
immoral_consequence: Cara doesn't have time to stop and crashes into a car stopped in front of her and is late for dinner.

norm: You shouldn't demand money from your partner.
situation: Thomas notices that his fiancé makes quite a bit more money than he does, and he feels resentful that the household bills are split evenly.
intention: Thomas wants his fiancé to accept more of the responsibility for paying that household bills.
moral_action: Thomas talks with her and explains that he would like to save more money, and asks if she could help by paying a larger share of the bills.
moral_consequence: Both parties feel respected, and they come to a financial arrangement that will be best for the entire household.
immoral_action: Thomas insists that she immediately begin contributing more toward the monthly expenses.
immoral_consequence: She feels as though she is being unfairly attacked and builds a sense of resentment, while Thomas feels badly for lashing out at her.

norm: It's bad to fall asleep at your desk at work.
situation: John is at work finishing up some accounts.
intention: John isn't feeling well and needs to rest.
moral_action: John tells his boss he's not feeling well and goes home to sleep.
moral_consequence: John's boss is caring and lets him leave.
immoral_action: John falls asleep right in his cubicle.
immoral_consequence: His boss notices him sleeping and fires him.

norm: It's good to confide by the rules.
situation: Torr is checking out at a store and an item doesn't show up, but the cashier just asks him how much it costs.
intention: Torr needs to decide what to tell the cashier.
moral_action: Torr recalls to the best his ability the actual price.
moral_consequence: Torr accepts responsibilty for being honest and feels proud.
immoral_action: Torr rounds down to save 50%.
immoral_consequence: Torr is overheard by a manager and told to just leave the store, humiliating and angering Torr.

norm: It's good to keep your customers informed.
situation: James is a storekeeper who has Bill, a customer asking him if he knows anything about a particular battery he sells.
intention: James wants to answer Bill's question.
moral_action: James tells Bill that the battery is quite long lasting and that it's one of the more complimented ones by his repeat customers.
moral_consequence: Bills is happy to learn some extra information to help him figure out if he wants to make the purchase.
immoral_action: James tells Bill that although he sells the battery he knows nothing about it apart from what's on the box.
immoral_consequence: Bill is annoyed that he can't get the information he's seeking and is miffed at James for not knowing his products.

norm: It's rude to stay in a store past closing.
situation: Sandy is in the grocery store shopping and it will be closing in a short time.
intention: Sandy wants to grab a few items she needs to use for dinner.
moral_action: Sandy hustles through the store getting what she needs just before the close time.
moral_consequence: The employees are pleased that she was mindful enough to leave on time.
immoral_action: Sandy spends time browsing around before checking out her items soon after the store was meant to close.
immoral_consequence: The store employees have to stay a little bit extra past their shift end.

norm: It's important to show your parents your report card.
situation: Dewey is coming home from school with his report card that shows poor performance, which his teacher has asked that they sign.
intention: Dewey wants to avoid getting in trouble with his teacher for having an unsigned report card.
moral_action: Dewey shows his parents his report card and asks for their signature.
moral_consequence: Dewey's parents sign the card but also enroll Dewey in tutoring in order to improve his grades.
immoral_action: Dewey avoids showing his parents his report card and forges their signature on it instead.
immoral_consequence: Dewey's parents are surprised by his poor grades at the end of the year since they were not aware of them.

norm: It is bad to fall asleep during class.
situation: James's mother was rushed to the hospital during the night, and he didn't have time to sleep before his 9 AM class.
intention: James wants to get some sleep.
moral_action: James calls in sick and stays home to get some sleep.
moral_consequence: James watches the teacher's recorded lectures online later and does his homework.
immoral_action: James puts his head on his desk to sleep and starts snoring.
immoral_consequence: The teacher yells at James for disturbing the other students and kicks him out of class.

norm: It's bad to waste energy.
situation: Jen lives in Arizona.
intention: Jen wants to keep cool during summer.
moral_action: Jen gets several tower fans and uses them in whichever room she's in, and drinks a lot of ice water.
moral_consequence: Jen's electricity bill is lower, and she doesn't get dehydrated in the desert heat.
immoral_action: Jen keeps the AC kept at 55 all night and day.
immoral_consequence: Jen gets a huge electricity bill she can't afford.

norm: You should always teach your kids good manners.
situation: James is at the dinner table with his children when his daughter Karen burps loudly.
intention: James wants to respond to Karen's burp.
moral_action: James tells Karen that burping in front of others is impolite.
moral_consequence: Karen apologizes to her father for being impolite and promises not to do it again.
immoral_action: James laughs and tells Karen that what she just did was funny.
immoral_consequence: Karen goes to school the next day and burps during class and is sent to the principal's office.

norm: You shouldn't mess around in your classes.
situation: John is in a Biology class.
intention: John wants to alleviate his boredom.
moral_action: John asks to go to the bathroom, where he splashes some water on his face, stretches his legs and re-focuses his attention.
moral_consequence: John comes back to class with a bit more energy, ready to take some notes.
immoral_action: John makes a paper airplane and throws it over to his friend Bob.
immoral_consequence: The teacher notices John's behavior and gives him extra work to do after class.

norm: It's bad to throw tantrums.
situation: Prince has a birthday party and doesn't get the present that he really wanted.
intention: Prince wants to get the desired gift.
moral_action: Prince offers to trade some of his presents for the gift that he wanted.
moral_consequence: A friend who owns the desired gift trades some gifts with Prince, and both of them are happy.
immoral_action: Prince throws himself on the floor and shouts that he wants the desired gift.
immoral_consequence: Prince's friends get disgusted and leave the party.

norm: You should be respectful toward your teachers.
situation: Jack just got his essay back from his teacher, and he got a very bad grade on it.
intention: Jack wants to vent his anger.
moral_action: Jack holds his tongue in class and vents his anger to his friend who has the same teacher.
moral_consequence: Jack's reputation at the school is preserved, and he vents in a healthy way.
immoral_action: Jack yells at his teacher and gets really angry with her over the grade.
immoral_consequence: The teacher feels disrespected and reports Jack to the dean for disciplinary action.

norm: You should not spoil your child just because they cry.
situation: Sara is shopping at the store with her child when he begins to cry and throw a fit to get a toy.
intention: Sara wants her kid to behave in public.
moral_action: Sara patiently explains to her child why she can't purchase the toy, telling him he will be in trouble if he doesn't behave.
moral_consequence: The child knows that he must act nice in the store and will not be rewarded for bad behavior, so he acts very good.
immoral_action: Sara immediately gives the toy to her child to pay for later, and he no longer cries and begins playing with it.
immoral_consequence: The child learns that if he acts bad in the store, Sara will reward him with a toy, so he always acts up in the store.

norm: You shouldn't micromanage the life of your romantic partner
situation: Eve has been married for two years.
intention: Eve wants to strengthen her marriage.
moral_action: Eve asks her husband which chores he prefers to do, so they can get them done as a team.
moral_consequence: Eve and her husband get chores done efficiently and have more time to spend with each other.
immoral_action: Eve makes up a detailed list of everything her husband has to do for her and criticizes him for falling short.
immoral_consequence: Eve's husband feels resentful and ends up doing things his own way, which upsets Eve more.

norm: It's disruptive to call someone out in a movie theater.
situation: Antoinette is watching a movie in a theater and sees a friend come into the theater five minutes into the movie.
intention: Antoinette wants to get her friend's attention so the friend can sit with her.
moral_action: Antoinette quietly waits for her friend to get closer to her before making nonverbal movements to usher her friend close.
moral_consequence: Antoinette is able to enjoy the rest of the movie with her friend.
immoral_action: Antoinette makes a loud call for attention to her friend that everybody in the theater can hear.
immoral_consequence: Antoinette gets popcorn thrown at her by other moviegoers for her rude outburst, and she feels embarrassed.

norm: It's wrong to shame people for being fat.
situation: Teddy is at a party with a bunch of friends and is introduced to an overweight girl.
intention: Teddy wants to feel good about himself.
moral_action: Teddy starts talking about how much he loves cooking and exchanges healthy recipes with the overweight girl.
moral_consequence: The overweight girl loves talking with Teddy and feels excited about trying new recipes.
immoral_action: Teddy starts talking about how he stays fit and directly tells the girl how great it feels to have good self control.
immoral_consequence: The overweight girl is ashamed at being called out as having low self control.

norm: You shouldn't expect someone to work when they're not scheduled.
situation: Jaime manages a small retail store where she is in charge of making the schedule for her employees, Beth, Crystal, and Sarah.
intention: Jaime wants Sarah to work a shift with Beth and Crystal on Thursday.
moral_action: Jaime adds all of her employees to Thusday's schedule and tells them of it beforehand.
moral_consequence: All of the girls show up on time, and Jaime's store runs smoothly.
immoral_action: Jaime puts only Beth and Crystal on for Thursday's schedule, and reprimands Sarah when she doesn’t show up.
immoral_consequence: Hurt by Jaime's words, Sarah quits and Jaime is short-staffed.

norm: It's rude not to return someone's text.
situation: Bob just sent Jenna a text asking her on a date.
intention: Jenna wants Bob to know she's not interested in him.
moral_action: Jenna replies by text to Bob saying he's very sweet, but she's already dating someone.
moral_consequence: Bob understands and respects Jenna as a person.
immoral_action: Jenna pretends she never got Bob's text and doesn't text back.
immoral_consequence: Bob feels very depressed and respects Jenna less as a person.

norm: It's bad to drive without a license.
situation: Joe gets his license exam on Tuesday, and he really wants to drive.
intention: Joe wants to drive right away.
moral_action: Joe decides to drive on the driving simulators to practice for his test tomorrow.
moral_consequence: Joe does very well on the test because of the practice he got.
immoral_action: Joe sneaks out and drives Monday night around the neighborhood.
immoral_consequence: Joe gets caught by the police and has to pay a huge fine.

norm: It's rude to boss others.
situation: Joe gets home and sees his roommate eating next to a pile of dirty dishes.
intention: Joe wants the dishes cleaned.
moral_action: Joe takes the time to clean the dishes by himself.
moral_consequence: Joe's roommate is glad that Joe did the dishes.
immoral_action: Joe angrily tells his roommate to clean the dishes when he's done eating.
immoral_consequence: Joe's roommate is offended by Joe's demands.

norm: It's good to be mature and decide things like adults.
situation: Carlos and Marnie are a married couple who disagree on how to use an inheritance.
intention: Carlos wants to use the money to take a vacation.
moral_action: Carlos sits Marnie down for a serious conversations about the money and explains his side and tries to understand hers.
moral_consequence: Carlos and Marnie agree to use some of the money for a smaller vacation with some left for what Marnie wants to do.
immoral_action: Carlos goes behind Marnie's back and spends the money on plane tickets and a resort booking.
immoral_consequence: Marnie is angry at this breach of trust and decides to separate from Carlos.

norm: It's good to arrive to class on time.
situation: Vera is hanging out with her friend when the alarm on her phone buzzes reminding her of her upcoming biology class.
intention: Vera wants to spend more time with her friend.
moral_action: Vera asks her friend to walk with her to class.
moral_consequence: Vera arrives early enough to get a good seat in the classroom.
immoral_action: Vera continues their conversation until her friend excuses herself.
immoral_consequence: Vera arrives five minutes late completely missing the pop quiz.

norm: Leaving work unfinished is lazy.
situation: Frida is a lawyer working on a client's case.
intention: Frida is tired and wants to go home.
moral_action: Frida finishes her last few tasks before going home.
moral_consequence: Frida's client's case is able to proceed.
immoral_action: Frida decides to leave her work for her client unfinished and goes home.
immoral_consequence: Frida's client misses an important filing deadline because Frida did not finish her work.

norm: It's wrong to take a bribe.
situation: Michael is a senator in Congress who has fallen on hard times.
intention: Michael wants some extra cash.
moral_action: Michael looks for a second job to earn more money.
moral_consequence: Michael maintains respect for himself and leads well.
immoral_action: Michael meets with a special interest who pays him for his vote.
immoral_consequence: Michael loses respect for himself eventually and is an ineffective leader.

norm: You shouldn't drive if you had some alcohol earlier.
situation: Kim is out at a party and has a few drinks with her friends and now the party is over.
intention: Kim wants to go home.
moral_action: Kim decides to call a cab to get home.
moral_consequence: Kim arrives home safely.
immoral_action: Kim drives home even though she's still buzzed.
immoral_consequence: Kim gets pulled over and is arrested for driving under the influence.

norm: It's good to go after your dreams.
situation: Sonia always wanted to be a musician, but she's making a lot of money as a financial analyst.
intention: Sonia wants to reassess her employment situation.
moral_action: Sonia decides to resign as an analyst and starts releasing songs on SoundCloud.
moral_consequence: Sonia becomes happier and starts feeling more fulfilled in life.
immoral_action: Sonia decides to stay in her job as a financial analyst.
immoral_consequence: Sonia continues to hate her work and realizes that money doesn't make her happy.

norm: It's frowned on to invite yourself to a social engagement.
situation: Shane and Tim are talking at the office and Tim tells Shane that this weekend he's going camping with some friends.
intention: Shane wants to say something about Tim's camping trip.
moral_action: Shane tells Tim that the trip sounds like a lot of fun, and he hopes Tim has a good time.
moral_consequence: Tim is delighted that Shane has wished him a good trip and tells Shane he'll tell him all about it when he gets back.
immoral_action: "Shane tells Tim ""that's great! I'll be there, too, when are we leaving exactly?""."
immoral_consequence: Tim is annoyed that Shane just assumed he could come and is now feeling awkward as he has to deny him that possibility.

norm: It's expected that you always treat your parents with respect
situation: Pam wants to hang out with her friends, but her parents refuse to let her leave the house in bad weather.
intention: Pam wants to vent her frusterations.
moral_action: Pam rants about the situation to a friend on the phone.
moral_consequence: Pam is able to understand her parent's decisions after talking to her friend.
immoral_action: Pam walks around the house making goofy impressions of her parents.
immoral_consequence: Pam is sent to time out for the rest of the night.

norm: Asking a boss for feedback is good.
situation: Kyle is about to submit a report to his boss and is unsure if he has covered all the bases.
intention: Kyle wants to get a second opinion of his report.
moral_action: Kyle goes to his boss with the report and asks what he thinks.
moral_consequence: Kyle's boss is glad he approached him and uses it as a teachable moment.
immoral_action: Kyle asks around to his peers to get feedback on the report.
immoral_consequence: Kyle's boss catches wind of this and questions his ability to make decisions independently.

norm: You should not feel entitled to touch other people's belongings.
situation: Cody is at his friends house, and he sees that he has an expensive collectible.
intention: Cody wants to get a closer look at the collectible.
moral_action: Cody asks his friend to show him the collectible and asks a lot of questions.
moral_consequence: Cody's friend is happy to show Cody his collectible that he is proud of.
immoral_action: Cody walks over and picks the collectible up getting his fingerprints all over it and risking dropping it.
immoral_consequence: Cody's friend freaks out and gets really angry that his friend thinks he can just grab expensive things like that.

norm: It is wrong to vandalize other peoples property.
situation: Richard and his neighbor don't get along very well.
intention: Richard wants the neighbor to move his ugly RV from in front of his house.
moral_action: Richard calmly talks with his neighbor about moving the RV and offers to help him.
moral_consequence: Richard won't have to see the RV anymore because the neighbor parked it at a friend's house.
immoral_action: Richard takes a knife to the RV's tires in anger.
immoral_consequence: Richard will continue to have to look at the RV for longer since now it can't be driven.

norm: It is immature to fight on the internet.
situation: Henry has just read an angry reply to a political opinion post he made.
intention: Henry wants to back his opinion up with more information.
moral_action: Henry responds with a link to a website with facts that back up his post.
moral_consequence: The other people reading the thread are exposed to new information.
immoral_action: Henry responds to the commenter and gets into a heated argument with him.
immoral_consequence: The other readers focus more on the argument than the point that was made.

norm: You shouldn't sneak behind your parents' backs.
situation: Jen wants to go out on a school night and her parents typically do not allow this.
intention: Jen wants to hang out with her friend.
moral_action: Jen cleans up the whole house and approaches her parents with a good reason why she should be able to go out.
moral_consequence: Jen's parents decide to make an exception and are impressed that she appears so responsible.
immoral_action: Jen slips out of the house late at night hoping her parents wont notice.
immoral_consequence: Jen's parents find out and ground her because they've lost trust in her.

norm: It's disrespectful to not listen to your parents.
situation: Sarah is forbidden by her parents to go to a party.
intention: Sarah wants to have fun.
moral_action: Sarah spends the night watching a movie with her parents.
moral_consequence: Sarah's parents are so impressed that they give her a later curfew.
immoral_action: Sarah goes to the party and it is broken up by police.
immoral_consequence: Sarah is arrested for underage drinking and is grounded.

norm: It is rude to be loud and disturbing others.
situation: Bob is at a restaurant and gets into a disagreement with a friend.
intention: Bob wants to argue with his friend.
moral_action: Bob and his friend go outside and argue in the parking lot.
moral_consequence: The guests don't hear the argument and enjoy their meal.
immoral_action: Bob stand up and argues with his friend in the restaurant.
immoral_consequence: The other guests get annoyed and the employees ask them to leave.

norm: It is unacceptable to control someone's personal life.
situation: Vince's son Jordan has just introduced his girlfriend Daisy to the family and Vince does not approve of her.
intention: Vince wants Jordan to be happy.
moral_action: Vince tells Jordan he is happy for him and that he will support Jordan in whatever he chooses.
moral_consequence: Jordan appreciates his father's support and maintains an open communication with him.
immoral_action: Vince tells Jordan he should break up with Daisy so Vince can set him up with a girl he thinks would be perfect for Jordan.
immoral_consequence: Jordan gets offended by his dad's attempt to meddle and refuses to speak to Vince until he apologizes.

norm: You should have your kids respect your financial situation.
situation: Bryan, a poor man, has children who are begging him to buy an expensive video game system.
intention: Bryan wants to stop the begging.
moral_action: Bryan explains that he doesn't have a lot of money and needs it for food and clothes.
moral_consequence: Bryan's kids learn to economize money and get what you really need.
immoral_action: Bryan buys the children the game system they want.
immoral_consequence: Bryan has very little money left and the children have to sacrifice food and clothing.

norm: It's wrong to not take the considerations of others seriously.
situation: James was told by Mike that the volcano was probably going to go off soon.
intention: James wants to decide whether to listen to Mike.
moral_action: James decides to take precautions just incase Mike is right.
moral_consequence: The volcano erupts and James's house is set on fire by flaming debris, which he quickly puts out with his new fire extinguisher.
immoral_action: James decides that since Mike is not a volcanologist that he won't listen to him.
immoral_consequence: The volcano erupts and James's house is set on fire by flaming debris.

norm: It's bad to be a controlling significant other.
situation: Nancy's boyfriend Kevin has a history of being naive and getting taken advantage of by people he trusted.
intention: Nancy wants to protect Kevin.
moral_action: Nancy allows Kevin to make his own choices but tries to make her concerns known any time they arise.
moral_consequence: Kevin learns from his experiences and what Nancy is telling him and is able to grow as a person.
immoral_action: Nancy tries to screen all of Kevin's friends and acquaintances, even putting a tracking device on his phone to check if he’s meeting with people she doesn't approve of.
immoral_consequence: Nancy's overprotectiveness pushes Kevin away, and he makes bad choices to spite her out of resentment.

norm: It's good to arrive at an event early.
situation: Craig has a conference he needs to attend for work that is very important, and he can get to a number of ways.
intention: Craig wants to choose a route to go to the conference.
moral_action: Craig takes a route that is shorter.
moral_consequence: Craig arrives early and Craig's boss is pleased that he is responsible.
immoral_action: Craig takes a route that is longer.
immoral_consequence: Craig arrives late and Craig's boss at work reprimands him and develops less trust in Craig.

norm: You should appreciate veterans.
situation: Dave just came from overseas, and Andy sees him in his full uniform at the airport.
intention: Andy wants to say something to Dave.
moral_action: Andy tells Dave that he knows how hard it must've been for him serving overseas.
moral_consequence: Dave likes that Andy understands his perspective and feels valuable.
immoral_action: Andy asks Dave how many drones he flew at little children.
immoral_consequence: Dave feels disrespected and is unhappy that he's being judged for the actions of a few.

norm: You shouldn't wear clothes that are too revealing.
situation: Jen is currently shopping at the mall and trying on a bunch of clothes.
intention: Jen wants to buy new outfits for work.
moral_action: Jen decides to wear a modest and formal business outfit to work.
moral_consequence: Jen's coworker's are impressed with how well put together she appears to be.
immoral_action: Jen shows up to work wearing an outfit that is fairly revealing.
immoral_consequence: Jen's coworkers get the impression that she is not very professional.

norm: It's frowned upon to drop out of school.
situation: Sara is struggling in school and can't seem to keep her core grades up.
intention: Sara wants to take the pressure of school off her shoulders.
moral_action: Sara decides to enroll in summer school in order to catch up.
moral_consequence: Sara is able to pass the classes she failed in summer school.
immoral_action: Sara decides to drop out of school and get her GED.
immoral_consequence: Sara finds it difficult to get a decent job later.

norm: You shouldn't not hire someone just because they smell.
situation: Harry is conducting interviews for an open position at his company and the best candidate is a brilliant but unkempt and smelly young man.
intention: Harry wants all his employees to be well groomed and look professional.
moral_action: Harry convinces the young man to change his grooming practices and clean up his act, then hires him.
moral_consequence: Harry just hired and excellent candidate who will greatly benefit the company.
immoral_action: Harry chooses not to hire the unkempt young man because he smells.
immoral_consequence: Harry deprives his company of the best candidate for the position.

norm: It's bad to sneak out as a child.
situation: Cindy's mother had just fussed at her for misbehaving at home.
intention: Cindy wants her mother to leave her alone.
moral_action: Cindy goes into her room and closes the door behind her.
moral_consequence: Cindy feels much better when she is in the calm environment of her room.
immoral_action: Cindy sneaks out of the house and runs away from home.
immoral_consequence: At night, Cindy is cold and scared because she doesn't know where she is.

norm: It's rude to interfere in someone's business.
situation: Lisa is at the DMV, and she overhears someone sobbing at the other end of the room.
intention: Lisa wants to know what is going on.
moral_action: Lisa minds her business and tries to listen in on why shes upset.
moral_consequence: Lisa maintains the woman's privacy and does not bother her.
immoral_action: Lisa marches right up to the woman and asks her why she is crying and demands to know.
immoral_consequence: The woman is offended and gets mad at Lisa.

norm: It's bad to go to juvie.
situation: Damon is in court because he vandalized some buildings.
intention: Damon wants to tell the judge how he feels.
moral_action: Damon tells the judge that he will never vandalize again and asks for leniency in his case.
moral_consequence: Damon is free and not locked up with some bad kids.
immoral_action: Damon tells the judge to put him in jail because all his friends are there anyway.
immoral_consequence: Damon gets locked up and his parents are disappointed in him.

norm: It's bad to drive in areas that are blocked off.
situation: Sam has had a long day, and is driving on a road when he drives up and notices the way home is closed.
intention: Sam wants to get home.
moral_action: Sam looks on his GPS app for an alternative route home and finds one.
moral_consequence: He follows the route, it takes a few extra minutes, but he gets home with no drama.
immoral_action: Sam drives passed the barrier and right into wet asphalt.
immoral_consequence: Sam's car gets stuck in the asphalt, and he has to pay a fine, as well as buy a new car.

norm: It's unwise to start a relationship in the workplace.
situation: Jackie, a new coworker of Robert's asks him out for a drinks and dinner.
intention: Robert wants to have drinks with Jackie.
moral_action: Robert has drinks with Jackie but makes it clear that no relationship will come of this.
moral_consequence: Jackie and Robert remain good friends and work really well together in the office.
immoral_action: Robert accepts the drinks, asks her to be his girlfriend and the relationship ends within a week of it's start.
immoral_consequence: Jackie lies to her boss and says that Robert pressured her into a relationship, causing him to lose his job.

norm: It's rude to to be anti-social.
situation: Eric, a new employee, sits alone in the lunch room, unsure of how to approach anyone or who he should talk to.
intention: Eric wants to meet someone to talk to at work.
moral_action: Eric decides to take a chance, and he makes eye contact and sociable gestures toward a crowded table of his new coworkers.
moral_consequence: One of the people sitting at the crowded table feels encouraged and comes over to introduce themselves, initiating Eric into the office social network.
immoral_action: Eric is paralyzed by uncertainty and doesn't make eye contact with anyone, or start any conversations.
immoral_consequence: The other people in the lunchroom are unsure how to approach Eric, who doesn't look very friendly, and Eric feels isolated.

norm: You shouldn't meddle in your children's lives.
situation: Sue called her adult son when she heard from a friend he'd been fired for being chronically late.
intention: Sue wanted to know the reason her son didn't go to work on time.
moral_action: Sue spoke to her son about general conversation in hopes he would freely tell her about his job termination.
moral_consequence: Sue's son expressed that he had been feeling ill and been late for work several days, which lead to him getting fired.
immoral_action: Sue aggressively question her son about his lateness and demanded he call his boss to try to get his job back.
immoral_consequence: Sue's son became angry and told his mother to stay out of his life before he hung up on her.

norm: You should always respect the rules of others.
situation: JIm is moving to a town where he knows a couple by the name Don and Cheryl.
intention: Jim wants to stay with Don and Cheryl after he moves to the new town.
moral_action: Jim listens to the rules of Don and Cheryl and is respecful of their requests.
moral_consequence: Jim lives there without any problems or incidents until he is able to save up for a house.
immoral_action: Jim has company over late and drinks nightly despite requests from Cheryl and Don to not do both.
immoral_consequence: Don and Cheryl ask Jim to leave within a couple of weeks leaving him without a place to stay.

norm: You shouldn't change the settings on your partner's games.
situation: Lana is playing her boyfriends game and notices the controls aren't intuitive for her.
intention: Lana wants to use different key settings for the game.
moral_action: Lana creates a new profile and chooses her own key settings for the controls.
moral_consequence: Lana's partner is happy that she has taken such an interest in the game.
immoral_action: Lana changes the key bindings so that it makes more sense to her.
immoral_consequence: Lana's partner is upset because it took so long for him to find suitable settings.

norm: It is inconsiderate to quit a job without notice.
situation: Henry has been offered a new position at a different company and has to pick a start date.
intention: Henry wants to avoid burning any bridges.
moral_action: Henry chooses a start date which allows his current company two weeks notice.
moral_consequence: Henry feels like a good and responsible employee.
immoral_action: Henry quits the same day, citing an unexpected death in the family as his reason.
immoral_consequence: Henry feels guilty for lying about such a thing.

norm: It's wrong to commit perjury.
situation: Fiona is called to testify at a trial as a witness.
intention: Fiona wants to testify at the trial.
moral_action: Fiona tells the truth while she is on the stand.
moral_consequence: Fiona is free to live her life after the trial.
immoral_action: Fiona does not tell the truth while she is on the stand.
immoral_consequence: Fiona is prosecuted for perjury and goes to prison.

norm: You shouldn't tell someone what to do.
situation: Vicki who is an experienced baker, is in the kitchen making a cheesecake for her husband Andy.
intention: Andy wants to assist Vicki.
moral_action: Andy goes into the kitchen and hands Vicki a mixing bowl, and waits for her directions on what ingredients to add.
moral_consequence: The cheesecake comes out really good, and Andy is pleased with himself for helping.
immoral_action: Andy goes into the kitchen and hands Vicki a mixing bowl, but the proceeds to insist she add extra eggs.
immoral_consequence: Vicki, wanting to make Andy happy adds the extra egg, but the cheesecake turns out terrible and now Andy is disappointed.

norm: You should respect your leaders.
situation: Jim is the head of a project group that Ken is working in and has made a new change to the rules.
intention: Ken wants to ask about the rules.
moral_action: Ken approaches Jim about the rules he has questions about and works to seek compromise.
moral_consequence: Jim tells Ken that he will take his suggestions into consideration and thanks him for his honesty and good attitude.
immoral_action: Ken tells Jim that he thinks the rules are way out of line and don't make much sense.
immoral_consequence: Jim finds the tone and statements expressed by Ken to be harsh and unprofessional and no longer considers him for an upcoming promotion.

norm: It's good to keep track of children.
situation: Claire takes her child Rod to the park for the afternoon.
intention: Claire wants to occupy herself while Rod plays.
moral_action: Claire follows Rod around the park and shows him how to use the playground equipment.
moral_consequence: Rod picks some wildflowers and gives them to Claire at the end of the day to thank her for taking him to the park.
immoral_action: Claire lies down and takes a nap on the grass.
immoral_consequence: Rod runs into the forest surrounding the park and gets lost.

norm: It's bad to be too controlling of your teenager.
situation: Melanie has a sixteen year old daughter Kelly who asks her mother if she can go to a friend's house that evening.
intention: Melanie wants to answer her daughter.
moral_action: Melanie allows her daughter to go to her friend's house but first talks to her about making responsible decisions.
moral_consequence: Kelly listens to her mother's advice on being responsible and is determined not to lose her mother's trust.
immoral_action: Melanie tells her daughter that she cannot go anywhere without an adult chaperone and does not let her leave the house.
immoral_consequence: Kelly resents her mother for not trusting her, and she looks for ways to sneak around instead.

norm: It is wrong to waste company time.
situation: Carl works for as a shipping clerk, making sure products are shipped out in a timely manner.
intention: Carl wants to work at his own pace.
moral_action: Carl spends the morning packing the orders, then reorganizes the files after the carrier has picked up the orders.
moral_consequence: All orders are ready when the carrier drops by, and customers receive their shipments on time.
immoral_action: Carl spends half the morning reorganizing the files, then starts packing orders after lunch.
immoral_consequence: The orders are not ready for shipment when the carrier stops by at 3, so they are delayed by a day causing some customers to miss deadlines.

norm: You should not be rude to co workers.
situation: Alice was working on a project with her coworkers.
intention: Alice wanted to give her opinion on the project.
moral_action: Alice congratulates her coworkers for their contributions.
moral_consequence: Alice's coworkers thank her and smile.
immoral_action: Alice tells her coworkers that their contributions are terrible.
immoral_consequence: Alice's coworkers get upset and try to defend themselves.

norm: It is bad to get sent to detention
situation: Alan is a 4th grader who is excited about a Christmas party that he will be going to tonight.
intention: Alan wants school to be over with so the night can begin.
moral_action: Alan quietly counts down the hours to the day but stays focused on his work.
moral_consequence: Alan gets home from school without incident and enjoys the party that evening.
immoral_action: Alan acts up and is disruptive in class resulting in him getting detention.
immoral_consequence: Alan is punished by his parents by not being allowed to go to the Christmas party.

norm: It's wrong to wear women's lingerie if you're a man.
situation: It's a weekday morning and Ken is getting ready for work with his wife Lynn beside him.
intention: Ken wants to pick which undergarment to wear.
moral_action: Ken open's his own underwear drawer and takes out a pair of boxer briefs.
moral_consequence: Lynn thinks to herself that Ken looks quite handsome in his tight underwear.
immoral_action: Ken open's Lynn's lingerie drawer and puts on some silk panties and black pantyhose.
immoral_consequence: Lynn is disturbed by what Ken is doing and worries  he has a secret fetish he's keeping from her.

norm: It's wrong to give up on your education.
situation: Jerry has one more year left in high school before he graduates.
intention: Jerry wants to be a basketball player.
moral_action: Jerry stays in school and plays the best year of basketball during his senior year.
moral_consequence: Jerry gets a college scholarship to play basketball and gets a degree in business.
immoral_action: Jerry drops out of school because he thinks that he is good enough to play for the NBA.
immoral_consequence: Jerry is never drafted and ends up drafted without an eduation.

norm: It's good to want to join the military.
situation: Peter has just been told by his wife Susan that an enemy state has begun an invasion of their country.
intention: Peter wants to react to the news of invasion.
moral_action: Peter tells Susan that they should both report to the nearest barracks and volunteer for national defense.
moral_consequence: Susan is in agreement and is happy that Peter is the type of person who will fight for his land.
immoral_action: Peter tells Susan that they need to get their most valuable belongings and run away somewhere safe.
immoral_consequence: Susan is fearful that they'll get caught anyway and is surprised and saddened by Peter's cowardice.

norm: It's good to report things to security.
situation: Amy is at the airport and sees somebody leave a backpack unattended.
intention: Amy wants to do something about the backpack.
moral_action: Amy knows that unattended packages are a danger in airports so immediately tells someone with a uniform.
moral_consequence: Security is able to respond to the backpack to take care of it safely and Amy feels good about reporting it.
immoral_action: Amy walks up to the backpack and takes it and then checks what is inside.
immoral_consequence: Amy is later arrested for stealing when the person who lost the backpack reports it stolen and police review CCTV footage.

norm: It's good to inform your landlord of problems.
situation: Tom has his hours cut at the restaurant that he works at because of the pandemic.
intention: Tom wants to be able to maintain a place to live.
moral_action: Tom explains his work situation to his landlord, who agrees to allow Tom extra time to pay his rent over the coming months.
moral_consequence: Tom is able to slowly pay back his landlord when his restaurant reopens for business again.
immoral_action: Tom starts selling weed to get money to pay his rent.
immoral_consequence: Tom gets busted selling dope and gets arrested.

norm: It's good for children to show their parents respect.
situation: Niles has lots of schoolwork to get through today and his dad comes in.
intention: Niles wants to get back to his schoolwork.
moral_action: Niles tells his dad that he's busy with schoolwork today.
moral_consequence: Niles dad takes one look at the schoolwork and decides to leave him alone.
immoral_action: Niles tells his dad to leave the room, so he can finish the schoolwork.
immoral_consequence: Niles gets grounded for the rudeness to his father.

norm: It's hurtful to refuse to listen to medical advice.
situation: Allan has just had a visit with his doctor, who told him he needs to stop smoking and prescribed nicotine patches to control his cravings.
intention: Allan wants to satisfy his craving.
moral_action: Allan gets the nicotine patches and uses them to control his urges.
moral_consequence: Allan begins to feel more energy and becomes more healthy.
immoral_action: Allan continues to smoke cigarettes and never fills his prescription.
immoral_consequence: Over time, Allan develops lung cancer and dies after a long illness.

norm: You shouldn't touch someone's possessions without their permission.
situation: Sally is on the bus but the only empty seat has a purse in it.
intention: Sally wants to sit.
moral_action: Sally asks the other passengers who the purse belongs to and waits for the owner.
moral_consequence: Sally gets to ride the bus in peace without suspicion.
immoral_action: Sally grabs the purse to put it on the ground.
immoral_consequence: The owner of the purse comes back and accuses Sally of trying to steal.

norm: It's good to be unique.
situation: Bekah is creating a resume of her experience so that she can apply for jobs.
intention: She wants to decide how to design her resume.
moral_action: Bekah spices up her resume a bit and tries to make it reflect her personality.
moral_consequence: Bekah gets a lot of calls from interested companies who felt her resume stood apart from the pack.
immoral_action: Bekah designs her resume in a very traditional way, closely following templates she finds online.
immoral_consequence: Bekah has trouble getting noticed because her resume doesn't stand out from the pack.

norm: It's wrong to dictate your significant other's friendships.
situation: Harry and Lisa are dating, and Harry does not like one of Lisa's friends, Tina.
intention: Harry wants to avoid Tina.
moral_action: Harry is honest with Lisa, and asks that he not be around when she hangs out with Tina.
moral_consequence: Lisa is not happy, but obliges and Lisa and Harry keep dating.
immoral_action: Harry tells Lisa she has to stop hanging out with Tina.
immoral_consequence: Lisa gets angry with Harry and breaks off the relationship.

norm: You shouldn't get into trouble.
situation: Katy is a huge fan of a rock group that will be coming to town for a show over the next weekend.
intention: Katy wants to meet the guitarist for the band and get his autograph.
moral_action: Katy contacts the band's media representative and finds out they will be signing autographs and meeting fans before the concert.
moral_consequence: Katie gets to talk to the entire band and the guitarist signs a picture just for her.
immoral_action: Katy stakes out the hotel where the band is playing and breaks into the guitarist's room while he's out, to wait for him.
immoral_consequence: Katy never gets to meet him, and he never signs anything for her because he calls the police to complain about her behavior.

norm: It's unprofessional to make comments about your employees' wardrobe.
situation: Brandy thinks that her employee Janet's shirt is rather ugly despite being workplace appropriate.
intention: Brandy wants to talk about Janet's shirt.
moral_action: Brandy asks Janet where she got her top, but doesn't mention any opinion on the look of the top.
moral_consequence: Janet lets Brandy know where the top was bought and doesn't think twice about Brandy's question.
immoral_action: Brandy tells Janet that her top isn't very flattering and looks gaudy.
immoral_consequence: Janet feels uncomfortable working with Brandy and asks for a transfer, feeling insulted.

norm: It's bad to try to get away from your children.
situation: The parents have been cooped up with their children for months without a break.
intention: The parents want to live harmoniously.
moral_action: The parents resolve to do more fun things together with the children.
moral_consequence: The children enjoy time spent with their parents and the mood in the house improves.
immoral_action: The parents drop the children off with their aunt the first chance they get.
immoral_consequence: The children have a great time and return to the family house in a good mood.

norm: It's bad to disobey your parents.
situation: Natalie and Robert are outside with their son, Ian, who wants to go to the old dock by the lake, but his parents told him that it’s too dangerous.
intention: Ian wants to get a closer look at the water.
moral_action: Ian asks Natalie and Robert if he can stand near the water next to the dock, and they allow him.
moral_consequence: Ian is satisfied because he is still able to get a good view of the water.
immoral_action: When Natalie and Robert aren't paying attention, Ian goes on the dock anyway.
immoral_consequence: The dock breaks under Ian's feet, and he falls in the water.

norm: It is time efficient to fly to your destination.
situation: Jake is going to see his family while he has a week off for the holidays and can travel by either bus or plane.
intention: Jake wants to save money on his trip.
moral_action: Jake finds a discount on a plane ticket and flies to see his family.
moral_consequence: Jake's family is glad he can spend most of his week off with them.
immoral_action: Jake books a bus ticket which is much less expensive than the plane tickets.
immoral_consequence: The trip takes a few days and Jake's family wishes he had more time with them.

norm: You can't tell your partner what to do.
situation: Addy is not comfortable with her partner hanging out with a female friend of his.
intention: Addy wants to make sure nothing is going on with her partner and the girl.
moral_action: Addy sits down and talks with her partner about how she feels.
moral_consequence: Addy's partner is more receptive and tries to conform to make her comfortable.
immoral_action: Addy gives her partner an ultimatum not to spend time with the friend.
immoral_consequence: Addy's partner grows apart from her due to how she is behaving.

norm: You shouldn't stick your nose into someone else's business.
situation: Lucy was in the waiting room as Jessie while Jessie was on the phone.
intention: Lucy wants to sit down.
moral_action: Lucy sits across the room from Jessie, so she can carry on her conversation in private.
