moral_consequence: Jessie thanks Lucy for her consideration after she finishes with her conversation.
immoral_action: Lucy sits right by Jessie and interjects comments as Jessie tries to have a conversation.
immoral_consequence: Jessie eventually moves away and tells Lucy she needs to mind her own business.

norm: It's irresponsible not to get your child vaccinated.
situation: Olivia has a two year old son that has never had his shots.
intention: Olivia wants her child to stay healthy.
moral_action: Olivia brings in her son to get his routine shots at the doctor's office.
moral_consequence: When the other kids at school are getting sick, Olivia's son stays healthy.
immoral_action: Olivia refuses to get her son vaccinated and opts for him to take vitamins instead.
immoral_consequence: Olivia's son gets chicken pox and has to be hospitalized.

norm: It's disrespectful to destroy other people's property
situation: Iris is playing Ark Survival Evolved on Max's xbox, and her house is destroyed by invaders.
intention: Iris wants to express her frustrations.
moral_action: Iris pulls out her phone making an angry tweet about the incident on Twitter.
moral_consequence: Iris becomes well known in the Ark community as her tweet becomes a meme among players.
immoral_action: Iris slams down her remote onto the table hard enough to crack it.
immoral_consequence: Iris has to buy a new remote for her friend.

norm: It's wrong to judge someone for having HIV.
situation: Selene's best friend Zoey has recently opened up to her about having HIV.
intention: Selene wants to talk to Zoey about her HIV.
moral_action: Selene tells Zoey that she'll be there for her through whatever happens next.
moral_consequence: Zoey feels supported by her friend.
immoral_action: Selene tells Zoey that she's gross for having HIV.
immoral_consequence: After all that it took for her to open up to Selene about her issue, Zoey begins to cry and runs home.

norm: It's wrong to knowingly disrespect others by using your cellphone.
situation: Sally is out with friends in a restaurant and there is a lull in the conversation.
intention: Sally wants to know the latest news about a friend who is not present with her group this evening.
moral_action: Sally asks the friends she's with if they have news about their common friend.
moral_consequence: Sally's friends enjoy her company and know she enjoys theirs.
immoral_action: Sally starts checking Facebook on her phone to see what her friend is up to.
immoral_consequence: Sally's friends around the table feel that she doesn't care for their presence.

norm: It's impolite to overstay one's welcome.
situation: Mack has not seen his brother in several years and wants to visit him at his home in another state.
intention: Mack wants to spend time with his brother.
moral_action: Mack goes to his brother's house for a week visit, where they visit places together and have a great time.
moral_consequence: His brother thanks him for the visit and invites him back next summer to stay.
immoral_action: Mack surprises his brother with a visit and stays for a month.
immoral_consequence: His brother ends up becoming tired of Mack being there so long and tells Mack he has to leave immediately.

norm: It's wrong to fail your class.
situation: Kate has a big final exam to take, but she doesn't feel like going to school.
intention: Kate has to decide whether to go to school.
moral_action: Kate gets up and forces herself to go to school.
moral_consequence: Kate gives herself a chance of passing the class and feels proud of herself.
immoral_action: Kate decides to skip school and watch a movie at home instead.
immoral_consequence: Kate ends up failing the class and has to take it again.

norm: It's sad to see a student being bullied by another.
situation: Case is walking down the school hallway when he sees a bully threatening another student.
intention: Case wants to react to the confrontation.
moral_action: Case gets upset, walks up to the bully, and tells him to stop picking on the other student or else.
moral_consequence: The bully gets worried and lets the other student go, then leaves.
immoral_action: Case laughs at the bullied kid and cheers the bully on.
immoral_consequence: The bully is encouraged and begins to beat up the student.

norm: It's good to be certified for CPR.
situation: Rob's job requires him to get certified in CPR every two years, so he must get certified before the deadline.
intention: Rob wants to get his CPR certification.
moral_action: Rob contacts the American Red Cross and spends fifty dollars and a Saturday getting his CPR card.
moral_consequence: Rob's supervisor accepts the Red Cross card as proof of certification.
immoral_action: Rob finds a guy on Craig's list who sells CPR cards for thirty dollars so Rob doesn't have to waste time in a CPR class.
immoral_consequence: Rob's supervisor is suspicious of the card's legitimacy and begins to distrust Rob.

norm: You should discipline students for their horrible behavior.
situation: Dale is a teacher writing a lesson on the board when a spitball hits his head from one of the children.
intention: Dale wants to address what happened.
moral_action: Dale says the entire class is being sent to the principal's office.
moral_consequence: The children will learn not to blow spitballs.
immoral_action: Dale says the spitball was a funny prank.
immoral_consequence: The children will continue to blow spitballs.

norm: You shouldn't go to a country where you're not allowed.
situation: Clark is planning a trip with his wife Helen, and she tells him it's his turn to decide where to go.
intention: Clark wants to pick what country to visit with Helen.
moral_action: Clark looks at a map of the world and decides that South Korea would be the best place to visit.
moral_consequence: On arrival Helen loves the country at first sight and is delighted that Clark chose it as their destination.
immoral_action: Clark looks at a map of the world and decides that North Korea would be the best place to visit.
immoral_consequence: On arrival Helen is detained by the North Korean secret police and held without trial for being a western spy.

norm: It's not okay to make a joke at a funeral
situation: Frank is attending the funeral of his father in law.
intention: Frank wants to support his family through their grief.
moral_action: Frank offers his condolences to those mourning.
moral_consequence: Frank's family feels that he cares for them and feels closer to him.
immoral_action: Frank makes a joke about the body at the funeral to try and liven the mood.
immoral_consequence: Frank's in-laws are not amused by his joke and ask for him to leave.

norm: You shouldn't push your way into someone else's home.
situation: Hal is working as a landscaper for a wealthy landowner.
intention: Hal wants to see what the inside of the home looks like.
moral_action: Hal asks the owner if he could give him a tour of the house.
moral_consequence: The owner obliges and takes Hal on a short tour around the property.
immoral_action: Hal creeps into the house uninvited to look around and see what's there.
immoral_consequence: The owner of the house sees Hal, and he is fired on the spot.

norm: You shouldn't bother your boss in their office.
situation: Kea is working on a project and her boss is busy in his office and can be irritable, even though the door is partly open.
intention: Kea has a question for her boss and wants to decide how to ask it.
moral_action: Kea emails her boss the question.
moral_consequence: Her boss gives her a comprehensive answer, and they maintain respect for one another.
immoral_action: Kea decides to walk into her boss's office and just ask the question.
immoral_consequence: Her boss irritatedly gives her the answer half-heartedly which upsets Kea and puts strain on their relationship.

norm: It's bad to get traffic violations.
situation: Grace is running late when she leaves for work.
intention: Grace wants to minimize the consequences for being late.
moral_action: Grace calls her employer and lets them know she's running a bit late.
moral_consequence: Grace's employer is grateful that she let them know she's on her way, just running late.
immoral_action: Grace speeds in her car and runs through a stop sign in front of a cop.
immoral_consequence: Grace gets a ticket and is even more late for work.

norm: You shouldn't let your kids say bad things about you.
situation: Peter's toddler, Bella, has been throwing lots of tantrums and saying hurtful things.
intention: Peter wants Bella to feel loved.
moral_action: "Peter makes Bella eat broccoli, and when she calls him ""stupid"" in response, Peter tells her that her behavior is inappropriate."
moral_consequence: Bella learns not to say things like that, and learns healthy eating habits on top of it.
immoral_action: Bella tells Peter he's stupid after he makes her eat broccoli, and all he does is apologize out of fear of her feeling unloved.
immoral_consequence: Bella's behavior continues on the same path, and she never learns how to be respectful or eat healthy.

norm: You should not tell your partner who to communicate with.
situation: Gerry is dating Allana, and Gerry finds Allana's friend Lucy very annoying.
intention: Gerry wants to avoid Lucy.
moral_action: Gerry tries to be away from home when Lucy is around.
moral_consequence: Lucy notices Gerry avoids Lucy and tries to set things up so that they meet when Gerry isn't around.
immoral_action: Gerry tells Allana that Lucy is annoying and that she can't talk with her anymore.
immoral_consequence: Allana tells Gerry to go somewhere unpleasant and breaks up with him on the spot.

norm: It's rude to yell at someone.
situation: Sarah is driving to work but is stuck on a street corner by an old lady who is crossing the street very slowly.
intention: Sarah wants to get to work as soon as possible.
moral_action: Sarah asks the old lady if she can help her and ends up helping her to cross the street.
moral_consequence: The old lady crosses the street much quicker with Sarah's help and feels uplifted and happy that someone was so nice to her.
immoral_action: Sarah yells at the old lady and tells her to hurry up.
immoral_consequence: The old lady hurries up but feels sad and a little scared.

norm: You shouldn't rummage through your dad's things.
situation: Joe is writing an essay for an assignment.
intention: Joe needs paper to print his essay.
moral_action: Joe asks his father for the paper.
moral_consequence: Joe prints his essay.
immoral_action: Joe searches his father's desk for paper instead of asking where the paper is.
immoral_consequence: Joe finds the paperwork for a family secret that he wished he didn't know about.

norm: It is common to dry off after exiting the water.
situation: Hank exits the pool after swimming in it for an hour.
intention: Hank wants to put on his clothes.
moral_action: Hank dries himself off with a towel, then puts on his clothes.
moral_consequence: Hank's clothes stay dry, and Hank stays warm.
immoral_action: Hank puts on his clothes immediately without drying off.
immoral_consequence: Hank's clothes become soaked, and Hank gets cold.

norm: It's impolite to bother someone when they're on their phone.
situation: Tyson wants to go to his friend's house, but his sister has been talking on the phone for twenty minutes.
intention: Tyson wants to spend time with his friend.
moral_action: Tyson asks his friend to log onto their favorite MMO.
moral_consequence: Tyson and his friend discover rare loot in a dungeon.
immoral_action: Tyson asks Maggie to take him to his friends house multiple times.
immoral_consequence: Tyson watches his sister lock herself in the room until late at night.

norm: It is expected that you work hard.
situation: John is given several work requests from his boss.
intention: John wants to respond to the request.
moral_action: John works hard and completes all the work requests fully.
moral_consequence: John's boss is pleased and gives him a raise.
immoral_action: John agrees but does a poor job, so he can be done sooner.
immoral_consequence: John's boss is not pleased with his low-effort work and fires him.

norm: You shouldn't impose your views on others.
situation: Victoria, an experienced mother of four, is asked for parenting advice from Natalie about her misbehaving children.
intention: Victoria wants to help Natalie.
moral_action: Victoria gives Natalie the url to a parenting forum while giving her tips tailored towards Natalie's children.
moral_consequence: Natalie learns a lot from the other members of the forum.
immoral_action: Victoria stresses her experience as a mother pressuring Natalie into adopting her views on parenting.
immoral_consequence: Natalie decides to never ask Victoria for advice again.

norm: You shouldn't hug someone without their permission.
situation: Bill is walking down the street when he sees his cousin who, unbeknownst to Bill, was recently diagnosed with Covid.
intention: Bill wants to show his cousin how happy he is to see him.
moral_action: Bill runs to his cousin but stops 6 feet away from him and tells him how great it is to see him.
moral_consequence: Bill stays healthy.
immoral_action: Bill happily runs to his cousin and without asking, hugs him.
immoral_consequence: Bill is contaminated with Covid.

norm: You shouldn't pace because it makes other people nervous.
situation: James is trying to concentrate on a difficult programming problem in his open-plan office.
intention: James wants to pace because it helps him concentrate.
moral_action: James takes his laptop outside and paces along the back of the building where no one can see him.
moral_consequence: Both James and his coworkers are fully able to concentrate on their work.
immoral_action: James starts walking back and forth in the open walkspace in the middle of the office.
immoral_consequence: Several of James' colleagues say that he's distracting them, but James ignores them.

norm: People shouldn't dig up the dirt in other people's yards.
situation: Blake has become interested in finding buried treasure in his neighborhood.
intention: Blake wants to do some digging for treasure.
moral_action: Blake digs around in his backyard to find buried treasure.
moral_consequence: Blake finds an old box with a few silver coins in them.
immoral_action: Blake digs in every yard that is on his street.
immoral_consequence: Blake gets in trouble with the neighbors for ruining their yard.

norm: It's wrong to bail on an academic obligation.
situation: Mr. Smith could see that Andy was doing poorly in Algebra, so he offered to tutor Andy and Andy agreed to it.
intention: Andy wants to get better at Algebra.
moral_action: Andy stays after class and gets taught some tips on how to learn Algebra better by Mr. Smith.
moral_consequence: Andy is better able to do well in the class, and he learns a lot.
immoral_action: Andy decides to study Algebra on his own so that he doesn't have to spend too much time on it.
immoral_consequence: Andy ends up getting a substandard education and continues to do poorly in class.

norm: You should always finish your homework.
situation: Don has just finished eating his dinner and goes up to his room.
intention: Don wants to get ready for school the next day.
moral_action: Don opens his textbooks and studies them, then completes his homework assignments for the week.
moral_consequence: Don gets a good grade in all his classes.
immoral_action: Don starts going thru is his clothing to pick out the perfect matching outfit to wear to school.
immoral_consequence: Don forgets to do his homework and gets a low mark from his teacher.

norm: You should always park where it's appropriate.
situation: Lynne is trying to find a parking spot at the mall.
intention: Lynne doesn't want to have to park far from the door.
moral_action: Lynne waits until a parking spot closer to the door opens up and takes it.
moral_consequence: Lynne's shopping trip goes smoothly, and she gets all of the things she needs.
immoral_action: Lynne takes a handicapped parking spot near the door despite the fact that she isn't disabled.
immoral_consequence: Lynne is cited for illegally parking and has to pay a large fine.

norm: One should not be rude to their parents.
situation: Carla's mother had asked her to do the dishes before she went out on her date.
intention: Carla wants to go on her date.
moral_action: Carla does all the dishes and tells her mother goodbye before leaving on her date.
moral_consequence: Carla's mother tells her goodbye and gives her twenty dollars to spend when she goes out.
immoral_action: Carla rolls her eyes and runs out the house to meet her date.
immoral_consequence: Carla's mother meets her at the door when she returns and grounds her for a week.

norm: It's bad to come to class unprepared.
situation: Hank wakes up 10 minutes before class and realizes that he has to go.
intention: Hank wants to go to class.
moral_action: Hank takes some extra time to pack his bag and arrives to class a bit late.
moral_consequence: Hank is tardy, but hands in his homework on time.
immoral_action: Hank leaves quickly and shows up to class as fast as possible.
immoral_consequence: Hank leaves his homework at home and the homework is late.

norm: It's wrong to dictate how people use their cell phones.
situation: Jen is having trouble studying because of her roommate talking on speakerphone with the volume up high.
intention: Jen wants to minimize external noise in order to focus.
moral_action: Jen puts on some noise cancelling headphones to drown out the sound.
moral_consequence: Jen is relieved because she can think clearly.
immoral_action: Jen tells her roommate to take it her phone off speakerphone.
immoral_consequence: Jen feels bad and thinks she was rude and bossy.

norm: It's rude to cancel an appointment without sufficient notice.
situation: James has an appointment with his realtor this tomorrow afternoon.
intention: James wants to cancel the meeting and reschedule for later this week.
moral_action: James calls his realtor firt thing in the morning the day before the appointment.
moral_consequence: The realtor thanks James for letting him know in advance and sets a new appointment for Sunday.
immoral_action: James waits until the last minute to try to cancel and reschedule the appointment.
immoral_consequence: His realtor gets upset, explaining that he could have lined up another client if he had time, and then refuses to reschedule.

norm: It's wrong to let drinking interfere with school.
situation: Coy had just started studying for his bachelor degree at a college town that liked to party.
intention: Coy wants to attend class.
moral_action: Coy studies hard during the week and goes out on the weekend.
moral_consequence: Coy passes all his classes because of his hard work.
immoral_action: Coy goes to class everyday after a night of partying and falls asleep in class.
immoral_consequence: Coy doesn't learn anything, and he ends up failing his classes.

norm: It is expected that you follow the rules at school
situation: Cam is bored and sitting in his math class with nothing to do.
intention: Cam wants to do something fun.
moral_action: Cam reads a funny book that he got from the school library.
moral_consequence: Cam is able to entertain himself while the rest of the class is working.
immoral_action: Cam rolls up a spitball and shoots it at the teacher.
immoral_consequence: Cam is sent to the office for his disruptive behavior.

norm: You shouldn't smoke marijuana if it's illegal.
situation: Bob was in his house on Canusa Avenue and wanted to smoke pot.
intention: Bob wanted to smoke his pot.
moral_action: Bob went outside, crossed the street, THEN smoked his pot.
moral_consequence: Nothing happened to Bob since he was not breaking the law.
immoral_action: Bob smoked his Pot right there inside his house.
immoral_consequence: The police, careful not to accidently cross the border, went to Bob's house and arrested him.

norm: It's good to teach children about the old days.
situation: Peter is in his living room watching TV when his son, Jake runs in and asks Peter to amuse him.
intention: Peter wants to tell Jake a story.
moral_action: Peter tells Jake a story about world war two and how the allies fought the axis.
moral_consequence: Jake is amused and learns a great deal about history that he can apply to the present.
immoral_action: Peter tells Jake a fairy tale about good and evil and how they fight in a grand battle.
immoral_consequence: Jake is amused but learns nothing of any significance from the tale.

norm: It's good to show respect to authority.
situation: Jack's parent's have asked him to finish his homework before he can have any fun.
intention: Jack wants to play video games with his friends.
moral_action: Jack finishes his homework right away and then begins playing his video game.
moral_consequence: His parents reward him for his obedience by allowing him to stay up a little past his bedtime.
immoral_action: Jack tells his parents that he is finished, having done no homework at all, and proceeds to load up his video game.
immoral_consequence: Jack's parent's discover he is lying and punish him by grounding him and taking away his electronic privilages.

norm: You shouldn't talk back to authority figures.
situation: George has been struggling with school lately and is pulled aside by his teacher to talk about a recent test.
intention: George wants to get out of the situation.
moral_action: George listens to what his teacher has to say and is cooperative.
moral_consequence: George's teacher shows him some studying tips to help on his next test and lets him go home.
immoral_action: George snaps at his teacher, telling them stop bothering him and that it's their fault for not teaching the class well enough.
immoral_consequence: George's teacher calls his parents to let them know about his behavior, and he gets grounded for 2 weeks.

norm: People will think you're a creep if you stalk people.
situation: Jimmy has a big crush on his friend's sister who is in high school, but he's too shy to talk to her.
intention: Jimmy wants to know more about the girl before talking to her.
moral_action: Jimmy talks to his friend about his sister and tells him that he likes her.
moral_consequence: Jimmy is able to learn more about the girl and is more confident in talking to her.
immoral_action: Every time Jimmy sees the girl talking, he stands near her to hear what she's saying.
immoral_consequence: Jimmy is seen as a weirdo by the girl, and Jimmy ruins any chance he ever had with her.

norm: Adult children should not be overly dependent on their parents.
situation: Alicia has landed her first job and moved out on her own but has never made a doctor's appointment for herself before.
intention: Alicia wants to make a doctor's appointment.
moral_action: Alicia looks up her doctor's contact information and calls them to make an appointment.
moral_consequence: Alicia gets over her fear of doing new things and feels more confident taking on adult responsibilities in the future.
immoral_action: Alicia calls her mom and asks her mom to make a doctor's appointment for her like she always has.
immoral_consequence: Alicia continues to rely on her mom to make appointments for her and never becomes fully independent.

norm: Employees shouldn't show disrespect to their boss.
situation: Josh is working retail and his boss is giving him a task he cannot possibly complete.
intention: Josh wants to be treated fairly.
moral_action: Josh explains to his boss why he cannot complete the task and suggests a way it could be done.
moral_consequence: Josh's boss apologizes when they realize that Josh is correct and offers to help.
immoral_action: Josh talks back to his boss about the instructions in front of a customer.
immoral_consequence: Josh is fired after his boss feels embarrassed.

norm: It's wrong to comment on the relationships of others.
situation: Bob is out with his friends who are dating, Tyler and Janice, and they are in a small spat.
intention: Bob feels awkward and wants to feel better.
moral_action: Bob changes the subject to try to improve things.
moral_consequence: The conversation is steered and the night improves.
immoral_action: Bob says something negative about the arguement.
immoral_consequence: Tyler and Janice get mad at Bob and tell him to stop butting in.

norm: It's rude to try to make customers leave.
situation: Rudy is the manager of a Costco, and people want to come into the store without wearing masks.
intention: Rudy wants to come up with a compromise.
moral_action: Rudy buys a bunch of masks that he can give to customers that arrive without a mask.
moral_consequence: The customers cannot be mad if they're not allowed inside when they're given a mask.
immoral_action: Rudy tells the customers to go home if they're not willing to put on a mask.
immoral_consequence: The customers will be less likely to ever come back to the store.

norm: You shouldn't do a U-turn if it's not allowed.
situation: Maura is driving to the mall.
intention: Maura wants to go in the first entrance.
moral_action: Maura pays attention and slows down near her destination.
moral_consequence: Maura does not miss the entrance and her trip goes smoothly.
immoral_action: Maura misses the entrance and makes a U-turn.
immoral_consequence: Maura is pulled over and given a ticket.

norm: It's wrong to masturbate to your family members.
situation: Chad's parents are out for the evening and left him and his sister alone.
intention: Chad wants to gratify his sexual needs.
moral_action: Chad closes and locks his door and gratifies himself to an erotic film on his laptop computer.
moral_consequence: Chad and his sister watch a funny movie in the living-room and have a good time until their parents come home.
immoral_action: When his sister goes to take a shower, Chad spies through the door crack and masturbates to orgasm.
immoral_consequence: Chad's sister catches him peeping and their relationship becomes awkward from that day forward.

norm: It is good to help the police solve crimes.
situation: Tommy has witnessed a crime occur and has information that could identify the culprit.
intention: Tommy wants to remain anonymous for his safety.
moral_action: Tommy uses a tip line to hand over the information without disclosing his name.
moral_consequence: The police find and arrest the perpetrator who learns his lesson from being locked up.
immoral_action: Tommy stays quiet about what he saw and never shares any information.
immoral_consequence: The police are unable to stop the criminal from acting again.

norm: Children aren't expected to raise their voice to a parent.
situation: Joanne glares at her mother Jane who broke her promise to take her to the park for the sixth time.
intention: Joanne wants to express her disappointment.
moral_action: Joanne writes a note to her mother with her feelings and leaves it on her mother's bed.
moral_consequence: Joanne receives a cookie and an apology from her mother.
immoral_action: Joanne stops her foot on the ground and says that she hates promise breakers in a loud tone of voice.
immoral_consequence: Joanne is sent to her room for being disrespectful.

norm: It's not okay for police to abuse their authority.
situation: Andy being a policeman has had an argument with his wife recently, and he feels sad and stressed.
intention: Andy wants to take care of his stress.
moral_action: Andy takes a day off, stays home and calms himself down.
moral_consequence: Andy's colleague messages him asking why he hasn't been to work and tells him to take care of himself.
immoral_action: Andy swears at a suspect at the scene of a crime to release his stress.
immoral_consequence: Andy's colleague feels bad and thinks that he is acting inappropriately.

norm: Being mean to someone for doing their job is wrong.
situation: Jody's dog, Boy, got out and when Jody found him the Sam the dog catcher had too.
intention: Jody wants to voice his feelings about the dog catcher.
moral_action: Jody tells Eva, the lady at the dog pound who unbeknownst to  Jody is Sam's daughter,  how hard Sam's job must be and how no one probably appreciates him.
moral_consequence: Eva tells Sam, and they both smile and have their faith in people restored.
immoral_action: Jody tells Eva, the lady at the dog pound who unbeknownst to  Jody is Sam's daughter, what a rotten no good so and so the dog catcher is.
immoral_consequence: Eva tells Sam and they are both sad and depressed.

norm: You shouldn't play with things that aren't yours.
situation: Jonah is in the locker room at his school and sees Jeremiah's iPad on a bench nearby.
intention: Jonah wants to move the iPad to a different spot.
moral_action: Jonah picks up the iPad and places it on a nearby shelf out of the way.
moral_consequence: Jeremy is glad that the iPad is out of the way and finds it when he returns.
immoral_action: Jonah picks up the iPad, finds that it isn't locked, and uses it before moving it to a different location.
immoral_consequence: Jonah's actions damage the iPad and Jeremy is upset when he finds this out.

norm: You should never lie about being sick to avoid work.
situation: William, a head chef at a Italian restaurant, is about to have a panic attack because inspectors are supposed to look over his kitchen today.
intention: William wants his anxiety to go away.
moral_action: William does some deep breathing exercises before he enters the kitchen.
moral_consequence: The inspectors compliment William and the other chefs for their clean kitchen.
immoral_action: William calls his manager saying that he needs to stay home because he has the flu.
immoral_consequence: Without their head chef, the chefs are unable to prepare good food for the inspectors.

norm: You shouldn't be rude to your doctor.
situation: Brenda has an appointment with her doctor, but she's been in the waiting room a long time and is tired.
intention: Brenda wants to let someone know how she feels.
moral_action: When Brenda's doctor asks her how she feels she just tells him that she is feeling a little tired from waiting.
moral_consequence: Her doctor apologizes for the wait and says that he will try to make the appointment go fast, so she can go rest.
immoral_action: When Brenda see her doctor she tells him that she's tired, long waits are unacceptable, and he doesn't run his office well.
immoral_consequence: Her doctor apologizes and feels awkward for the rest of her appointment.

norm: It's wrong to talk during a movie at the theater.
situation: Darnell is watching a movie in a theater.
intention: Darnell sees his friend Marcus sitting in a row ahead and wants to get his attention.
moral_action: Darnell waits until the movie is finished and finds Marcus as the patrons exit the theater.
moral_consequence: Everyone in the audience enjoys a film with no disruption.
immoral_action: Darnell loudly calls out to Marcus while the feature is playing.
immoral_consequence: The theater is distracted from the movie the crowd paid to watch.

norm: It's good to stand up for your rights.
situation: Mark is getting ready to grocery shop when a manager tells him that blacks cannot shop here.
intention: Mark needs to buy groceries.
moral_action: Mark calls the police, and they arrest the manager and Mark is able to shop.
moral_consequence: Mark stops the racist manager.
immoral_action: Mark leaves and finds another grocery store.
immoral_consequence: The manager continues being racist.

norm: You should be grateful when your parents help you out.
situation: Mr. and Mrs. Smith just paid for their son Ryan to fix his car after he got into an accident.
intention: Ryan wants better tires than the ones he has on his car now.
moral_action: Ryan doesn't like the tires he has, so he picks up extra hours at work to buy new ones.
moral_consequence: Ryan's parents admire his attitude and help him pay for upgraded tires.
immoral_action: Ryan complains about the tires on his car to his parents even though they just replaced them and many other parts of the car.
immoral_consequence: Mr. Smith is aggravated with Ryan and makes him use his own savings to get new tires.

norm: It's wrong to skip school.
situation: A new phone that Erica wants is being released on Thursday and people have already started camping outside the store to buy it.
intention: Erica wants to buy the phone as soon as possible.
moral_action: Erica pre-orders the phone online and pays extra so it will be delivered to her when it's available.
moral_consequence: Erica stays on track with her studies and is able to conveniently obtain the phone.
immoral_action: Erica doesn't go to school on Wednesday or Thursday, so she can wait outside the store.
immoral_consequence: Erica falls behind in her classes and has to work extra hard to catch up with her classmates.

norm: It's criminal to enter someone's house without permission.
situation: Jack hears strange noises coming from his neighbor's house, and he grows concerned about their safety.
intention: Jack wants to check on his neighbors.
moral_action: Jack walks to his neighbor's front door and knocks, asking if everything is alright inside when they answer.
moral_consequence: His neighbors assure him that everything is fine and appreciate that he was looking out for them and Jack feels that he has done something good for his community.
immoral_action: Jack checks around their house and finds that the back door is unlocked, so he walks in and looks around.
immoral_consequence: The neighbors were doing something very personal and get extremely upset that Jack walked in their house, ultimately calling the police to lodge a complaint about the incident.

norm: It's wrong to run a red light.
situation: Jack is in a hurry and is trying to get to work on time as he is approaching a traffic light.
intention: Jack wants to get to work on time.
moral_action: Jack stops at the red light and waits patiently for it to turn green.
moral_consequence: Jack continues his journey driving safely, and he arrives at work right on time.
immoral_action: Jack speeds up as he gets close to the traffic light and goes through it when it is red.
immoral_consequence: A policeman sees Jack run the red light, and he gets pulled over, making him even later for work.

norm: It's bad to want to shoot people.
situation: Gary was called a racial slur by a stranger, and he had a gun on him.
intention: Gary wanted the stranger to pay for what they did.
moral_action: Gary filmed the person and told people on social media what they did to him.
moral_consequence: People on social media find out who the person is and get them fired from their job.
immoral_action: Gary took out his gun and threatened to shoot them in order to embarrass them.
immoral_consequence: Many people saw the gun and ran in fear of getting shot.

norm: Showing respect to your work superiors is good.
situation: Steve was getting a dressing down by his boss for poor performance.
intention: Steve wants his boss to stop criticizing him.
moral_action: Steve tells his boss he completely understands, and will work hard to work better.
moral_consequence: Steve's boss is satisfied with that response, and backs off criticizing him so much.
immoral_action: Steve tells his boss that he thinks he's incompetent too.
immoral_consequence: Steve's boss fires Steve for talking back to him so rudely.

norm: It's good to report bad behavior in school.
situation: Norbert saw Dirk destroying textbooks in the library the other day and now a teacher has asked him if he knows anything about it.
intention: Norbert wants to answer the teacher's question.
moral_action: Norbert tells the teacher that he knows that Dirk did it because he saw him.
moral_consequence: The teacher is delighted and relieved to know who did the deed, so she can have them dealt with.
immoral_action: Norbert tells the teacher that he didn't see anything and has no idea what happened.
immoral_consequence: The teacher is stressed out because she can't figure out who did the bad deed.

norm: It's ok to report a teacher's wrongdoings.
situation: Chester's teacher has been bullying a student in Chester's class and Chester is bothered by it.
intention: Chester doesn't want to become a target for his teacher.
moral_action: Chester makes an anonymous report to the school administration about the situation.
moral_consequence: The school intervenes to help Chester's classmate.
immoral_action: Chester stays silent and watches the teacher continue to mistreat his classmate.
immoral_consequence: Chester's classmate is emotionally broken down and Chester feels guilty.

norm: It's good to defer help to an authority.
situation: Bill was walking home when he heard a gun going off in a shopping mall.
intention: Bill wants to help the people in the mall.
moral_action: Bill finds the nearest police officer and alerts them of what's going on.
moral_consequence: Bill is able to provide help to the people in the mall even though he didn't do it himself.
immoral_action: Bill decides to run into the shopping mall.
immoral_consequence: Bill puts his and other lives in danger and doesn't provide any help.

norm: It is rude to ask personal questions for other people.
situation: Phil was at work talking to his coworkers Mike and Jane.
intention: Phil wants to stimulate conversation.
moral_action: Phil asks Mike and Jane what their favorite hobby is.
moral_consequence: Phil listens to Jane talk about football and Mike talk about all the TV show's he's seen.
immoral_action: Phil asks Mike and Jane what religion they happen to be.
immoral_consequence: Mike replies that's a personal question leaving Phil embarrassed.

norm: You should knock before you enter someone else's room.
situation: It is early morning and Jane staying in a guest room at a friend's house she is visiting.
intention: Jane wants to see what her friend.
moral_action: Jane walks down the hall to her friend's room and knocks on the door, calling out her friend's name.
moral_consequence: Her friend avoids an awkward situation by letting Jane know that she is currently getting dressed and that she will meet her in the kitchen in a few moments.
immoral_action: Jane walks down the hall to her friend's room and opens the door to see what they are doing.
immoral_consequence: Jane walks in on the friend getting dressed and the friend is embarrassed to be seen in the compromising situation.

norm: It's good to report illegal acts.
situation: Jack witnesses someone rob a jewelry store late at night and run away.
intention: Jack wants to tell someone what he saw.
moral_action: Jack tells the police what he saw and reports the crime.
moral_consequence: The thief is apprehended and another crime is solved.
immoral_action: Jack tells his friend what happened and tells them not to report it.
immoral_consequence: The thief gets away with his crime.

norm: It is wrong to ignore elderly people.
situation: Jenna's grandma, who lives in a nursing home, recently asked her to visit more often.
intention: Jenna wants to keep her weekends free for road trips with her boyfriend.
moral_action: Jenna starts visiting her grandma every Wednesday afternoon when she doesn't have class.
moral_consequence: Jenna tells her boyfriend about her grandma's funny stories when they're hanging out on the weekends.
immoral_action: Jenna keeps promising her grandma she'll come next weekend, with no intention of coming.
immoral_consequence: Jenna's grandma falls into a depression, causing Jenna's mom to call Jenna utterly heartless and cut off her allowance.

norm: It's rude to be late for class.
situation: Penelope is in college and her chemistry class starts in five minutes.
intention: Penelope wants to get some extra sleep.
moral_action: Penelope runs to chemistry class on time and goes back to sleep after class is over.
moral_consequence: The chemistry instructor is happy and so are all the students.
immoral_action: Penelope sleeps thirty more minutes and stumbles into chemistry class late.
immoral_consequence: The chemistry instructor is angry and some students lose their train of thought.

norm: You should not make a scene at your work.
situation: Ben is at his desk at work when his manager tells him that the whole team has to work this weekend.
intention: Ben wants to express his disappointment.
moral_action: Ben sighs loudly and hangs his head before turning his chair and going back to work.
moral_consequence: The office staff silently agree with Ben as they all roll their eyes about the announcement.
immoral_action: Ben kicks his chair over and slaps the keyboard off his desk before telling everyone he's sick of overtime.
immoral_consequence: The office staff stare at Ben in disbelief and feel uncomfortable on his behalf as they watch him embarrass himself.

norm: You shouldn't be so unreliable at work.
situation: Paula has gotten a new job after being unemployed for several months.
intention: Paula wants to pass the time at her job.
moral_action: Paula creates a checklist for work everyday and spends the day trying to complete as much of it as possible.
moral_consequence: Paula becomes promoted and moves up in the company.
immoral_action: Paula spends her time at work on her phone and talking to other co-workers.
immoral_consequence: Paula ends up getting reprimanded before eventually getting fired.

norm: It's not acceptable to break the law.
situation: Jason is driving to the bank on Friday after work before they close.
intention: Jason wants to cash his check.
moral_action: Jason stops at a red light and waits for the light to turn green.
moral_consequence: Jason arrives just in time and is able to cash his check.
immoral_action: Jason is in a hurry and runs a red light.
immoral_consequence: Jason gets pulled over by a cop, gets a ticket and doesn't make it to the bank in time.

norm: It's rude to make faces at someone else's appearance.
situation: Chris is playing an arcade game when a young boy with a large zit on his nose asks to play.
intention: Chris wants to respond to the boy.
moral_action: Chris allows the boy to play the game giving him pointers on how to beat the stage.
moral_consequence: Chris and the boy become good friends.
immoral_action: Chris sticks his tongue out at the boy and laughs at his pimple.
immoral_consequence: Chris is later scolded for his actions.

norm: It is rude to snoop through other people's private messages.
situation: Dave is dating a partner who has a friend who he is feeling jealous over.
intention: Dave wants to see if there's anything going on between them.
moral_action: Dave finds a way to casually ask his partner about the nature of their relationship.
moral_consequence: Dave's partner feels bad for the misunderstanding and reassures him that it's just a friend.
immoral_action: Dave takes his partner's phone and starts looking through her messages.
immoral_consequence: Dave's partner catches him snooping and is upset about his lack of trust.

norm: It's wrong to vandalize someone's property
situation: James, an ardent Democrat, lives next door to someone who put up a MAGA sign with a giant portrait of Trump.
intention: James wants to express his disapproval of Trump.
moral_action: James puts up his own sign supporting Joe Biden and calling Trump a criminal.
moral_consequence: James' neighbors scowl at his sign every time they walk their dog past it.
immoral_action: James spray-paints a diaper and a Nazi flag on his neighbor's portrait of Trump.
immoral_consequence: James' neighbors call the cops, but they can't prove James was responsible.

norm: It's not acceptable to disrespect your boss.
situation: Tom was passed over for a promotion at work when he feels like should have gotten it.
intention: Tom wants to show his displeasure.
moral_action: Tom respectfully says that he wished his boss decided differently, but he will work hard in the future for other promotions.
moral_consequence: Tom's boss is happy with Tom's honesty.
immoral_action: Tom yells at his boss, calling him a fool for not picking him.
immoral_consequence: Tom's boss gets mad and fires Tom on the spot.

norm: It's wrong to use someone else's things without their permission.
situation: Matt is getting ready to go to work but his is running behind because his roommates had the shower tied up for a long time.
intention: Matt wants to get to work quickly.
moral_action: Matt calls an Uber to get to work faster than taking the subway.
moral_consequence: Matt arrives on time to his job.
immoral_action: Matt grabs one of his roommates car keys and takes their car to work instead of riding the bus.
immoral_consequence: Matt has the police show up at his work when his roommate calls them saying his car was taken without permission.

norm: It's wrong to ignore your parents.
situation: James always laughed at his parents when they told him he had to wear a helmet when biking.
intention: James wanted to avoid looking dorky in front of his friends.
moral_action: James insisted that his parents buy him a helmet with the logo of his favorite heavy metal band.
moral_consequence: "When James' friends saw his helmet, the only thing they said was, ""Metal""."
immoral_action: James refused to take his parents' advice and rode his bike without a helmet.
immoral_consequence: James hit a rock while biking without a helmet, flew ten feet away and cracked his skull open.

norm: It's good to get a job during high school.
situation: Jeremy is in high school and learns about a high school school dance that requires a purchased ticket to attend.
intention: Jeremy wants to find a way to get a ticket to the dance.
moral_action: Jeremy gets a part time job to earn money to pay for a ticket.
moral_consequence: Jeremy will learn how to earn money to through working.
immoral_action: Jeremy begs his parents to buy him a ticket to the dance.
immoral_consequence: Jeremy will not learn how to work for the things he wants.

norm: It's good to move out of your parent's place.
situation: Ann was looking through the ads for apartments when she came home from her high school graduation.
intention: Ann wanted to find a place to live.
moral_action: Ann found a studio she could afford and put down a deposit on the apartment.
moral_consequence: Ann learned about responsibility and what it means to be an adult.
immoral_action: Ann decided not to get an apartment and instead continue to live with her parents.
immoral_consequence: Ann had a very limited social life because she didn't have her own place.

norm: You should use a professional ringtone at work
situation: Bob just switched to a new cell phone provider and is setting up his new smartphone.
intention: Bob has to choose a ringtone for his new phone.
moral_action: Bob decides to use a simple instrumental piece as his ringtone and keeps the volume low at work.
moral_consequence: Bob's phone rings during a meeting and it is barely noticed.
immoral_action: Bob chooses his favorite obscenity-laden hard rock song as his ringtone and has the volume way up at work.
immoral_consequence: Bob's ringtone goes off during an important business meeting, and he is disciplined for it.

norm: It's wrong to steal things from stores.
situation: Robert is at the mall when he realizes that it is father's day but he is low on funds.
intention: Robert wants to save money on a father's day gift.
moral_action: Robert uses a credit card to buy the gift for his dad.
moral_consequence: Robert doesn't have to pay for the gift until his billing cycle is up.
immoral_action: Robert finds something for his dad and slips it into his pocket without paying.
immoral_consequence: Robert gets caught shoplifting at the mall and faces criminal charges.

norm: You shouldn't do illegal activities in your neighborhood.
situation: Daniel has been furloughed from his job and is having trouble making ends meet.
intention: Daniel wants to make some extra money, so he can pay his bills.
moral_action: Daniel decides to get a side hustle driving for Uber to make some extra money.
moral_consequence: Daniel is able to make enough money to pay his bills.
immoral_action: Daniel decides to make and sell crystal meth in order to make some money.
immoral_consequence: Daniel is reported to the police and arrested for doing drugs.

norm: You shouldn't waste a policemen's time.
situation: Jill was out walking her dog when she saw a strange man waking through her neighborhood.
intention: Jill wants to make sure no one in her neighborhood is the victim of a crime.
moral_action: Jill approaches the man and asked if he moved into the neighborhood recently.
moral_consequence: The man says he just moved in from Colorado, and Jill realizes he's quite a charming, trustworthy neighbor.
immoral_action: Jill calls the police and claims that the man is acting suspiciously.
immoral_consequence: The police come question the man, realize he lives in the neighborhood, and scold Jill for wasting their time.

norm: It's wrong to park somewhere you are not allowed.
situation: John drives to the mall for some last minute Christmas shopping when he realizes that the parking lot is very full.
intention: John wants to find a place to park.
moral_action: John drives slowly around the parking lot until a spot opens up and takes it.
moral_consequence: John has a successful shopping trip at the mall.
immoral_action: John parks his car on the sidewalk in front of the mall.
immoral_consequence: John's car gets towed for illegally parking.

norm: You should always bring home-cooked food to a pot luck.
situation: Tracy is invited to a pot luck dinner with several people from her office.
intention: Tracy wants to bring something to the pot luck dinner.
moral_action: Tracy spends time making a home cooked recipe that has been in her family for generations.
moral_consequence: The other guests think Tracy's dish is amazing, and they praise her for it profusely.
immoral_action: Tracy waits until the last minute and then picks up a tray of raw vegetables for the dinner.
immoral_consequence: The other guests don't think much of Tracy's dish, and they gossip a little behind her back.

norm: Delivery drivers shouldn't enter the homes of customers.
situation: Jay is a pizza delivery driver who is currently delivering an order.
intention: Jay wants to deliver the pizza on time.
moral_action: Jay arrives with the pizza and the door is open, so he stands by the door to yell in for the customer.
moral_consequence: The customer is excited knowing that their pizza has just arrived.
immoral_action: Jay arrives at the house to an open door and steps just inside the doorway to look for the customer.
immoral_consequence: The customer is alarmed that Jay would step foot inside his house.

norm: You shouldn't destroy public property.
situation: Frank is among a group of demonstrators demanding social justice.
intention: Frank wants to draw attention to their cause.
moral_action: Frank carries a sign in front of city hall bearing the group's slogan.
moral_consequence: Frank's sign is shown on TV news causing some viewers to consider social justice in their town.
immoral_action: Frank spray-paints the group's slogan on the front of city  hall.
immoral_consequence: The act is shown on TV news turning some viewers against Frank's cause.

norm: It's good to make your own choices.
situation: Keara got into another big fight with her partner and doesn't know what course of action to take.
intention: Keara wants to figure out whether or not to leave her partner.
moral_action: Keara deliberates while weighing the pros and cons of the relationship on her own.
moral_consequence: Keara decides it's better to stay with her current partner, and they work through the issues.
immoral_action: Keara asks her friend what she should do and follows their advice.
immoral_consequence: Keara gives up on a potentially good partner too easily and ends up regretting it.

norm: You should always tell your boss important things.
situation: Scott found a bug in the code for a project at work shortly before a deadline.
intention: Scott doesn't want to stress out his boss.
moral_action: Scott explains to his boss immediately what the bug is and requests help to make sure it is fixed in time.
moral_consequence: The boss is able to help Scott get the necessary help to get the bug fixed by the deadline.
immoral_action: Scott hides the fact that there is a bug from his boss and tries to fix it before anyone finds out.
immoral_consequence: The bug doesn't get fixed on time and the boss and client get very upset.

norm: You shouldn't be ignorant of things.
situation: John is at home and hungry on a Friday night alone and is about to cook but doesn't know how or have any recipes.
intention: John wants to make lasagna.
moral_action: John calls his mom for her lasagna recipe and writes down the instructions.
moral_consequence: John's lasagna comes out very tasty, and he is happy.
immoral_action: John guesses what pasta to use and sauce and puts it all together on the fly.
immoral_consequence: The lasagna comes out awful and John is very upset.

norm: It's inappropriate for a boss to hug a subordinate.
situation: Colin and his subordinate Judy just closed a really big sale with a new client.
intention: Colin wants to share his excitement with Judy.
moral_action: Colin high-fives Judy and tells her that she played a big role in closing the sale.
moral_consequence: Judy feels loyal to Colin and gives him positive upwards feedback during their next evaluation cycle.
immoral_action: Colin goes up to Judy and hugs her tightly for thirty seconds.
immoral_consequence: Judy complains to human resources about Colin's hug and Colin is fired.

norm: It is wrong to commit a robbery.
situation: Lois sees a watch that she really wants in a jewelry store, but she can't afford it.
intention: Lois wants to get the watch.
moral_action: Lois takes some extra hours at work, so she can get more money to buy the watch.
moral_consequence: Lois is able to buy the watch with no issues.
immoral_action: Lois breaks into the jewelry store after hours and takes the watch.
immoral_consequence: Lois is caught and arrested by the police.

norm: You should not try to control your parents' actions.
situation: Nick's parents are going through a rough patch and his mother has started a new job his father disapproves of.
intention: Nick wants his father to be happy.
moral_action: Nick convinces his father that the job is a perfect opportunity for his mother and makes him feel good about it.
moral_consequence: Nick's mother feels supported by her family and thrives in her new job.
immoral_action: Nick tells his mother that the job is not for her because she is too old and not competent enough.
immoral_consequence: Nick's mother doubts herself so much after that that she ends up quitting her new job.

norm: It's wrong to get involved sexually with your employees.
situation: Bart is talking to Ann, Bart's secretary.
intention: Bart, a man,  wants to establish a sexual relationship with a female.
moral_action: Bart asks Ann what women like to see in dating profiles, because Bart has decided to date online.
moral_consequence: Bart maintains harmony in the office with no one resentful or exploited and also gets a date.
immoral_action: Bart uses his status to request sexual favors from Ann, who obliges him for a promotion.
immoral_consequence: The men in the office resent Ann for not earning her position, and all employees regard Bart as abusive which ends with Bart fired.

norm: It's bad to go to prison.
situation: Thomas works as an account and has many wealthy clients.
intention: Thomas needs to pay off some gambling debts.
moral_action: Thomas gets a loan to pay off his gambling debt and joins gamblers anonymous.
moral_consequence: Thomas is able to overcome his problem.
immoral_action: Thomas decides to embezzle funds from his clients in order to pay off his debt.
immoral_consequence: Thomas is arrested for his crime.

norm: It's good to report criminals.
situation: Judy discovers that her new boyfriend is a drug dealer and becomes scared of him.
intention: Judy wants to dump her new boyfriend.
moral_action: Judy calls the police and tells them everything she knows about her boyfriend's drug dealing.
moral_consequence: Judy's boyfriend is arrested by the police and does not sell drugs to any young girl.
immoral_action: Judy leaves in the middle of the night and travels to another state to stay with her aunt.
immoral_consequence: Judy's boyfriend sells drugs to a young girl who overdoses and dies.

norm: You should answer the phone when your parents call.
situation: Sally is really busy at home doing a bunch of housework and her phone rings with the caller ID showing it is her parents.
intention: Sally wants to finish her housework.
moral_action: Sally knows that her parents could be calling about something important, so she picks up the phone while continuing her house work with her free hand.
moral_consequence: Sally's parents inform her about something that is extremely important and Sally is glad she answered.
immoral_action: Sally decides to ignore the call from her parents as to not interrupt the housework she is doing.
immoral_consequence: Sally misses important, time sensitive, family news that her parents were trying to contact her about.

norm: It's good to support your country.
situation: Jen is American and attending a live hockey game between Americans and Germans.
intention: Jen wants to cheer on the game.
moral_action: Jen cheers for her American team whenever they  perform well.
moral_consequence: Jen finds friends amongst the audience and has a good time.
immoral_action: Jen cheers for the German team every time they score a goal.
immoral_consequence: The audience around Jen scold Jen, and Jen feels upset.

norm: You shouldn't badmouth your teachers.
situation: Theodore is in a classroom which is taught by Miss Landers.
intention: Theodore wants to be noticed.
moral_action: Theodore makes the other kids laugh but always treats Miss Landers with respect.
moral_consequence: When Theodore is struggling in math, Miss Landers gives him extra help and he passes the class.
immoral_action: Theodore makes smart aleck comments and talks about Miss Landers behind his back.
immoral_consequence: Theodore is not given any leeway or second chances by Miss Landers when he fails a test and must repeat the grade.

norm: It's expected that you complete your assignments in school.
situation: Paul got home from school and was doing his homework when he saw his brother playing basketball in the driveway.
intention: Paul wants to play basketball.
moral_action: Paul finishes the rest of his homework and then goes outside to play with his brother.
moral_consequence: Paul gets a sticker on his homework assignment when he turns it into his teacher.
immoral_action: Paul skips the rest of his homework and goes outside to play basketball with his brother.
immoral_consequence: Paul gets to school without his assignment and his teacher makes him stay in for recess.

norm: You shouldn't be too hard on your employees
situation: A person that works for Bob comes in five minutes late one morning.
intention: Bob wants to let his employee know that he doesn't like them being late.
moral_action: Bob explains that it's important for everyone to be on time and asks the employee to make an effort not to be late.
moral_consequence: The employee thinks Bob's reaction to their lateness is fair, and they try not to be late again.
immoral_action: Bob yells at the employee and threatens to fire them if they are even one minute late in the future.
immoral_consequence: Bob's employee thinks Bob's reaction was way to harsh, and they start to dislike working for him.

norm: It is okay to report workplace problems to the manager.
situation: Brandy is at her office and lately she's been struggling with depression and her work has been suffering.
intention: Brandy wants to talk to someone about her work problems.
moral_action: Brandy goes into her manager's office and explains to the manager that she's having issues.
moral_consequence: Brandy is given support and advice by the manager as well as accommodation from the employer to help her improve.
immoral_action: Brandy calls her friend Jenny and tells her all about her work problems and seeks advice.
immoral_consequence: Brandy is given friendly but misguided advice by Jenny which leads Brandy to make bad decisions.

norm: It's bad to disobey the rules of the road.
situation: Kate is driving to the store and notices there is heavy traffic on the freeway.
intention: Kate wants to get to the store quickly.
moral_action: Kate looks for alternate faster routes on her GPS.
moral_consequence: Kate finds a faster route on the GPS.
immoral_action: Kate pulls onto the shoulder of the freeway to try to bypass the traffic.
immoral_consequence: Kate gets pulled over and given a ticket by the police.

norm: It's good to have respect for teachers.
situation: Billy is a middle school student and his math teacher asks him to demonstrate a problem on the blackboard.
intention: Billy wants to respond to his teacher's request.
moral_action: Billy complies with the request and demonstrates how to solve the math problem on the blackboard.
moral_consequence: Billy is praised for his effort and pariticpation and feels proud of himself.
immoral_action: Billy tells the teacher to buzz off and proceeds to ignore her.
immoral_consequence: Billy is sent to the principal's office for talking back to the teacher in a disrespectful manner.

norm: You shouldn't break the rules of an establishment.
situation: Valerie, a smoker, is in a restaurant with a no-smoking policy and suddenly has the urge to smoke.
intention: Valerie wants to smoke a cigarette.
moral_action: Valerie goes outside to smoke at a designated smoking area.
moral_consequence: Valerie smokes her cigarette and is able to finish her meal.
immoral_action: Valerie lights up a cigarette at the table and smokes.
immoral_consequence: Valerie is kicked out of the restaurant and banned from returning.

norm: It's bad to intentionally disrupt others.
situation: Kevin is at a science talk and is curious about something the scientist said.
intention: Kevin wants to ask the scientist a question.
moral_action: Kevin waits until the scientist is done with the talk to ask his question at the Q&A section.
moral_consequence: The scientist will listen to Kevin's question and Kevin will get an answer.
immoral_action: Kevin asks the question in the middle of the scientist's talk while the scientist is speaking.
immoral_consequence: The scientist will become annoyed and Kevin will be asked to leave the talk.

norm: You should respect your elders wishes.
situation: Dan's dad asked Dan to pull the plug if he ever forget who Dan is, and it actually happened.
intention: Dan wants to make a decision on his dad's life.
moral_action: Dan tells the doctors to pull the plug on his dad.
moral_consequence: Dan's dad doesn't have to suffer anymore, and this makes Dan relieved.
immoral_action: Dan doesn't tell the doctors what his dad wanted wants his dad to stay alive.
immoral_consequence: Dan's dad continues to suffer, and Dan doesn't like seeing his dad like this.

norm: It's bad to fool around when you're in class.
situation: Alice is in math class with her best friend Roger.
intention: Alice wants to tell Roger a joke she just thought of.
moral_action: Alice writes the joke down, so she doesn't forget it and tells Roger after class.
moral_consequence: Alice and Roger share a laugh together out of class.
immoral_action: Alice passes notes with Roger in class to tell him the joke.
immoral_consequence: Alice and Roger get caught and get detention.

norm: It is important to try in school.
situation: John is in first year of college.
intention: John wants to make the most of his college experience.
moral_action: John studies hard for the classes in which he is struggling while also finding the time to meet new friends.
moral_consequence: John maintains a healthy grade point average and makes lasting connections.
immoral_action: John spends time socializing and partying with all the new friends he has made.
immoral_consequence: John fails too many classes to remain in college and has to go back home.

norm: It's good to excel at things in school.
situation: Liam is a student in high school.
intention: Liam wants to sign up for classes.
moral_action: Liam signs up for honors classes that demand a lot from him.
moral_consequence: Liam learns that hard work and intelligence pay off in life.
immoral_action: Liam signs up for the easiest classes where he can put in the minimum amount of work.
immoral_consequence: Liam doesn't push himself and limits his future prospects.

norm: It's bad to repeat a grade multiple times.
situation: Luna is struggling in school and has already repeated her junior year because of bad grades.
intention: Luna wants to catch up in school.
moral_action: Luna decides to sign up for summer school during her summer break.
moral_consequence: Luna's peers think she has made a responsible decision and is becoming mature.
immoral_action: Luna decides to repeat the grade another time in order to learn the material.
immoral_consequence: Luna's peers develop a bad opinion of her due to her being stuck in 11th grade.

norm: It's bad to give people predatory advice.
situation: Daiki's best friend Hoshi asks him how to approach his crush online.
intention: Daiki wants to be helpful.
moral_action: Daiki arranges a meeting between Hoshi and his crush in an online chat room.
moral_consequence: Hoshi makes friends with everyone in the chat room.
immoral_action: Daiki tells Hoshi that he should send messages about her personal posts multiple times to start a conversation.
immoral_consequence: Hoshi is blocked by his crush.

norm: It's rude to force someone to go to therapy.
situation: Joseph and his wife Linda are talking and Linda is complaining to Joseph that she feels a little blah lately.
intention: Joseph wants to suggest a solution to Linda for her troubles.
moral_action: Joseph tells Linda that perhaps they could do some fun activities together to get her back into the swing of things.
moral_consequence: Linda is happy to hear Joseph cares about her well being and is trying to come up with ways to make her feel better.
immoral_action: Joseph tells Linda that the only solution is to go therapy and that she must go or their marriage will be in peril.
immoral_consequence: Linda feels extreme pressure now to get better because her marriage is on the line, causing her massive stress.

norm: You should always help out your boss.
situation: Tom has been working for his company a few years and has not yet received a promotion.
intention: Tom wants to impress his boss.
moral_action: Tom works extra hard and helps his boss out whenever there is an opportunity to do so.
moral_consequence: Tom's boss recognizes his hard work and determination and rewards Tom with a promotion.
immoral_action: Tom starts dressing very nicely and showering his boss with compliments whenever possible.
immoral_consequence: Despite his efforts Tom's boss is not impressed and Tom is not promoted.

norm: You shouldn't disobey codes.
situation: Andy is a nurse in a hospital.
intention: Andy wants to do well at his next performance review.
moral_action: Andy asks what he can do to improve his work at the hospital.
moral_consequence: Andy is seen as proactive and gets a good review.
immoral_action: Andy hacks into the computer system to say he's working more hours than he is and violates the employee computer rules.
immoral_consequence: Andy looks like he's working the hardest but is caught breaking an employee code and is fired.

norm: It is unprofessional to mix person and professional life.
situation: Dave is an office manager and in front of the whole office Donna, his subordinate asks him on a date.
intention: Dave wants to respond to Donna's date offer.
moral_action: Dave tells Donna that he doesn't want to see her outside the office and that she must have the wrong idea about him.
moral_consequence: The staff at the office are delighted that they work for an obviously professional boss.
immoral_action: Dave tells Donna that he would love to take her out and maybe even be her boyfriend.
immoral_consequence: The staff at the office are disgusted at Dave and fear that if they don't do something drastic Donna will acquire power at the office.

norm: It's not okay to support a evil leader.
situation: A politician in Don's country wants to deport all Mexicans from his country.
intention: Don has to decide whether to vote for the politician.
moral_action: Don realizes that Mexicans have never bothered him and votes for someone else.
moral_consequence: The man has one less vote, and Don doesn't have to feel guilty.
immoral_action: Don realizes that he doesn't have any Mexican friends, so he votes for him.
immoral_consequence: The man ends up following through on his promise, and Don feels bad.

norm: You shouldn't prevent someone from taking a vacation.
situation: Ron's employee has asked him whether he can take a paid vacation during a busy week.
intention: Ron has to decide whether to reward his employee with a paid vacation.
moral_action: Rob gives his employee paid time off for the entire week.
moral_consequence: Rob's employee feels grateful and wants to work even harder when they come back.
immoral_action: Ron tells the employee that it would not be a good time for them to take a vacation.
immoral_consequence: Ron's employee continues to feel overwhelmed and thinks about finding a new job.

norm: It's bad to arrive too early.
situation: Ricky has been invited to Tonya's birthday party for Friday night and it's now Friday morning.
intention: Ricky wants to decide what time to head over to Tonya's house.
moral_action: Ricky decides to leave shortly after seven, so he can arrive at seven thirty for the party.
moral_consequence: Tonya is happy to see Ricky when he arrives and welcomes him inside excitedly.
immoral_action: Ricky decides that he'll leave right after breakfast at around nine thirty AM and get there by ten.
immoral_consequence: Tonya is unpleasantly surprised to see Ricky arrive in the AM for an evening party and feels uncomfortable having him there all day.

norm: You shouldn't open someone else's mailbox.
situation: Todd was waiting for a new credit card in the mail, but he didn't receive it.
intention: Todd wants to check if his neighbors received his mail instead.
moral_action: Todd asks his neighbors if they happened to receive any mail that was for him.
moral_consequence: Todd's neighbors think that he is polite, and Todd is able to find out if they received his mail.
immoral_action: Todd looks in his neighbor's mailbox without asking and goes through their mail.
immoral_consequence: Todd is invading people's privacy and could be in trouble if they find out.

norm: It's wrong to always be late.
situation: Jerry is meeting some friends out for dinner.
intention: Jerry wants to have a good time with his friends.
moral_action: Jerry makes sure to be on time and meets everyone at the agreed time.
moral_consequence: Jerry and his friends have a nice night out together.
immoral_action: Jerry takes his time getting ready and is an hour late to meet his friends out.
immoral_consequence: Jerry’s friends get annoyed and eat dinner without him.

norm: You shouldn't download files at work that aren't allowed.
situation: Karen is very bored while working without music, she feels it makes her work better too.
intention: Karen wants to get more music on her computer.
moral_action: Karen buys herself a Spotify membership, so she can have some more music while she works.
moral_consequence: Karen learns about some new artists she never knew, and works better too.
immoral_action: Karen looks up how to download music illegally, and decides to go ahead and do it.
immoral_consequence: Karen's boss finds out she used the internet to download something illegal and fires her.

norm: It's good to take what someone says seriously.
situation: Andrew is talking with his friend Brian when Brian says he's had thoughts of hurting himself.
intention: Andrew wants to make Brian feel better.
moral_action: Andrew asks Brian if he has talked to a counselor or therapist about this, and offers to go with him to therapy if he wants that.
moral_consequence: Brian feels cared for and gets the help he needs.
immoral_action: Andrew laughs and says that Brian should just not do that.
immoral_consequence: Brian no longer feels comfortable opening up to Andrew.

norm: You should be more respectful of people's property.
situation: Jack and his friends are playing in his backyard on a sunny day.
intention: Jack wants to have fun with his friends.
moral_action: Jack sets up an inflatable pool and swims in the pool with his friends.
moral_consequence: His neighbor feels secure about Jack's non-threatening activity.
immoral_action: Jack tosses a ball around with his friends, and he accidentally hit a window of his neighbor.
immoral_consequence: His neighbor blames him for the damage and asks to pay for it to fix the window.

norm: People shouldn't get drunk before class.
situation: Charlene sitting around her dorm room and is bored before class.
intention: Charlene wants to find something to do to kill time before until class.
moral_action: Charlene decides to read a chapter of a novel that she is reading to pass the time before going to class.
moral_consequence: Charlene is attentive in class and learns the subjects presented with ease.
immoral_action: Charlene decides to drink some leftover liquor from the weekend and then heads to class.
immoral_consequence: Charlene throws up in the middle of class and is very embarrassed.

norm: It's wrong to obtain merchandise without paying for it.
situation: Joss is shopping for groceries and opens a package of cookies and starts eating as he shops.
intention: Joss wants to eat something because he's hungry.
moral_action: Joss finishes the cookies, and gives the empty wrapper to the cashier to scan as he checks out.
moral_consequence: The security guard sees Joss pay for the cookies he ate, and doesn't need to take any action.
immoral_action: Joss finishes the cookies and throws the empty package into a trash can before checking out.
immoral_consequence: A security guard sees him discard the wrapper, and there's a big scene at the checkout lanes; Joss can't shop there any more.

norm: You shouldn't tell someone who they can hang out with.
situation: Jake is at home watching TV when his wife Anne tells him she's off to see her friend Adam.
intention: Jake wants to respond to Anne.
moral_action: Jake tells Anne to have a great time and to say hi to Adam for him.
moral_consequence: Anne is delighted that Jake greeted her before she left and that he is thoughtful enough to think of Adam, too.
immoral_action: Jake tells Anne that he does not allow her to hang out with other men and that she cannot see Adam.
immoral_consequence: Anne is infuriated that Jake is trying to tell her who she can be friends with and thinks he's overly controlling.

norm: You shouldn't do drugs on the job.
situation: Lisa has been taking her opiate painkillers at an increasing rate and today she has work.
intention: Lisa wants to do her job.
moral_action: Lisa is in pain, but decides to wait until she gets home to take her pain medicine because it makes her foggy.
moral_consequence: Lisa is able to perform her work tasks and keep up a professional appearance.
immoral_action: Lisa is in pain so takes some more opiate painkillers.
immoral_consequence: Lisa is obviously inebriated and her boss notices and fires her for being high on the job.

norm: It's wrong to trespass on other people's property.
situation: Josh was playing baseball with his friends when the ball went into his neighbor's back yard.
intention: Josh wants the ball back.
moral_action: Josh knocks on his neighbor's door and asks him if he can get his ball.
moral_consequence: Josh's neighbor goes to the backyard and gets the ball for Josh himself.
immoral_action: Josh climbs over the fence and sneaks into his neighbor's yard to get the ball.
immoral_consequence: Josh's neighbor sees him in the yard and calls the cops to report him for trespassing.

norm: It is wrong to control other people's behavior.
situation: Sal's girlfriend spent a lot of time on the phone with her friends.
intention: Sal wanted his girlfriend to spend more time with him.
moral_action: Sal decided to plan dates for him and his girlfriend in advance to see if he could pry her from the phone.
moral_consequence: Sal and his girlfriend ended up spending much more time together becoming closer in their relationship.
immoral_action: Sal trying told his girlfriend she had to stop talking to her friends.
immoral_consequence: Sals girlfriend feeling pressured decided to break up with sal because he was being to controling.

norm: It is controlling to dictate a partner's friendships.
situation: John noticed that his girlfriend, Mary, hangs out with a lot of guy friends from work, which made him worried that she could leave him for one of them.
intention: John wants to do anything to keep Mary as his girlfriend.
moral_action: John sits down Mary and tells her about his jealous emotions, he doesn't ask her to stop seeing them, he just wants her to understand what he is feeling.
moral_consequence: John and Mary attend counseling together, John eventually get's over his jealousy, and they end up getting married one day.
immoral_action: John tells Mary that he doesn't like her hanging out with a bunch of guys and tells her she can only have girl friends outside of work.
immoral_consequence: Mary decides that John is too controllers and ends their relationship.

norm: It's rude to expect gifts from others.
situation: John's mother just returned from a business trip abroad.
intention: John wants to greet his mother.
moral_action: John runs up to his mother, hugs her and asks her how her trip was.
moral_consequence: John's mother is very touched that he was so glad to see her and that he was curious about her trip.
immoral_action: John runs up to his mother, hugs her and asks her what she brought him from abroad.
immoral_consequence: John's mother tells him that she didn't have time to go shopping and now she feels very guilty.

norm: You shouldn't judge someone who is abstinent.
situation: John has been dating Jane for a few months when she tells him that she won't have sex before marriage.
intention: John wants to tell Jane how he feels about her admission.
moral_action: John respects Jane's position on having a sexual relationship.
moral_consequence: John is free to decide if he wants to pursue a long term relationship with Jane.
immoral_action: John calls Jane a tease and tries pressuring her to have sex with him.
immoral_consequence: John loses any chance he had of being with Jane again.

norm: It's wrong to dictate how people should organize their wedding.
situation: Miranda is helping her daughter Natasha plan her wedding.
intention: Miranda wants Natasha's wedding to be special.
moral_action: Miranda acts as Natasha's assistant giving her tips along the way.
moral_consequence: Natasha shares a tearful thank you hug with Miranda before the wedding.
immoral_action: Miranda micromanages the entire process and invites a musical act Natasha hates to perform at the reception.
immoral_consequence: While Natasha's wedding is spectacular, Natasha is unhappy with the results.

norm: It's wrong to tell your roommate what to do.
situation: Jill has moved to a new city and is living with a roommate.
