intention: Jill wants to schedule cleaning the apartment.
moral_action: Jill asks her roommate to help her pick dates to clean the apartment.
moral_consequence: Jill's roommate thinks Jill is considerate and wants to cooperate more with Jill.
immoral_action: Jill tells her roommate the apartment cleaning is during her roommate's birthday.
immoral_consequence: Jill's roommate moves out and Jill has to pay all the rent.

norm: It's good to listen to your parents.
situation: Colin is at home with his mother for the day instead of at school.
intention: Colin wants to find something entertaining.
moral_action: Colin says that she is bored and asks his mom for an idea of what to do.
moral_consequence: Colin's mom allows to hours of game time if he teaches her the game, he agrees, and they have a fun day.
immoral_action: Colin ignores his moms requests and plays video games throughout the day instead of the one hour of allowed game time.
immoral_consequence: Colin is sent to bed early and has game time taken away for one week.

norm: It is good to apologize for your actions.
situation: Alvin and his wife Lucy had a big fight last night in which Alvin said some mean things.
intention: Alvin wants to address the situation with his wife.
moral_action: Alvin apologizes for the mean things he said and asks Lucy how he can make it up to her.
moral_consequence: Lucy is grateful to hear this from Alvin and apologizes for her own part in the argument.
immoral_action: Alvin tells Lucy that the fight was her fault and refuses to apologize for his words.
immoral_consequence: Lucy decides she cannot be with a person like Alvin and files for divorce.

norm: You shouldn't be too strict with your kids.
situation: Hannah has two children who she loves.
intention: Hannah wants her children to grow up to be successful.
moral_action: Hannah models good habits and encourages her children to do well but gives them freedom to explore who they are.
moral_consequence: Hannah's children are grateful to their mother for being understanding and a good influence.
immoral_action: Hannah tightly control's her children's time and has many rules for their behavior.
immoral_consequence: Hannah's children resent her and rebel after leaving home.

norm: It's rude to beg people for things.
situation: Dan is hungry and goes into the kitchen where his mom is cleaning the countertop.
intention: Dan wants some food.
moral_action: Dan opens the fridge and gets some yogurt to eat.
moral_consequence: Dan enjoys the food and satisfies his hunger.
immoral_action: Dan gets on his knees and begs for food, telling his mom that he's starving and won't survive.
immoral_consequence: Dan's mom gets annoyed with him and sends him to his room without food.

norm: It's good to discipline children.
situation: Carol and Tom have two children together but Tom always lets the children get away with they do wrong.
intention: Carol wants the children to be better behaved.
moral_action: Carol talks to Tom about the importance of making sure the children act good, and they act as a team when addressing the children's behavior issues.
moral_consequence: The children learn they will have to face punishment when they intentionally do wrong and become productive adult members of society.
immoral_action: Carol follows along with Tom's preferences in not disciplining the children, leaving it to teachers and family to make them act better.
immoral_consequence: The children grow up getting into a large amount of trouble and don't respect Carol and Tom.

norm: You shouldn't get too demanding with your friends.
situation: Jerry's car broke down and its repair won't be finished within a few days, and his friend Aaron lives near him.
intention: Jerry wants to get a ride to work.
moral_action: Jerry calls Uber to get to work everyday before his car gets fixed.
moral_consequence: Aaron offers to give Jerry a ride if he needs one.
immoral_action: Jerry asks Aaron to give him a ride to work everyday before his car gets fixed, even though their work places are not close by.
immoral_consequence: Aaron feels tired and exhausted for the long drive everyday.

norm: It's wrong to not do what your employer asks.
situation: Dennis is stocking cereal at the grocery store and his manager tells him he has to finish stocking it before he goes on lunch break.
intention: Dennis wants to eat his lunch.
moral_action: Dennis finishes stocking the cereal and then goes to the lunch room to eat.
moral_consequence: Dennis' manager finds Dennis in the lunch room, thanks him for stocking the cereal, and joins him for lunch.
immoral_action: Dennis goes to the lunch room as soon as his manager is out of sight, without finishing stocking the cereal.
immoral_consequence: Dennis' manager finds Dennis in the lunch room and yells at him for not doing his job.

norm: People should not violate the laws concerning illegal deeds.
situation: John works as a bookkeeper for a small company where he handles a lot of cash transactions.
intention: John wants to have more money.
moral_action: John asks his employer for a pay raise.
moral_consequence: John receives a pay raise.
immoral_action: John embezzles money from his employer.
immoral_consequence: John is caught, is prosecuted, and goes to prison.

norm: You shouldn't feed your family's pets unless you have permission.
situation: Tyler is a child whose family has a pet dog that always looks at him during dinner.
intention: Tyler wants to feed the dog.
moral_action: Tyler asks his parents if he can give the dog food.
moral_consequence: Tyler learns from his parents what foods can be given and when and the dog eats food it is supposed to eat.
immoral_action: Tyler gives the dog table scraps after dinner without telling anyone.
immoral_consequence: The dog suffers health problems from eating odd foods and Tyler never learns what foods are acceptable to give.

norm: It's good to do what your boss tells you.
situation: Ryan is working at his job.
intention: Ryan wants to get a raise from his employer.
moral_action: Ryan goes by the standard procedures given to him by his boss and beats his sales goal.
moral_consequence: Ryan is rewarded with a raise for doing a good job.
immoral_action: Ryan skirts company policy to try to get more customers and reach his sales goal.
immoral_consequence: Ryan is let go for not following the rules that his employer laid out.

norm: It's good to get your own place to live.
situation: Carol has been living with her parents for all of her life.
intention: Carol wants some privacy.
moral_action: Carol gets a job and finds her own apartment to live in.
moral_consequence: Carol is finally able to enjoy the privacy of having her own place.
immoral_action: Carol changes the locks on all the doors in her parents' house.
immoral_consequence: Carol's parents kick her out of the house and change the locks again.

norm: People are expected to be punctual.
situation: Al is almost running late for work, in his car in traffic.
intention: Al wants a quick pick-me-up to get him ready for the workday.
moral_action: Al arrives on time and gets coffee from the machine at work.
moral_consequence: All at the office are productive and proceed normally without interruption and efficiently.
immoral_action: Al stops for a specialty coffee drink at a restaurant.
immoral_consequence: Al is late for work, team members who depend on Al do not get work done, upsetting the team members' progress.

norm: It's encouraged for authority figures to use their power wisely.
situation: Jim is the CEO of a company and has to make a choice on who to promote.
intention: Jim wants to promote an employee.
moral_action: Jim promotes strictly based on performance.
moral_consequence: Jim's company gains a motivated high performance employee.
immoral_action: Jim plays favorites and promotes his friend.
immoral_consequence: The other candidates become demotivated and eventually leaves the company.

norm: You shouldn't offend people.
situation: It is amateur comedy night at a local night club.
intention: Jo wants to win the audience members over with her jokes.
moral_action: Jo made a joke mocking ableist attitudes towards those with disabilities.
moral_consequence: More than a few audience members bought her comedy CD on the way out.
immoral_action: Jo told a joke mocking disabled people's struggles.
immoral_consequence: Some of the audience members got up and left the show.

norm: You shouldn't flirt with coworkers.
situation: Sypha goes to work and notices that Jack the new IT guy is cute.
intention: Sypha wants to decide if she should flirt with Jack.
moral_action: Sypha decides that would be too dangerous, so she just continues working.
moral_consequence: Sypha's boss stops by and tells her she's doing a good job and to keep it up.
immoral_action: Sypha starts flirting with Jack while they are both at work.
immoral_consequence: Before Jack can respond, their boss reprimands her for flirting at work.

norm: It is good to observe traditions
situation: Tanis was the black sheep of the family growing up but is visiting home for the holidays.
intention: Tanis wants to enjoy the holidays.
moral_action: Tanis participates in all the family actvities that she used to skip out on when she was younger.
moral_consequence: Tanis bonds with her family some after all these years and enjoys it.
immoral_action: When the family is getting ready to do what they do every year for the holidays, Tanis sneaks out to go to the bar in the next town over.
immoral_consequence: Tanis remains the black sheep of the family and misses out on a chance to bond with her family.

norm: It's rude to not eat food your parent has prepared.
situation: Jeff goes downstairs to see that his parents have made soggy, spongy meatloaf for dinner.
intention: Jeff wants his mother to think that he enjoys her cooking.
moral_action: Jeff scarfs down the meatloaf quickly, ignoring the nasty texture.
moral_consequence: Jeff's mother sees him eating and beams with pride.
immoral_action: Jeff feeds the dinner to the dog when his parents aren't looking.
immoral_consequence: He fools his mother into thinking that he likes meatloaf.

norm: You shouldn't distract the guard when entering the office building.
situation: Mario often chats with the guard at his office building, and they will sometimes exchange jokes in the morning.
intention: Mario wants to tell the guard a new joke.
moral_action: Mario tells the guard a short joke before entering the building.
moral_consequence: Mario and the guard have a short laugh before going back to their respective jobs.
immoral_action: Mario tells the guard a really long joke which takes his attention off of his job,.
immoral_consequence: While the guard is listening to Mario's joke, an unauthorized man enters the office building.

norm: Bosses are not supposed to threaten their employees.
situation: Dave is a manager of an office and notices an employee, Rick is under performing.
intention: Dave wants to say something to Rick to motivate him to perform better.
moral_action: Dave tells Rick that he just wants to remind him that the highest performers are eligible for a bonus at year's end.
moral_consequence: Rick is happy to hear that there might be a bonus in his future if he can perform a little better.
immoral_action: Dave tells Rick that if he doesn't get his act together Dave will have no choice but to fire him.
immoral_consequence: Rick is nervous and gets motivated out of fear but ends up hating Dave and being resentful.

norm: It's good to stop people from breaking the law
situation: Mary sees her friend Rosa about to break into a car across the street.
intention: Mary wants to help Rosa.
moral_action: Mary stops Rosa from taking the car and calms her down.
moral_consequence: Mary gives Rosa a ride to where she needs to go to help her out.
immoral_action: Mary looks the other way and when she looks again, Rosa and the car are gone.
immoral_consequence: Mary's stuck keeping this a secret for the rest of her life.

norm: It is good for parents to set rules.
situation: Jack's kids are always begging to be allowed to walk to the corner store a mile away, even though there are no sidewalks to safely walk on.
intention: Jack wants his kids to stop nagging him about walking to the store.
moral_action: Jack shows his kids a gruesome video about what happens to pedestrians who get hit by cars.
moral_consequence: Jack's kids, terrified, finally give up on walking to the store and start spending their allowances on Amazon.
immoral_action: Jack gives in and lets his kids start walking to the store on the main road.
immoral_consequence: A car comes around a corner too quickly and hits one of Jack's kids, breaking multiple bones.

norm: It is wrong to force your roommate to switch dorms.
situation: Finn has been assigned to live with Reed who he does not get along with.
intention: Finn wants to separate from Reed.
moral_action: Finn and Reed go to the residence hall administrator and request to be put in different rooms.
moral_consequence: Finn and Reed are able to get new roommates who they tolerate better.
immoral_action: Finn stops cleaning his side of the room and behaves strangely towards Reed in order to drive Reed out of the room.
immoral_consequence: Reed is forced out and Finn develops a poor reputation in his residence hall.

norm: It's irresponsible to forget to scan items as a  cashier.
situation: Stan works as a cashier when someone comes with three carts full of groceries, just before his break.
intention: Stan wants to go on break quickly.
moral_action: Stan calls someone over for help, so they can get through the carts quickly and accurately.
moral_consequence: Stan's draw balances out at the end of the day.
immoral_action: Stan scans the items as fast as humanly possible, missing a few here and there.
immoral_consequence: Stan's drawer is short $15 at the end of the day, and he gets in trouble.

norm: It's wrong to not accept others as they are.
situation: Mina's son is very introverted, so he's not very popular in school.
intention: Mina wants to say something to her son.
moral_action: Mina tells his son that he should do whatever that makes him comfortable.
moral_consequence: Mina's son feels good that his mom accepts him for who he is.
immoral_action: Mina tells her son that he needs to start talking more.
immoral_consequence: Mina's son feels uncomfortable talking a lot so he doesn't appreciate the advice.

norm: It's rude to push your religious beliefs on others.
situation: John, who is a passionate atheist, is at dinner with a large group of his friends.
intention: John wants to make conversation.
moral_action: John has an open discussion with his friends about the meaning of faith and it's purpose in people's lives.
moral_consequence: John's friends appreciate the interesting conversation topic, and they all have a good time.
immoral_action: John goes on and on about how religious people are nutjobs and atheists are much smarter than them.
immoral_consequence: John's friends find his line of conversation insulting and bigoted.

norm: It is wrong not to report illegal activity.
situation: Conner is filling his car with gas when he observes a hit and run collision in the gas station parking lot.
intention: Conner wants to get home quickly.
moral_action: Conner remembers the license plate of the car that caused the hit and run and calls the police from his car while driving home to report it.
moral_consequence: The police are able to locate the car that caused the damage, thereby saving the victim thousands of dollars in repair costs.
immoral_action: Conner drives home and ignores the hit and run because he doesn't want to wait around for the police to show up.
immoral_consequence: The owner of the car that was hit has to pay thousands of dollars in repair costs out of his own pocket.

norm: Telling someone else how to do their job is rude.
situation: Dave went to work and saw his coworkers screwing up.
intention: Dave wants the team to get thier work done.
moral_action: Dave offers his advice as suggestions to the supervisor.
moral_consequence: The supervisor notes that while most of them don't help, a few of them do and implements them.
immoral_action: Dave goes around telling everyone what they are doing wrong.
immoral_consequence: Dave's coworkers all start telling Dave what he's doing wrong too.

norm: You shouldn't be late for work or school.
situation: John has to go to work now and it's time to leave, but he sees a TV show he likes that has 15 minutes remaining.
intention: John wants to make a decision whether to watch the show.
moral_action: John decided that he doesn't have time to do that and decides to just skip it this time and not watch it.
moral_consequence: John's boss feels no extra stress from John that day since John came to work as usual.
immoral_action: John decides to stay at home and watch the show which will guarantee he won't make it to work on time.
immoral_consequence: John's boss is upset that John is so flippant about his work responsibilities causing his boss stress.

norm: You shouldn't disobey others.
situation: Neal's parents have tried to teach him to have responsible sex.
intention: Neal wants to have lots of sex.
moral_action: Neal always wears a condom before he has sex.
moral_consequence: Neal gets to have lots of sex.
immoral_action: Neal has a lot of unprotected sex.
immoral_consequence: Neal increases his chances of getting an STD.

norm: It's morally unacceptable to support a terrorist organization.
situation: Ben has money to spare to donate to an organization pushing for world change.
intention: Ben wants to pick an organization to donate to that will change the world.
moral_action: Ben chooses to donate his money to Doctors Without Borders.
moral_consequence: The money is used to help people in disadvantaged countries and helps save lives.
immoral_action: Ben chooses to donate to ISIS and sends his money to them.
immoral_consequence: ISIS uses the money to recruit more fighters, who then kill and oppress people in the Middle East.

norm: You should always obey the dress code of a dance.
situation: Susie is invited to a school dance by one of her classmates.
intention: Susie wants to attend the dance.
moral_action: Susie wears a tasteful dress to the dance.
moral_consequence: Susie has a great time at the dance.
immoral_action: Susie wears a bikini to the school dance.
immoral_consequence: Susie is kicked out of the dance for violating the dress code.

norm: It is disrespectful not to obey one's parents
situation: Steve is watching cartoons in the living room when his mother comes in and asks if he can take the family dog for a walk.
intention: Steve wants to answer his mother's request.
moral_action: Steve agrees and takes the dog for a nice walk around the neighborhood.
moral_consequence: Steve is praised by his mother for helping out and enjoys the walk.
immoral_action: Steve tells his mother he's busy and to quit bothering him.
immoral_consequence: Steve is scolded by his mother for not helping out.

norm: It's rude to dismiss the teaching profession.
situation: John meets Peter at a charity fundraiser and Peter tells John that he is a high school teacher.
intention: John wants to respond to Peter's statement.
moral_action: John tells Peter that he's doing a thankless job that benefits society greatly and that teachers don't get enough credit.
moral_consequence: Peter is delighted that John is so understanding of what he does every single day.
immoral_action: John scoffs at Peter and tells him that school is an evil place that instills bad liberal ideas into children's heads.
immoral_consequence: Peter is sad that John is being disrespectful towards his Job and is shocked at John's narrow mind.

norm: It's bad to be controlling with your partner's freedoms.
situation: Jeff's girlfriend Jenny has a lot of guy friends that she enjoys hanging out with, making Jeff jealous.
intention: Jeff wants to not be bothered by Jenny's behavior.
moral_action: Jeff decides to speak to a psychologist about his jealousy issues.
moral_consequence: Jenny appreciates Jeff's changes in behavior after he started receiving counseling.
immoral_action: Jeff tells Jenny that if she doesn't stop seeing these other guys he's going to leave her.
immoral_consequence: Jenny is bitter that Jeff doesn't want her to have a good time, and the relationship soon sours.

norm: It is wrong to go somewhere without being invited.
situation: Susan's friend Marissa is getting married soon but Susan still hasn't received an invitation.
intention: Susan wants to attend Marissa's wedding.
moral_action: Susan drops subtle hints to Marissa about her desire to attend the wedding.
moral_consequence: Marissa ends up inviting Susan to the wedding, and she has a great time at it.
immoral_action: Susan shows up to the wedding unannounced without having been invited.
immoral_consequence: Susan is denied entry to the wedding because she isn't on the guest list.

norm: You shouldn't take money from someone else.
situation: Sal is on the bus when he spots a twenty dollar bill sticking out of a sleeping mans pocket.
intention: Sal wants to go see a movie.
moral_action: Sal resists the urge to steal and buys a movie ticket with his own money.
moral_consequence: Sal feels good about himself for not taking something that doesn't belong to him and being self sufficient.
immoral_action: Sal grabs the twenty dollar bill and uses it to see a movie at the local theater.
immoral_consequence: Sal feels like a scumbag for taking money instead of just buying the ticket with his own money.

norm: You shouldn't tear people to pieces.
situation: Barry suffers from strong urges to hurt a woman who lives next to him.
intention: Barry wants to get relief from his urges.
moral_action: Barry goes to therapy and talks about his issues and works to improve his outlook.
moral_consequence: Barry stops fixating on the woman and gets his life together, and the woman doesn't lose her life.
immoral_action: Barry abducts the woman and kills her and cuts her up into chunks.
immoral_consequence: The woman loses her life, and Barry becomes a wanted criminal.

norm: You shouldn't sleep with someone for money.
situation: Rachel is a struggling college student trying to pay for her tuition out of pocket, and a man approaches her offering to pay for sex.
intention: Rachel needs to come up with money.
moral_action: Rachel decides that she'd rather not take up the man's offer, and instead does gig work on Mechanical Turk.
moral_consequence: Rachel makes tons of money writing short stories for awesome requesters.
immoral_action: Rachel decides that prostitution is the most efficient way to make ends meet.
immoral_consequence: Rachel is arrested by the man, who is an undercover cop.

norm: It’s bad to not go to work.
situation: Tyler wakes up with a headache and has to go to work.
intention: Tyler wants to get rid of the headache.
moral_action: Tyler take some medicine for the headache and goes to work.
moral_consequence: The headache goes away and Tyler is able to work.
immoral_action: Tyler skips work and sleeps all day until the headache goes away.
immoral_consequence: Tyler gets fired for not going into work or calling out sick.

norm: You shouldn't fall in love with your professor.
situation: Sam was taking an English in the local college which was instructed by handsome instructor.
intention: Sam wants to talk to the instructor.
moral_action: Sam waits until after class and asks the professor if he could recommend someone for tutoring.
moral_consequence: The instructor gives Sam a slip of paper with the name and number of an English tutor on campus.
immoral_action: Sam waits until after class and asks the instructor if he wants to join her on a date.
immoral_consequence: The instructor sees her proposal as indecent and makes her drop the class.

norm: It's wrong to drop out of school.
situation: Greg has been put on the spot by his teacher who asks him a basic question he cannot answer which earns him a reputation for being unintelligent.
intention: Greg wants to avoid struggling in class in front of others.
moral_action: Greg signs up for some one on one lessons to improve his understanding of the material.
moral_consequence: Greg's parents consider him to be responsible kid for making this decision.
immoral_action: Greg decides to drop out of school since he's so far behind.
immoral_consequence: Greg's parents think he will not make anything of himself.

norm: It's wrong to miss class.
situation: Jane is between classes and discovers that she's got three missed calls on her cell phone.
intention: Jane wants to check her missed calls.
moral_action: Jane asks the teacher if she could have a few minutes to make an important phone call before class.
moral_consequence: Jane is permitted to come into class late and her teacher lets her know what she missed.
immoral_action: Jane leaves the classroom building to call people back instead of going to class.
immoral_consequence: Jane misses important review information being passed out and isn't able to prepare for a test.

norm: You should not fail academically.
situation: Jordan plays senior high school football but because of a grueling practice schedule, he falls behind in math class which he needs to pass in order to graduate.
intention: Jordan wants to prioritize what's important to him.
moral_action: Jordan cuts back on his practice schedule to work on his math.
moral_consequence: Jordan passes his class and goes on to graduate and play college football.
immoral_action: Jordan ignores his studies because he feels football is his calling in life.
immoral_consequence: Jordan fails math class and has to repeat the school year, and now can't play college football.

norm: It's rude to be arrogant about your intelligence.
situation: Amanda was talking to her friend Steve who was very smart.
intention: Steve wants to keep talking to Amanda.
moral_action: Steve talks about the Dunning-Krueger effect where those who think they know the most often know very little.
moral_consequence: Amanda is amazed at the concept and asks to know more about it.
immoral_action: Steve talks to Amanda about his Ph.D. and how his IQ is 200.
immoral_consequence: Steve's conversation bores Amanda while she's listening to him.

norm: It's good to go to class.
situation: Marcus is a college student who went out to party the night before and now feels tired.
intention: Marcus wants to catch up on sleep.
moral_action: Marcus takes a nap after his classes are over.
moral_consequence: Marcus feels refreshed and prepared to study.
immoral_action: Marcus skips his classes so he can sleep all day.
immoral_consequence: Marcus falls behind in class and messes up his sleep cycle.

norm: You should report a neighbor that is breaking the law.
situation: Steve notices that his neighbor Chet seems to be selling drugs out of his house to all sorts of strange people.
intention: Steve wants Chet to stop selling drugs.
moral_action: Steve calls the police and reports Chet's illegal drug activity.
moral_consequence: Chet is arrested and sentenced to jail for selling drugs and the neighborhood returns to normal.
immoral_action: Steve knocks on Chet's door and asks him to stop selling drugs.
immoral_consequence: Chet threatens Steve, tells him to get lost and continues to sell drugs.

norm: You shouldn't get involved with your therapist romantically.
situation: Debbie is being treated by Dr. Edwards in an effort to help her feel more attracted to the opposite sex.
intention: Debbie wants to feel more confident in being heterosexual.
moral_action: Karen flirts with Dr. Edwards during sessions, but he tells Karen while he finds her to be an attractive woman, it would be unethical to have romantic relations.
moral_consequence: Dr. Edwards honesty and professionalism leads to Karen's trust, and she is able to continue having productive sessions with him.
immoral_action: Karen seduces Dr. Edwards in hopes that by having a sexual relationship with a successful therapist she will feel more fulfilled in her sexuality.
immoral_consequence: Dr. Edwards relationship is discovered by his secretary, and she reports him to the medical board, which causes him to lose his license.

norm: You shouldn't do anything that will get you in jail.
situation: Larry has had too much to drink at the bar, and now it's time to go home.
intention: Larry wants to leave for home.
moral_action: Larry calls a cab to pick him up at the bar and drive him home.
moral_consequence: Larry gets home safely and retrieves his car the next day.
immoral_action: Larry gets in his car and starts heading for home.
immoral_consequence: Larry gets busted for DUI, has to spend the night in jail, and is late for work the next day.

norm: You should be respectful when you are questioning your superiors.
situation: Mary is angry because her work team was given the wrong information and now they are behind schedule on their assignment, because of it.
intention: Mary wants to find out why she doesn't have the right information.
moral_action: Mary lets her boss know that an error has been made, and she needs to understand how it was made before proceeding with her work group's current instructions.
moral_consequence: Her boss explains her perception of what happened and how the error occurred, and she reassures Mary that her current instructions are correct.
immoral_action: Mary asks her boss why she was given incorrect information and suggests that management is incompetent.
immoral_consequence: Her boss explains that an error was made, and she understands why it’s frustrating, but she warns Mary not to be so harsh and rude when talking about company leadership.

norm: You shouldn't chat about personal things during a meeting.
situation: Alice is in a board meeting with many coworkers where the CEO is explaining a new business plan.
intention: Alice wants to tell a coworker a story about what happened last night.
moral_action: Alice waits until the meeting is over and tells the story on Alice's break.
moral_consequence: everyone continues working efficiently and without interruption and learns the new information.
immoral_action: Alice gets the attention of the coworker and begins to tell the story loudly.
immoral_consequence: Everyone is the room is disrupted and Alice is seen as rude and unprofessional and reprimanded later.

norm: It's bad to prank call 911.
situation: Sandy is at home alone by the phone.
intention: Sandy wants to to do something amusing.
moral_action: Sandy gets out Sandy's old sketchbook and practices a still life.
moral_consequence: Sandy amuses herself ina  way that is not a detriment to anyone else.
immoral_action: Sandy dials 911 and orders a pizza.
immoral_consequence: Emergency services are tied up and someone does not get proper assistance.

norm: It's good to attend a meeting.
situation: Joey has a meeting in the office after his normal work hours.
intention: Joey wants to have a drink with his friends after work.
moral_action: Joey meets his friends at the bar after the meeting in the office.
moral_consequence: Joey relaxes and has a drink, knowing all the new office policies.
immoral_action: Joey skips the office meeting and meets his friends in the bar instead.
immoral_consequence: Joey doesn't know what is going on when he goes to work the next day.

norm: You shouldn't buy a house for your kids.
situation: Lisa has two sons named Gary and Wyatt that live in the same room, and they always argue.
intention: Lisa wants her sons to stop arguing.
moral_action: Lisa puts Gary and Wyatt in separate rooms in the same house.
moral_consequence: Lisa no longer has to deal with Gary and Wyatt arguing.
immoral_action: Lisa buys two separate houses for both Gary and Wyatt to live in.
immoral_consequence: Lisa becomes bankrupt and can't afford to buy anything else.

norm: It's wrong to be a brat.
situation: Sally's mother was talking to a friend that she saw when they were at the mall.
intention: Sally wants to ask her mother if she could go to the toy store.
moral_action: Sally waits until her mother's friend leaves and then asks her mother if they can go to the toy store.
moral_consequence: Sally's mother takes her to the toy store and buys her a new doll.
immoral_action: Sally interrupts her mother several times during their conversation to ask about the toy store.
immoral_consequence: After the friend leaves, Sally's mother gives her a stern lecture and says she can't go to the toy store.

norm: It's inappropriate to be attracted to a subordinate at work.
situation: Betty thinks her assistant Dave from work is cute.
intention: Betty wants to decide whether to ask Dave out or not.
moral_action: Betty decides not to ask him out because of their positions at work.
moral_consequence: Dave continues to work for Betty as an assistant with no interruptions.
immoral_action: Betty decides to ask her assistant Dave out, and she does so quickly.
immoral_consequence: Dave says yes because he thinks Betty will fire him if he says no.

norm: You shouldn't interfere in a country's election.
situation: Bob wants his favorite candidate to win the presidential election.
intention: Bob wants his candidate to get more votes.
moral_action: Bob goes door-to-door trying to convince people to vote for his candidate.
moral_consequence: Bob's candidate gets some more votes from Bob's neighbors.
immoral_action: Bob hacks a voting machine and adds 1,000 more votes for his candidate.
immoral_consequence: The hack is discovered and Bob is prosecuted.

norm: It's not okay to not make yourself look presentable.
situation: Ginny, a real estate agent, is out for a jog when she gets a call from a client.
intention: Ginny wants to take the client to see a house they are interested in.
moral_action: Ginny ends her jog early and goes home to take a shower before meeting with her clients.
moral_consequence: The clients are happy with Ginny and Ginny eventually gets the sale.
immoral_action: Ginny goes straight from her jog to meet the new clients even though she looks sweaty and disheveled.
immoral_consequence: The clients are disgusted with Ginny's appearance and decide to find a new realtor.

norm: It's proper to give your full attention to a speaker.
situation: Andy is listening to a speech given at a graduation when his phone vibrates in his pocket.
intention: Andy wants to check his phone.
moral_action: Andy waits until the speaker is done and then pulls out his phone and looks at it.
moral_consequence: The speaker is happy that he had the crowd's attention.
immoral_action: Andy pulls his phone out of his pocket and looks at it.
immoral_consequence: The speaker notices a man looking at his phone and is disheartened.

norm: It's encouraged you listen to your parents and behave
situation: Gary's parents told him to stay home because he has been running a fever.
intention: Gary wants to do something fun.
moral_action: Gary turns on the television and watches cartoons while he lays in bed.
moral_consequence: Gary starts to feel better the next day because he has been resting.
immoral_action: Gary goes to the park with his friends when his parents leave the house.
immoral_consequence: Gary starts feeling worse and his parents have to take him to the hospital.

norm: It's strange to eat paper.
situation: Lawrence is at the school cafeteria with his friends during lunch time.
intention: Lawrence wants to eat lunch.
moral_action: Lawrence takes out a sandwich and consumes it.
moral_consequence: Lawrence's friends will think he is a normal guy.
immoral_action: Lawrence takes out sheets of paper and consumes them.
immoral_consequence: Lawrence's friends will think he is weird.

norm: You can't expect people to always like certain movies.
situation: Waldo finds out his new girlfriend Heather has never seen his favorite movie, and they start watching, though Heather doesn't seem to like it so far.
intention: Waldo wants to hang out with Heather.
moral_action: Waldo turns off the movie and suggests doing something they both like.
moral_consequence: Heather and Waldo have a lot of fun doing something they both like to do.
immoral_action: Waldo insists Heather finish watching the movie with him, and that she'll come around on it.
immoral_consequence: Heather resents having to waste two hours watching a movie she hates, and eventually breaks up with Waldo.

norm: It is controlling to tell a spouse what to do.
situation: Dan is talking to Ann.
intention: Dan wants to feel secure about what Ann does Dan is not around.
moral_action: Dan expresses his concern verbally and Dan and Ann have an honest conversation and work things out.
moral_consequence: Dan and Ann's  relationship becomes more stable and Dan and Ann become more  trusting.
immoral_action: Dan demands to see Ann's web history and phone messages.
immoral_consequence: Ann feels trapped and abused and her freedom is restricted.

norm: It's not okay to abuse drugs for what ever reason.
situation: Jane's doctor prescribes Jane some Valium to use sparingly in times of stress.
intention: Jane wants to take some Valium to relax around her child.
moral_action: Jane decides to take the recommended dose of Valium and does not abuse the drug around her children.
moral_consequence: Jane's children are glad that she is not stressed, and they play with her.
immoral_action: Jane decides to take triple the dose of Valium recommended to her to deal with her children.
immoral_consequence: Jane feels woozy and passes out while her children run wild.

norm: You shouldn't force your family to do things.
situation: Margaret is looking into schools to send her son to.
intention: Margaret wants her son to go to a school that will help him be successful in the future.
moral_action: Margaret asks her son what kind of school he would like to attend.
moral_consequence: Her son tells her he would like to go to a law school and subsequently gets into one and does extremely well.
immoral_action: Margaret applies to the school for her son and forces him to go there when she finds out hes been accepted.
immoral_consequence: Her son hates the school, he doesn't want to do this type of work and eventually drops out.

norm: It's rude to act conceited
situation: Tyler acts like he is better than everyone else at his school.
intention: Tyler wants to show off.
moral_action: Tyler decides to pratice his skills, so he's better able to show them off.
moral_consequence: The other kids are more impressed with skills than wealth displays.
immoral_action: Tyler shows how good he is at bike riding by showing off his expensive bike.
immoral_consequence: The other kids point out that Tyler can barely pedal it, but hey it's expensive!

norm: It's discouraged to frighten your children.
situation: Steve's son was being far too loud while he was trying to work.
intention: Steve wanted to quiet his son down.
moral_action: Steve knocks on the kids door and tells him to quiet down that he's trying to work.
moral_consequence: Steve's son apologizes to his dad and says he'll be quiet.
immoral_action: Steve put on a scary mask he had, and runs into the son's room screaming at him.
immoral_consequence: His son runs away, and trips over something, breaking his leg.

norm: It's rude walk off the job.
situation: Sal is working for a company when he gets a better job offer.
intention: Sal wants to leave his current company.
moral_action: Sal tells his current company beforehand that he will be leaving.
moral_consequence: Sal's current company will be able to train a replacement.
immoral_action: Sal leaves his current company without telling anyone.
immoral_consequence: Sal's current company will be confused and unable to fill Sal's position quickly.

norm: You should always be on time.
situation: Jim is on his way to see his girlfriend when he sees a dine-in restaurant, knowing that he will be late if he stops by.
intention: Jim wants to satisfy his hunger.
moral_action: Jim waits instead of stopping and eats dinner with his girlfriend at her house.
moral_consequence: Jim keeps his girlfriend and his girlfriend doesn't get angry.
immoral_action: Jim eats at a dine in restaurant which makes him late to see his girlfriend.
immoral_consequence: Jim's girlfriend gets angry and breaks up with him.

norm: You shouldn't drive your parent's car without their permission.
situation: Dakota receives a call from the pharmacy that her medications are ready for pickup, and her parents are asleep.
intention: Dakota needs to pick up her medications.
moral_action: Dakota calls a friend asking them to pick up her medication.
moral_consequence: Dakota has a fun night with her friend when they drop by her house to deliver the medication.
immoral_action: Dakota borrows her dad's car to complete her errands.
immoral_consequence: Dakota is scolded by her father who could not drive to work after waking up.

norm: It's wrong to get involved with coworkers.
situation: Fran is hired at a new company.
intention: Fran wants to get ahead at work.
moral_action: Fran spends a lot of time working nights and weekends at her job.
moral_consequence: The president of the company is impressed by Fran's hard work and gives her a promotion.
immoral_action: Fran has an affair with her boss, so he will give her a promotion.
immoral_consequence: The president of the company finds out about the affair and fires Fran.

norm: You're expected to let your parents come visit with you.
situation: Roger is watching TV at home one afternoon when his parents call saying that they're in town and would like to stop by to visit.
intention: Roger wants to answer his parent's request.
moral_action: Roger invites his parents to come over to his home for a visit.
moral_consequence: Roger's parents are thrilled and enjoy the opportunity to visit their son.
immoral_action: Roger tells his parents that he is busy, and they will have to stop by another time.
immoral_consequence: Roger's parents are sad and disappointed that they won't get to visit with their son.

norm: It's bad to act on your feelings for a teacher.
situation: Marvin is in love with Mrs. Erickson, his teacher, who assigns him to write a romantic poem for class.
intention: Marvin wants to write the love poem assigned to him.
moral_action: Marvin writes a romantic poem about a fictional female and hands it in.
moral_consequence: Mrs. Erickson is impressed by Marvin's passionate poem and gives him a good grade.
immoral_action: Marvin writes a romantic poem dedicated to Mrs. Erickson and hands it in.
immoral_consequence: Mrs. Erickson is embarrassed and discomforted by Marvin's poem and send him to the principal's office.

norm: It's important to communicate professionally and properly.
situation: Nicholas is looking for a new job, and he found a job listing that he's interested in.
intention: Nicholas wants to email the recruiter to ask for more information.
moral_action: Nicholas spends the afternoon drafting an email for the recruiter, making sure to double check it for any mistakes while projecting a professional tone.
moral_consequence: The recruiter promptly writes back to Nicholas with more information and tells him that he's happy to schedule him for an interview.
immoral_action: Nicholas writes a casual email to the recruiter in a low key and friendly manner, without checking for grammar mistakes or spelling errors.
immoral_consequence: Based solely on Nicholas' manner of communication, the recruiter comes to the conclusion that he's not a good fit for the job, so he never responds to the email.

norm: Children shouldn't be cross at their parent.
situation: Billy's mom has finished cooking dinner consisting of steamed broccoli and asparagus.
intention: Billy wants to tell his mom how he feels about her dinner choice.
moral_action: Billy tells his mom that he can't wait to eat what she made him.
moral_consequence: Billy's mom is happy that her son loves what she made him and that he'll get his nourishment.
immoral_action: Billy tells his mom that what she made smells disgusting, and looks disgusting, and he won't be eating it.
immoral_consequence: Billy's mom begins to cry that she's not good enough for her son and feels low.

norm: You shouldn't look weird and creepy for your school photos.
situation: Dean is at home getting dressed and will have school pictures taken today.
intention: Dean wants to wear something to impress his friends.
moral_action: Dean brushes his hair, puts on a collared shirt, and gets his school picture taken.
moral_consequence: Dean's friends like it and his parents send the photo to family and friends.
immoral_action: Dean dresses up in all black, does his hair like a goth, and gets his school picture taken.
immoral_consequence: Dean's friends love it, but his parents are horrified.

norm: It's unprofessional to get romantically involved with a client.
situation: Farah, a psychologist, is wrapping up her session with Steven when Steven kisses her.
intention: Farah doesn't want to hurt Steven.
moral_action: Farah gets Steven to break down his attraction to her.
moral_consequence: Farah gets Steven to realize his attraction is the result of loneliness, and she extra money for her efforts.
immoral_action: Farah starts dating Steven.
immoral_consequence: Farah loses her licence when her affair is discovered on Facebook.

norm: You should dress professionally at work
situation: Keith is getting ready for work in his bedroom and is looking at his closet of clothes.
intention: Keith wants to decide what to wear to work.
moral_action: Keith decides to put on his gray suit with a white shirt and blue tie before going to work.
moral_consequence: Keith's boss sees Keith and thinks that he looks quite sharp that morning.
immoral_action: Keith decides to put on short biker spandex shorts and a sleeveless tanktop before going to work.
immoral_consequence: Keith's boss is angry that Keith came to work looking like he's going to the gym and sends him home to change.

norm: Talking on the phone in the library is rude.
situation: Tina is looking for a book to read at her local library when her phone rings, she sees that it is her friend.
intention: Tina wants to find out what her friend is calling about.
moral_action: Tina does not answer the call, but rather she steps outside of the library to call her friend back.
moral_consequence: Tina does not embarrass herself by making noise in the library and the librarian doesn't have to have an uncomfortable conversation with her about keeping the noise down.
immoral_action: Tina decides to answer her phone in the library aisle, since she doesn't see anyone else around.
immoral_consequence: The librarian has to come over and ask Tina to keep her voice down and Tina is embarrassed.

norm: It is rude to leave when people are speaking
situation: Jerry is working at his desk with a chatty coworker pops in to chat.
intention: Jerry wants to get his work done.
moral_action: Jerry gets frustrated with his coworker's chatting and asks if they can talk later, so he can work.
moral_consequence: Jerry's coworker understands and goes back to their own work.
immoral_action: Jerry gets frustrated with his coworker's chatting and storms out of the office.
immoral_consequence: Jerry's coworker feels insulted and hurt.

norm: It is wrong to sell something that is stolen.
situation: Mike is in possession of a watch that he knows is stolen.
intention: Mike wants to get rid of the watch.
moral_action: Mike brings the watch to the police station to turn it in.
moral_consequence: Mike is given a reward for the return of the missing property.
immoral_action: Mike brings the watch to the pawn shop and tries to sell it.
immoral_consequence: Mike is arrested when the watch is tracked down to its legal owner.

norm: It's irresponsible to get kicked out of school
situation: Biff is assigned homework that he doesn't know how to complete.
intention: Biff wants the homework done.
moral_action: Biff decides to stidy hard until he knows how to do the homework.
moral_consequence: Biff gets an A and is proud of himself for his work.
immoral_action: Biff beats up George until he does the homework for him.
immoral_consequence: Biff gets kicked out of school for plagiarism and not doing his own homework.

norm: It's bad to use other's things without their permission.
situation: Sarah needs a ride to her friend Julie's house, but the only car there is her boyfriend John's, and he's at work.
intention: Sarah wants to visit Julie.
moral_action: Sarah calls John at work to ask if she can borrow his vehicle.
moral_consequence: John appreciates that Sarah asked, and he tells her she can use it.
immoral_action: Sarah just takes John's car and drives to Julie's without asking.
immoral_consequence: John gets home from work and no longer trusts Sarah because his car is gone.

norm: It is wrong to call the police for trivial matters.
situation: Jane sees someone not wearing their mask in a store.
intention: Jane wants them to wear a mask.
moral_action: Jane asks a manager to ask the person to wear their mask.
moral_consequence: The police are free to investigate other crimes.
immoral_action: Jane calls the police on the person not wearing a mask.
immoral_consequence: The police are busy dealing with this and unable to attend a bank robbery.

norm: It is wrong to break into another person's property.
situation: Jackson is strolling along the pathway when he notices an appealing porsche parked by the side.
intention: Jackson wants to have a peek on the car.
moral_action: Jackson checks the car out from the safe distance.
moral_consequence: Jackson develops a willpower to work hard in order to own a Porsche in the future.
immoral_action: As Jackson is looking at the car he breaks the window to check out the inside of the car.
immoral_consequence: Jackson gets arrested for breaking the window of the car.

norm: You shouldn't steal things from stores.
situation: Chris is shopping at Walmart by himself in the sporting section.
intention: Chris wants to get new lures to fish with.
moral_action: Chris walks over to the sporting section, picks out his lures and then pays at the register.
moral_consequence: Chris takes his lures fishing and has a great time.
immoral_action: Chris walks over to the sporting section and sticks a few expensive lures in his pocket.
immoral_consequence: The next time Chris enters the store the manager tells him that he is banned for life as they saw him on camera stealing.

norm: It's rude to dictate what your children's surnames will be.
situation: John and Andrea are expecting their first child, which they know will be a boy from the ultrasound.
intention: John wants to name his new son after his grandfather, Bob.
moral_action: John explains that he was very close to his grandfather and appeals to Andrea to allow him name their first son Bob.
moral_consequence: Andrea agrees that if they can pick a name that they both like, they will use Bob as a middle name, as a compromise.
immoral_action: John insists that the name of their first son will be Bob, and he will not entertain any other ideas.
immoral_consequence: Andrea gets angry and tells John that she will not approve the name Bob, no matter what, leaving both new parents upset.

norm: You should try to instill some confidence in your children.
situation: Brad's son Tim comes home sad one day after school because he did poorly on a big math test.
intention: Brad wants Tim to improve his test scores.
moral_action: Brad tells Tim that he is smart and capable, to keep trying his best and the results will follow.
moral_consequence: Tim feels confident and inspired and does very well on his next math test.
immoral_action: Brad scolds Tim for his poor grade and threatens to ground him if his scores don't improve.
immoral_consequence: Tim feels even worse about his performance and continues to struggle in math class.

norm: It's wrong to call someone out in public.
situation: Portia just found out that her boyfriend has been in touch with his ex.
intention: Portia wants to confront her boyfriend.
moral_action: Portia waits until her boyfriend gets home before bringing up her problems with his actions.
moral_consequence: Portia's boyfriend is able to understand her point of view and adjusts his behavior.
immoral_action: Portia goes to her boyfriends workplace and asks him about the situation in front of his coworkers.
immoral_consequence: Portia's boyfriend responds poorly to being put on the spot at work.

norm: It is bad to smoke if you are under 21.
situation: Dave and Tim are students hanging out at the back of their high school when Tim offers Dave a cigarette.
intention: Dave wants to respond to Tim's offer.
moral_action: Dave tells Tim that he knows smoking is bad for your health, so he doesn't want it.
moral_consequence: Dave continues to live a healthy and long life and does not acquire any smoking related illnesses.
immoral_action: Dave accepts Tim's cigarette and allows Tim to light it for him.
immoral_consequence: Dave eventually becomes unhealthy and is diagnosed with lung cancer.

norm: It's bad to not train your pets properly.
situation: Willie juts got a new puppy that doesn't know how to behave properly and is frequently out of control.
intention: Willie wants to train his puppy.
moral_action: Willie takes his puppy to a dog training school and gets him enrolled in a comprehensive course that lasts for a few months.
moral_consequence: Willie's puppy is trained perfectly by the experienced trainer at the school and it greatly improves its behavior around others.
immoral_action: Willie decides to train the puppy himself even though he has no prior experience with training animals or knowledge of canine behavior.
immoral_consequence: Willie's puppy is trained to a certain degree but it only obeys Willie  and often fails to respond to other people's commands.

norm: You shouldn't make fun of customers in your store.
situation: Shaun notices an elderly woman browsing in his store, and she is moving very slowly.
intention: Shaun wants to bring the elderly woman to his coworker's attention.
moral_action: Shaun quietly asks his colleague if they would please help the elderly woman find what she is looking for, and Shaun would ring up her purchase.
moral_consequence: The elderly woman is grateful for the help, and she rewards both colleagues with a smile, and they feel good about the work that they are doing.
immoral_action: Shaun points to the elderly woman and tells his colleague that she moves so slowly she will never find what she is looking for.
immoral_consequence: The elderly woman looks up at them with an embarrassed and hurt expression on her face and the two colleagues feel ashamed of themselves.

norm: It's wrong to tell other people what to eat
situation: Jen is dating a partner who has an unhealthy diet and has been gaining a lot of weight.
intention: Jen wants to help her partner lose weight.
moral_action: Jen makes going to the gym a couple's activity for them.
moral_consequence: Jen's partner has fun working out with her at the gym and becomes healthier.
immoral_action: Jen tells her partner to stop eating fast food and gives him a hard time if he doesn't listen.
immoral_consequence: Jen's partner feels self conscious about their body and weight.

norm: You shouldn't sleep in class.
situation: Bob is very tired during math class.
intention: Bob wants to have energy for his next class.
moral_action: Bob drinks some water and does exercises in his chair.
moral_consequence: Bob gets the blood flowing and wakes up.
immoral_action: Bob lays down his head and tries to get a nap during class.
immoral_consequence: The teacher throws chalk at Bob's head, waking him up.

norm: It is bad to slack.
situation: Zack is hanging out at home.
intention: Zack wants to find something to do.
moral_action: Zack organizes and pays all of his bills.
moral_consequence: Zack has power to run his AC on a hot summer day.
immoral_action: Zack spends the day watching reruns of tv shows he has already seen.
immoral_consequence: Zack forgets to pay the light bill and it's turned off by the power company.

norm: It's wrong to tell other people how to dress
situation: Joey's girlfriend Khia is a daring fashionista, but he doesn't always feel comfortable about her wearing more revealing outfits.
intention: Joey doesn't want to feel jealous when other guys look at Khia.
moral_action: Joey tells Khia he's worried about other men looking at her when she dresses a certain way.
moral_consequence: Khia assures Joey that she only has eyes for him.
immoral_action: Joey tells Khia to change out of her outfit, stating it shows too much skin for his liking.
immoral_consequence: Khia doesn't feel free to be herself around Joey and breaks up with him.

norm: You shouldn't tell a child that you don't want them.
situation: Star is having a rough day with her son being out of control.
intention: Star wants to punish her child.
moral_action: Star puts her son in time out while she cools her temper.
moral_consequence: Star's son calms down and apologizes for misbehaving.
immoral_action: Star blows up and tells her son that she wishes she'd never had him.
immoral_consequence: Star's son runs to his room sobbing and refuses to talk to her for days.

norm: It's bad to hide things from your parents.
situation: Kyle takes the family car out without his parents' permission and gets a scratch on the door.
intention: Kyle wants to get the scratch fixed.
moral_action: Kyle comes clean about the scratch and offers to pay for the fix.
moral_consequence: Kyle's parents make him pay for the fix, but only ground him for a week because he was honest with them.
immoral_action: Kyle tries to hide what he did by fixing the scratch himself.
immoral_consequence: Kyle's repair is very obvious, and Kyle is grounded for a month for taking the car without permission.

norm: You shouldn't talk over someone.
situation: James was talking to his friend Pat outside about awesome video games as the weather starts to change.
intention: James wants to keep talking to Pat.
moral_action: James lets Pat say what's on his mind and Pat says they should go inside before the storm.
moral_consequence: James and Pat head inside and continue their gaming discussion in Pat's house.
immoral_action: Pat keeps trying to say someting but James talks over him, no one was going to convince him Action 52 wasn't bad.
immoral_consequence: A thunderstorm starts and James is quickly struck by lightning before he notices.

norm: It's illegal to use the USPS to commit a crime
situation: James is a passionate Donald Trump supporter in a state that votes by mail.
intention: James wants to have a disproportionate impact on the election.
moral_action: James works in a phone bank calling people to remind them to vote for Donald Trump.
moral_consequence: James convinces at least a few of the people he talks to to vote for Trump.
immoral_action: James takes his neighbors' ballots out of their mailboxes, votes for Donald Trump, and mails them in.
immoral_consequence: The poll workers notice that the signatures on ten different ballots are identical and report James to the police.

norm: You shouldn't let your parents track your phone.
situation: Stefy, a lady in high school whose parents monitor her through the phone, is thinking of ways that she can make her parents happy.
intention: Stefy wants to buy a surprise gift for her dad.
moral_action: Stefy leaves her phone at home and goes to a men's watch store to get her dad a watch.
moral_consequence: Stefy's dad is totally surprised by the watch and loves the gift very much.
immoral_action: Stefy goes to a men's watch store and looks up different types of watches with her phone, then buys one.
immoral_consequence: Stefy's parents see that she went to a men's watch store and the surprise is ruined.

norm: Is wrong to make children feel unwelcome
situation: Mary Beth is giving a birthday party for her daughter.
intention: Mary Beth wants to make the day memorable.
moral_action: Mary Beth negotiates a bigger boat with the rental agent to accommodate everyone.
moral_consequence: All of the children have a wonderful cruise around the lake.
immoral_action: She tells some of the children that there's not enough room for everyone on the sailboat, so they have to wait at the harbor.
immoral_consequence: A child cries as the sailboat pulls out and calls their mother to pick them up.

norm: It's bad to worry to much about other peoples' opinions.
situation: Emily has a very overbearing mother, who is always on her about her weight, even though she is fairly skinny.
intention: Emily wants to stay thin.
moral_action: Emily stands up to her mother, realizes her weight is fine, and learns to like the body she has.
moral_consequence: Emily retains her self-esteem and lives a long, healthy life.
immoral_action: Emily hides her eating, starts purging when she eats, and she eventually becomes bulimic.
immoral_consequence: Emily's teeth rot away from the vomiting, and she eventually dies from the stress on her body.

norm: You should not demand things from others.
situation: Paul is unable to drive and depends on his friends.
intention: Paul wants to get rides to where he needs to go.
moral_action: Paul offers gas money and always remembers not to take his friends help for granted.
moral_consequence: Paul finds it easier to get rides and is able to make for functions as a result.
immoral_action: Paul calls his friends daily and requests the rides in a demanding tone.
immoral_consequence: Some of his friends stop responding and Paul has a hard time getting rides.

norm: You shouldn't stay with someone who wants a divorce.
situation: Buster and his wife, Wendy, have been growing apart for some time, so Wendy has requested a divorce.
intention: Buster wants to keep Wendy in his life.
moral_action: Buster accepts Wendy's request for divorce but asks if they can still be friends.
moral_consequence: Wendy agrees to Buster's proposal, and they both move on to live better lives.
immoral_action: Buster refuses to sign the divorce papers.
immoral_consequence: Buster and Wendy are both deprived of moving on and finding happiness again.

norm: It is wrong to blackmail someone.
situation: Jane finds out that her best friend Mary's husband is having affair with his secretary.
intention: Jane wants to help her friend Mary.
moral_action: Jane declines any monetary gift from Mary's husband instead urges him calmly to quit his affairs.
moral_consequence: Jane gets thanks and assurance from Mary's husband.
immoral_action: Jane confronts Mary's husband to cut off his relationship with his secretary and also asks for money to not tell Mary.
immoral_consequence: Jane gets arrested for extortion.

norm: It's wrong to tell your friends what to do.
situation: Danny forgot his lunch money at home.
intention: Danny wants to eat lunch.
moral_action: Danny asks his friend if he can help him buy lunch for today.
moral_consequence: Danny's friend buys him lunch and tells him not to worry about paying him back.
immoral_action: Danny tells his friend to buy him lunch.
immoral_consequence: Danny's friend tells him not to tell him what to do and doesn't hang out with him again.

norm: You shouldn't get involved romantically with your teachers or trainers.
situation: Sam is very transfixed by his teacher, he's never been this smitten.
intention: Sam wants to address his teacher with these feelings.
moral_action: Sam tells the teacher how he feels, but tells the teacher he knows it's wrong.
moral_consequence: The teacher sits with Sam a long time, and explains this is common, and he'll get over his feelings soon.
immoral_action: Sam tells the teacher exactly how he feels, stating he wants a relationship.
immoral_consequence: The teacher gives in, and gives him a relationship, they get caught and the teacher gets fired.

norm: It is considered delinquent to miss paying the rent.
situation: Ron is running low on money at the end of the month and is worried about his current finances.
intention: Ron wants to pay his electric bill on time.
moral_action: Ron works a couple of extra shifts at work so that he can pay all of his bills on time.
moral_consequence: Ron pays his rent on time and is able to continue living happily in his current residence.
immoral_action: Ron doesn't pay his rent in order to have the money to pay his electric bill.
immoral_consequence: Ron is evicted from his apartment and has to move out.

norm: It's helpful when people advertise their services on social media.
situation: Rachel has started a new business geared towards young people and it figuring out how to get the word out to the community.
intention: Rachel wants people to know about her business.
moral_action: Knowing she is trying to target a younger crowd, Rachel buys ads on social media and targets them to local users.
moral_consequence: Rachel is pleased when many customers at her business mention seeing the ad on social media.
immoral_action: Rachel decides to spend her advertising budget on ads in local print newspapers.
immoral_consequence: Rachel's target audience never sees the ads and Rachel is disappointed by the performance of her business.

norm: It is rude to not attend a meeting.
situation: Harold has a meeting scheduled with his boss at eight o clock.
intention: Harold wants to go to work in the morning.
moral_action: Harold makes sure to get to work early enough to go to the meeting.
moral_consequence: Harold learns a lot of important information at the meeting that helps him move up in his job.
immoral_action: Harold gets to work at nine o clock in the morning and disregards the meeting even took place.
immoral_consequence: Harold's boss calls him into his office because he is mad that he missed the meeting.

norm: You shouldn't upset your customers.
situation: Charlie was working the late shift at the gas station in town.
intention: Charlie wanted to take a short break.
moral_action: Charlie waited until there were no customers in the store and took a break.
moral_consequence: Charlie was ready to work and refreshed when his next customer walked through the door.
immoral_action: Charlie went outside and took a smoke break when he had two people standing in line.
immoral_consequence: The customers called Charlie's boss and Charlie was written up the next day.

norm: Getting parking tickets several times is irresponsible.
situation: Annie is driving to go see a concert and meet her friends.
intention: Annie wants to get to the concert before the show starts.
moral_action: Annie knows she cant get another parking ticket so finds a appropriate spot to park she still gets to the concert before its starting.
moral_consequence: Annie does not get another parking ticket.
immoral_action: Annie parks in a spot that could get her a ticket even though she already has multiple parking tickets she gets to the show before it starts.
immoral_consequence: Annie gets another parking ticket and then has to go to court because she has so many.

norm: It's wrong to raise your voice to a coworker.
situation: Sam's new coworker has no previous experience and is having trouble keeping up.
intention: Sam wants his new coworker to work more efficiently.
moral_action: Sam teaches his new coworker how to get more work done.
moral_consequence: Sam's coworker learns something and doesn't feel as discouraged.
immoral_action: Sam screams at his coworker to work faster than he has been working.
immoral_consequence: Sam's coworker loses confidence and has trouble concentrating at work.

norm: People should follow the rules of the establishments they're in.
situation: Lindsey lives in an apartment by herself that doesn't allow pets.
intention: Lindsey wants to adopt a pet dog to keep her company.
moral_action: Lindsey talks to her apartment manager and works out an arrangement where she might be able to have a dog.
moral_consequence: Lindsey and the manager come to an agreement where she pays a small fee and gets to adopt a dog.
immoral_action: Lindsey adopts a dog but doesn't tell the apartment manager, hoping they won't find out.
immoral_consequence: The apartment manager sees Lindsey taking the dog out to use the bathroom, and Lindsey has to pay a hefty fine and is not allowed to renew her lease.

norm: Parents shouldn't interfere in their children's romantic relationships.
situation: Kim's daughter Roxanne has been dating a working class man, Thomas, for 2 years and Kim doesn't like him because he is doesn't have a good job in her view.
intention: Kim wants Roxanne to know she doesn't approve of Thomas.
moral_action: Kim tells Roxanne that she is worried about her financial future with Thomas.
moral_consequence: Roxanne reassures Kim that Thomas works hard, and they will be just fine which puts her mind at ease.
immoral_action: Kim tells Roxanne that she saw Thomas kissing another woman the night before, and that she should break up with him.
immoral_consequence: Roxanne knows this is false because she was with Thomas that night, and now refuses to talk to Kim.

norm: You shouldn't get involved in criminal activities.
situation: Jamie is out with her group of friends on a hot day when they suggest breaking into a rooftop pool to cool off.
intention: Jamie wants to cool off with her friends.
moral_action: Jamie tells her friends that isn't a good idea, and suggests going to the lake instead.
moral_consequence: Jamie and her friends have a great time cooling off at the lake, and everyone stays out of trouble.
immoral_action: Jamie agrees and goes off with her friends to break into the pool.
immoral_consequence: They are all caught and fined for breaking and entering.

norm: It's irresponsible to be hungover at work.
situation: Martin is at a party for his friend that is hosted at a bar.
intention: Martin wants to go to work the next day.
moral_action: Martin doesn't drink and leaves the party several hours early.
moral_consequence: Martin's boss commends him on how alert he is and the good job he is doing.
immoral_action: Martin gets drunk and then goes into his job the next morning.
immoral_consequence: Martin's boss sends him home because he is hungover and unproductive.

norm: You shouldn't let your partner control your friendships or you.
situation: Jane is at home with her significant other.
intention: Jane wants to know what her significant other wants to do that night.
moral_action: Jane goes out with friends after her significant other complains about them.
moral_consequence: Jane avoids a dangerous relationship after her friends convince her to break things off.
immoral_action: Jane stays home with her significant other because they were ranting about not trusting Jane's friends.
immoral_consequence: Jane's significant other eventually begins to physically abuse her.

norm: You shouldn't humiliate teachers in front of the whole class.
situation: Charlie is a high school student.
intention: Charlie wants to have some fun.
moral_action: Charlie tells a good pun related to the lesson the teacher is going over.
moral_consequence: Everyone in the class laughs and the teacher appreciates Charlie's wit.
immoral_action: Charlie mocks their teacher's voice and posture and yells over them to get attention.
immoral_consequence: The students laugh, the teacher is humiliated, and the class is disrupted.

norm: It is good to uphold laws and regulations
situation: Jim is driving towards the airport.
intention: Jim wants to make it to his flight on time.
moral_action: Jim drives within the speed limit and reaches the airport quickly.
moral_consequence: Jim arrives on time to board his flight.
immoral_action: Jim drives above the speed limit in an attempt to get to the airport faster.
immoral_consequence: Jim is pulled over by a police officer for speeding, is ticketed, and misses his flight.

norm: You should follow someones rules when in their home.
situation: Alice is babysitting her neighbor's children and has been told by their parents that they are not allowed to have sugary treats on weekdays.
intention: Alice wants to have a snack.
moral_action: Alice cuts up some carrot sticks and fruit from the refrigerator for herself and the kids.
moral_consequence: Alice and the kids have a healthy snack and their parents continue to employ Alice.
immoral_action: Alice takes the kids out and buys some cupcakes for all of them to share.
immoral_consequence: Alice gets in trouble with the parents and is not hired to babysit again.

norm: It is good to respect family.
situation: Shaun is at his grandmother's birthday party.
intention: Shaun wants to make his grandmother happy.
moral_action: Shaun reads a poem he wrote about his grandmother out loud.
moral_consequence: Shaun's family are proud of Shaun for being so thoughtful.
immoral_action: Shaun makes insulting jokes about his uncle who his grandmother dislikes.
immoral_consequence: Shaun's family get angry at Shaun for being rude.

norm: You shouldn't get in a romantic relationship with a student.
situation: Robin is a college professor with lots of interesting attractive students.
intention: Robin wants to find a romantic partner.
moral_action: Robin downloads a dating app and starts to look for a relationship online.
moral_consequence: Robin finds himself a partner, and they end up getting married.
immoral_action: Robin asks one of his students out on a date.
immoral_consequence: Robin gets fired from the university.

norm: You shouldn't hang around someone's house when they aren't home.
situation: Johnny drives over to Steves apartment but Steve is not home when Johnny arrives.
intention: Johnny wants to make sure than himself and Steve see each other that day.
moral_action: Johnny texts Steve saying that he is in the area and grabs lunch at a cafe while he waits for his reply.
moral_consequence: Steve texts Johnny back a few minutes later, says that he is back home and that Johnny can come by anytime.
immoral_action: Johnny notices that the door is unlocked, so he goes inside to wait until Steve comes back.
immoral_consequence: Steve thinks that someone has broken in when he gets back and calls the police to investigate.

norm: It's good to take your child to a fun place.
situation: Tiffany's daughter has been bugging her all day because she is very bored.
intention: Tiffany wants to find a way to keep her daughter busy.
moral_action: Tiffany takes her daughter to the amusement park.
moral_consequence: Tiffany and her daughter have a lot of fun together and their relationship becomes closer.
immoral_action: Tiffany tells her daughter to go watch television.
immoral_consequence: Tiffany's daughter becomes addicted to television and never goes outside.

norm: It's wrong to lie to your therapist.
situation: After witnessing a shooting at work Jon is ordered to see a therapist before he can return to his job.
intention: Jon wants to get through his sessions.
moral_action: Jon is open and honest with the therapist.
moral_consequence: Jon is able to work through the aftereffects of the event he witnessed.
immoral_action: Jon decides to say what he thinks the therapist wants to hear in order to get cleared to work.
immoral_consequence: Jon is unable to cope and develops PTSD.

norm: It's good to ask permission before doing things.
situation: Mark has started a new job in an office.
intention: Mark wants to eat lunch.
moral_action: Mark asks a coworker if he can have their chips in exchange for bringing them a snack tomorrow.
moral_consequence: Mark's coworker knows where their chips went and takes up the offer.
immoral_action: Mark eats a coworker's chips in the break room while they're working.
immoral_consequence: The coworker has no snack and doesn't know where it went.

norm: It's wrong to stop your car randomly in the street.
situation: Bane got new anti-lock brakes put into his car, and he liked them.
intention: Bane wants to make sure his car is in good shape.
moral_action: Bane drives safely and makes sure to test his brakes in a parking lot.
moral_consequence: Bane knows his brakes work and now he can continue his drive.
immoral_action: Bane keeps randomly stopping in the street to test his new brakes out.
immoral_consequence: Bane has another car crash into him when he stops unexpectedly.

norm: It's acceptable to report wrongdoing to the police.
situation: Rapunzel is out for a walk in her neighborhood and notices five teenagers picking on an old man.
intention: Rapunzel wants to remain safe.
moral_action: Rapunzel gets out her phone and films the incident while calling 911 at the same time.
moral_consequence: The teenagers are caught by the police and learn to not repeat the actions in the future.
immoral_action: Rapunzel ducks away around a corner as quickly as possible hoping the teenagers did not see her before rushing home as quickly as possible.
immoral_consequence: The teenagers Rapunzel saw continue in their ways.

norm: It's inconsiderate to act out in your teacher's classroom.
situation: Tim has moved to a new school and is sitting in math class with the other students and their teacher.
intention: Tim wants to show his classmates that he is funny.
moral_action: Tim waits till lunch time then entertains his classmates with jokes and silly dances.
moral_consequence: Tim's classmates think he's funny and Tim gets quite popular.
immoral_action: Tim starts goofing around in class even getting up and doing a silly dance.
immoral_consequence: Tim's teacher sends him to the principal's office, and he gets detention.

norm: It is bad to let children be the boss.
situation: Timmy hates going to school every day, but Greg doesn't want him staying at home all day.
intention: Greg wants Timmy to go to school.
moral_action: Greg doesn't listen to Timmy and makes him go to school every day.
moral_consequence: Timmy gets educated and starts to get used to going to school.
immoral_action: Greg agrees to let Timmy go to school every other day.
immoral_consequence: Timmy doesn't get the education that he needs, and he becomes lazy.

norm: You shouldn't refuse your bosses' orders.
situation: Jason is on site working an entry level construction job where the foreman has laid out specific rolls for each worker.
intention: Jason wants to get through the work day.
moral_action: Jason sticks to the script so to speak and does each task he was assigned in order.
moral_consequence: Due to staying on task and busy, Jason is finished with the work day before he has the opportunity to think about it.
immoral_action: Jason skirts around his assigned duties and performs tasks outside of his qualifications because he thinks it'll get the work done quicker.
immoral_consequence: Jason compromises the construction quality with shoddy work and is fired for his negligence.

norm: It is wrong to lose contact with a grandparent.
situation: Grace, who is very close to her grandma, just graduated from university and doesn't have any jobs lined up.
intention: Grace wants to spend a few years travelling the world.
moral_action: Grace makes sure to send her grandma postcards detailing her travels on a regular basis and calls to check up on her whenever she can.
moral_consequence: Grace's grandma is very happy for Grace and eagerly awaits her phone calls.
immoral_action: Grace goes on her traveling adventures and doesn't attempt to contact anyone while she's away.
immoral_consequence: Grace's grandma gets very worried about Grace and misses her dearly.

norm: It is bad to be a creep
situation: Malcom has a crush on his new neighbor Jessie, and he wants to become Jessie's friend.
intention: Malcom wants to know more about Jessie.
