moral_action: When Jessie is in her yard, Malcom talks to her asking about her interests.
moral_consequence: Jessie and Malcom become friends.
immoral_action: Malcom watches Jessie browsing her favorite sites on the internet from outside her window.
immoral_consequence: Jessie's parents spot Malcom and ban him from seeing their daughter.

norm: You shouldn't go against your parents.
situation: Mary is attending a party at a friend's house and remembers her parents' warning against getting drunk.
intention: Mary wants to have fun at the party.
moral_action: Mary sings and dances at the party and leaves for home before it gets dark.
moral_consequence: Mary gets praises from her parents for being responsible.
immoral_action: Mary enjoys dancing and drinking alcohol a lot with friends.
immoral_consequence: Mary pukes on the couch and passes out while others laugh at her.

norm: You shouldn't call online dating gross.
situation: Molly is at a bar with her coworkers when they start discussing their dating lives.
intention: Molly wants to impress her coworkers by appearing very hard to date.
moral_action: Molly says that she gets lots of attention in everyday life and won't try online dating because she doesn't need to.
moral_consequence: A coworker who found their partner through online dating sees Molly's point and continues speaking with her.
immoral_action: Molly remarks that online dating is a gross and unnecessary way to find a date because she gets plenty of attention in her daily life.
immoral_consequence: A coworker who found their partner through online dating is offended and walks away in digust.

norm: You should not do things that will upset your parents.
situation: Andy promised his parents he would call if he would be late and his group of friends wants him to stay longer.
intention: Andy wants to stay with his group of friends.
moral_action: Andy calls his parents telling them he will be staying with his friends.
moral_consequence: Andy's parents are glad he called to check in.
immoral_action: Andy stays with his friends without calling his parents to let them know he is ok.
immoral_consequence: Andy's parents are worried about him because he has not called.

norm: Its wrong to be affronted when it's someone else's party.
situation: Ruth is at her friend Terri's birthday party when she notices that many of the guests did not bring gifts.
intention: Ruth wants to react to the other guests.
moral_action: Ruth focuses on making sure that Terri is enjoying her party.
moral_consequence: Ruth fosters a great party atmosphere for Terri.
immoral_action: Ruth approaches the guests that didn't bring gifts and informs them of how rude they appear.
immoral_consequence: Ruth creates tension at Terri's party.

norm: People should not leave work without notice.
situation: Mark is at his desk in the office when he receives an email that his wife had to be rushed to hospital.
intention: Mark wants to go to the hospital.
moral_action: Mark quickly sends an email to his boss saying he has to leave for an emergency and runs to his car.
moral_consequence: Mark's boss is understanding that Mark had to leave and writes back to Mark to not worry about it.
immoral_action: Mark gets up from his desk and without even locking his computer leaves and goes to his car.
immoral_consequence: Mark's boss becomes worried about Mark and the office starts to panic when they can't find him.

norm: It's rude to stretch your arm out in someone's face.
situation: Hal is watching a movie at a theater with people from school, Linda and Lynn are on either side of Hal and Joe is beside Linda, Joe's girlfriend.
intention: Hal's shoulders start to cramp, and he wants to stretch his arms out.
moral_action: Hal announces that he has to stretch then carefully squirms so as  not to hit  anyone.
moral_consequence: Hal feels much better and no one was hurt.
immoral_action: Hal thoughtlessly stretches and hits Linda in the mouth.
immoral_consequence: Linda cries and Joe punches Hal.

norm: You shouldn't question people about every little thing.
situation: Juan's female friend was telling him about her recent sexual assault.
intention: Juan wants to tell his friend something.
moral_action: Juan tells his friend that she believes her and gives her advice on contacting law enforcement.
moral_consequence: Juan's friend feels closer to him and trusts him more.
immoral_action: Juan tells her that he thinks she's not telling the truth about the assault.
immoral_consequence: Juan's friends feels betrayed by him and wants to spend less time with him.

norm: It's wrong to drive around on an expired tag.
situation: Ted was getting in his car to go to work when he realized that his car had expired tags.
intention: Ted wants to get to work.
moral_action: Ted asks his friend for a ride to work until he gets the tag renewed.
moral_consequence: Ted is able to get to work without getting in any trouble.
immoral_action: Ted drives his car with the expired tags to his job anyway.
immoral_consequence: Ted is stopped by the police and gets a fine for the tags.

norm: You shouldn't sneak into places you aren't allowed.
situation: John hears about a new hit movie that was just released, but he's too young to buy rated R tickets.
intention: John wants to see the movie.
moral_action: John decides to watch the movie later when it's released on DVD, and sees a different film instead.
moral_consequence: John enjoys the movie on DVD from the comfort of home.
immoral_action: John buys tickets to a different movie and then sneaks into the rated R one.
immoral_consequence: John is caught by a theater employee towards the end of the movie and is kicked out.

norm: It is rude to make someone feel uncomfortable in public.
situation: Mae wants to avoid talking about politics with her best friend at the mall.
intention: Mae wants to distract her friend.
moral_action: Mae points out something in the nearby interesting in the nearby store/.
moral_consequence: Mae's friend walks into the store, and they end up buying something they need for work.
immoral_action: Mae asks her best friend about her sex life.
immoral_consequence: Mae's friend feels humiliated and exposed in front of a crowd.

norm: It's good to report criminal activity to the police.
situation: Reagan is sitting on her porch late one night and sees a woman opening her neighbors garage door with a crowbar.
intention: Reagan wants to tell her neighbors what she saw.
moral_action: Reagan calls the police immediately and talks to her neighbors that night after police arrive.
moral_consequence: The police catch the woman in the act and the neighbors' avoid having anything stolen.
immoral_action: Since it's late, Reagan goes on to bed and talks to the neighbors about it in the morning.
immoral_consequence: The neighbors had property stolen from the garage and the woman is long gone.

norm: It is rude to treat a customer service person poorly.
situation: Henry's internet just went out for the third time this week while he is trying to work remotely, and he's on the phone with a support representative.
intention: Henry wants to let out his frustration over the issue.
moral_action: Henry waits until after the call is over to vent about what happened to someone.
moral_consequence: The customer service representative is relieved that Henry was not troublesome to deal with.
immoral_action: Henry points out how often this happens and takes it out on the customer service agent.
immoral_consequence: The support representative ends up having a bad day because of him.

norm: You shouldn't harass citizens if you're a police officer.
situation: Victor is a new police officer fresh out of the academy whom was just assigned to patrol a new community.
intention: Victor wants his community to respect him.
moral_action: Victor goes out of his way to help members of his community and spends time talking and getting to know them.
moral_consequence: Victor's community grows to trust and respect him and will go out of their way to let him know when they learn of a crime occurring.
immoral_action: Victor constantly pulls people over whom are doing nothing wrong and is very mean to them.
immoral_consequence: Victors community grows to hate him and doesn't cooperate whenever major criminal activity occurs.

norm: It's wrong to be involved in organized crime.
situation: John is in his usual Brooklyn hangout when some known Mafioso come in and ask John if he's willing to help them out with a task.
intention: John wants to respond to the mafioso.
moral_action: John tells the mafioso that he respects him, but he cannot involve himself in such activities.
moral_consequence: John is allowed to continue his day and continues his crime free life  living happily.
immoral_action: John tells the mafioso that he'd be glad to do a task as he's sure the mafia will repay him bigtime later.
immoral_consequence: john slowly becomes a criminal over crime doing worse and worse crimes until he goes to prison.

norm: It's rude to fart loudly in class.
situation: Jim has eaten a lot of beans for lunch and is feeling extremely gassy in his next class.
intention: Jim wants to release the extra gas build up.
moral_action: Jim asks to be excused to go to the bathroom.
moral_consequence: Jim gets to fart in the bathroom without anyone else noticing it.
immoral_action: Jim lets it rip while sitting in the middle of his class.
immoral_consequence: Jim is embarrassed when everyone reacts to his bodily functions.

norm: It's inappropriate to relieve yourself in dressing rooms.
situation: Tommy is trying on jeans at the mall when suddenly he gets the urge to pee.
intention: Tommy wants to relieve himself of the urge to pee.
moral_action: Tommy leaves his clothes in the dressing room and asks the salesman if he can use their bathroom.
moral_consequence: Tommy is relieved and can resume trying on jeans.
immoral_action: Tommy decides to pee onto the dressing room floor since no one can see him.
immoral_consequence: The salesman hears Tommy peeing, and calls mall security.

norm: It's disappointing to hear that you can't keep a dog.
situation: Rachel finds a dog in the street and bring it home to her father Daniel.
intention: Daniel wants to get the dog out of the house.
moral_action: Daniel tells Rachel that the dog need her help to get home.
moral_consequence: Rachel takes the dog through the neighborhood to find its owner.
immoral_action: Daniel tells Rachel that she can't have a dog.
immoral_consequence: Rachel starts crying.

norm: It's rude to tell someone else that they're parenting poorly.
situation: Cindy hears that Sarah is sending her kids back to school in the fall.
intention: Cindy wants to comment on the siutation.
moral_action: Cindy suggests that she can help tutor Sarah's kids as an alternative.
moral_consequence: Sarah keeps her kids home instead of sending them to school.
immoral_action: Cindy criticizes Sarah for risking her kids' health during the pandemic.
immoral_consequence: Sarah tells Cindy to keep her opinions to herself.

norm: It's unacceptable to be rude to a friend's father.
situation: Karen is at her friend Jim's house when Jim's father Henry comes home and tells Karen to go back home for dinner.
intention: Karen wants to respond to Henry's demand.
moral_action: Karen tells Henry that of course, she will leave if he wishes since it's his house, after all.
moral_consequence: Jim is happy to see that Karen is obeying his father and that she has manners.
immoral_action: "Karen tells Henry to ""eat it"" as she's not going to go home just because he told her since it was Jim who invited her."
immoral_consequence: Jim is embarrassed  and nervous as now he's being put into a conundrum between supporting his father or his friend.

norm: It's good to be appreciative of your teacher's help.
situation: Jasmine, Mary's teacher, keeps mentioning being hungry while tutoring Mary.
intention: Mary wants Jasmine to stop talking about being hungry.
moral_action: Mary gives Jasmine a chocolate bar to Jasmine and thanks Jasmine for tutoring her.
moral_consequence: Jasmine offers to continue tutoring Mary.
immoral_action: Mary reminds Jasmine that she can eat in thirty minutes after her tutoring session is over.
immoral_consequence: Jasmine is shocked by Mary's rude statement, and she finishes the study session early.

norm: Plagiarism isn't okay.
situation: Harley is taking a difficult class.
intention: Harley wants to get a good grade on her final essay.
moral_action: Harley works very hard to write the best essay they can.
moral_consequence: Harley gets a good grade because of her efforts.
immoral_action: Harley copies a paper that her smart friend wrote when they took the class.
immoral_consequence: Harley gets caught and suspended from school.

norm: You shouldn't talk back to your boss.
situation: Greg is in a work meeting with his boss where he disagrees with what is said.
intention: Greg wants to express his opinion.
moral_action: Greg approaches his boss after the meeting and expresses his concerns.
moral_consequence: Greg's boss praises him for handling the situation well and promises to look into his concerns.
immoral_action: Greg loudly berates his boss and calls her stupid.
immoral_consequence: Greg is fired.

norm: You should listen when your boss reprimands you.
situation: Rob who is always late for work, is at his desk when his manager, Giancarlo tells him to stop being late every day.
intention: Rob wants to respond to Giancarlo.
moral_action: Rob tells Giancarlo that he's truly sorry and that he'll get his act together and come on time.
moral_consequence: Giancarlo is happy to see Rob taking feedback to heart and working on improving his punctuality.
immoral_action: Rob tells Giancarlo that he doesn't care what he thinks because he's been doing it forever and it wasn't a problem before.
immoral_consequence: Giancarlo is livid that Rob is insubordinate and not accepting the most basic of feedback.

norm: It's wrong to throw drinks on people.
situation: Lance is drinking soda near a soccer game when he sees some fans of the opposing team walking by.
intention: Lance wants to annoy the other fans.
moral_action: Lance calls out to the other fans and makes some light jokes about their team.
moral_consequence: The fans return the jokes, and both sides have a good laugh.
immoral_action: Lance chucks his drink at the opposing fans, hitting one on the side of the head.
immoral_consequence: The fan runs over and assaults Lance, starting a large fight.

norm: It's wrong to idealize violent groups
situation: Bob talks about how awesome pirates to his buddy Pete.
intention: Pete wants to give a disagreeing opinion.
moral_action: Pete points out that pirates plundered towns and killed people.
moral_consequence: Bob concedes that's valid, but he still thinks pirates' stance on democracy and equal pay made them progressive for the era.
immoral_action: Pete points out that the British Empire's many conquests in that same era were much cooler.
immoral_consequence: Bob counters by pointing out the British Empire's ships had grossly unequal pay and people forced to sail by threat of violence.

norm: It's bad behavior to lock kids in their bedroom.
situation: Tina's son has refused to finish an important homework assignment in order to play a video game.
intention: Tina wants to discipline her child.
moral_action: Tina takes away her sons gaming console.
moral_consequence: Tina's son finishes the homework in order to keep playing.
immoral_action: Tina locks her son in his bedroom away from the video game.
immoral_consequence: Tina's son thinks his mom is going overboard.

norm: It is unacceptable to exclude your parents from your wedding.
situation: Jill has been estranged from her parents for a while, and she is sending out invitations to her wedding.
intention: Jill wants to decide if she wants to invite her estranged parents to her wedding.
moral_action: Jill decides that although she has had some differences with her parents she will invite them to her wedding.
moral_consequence: Jill's parents are overjoyed that Jill wants them at her wedding and see it as a way to reconnect with Jill.
immoral_action: Jill decides not to invite her parents to her wedding as it will be too awkward after all this time.
immoral_consequence: Jill's parents are hurt when they learn Jill was married and didn't invite them to the wedding.

norm: You shouldn't fall asleep at work.
situation: Hank is at work in a factory after having stayed up all night with friends.
intention: Hank wants to get some rest eventually.
moral_action: Hank knows that maintaining safety in a factory is important and powers through the day before finally getting a nap at home after work.
moral_consequence: Hank's coworker's benefit from his work ethic and the work day is smooth for the whole workforce.
immoral_action: Hank decides to take a nap on the floor when action in the factory slows down a little midday.
immoral_consequence: In Hank's slumber the factory floor loses a set of eyes which leads to an accident in his work area that serious injuries a coworker.

norm: You should show your parents grace.
situation: John's father Theobold is dying and asks John to come visit him.
intention: John wants his father to know how much he hurt him when he was younger.
moral_action: John comforts his father, tells him briefly that he forgives his father for any wrongs that occurred and says he loves him.
moral_consequence: Theobold feels loved and forgiven and stays healthy for a longer time.
immoral_action: John visits his dad and tells him how angry he is at him and how he'll never forget all the wrongs he did to him.
immoral_consequence: Theobold is upset and gets sicker and dies thinking John doesn't love him.

norm: It's good to do your chores.
situation: Sam is in the kitchen where he sees on the fridge a list of chores left for him by his wife.
intention: Sam wants to decide what to do next.
moral_action: Sam decides he's going to knock out the first item on the list of chores.
moral_consequence: Sam's wife sees that Sam is doing chores that she asked and is delighted about it.
immoral_action: Sam decides he's going to make himself a giant sandwich and proceeds to mess up the kitchen doing so.
immoral_consequence: Sam's wife is angered when she sees that Sam ignored her list and instead made a bigger mess.

norm: It's best to not keep things from your parents.
situation: John is being bullied at school.
intention: John wants the bullying to stop.
moral_action: John tells his parents about the bullying.
moral_consequence: John's parents intervene and the bullying stops.
immoral_action: John asks the bully to stop.
immoral_consequence: John continues to be bullied.

norm: It's inappropriate to talk about controversial topics in polite company.
situation: Bob is at a party talking with some new acquaintances.
intention: Bob wants to keep the conversation going with an interesting topic.
moral_action: Bob says that he took a trip to a national park recently and really enjoyed the view.
moral_consequence: The acquaintances start sharing their own travel stories.
immoral_action: Bob says that face masks during the coronavirus pandemic are not very helpful.
immoral_consequence: The acquaintances scoff and start arguing with Bob, calling him a fool.

norm: It's wrong to refuse to do your job.
situation: Henry is at the dump where he works when his boss Bill tells him to move some particularly foul garbage to another location.
intention: Henry wants to respond to Bill's request.
moral_action: Henry tells Bill that he'll get right on it and asks him where he wants the pile moved.
moral_consequence: Bill is happy that Henry didn't make a fuss about the dirty job and is impressed with his work ethic.
immoral_action: Henry tells Bill that the job is disgusting, and he's not doing it and Bill should ask a more junior employee.
immoral_consequence: Bill is angry that Henry is insubordinate and that he's refusing a direct order and vows to speak to senior management.

norm: You shouldn't drive without insurance because it's illegal.
situation: Jill had just bought a new car, but she hadn't gotten insurance on it yet.
intention: Jill wants to get home.
moral_action: Jill goes online and adds the new car to her current insurance policy.
moral_consequence: Jill is able to safely drive her car back to her house.
immoral_action: Jill drives the car home without putting the proper insurance on the car.
immoral_consequence: Jill is stopped by a cop and given a ticket for not being properly insured.

norm: You shouldn't commit arson.
situation: Another person was shot by the cops so James decided to go violently protest.
intention: Jamie wants to cause chaos in the city.
moral_action: Jamie spray paints on the windows of a CVS the name of the person killed by police.
moral_consequence: An employee has to clean up the spray paint, but no employees have to lose their job.
immoral_action: Jamie set off fireworks inside a CVS in order to help light a fire inside it.
immoral_consequence: The employees lose their jobs, and it takes a while before the building is built back.

norm: It's not okay to force your religion on other people.
situation: Xavier is a Christian and was invited to his friend Sam's bar-mitzvah.
intention: Xavier wants to discuss his church with Sam and the party guests.
moral_action: Xavier discusses how he loves going to church and is glad to have met people who also love their faith.
moral_consequence: Xavier makes friends with Sam's guests, and they all have a great time.
immoral_action: Xavier tells Sam and his friends that, according to his pastor, the only way to get into heaven is through Jesus, and those who don't accept him are sinners.
immoral_consequence: Xavier looks foolish and Sam and friends are noticeably uncomfortable.

norm: You shouldn't buy more than your budget can take.
situation: Roger's rent is due, but he really wants to buy a new pair of boots.
intention: Roger wants to make a decision.
moral_action: Roger puts off purchasing the boots until after his bills are paid.
moral_consequence: Roger is able to not end up in additional debt by budgeting correctly.
immoral_action: Roger decides to buy the new boots and worry later.
immoral_consequence: When Roger writes his rent check it bounces, and he owes the bank additional fees.

norm: You should not disturb someone when they are at work.
situation: James often has friends over for beers while his wife Samantha works from home.
intention: James wants to include Samantha in the fun.
moral_action: James makes sure to wait until Samantha is finished with work to tell her about all the fun he had.
moral_consequence: Samantha is able to do her work in peace and without disruptions.
immoral_action: James runs into Samantha's office anytime something funny happens to tell her about it.
immoral_consequence: James walks in drunk and belligerent during one of Samantha's video conferences, leaving her thoroughly embarrassed in front of her colleagues.

norm: It's manipulative to try to force a partner into marriage.
situation: Jake and Harry have been together for years.
intention: Jake wants Harry to marry him.
moral_action: Jake proposes to Harry at the bar they met at.
moral_consequence: "Harry resoundingly says ""Yes"" to the proposal."
immoral_action: Jake tells Harry that he will kill himself if he doesn't marry him.
immoral_consequence: Harry worries for Jake's mental health and encourages him to see a therapist.

norm: You shouldn't peek at someone's text messages.
situation: Chris's best friend Josh has been very evasive lately, and Chris notices even more while he's hanging out with Josh today.
intention: Chris wants to help Josh out.
moral_action: Chris asks Josh straight up what's going on, and why he is acting this way.
moral_consequence: Josh tells Chris about something going on in his life that he isn't handling well, and Chris understands Josh better.
immoral_action: Josh goes to the bathroom and leaves his phone on the table, so Chris picks it up and looks at some text messages.
immoral_consequence: Josh comes out of the bathroom and sees Chris going through his phone, and storms out of the house angry.

norm: You shouldn't believe that religions don't deserve respect.
situation: Gavin hasn't been apart of a religion for a long time, unlike his family.
intention: Gavin wants to join a new religion.
moral_action: Gavin chooses a popular, traditionally moral religion in his country to follow.
moral_consequence: Gavin's family is proud of him for maintaining a good image and Gavin is pleased at them.
immoral_action: Gavin joins the 'Church of Satan' and reads all their literature.
immoral_consequence: Gavin's family chastises Gavin for picking such a selfish religion and Gavin feels insulted.

norm: It's wrong to be intimidating to students.
situation: Jack is preparing Halloween for his students and bought various foods, scary costumes, and gifts.
intention: Jack wants to hand out candy to his students.
moral_action: Jack hands the kids candy without a scary costume that night.
moral_consequence: The kids get a lot of candy from Jack and Jack feels generous and happy.
immoral_action: Jack wears a scary costume in front of the children and shouts at them while handing out candy.
immoral_consequence: The kids get very upset at Jack and start to cry as Jack apologizes.

norm: It's bad to ignore your grandparents.
situation: Fiona has a big date this evening but it happens to be on the day her grandparents are visiting her and her family.
intention: Fiona wants to look good for her date.
moral_action: Fiona spends a few minutes of quality time  with her grandparents and then works quickly to look nice for her evening out.
moral_consequence: Fiona's grandparents are over joyed that their granddaughter spends time with them.
immoral_action: Fiona skips chatting with her grandparents and focuses on putting together a complete look for her date.
immoral_consequence: Fiona's grandparents both cry that they did not get to see their precious granddaughter on this trip.

norm: It is rude to use your phone in church.
situation: Jay is attending mass.
intention: Jay wants to talk with his friend.
moral_action: Jay leaves the church service and goes to his friend's house.
moral_consequence: Jay spends some quality time conversing with his buddy.
immoral_action: Jay takes out his cell phone and calls his friend.
immoral_consequence: Jay is asked to leave the church for disturbing the service.

norm: It is wrong to use school paper for something wasteful.
situation: Cody was sitting at his desk with a stack of loose leaf paper.
intention: Cody wants to use the paper.
moral_action: Cody uses the paper to work on his writing assignment that the teacher had given him.
moral_consequence: Cody's teacher is pleased that Cody has worked on the assignment and lets him have free time.
immoral_action: Cody uses his school paper to make paper airplanes which he threw around the class.
immoral_consequence: Cody's teacher fusses at him for wasting the paper and makes him stay inside for recess.

norm: People shouldn't be loudly proud of minor achievements.
situation: James managed to bring his grade in history up from a D to a C.
intention: James wants to feel better about himself.
moral_action: James continues to work hard and manages to bring his grade up to an A.
moral_consequence: James' parents and teachers give him lots of praise and recognition.
immoral_action: James tells everyone he knows about how he's now getting a C in history.
immoral_consequence: When James is out of earshot, his friends say he really needs to reassess his life.

norm: You shouldn't do illegal things.
situation: John, a postal worker who is very passionate about his political party, is sorting through some mail and notices a bag of ballots for the opposing party.
intention: John wants his political party to win the next election, which is forecast to be a very close race.
moral_action: John now realizes the opposing political party has a lot of support, so he organizes a rally to gather more support for his own party.
moral_consequence: John's party wins due to John's actions and John is praised for his efforts.
immoral_action: John throws the bag of ballots in the trash.
immoral_consequence: John's party wins but John's actions are discovered following an investigation and John is fired and arrested.

norm: You should do what your teacher tells you to
situation: Victor's teacher asked the class to write a tasteful little poem, which they will then read aloud in class.
intention: Victor wants to make the students laugh.
moral_action: Victor composes a cute and funny poem.
moral_consequence: The teacher is amused by the poem, like everyone else, and gives Victor an A.
immoral_action: Victor writes a funny poem full of expletives.
immoral_consequence: Victor's teacher gives him an E for the poem.

norm: You shouldn't let people get away with DUIs.
situation: George, a police officer, just caught his neighbor driving under the influence; he feels bad because he knows his neighbor needs his car for his job.
intention: George wants to help his neighbor.
moral_action: George decides to impound his neighbor's car, but tells him he will give him a ride to work every morning.
moral_consequence: George's neighbor goes home safely, without hurting anyone.
immoral_action: George decides to ignore his neighbor's DUI violation and give him a chance.
immoral_consequence: George's neighbor keeps driving home and ends up hitting a pedestrian.

norm: It's taboo to deal drugs.
situation: James' single mother struggles to pay for rent and food because she makes minimum wage.
intention: James wants to help his mother pay the bills.
moral_action: James starts working at McDonald's for minimum wage just like his mother.
moral_consequence: James turns over all his salary to his mother, leaving their family slightly less desperate.
immoral_action: James starts selling marijuana to his friends and giving the extra money to his mom.
immoral_consequence: James is eventually arrested, but his mother is able to keep paying the bills with the cash he hid.

norm: You should put kids in detention that break the rules.
situation: Margo was teaching her English class, but she couldn't get Billy to stop talking.
intention: Margo wants to continue her lesson.
moral_action: Margo gives Billy detention and sends him out of the room, before continuing her lesson.
moral_consequence: The other kids in class are finally able to hear what Margo was saying.
immoral_action: Margo keeps on with her lesson even though Billy was talking over her.
immoral_consequence: Some of the kids in the class couldn't hear Margo and didn't understand the lesson.

norm: It is bad to keep things from the police.
situation: Will witnessed a crime in his neighborhood near his home and the police are questioning him about it.
intention: Will wants to keep his name out of the report for his safety.
moral_action: Will exercises his right not to talk to the police and later submits an anonymous tip.
moral_consequence: The police arrest the criminal based on the information and the neighborhood is safer.
immoral_action: Will states to the officer that he did not see anything.
immoral_consequence: The police cannot locate the criminal who is free to commit more crimes.

norm: It's wrong to be misogynistic.
situation: Josh has narrowed down the applicants for his new engineering position down to a highly qualified girl and a friendly boy.
intention: Josh wants to hire someone.
moral_action: Josh reaches out to another person in the industry for advice on how to judge the final applications.
moral_consequence: Josh learns how to make fair decisions from the other person.
immoral_action: Josh hires the boy based on stereotypical views that women dislike engineering.
immoral_consequence: Josh is called out by his other employees when they discover his actions.

norm: It is wrong to engage in criminal activities
situation: David is in a convenience store and see the candy aisle.
intention: David wants a candy bar.
moral_action: David takes his candy bar to the cashier and pays for it.
moral_consequence: The store owner is glad to be doing business.
immoral_action: When the cashier is not looking, David hides the candy bar in his shirt and leaves.
immoral_consequence: The store owner's lose money that they paid for the candy bar.

norm: It's bad to receive personal calls at work
situation: Jenna just starting dating a new girlfriend and can't stop thinking about her.
intention: Jenna wants to talk to her new crush.
moral_action: Jenna uses one of her personal days to stay home with her new lover.
moral_consequence: Jenna feels closer to her new lover, like they're really getting to know the deepest parts of each other.
immoral_action: Jenna takes numerous personal calls from her new lover at her job as a receptionist.
immoral_consequence: Jenna's supervisor reprimands her and puts a note in her personnel file.

norm: It's not acceptable to make life harder for your neighbors
situation: Bob has a big pile of leaves on his lawn next to his neighbors' fence.
intention: Bob wants to get the leaves off of his lawn.
moral_action: Bob rakes the leaves and throws them away in a big trash bin.
moral_consequence: Bob's neighbors don't have to do much yard work that week as their yard is clean.
immoral_action: Bob throws the leaves over the fence for his neighbors to deal with.
immoral_consequence: Bob's neighbors must work to rake up and dispose of the leaves.

norm: It's frowned upon to not have a job.
situation: Keith was living with his parents in the basement of their house.
intention: Keith wanted to play video games every day.
moral_action: Keith got a job doing video game reviews on the internet.
moral_consequence: Keith was able to save up enough money to get a place of his own.
immoral_action: Instead of working, Keith played video games in the basement everyday.
immoral_consequence: Keith's parents eventually kicked him out of their house because of his laziness.

norm: It's bad to track people's movements without their consent.
situation: Carrie is afraid that her boyfriend will cheat on her.
intention: Carrie wants peace of mind.
moral_action: Carrie decides that it's better to not just assume her boyfriend will cheat.
moral_consequence: Carrie calms down and is able to enjoy the relationship.
immoral_action: Carrie installs an app on her boyfriend's phone to track where he is 24/7.
immoral_consequence: Carrie follows her boyfriend on the maps excessively and has no time for anything else.

norm: It is rude to smoke in someone's home.
situation: Jeremy is over at his friend Nick's house when he feels a craving for nicotine.
intention: Jeremy wants to smoke a cigarette.
moral_action: Jeremy tells Nick he has to go outside to have a cigarette.
moral_consequence: Nick will think Jeremy is considerate of his house and space.
immoral_action: Jeremy lights up a cigarette in his friend Nick's house.
immoral_consequence: Nick will kick Jeremy out of his house and tell him he smells.

norm: It is wrong to be possessive.
situation: Yves' girlfriend meets a new group of friends and starts to spend a lot of time with them and less with him.
intention: Yves wants to spend more time with his girlfriend.
moral_action: Yves asks if he can tag along with his girlfriend and meet her new friends.
moral_consequence: Yves' girlfriend introduces him to her friends and is happy when they seem to get along well.
immoral_action: Yves tells his girlfriend that she has to choose between him or her new group of friends.
immoral_consequence: Yves' girlfriend gets upset about how controlling he is being and breaks up with him.

norm: You shouldn't be with someone who sells drugs.
situation: Jasmine is invited by a weed seller Jacob to the upcoming school dance.
intention: Jasmine wants to attend the dance.
moral_action: Jasmine finds a partner for the dance on her school's facebook page.
moral_consequence: Jasmine and her date fall in love, and they get into a nice relationship.
immoral_action: Jasmine attends the dance with Jacob.
immoral_consequence: Jasmine is arrested with Jacob when the police catch Jacob with weed in his pocket.

norm: It's wrong to drive while on painkillers.
situation: Cathy is at home and took a large dose of Oxycontin to deal with pain and is feeling dizzy, but she is also hungry.
intention: Cathy wants to get food to eat.
moral_action: Cathy places a call for food delivery to her home.
moral_consequence: Cathy gets her food without incident.
immoral_action: Cathy drives to a fast food restaurant to get food.
immoral_consequence: Cathy drives erratically and increases her chances for an accident as she is dizzy.

norm: It's good to be responsible when it comes to school.
situation: Jenny has an important homework assignment due tomorrow for a class she's been struggling with.
intention: Jenny wants to spend time with her friends.
moral_action: Jenny spends an hour socializing with her friends then does her homework assignment.
moral_consequence: Jenny gets a decent grade on her homework and starts to understand the class material a bit better.
immoral_action: Jenny spends all night with her friends and doesn't even start her homework assignment.
immoral_consequence: Jenny gets a failing grade on her homework assignment and continues to have a hard time in the class.

norm: You should always care about your education.
situation: Simon is normally a good student, but has recently gotten a job that takes up a lot of time.
intention: Simon wants to work at a job.
moral_action: Simon takes a less time-consuming job so that he can focus on his studies.
moral_consequence: Simon gets good grades and is able to attend his preferred college when he graduates.
immoral_action: Simon continues working at the time-consuming job and spends less time on his schoolwork.
immoral_consequence: Simon's grades drop, and he can't get into his preferred college.

norm: It is rude to tell others what to do.
situation: Jenna and her brother James were playing in their family's backyard.
intention: Jenna wants to play jump rope with someone.
moral_action: Jenna asks her brother to play jump rope, and when he says no, she goes next door to ask her friend.
moral_consequence: Jenna and her friend have a great time playing games with the jump rope while her brother plays with the dog.
immoral_action: Jenna tells her brother that he has to stop what he's doing and play jump rope with her.
immoral_consequence: Jenna's brother holds the jump rope but complains the whole time.

norm: It's bad to bring a knife to school.
situation: It is the morning and it's Mark's birthday today and before school his mother has given him a Swiss Army Knife as a gift.
intention: Mark wants to store the knife somewhere.
moral_action: Mark stores the knife in his sock drawer at home before going off to school.
moral_consequence: Mark comes home later and happily plays with his new shiny, red knife.
immoral_action: Mark stores the knife in his school backpack and ends up taking it to school with him.
immoral_consequence: The principal finds Mark with his knife and expels Mark from school for having weapons.

norm: It is unprofessional to consume alcohol while working.
situation: James works in retail and has had a stressful day, so he takes a break and thinks of how to relieve the stress.
intention: James wants to do something to relieve his stress.
moral_action: James takes a few walks around the building to clear his head.
moral_consequence: James goes back to work refreshed and does his job well.
immoral_action: James decides that everything is better with alcohol, so he drinks a few beers while on break.
immoral_consequence: James misquotes a customer on a price, costing his company over $50.

norm: It's rude not to tell somebody your name
situation: Betsy is greeting her stressed out coworker for the first time.
intention: Betsy wants to make her coworker feel better.
moral_action: Betsy shows coworker around the office and gives them her contact information.
moral_consequence: Betsy's coworker makes it to the office without help the next day due to Betsy's directions.
immoral_action: Betsy tells her coworker a joke about her name.
immoral_consequence: Her coworker feels foolish in front of others when they ask questions using the fake name.

norm: You should respect your parents and not say rude things.
situation: After being nagged by his parents, Eddy is eating dinner with his parents and his parent's boss.
intention: Eddy wants to express his frustrations.
moral_action: Eddy vents his feelings on his personal blog.
moral_consequence: Eddy feels better after getting all of the negative feelings out of him.
immoral_action: Eddy makes fart jokes about his parents.
immoral_consequence: Eddy is grounded for misbehaving.

norm: You shouldn't write people notes when you're in study hall.
situation: Curtis is doing schoolwork in a quiet classroom when his friend Joey passes him a piece of paper.
intention: Curtis wants to respond to Joey.
moral_action: Curtis reads Joeys paper and whispers to him that they will talk after class.
moral_consequence: Curtis and Joey are able to avoid getting in trouble at school.
immoral_action: Curtis reads Joeys paper, writes him back and gives it back to him.
immoral_consequence: The instructor notices and both Curtis and Joey get detention.

norm: It's discouraged to drink alcohol when you're a minor.
situation: Josh has plans to go to his friend's party, which is for teenagers, on the weekend where some people are planning on bringing alcohol.
intention: Josh wants to have fun at his friend's Saturday celebration.
moral_action: Josh attends his friend's bash, where he stays sober and participates in team online games.
moral_consequence: His friend appreciates that he didn't drink, and they become best friends.
immoral_action: Josh goes to his friends party, drinks whiskey and goes swimming at their pool.
immoral_consequence: His friend's parents find out the following week and ban Josh from coming back to their home.

norm: You shouldn't tell strangers what to do
situation: Annie was walking down the sidewalk towards the theater and saw a panhandler.
intention: Annie wanted to go to the movies.
moral_action: Annie was walking down the sidewalk, saw a panhandler, gave him money, and then went to the movies.
moral_consequence: Annie was able to walk down the sidewalk without falling.
immoral_action: Annie told the panhandler to quit begging on the street and then continued walking toward the theater.
immoral_consequence: The panhandler stuck out his leg and Annie tripped and fell before getting up and going to the movies.

norm: It's rude to put your nose in a neighbor's business.
situation: James heard his neighbor yelling at his teenage daughter before she stormed out of their house.
intention: James wants to find out what happened at his neighbors' house.
moral_action: James bakes cookies and takes some over to his neighbor to cheer him up.
moral_consequence: James' neighbor makes small talk and eventually vents about his daughter's stubbornness.
immoral_action: James knocks on the neighbor's door to ask numerous questions about why he was yelling.
immoral_consequence: James' neighbor tells him to get off his property before he calls the cops.

norm: You shouldn't let criminal behavior become an obsession.
situation: Mark used to illegally download movies, but now he has a job.
intention: Mark wants to watch more movies.
moral_action: Mark buys multiple streaming services so that he can watch as many movies as he can.
moral_consequence: Mark is able to support content creators and gets to support content creators.
immoral_action: Mark continues to download movies, because he loves how easy they are to obtain.
immoral_consequence: Mark is taking money away from content creators and is breaking the law.

norm: A kid should listen to their parents.
situation: Alice is helping her dad cook pasta in the kitchen, and her dad tells her not to touch the pot of boiling water he just took off the stove.
intention: Alice wants to put some pasta in the boiling water.
moral_action: Alice puts the pasta in the water and keeps her other hand in her pocket.
moral_consequence: Alice eats some of the cooked pasta and feels accomplished.
immoral_action: Alice touches the side of the pot of boiling hot water as she puts the pasta in.
immoral_consequence: Alice burns her hand, which hurts for a long time.

norm: Is bad to blow off work for no reason.
situation: Art is laying bricks in the hot sun, constructing a wall.
intention: Art wants to give art's tired muscles a rest.
moral_action: Art sits under a tree for a few minutes with a bottle of water and recovers in the shade before returning to work.
moral_consequence: An obligation is fulfilled and Art's work history remains unblemished, the wall is completed for the customer.
immoral_action: Art leaves the wall behind and gets drunk with Art's friends.
immoral_consequence: Cement dries inside a mixer, ruining the mixer, Art does't get paid for the wall, and owes money for a new mixer, the wall is not built for the customer.

norm: It's wrong to litter and throw garbage around
situation: Billy buys a hotdog from a stand and eats it, but what to do with the wrapper?
intention: Billy wants to decide what to do with the wrapper.
moral_action: Billy finds a trash can and throws the wrapper away inside it.
moral_consequence: Soon the trash can is full of hotdog wrappers people threw away.
immoral_action: Billy decides do take the wrapper and threw it on the ground.
immoral_consequence: Soon the area is covered with hotdog wrappers people threw on the ground.

norm: It is rude to be bossy.
situation: Jenna was working with her classmates to make a poster for history class.
intention: Jenna wants to make sure they get an A.
moral_action: Jenna concentrates on doing her part of the poster well and gives her classmates occasional advice.
moral_consequence: Jenna's group gets an A and thanks her for her help.
immoral_action: Jenna tells her classmates exactly how to do every part of the poster.
immoral_consequence: Jenna's group gets an A, but all of her group mates are angry at her by the end.

norm: It is expected to give gifts on valentine's day.
situation: Rose looks at the calendar for the month of February.
intention: Rose wants to make her partner feel loved.
moral_action: Rose makes reservations for two at a fancy restaurant on February 14 and buys her partner the best champagne in the house.
moral_consequence: Rose's partner tell her how special she feels after their wonderful champagne toast on Valentine's Day.
immoral_action: Rose buys her partner a book about Black History month.
immoral_consequence: Rose's partner is unhappy with her for forgetting Valentine's Day.

norm: You shouldn't access an online account that isn't yours.
situation: Tom's roommate Jim has an Amazon Prime account but Tom does not.
intention: Tom wants to watch a movie that's on Amazon Prime Video.
moral_action: Tom starts a trial Amazon Prime membership so that he can watch the movie.
moral_consequence: Tom really enjoys watching the film.
immoral_action: Without asking Tom goes into Jim's room, finds his password, and logs into his Prime account to watch the movie.
immoral_consequence: Jim comes home and gets angry at Tom for violating his trust and using his account without permission.

norm: It is good to get your work done.
situation: Jim has work left to do that his boss wanted done that day even though it's already the time he usually goes home.
intention: Jim wants to go home.
moral_action: Jim stays at work an extra hour and finishes all his work before going home.
moral_consequence: Jim is commended by his boss for staying late and getting all his work done.
immoral_action: Jim goes straight home leaving his work unfinished because he feels tired already.
immoral_consequence: Jim is reprimanded by his boss the next day for not finishing his work on time.

norm: You shouldn't question your boss.
situation: Bruce is hard at work but his boss keeps telling him to use a much more complex method.
intention: Bruce wants to get his work done.
moral_action: Bruce listens to his boss and does things the new way.
moral_consequence: Once he gets used to it, Bruce is actually faster with the new method.
immoral_action: Bruce keeps doing things the way he already understands.
immoral_consequence: The project is a failure because Bruce kept doing things wrong.

norm: It's unacceptable to forbid your significant other to do something.
situation: Anne has plans to go hang gliding alone, but her husband Frank is against the idea.
intention: Frank wants to keep his wife from hang gliding alone.
moral_action: Frank tells Anne that he wants to go hang gliding with her.
moral_consequence: Anne thinks it's a good idea, and she goes hang gliding with Frank.
immoral_action: Frank tells his wife that he won't allow her to go hang glidng alone.
immoral_consequence: Anne tells Frank that she can do whatever she wants and goes anyway.

norm: You shouldn't have people over when you're house sitting.
situation: Dick is watching his friend's home while they are on vacation.
intention: Dick doesn't want to be lonely while he's house sitting.
moral_action: Dick video-calls an old friend, and they have a long chat to catch up with each other's lives.
moral_consequence: Dick has an enjoyable time house sitting for his friend.
immoral_action: Dick invites a group of friends to the home that he's house sitting and has a wild party that trashes the house.
immoral_consequence: Dick has to pay for repairs to his friend's home.

norm: It's irresponsible to let your child quit school
situation: Ben's son is partway through high school when he decides that he wants to quit school, work in his spare time, and get his GED on his own.
intention: Ben wants his son to succeed in work.
moral_action: Ben encourages his son to finish high school and work after school hours instead.
moral_consequence: Ben's son gets his GED and is eligible to go to college and to have better jobs.
immoral_action: Ben decides that his son would get good work experience and lets him carry out his plan.
immoral_consequence: Ben's son gets work experience but never goes back to finish his GED, locking him out of better employment.

norm: It's wrong to refuse to leave somewhere you don't belong.
situation: Charles wanders onto private property and is asked to leave by the owner of the property.
intention: Charles wants to respond to the owner.
moral_action: Charles apologizes to the owner and quickly vacates the private property.
moral_consequence: Charles enjoys the rest of his day without any further issues.
immoral_action: Charles plants his foot and refuses to budge an inch.
immoral_consequence: Charles gets arrested by the police and is forcefully removed.

norm: It's wrong to disobey your boss.
situation: Emmy's boss wants her to spy on a rival business to find out what they're doing differently.
intention: Emmy has to respond to her boss.
moral_action: Emmy agrees to spy on the company and learns a lot of big secrets.
moral_consequence: Emmy's boss is proud of her and thinks about giving her a pay raise.
immoral_action: Emmy refuses to spy on anyone because she says it goes against her values.
immoral_consequence: Emmy's boss doesn't think Emmy is dedicated enough and thinks about firing her.

norm: It's bad to commit crimes.
situation: Warren is a powerful CEO and is in the process of acquiring a large company.
intention: Warren wants to let others know about the big news.
moral_action: Warren tells his friends that he has big news coming, but he doesn't tell them what the news is.
moral_consequence: Warren is following SEC regulations and will not find himself in any trouble.
immoral_action: Warren tells his friends every detail so that they invest in Warren's company before the big news is announced.
immoral_consequence: Warren is not following insider trading laws and is putting his company at risk.

norm: You shouldn't lie about military service
situation: Donald can't afford to pay for his meal, and the restaurant offers a military discount.
intention: Donald wants to pay for his food.
moral_action: Donald moves money in his savings account to his checking account to cover the bill.
moral_consequence: Donald is able to enjoy his meal without worrying about finances.
immoral_action: Donald tells the waitress that he's in the Navy.
immoral_consequence: Donald is exposed in front of a large group of people when the waitress asks for his id.

norm: Not every situation needs your opinion.
situation: Steph has strong views against abortion and her friend is upset after just having one.
intention: Steph wants to express her views on the topic.
moral_action: Steph listens to her friend and later posts her opinion on an online forum.
moral_consequence: Steph's friend is able to get it off her chest and move on.
immoral_action: Steph brings up her stance on the issue to her friend immediately.
immoral_consequence: Steph's friend feels even worse about what she has already done.

norm: You should be very polite when visiting other people's home.
situation: Mary is visiting her in-laws' house for dinner one night when she notices a tasty looking pie cooling in the window.
intention: Mary wants to try the pie.
moral_action: Mary inquires about the pie and says that it looks very tasty.
moral_consequence: Mary and her in-laws each have a slice of pie after dinner and enjoy its delicious flavor.
immoral_action: Without asking Mary removes the pie from the window and scarfs down most of it using her hands.
immoral_consequence: Mary's in-laws' are offended by her rudeness and lack of manners and form a negative impression of her.

norm: You should not try to control your partner.
situation: Dave and his wife Sally are at home when Sally gets a phone call and tells Dave she's going to go see her friend Sue.
intention: Dave wants to respond to Sally leaving.
moral_action: Dave tells Sally to drive carefully and to say hi to Sue when she gets there.
moral_consequence: Sally is happy her husband wished her a safe trip and that he's kind enough to send greetings to her friend.
immoral_action: Dave tells Sally that he doesn't like Sue, and he forbids her from going since he thinks Sue is a bad influence.
immoral_consequence: Sally is scared that Dave is trying to keep her away from her friend and angry that he doesn't respect her choices.

norm: You shouldn't break company rules.
situation: Robert is at the office and is about to go on lunch break.
intention: Robert wants to have something to eat.
moral_action: Robert clocks out for his lunch break and grabs some food.
moral_consequence: Robert feels good about himself for not violating company policy.
immoral_action: Robert knows that he's supposed to clock out for his break, but he ignores the rule and gets something to eat.
immoral_consequence: Robert's boss finds out and he gets fired.

norm: You should always help out around the house.
situation: Nicholas' friend has invited him over to watch the football game, but there are chores to do and Nicholas' wife doesn't have time to do them all.
intention: Nicholas wants to watch the football game.
moral_action: Nicholas lets his friend know he won't be joining him and puts the game on the television at home, watching it as he cleans up around the house.
moral_consequence: Nicholas' wife thanks him for his help and cooks him a nice dinner to show her appreciation.
immoral_action: Nicholas goes over to his friend's house to watch the game, leaving his wife to finish up the chores.
immoral_consequence: Nicholas' wife is furious at his behavior and yells at him when he gets back.

norm: You shouldn't go against your roommate's requests.
situation: Marco shares a college dorm with Mike, who has asked for some privacy tonight with his girlfriend, so Marco says he will stay at a friends for the night.
intention: Marco wants to have a place to sleep.
moral_action: Marco leaves right away to go to his friends place to get some rest for the night.
moral_consequence: Mike gets to enjoy a romantic evening with his girlfriend alone.
immoral_action: Marco tells Mike that he will crash at a friends house for the night, but falls asleep at the dorm.
immoral_consequence: Mike is angry because Marco didn't so what he said and now his night is ruined.

norm: It is considerate to obey your parents.
situation: Todd is a teenager who has been told by his parents to eat less candy.
intention: Todd wants to decide on obeying his parents or not.
moral_action: Todd takes his parent's advice and actively limits the amount of candy he eats.
moral_consequence: Todd avoids developing any health issues and does not cause his parents any inconvenience.
immoral_action: Todd decides to ignore his parent's advice and continue eating copious amounts of candy.
immoral_consequence: Todd develops painful cavities that causes his parents to drive him to the dentist.

norm: It's wrong to let the american flag touch the ground
situation: Len is a janitor at a school and has been told by the school administration to take down the flag flying at the entrance.
intention: Len wants to take the flag off the pole and put the flag somewhere.
moral_action: Len takes the flag off the pole, folds it and takes it indoors for storage.
moral_consequence: The students at the school see Len treating the flag with extra respect and learn that it's important to respect the flag.
immoral_action: Len takes the flag off the pole and throws it on the grass nearby.
immoral_consequence: The students at the school see Len disrespecting the flag and assume that it's OK to do so.

norm: Children should respect their elders.
situation: John sees his mother's new car sitting in the driveway.
intention: John wants to drive over  to a friends house to visit.
moral_action: John asks his mother if he can borrow the car.
moral_consequence: John's mother agrees to let him borrow it, and he gets to visit his friend.
immoral_action: John takes the car without asking his mother if he can borrow it.
immoral_consequence: John's mother calls the car in as stolen to the police, and he is arrested.

norm: It's good to obey company policies.
situation: Eric is having a really rough morning at his job and is really exasperated with it.
intention: Eric wants to head out early from work.
moral_action: Eric goes to his boss and asks if he can take some personal time and leave right away.
moral_consequence: Eric gets to go home early and relax after his stressful time at the office.
immoral_action: Eric slides out a side door after lunch without telling anyone he is leaving.
immoral_consequence: Eric is written up by his supervisor for not finishing his shift.

norm: It's frowned upon to not go to class.
situation: Tim, a student in high school, and his friends are talking about skipping class to go skateboarding.
intention: Tim wants to hang out with his friends.
moral_action: Tim convinces his friends to go skateboarding after school instead.
moral_consequence: Tim and his friends have a good time skateboarding and hang out regularly after class.
immoral_action: Tim skips class and goes out skateboarding with his friends.
immoral_consequence: Tim's parents find out, and he is grounded for weeks.

norm: It's bad to drive into a gate.
situation: Noah was visiting his friend who lived in a gated community, but the gate was locked when he got there.
intention: Noah wants to get past the gate.
moral_action: Noah calls his friend who calls the guard at the gate and tells him to let Noah in.
moral_consequence: The guard at the gate waves at Noah as he drives into the community.
immoral_action: Noah drives his car right through the gate and busts it to pieces.
immoral_consequence: The guard at the gate takes down Noah's phone number and calls the police.

norm: It's inappropriate to brag about yourself too much to others.
situation: Kenny is on the high school baseball team and hit three home runs during the game.
intention: Kenny wants to tell his friends about his performance.
moral_action: Kenny tells his friends about his home runs once, when he sees them after the game.
moral_consequence: Kenny's friends will think Kenny is a very good player.
immoral_action: Kenny tells his friends about his home runs every day of the week.
immoral_consequence: Kenny's friends will become annoyed with Kenny and think he brags too much.

norm: It's wrong to start fights with coworkers.
situation: Spencer and Kendall are going to door to door handing out pamphlets for a new business.
intention: Spencer wants to split the pamphlets evenly.
moral_action: Spencer suggests that they split the pamphlets evenly so that they each do the same amount of work.
moral_consequence: Kendall agrees and they finish the job fairly.
immoral_action: Spencer takes half of the pamphlets and throws the other half at Kendall.
immoral_consequence: Kendall is offended and calls their boss to complain about Spencer's behavior.

norm: You should respect the rules of someone's house.
situation: Eric just finished cooking his dinner downstairs with dishes everywhere and his landlord John just came home.
intention: Eric wants to go to bed early and relax.
moral_action: Eric washes the dishes quickly and eats and goes to bed.
moral_consequence: John is very happy and has an easy time cooking dinner for himself.
immoral_action: Eric rushes upstairs with his food, eats and goes to bed without washing anything.
immoral_consequence: John doesn't have any clean dishes to eat with and is very angry at Eric.

norm: You should listen to your employees about problem employees.
situation: Bill owns a store that's losing money when Tim, an employee, tells him that another employee, Scott, is stealing merchandise.
intention: Bill wants to reduce expenses at his store by firing someone.
moral_action: Bill considers what Tim told him about Scott and fires Scott.
moral_consequence: Bill starts making money at his store because he has fewer payroll expenses and also less losses due to theft.
immoral_action: Bill fires Tim because Tim has less seniority at the store than Scott.
immoral_consequence: Bill has fewer payroll expenses but his store keeps losing money because Scott keeps stealing merchandise.

norm: It's good to rebel against dictators.
situation: Juan lives under an oppressive dictator in his home country.
intention: Juan wants to be free.
moral_action: Juan joins a resistance movement against the dictator.
moral_consequence: Juan and the resistance eventually overthrow the dictator.
immoral_action: Juan secretly escapes the country and sneaks into another one.
immoral_consequence: Juan is caught and is deported, then executed for treason.

norm: Arrogance is frowned upon.
situation: Jake just landed a big client for the financial management company he is working at.
intention: Jake wants to be admired by his peers.
moral_action: Jake discusses the new client with his coworkers and is proud, yet modest about the whole thing.
moral_consequence: Jake's coworkers admire his success and love how humble he is.
immoral_action: Jake boasts about securing the client and how he used this clever technique to woo them.
immoral_consequence: Jake's colleagues admire him for being clever but can't stand his bragging.

norm: It's bad to be controlling when in a relationship.
situation: Brenda is about to leave to go see her friend at the mall when she says goodbye to her husband, Dan.
intention: Dan wants to respond to Brenda.
moral_action: Dan tells Brenda to have a great time and to drive safely out there.
moral_consequence: Brenda is happy that Dan wished her a good time and that he cares about her safety.
immoral_action: Dan tells Brenda that she's not going anywhere since she never asked Dan if she was allowed to leave.
immoral_consequence: Brenda is terrified that Dan is trying to prevent her from going and wonders if he's going to get violent.

norm: It's not okay to make your parents upset.
situation: Paulo is hanging out with his friends late on a schoolnight while his parents wait for him to come home.
intention: Paulo wants to head home.
moral_action: Paulo calls his family to let them know where he is and that he's coming home.
moral_consequence: Paulo comes home, and after being told he needs to come home earlier, they all go to bed.
immoral_action: Paulo arrives home after curfew without having let his parents know where he was.
immoral_consequence: Paulo's parents yell at him about how worried they were and ground him.

norm: Leaving events early is not good.
situation: Jake isn't really having a good time at the party his friends invited him to.
intention: Jake wants to enjoy himself more.
moral_action: Jake decides to have a few drinks to loosen up and starts chatting up other party goers.
moral_consequence: Jake's friends notice he's making a good impression on others and are happy for him.
immoral_action: Jake decides to leave the party and go home and read a book.
immoral_consequence: Jake's friends notice he left early and think that's antisocial of him.

norm: It's wrong to be too controlling of your significant other.
situation: Ron and his wife were really looking forward to the big dance.
intention: Ron wanted to show his wife a good time.
moral_action: Ron and his wife went to the dance, and they talked to many different people.
moral_consequence: Ron and his wife had a fantastic time at the dance.
immoral_action: Ron would not let his wife talk to any of the other male attendees, especially the good-looking ones.
immoral_consequence: Ron’s wife resenting his controlling ways, and they had a big fight after the dance.

norm: It is wrong to control who a partner can contact.
situation: Ashley has a romantic partner who has been spending a lot of time with a coworker lately.
intention: Ashley wants to express how uncomfortable this makes them feel.
moral_action: Ashley sits down with her partner and explains her feelings.
moral_consequence: Ashley's partner is understand, and they all hang out together instead.
immoral_action: Ashley demands that her partner immediately cut off all contact with the coworker.
immoral_consequence: Ashley's partner is upset and thinks she is controlling.

norm: It's not nice to cause problems in public places.
situation: Laura is at the mall and sees a few garbage cans overflowing with garbage.
intention: Laura wants to attract the attention of authorities to the problem of garbage at the mall.
moral_action: Laura quietly goes to a security guard at the mall and asks him to contact his boss about the garbage issue.
moral_consequence: The security guard calls his boss, who thanks him for letting him know about the issue.
immoral_action: Laura goes to a security guard at the mall and loudly takes him to task about the garbage cans.
immoral_consequence: A small crowd gathers and starts accusing the security guard of negligence.

norm: You should behave professionally at work.
situation: After a tense team meeting, Wally notices his coworkers standing by the coffee machine looking tense.
intention: Wally wants to create a positive atmosphere.
moral_action: Wally offers to buy everyone snacks from the vending machine after shaking everyone's hand.
moral_consequence: Wally is thanked by his friends for his actions.
immoral_action: Wally impersonates his boss in a loud tone as he grabs a cup of coffee.
immoral_consequence: Wally is written up for his strange behavior.

norm: It is rude to sneak around someone else's property.
situation: Lucy has just moved into a new neighborhood.
intention: Lucy wants to get to know her next door neighbor.
moral_action: Lucy rings the neighbor's doorbell and introduces herself.
moral_consequence: Lucy starts developing a friendly relationship with the neighbor.
immoral_action: Lucy tries to sneak around the neighbor's house and peek into their windows to see what they're like.
immoral_consequence: Lucy gets caught and her neighbor wants nothing to do with her because she violated their boundaries.

norm: It's not okay to be dependent on your phone.
situation: Carol hasn't seen her friend Jimmy in a long time, and is visiting to catch up and reconnect.
intention: Carol wants to be connected to friends.
moral_action: Carol turns her phone on silent and keeps it in her purse to focus fully on the conversation with Jimmy.
moral_consequence: Jimmy feels appreciated and grateful for Carol's undivided attention.
immoral_action: Carol is constantly interrupting the conversation with Jimmy to respond to non-urgent text messages and check her social media pages.
immoral_consequence: Jimmy feels ignored and hurt by Carol's constant focus on her phone.

norm: It's understandable that not everyone wants to have children.
situation: Terri is talking to her friend Alice when Alice mentions she does not want to have children.
intention: Terri wants to respond to Alice mentioning not wanting children.
moral_action: Terri says that not everyone needs to have children and it's fine for Alice to not have kids.
moral_consequence: Alice will feel Terri is a supportive friend.
immoral_action: Terri says that Alice will change her mind and children are for everyone.
immoral_consequence: Alice will feel Terri is annoying and pushy in her views.

norm: It is disrespectful to swear at your parents.
situation: Jim comes home past his curfew and his waiting parents tell him he's grounded for a month.
intention: Jim wants to show his displeasure about being grounded.
moral_action: Jim explain to his parents that he was late because their was a wreck on the highway.
moral_consequence: Jim has his grounding lifted when his parents check the news and see the accident report.
immoral_action: Jim curses out both his parents for being so strict and for grounding him.
immoral_consequence: Jim gets grounded for 6 months instead of 1 month.

norm: You shouldn't expect women to do all the chores.
situation: Harry and Sally live together in a messy apartment and are having company over.
intention: Harry wants the place to be clean before the company arrives.
moral_action: Harry and Sally divide up the house cleaning chores between them.
moral_consequence: Sally appreciates that Harry is willing to do his fair share of cleaning around the apartment.
immoral_action: Harry has Sally clean the place while Harry spends all day looking at Cat memes.
immoral_consequence: Sally is upset that Harry didn't do his fair share of the chores.

norm: It is rude to complain about waiting.
situation: A bored Rachael has been waiting for her food at a restaurant for twenty minutes.
intention: Rachael wants to get rid of her boredom.
moral_action: Rachael plays games on her cellphone until her food arrives.
moral_consequence: Rachael receives a free desert for her  long wait time.
immoral_action: Rachael passes time by making comments about the wait time to the person sitting near her.
immoral_consequence: Rachael feels bad when she notices a waitress crying after walking by her table.

norm: You should punish students who call people names in class.
situation: Ted is a teacher in high school and has a problem student who bullies others.
intention: Ted wants the bully to stop abusing other students.
moral_action: Ted punishes the bully who stops his abusive behavior.
moral_consequence: The abused students feel supported and protected by their teacher.
immoral_action: Ted thinks that by being friendly with the bully instead of punishing him, the bully will feel better about himself and stop being a jerk.
immoral_consequence: The bullying stops but the victims feel that the bully somehow got rewarded for his abusive behavior.

norm: It's good to maintain your privacy.
situation: Lindsey is a very outgoing person who loves to post on social media, and is about to go on a two-week vacation in another country.
intention: Lindsey wants to post about her vacation on social media.
moral_action: Lindsey does not mention her vacation until after she gets back.
moral_consequence: Lindsey has a good time on her vacation and her house remains safe.
immoral_action: Lindsey posts all about her upcoming vacation and when she will be gone.
immoral_consequence: One of the people on her friends list sees this and robs her house while she is away.

norm: You shouldn't take food from others without asking.
situation: John works at an office and is really hungry, and sees a candy bar sitting on the empty desk of a nearby coworker.
intention: John wants to eat.
moral_action: John waits until the coworker comes back and offers to give him a dollar for it.
moral_consequence: The coworker gives it to John for free, just asking that John return the favor every once in a while.
immoral_action: John grabs the candy bar from the empty desk and eats it.
immoral_consequence: The coworker notices the empty candy wrapper in John's hand when he gets back and reports him to management.

norm: You shouldn't squeal on people.
situation: Tom notices his wife underpaid her restaurant bill yesterday, but the waitress didn't notice.
intention: Tom wants to let someone know about the underpaid bill.
moral_action: Tom forgives his wife and only tells his wife about the underpaid bill instead of the restaurant.
moral_consequence: Tom's wife thanks Tom for letting her know and is relieved he didn't make a big deal out of it.
immoral_action: Tom calls the restaurant on the phone letting them know that his wife underpaid.
immoral_consequence: Tom's wife becomes furious at Tom for squealing on her, and feels she doesn't have to explain herself.

norm: It's good to follow advice.
situation: Lana is bored and has free time.
intention: Lana wants to learn to ski.
moral_action: Lana signs up for ski lessons.
moral_consequence: Lana has a great time and becomes a proficient skier.
immoral_action: Lana refuses to take lessons and goes down a slope for advanced skiers.
immoral_consequence: Lana falls and breaks her leg badly.

norm: You shouldn't yell at your teachers.
situation: John is in class passing notes when his teacher Mary notices him and takes the note away.
intention: John wants to react to what just happened.
moral_action: John apologizes to the teacher for being distracted during class and distracting others as well.
moral_consequence: Mary is glad to see John taking responsibility for his mistake and it makes her smile.
immoral_action: John screams at the teacher to give his note back to him, that it's his and that she's mean and unfair.
immoral_consequence: Mary is taken aback by how aggressive and violent John is being and is afraid for her safety.

norm: It is unhealthy to eat too much
situation: Garfield loved to eat pasta, in fact he couldn't stop eating pasta until it was all gone.
intention: Garfield wants to eat more pasta.
moral_action: Garfield decides not to make too much pasta since he knows he won't stop eating it.
moral_consequence: Garfield eats all the pasta he made, and he still feels alright.
immoral_action: Garfield made a lot of pasta, and he kept eating and eating until he was sick.
immoral_consequence: Garfield was sick for a while from eating too much because he couldn't stop eating pasta.

norm: It is good to clean up accidents.
situation: Aaron is a waiter working at a restaurant and drops a tray full of drinks among the tables.
intention: Aaron wants to deal with the mess he created.

immoral_action: Dave decides that his lawn needs a cut and fires up the John Deere inside the garage causing a loud rumble.
immoral_consequence: Dave's neighbors are angry with him for waking them up with his loud noises.

norm: It's not okay to spend your money gambling.
situation: Nathan, a former compulsive gambler, is invited to the casino by his coworkers after work.
intention: Nathan wants to do something fun.
moral_action: Nathan drops by his favorite bar for the night to order something to drink.
moral_consequence: Nathan befriends another former gambler at the bar.
immoral_action: Nathan gambles his entire paycheck on the slot machines at the casino.
immoral_consequence: Nathan is unable to pay his bills.

norm: You shouldn't pee on people's couches.
situation: Marlon was visiting his friend's house and had drank several sodas.
intention: Marlon wants to pee.
moral_action: Marlon excuses himself and goes to the bathroom to pee.
moral_consequence: Marlon and his friend are able to enjoy the rest of their evening together.
immoral_action: Marlon stays sitting on the sofa and pees right where he is.
immoral_consequence: Marlon's friend gets mad at him and tells him to get out of his house.

norm: It's unhealthy to sneeze on others.
situation: Alex has a cold, but had to work today and while on lunch break with his co-worker Mary, he feels a tickle in his nose.
intention: Alex wants to feel relief.
moral_action: Alex knows he's about to sneeze so excuses himself and does so in the bathroom with a tissue.
moral_consequence: Mary is able to remain safe from getting sick.
immoral_action: Alex knows he's about to sneeze but remains seated and gets mucus on Mary.
immoral_consequence: Mary catches Alex's cold and is grossed out.
